text_: This worked it syncs with my phone but short of it ringing when my phone rings that all it was good for. It did not sync with my calendar and when someone calls their name dose not come up on their phone number. So I gave to my 6 year old to play with.
I've used your product before on other tablets. I don't think I would used anything else. Great packaging, like triple layer protection. Fast shipping and easy to apply.

I grabbed this for Zoom calls after my headset died, and it's been a lifesaver. My coworkers immediately noticed how much clearer I sounded. Setup was literally just plug and play, no drivers needed. For anyone doing remote work or online classes on a budget, I'd definitely recommend snagging this little thing.

love it. charges fast. really fast. really really fast:) When plugged in, and in use (screen on), it still charges, unlike previous adapters i used that died on me on long drives even though they were plugged in and charging.

I was looking for something to protect my Kindle DX in transit. I decided I would rather just take the Kindle out to read than get the kind of case designed to hold the Kindle while you read. I use the Kindle mostly at home, so for most of the time I don't need a case, and when I do go somewhere with it, I can simply take it out of this case to use. This case is ideal for my purposes. It is extremely light--3.5 ounces, according to the Amazon Product Details--and very well padded. It's easy to fit the Kindle in and take it out. The case seems well-made and sturdy. I would like it even better if it had a handle or strap, but that is not enough of a drawback to take away a star. In sum, a fine product that does exactly what I want.

Got these hoping for a noticeable bump in clarity over my factory door speakers, and honestly the mids are decent and they stay clean even when I push them. But they just don't have much presence, I expected them to keep up better with the rest of my setup. I'm running them off a decent head unit, not a separate amp yet, and at moderate volume they're fine for talk radio or background music. Once the volume goes past halfway, they get thin and kinda disappear. Not terrible for the price, but if you're looking for something that fills the cabin without extra power, these might leave you wanting. They're okay, just underwhelming.

I've had this camera for about three years now and while it started off great, things went downhill fast. After the first year, the mode dial got super loose and sometimes it switches settings on its own mid-shot. The rubber grip peeled off in chunks, leaving sticky residue that's impossible to clean. Battery door doesn't click shut anymore so I have to tape it closed. Outdoor shots still look decent but the lens has developed a weird haze that softens everything. For something this pricey, I expected way better build quality. Feels like it wasn't made to last beyond a couple vacations. Pretty disappointed honestly.
Had a little problem seeing the drive with Windows 10. Emailed vendor and got instruction responses promptly. It's a little noisy; but, works. Great value for $20.

I bought this to reduce the number of remotes for my TVs. It works very well with all of my TVs which have cable hookup. You need to check and see if it is compatible with sattelite TV as a friend of mine could not get it to work with a sattelite box hookup. I found it easy to program and it works fine for my purposes.
Honestly thought they'd sound thin for the price but the detail is wild. Snug fit and zero outside noise, perfect for zoning out after a long shift.

The design is convenient for plugging in dc adapters (bricks). The cord to the wall outlet is long and rotates at the surge protector. I'll hopefully never find out how well it functions as a surge protector, but at least the specs are good.

I am technologically challenged and need my son's help to do most everything electronics related. I bought this switch with the idea that if I could not make it work I would ask my son for help. I have a TV with only two HDMI ports and needed to connect it to a DVD and a Roku as well as my cable modem. Guess what! By following the advice in other reviews, I have a working setup. I still don't understand what the 5v charger input is supposed to do or why the "Select" button doesn't select. Also, I don't need to use the remote control (I already have a handful of remotes and don't want to juggle another). It seemed to take forever to get the switcher, but considering the price and the utility of the thing, I am a happy buyer.

I bought this product as well as the TP-LINK AV500 powerline starter kit. Both are great products and can recommend both. One thing to keep in mind, they will be no better than the internet you have coming from your router. I did a YouTube review on both. If your like to see the video here's the link https://youtu.be/yhxNzGU2Nq4.

I didn't realize for years that I didn't have a speaker on the MB is used to build my own PC. I thought the reason I wasn't hearing the boot up beeps was because of my hearing loss. Then I discovered that my cpu fan wasn't running and there wasn't any alarm for overheating nor stopped fan! Yikes. Now the beeps are back! New fan and cooler cpu.
5 stars for functionality but to big for my purpose. Very nice case though.

Came home to find the box sitting on my porch in perfect condition, no dents, no crushed corners, and the packaging inside was seriously impressive. The case was snug in a soft sleeve with a silica packet, which was a nice touch. First impression pulling it out: solid but not bulky, with a smooth finish that feels way more premium than I expected. Buttons click perfectly.
The best there is. Uses a DVI video connection, should be HDMI in 2016. That is a little out of date, had to buy an adapter, no problem though. Love it.
My router was at one end of the house and my PC at the other. I ran this the length of the basement. I was getting 25-40 bps and with this, I am getting a consistent 866 bps. Fantastic.

I purchased a battery for my MacBook Pro computer from AUIO and I am very pleased. It was a Siker brand and showed only one cycle. It truly was a very easy installation. Nicholas with AUIO really takes care of his customers and is very concerned with my being totally satisfied with my purchase. I would totally recommend him for any of your battery purchases.
This lens does not fit the Pentax KR you can see on the mount there is no auto focus connection. Had to return it.

I was skeptical, but these completely eliminated the faint buzzing sensation I'd feel in my fingertips after a long drawing session. No more tingly hands, and I can finally binge-read in bed without that weird electric hum messing with my focus.
Not making it draw high current (<1amp). That said, it works as supposed to as far as providing space saving in comparison to a straight in plug.

Used these earbuds for a few months now and the cord near the jack is already starting to fray. Sound is decent for the price, but I expected them to hold up better with regular, careful use. Disappointing.

I love this stand. I bought it so that I could set up my iPad to use for my guestbook at my wedding and it worked wonderfully. It was sturdy but still easy to adjust for my non-tech-savvy relatives. The only downside (and this is not related to the product) is that I bought it as an warehouse deal which said "minor cosmetic damage possible" but instead it was missing a whole piece. There are supposed to be little foam stickers for the corners that help keep it gripped, but it works fine without them so I just went with it. I'm considering also purchasing the one that mounts on the wall for use in my kitchen.

Great aspect ratio and an affordable way to get in to the ultrawide monitor world. Got it primarily for gaming, and I couldn't be more happy. Paired with a GTX 970 SSC it runs buttery smooth. Colors look great. It may not be the highest resolution out there, but it just looks so great you won't even realize it. Everything is crisp and sharp, and the extra perspective in games is a huge plus.

I wasn't sure this little IR illuminator would do much, but it completely surprised me. The glow is totally invisible to the naked eye, yet my night vision camera picks up a crisp, wide field. Mounting was simple and it barely draws any power. Honestly performs way better than I expected for the price.

I've been using this aux cable daily for almost two months and it's held up perfectly. No crackling or loose connections, even after tossing it in my bag constantly. The woven jacket still looks brand new and doesn't snag. Honestly, I'm surprised how sturdy it is for the price. Definitely a solid, long-lasting pick.
Perfect for my small home office setup. Highly recommend to anyone needing decent sound without the clutter.
For under twenty bucks this 128GB stick is a steal, fast transfers and the slider feels solid.

Got this for my son's school Chromebook and honestly didn't expect much for the cost. The padding is surprisingly solid, zippers work smoothly, and the pocket fits the charger perfectly. For under twenty bucks it's a steal, keeps everything protected and easy to carry.

I'm a photojournalist and bought this monitor after my old 21" CRT died. I've been very pleased with the HP W2408H over the last couple of weeks, but it does need calibration. If you are a gamer or general user, you will love the brilliant picture straight out of the box. For photographers and editors, the colors are too bright and saturated, too "juicy" for critical use. I uninstalled the monitor's software, dragged out my venerable $100 Pantone ColorVision ColorPlus (the consumer colorimeter sensor for monitors) and it gave me a nice, clean image in minutes. A worthwhile investment for anyone. The monitor's wide-screen real estate makes editing hundreds of photos quite a bit easier than with the old square CRT. It also uses less power and produces less heat than a CRT, a nice plus these days. The image is smooth, even and neutral with calibration. Like any LCD, though, you do need to view the screen "on axis." Just to verify what others have said, the built-in speakers are total garbage: weak, tinny, and pointed out the rear so you cannot hear what little sound they do produce. You definitely need external speakers. With the exception of the crummy speakers and the iffy calibration software, the monitor is excellent. Five stars.
Thought the cheap price was a steal, but this thing barely functions. My old one from five years ago runs circles around it. Total letdown.
As a fan, it does the job, but rather loudly. If you're looking for a silent rig, these are not for you.

Potentially good earphones with two major issues. I can rate them 2.5 to 3 stars based on the units I received. First of all, the construction quality is really good. Buttons, earbuds, cables, eartips - everything is made of very good quality. The aluminium case is another pleasant bonus. Speaking of sound quality, it's good but not great. I felt the bass is weak even after tweaking equalizer on my laptop as well as phone. Audio (especially music) feels little flat as compared to other earphones I tested. Having said that, it doesn't mean it's bad and is surely acceptable for daily use. The volume controls operate individually (independent of source volume) so that's a very good feature. Range is very good. I didn't notice audio breaking while walking away from source unless I went too far. As compared to other earphones I've tested, these have larger bluetooth range. Two major issues these earphones have are battery life and in-ear fit. In-ear fit is not quite good. It keeps falling off the ears even after trying all eartips and earfins. I found mid-sized eartips better fitting than others. It's certainly manageable when you are doing a desk top and not moving around much. They fell off once when I was walking around in office. Battery life is the biggest disappointment. The earphones get fully charged in less than 2 hours (as is the case with most of other earphones) but I got "battery level medium" audio prompt within first half hour of use. I used then for another 40 minutes and I got "battery low" prompt and within few minutes they switched off as battery was completely off. So I could use them for less than 1.5 hours after fully charging them. I'm not sure if the battery issue is by design or if the units I received have this defect. Conclusion - These earphones surely have potential but aforementioned two major issues disappoint you. If they at least had good battery life, they surely deserve 4.5 stars.

Our Netgear router was needing a restart several times a day and gave us poor coverage in our new house. The ASUS router has functioned flawlessly, gives great coverage across both floors of our house and hasn't needed a restart yet! I love the UI and the ability to look at the connected devices. The dual band is great!
Battery worked fine but screwdriver not included. Used battery to replace Forerunner 305 wrist GS battery with little modification involved.
Switching to this camera made photography fun again, fast autofocus, vivid colors, and the compact body fits my everyday bag perfectly. Worth every penny for the upgrade.

I grabbed this mainly for casual phone VR stuff, and honestly it's okay but nothing special. Setup was clunkier than I thought, and the lenses have a pretty narrow sweet spot, I'm constantly adjusting the head straps to keep things clear. It fits my Pixel fine, but light leaks in from the sides kill immersion. For the price I wasn't expecting premium, but I did expect a little less fiddling just to get a decent picture. It works, just not as smoothly as the product page made it sound.
it is a ok. it fit me good. I will call again in the near future. i am very glad
I'd tell any DIY product photographer to grab this, the thick paper holds up great and the deep, even tone saves so much editing time. Just keep a couple clips handy.
Holds my small monitor snug but the tilt adjust is a bit stiff to get just right.

It does the job for pulling photos off my SD and microSD cards, and the USB port is handy in a pinch. For the price, I can't complain too much. But honestly, the thing is clunky. It's noticeably bulkier than Apple's own adapter and sticks out awkwardly from the iPad. Build quality feels a bit cheap, like the plastic casing might crack if you toss it in a bag. I've had it disconnect a couple times if I bump the tablet while it's plugged in, which is annoying. For occasional use on a budget, it's fine, just don't expect it to be a smooth or elegant solution. You get what you pay for.
When i got my PS3, there was only one missing link to complete my total entertainment center and this is it.
Now I don't have to keep my wife awake while I watch TV. I can stay up later and watch the DVR without disturbing her sleep.

Plugged this into my desktop and it just worked right away. WiFi is snappy on both bands and range reaches my back patio without drops. Bluetooth paired my earbuds on the first try with zero fuss. Whole setup felt effortless. Honestly surprised how smooth everything runs, solid piece of hardware.

These sound way better than I expected for the price, crisp highs and surprisingly deep lows. They don't quite hit reference level, but for everyday listening they're warm and immersive. Just a solid, no-regret purchase that punches above its weight.

I am a professional filmmaker and videographer. I've been working in the industry for six years, and I just wrapped up principle photography on a feature length PBS documentary. I shoot 1080p high definition HDV video and the ONLY mini-DV tape stock I trust is Panasonic AY-DVM63PQ, better known as Panasonic PQ, for "professional quality." Although this tape is designed for DV video, I shoot HDV (high definition video) on it all the time. In my experience, it performs just as well as HDV tape, if not better. I have shot literally hundreds of hours of 1080p high definition video on this tape stock, and I've almost never had a dropped frame. Do NOT buy the cheaper consumer-grade tapes (e.g. DVC tape). Those cheap consumer tapes are a recipe for disaster. If you use cheap consumer tapes, frames will be dropping like flies. The bottom line is that professional work requires professional tape, and professional tape doesn't get better than this. This tape is incredibly reliable, and perfect for archiving. I just pulled some archived tapes off the shelves, 3-4 years old and still playing back at perfect quality, no signs of degradation at all. This stuff LASTS! Unbeatable quality for the price. Worth every penny. Only buy NEW (used tape is not reliable) and be sure to buy the FIVE PACKS which offer the best deal for your money.
Worked fine the first two weeks, then just died on me. The plastic casing also cracked near the plug. Total junk, don't bother.

Budget MOBO is what you might be thinking but don't let this little thing fool you. I have this paired with my 4700K that is OC'd to 4.5ghz, now you are probably asking how I OC'd since this board isn't a Z Series board but it doesn't matter. This MOBO was one of the adapters of the Non-Z OC, just download the proper BIOS and you can do it too without having to worry about voltages. This product is solid and I recommend it specially if you know what you are doing!
Fits the GPU well, but wish it had better airflow.

Excellent units with rechargeable batteries (included) and the privacy codes necessary to modify the basic channels if desired. These units are able to communicate universally with other units, even if a privacy code is being used. We had originally purchased a less expensive toy-like Motorola set for use with our car club but they were lacking the privacy code capability used by our club and thus useless to us. They were returned for these units and we are now in full communication again.
Honestly, it feels just as solid as the pricey ones but for way less cash.

I love this bluetooth speaker. The sound is great and it is small enough to travel with. This thing is well built and tough and it can easily be used in any outdoor events such as hiking, biking, camping, etc in any weather conditions. I was able to pair it to my ipod and iphone in a matter of seconds. I cant wait to use it on my next outdoor adventure.

I loved this device when I first received it. It is slim, lightweight, had a good feel to the keys and was a USB device. However, after about a month of using it a few of the keys started to kind of stick. Next thing I knew, it was causing my computer to go crazy. I wasn't able to type using it was flashing text. As soon as I disconnected the device my computer worked perfectly again. I can only assume one of the keys was permanently stuck (although I can't figure out which one) and so it was trying to type that character over and over. I am just out of the return window and so I am stuck with it. Luckily it wasn't more expensive than it is. I believe I saw a few comments here about people with similar experiences. Please believe them as I think this is likely to happen to you too. Oh yeah, I am a very light computer user. This was only used for personal business not a work computer.
I wasn't sure it'd feel this premium for the price, but it snaps on perfectly and barely adds any weight. Honestly exceeds what I hoped for.

This thing showed up a full day early, which was a nice surprise. Box was pristine, thick foam inserts holding everything snug, zero scuffs or rattling. First impression pulling it out: the slim bezel and matte black finish feel way more premium than I expected at this price. Literally one cable to connect, and Windows recognized it instantly. The stand snaps in without tools and is rock steady. Powering it on, the screen is crisp right out of the box, no dead pixels, and the default brightness was perfect for my home office. Really pleased.

Bought these for a trip to Italy and they worked flawlessly the entire two weeks. Plug fit snug in every outlet we tried, no wiggling or falling out. Used them with our dual-voltage hair dryer and phone chargers without any issues. Compact enough to toss in a carry-on without adding bulk. Honestly surprised by the build quality for the price, feels solid, not flimsy. If you travel often and know your gear's voltage specs, these are a no-brainer.

that such a simple little thing would help me so much!? This has a very nice weight to it, so it stays in place. It's not HEAVY, but it's enough to keep it where you put it. I had two cords on my nightstand that i had to wedge between the clock and the lamp, or they would fall behind the nightstand. This solved the problem nicely. I love Quirky items. Browse their stuff.
These cords fixed the flickering I was dealing with on my second screen. Picture is super crisp now and everything just feels snappier. Definitely picking up a few more for my desk setup.

Nice quality and it worked well for our online wedding ceremony! We set it up on a fireplace mantle behind the officiant and it picked up all our voices well. The mic was hooked up to a hidden Linux netbook that recorded the audio while a camcorder recorded the video. Sound and audio were then fed into a Webex session. No complaints on the audio at all.
Arrived fast in a sturdy little box, felt well-protected. Plugged it in and it just worked immediately.

Got this for Christmas last year. Had 2 6.5s and a bridged 12 hooked up. Ran 0 gauge from battery and ground and 12g to speakers. Worked well for about 6 months. Around July or August it started having issues channels failing in an odd way. Any amp Ive had in the past that has gone bad the channel stayed dead. It developed a whine in one speaker and then channels would randomly cut out until you turned it off for a few minutes and back on again. This would cut out in one channel one day and another on a different day. Rechecked wiring and all is good. Distortion is bad at higher volumes and bass is very muffled. For the price it sounded as expected and for the time it did work it worked ok. I did expect it to last longer than it did.
This tower is big for a mid tower but I like it Came warped in plastic Clean Nice paint job Cant complain yet great tower!!!

My cable management was a nightmare until I started using this. Being able to power my router and a small USB hub from one compact brick cleared up so much clutter behind my desk. Super stable output, no drops, and it barely gets warm.

I keep these stashed all over, car, nightstand, laptop bag. The micro-woven texture grabs fingerprints off my tablet without leaving lint or streaks. Way better than using my shirt. Finally a cloth that actually lifts grime instead of just smearing it around.
Very sturdy surge strip for a solid price. Haven't had my house struck by lightning, so I can't comment on the effectiveness, but I'm trusting $1000 of hardware to it.
Sound is not that great; get what I pay for

Unfortunately this particular unit did not work in our walkies. I'm putting at "I Like It" as it wasn't that it would work just the stems on the plug were too long for the particular motorola we use at work so I had to return.

I've ordered a ton of electronics online and the way these arrived honestly surprised me. The box was sturdy, no dents, with tight foam inserts keeping everything snug, no rattling at all. Peeling off the protective film was super satisfying, and right out of the gate they felt really premium in hand. Pairing took maybe ten seconds with my phone, and the ear cushioning had that fresh, plush feel you only get with careful packaging. I wore them for a solid three hours the first day while catching up on a podcast and tackling yard work, and they stayed put without any pinching. Solid first impression all around.

I highly recommend this case. Especially if you use your phone for things like watching video, reading documents and such. The case is very protective at the corners which is where a phone gets most damage. The two features i love are the standing slot and the auto on. Whenever you open the case the screen automatically turns on. This feature doesnt waste any battery and it saves you if your power button is losing. you don't even have to latch it everytime, I only latch it when i put it in my pocket. Normally i just let the cover down until the latch is resting on the edge and it shuts the screen off. The inside is very soft and won't scratch the screen and the outside feels great. Nothing i can find wrong with it except i wish it covered the edges of the phone more like it does the corners.

I got this thing back in March and it worked fine out of the box on Windows 10, no driver nonsense needed. Fast forward four months of light use, maybe two calls a week, and the mic just gave up. At first people said I sounded muffled, then it turned into a crackly mess, and now it's basically dead. The plastic housing also started separating near the USB connector for no reason. I don't toss my gear around, it literally just sits on my desk. Tried different ports, different computers, same result. For something that barely moved, it should've lasted way longer. Total waste of money, flimsy build, zero longevity.

I picked this up to do a general site survey around the house and ended up blown away by what this little adapter can do. Setup was completely painless on my Linux laptop, and the range absolutely smokes the built-in card. I'm pulling stable connections from the far corner of the backyard where my phone drops off entirely. The external antenna makes a massive difference, and it's handled everything I've thrown at it without dropping once. Build quality feels solid too, doesn't feel flimsy like some of the cheap dongles I've tried. If you need serious wireless range or just a reliable adapter that doesn't quit, this thing is a steal. Highly recommended.

I normally use a Noizfree headset, which has stellar sound quality. This one drops out the lower frequencies, and even the remaining ones just sound vastly off.. It's very hard to listen to music with this thing with all the distortion and missing frequencies. Tried different levels of volume on both the unit and the phone. Even plugging my noizfree set into the unit and bypassing the neckloop makes very little difference. The neckloop is mono as expected, but the jack is stereo capable. I suspect something is off with their bluetooth implementation.. it sounds like an AM radio. Even given the limitations of Bluetooth audio, the unit itself feels and sounds cheap. The volume control on the side is audibly noisy, when you change volumes you can hear it scratching. As others have noted, pairing can be a pain... it did work with a Galaxy S3 and S4 after a few attempts.

This lasted about 1 month and then started to come apart from regular use around my arm at the gym. I had to sew it back together but it has been a frustrating purchase. I used it with the IPOD classic pictured in the icon.
Had this thing for months now, still solid as day one no issues.

The screen looks crisp and the build is solid, but the battery drains crazy fast even with light use. I'm lucky to get four hours, which makes it frustrating for travel. I wanted to like this tablet, but the poor battery life just kills the experience for me.
Excellent product, charged both batteries quickly, fits perfect and works the same as stock battery.

I grabbed this to cover a dead zone in my garage. Setup took maybe five minutes, plug it in, press WPS, done. I wasn't expecting miracles, and it's true you lose some speed on the extended network. I'm seeing about half the bandwidth I get near the main router, but for streaming music or pulling up a YouTube tutorial out there, it's perfectly solid. Range is slightly better than I expected given the price. Just don't expect full fiber speeds through concrete walls and you'll be happy.
Very pleased with product. Was easy to put screen protector on and cleans off well (finger prints). I would buy again.
Low-cost production, without any of printed instructions. Is not worth that money. Such a large mouse does not have a place where you can put the USB receiver
Perfect for small living rooms. Pairs instantly with my TV. Great for movie nights. I'd tell any casual viewer to grab one.
Gets very warm if you plug in multiple devices, otherwise works great.

Great quality product, I purchased for HP 7 android tablet, doesn't fit quite right. I took a change in this purchase and it is my fault, not the fault of the product because HP Slate is slightly different than the tablet I have. The product is excellent and would have been really nice if it fit my product like a glove.

I've already returned this case. The bottom piece would not say snapped on at all. The keyboard cover was great and the colors of the case were vibrant and really flashy. If the bottom piece was fixed, this case would be great. But as it is? Save your money.
I've been using this for a few months now and it's held up great through daily shoots. Build feels solid and I haven't noticed any wobble or wear on the joints.

Buggy when setting up m. 2 wound up buying a pcie m. 2 extension card which defeated the purpose of this board. A simple bios update would fix it but a this is a dying socket type don't hold your breath.

Having used an Apple wireless mini keyboard with a Macbook Pro at my former job, I wanted to find something similar to use with the Dell laptop provided by my new employer. A full sized keyboard takes up too much desk space, and I had gotten used to the mini layout. This was the most similar product I could find. I've been using this keyboard and mouse for a few days now and have no real complaint. The layout is familiar, so I'm not fatfingering things. The mouse is comfortable and responsive - pretty much the same size and shape as the compact Logitech mouse I use with my home system. Good things: - Keyboard and mouse are both solid and well made - They both *look* solid and well made - attractive design and finish - They both work as they should, and well Not so good things (and this is a fairly subjective list): - Keyboard is a bit loud and clicky for my taste - Took a bit of time to figure out why they keys did not appear mapped to the right characters - the num lock was on. This may be a problem with my laptop, and not the keyboard - I wish the mouse had rubbery sides - I wish the keyboard had a power switch In summary, I'm satisfied with the product and consider the price very reasonable. I would recommend it to anyone who prefers a mini keyboard.
I honestly expected a bit more heft for the price, but it's holding my 27-inch screen steady. Setup took under ten minutes and feels secure enough.

I was honestly surprised by how much laptop I got for the money. The screen is huge and crisp, perfect for streaming shows without squinting, and the sound is decent enough that I don't always bother with headphones. It's not ultra-light, but for the price I expected some bulk, and it still fits fine in my backpack. Performance has been smooth for everyday tasks like email, spreadsheets, and way too many open browser tabs. Battery life gets me through most of a workday if I'm not pushing it hard. My only small gripe is the trackpad feels a little plasticky, but a cheap wireless mouse solved that. Overall, hard to beat for the price.
I like the fact that it does not have to be recharged so often and more for the fact that it works with all of my electronics. I am a gadget person and this is an awesome tool for me.
Perfect for birders who want lightweight, sturdy scope support.
This is the Mercedes of home networking routers. Fills my house with a strong signal. the 5GHz band is perfect with my Nvidia SHIELD. Great management interface.

I expected these remotes to be just okay, but they're surprisingly solid. Signal punches through walls with zero lag, and pairing them to our secondary TVs took seconds. Way better range and feel than the flimsy ones I've tried before.

Got this way faster than expected, shipped same day and arrived in a snug little box with zero damage. The packaging felt premium, not just tossed in a bubble mailer. First impression: the material looks clean and sturdy, and the included stylus is a nice surprise. It's a bit bulkier than I imagined due to the inner velcro setup, and I wish the magnetic closure triggered sleep mode, but overall it's a solid, practical case that feels well-made right out of the box.
Good basic case with pretty good stand capabilities for the price range.

Great camera that easy to use, take gorgeous pictures and is easy on the wallet. It's a little small, so if you have large hands, you may be a bit uncomfortable with it, but otherwise, it's great value for the price.
Arrived fast in perfect packaging, these feel sturdy and ready for our next road trip.

I wanted to like this. It seemed like it would be perfect for travel - I wouldn't have to haul chargers, just cords & this strip. Well, I just tried everything out. The 3 power outlets all work, but only 1 of the 3 usb ports works. Guess I picked the wrong power strip. I don't I know how hard it will be to return or exchange it, but due to 2 of 3 usb ports being dead, its not much better than any cheap old power stripI have laying around. Glad I ordered far ahead of travel so I can find an alternative. Will change / update 1 star review if I get a replacement on which all features work.

I've purchased two of these mice. The first lasted about 3 years before the double click issue became so frustrating it was not longer usable. I did like the feel and other features of the mouse, so I thought I'd give it another chance and purchased another mouse. Keep in mind this was when the price was still $45. That mouse had the same click issue but happen much quicker, I'd say 3 months. Since then I have moved to a $10 wireless mouse and it has outlasted both of these "quality" mice. VicTsing MM057 2.4G Wireless Portable Mobile Mouse Optical Mice with USB Receiver, 5 Adjustable DPI Levels, 6 Buttons for Notebook, PC, Laptop, Computer, Macbook - Black I've purchased a bunch of Logitech items. While they seem to be decent quality at first, they tend to degrade quickly. I've withheld from purchasing Logitech items until they can regain reputation.

I picked this up hoping for decent sound and it completely blew me away. My whole setup came alive with crisp highs and solid low end that doesn't distort. Really impressed with how clean everything runs even at higher volumes.

Beautiful television. The picture is bright and colorful. The TV is well built and mounts easily to a wall. I had an issue where the One Connect Mini that came with the TV didn't work. I spent about 3 hours on the phone with Samsung customer support, and I was sent a new one within 4 days. Now the TV works perfectly. I love all of the apps that come with the TV. I haven't had the chance to watch an 4K content as of yet, but I'm sure it will be amazing. This is my third Samsung TV, and they last and have great picture quality. The remote takes some getting used it, as it is pointer-based, but I use a Logitech Smart Control for my home theater.
Bought it as a Christmas gift for a friend he likes them. I tried them on I think they made holding the phone to slippery.

The Bose Solo 15 is a compact TV/computer sound system that improved the sound we get when watching TV. For us it fixes the previous problem of turning up the sound to hear whispered conversations on TV or videos and then having the music or sound effects blare deafeningly, throughout movies and TV we watch. It is black and one piece that is three inches high, 25 inches long and 14 inches wide. It is designed to have your flat screen TV, weighing up to 75 pounds, set on it, which is convenient. It should be noted that this unit is similar to other `soundbar' types of speaker systems; therefore if you sit too far from your TV you may not fully experience left and right channel separation. Set up of this Bose is a snap with one optical connector or two RCA connectors, plus it needs to be plugged into an AC source. The remote which is included can be used for gaming systems and Blu-ray players. This unit would be ideal for use with big monitor computer setups, especially for gaming since the bass quality is tunable by an easy to reach single knob on the back of the unit. If your TV is in a cabinet take the Bose dimensions into account before ordering it.

This thing rocks. Bought it to drive a sub in my truck and it's way more than I expected for the cost. Install was a breeze and everything I needed was in the box. It runs cool, stays quiet, and the bass is tight and clean. Honestly couldn't be happier with the whole experience.

This is part of an alarm system and you need one if you have a garage door. The plunger needs to be set different,there is no need for it to have to good as far up in the unit as it does. You have to get it set so it pushes all the way up. There is no lee/way on this. I got it ok after three tries.

It is very sturdy and well built and works with my Windows 10 operating system. I haven't tested it by putting in the washer or dropping it on the ground, but it claims it can survive some abuse. I would believe it based on how rugged it is. It is very small so make sure you do not lose it. My only complaint is that I wish they made these in a 256GB or 512GB models.
Simple and functional. Very responsive and reliable. Have few of those for several years and I keep purchasing them.

I recently bought this product, tried to install using installation CD, which locked up on the 9th step. Called tech support, was told the product, which was bought that day, was no longer under warranty as Lynksys bases warranty on production date, not date of purchase,(i'm not making this up!) so warranty expired while on Staples shelf. "Manilla Mary" told they would believe me this one time and give me a one time complimentary tech support session. Declined and went and exchanged for another of the same model, which had 9 months remaining on the one year warranty, spent 9 (yes, 9, i'm not joking) hours with "Rangoon Randy" "Phillipine Phil" etc over three nights only to find they are clueless on setting this up. I still don't have a functioning router and don't know what the answer is but Lynksys most DEFINITLY isn't it.
After a month, the buttons got mushy and the case started cracking. Disappointed.
This cable really makes the connection for me. Nice and thick too.

I grabbed this mouse for travel since it's compact and skips the USB dongle mess. Pairing was easy enough, and it fits fine in a small bag pocket. That's where the excitement ends. The tracking feels slightly laggy compared to my old Logitech, especially on uneven surfaces like a couch cushion. Scroll wheel is noisy and a bit stiff. Battery life is okay but nothing special, I'm changing it more often than I expected for casual use. It works for basic tasks, but I was hoping for smoother performance given the price. Not terrible, just underwhelming. If you need something ultra-portable and don't mind a few quirks, it's passable. I'll probably keep it as a backup.
Had this on my Kindle for over eight months now, tossed in bags daily. Still looks brand new, no peeling or cracks. Solid protection without adding bulk.

I use this to backup pictures and videos after my old external hard drive got full. It has faster read and write speeds than my old drive (which was also USB 3) and it can hold much more (my old drive was 1TB which formatted down to around 900GB). Sturdy construction and it fits great inside AmazonBasics External Case. Very happy with its performance and portability.
Snapped it into my Hero 3+ and the 4K footage is super stable, no lag at all.

Was thinking about getting a AirPlay device but decided on getting this Bluetooth wireless mobile speaker. It sounds great and is easy to set up. I was worried about sound quality of Bluetooth vs AirPlay but it does not seem like an issue. Also Bluetooth will support devices beyond Apple products.
Battery lasts through our whole outdoor movie night setup without needing a recharge.
This TV mount worked well and came with everything listed in the description. I would purchase this again.
Plugged into my Windows laptop for Zoom calls and it just worked. Crisp video and clear audio all meeting long.

For the price I expected at least passable stability, but nope. Used it four times now and every clip has micro-jitters that ruin the shot unless I'm on polished concrete. Any texture in the ground and it's basically unusable without heavy stabilization in post. Feels sturdy in hand but the small hard wheels just transmit every pebble. If you're shooting anything beyond a studio floor, save your money and get something with actual suspension. Disappointing value overall.

It gets the job done once you figure out the quirks, but runtime is way shorter than expected. Mine lasted barely 12 minutes before cutting out. The whole setup feels a little clunky overall, and it really could use a basic battery level display so you're not left guessing mid-use. Okay, not great.
Arrived super fast, packed like a tank, and fired right up, instant smile on my face.
Does not work good with GoPro silver 4 when recording 1080p over 2 mins at a time.

Really easy install for Windows Vista Home basic. Plugged the card in and it was detected. Did not have to install any software. Plugged in my network settings and was on the internet in 5 minutes. Good connection, but my laptop is faster and it also has a g wireless card. Only reason I gave 4 out of 5 stars. At this price & ease of use, worth the investment!

I've had this tablet for about five months now, and the build quality is honestly disappointing. The back casing started creaking after a few weeks, and now there's a noticeable flex near the charging port. The screen picked up faint scratches way too easily, even though I've only used a soft cloth for cleaning. Worse, the power button has become mushy and unresponsive, I have to press it multiple times just to wake the thing up. After light daily use, the battery cover is already warping slightly at one corner. For a device that's supposed to be portable, it feels fragile and poorly put together. I'm not rough with my gadgets at all, but this one's aging like it's been through years of abuse. Really not built to last.

I got these at a discount for my honest review. These come in handy for cleaning your screen on your electronics. They are not real big, so I also carry a couple in my purse to clean my glasses They work well.

I picked up the Fi70 just hoping to add some decent sound to my living room without completely draining my bank account, and honestly, I'm shocked at what I got for the money. The low end is clean and punchy, not just a muddy thump, and the highs stay crisp even when I really crank it. Streaming over Bluetooth was dead simple, paired in seconds and hasn't dropped once. I've had friends assume I spent triple what I actually paid. If you want a serious audio upgrade that doesn't feel like a compromise, this thing absolutely delivers. Best home audio purchase I've made in years.
Big at first, but for the 80$ I spent on it, this was 100% worth the buy! you get used to it!

Great card. Thanks to the review posted by other users, I already knew it will work with Linux. Plugged it in and I was surfing in a minute. Great replacement for the on-board NIC that went bad in my laptop. No need to install any drivers or re-boot in Linux.

I grabbed these mainly for the macro lens but ended up loving the whole kit. The glass is surprisingly clear for the price, and I've been getting sharp close-ups of flowers and insects with zero vignetting. Honestly didn't expect this level of quality without spending way more. Super happy with the results.

What! Why would I want to make my Windows laptop a Chromebook. It's for my 10 year-old daughter. I got tired of rescuing her from the PC whenever she ended up somewhere no child should be in the Windows OS like the control panel or fiddling with files in Windows Explorer. The USB drive is big enough to house the entire Chrome OS on it with enough room for wallpapers and such and its bootable. It is small and hardly noticeable which allows the notebook to still feel portable without removing the drive. Great product, amazing price.

Bought this for my dual 24-inch screens and honestly expected it to be flimsy at this price. Nope, rock solid, super easy to assemble, and the clamp holds tight on my wooden desk. Freed up so much space. Just make sure your monitors are the same size and you're golden.

I bought this to use in transferring data among my various netbooks, laptops and desktop computers I have at home and in my workshop. Seemed to perform just fine when connected directly to onboard USB ports, but very, very sensitive to being connected via powered USB hubs. With one system (WinXP home with a powered USB hub) it totally took down the USB bus as long as it was plugged in - not good at all. So - seems OK as long as you're not using a USB hub. But unfortunately it's a pain for me to disconnect something else in order for me to plug directly into an onboard USB port on the one system.

We have a 3.5 year old who uses the iPad a little bit about three or four days a week. Needless to say, protecting the investment in the product is of paramount importance. This cover is touted as "kid proof" and so far it has been exactly that. It's replacing an OEM cover from Apple, which in retrospect was a waste of money by comparison. It fits like a glove and so far has been good protection. We have, fortunately, not had occasion to do a serious drop test with it, but it has withstood drops from the couch and table just fine. I'm quite pleased with it.
Really Nice. If you need a good Reflector with diffuser you should buy this now
Super impressed with the range on this thing. Setup was a breeze and now I get solid coverage way out in the barn.

Had these in my F-150 doors for about 14 months now, paired with an aftermarket head unit. They've held up great through brutal summers and plenty of dusty backroads. The tweeters are still crisp without any distortion creeping in, and the mid-bass remains tight even after countless hours of use. I did notice the highs can get a bit sharp on certain tracks, so I dialed back the EQ a notch. Installation took some patience with the adapter rings, but nothing snapped or loosened over time. Overall, they've proven tough and reliable. Solid bang for your buck if you want clarity that lasts.

Purchased for a room with studs that were 24" apart. Worked well and holds up a pretty heavy tv. Moves easily and I have had no issues with it. Installation was straightforward. The tilt adjustment levers on the back are not designed well and will stop against the tv, preventing you from adjusting it. The levers are just a tad bit too long.

I bought these hoping for something reliable during my commute, but they've been a letdown. The sound is okay for the price, nothing crisp, just flat mids, but the real problem is how fast they drain. I'll get maybe 40 minutes into an audiobook and they're dead, even after sitting in the case overnight. The case itself feels flimsy, and half the time it doesn't seem to charge them fully no matter how long I leave it plugged in. For earbuds advertised with decent playtime, it's pretty disappointing. I wasn't expecting premium quality, but I did think they'd last through a single gym session without quitting.
good when it lasted but poor quality and reliability do not warrant the purchase. died after 6 months of use.

The box showed up pretty beat up, which made me nervous, but everything inside looked okay at first glance. Packaging felt a little flimsy for an electronic item. Haven't tested it heavily yet, but it powered on fine. Just wish they'd shipped it with better protection.
Simply put, these are blazing fasts drives at an excellent price point! Much faster than the basic Kingston SSDNow 240GB, nice for an OS with a few of your favorite games!
For the price, I honestly expected these to feel flimsy, but they're surprisingly crisp and solid. The included case is a nice touch too. They've held up way better than I thought they would.
I bought this for my suv I ask the the men who was install my alarm so he said the fuse in my car is too sensitive to jump so I still have in the box the camara working fine

I was nervous switching to Mac after decades on Windows, figuring there'd be a steep learning curve and maybe some buyer's remorse. Honestly, it exceeded every expectation I had. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and within a day the trackpad gestures and overall flow felt completely natural. The build quality is on another level, solid aluminum, no flex, and it stays cool and silent even with a dozen tabs and Spotify running. Battery life genuinely lasts my full workday without hunting for an outlet. I'm not doing anything heavy, just browsing, streaming, and light photo editing, but everything feels snappy and polished. Wish I'd made the jump sooner instead of overthinking the price.

I was basically hoping for a knockoff Apple Magic Keyboard feel without paying that premium, and this gets surprisingly close. The keys are slightly mushier than what I remember on a Mac, but after a day I stopped noticing. The layout is compact without feeling cramped, which was my main worry. Pairing with my Lenovo laptop was instant and it hasn't dropped connection once. My only nitpick is the plastic body picks up fingerprints way faster than expected, so it looks a bit grubby by lunchtime. Battery life seems solid so far, I'm still on the included AAs after a week of heavy use. For the price, it punches above its weight.
Runs fine for a couple months, but the plastic clips started cracking after a few weeks. Still going though.

Description says that it works with 7 to 10 inch devices. I tried putting an 8in Dell Venue into the stand and it was too narrow to be held by the arms in portrait mode, and in landscape the arms do not open enough to allow the tablet to fit in. It does work well with a 10" device though.

Love this wrist strap! It was comfortable the whole trip and i carried my camera, ready for action, for many hours at a time without fatigue. This says a lot, since i have nerve damage from carpal tunnel issues. I could not use the neck strap that came with my camera as I was afraid it would hook the LCD and break the thin connector when i had the LCD tilted. I also got the sling that pairs with this wrist strap, but i didn't use it on the trip, as the wrist strap was handy and comfortable. The sling and the wrist strap share the same clip, so they are easily interchangable. Maybe next trip.

I bought this product a year ago. I got the pink one and it's quite eye catching. But beyond that, it does what it promises to do. Nothing changed with the product and it does protect my Ipod touch. It's sturdy and comfortable to use. I will recommend it to anyone.
they work as advertised. I installed one on my Canon D30 and it floats. It sure will help when I go Kayaking.
Perfect for my Raspberry Pi setup. Plug-and-play on Linux, no drivers needed. The blue activity LED is a nice touch. Solid build for the price.

Does the job. Plenty of room for backups on both my and my spouse's computers. I would prefer a cable with standard USB on both ends. I do not like the rather flimsy dual mini connector at the drive end. I've had this type of connector before and it failed to make a reliable connection after less than a year of use on another brand backup drive.
After trying 3 different models, this one it has the best reception from my phone and tablet!! It works as a transmitter too if you have an equipment without Bluetooth like an old mp3 player.

Bought this because I thought it was time to upgrade my old Harmony One. The physical Harmony Home remote is fantastic. I love the simple design, love the fact that you can program buttons to do two different things depending on if you long press or short press. Love that it's RF. If Logitech threw an LCD and backlight on this thing, it would be the best remote they've ever created. Where the problems begin is there's no LCD on the remote. Your smartphone is supposed to take on that task and unfortunately, the app that makes this possible just sucks. Sometimes it's slow to connect to the hub, sometimes it crashes, sometimes the hub just stops responding to it. More often than not, I would grab the OEM remotes because it was so much faster and less frustrating. That's not a good sign. I actually managed to put up with the app for a while, but the thing that had me running back to my Harmony One was when something gets out of sync (happens more than you think), there's no "help" button on the remote. I had to dig my phone out, unlock it, launch the app, wait for it to find the hub, hope the app doesn't crash, then fix the sync problem. This would take me 2 seconds to fix with my old Harmony One. With Harmony Home, it takes a good minute. I don't like the direction Logitech is going with the smartphone thing. They need to focus on making good physical remotes. Does anyone really enjoy using their smartphone as remote? It's clumsy and takes too long to do anything with. I cringed every time I needed to launch the Harmony app to do something. I need tactile buttons Logitech!

This product is exactly what I wanted when I read the specs and ordered it. I have had no problems with it, and I highly recommend it. I was also happy to know in advance, before placing the order, that there were no shipping charges.

The wife dropped our iPad 2 and the digitizer completely shattered. Most folks think it's the screen, but the screen actually sits down below this. It took quite a while to remove all the broken glass, but once I did installation of this was simple. Looks almost like a brand new iPad.

Arrived two days early in rock-solid packaging with plenty of bubble wrap. Pulled it out and it looked absolutely flawless, not a smudge on the glass. Mounted it immediately and the autofocus snaps right in. Feels way better than expected.
The pairing process was a nightmare and it disconnected constantly. Total waste of money.
I work on alot of computers for people. Never went wrong with APC batteries. Fast shipping and a genuine replacement part. APC has a good reputation and couldn't be happier with my purchase..

I was honestly shocked at how fast this thing arrived, ordered late Thursday and it was on my doorstep Saturday morning. The packaging was rock solid, double boxed with dense foam inserts so nothing had a chance to rattle around. Unboxing felt premium right away; the fabric grilles were spotless and the remote had that nice heft to it. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and firing it up for the first time literally made me grin. Just a great out-of-the-box experience all around.

I am ashamed to say that I was enough of a sucker to have gone through three of these cassette adapters. The sound quality was terrible and for some odd reason the cables split exposing frayed wiring that eventually breaks and severing the connections also the tape mechanisms constantly make clicking and snapping sounds while in operation. I do not recommend this product to anyone. Try Sony's adapters.
Working ok just not great at night or when it has a little water on it. But for the price I would say it is doing its job for now.

it does the job. the fans aren't any louder than the ones built into my laptop. truthfully i can't tell the difference in the sound. i would recommend it fully. it puts the computer at the right hight and angle too.

Look, this changed my life. My wife is not as happy because now I spend money trying to make my favorite games go 144hz at full detail all the time. But I am happy and out of her hair more often so she is fine with it. But seriously. If you can push 60 fps at 1080p all the time and have an AMD card then you should look into getting this. The difference between 60fps with VSync and 70-100 on FreeSync is amazing. Things look more real. Just do it.

I've been using this silicone cover on my MacBook Air for a few months now and honestly it's a small thing that makes a big difference. I'm not super careful with snacks near my laptop, so this keeps crumbs and everyday grime off the actual keys. The grey matches the space grey finish almost exactly, which was a nice surprise. It does add a slight resistance when typing, but I adjusted within a day and now I barely notice. I'd recommend this to students or anyone who works in slightly dusty spaces, or just someone who eats lunch at their desk way too often. It's also super easy to peel off and rinse clean, which I do about once a week. Great little investment.
I drove 100 miles an hour And it stayed on

I had a chance to listen to these at a local audio shop. I wasn't too impressed at all. Really disappointing sound quality considering the price. The mids are what's really bad I think--they have a tin-can quality to them that's not pleasant or realistic. This is common in the Beats line, I think the Studios are slightly better but they have other issues. For the same price or less, you could get some really great headphones. Check out AKG's Q-701, or Sennheiser Amperiors, or Audio-Technica M50's, or Shure 840's. They're all much more realistic sounding with clearer mids, smooth highs, and tight realistic bass. You'll learn to love and appreciate the more realistic sound other headphones provide, and your wallet will appreciate it too.

Arrived way faster than I expected and the box was surprisingly sturdy, no dents or loose bits. First impression is it's a neat little unit, super lightweight. Setup took maybe ten seconds, and the button click to switch inputs is satisfying. For the price, I'm honestly impressed. Perfect for my messy desk setup.

Really solid little adapter for the price. I grabbed this mainly to connect an external SSD to my laptop, and it's been flawless. Sustained transfers hover around 400-450 MB/s with a good USB-C 3.1 cable, which is more than enough for my photo backups and video work. Build quality feels sturdy, the plug seats snugly without wobbling, and it doesn't get alarmingly hot under load. I've also used it with flash drives and a card reader, all worked first try. For under ten bucks, it punches way above its weight. If you just need a dependable USB-A port on a modern machine without spending a fortune, this does exactly what it should.
Perfect for hobbyist photographers who need a no-fuss, durable cold shoe mount.
Very pretty and offers great protection. My son loves it!
Arrived fast, packed well, and worked perfectly right out of the box.

I needed something sturdy but not bulky for daily commuting with my Surface Pro and accessories, and this bag nailed it. The padding gives me real peace of mind when I toss it in the car, and the zippers glide smoothly without snagging. I can fit my charger, notebook, snacks, and even a light jacket without it looking overstuffed. Honestly, it just makes my mornings easier. Great buy.
Nice Raspberry Pi heat sink set. Probably not needed as the RPi always seems to run cool for me. Maybe with 100% video decoding running. Other looks good.

I picked these up thinking they'd replace my old bookshelf speakers for movie nights, but the volume just doesn't fill my living room the way I expected. Dialogue is clear enough when I'm sitting right in front, but action scenes lose their punch from the couch. The subwoofer adds some nice low end for music, yet overall loudness feels limited. It's an okay setup for a desk or small room, but for a home theater vibe it left me wanting more.

This sleeve fits just about any laptop - I had no trouble getting my Gateway with a 14" screen to fit in it with room to spare. I think I could also slide a pad of paper in too. Neoprene should provide protection against light rain but I would not go so far as to say this is waterproof. The external padding provides some protection against bumps and scratches but will not help much against really rough treatment.

This thing is a lifesaver for product shots. The white balance is super easy to dial in, and the material diffuses my speedlight perfectly without any weird hotspots. Set up was a breeze, and it folds down small enough to stash behind my door. Totally worth it.

I ordered this thing hoping for a reliable light stand for my home studio setup, but the fourleg base is just a headache. I have hardwood floors and those extra legs make it wobble on anything that isn't perfectly flat, plus they constantly snag cables. The locking collars feel gritty when you tighten them, like they're full of sand. I tried using it with a small softbox and the whole rig leaned dangerously after ten minutes. No matter how much I fiddled with the leg angles, it never felt planted. I'm back to using my beatup old tripod stand because at least it stays put. Real letdown.
This item did not work when plugged into my car slot. Tried both my cars and did not work. Wouldn't suggest anyone to waste their time on this.

I am really impressed with the Skyfi3 XM Satellite Radio. It is lightweight, the right size to fit in you shirt pocket or pants pocket and has the right functionality to serve its purpose. The sound quality is good with enough volume to overcome most external sounds, in particularly for me, a lawn mower. Its mobility allows it to be used in your home, car and outside walking or jogging. You have to purchase the accessories for the home and outside functionality. I also purchased the home kit since the SKyFi3 comes with a car kit. I purchased the wireless router in order to transmit the signal from the south side of my house to a location inside my house. It is absolutely necessary to have clear view of the southern sky in order to receive the satellite signal. It has worked wonderfully after setting up the antennae outside in a covered plastic container. The antenna is supposed to be weatherproof but I am not taking any chances. It took me about 2 hours to read the directions for each device and get everything working. Even the car installation was very easy and worked once all the parts were connected. I have had one glitch with the device. I tried to turn it on one day after not using it for a week and it wouldn't come on. After much anxiety I read the manual and it explained how to reset the device. I like most it ability to set up recording schedules for your XM stations. These can then be sorted by various means to access the recordings. The device has many functions and for the $26.99 including S&H for the car kit it was well worth it. I highly recommend this device and its accessories.
so far so good, i bought it for my mother in laws ipad and it has held up
The folks at Belkin make quality products, and this case is great companion for the Kindle Fire HD 7". Really great of you are watching a movie or TV show. Recommended.
Works as advertised. Unfortunately I purchased an iPod Touch pretty soon after which obviously relegated the Zen to the junk pile. Poor little fella.
Perfect case for anyone wanting style and solid drop protection.
Picked this up for a small home networking job and it turned into a total headache. Flimsy build, impossible to terminate cleanly, and the performance was spotty at best. Absolute waste of money.
So far my husband likes it and haven't had any issues even with the heart monitor.
This thing holds my patio umbrella rock-solid even in gusty wind. Perfect for anyone with a small deck setup.
I have a TV on my back deck, we had a couple of storms and it really protects.

For the price, I wasn't expecting much but this little light totally surprised me. It's compact, lightweight, and the clamp grabs onto shoe laces or straps without budging during a run. I also love that it screws right onto my small tripod for hands-free use when I'm working in tight spaces. The beam is smooth and wide enough to see clearly without blinding you. Build is mostly plastic but feels decent for what it costs, and the rechargeable battery lasts longer than I thought it would. Simple toggle operation means no fumbling with modes. Honestly, you get a lot of practical lighting for very little money. Great little workhorse.
Nice quality cable and it works as expected. There is no noticeable drop in communication speed or quality between a printer and a computer.

I've been using this cable daily for about three months now and it still looks brand new. The braided jacket hasn't frayed at all, and the slim connectors haven't loosened up despite getting yanked a few times. Honestly didn't expect this kind of durability for the price.
Great for the price! Makes my internet way more stable and my online game experience way better.

I grabbed these on sale and honestly didn't expect much for under a hundred bucks, but they've been a total game changer for my open office hellscape. Right out of the box the build feels solid, not chintzy. Flipping the ANC switch instantly hushes the constant printer hum and chatty coworkers, it's not total silence but close enough that I can focus without cranking the volume. Battery lasts my whole workweek plus a few dog walks. Sound-wise, bass is punchy without muddying vocals, and podcasts are crystal clear. Comfort is solid for hours; no sweaty ear pressure. For this price, I'm genuinely shocked at how premium they feel and perform.

Does what it is supposed to for about 6 months then the 2nd output starts to loose sync (may be a heat issue). Will be watching a movie on the 2nd output and the TV will loose signal, then about 10 seconds latter the signal will return. This only happens on the 2nd output as others have had the same problem. Also sometimes the switching wouldn't switch on the first try, would change to a different input and back then it will work (did this from the time it was new). I just programmed my remote to automatically switch back and forth when I turned on my TV.
For the price, it's hard to beat. Keys feel decent, and it works straight out of the box without any hassle.

It is pleasantly smaller than expected. The hooks fit the headphone strap but they don't dwarf my headphones like I thought they would. The thing itself it more flexible than I expected. It's not alarming but I just expected cast aluminium or plastic but this is soft rubber.

For the money, this receiver punches way above its weight. The audio is crisp and full, making movie nights feel like a real theater experience. Setup isn't plug-and-play, you'll spend some time with the manual and remote, but once dialed in, it's rock solid. I mainly use it for streaming music and Blu-rays, and it handles both effortlessly. If you want clean, powerful sound without dropping a fortune, this AVR-154 is a steal. Just budget a little patience for the initial configuration.
The tip feels so fragile I'm scared it'll snap any day. Fine for basic phone scrolling, but skip this if you need precision. I'd only suggest it for kids or very light, occasional use.

Worked fine with my older DVD player but totally blanked on a newer portable projector, likely a sync timing mismatch. The main leads feel flimsy and unshielded, which probably doesn't help. If you're using it with basic, low-res gear, it'll get the job done.
Looks to be of a good quality. I've tested it in and my lab and everything seemed to work properly.

This battery allows me to shoot all day without worrying about how much juice I have left. This kind of peace of mind is priceless. A professional battery for professional photographers that gets the job done.
Order arrived a day early in a tidy little box. Popped them on, and the seal is just perfect, my IEMs finally feel secure and comfy again.

Bag is much smaller than picture will have you think. But description to the product. Is spot on. So keep that in mind. I was able to fit into my bag: One camera/one lens/ (5)memory cards/ (3) batteries./ (3) filters and your out of room. Numbers are for safe carry nothing bulging or crammed into bag.

Snagged this Sony player for a steal, honestly, I was skeptical at that price point. Hooked it up to my 46" LED and the picture over HDMI is crisp as heck. Discs load quick, no hiccups. Solid build quality for the money. Totally worth it if you just need reliable playback without breaking the bank.
Very easy to install this on our new Fire HD7. Clear and works well to protect the screen.
Surprisingly solid for the price. Holds up way better than I expected, zero interference or dropouts.
For the price, this sensor kit is a solid deal. Setup was easy and the door sensors are reliable, though the motion detectors occasionally lose connection. Still worth it.
Works like a charm behind my wall-mounted TV. The low-profile right-angle connector made cable management so much cleaner without any signal loss.

This unit is a great buy. It is easy to set up and built well. I mounted an epson hd projector tp the wall with it. The epson jas three mount hiles which requires one of the arms to be rotated away only using 3 arms like a triangle bit even so it is pretty sturdy. I would recommend using some locktite on the screws or hiding the cables begind a vertical conduit or cable hider though. Bumping the wires can cause it to shift a bit. That said, it is a simple fix. I am very happy with it overall.

I read a review here that said the item worked for their Infinity Basslink. I purchased this for my Infinity Basslink and it did not work. Thought it may be a defective unit so I returned it for another. Still doesn't work. The Basslink recognizes there is a remote and cuts the gain, but doesn't recognize any signals from the remote itself. The Basslink uses 4 wires, this item has 6.
Had this set for about six months now, still holds a solid charge after dozens of shoots.

Pros: The cable worked and it is very cheap. Cons: There is a very noticeable fuzziness compared with HDMI. When I plugged in the VGA the image and set the appropriate resolution the image looked horrible. I then tried HDMI and fonts and images are crisp. This VGA is basically unusable. I am using this with my PC and a Samsung display at 1920x1080.

Grabbed this for a budget AM3+ build I'm keeping alive for my garage workshop PC. Honestly wasn't expecting much for the price, but these sticks booted right up at the rated 1600MHz without me having to poke around in the BIOS. Timings were detected correctly on the first try, which is more than I can say for some pricier kits I've used. I mostly run Spotify, a couple diagnostic programs, and a browser with way too many tabs open, zero hangs or blue screens after two weeks of daily use. Heat spreaders are low-profile enough to clear my chunky aftermarket cooler, which saved me a headache. Solid little workhorses if you need dependable DDR3 without any fuss.

Got this for my daily commute, and honestly, it's a steal for what it costs. The wheels roll smooth even on uneven sidewalks, and converting it to a backpack takes barely any effort once you do it a couple times. Material feels sturdy, zippers don't snag, and there's plenty of room for my lunch, laptop, and a change of clothes without bulging awkwardly. I went with the all-black version, looks way more professional than I expected for the price point. For under fifty bucks, I wasn't expecting much, but it's held up great through rain and crowded trains. Definitely punches above its weight.

I've never worn wireless earbuds before and I was a little concerned about how much they might way and just the overall comfort of them and then they arrived. These things are light and to be honest they are quite comfortable as well. I read several reviews stating that they would fall out of the persons ear so I was a little worried about that as well. I did not have that problem at all. They paired up to my phone easily and quickly and then I popped them in my ears and went to go walk down the street to get the mail. Fit fine and did not jar loose on the short walk. I then took them for a short 4.5mile run the next morning on a combination of sidewalks and dirt paths and the only time I had to readjust the earbud was when the wire that connects the two sides got pulled on by the collar of the shirt I was wearing. Other than that they stayed put and sounded great. I have not run out of battery on them and I have used them on and off for the past couple of days around the house and at the gym. The only thing I would change would actually just be adding an option for additional colors to choose from, but the bright yellow definitely makes them hard to misplace. They sound great. They stay put They are light I like them and I will be using them for all of my future runs and gym workouts. I received this product at a discount in exchange for an honest and unbiased review.
This is a GREAT monitor, that does exactly what a monitor should do - allows me to SEE any and all websites as crystal clear as though I was watching HD TV.

I've had this tracker for about three months and it's held up well through sweaty workouts and daily bumps. Battery still lasts a full week. I do wish the step counter was a bit sharper on slower walks, but overall it's a solid, sturdy little device.
I never bought this must be some add on somewhere or to make someone a profit don't even sign of Mr.

What more can I say? If you know what cable you are looking for, and you are looking for this kind of cable, you will receive this kind of cable. These are just wires with their respective connectors. Verdict: I RECOMMEND THIS PRODUCT.
Short and simple this item is overpriced and a total disappointment. Going straight back for a refund.

This Fintie iPad Pro 12.9" case is great. The case fits my iPad Pro perfectly, the cutouts are all precisely placed and the wake/sleep feature works perfectly. I usually don't like to use snap-in cases because ones I've had in the past don't hold my iPad securely in the case... corners are always snapping out randomly which makes me feel like the iPad itself could just fall out... but this one seems very secure and well-made, and I haven't had any problems with the corners popping out yet. And the design is beautiful, just as vivid as the photo in the ad. Especially for the price, this is a great deal. I deducted one star because the case seems a bit heavier than I expected, but overall, I highly recommend it.
My phone's USB-C cable needed a firm push at first, but after a couple uses it loosened up. Charge speed and port layout match what I expected for the price.

Got these for my nephew who's just getting into mixing his own tracks, and he's over the moon. The clarity in the mids really surprised me, vocals and acoustic instruments come through with a warmth you don't expect at this price. I'd recommend them to anyone dipping a toe into home recording or just wanting to hear layers they've been missing in their favorite albums. They're forgiving on the ears during long sessions, which my nephew definitely puts them through. If you're on the fence and want something that punches above its class for critical listening, grab a pair.
Switching to this was a game changer for my wrist pain. If you spend all day at a desk, just get it. Seriously comfy once you adjust.
My dad received this as a gift. He loves photography and this made him think he was getting a new lens. Excellent work maKing a replica! Good deal, good seal, nice cosmetics and texture.

I purchase this sleeve when I purchased my Acer 15 inch Chromebook and it does fit the laptop just fine but because of the large size it should have handles as it is too large and too heavy to one hand it like you would a tablet in a sleeve.

this is a good buy for anyone who is looking for a replacement mic. mine broke on my laptop and this was the best product for what I paid for it. was kind of skeptical at first the price was too low, but decided to buy it anyway since amazon has never let me down. I'm very happy with my mic, Thanks seller.

I've been running a pair of these Energy Takes as my rear surrounds for just over two years now, and they're holding up like champs. Mounted them on adjustable wall brackets in a living room that gets a lot of afternoon sun and occasional humidity, and the finish hasn't faded or peeled at all. Sound-wise, they still deliver that crisp, room-filling clarity I noticed on day one, no distortion, even during intense movie marathons where my receiver's been cranked for hours. I was a little worried the small cabinets might loosen up over time, but everything's still tight and rattle-free. Honestly, they've outlasted a couple of other components in my setup. Solid little workhorses.
Not comfy at all (on my head at least). Was waaay to tight so I would not recommend these headphones to anyone ever.
I expected good single-core speed, but the multitasking smoothness blew me away. Handles my workloads easily, runs cool with a decent cooler, and overclocking was painless. Perfect upgrade.

Instruction suck. But I went online and afyer figuring it out I love it. The only bad part is you have to change setting in the camera (Canon) and if your target moves and you just want to use your shutter you have to change it back in menu or tou're in timer modr

I've had the G3 for about eight months now, mostly hiking and a couple trips to the coast with sand and salt spray. The build has held up better than I expected, no creaks, dials still turn with decent resistance, and the flip-out screen hasn't gone floppy. I did drop it once onto packed dirt from waist height; the body got a small scuff but everything works fine, lens mount stayed tight. The rubber grip is starting to show a little shine from use, but it's not peeling. Sensor stayed dust-free despite frequent lens changes outdoors. Only reason I'm not giving five stars is the rear dial feels a bit mushy compared to day one, but functionally it's still solid. For a lightweight travel setup, the durability is genuinely reassuring.
It was everything I expected it to be. Really happy with this purchase. It was clear and has focus.
Fits perfect. No issues, no complaints. Good quality. Doesn't dissapoint.

I've used this camera ever since attending a One Direction concert. It made the concert so memorable because of the hi def recording feature. The pictures all turned out wonderful. It's an easy camera to use and I'm very happy with my purchase

I grabbed this for my covered patio, and it's been perfect for casual hangs. Sound is clear and gets louder than expected without distorting. The auto-off when Bluetooth drops is a lifesaver since I constantly wander away mid-project. Battery lasts me several weekends easily. I'd recommend it to anyone wanting affordable, worry-free background music in a workshop or outdoor nook.
Arrived two days early in a sturdy box with zero damage. Took it out and the build quality instantly impressed me. Feels premium right out of the gate. Super happy with this purchase so far.

This is one of the best Mac laptops on the market, period. (The best PC laptop on the market, in my opinion is the Sony VAIO Z series with the high res screen option). I recently traded the 15" for this 13" and although I greatly miss the 15" screen and the wonderful speakers on the 15" (the 13" speakers are good but not as rich and bassy as the 15") but overall I love this smaller machine.
