Let me get what I don't like out of the way: --wish the screen resolution were much higher (compare to the Sony VAIO Z series which has such outrageous screen resolution) --wish it were a bit lighter that 4.5 lbs (although this machine is exceptionally sturdy, so willing to deal with a little bit heavier than similar size machines) And that's it for the wish list. Now what I absolutely love: --the ergonomics -- way better than anything else out there, PC or Mac. --backlit keyboard, comfortable typing area (feels luxurious to rest your hands on the surface of the textured aluminum area) --fast OS (Snow Leopard), fast machine, great power management --first machine i've owned that doesn't get overly hot, in fact even barely warm What I do highly recommend though is to get -- if you can afford it -- is a 256Gb SSD (Corsair). This gives the machine extra speed, better battery power, and true longevity. No need to worry about damaging the disk while traveling, defragmenting (oh, so glad that era is over). This is an expensive option ($650 or so) but really well worth it by a long shot. The SSD equipped machine will last you a long time.
This clamp works great until you strap on a bigger flash. It struggles a bit with the 580ex II but works great with the 430ex series.

Battery life on this unit is solid, I get nearly a full workday out of a charge. The clip feels a bit flimsy though, and I'm worried it might snap if I'm not careful. Still, it gets the job done well enough.

Arrived fast in a slim, well-padded box. Feels sturdy and lightweight right out of the package. Haven't tested the radio outdoors yet, but the CD playback is smooth and clear. Perfect little nightstand companion so far.

Works perfectly. My only qualm is that it has more ports than I need, but it was the best value of the options I reviewed so the extra ports don't bother me. Who knows, maybe I'll need them in the future - and not I have them just in case.

Well, I've had this tripod for 4 months now. I hoped (like many others) that it was a solid aluminum beast, but the truth is, it isn't. It's mostly hard black plastic. The plastic is OK, but it broke on me on the plastic where the legs connect to the central member. I purchased a Dolica Proline at the same time as this one. So far it has been a better, more durable tripod (overall). This one out-performs the Dolica if it's being used for shooting videos though. The Sony name is good, but this product is relatively brittle. I would recommend it only for someone who will use it for light-duty purposes. I give it 3.25 stars (despite the fact that it broke on me.
Came super fast in sturdy packaging, looked even sleeker in person, and fired right up, couldn't be happier with this thing.

I use this keyboard to manage servers for the company I work for. Its very compact and the capability to get both mouse and keyboard over a single USB is amazing! 5 stars to the seller and manufacturer for this great protective case.

Great Product. Works well with little fuss and is cheap enough to buy several for your home. Reliable, quiet, and compact. Please note this is not a battery backup UPS but simply a line conditioner. It provides the same protection as a UPS just does not provide battery power in the event of a total loss of power.

I'll purchase again given the good deal and all the positive reviews but I had these for 2 weeks. The white ones mesh cap developed a hole in it and I just tried the pen side on the black one and as soon as I pulled the cap the threads holding the pen in stripped and the whole thing fell apart. Cheap price cheap product. It looks like for the most part their made well but quality is hit or miss

The box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, one corner completely crushed. Opened it up and the unit itself had a faint scuff already, like maybe a return got resold as new. No padding inside besides a flimsy piece of cardboard, so everything rattled around during shipping. Plugged it in and it just feels cheap, lightweight plastic that creaks when you handle it. First power-on gave me a blinking error light before it finally recognized the drive. Not a great start honestly.
I may buy some more for replacements when the ear pieces wear out from normal use. Our kids are pretty tough on these.

The first delivery arrived with spider web cracks running through it. The screen protector is glass, and while it is adequately packaged for normal shipping, I suspect that the corner of a box on the delivery truck caught it. The return process was very quick and easy. Once I received the replacement, installation was simpler than many plastic protectors I've worked with on other devices. I like the feel of the glass versus the usual plastic protectors. This is the third MoKo product that I've purchased; all three products appear to be of high quality materials and design.

I would rate this a 5 star tower if it hadn't arrived with a front grill with a rusted section. It's right in the bottom right-hand corner so I'm sure when the fan lights up it will be painfully obvious. It's only on the "grill" part so if the vendor could replace this piece for me I would gladly revisit my rating. Photo attached. It's worse in person because the flash from the camera washes it out a bit. Update: Vendor replaced front panel at no cost to me and without hesitation. Amending review to 5 stars. I've had the product for over 1 year and it's great. I even bought a second one but with the USB 3.0 front input. Both are fantastic. I highly recommend and will buy more myself.

My artist daughter ALWAYS uses this glove when she works on her Wacom Cintiq tablet. She says it makes working with the pen so much better, and she will not go back to drawing without it. She has two of these gloves, just in case she misplaces one of them. Highly recommended product by my daughter.

I bought this for my 6-year-old after she kept waking me up at random hours, and honestly it's been a game changer. The pink silicone exterior is much softer and sturdier than I expected, she's dropped it twice already and there isn't a scratch. Setup took maybe three minutes; the top buttons feel intuitive even for small hands. The glowing pink display is bright enough to see at night but not so intense it keeps her awake. She actually looks forward to setting her own alarm now, which I didn't think would happen this fast. For the price, I figured it would feel cheap, but it's surprisingly well-made. Very happy with this little clock.

On a multiple item order, item was received a week prior than all other items order shipped under the same property. It is little oversensitive on the mouse movement. I'm sure there is a sensitivity adjustment but haven't spent the time to look for it yet.
The grip felt nice, but the cutouts didn't line up with my buttons.
Perfect for photographers on the go, this reader pulls footage from my trail cam to my tablet instantly. Highly recommend it to any outdoor enthusiast.

I'm not sure where the review went that I already left for this product a few days ago. He's a brief re-post. Nice mount, easy install. Needs adjustment for tightening part that mounts directly to monitor.
it works on my asus computer. my laptop only have one HDMI output and this works totally fine as a USB monitor device

Have been using this mouse for around 4 years now. Has served me wonderfully through thousands of games of League and countless hours on minecraft. This mouse senses any damn surface you put it on; carpet, pant leg, bare desk, whatever. It even worked semi decently on a glass top table. Still works great. Gets a 5-star because it fits a $10 pricetag WONDERFULLY.
Fits our tiny RV kitchen like a glove and the digital display is so easy to read even in low light. Way brighter than the original microwave it replaced.
The cap snaps on securely and stays put in my bag. No more worrying about dust or losing it on a shoot. Simple, solid design.

I've been using this little transmitter for a few months now, mostly for road trips and my daily commute. For the price, I honestly wasn't expecting much, but the sound comes through surprisingly clear with barely any hiss or crackle, way better than the cheap knockoff I had before. Setup takes like two seconds, and it pairs instantly every time. My only real gripe is the power cable. It's kind of flimsy where it plugs in, and if your car's outlet is tucked into a tight spot, you have to be gentle or it'll lose connection. Not a dealbreaker, just something to watch. Solid value overall.
Fits my camera like a glove, flash and all. Solid build and it just feels great in hand every time.
Arrived fast and well-packed. First use, and it spread smooth, temps dropped instantly. Super impressed.
For the price, this cable is a steal. It charges my headphones quickly and the braided design feels way more durable than the stock cord.

For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but this little drive is a steal. It feels solid and clicks right into my laptop without any fuss, instantly recognized on Windows 11. Transfer speeds are snappy enough for my daily backups and file shuffling. It's so compact I just leave it plugged in permanently; you barely notice it's there. Honestly, I've paid way more for flimsier storage. This thing delivers real bang for your buck.

Like others have said, the buttons and volume control are not easy to use, it does take a little work. Overall though I think this is a really good product. The player fits securely, and while running I've never had a problem with it sliding down my arm. I don't ever really look at the screen while I'm running, and I can't imagine why anybody would want to. I did however make sure I had a screen cover on my player to keep the plastic one on the armband from scratching. Another really nice thing about this is that it is washable, several of these that we looked weren't.

I've owned quite a few similar sets in black, and it never occurred to me that there could be good versions and bad versions of this sort of simple product. Well, I was wrong. There is at least one bad version and it's this one. Cons: - Will not straighten out, not at all, it strongly insists on remaining curly, which makes it far less inconspicuous as it refuses to lie and kinks up - Pinches your fingers badly, really, really badly. Seems impossible, I know, but seriously, it hurts. The plastic they used is just wrong for this application, it's so stiff it I'm typing this on fingers that are raw from having to spread it open to put the cables inside. - It is NOT white, it's an off white, so it doesn't blend in as well with real white as it should. Pros: - Umm, there seem very few alternative products... so I guess mere existence is a pro.
The cable is nicely built with good connectors. It is flexible and can fit in tight places. The connectors have retaining clips so the cable will not pop off.
I like having black and white as well as different sizes to choice from.
Nice reasonablely priced set of speakers. Easy to hook up and do not take a lot of room.
I bought this to upgrade my slow running work laptop. The speed of this ssd is awesome. My laptop now runs perfectly even with only 4Gb of ram running Windows 7.
Honestly didn't expect these to outperform my pricier Sony discs, but every burn completed without a single hiccup across multiple drives. Zero coasters so far.

I was really excited when the package showed up because it arrived two days earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. But the box itself was pretty beat up and had clearly been tossed around, no extra padding inside, just the case loose in a thin mailer. Right out of the package, the plastic smelled super strong, like a chemical factory, and the edges felt rougher than I thought they'd be from the photos. The color was also a bit duller in person. I figured maybe it just needed to air out, but the first impression wasn't great. For something that's supposed to protect an expensive device, the whole unboxing experience made me question how much care went into making it. Not off to a good start.

This is my second Rockville purchase and both seem to be decent quality. However this unit seemed to be beat up slightly and came with no instructions. Possibly used. The unit is lighter than I expected but definitely added extra power! I've attempted to contact customer service several times with no success and is discouraging.

My Walkman arrived a day earlier than expected, which was a nice start, but the packaging was pretty bare-bones, just a thin cardboard sleeve inside a larger box with almost no padding. No damage to the unit itself, but it didn't feel very secure. First impressions were decent: the build is light and the screen looks crisp enough, though the plastic back has a slightly cheap feel for the price. Setup went smoothly and the buttons are responsive. I haven't had it long enough to judge battery life or long-term reliability, but unboxing it didn't exactly scream premium. It works fine so far, just wish the presentation and overall feel matched the cost a bit more. Solid, but not special.

For the money, I expected way more. Setup felt clunky and the app kept crashing, which wasted a good chunk of my evening. I mainly bought this to simplify my living room setup, but it still can't handle my soundbar's subwoofer level adjustment, so I'm constantly reaching for that original remote anyway. The button layout looks clean, but the volume rocker is mushy and imprecise. For a device at this price point, basic functions should just work out of the box without weird workarounds. It technically controls some things, but calling it a universal solution feels generous. I'd return it if I hadn't thrown the packaging out. You're basically paying a premium for half-baked convenience.

This is my second purchase of the Infinity Kappa 693.91 3-way, 6 x 9 co-as speakers. They are great sounding in both cars (Corvette/Van) and can handle a lot of volume. I would highly recommend them and would purchase them a 3rd time if needed.

Of all of the 128GB SSD drives available, this one has proven to last the longest in read/write testing - over 2 PETABYTES before failing. I use this on my home server (Gigabyte Z170X Gaming 7, Windows Server 2012R2, SQL Server 2012, Visual Studio 2014, etc. etc. ) Very fast loading. I have a total of 12TB of storage on the server; several of the drives are SSD and this beats them, hands down. When the Samsung SM961 m. 2 comes out, I'll move this drive to my laptop. Here's the proof on the durability and longevity: [.

Worked fine for about ten minutes, enough to trace three cables in my basement. Then the sender unit's power LED just froze solid green and nothing would revive it. Fresh batteries, reseating connectors, switching ports, zero luck. For something that costs this much, I expected more than a disposable tester. The receiver feels flimsy too, like a cheap toy. Honestly, a basic tone-and-probe kit at half the price would have been smarter money. I can't recommend this when it dies before finishing one small job. Total waste.

I picked up this system to keep an eye on things around the house, and overall it's a mixed bag. The daytime picture is crisp enough, and I was pleasantly surprised by the night vision clarity, definitely a strong point. Setup wasn't terrible, but getting the cameras positioned just right took some trial and error. My main gripe is the app. It works fine on my home network, but stepping outside that is a different story. I've never once managed to pull up a live feed while I'm away, which kinda defeats the purpose for me. Notifications are hit or miss too. It's decent for local monitoring, but if remote access matters to you, just be aware it might not deliver. Three stars feels fair.

really nice case. We purchased a chromebook for my 5 1/2 and 7 year old daughters and they love the color. It holds the tablet and cord well and even has Velcro to connect the handles. Very happy with the purchase; reasonably priced too.
This is so much nicer than the picture shows. It's just like one that is more expensive. I'm very pleased with this purchase.
This little book light is great for the price, the light is broad and spreads across both pages and the clip is secure enough not to fall off.
perfect fit. replaced the front door speakers in 2003 chevy and was back in business in 10 minutes. range and volume were just as expected.
Fits earbuds and tiny USBs fine, but anyone with travel documents should skip it.

I've been in love with the Carl Zeiss lenses for years now, but my older Sony digital had such a slow shutter I was missing pics of my son. This camera takes really fast pictures!!! I also love the setting that allows me to force the camera to focus on a childs face before anything else in the frame. The blue color is stylish, and its small enough to fit in a front pocket. Looks great in a Kroo Camera Case Premium Quality Carrying Case for Sony Cyber-shot + Bonus Bundled with Universal LCD Screen Protector Kit + Cloth in light blue.

I have an ASUS transporter tablet and my original cord when bad. I needed a new one and I order it through Amazon. I am very pleased with the cord, and if I ever need a another one I will definitely come to Amazon.

Picked this up to replace a dead drive in my home office rig. For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but it installed in minutes and burns discs without a hiccup. Reads everything I've thrown at it too. Solid little workhorse that gets the job done without draining my wallet. Would absolutely buy again.
Got more than I expected for the money, sharp and quick.
The night mode on this camera is seriously impressive, captured stars I didn't even know were there.

I've now had this TV for a year. It has been nothing short of a nightmare. I have a sony ps3 and an iPhone. Streaming to it from the phone is hit or miss. My internet is well over spec for this to function but it barely ever does. Most days we decide to watch something on the TV it just doesn't want to work. The playstation is wired to it and gets unrecognized by the TV so often and decides not to work anymore. Yes my ps3 works just fine on real tvs. It's updated as much as it can be. There is just no reason to make such a dumb product as this. Why can't it just be a TV and play whatever I play on it? Sony has killed my enthusiasm for all things entertainment. When it works, the image and sound are fantastic. If you don't mind being let down 6 out of ten times you try using it, that might work for you. I'm writing this now because I thought at first it was my inexperience with this kind of product that caused issue. But the internet and streaming technology can't be blamed for the issues with sony's own gaming console. zero stars.

I grabbed these mainly for work calls and podcast listening during my commute, and honestly for under ten bucks I'm pretty impressed. They're lightweight and sit comfortably in my ears for a couple hours without getting annoying. Sound is clear enough for voice and casual music, nothing mind-blowing but definitely not hollow or harsh like other budget pairs I've tried. The passive noise blocking is a nice surprise; it dulls the bus engine rumble enough that I don't have to crank the volume. My only small gripe is the inline control module feels a bit clunky when I'm walking, but if I clip it to my collar it stays put. For the price, these are a solid everyday beater pair.
Fits my iPod 5 perfectly without feeling bulky. The matte finish gives a nice grip. I'd suggest this to anyone who wants simple, reliable protection.

Though the case is great, fairly thin, versatile, sturdy and protective. The downside, smell is overpowering, I literally had to let it air out for a day. Upon minutes of opening it I could feel the cancer forming in my body from teh horrible chemicals used to make this thing. If you have a day to let it air out it will be worth the money spent.
For the price, this thing is a steal. Runs everything smooth, storage expansion is a breeze, and it just keeps improving. Honestly feels like a premium experience without the premium cost.

I grabbed these on a whim during a sale, and for the cash I dropped, I'm genuinely surprised. The build is solid, nothing feels flimsy, and the three tip sizes helped me get a snug fit without any pressure after a couple hours. Sound-wise, the low end thumps hard, perfect for workout playlists or EDM, but the highs can feel a bit recessed, so acoustic tracks lose some sparkle. Mids are decent, vocals come through clean enough. Battery life holds up fine for my daily commute. If you want a bass-forward daily driver that doesn't demolish your wallet, these punch well above their price point. Not flawless, but definitely a solid value.

I grabbed this for camping trips and occasional tailgates, and it gets the job done without fuss. Picture is surprisingly clear for its size, and tuning in stations is straightforward. The included battery pack is its weak spot, mine barely held a charge after a month, so I'd suggest picking up a decent set of rechargeables and the dock right away. For anyone who needs a compact screen for a cabin, RV, or power-outage backup, it's a solid little workhorse once you swap out the power source.
It has been 4 months and it still works like a champ.

I've had this flash drive for about four months now, and it's been through some rough treatment. It's survived a couple drops onto concrete and one accidental trip through the washing machine, still works perfectly. I use it daily to shuttle large video files between workstations, often 50GB or more, and it hasn't hiccuped once. The metal casing has held up nicely too, just a few scuffs. Honestly surprised it's this tough for the price.
Great product for great price. You can also use this on your Raspberry PI.
I grabbed this for my video editing scratch disk and the sustained write speeds never throttle, even with huge 4K files.

I grabbed this for group backpacking trips and it's been a game changer. Pairs instantly, range is solid, and I don't have to sprint back for timer shots anymore. If you hike with buddies or shoot solo in weird spots, this little clicker saves so much hassle. Totally recommend for outdoorsy folks.

These were highly recommended and I think they sound quite good, at least for my purposes. I've used them 3 times but unfortunately, the earhooks have fallen apart every time. This is really a problem, since I got these to use while jogging. It doesn't help that the earhooks are still a bit too big for my ears. Ok, so my ears are small - but not freakishly so! So my search for suitable earbuds continues. They work for some people, but not me.
Had this in my kid's PC for a few months now and it's still rock solid, no hiccups at all.

This has been very useful to me. I tossed out my netgear wireless router, and replaced it with a raspberry pi. This unit lets me have a single plug for the raspberry pi, since it can power it, and other peripherals such as a wireless access point.
Had this laptop for about eight months now and it's held up great. No sluggishness, no keyboard issues, and the hinge still feels solid. Perfect for daily classwork and streaming.

Charger works well. The headset drops right on or sits on top if the case is closed nd charges reliably. I liked that I could just throw the case (with headset inside it) and off I go. Unfortuntely very unhappy with the headset but loved the case.

These are cute and my kids were excited about the color choices but we purchased two pairs and both hurt the kids' ears. They refuse to wear them anymore and we had to replace them with a different brand.

Bought this six months ago hoping to extend WiFi to the garage. At first it worked okay, but around month four the plastic housing started feeling sticky and warped near the vents. Then it began dropping connections randomly, like every couple of hours, and I'd have to unplug it to reset. Last week it just stopped powering on entirely, no lights, nothing. I tried different outlets and cables, but it's dead. The prongs also got wobbly over time, so it barely stayed in the socket. For something that sits indoors with no abuse, it shouldn't degrade this fast. Disappointed, because setup was simple enough. Now I'm shopping for a more durable replacement.

I couldn't believe how cheap this is for the protection it has. I don't take my tablet out of my house often but i definitely enjoy it's comfort on the couch and in bed. I bought these to hang on my iPhone so I can listen to music and books without all the tangled cords. The first one I bought worked so well I bought 4 more for friends. How much do I love Samsung SSDs? Too much, probably. Great price for when you want that insane improvement in all things speed. Use this as a boot drive and put all your data on a 7200RPM HDD.

I have been using the Centon Electronics Flash Memory Card (S1-SDXU1-64G) for the past few weeks in my Samsung digital camera and also my Sony digital videocamera. This high-performance SD card, with an impressive 64GB of storage, doubled the capacity of my previous SD cards. While not an issue with my digital camera, the card has already eliminated the nuisance of running out of storage on my video camera (with 16GB of internal storage) while traveling/sightseeing. The Centon SD card eliminates the need to connect either camera directly to my computer in order to download the photos/videos. My iMac has an SD slot but I use a USB SD card reader for my MacBook Pro while traveling. And it is fast, and that means I can take more photos faster... and I also don't have to wait for my video camera in-between shots. In addition to cameras, this Centon SD card is also compatible with some models of Android smartphones. Two thumbs up for the Centon SD card!

I've been holding off on a mesh upgrade for way too long, mostly because I didn't want to spend a weekend troubleshooting. This thing flipped that whole experience on its head. The dedicated backhaul channel is a game-changer for me, my old extender setup constantly choked when more than a couple devices were streaming, but now I can have a 4K movie running, someone on a video call, and my security cameras uploading without a hiccup. The node placement guide in the app actually made sense, and I had the whole house blanketed in strong signal within twenty minutes. Really impressed by how it handles device handoff when I walk from the garage to the back patio.
For the price, this little reader is a steal. Works instantly with my old laptop and handles every card I throw at it.

I've used this battery on a D50, D80 and D200. I bought 2 EN-EL3e's because most of my Nikon bodies provide very conservative battery power readings. If you are a Nikon user, you'll probably realize that Nikon has geared their Nikon digital camera bodies with memory card space and battery power meter with a "glass is half empty" approach. I was doing a shoot with my D80 and I thought I had a defective EN-EL3e because after 200 shots, the battery meter stated I had 44% battery power left. I ended up shooting well over 1000 shots and the meter was flickering from 30% to 60%. The same behaviour happened with my D50 and D200. Bottom line, Nikon's digital camera bodies have very inaccurate battery level meters. The EN-EL3e has a TON of juice on 1 charge and I am very pleased with it.
Does the trick. Nothing fancy. For the price you can't beat it.

UPDATE 8/8/2012: I haven't had the case/cover for 3 months, yet...and it's already showing signs of falling apart. I use the case/cover at home and for one class I'm taking, and after a short 3 months it's surface material is cracking and starting to peel off where the cover folds (multiple locations). I do like the case, but it's going to look very ratty in a few more months. At this rate I'll need a case every 3-4 months and that quickly ads up over a year's time frame (which would be an acceptable amount of time for a single case/cover to survive). Old Review: Great case for the iPad. This thing goes in and out of my back pack, loose leaf binder, and around campus. It's a simple design-in black-and most folks don't know it's an iPad until I open the cover. I just need to learn and not show off, so this fits the need perfectly. I would have given 5 stars, but I find the cover sometimes moves enough that the unlock screen will come on. In a side-by-side comparison, the Apple screen cover has about twice the hold of this one, but this one protects the whole device and helps my hands grip what would otherwise be the difficult to handle smooth aluminum back.
This board handles my FX-6300 overclock like a champ, VRMs stay surprisingly cool.
Arrived crazy fast, packed like a tank, and lit up my whole setup instantly.

I've been using this little camera mostly for hiking clips, and the compact design really shines when you're scrambling up trails one-handed. The 4K footage looks crisp and smooth, way better than I expected at this size. Stabilization does a solid job, too. My main gripe is the internal mic placement, it picks up every tiny lens movement and button press, which gets annoying in quiet nature shots. A firmware update to reduce that mechanical noise would make a huge difference. For the price, it's a handy travel companion that nails video quality, just plan on adding an external mic if you care about clean audio. Still happy overall.

this particular one i had to send back because it was the wrong one. i needed it for Nikon. but this works perfect. make sure it says 1/8000 sync. because there is a 1/250 that does not work past 1/250

Accidentally broke a previous set that I loved so I had to buy another. This refurbished set works just as well. My only issue is that the retraction button doesn't work as well as my previous set. It's a little annoying that I have to press it so hard and now it's really slow. Other than that a great product.

I've been using this case for a few weeks now and overall I'm really happy with it. The material feels soft but sturdy, and the light tan shade gives my camera a nice vintage look without being too flashy. It hugs the body perfectly, so I don't worry about bumps or scratches when it's in my bag. The only reason I'm not giving five stars is the top flap, it doesn't stay attached when the camera is hanging, so I end up stashing it separately while I'm out shooting. A little tether or magnet would've made a huge difference. Still, for the price, it does exactly what I need and keeps my gear safe day to day.

For today's standards, this is a nice, functional VCR that I would recommend to anybody who needs one. But if you are looking for a comparable replacement for your 80s or 90s VCR, you will most likely be disappointed. This LED display, for one thing, is lacking. While VCRs used to show the tape counter while a tape is being played, rewinded, or fastforwarded, this VCR only displays the words "PLAY" or "STOP" or "REW", etc. There is a very noticible delay when rewinding or fastforwarding, which can get very annoying at times. The VCR also does not have any useful gimmicks, like Sony's Commercial Skip, that seperate it from any other brand or model. My advice is this: If you 1) already have a seperate DVD player for the same TV or 2) you do not plan to watch DVDs in the near future, then I would definitely buy this VCR model. If not, then you should definitely look into buying a VCR/DVD combo, which in general do not have the problems mentioned above. You can find an excellent one for just $100-150.

I wanted a wireless setup without spending a ton, and for the price this keyboard gets the job done most days. The overall build feels decent enough, and connectivity has been solid. Where it falls short is the awkward key layout around where the numpad should be, even after months of daily use, I still hit Insert when I mean Delete, which gets old fast. The separate numpad is handy in theory, but it chews through batteries way quicker than the main board, so I rarely bother replacing them anymore. If you're on a budget and don't mind those quirks, it's a fair value. Just don't expect a seamless experience out of the box.
Box arrived dented and the laptop inside wasn't even wrapped properly. Powered it on and was immediately hit with sluggish, annoying junk.

I've long shied away from bluetooth earbuds because it's annoying when the battery dies, but my corded earbuds were a hassle on runs and such. They didn't stay in my ears well and I would catch the cord and rip them out of my ears. The Phaiser earbuds have changed my tune (pun intended) on BT earbuds. The sound quality is great, and they are really solid in my ears. I've never had them come out or even start to come out. Battery life is pretty solid. My only complaint is a very minor one, and that is that most of the time when I turn them on I get a message saying how much battery life is left, but sometimes the messages doesn't happen. One other thing I would change is that the case that comes with it is big enough for the earbuds and pretty much every accessory and spare part it came with. I would have preferred a more compact case for just the earbuds, as I don't need to carry around the parts that are too small or too large for my ears. Oh, and the magnets on the earbuds are a nice bonus.
Does the job, just expected fuller bass for the price.

I have owned one of these for some time and recently got a new one for a second computer. It's a great quality keyboard that lacks a lot of the new fangled style and shape, (thank God) of other keyboards that cost so much more and are at times far from the coolest thing around. I'm speaking of the so called wrap around keyboards that supposedly are more realistically or comfortably designed for our hands. Sometimes even certain keys are bigger or curled around or something or other. My experience with them are that most have been far from the "better mousetrap" to be sure. I have wavered in the past from the basic, classic idea that this keyboard is, but not anymore. Not only has Amazon gotten it dead on with this baby, but they have also managed to improve it a little without messing up the works. The letters on my old keyboard are not fading at all, (something that always happens on cheap keyboards with the letters used most often), but they have also separated the keys nicely so that its easy to clean and not hit two keys at once when you have fat fingers like me. Also, this keyboard has a slight built in elevation in the back that is a better quality than the little tabs that other keyboards have that are are often flimsier. And lastly this keyboard stays put very well with good solid feet to prevent you chasing it around your desk while working. The only thing about this keyboard that I personally had a problem with is its USB plug. My computer is so old it still has the rounded plug in the rear for a keyboard. Fortunately adapters for such old time 'puters are easy to be found and I had one already. In conclusion, if you believe in sometimes, (make that often) older or classic being better, I highly recommend this Amazon basic. Its very reasonably priced and top quality for the dollar...
Thought it'd be louder for the price, pretty weak.

So we bought face plates with removable connectors so we can replace them with these adapters and take the modular route with ready made microphone cables.

The feel of the radio is great for the price range. the cross band repeat is a nice feature. The display is good, easy to read. Now the bad... -Chrip, not there yet. It actually corrupts several of the channel settings when I push this to the radio. This may change in the future, but with the latest (as of Dec. 1 2014) daily release...not there yet. - The Wouxen software, not good. Its better than the Baofeng software, but I still find it challenging to move memory slots around. Also, I want to include in memory several law enforcement frequencies, WX and PUD frequencies, but there is no way that I can see to disable TX? Whats up with that? On other radios, I can add frequencies to my channel memories, but I can disable TX for those. On the KG-U8D I see no way to do this. So I only hope that my kids don't get a hold of the radio and key down on those channels. Other than that, I find the Intermod on the radio not bad compared to other Chinese radios. Its not a yaseu but at a 1/3 of the price its not bad.

My plans for this card included testing it using an ExpressCard 34 card reader installed in my older MacBook Pro and using it for the DVR function in a stand-alone surveillance camera. Well, the laptop died and the surveillance cam uses a micro-SD card. I decided to try something completely different. I bought a Sony MRW-F3 card reader, which is specifically compatible with SD HC and SD XC cards. I then plugged the reader and card into my desktop Mac. Using Disk Utility, I reformatted the card as a bootable Mac OSX drive. I then installed Mac OSX 10.6 (Snow Leopard) on the card and ran Software Update until the installation was current (10.6.8). The desktop machine was booting from the card after the initial install. I copied some diagnostic software from my hard drive and did any setup needed for that software. What I ended up with is a bootable external drive with diagnostic software. It is recognized as a startup volume by both the Startup Disk system preference and choose boot volume (option key) at startup. The computer boots quickly from the SD card and I did not find any issues running it from the card. The installation of the OS and diagnostic software uses a little more than half of the 16GB capacity. Unfortunately, this did not help my defunct laptop, which does not recognize any kind of drive, internal or external. On the other hand, I now have a quick, easy method of running maintenance routines and diagnostics on the boot drives of my two desktop Macs and any other Mac that can boot into OS 10.6. This is not how this card is typically used, but I wanted a challenging situation for the card and turning it into a boot drive seemed to fit the bill. The card is fast and reliable in this unconventional application.

Nice sound and stays on neck, but ear buds a bit uncomfortable for me, especially to sleep in, disconnects unexpectedly from any/all of my samsung devices. Get the samsung level app to go with it (but not needed). Good battery life, needed to try a few times to pair and connect. The samsung circles feel better, but feel flimsy compared to the better constructed and better sounding level u. The neck accessory that came with the circle was a bit uncomfortable and slips off neck, but the levels stay in place. Because of disconnect issue, level u not worth the high price, but would be if that was fixed!

I've had these for about three months now and the build quality is just not holding up. The charging case hinge started getting loose after a few weeks, and now the left earbud rattles if I move my head too fast. Sound was okay at first but the mesh over the speaker is already peeling. For the price, I expected them to survive a daily commute without falling apart. Really disappointed in how quickly they've degraded with normal use.
What's the point of buying a wireless keyboard and mouse if their range isn't much longer than the wires on my old ones?
I bought this as an extra long cable for using it in the car for a long road trip. Cable working great so far.
Got this for my kid's tablet and honestly it's a steal for what it costs. Sturdy enough to survive daily drops and still looks clean. Totally worth it.
No issues at all, card handles burst shooting perfectly.
Great, I only wish that you could roll in the entire wire.

I should have listened to the reviews before picking this up. I needed a simple way to back up family photos and documents, and the setup process alone had me pulling my hair out. The interface feels like it's from a decade ago, clunky and slow to respond. Once I finally got it running, the transfer speeds were laughable, a single folder of vacation pictures took over an hour. It disconnected from my network randomly, and I'd have to reboot it constantly just to find it again. For the price, I expected something reliable, not a headache that makes me dread doing basic backups. It's now sitting unplugged on my shelf while I look for an alternative that actually works.
Totally worth it, my tablet feels super secure now.

Good news! This not only works with PC and Mac, but also on my Linux. It paired immediately with the speakers I tried. The only drawback (and this isn't the fault of the dongle) was that I had to fiddle around a bit to find out how to select the external speakers rather than the default on-board speakers. So be advised: if you think it's not working, do check your output settings to be sure that it's not a problem with your default selection.

...or any of the other 'slim' Powershot pocket-sized cameras that are the same size. It fits in snugly and provides great protection. The case is well padded. The carabiner on the one we received is elongated rather than round as shown in the picture here. It comes off if you don't want it on there.

Bought for my 5 year old son, with the thought that he could put this on his iPod, and then side the ipod into a different case (flip-close case) for extra protection (his ipod was too loose in the flip-close case, and fell out, screen cracked, and we just had it fixed). So this is good, but a bit difficult to take off once it's on (I was afraid I might crack it). But seems to provide pretty good protection against minor drops and such.

I've had this adapter for about five months now. The casing started separating near the port after daily plugging and unplugging. It still functions, but I have to press it together sometimes to maintain the connection. For the price, I expected it to hold up better physically. Not terrible, but the build quality feels a bit flimsy over time.
Very cleanly designed case. This will become my default go to case for pc builds

For the price, this thing's a steal. I mainly needed two USB-C ports and it delivers, but the extra HDMI and USB-A slots are a nice bonus even if I rarely use them. Build feels solid, transfer speeds are consistent. Can't complain, cheap, effective, and ready if my setup ever expands.
This is the only earbud that fits my particular aural cavities, and the sleek controls have become a welcome addition to my daily travel. I dread the day when my last pair breaks!

For under $15, this little mouse is a steal. It's comfy enough for all-day work, the scroll wheel feels precise, and it glides without a hitch. I wasn't expecting much at this price, but it handles spreadsheets and casual browsing perfectly. If you need a reliable, no-fuss backup mouse, just grab this one.

The camera is amazing in every way except for that it has a noticeable shake on the outside edge when shooting in 4k mounted on a tripod. This is a very well known issue with this camera and they claimed to have fixed it with a firmware patch but after applying the firmware the problem still remains. Even when you disable the steady shot feature it still happens so something else is causing the optics to vibrate inside of the camera. Sony has been little help so far with correcting the issue and even claimed the couldn't tell when I sent them a video clearly illustrating the problem. The truth is the issue isn't that bad and you have to 'notice it' and in all honestly the other features of the camera still make it my primary camera with anything I shoot for Barnacules Nerdgasm on YouTube.

I'm totally recommending this to anyone with a small apartment or a cluttered desk. The extra mounting sleeve is a genius space-saver, so I just pop the arm between my bedroom and workstation without fuss. It's sturdy, simple to adjust, and perfect for folks who hate buying multiples.

My 8 year old uses this for his iPad mini. It's been dropped a few times and it's helped up well. Didn't want to spend so much money on the otter box, since I needed to buy 2. (His brother also was gifted an iPad mini) Glad this worked out for us.

Overall this TV has an awesome picture. White is bright brilliant white, and it almost looks like a samsung panel due to its glossy look, just instead of samsung logo you see TCL. This TV was 429, does 1080p and has tons of hook ups. More than most people will need. I also own a 55" LED Sony TV, and I dare say picture quality is barely below the sony, and may look better on cable, due to the compression used, and the fact it doesnt need to strech the compressed image another 15 inches. My only complaint for all TCL TV's at this time, is the lack of universal remotes being able to control them. I have a 2 TCL TV's right now (the 32 inch and 40 inch) and my Comcast DVR remote cannot control them. Their is no code in the manual for TCL at all, and when you use the "Search for code" feature, i went through it about 200 times, and could not get it to shut the TV off. I have also read may posts about people having this same issue. As best as I can tell at this time there are only 2 remotes confirmed to work with these TV's 1 being a 29 dollar logitech and the other being a Sony universal remote for about 15 bucks. Thats why I knocked off 1 star. Other than that so far this TV has been great, gives good picture quality, and OK sound from a normal TV setup. Definately a great bedroom TV for Videogames, TV, Movies, etc. Could even be used as a living room tv depending on the circumstances.

I have a single cable running from my router to the TV room and my Sony Blu-ray is connected to it. I wanted to add my laptop because we stream from Viki and that's not on the Sony unit and only use 1-device at a time. I tried an "Ethernet Splitter" RJ45 CAT 5 6 LAN Ethernet Splitter Connector Adapter PC but the description doesn't explain it fully enough and it didn't do what was expected. The splitter neither split nor allowed a shared connection. They allow you to run a second line over the same cable if you connect two lines to the router by using a connector at each end which allows you to use all 8-wires in a cable rather than the 4 normally used and it took a few You Tube videos to figure that out. Since I didn't have an extra port on the router I tried this device and it's perfect for what I needed. Just push a button and your signal goes to another device exactly like it should. Wish I had purchased this item first.

For under twenty bucks, this case is a steal. The fit is perfect, buttons click nicely, and the cover stays shut without a magnet flap dangling. Feels solid without being bulky. Honestly didn't expect this quality at the price point. Already tossed it in my bag a dozen times and the iPad Air still looks brand new.

I bought this thinking it'd be a simple plug-and-play backup drive that quietly does its thing in the background. Instead, it's basically a dumb external drive with extra steps. It doesn't automatically grab files from your computer, you have to drag everything over yourself, which defeats the whole point. My old Seagate from five years ago handles scheduled backups better than this. Feels like I paid more for less convenience. Pretty disappointing for a brand like WD.

Be Careful - this will not work with your Toyota if you have the Navigation system. The Navigation disc in most Toyota cars use the port on the back to connect the DVD player (data) to your radio. Therefore, there is no place to plug this in.... Recommend calling them first before ordering.

Honestly, these hold up just like the OEM one. For half the price, getting a pair is a steal. I've run them through several long shoots and the runtime is spot-on. They click right into the camera with no fit issues.

This thing showed up in a box two sizes too big with barely any padding, so I was half expecting the worst when I pulled it out. Luckily, the lens itself was double-wrapped in bubble wrap and didn't have a scratch. First impression pulling it out of the foam: it's got some real heft. Feels solid, the zoom ring is dampened nicely, and the glass was spotless right out of the sleeve. Popped it on my body and the autofocus locked in quick and quiet. I'm knocking off one star just because the outer packaging was sloppy, but the lens itself is impressive right from the unboxing. Really looking forward to putting it through its paces this weekend.

Bought this for use as a simple, two-source video switcher to use with the Epiphan Webcaster X2. Works seamlessly when both sources are the same resolution (eg both 1080p, or both 1080i) but produces a glitchy output if the input resolutions differ (one 1080i and one 1080p). Small and portable, and works pretty well. I wish I could figure out a way to use the USB port for controlling the switch. If anybody's figured this out, do let me know!
Microsoft LifeCam NX-6000 Webcam - This product works very well and I am very satisfied with it.

I've been using this Y connector to split audio from my DAC into two separate amps and the signal remains dead quiet, no hum or static at all. The gold-plated tips snap in securely and the flexible jacket makes routing behind my desk a breeze. Honestly, it outperforms cables I've paid triple for.
Bought this hoping to expand my laptop's ports, but it actually corrupted two USB drives. Completely useless and dangerous.
Nice beginner lens filter kit. I actually the lens cap the best, now I will not loose anymore caps Well built and Goja was a great company to buy from
Works just fine. I use it for travel. No issues with it at all.

For the price, this little drive is a steal. I grabbed the 256GB version on sale and it's become my go-to for moving video projects between my desktop and laptop. I'm getting around 130MB/sec write speeds on my newer machine's USB 3.0 ports, which is plenty snappy for 10GB file transfers. It does heat up a bit during long transfers, but nothing concerning. On my older laptop, speeds dip noticeably, so just know your setup matters a lot. Build quality feels solid, and the compact size slips right into my pocket. If you need blazing speeds on any random computer, you might be frustrated, but for the money, I'm genuinely happy with the real-world performance.
Had this camera mounted outside for over six months now through rain and heat, still works like new. Very solid build.
It is Bluetooth 3.0, nano USB adapter that just works. It has a good range and never skipped a beat. No fuss and no complaint.

I honestly wasn't prepared for how much this thing fills the room. Compared to my old 10-inch sub, the depth and tightness are on another level. It blends seamlessly with my bookshelf speakers and never sounds boomy. Setup took maybe ten minutes. Bigger than expected, but totally worth the floorspace for that clean, rumbling low end.

For the price, this Acer Iconia punches way above its weight. I mostly needed something for light work and streaming on the couch, and it handles both without breaking a sweat. Battery life surprised me too, lasts through a full workday easily. Build feels solid, not cheap at all. Honestly, you'd pay twice as much for a comparable brand-name tablet.

Needed some long cables to hook up charging stations for my wife and my Kindles at our bed. These do the trick. Nicely constructed with solid feeling connectors. Best thing about these is they are supple but not flimsy. Many cables have a tendency to revert to their coiled status after long periods of being packaged which makes running behind furniture and such a royal pain. These go where you put them and stay there, lying flat and not trying to curl up out of reach under the bed. Most cables are good and these are better than most. Both ends plug in solidly without undue force and with no feeling of "slop". I've had many that tend to slide back out of the Kindle end of things without warning. These do not do that.
This works really well, i know some games aren't meant to handle this but the ones that can, my goodness. Thank you for offering this product.
This is a very nice little case and I love the owls!
Quite with lots of power. I hope this lasts a long time.

I've had these for about six months now and they've held up way better than I expected. I use them almost daily for workouts and even accidentally left them in a damp gym bag overnight a couple times. No issues with connectivity or sound cutting out. The silicone tips are still intact, no tearing or loosening. Bass isn't super punchy but clarity is solid across podcasts and music. Battery life is consistent, usually getting around seven hours. Only minor gripe is the charging case hinge feels a little flimsy compared to the buds themselves, but it's never actually broken. For the price, the long-term durability alone makes them worth it.
I purchased these for my spouse and she loves them. She says that they are very comfortable and the sound is great
The plastic mounting threads feel flimsy, but the metal rings themselves are solid for macro shots.

I'd only recommend this to someone who never plans to take it outdoors or even move it from a shelf. The image quality is fine indoors with controlled lighting, but the moment you try to use it in bright sun, good luck. The screen glare is brutal, and the missing lens cap means you're constantly wiping smudges off with your shirt. I lost the flimsy cap within a week and Sony's official replacement costs way too much for what it is. If you're a beginner who only shoots inside and doesn't mind babying your gear, maybe it's okay. For anyone else, you'll just end up frustrated by the little oversights.

Item works as advertised. Good for baby monitoring and/or watching property. Quick delivery and works great. Small but looks like a normal clock. Motion Activated. Easy playback. Company has contacted me several times to be sure I was happy with their product. Company appears to stand behind their product.
For what I paid, this little speaker is a steal. Pumps out rich, room-filling audio and the battery lasts all day. Perfect for camping trips or just chilling on the porch.
Packs a lot of features for the low cost, really solid little unit.
Wow, little fan moves air well and really quite.
Works great with my camcorder, just took forever to arrive.

I've owned several of these (I lose them frequently), and I love them. They would be a perfect 5, except they come with the terrible U3 software, with no indication of how to remove it. Here is how to remove it: [.
Nice sound. For the price range it's a great option. Use it by the pool. Does seem to take a bit of time to sync.

I wasn't sure a budget case could feel this premium, but the airflow and build quality completely surprised me. Cable routing was painless and my temps dropped noticeably. Really happy with this purchase.

It does as advertised, mostly. It's on the buggy side as sometimes my mouse gets "lost" and neither machine works with input and I have to unplug the cable and plug it back in. As my laptop is right on the desk it's no problem but if you have this tucked away it could be an issue. Turning the other computer into an extended monitor is really neat and very helpful when I need a third screen for work. Normally either a KVM or switching inputs would solve this except my gsync monitor only has one input and a KVM limits the frame rate and breaks gsync so that's not a solution for me where this is. I've used a few different KM switches and they are all problematic, this is the most seamless solution I've found yet.

I shoot a lot of macro outdoors and the glare on the rear screen used to drive me nuts. This hood completely blocks the sun, so I can finally nail focus without guessing or cupping my hand awkwardly. It snaps on tight, stays put even when I'm crouching in weird positions, and folds flat into my bag. Honestly, I wish I'd bought it years ago.
As long as they don't lose any files from my weddings I love them. I have never lost files from a wedding so I worry when I purchase new CF cards. So far so good.

Helps to keep my Jaybird Bluebuds in ear without the wings, works great, hold up long, don't get too nasty and i just wipe off on shirt if they come out with a friend (sorry gross but it doesn't look dirty afterwards)
Can't even recommend this to my worst enemy, just skip it.

For the price, this little transmitter is a solid deal. I grabbed it mainly to pipe music from my computer to an old stereo in the garage, and it does that job without any fuss. Setup was basically plug and play once I found a clear frequency. The build feels a bit lightweight, but it's not like I'm moving it around much. Sound quality on the higher power setting is perfectly fine for casual listening, no noticeable hiss or drift. The lower setting is pretty weak, barely covering a single room, so I just leave it cranked. Considering what dedicated wireless audio setups cost, this thing gets the job done for pocket change. It's not fancy, but it absolutely delivers on the core function.

Got this to hook up my office PC without running a long cable across the hallway. Pairing took like ten seconds and the pass-through outlet is handy since I'm short on wall plugs. But the speed is wildly inconsistent. I pay for 200Mbps, and sometimes I'll get 90, other times it drops to 15 for no reason. Streaming a 4K video is a coin toss. It also runs surprisingly warm even when nothing heavy is going on, which makes me nervous. For basic browsing it's fine, but if you need stable bandwidth, look elsewhere.

Box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked, with zero padding inside, just the tablet rattling around. Pulled it out and the screen already had a faint scratch. Turned it on and the whole thing feels flimsy, with a weird plastic smell. Honestly, before I even tested anything, I already regretted this purchase.
Sticks well, but over time about a year it showed ripples where I slide it in the iLuv case. I need to learn how to get it replaced.
THE BEST CASE I EVER BOUGHT, IT FITS PERFECT, IT IS BEAUTIFUL, WILL BUY ANOTHER ONE!
Placed the old laptop drive (with some bad sectors) in this housing and recovered most of my data. Inexpensive, but has a solid case. Would definitely recommend.

I got this for toting my work laptop back and forth, and it is perfect, most especially for the price. There were no noticeable finishing issues. Nicely made, with decent padding in the straps and back. There is sufficient room for a large laptop, cords and cables, some notebooks, and whatever else I need for the workday. It would not have been sufficient for school textbooks and the like, but is more than adequate for work. I'm very petite, and while the bag is small, the straps and overall design are a bit out of scale for me, so if you are small also, you might feel a bit like a tortoise with too big a shell. The straps and the size of the pack are somewhat unwieldy- I'm 5'3" and hover around 125 lbs depending on recent pasta/beer/pie intake. I've read some complaints about the Tannin color- a dark red wine color. It's a traditionally 'feminine' color (whatever that means) - don't buy it if you don't wearing things that are the color of cabernet sauvignon/merlot/syrah and do buy it if you do.
Works with cat5e. I'm glad that you it also had a IR extender.
I love to shoot in low light with no flash. This film is perfect for that. I've pushed it to one stop with great results. Great contrast and tones.
I was able to upload photos on my laptop from my microchip. Pro: It's affordable, ANKER has the best customer service and 16-months warranty! Con: Make sure it's inserted in to read the card.

I wasn't sure this tiny box could drive my big old floor speakers, but it totally does, clean and punchy with zero hiss. The soundstage feels wider than my old receiver ever managed, and it barely gets warm. For the price, I expected compromises, but honestly it outshines gear I've spent three times as much on.

Arrived a day early and the box was in perfect shape, no dents or crushed corners, which I always appreciate for something I'm gonna be hauling gear in. Right out of the packaging, the material feels solid and the zippers glide smoothly. The padding on the shoulder straps is immediately noticeable, really plush compared to my old bag. I've only loaded it up once so far with a smaller mirrorless kit, and for that it's pretty much ideal, snug but not a struggle. If you shoot with a gripped body or a chunky zoom attached, you'll probably find the main compartment a bit optimistic. Overall, build quality and first impressions are strong, just know the camera insert runs small.
This right angle viewfinder was purchased at my husband's request. It arrived on time and just as described.

This case is well designed for the original version of the Pro because it's single viewing angle matched what the OEM stand provided. On the Pro 2, the second viewing angle is very help, but the build of this case eliminates it as an option without removing it from the case.

I've had these for about six months now and they've taken a real beating on hiking trails, kayak trips, and even a sandy beach vacation. No fogging, no scratches on the lenses, and the rubber coating still looks brand new. They've been dropped twice on rocky ground and somehow came away without a single crack. For someone like me who just wants to grab and glass a ridgeline without fiddling with dials, these are perfect. Super clear at a distance, and the simplicity means there's literally nothing to break. Honestly expected much less at this price point, but they've outlasted my more expensive pair already.
If you have a Promaster XC tripod, you're probably purchasing this because you need a replacement or you'd like a second for convenience. I fall into the latter, and this has worked great.

I wasn't expecting the case to feel this sturdy for the price, it fits my Fire perfectly and the stand positions are actually stable. The magnet snaps shut every time and waking the screen works without a hitch. Way better than the flimsy sleeve I had before.

This digitizer was way better than I expected for the price. Popped it in after a nasty drop cracked the top glass and honestly, the touch response feels snappier than the original. Colors look crisp too. Saved me a ton versus buying a new tablet. Super happy with how it turned out.
Thought I'd get at least a year out of it, but it died in six weeks.
horrible....can't format to fat32.....slow as hell, pisses me off everytime I have to copy files into it.

It's fast and durable. It fits well on my key ring but most of all, it writes and reads data quickly. It would be great if it was a little smaller but I guess one cannot have everything that he wants. I would certainly buy again. It's great to have 64 GB available at all times since I am able to carry it everywhere I go.

Fits my Nikon P500 like a glove with a bit of extra room for a spare battery. The padding feels sturdy enough for day trips. I'd suggest it to casual shooters who just need basic protection without spending a fortune.

I've had this little A3 for about six months now, and it's held up great through daily pocket duty and a couple accidental drops. The plastic body doesn't show much wear, buttons still feel clicky, and the screen's scratch-free despite my keys sharing the same pocket. Battery life hasn't faded at all either. I mostly use it for podcasts and occasional recording, and it's been a tank. For the price, I expected way less durability. Really impressed it's still running like day one.
Worked just fine and seemed to help keep laptop a little cooler.
The battery lasts way longer than I expected.
Shake the walls!!! This thing is awesome, lotta nice features, super easy to use, and doesn't heat up that much. I love the sound outta this.
This cable held up perfectly on a 12-hour drive, never lost connection once. Super convenient length for backseat charging.
Had this thing for a few months now, still going strong even after a couple drops.
this is a good harddrive for being refurbished that i bought one more i am very satisfied with the perfomance of it and would recomend it from gohardrive i will be buying more from them!!

I've been using this case for a little over a week now, and for the cost, it's a solid pick. The keyboard connects without any fuss and battery life seems decent so far. The build isn't premium, but it feels sturdy enough for daily use. The auto wake feature works most of the time, though occasionally I have to adjust the iPad slightly. Honestly, it's hard to beat at this price point, you definitely get good bang for your buck.

I've had this enclosure for about six months now, using it weekly as a backup drive for my old laptop. It's held up surprisingly well after a few accidental knocks off my desk, no data loss or weird noises. The aluminum shell does a decent job with heat, though it gets warm during long transfers. The cable connection feels a little loose sometimes, but it's never actually disconnected mid-task for me. Overall, solid value for the price once you get past that initial finicky setup.

I've had this grip for a few months now and honestly can't imagine using my E-M5 without it. For the price, it's a steal considering how much it transforms handling, especially with longer lenses where the extra heft and balance make a real difference. Installation took seconds, zero wiggle once locked on, and the added shutter button plus dial feel completely natural in portrait orientation. I was worried about cheap materials at this cost, but it's machined beautifully with a nice textured finish. Battery access through the grip is actually more convenient than the body alone. If you're on the fence, just grab it, best accessory bang for your buck hands down.
Barely lasted ten months before the casing cracked and two ports stopped working entirely. Definitely not built for daily use.
Feels solid in hand and handles drops like a champ, plus it looks sleek doing it.

I went into my first build expecting a headache, but this board made it surprisingly painless. Everything was clearly labeled and POSTed on the first try. The BIOS interface is actually pretty intuitive once you poke around, which was a relief. Rock solid stability so far and it handles my daily workload without breaking a sweat. Exceeded my expectations.

I figure if verbatim makes a great dvd-r why not a great cd-r so I ordered it and I was not dissapointed. The hundred pack is just what I need and I haven't made a coaster out of one yet. When comparing prices I found it is cheaper to order the hundred pack which records just as good as the fifty pack of the more expensive ones.

Four Stars for the iGadgitz armband, it is simple and performs well. So far the velcro has held up and it fits my iPod perfectly. The screen is not super sensitive and sometimes it takes a few try to change the volume or move around the iPod menu to change a playlist. I haven't noticed any tears in the armband material and it has held up well to my various workouts in the gym and on the courts. Solid product, good price. If any issues crop up (good or bad) I will edit review as necessary.
I'm sure this is good ram. It's the Corsair website that said it would work on my older MB when it didn't.

This one works with my Chevy factory cassette deck and my iPod. Perfect. Now the backstory: I had another maker's cassette adapter but the deck kept rejecting it. I purchased an iriver AFT 100 Mobile FM Transmitter and have been really underwhelmed. In frustration, I came back to the cassette adapter concept. Thanks to a previous reviewer who mentioned that this adapter works with GM factory cassette decks and others won't. I purchased this adapter and the Griffin 9749-PJBLK PowerJolt Auto Charger for iPod (Black) and it's a near-perfect solution for me. There's one more wire to deal with, but the sound quality (no static, no auto shutoff) is worth it. Update 3/28/09: After about a year and a half of occasional usage, the tape deck stopped liking the adapter. It would try to change sides a couple of times and then eject the adapter. Maybe something wore out? I purchased a replacement unit and it works fine, so it wasn't the deck. Someday I'll own a car with an iPod port... Update 12/8/09: The second one stopped working last month--that is to say, the tape deck started rejecting it. Eight months of very occasional use? I would really like to know what part is wearing down and causing this. I purchased another new one and we'll see how long that one lasts. If this one doesn't work out, maybe I'll just go buy a handful of cheap ones at the 99 Cents Only store. Update 1/24/10: The third one stopped working, but I don't think it's the adapter; I think it's my cassette deck. So I can't really blame the adapter. I purchased an inexpensive FM transmitter (Apple iPod FM Transmitter Car Kit) and it works great so far, with a lot less frustration.

I have Comcast 75Mb service. I had a very old zoom 5341J (8x4) running until it died. I put back in my even older Zoom 5341 (4x4) modem while I looked for a replacement. I tried the Arris SB6141 (8x4) which worked well until it died. So I decided to try something else and liked the idea of this 16x4 model. However, once I replaced the Zoom, it would only achieve 55 Mb/s download. I tried multiple power resets, but even though it bonded with 16 download channels I would peak about 55 Mb/s. I put the very old Zoom (4x4) back in and it went back to 82Mb/s, There are no configurable parameters. I had expected at least the same performance and possibly better than the very old Zoom.
Great little case for Canon compact cameras, like Canon 130 IS, and Canon 320 HS. Snug fit, easy to get out, good protection. Feels well-made and durable.

Box showed up in two days with zero damage. Unboxing felt premium, and the build quality surprised me right away. Fired it up and the sound instantly filled the room, seriously impressive for the price.
It doesn't seem to receive as well as a ground plane antenna but the SWRs are fine and it has held up in a few very nasty wind storms.
For the price, this cable is a steal. Handles my PS5 to monitor flawlessly with zero lag or dropouts.
I've tried a few DVRs over the years, but TiVo makes it effortless. I'd recommend it to anyone who hates missing shows or fumbling with clunky menus.

It worked on Windows 10. Software was mehh. Unfortunately it can only output at 480P and I needed a 16:9 aspect ratio for my purposes. Ultimately had to go with another solution but it was cheap and worked well for what it was. Hardly any latency in preview modes on various capture software I tried out.
Does exactly what it should, way more solid than I expected for the price, perfect fit for my setup.

Serves it's purpose but we actually took the plastic piece off that covers the lens because my daughter felt like it was making her pictures more blurry and unclear. The only reason she wanted it was for the selfie mirror anyway to make sure that she would be getting her face in the picture.

I was worried these would be bulky or cheap-feeling, but they're surprisingly compact and the rubbery material feels way more durable than hard plastic.
