Arrived a day early in a sturdy, no-nonsense box with molded inserts that kept everything snug. Setup took maybe ten minutes and the signal reached my backyard workshop right out of the gate. The web dashboard feels snappy and clean, way more polished than I expected. Genuinely impressed with how solid and refined this thing feels from the start.

This diffuser works quite well. Please see the 3 pictures I uploaded. The figure was 5.5" high and 3 feet away. The wall was 14" behind the figure. Pictures were taken at 70mm zoom setting (full frame equivalent of 105mm). Even with the camera at automatic mode, I still had to increase exposure by +2/3 stops to get equivalent exposure as the picture w/o diffuser. If your lens does not rotate during focus, then you should attached it close to the front of the lens. When you zoom to telephoto, the diffuser is pushed even farther from the flash, making the diffused lighting even more effective. This is great for macro photography. The diffuser is rather large (12" total height). When attached to my Nikon 18-105mm lens with a 67mm filter, the hole and the elastic band are almost at their maximum. The diffuser flops in the wind a bit and will flop even more if your lens is smaller. I found it easier to mount the diffuser with the lens cap still on the lens. The carrying pouch is much bigger than needed. You can just wrap the folded diffuser with a rubber band and put it in your pocket. I don't think getting it slightly dirty will affect the use of it at all. Overall, quite happy with this diffuser.
Arrived a day early in secure packaging. The cables feel sturdy right out of the box and connected instantly to my phone without any fuss.

good for basic and basic action shoots. not for pros but for mid range price and use for basic action like sports, great.... great for beginners or mid users. company sent basic in mail and had no issues.
I honestly didn't expect this much sharpness for the price. It blew my expectations out of the water, especially for casual outdoor shots. Really happy I went with it.

Bought this for daily commuting and it's been a total letdown. The straps started fraying within two weeks, and the padding is so thin my shoulders ache after a short walk. The main compartment barely fits my laptop plus a notebook, anything else feels like a fight. Honestly, I've used grocery totes that held up better. Complete waste of money.
Pretty much what I hoped for, screen sensitivity is just okay.

Pros: Picture quality is still good, Canon has not lost it's touch after ten years of making Elphs. The size is awesome, I can place it anywhere I'd like. With a three inch LCD, who needs a view finder - does anyone still use a view finder? It offers a lot of scene modes and they are not that difficult to get to, it just takes a little time to get used to the buttons. The 6.0 megapixel and 3x optical zoom and UA lens is all you need. This Elph now offers a new ISO 800, which reduces image blur and expands low-light shooting ability. It's camera solution CD-ROM software has improved a lot, so it's easier to download, edit, and view your pictures. Cons: It's features are a bit basic but it offers just enough for those point and shot customers. So if you want a lot of manual features you might want to pay more for another camera. The LCD might get thumbprints very easily but I have one word for you: invisibleSHIELD. Amazons might sell one for this camera, if not, do a web search. It doesn't come with a case or bag; the manual is boring and just like all the others - you may need to buy a book if photography is a hobby for you. The Function/Set buttons might be too small, but I have small hands so I'm cool with their size. Conclusion: The Canon PowerShot SD630 is an solid ultracompact camera, with performance and photo quality that more than make up for its few design flaws and it's price. It's a good shooter, and its feature set makes it quite flexible for a camera of its size. That said, it's basically a more expensive version of the PowerShot SD600 with a larger screen and no optical viewfinder.

Bought this to use with my Zoom H1n recorder. Completely dead on arrival, no power, no signal, nothing. Tried it in multiple devices just to be sure, same result. At this price I'm not wasting time on a return, but it's still annoying. I'd only recommend this to someone who enjoys gambling with cheap dongles that might not work. If you actually need your gear to function when you press record, skip this one and spend a few extra bucks on something reputable.

I wasn't sure what to expect at this price, but it's way sturdier and more responsive than I assumed. Setup took two minutes and the sound is crisp without any distortion. Honestly feels like a premium gadget for a budget cost.
Arrived in perfect condition, double-boxed and super secure. Took it out and was immediately impressed by the solid build. Setup was a breeze and it just feels premium right out of the gate.
For the price I can't complain much, but it's a coin toss if it connects.
Honestly, I expected basic performance at this price point, but the feature set absolutely smokes gear costing twice as much. Rock solid stability so far.
I like the blue lights and it works really well.
great item! no more need to sit right by outlet. made well. my old style still works after years with ipad 2. now with ipad air, new style is very good after months of use.

It does what it says - it provides a stable mount for up to two 2.5" drives in a regular internal hard drive slot. Here are the problems: It's not compatible with the newer "toolless" cases that use posts rather than screws to secure the hard drive. The posts will not fit through the holes. Luckily, thermaltake does provide all the screws you need - just pop the posts out of your hard drive tray or mounting bracket and replace with the larger of the two sizes of screw they provide. It is also difficult to access the screw hols for the lower of the two drives in this mount. If you need to mount two drives, do the lower one first, and find a small screwdriver. Finally, the connectors for the hard drive will be recessed about one inch into the bay compared to the rest of the drives. If you have a small or a very populated drive bay, it will be difficult to access those connectors, and if you have the angled SATA connectors, you will not be able to connect two drives in this bay, at least one drive will need a straight SATA connector. I only needed it to mount one hybrid SSD/HDD, but I do like knowing that even though it will take some effort, I can mount a second 2.5" drive if I need to.

Honestly, these headphones were quite mindblowing, and a solid step up from the Panasonics which are so popular here on Amazon. The sound quality made me feel ecstatic, and seems appropriate for all genres. I simply love blasting these things and hearing a surprisingly high level of detail in my music. Plus, the wires stay smooth and separated. Sadly, the earbuds don't isolate noise so well, as I often ride bike / go jogging with these, you can hear the wires rubbing and the wind interference, causing you to boost the volume in such situations. But for every other user, you can't go wrong!
One of those things bought mostly because it LOOKS useful. Seldom use it but when I do it's as close to perfect as you can get for what it's made for.

These JBL Stereo Headphones (ear buds, really) have brought music back to my ears. Since losing most of the hearing in one ear (and about 20% in the other), my experience of music was drastically altered. Stereo became an aberrant mono, movement became stuck in place, and range became unpleasantly narrow. Amazingly, these headphones compensate for much of that, and music sounds rich and clean once more. I cannot explain the acoustics that make this happen, but I am thoroughly surprised by and appreciative of it. (The first music I listened to was a pre-hearing loss favorite Van Morrisons Poetic Champions Compose. The alto and soprano saxophones have reclaimed their divinity.) The rated frequency response is 10 22K hertz. The buds are comfortable and fit securely; I did not need to use the optional silicone tips. By the way, I periodically clean the bud tips with a nearly dry, non-alcohol, fragrance-free baby/body wipe. You might notice any human dirt with each use, but the grubbiness does build up over time.

The grip isn't tight enough for an iPad that isn't in a case, also the unit can rattle while driving. Finally, I find that my toddler constantly kicks the unit when an iPad isn't installed. More than once she managed to turn and screw off the back with her foot. A better system would hold the screen between the seats away from toddler feet and rattle less.

I grabbed these mostly for late-night listening, and they've quickly become my daily go-to. Right out of the box, the plush memory foam cushions sat snug without any weird pressure points, even during a three-hour playlist session. The passive noise blocking is surprisingly effective, my partner's gaming rig fades to a soft hum. What really got me was the warm, balanced profile; bass feels present but never muddy, and acoustic tracks have this lovely open airiness. Honestly, they've made me fall back in love with just sitting and absorbing an album.

I have had this lens for about 3 weeks and I'm very happy with it. I take my camera everywhere so i don't normally have a plan. I wander around seeing what I encounter so a zoom like this is perfect for me. I have found the focus to be fast and accurate. I'm using this with a Nikon D5100. I can't disagree with the comments regarding some of the limitations, but for what I do it is great for me. Here's a shot I took the other day [...] I'll add that I got this at my local camera shop. They got the Nikon mount in before Amazon did.

I've had my XTi for just over a month now (bought from Amazon) and it's really improved my photography. Others have even commented that I've "shifted to a new gear". Features are a nice improvement from the DSLR-wannabe I had before (Panasonic FZ30) but it's the image quality that's really the big step up. Get the best-quality lenses for this (*not* the kit lens) and you'll be very happy. Pros: * Image Quality * Ergonomics (most common settings are easy to change, good displays) * Battery life (200 shots with 50% flash and battery still said 100%) * The user manual is decent, though not stellar. * Long exposure noise reduction feature is very good. * Good set of customization options (though the manual doesn't explain them enough). Cons: * Canon's organizer/editor software, Digital Photo Professional, always crashes on my Windows PC and technical support was no help. * Hand grip is too small (height and volume) for my large hand. Make sure you handle one first. I had to train myself to squish all three fingers into the space provided so my pinky didn't hang off the bottom. This is a real step backwards from the original Digital Rebel. * Some settings that I use a lot are hidden too far in the menus. * Sensor dust cleaner won't get everything. I had to manually blow dust off the sensor (with a blower, *NOT* my breath!) about 1 week after I got this, though no sensor dust since. Conclusion: I'm *very* glad I bought this camera and look forward to many years using it. BTW, Amazon farms this order out to another vendor, so you won't get free shipping and you'll have to contact that vendor separately to get an order tracking number. If you care: You can see my photos with this camera on Flickr at: [...] I ordered the body w/o the kit lens, and instead got the Canon 17-40mm L zoom and the Canon 70-300mm IS zoom. At some point I'll add the Canon 50mm 1.8 lens.

Arrived two days early in a neat little box, nicely wrapped, no crushed packaging at all. Popped them in and was instantly impressed by how comfortable and secure they feel. The sound is crisp and balanced right out of the gate, and the inline remote actually feels solid and responsive. Great first impression.

The Battery keeps discharging - almost every time I need to use it, it's dead or near dead. After a few months of use it will only charge to 90% and once you take if off the USB charger, it drains in about 1 week when not used at all, and when I did attach it and begin using it for a few minutes the charge shows 50%. I have a DUD! Had the battery BacPac worked as advertised it would have been useful. The battery has it's own usb port for charging and a small meter on the back which you can check by pressing a small button on the side of the battery - I suspect the electronics inside the battery is defective.

I picked up this Panasonic set in late 2010 thinking I was getting a solid mid-range TV. Fast forward to March 2012 and the screen just went black in the middle of a movie. No warning, no power surges, just dead. I've moved twice with this thing and babied it the whole way, never left it on overnight, always used a surge protector. Picture was decent when it actually ran, but what's the point if the panel craps out after barely a year of normal use? Really disappointed in the build quality here. For the price I expected at least three or four years of daily watching, not a fancy paperweight. Never buying Panasonic again.

I've tried a bunch of outdoor cams over the years and this little dome unit honestly surprised me. Unboxing was simple and I had it up under the eaves in maybe twenty minutes. The image is crisp, way better than I expected for the price, and the night vision actually reaches the far end of my driveway without turning everything into a grainy mess. Color accuracy during the day is spot on and the wide angle covers my whole front porch perfectly. We've had heavy rain and a few 90-degree days and it hasn't flinched once. The app setup was painless and motion alerts come through fast without spamming my phone. Solid build, smooth performance, zero regrets.
Had this case for a few months now, still looks brand new after daily drops.

You're paying less than 10bucks for a pair of headphones. If you're and audiophile and crystal clear sound and deep bass are your thing, don't get them. For normal use they cancel out surrounding sound, don't sound 'tinny' like other cheap earbuds. I actually only have two complaints. The volume control is a little heavy so if you are moving around and don't clip the audio control somewhere it will pull it out of your ear (that's why they provided a clip) and since the cord comes shipped wrapped up very tightly, the cord is bent all over the place back and forth and will take quite a bit of time to straighten out. Wrapping it around the provided 'cord manager' will cause this as well if left for long periods of time. Overall, great earbuds. Using with my ipad for gaming/movies and iphone for music and games.

For less than twenty bucks, this thing is a steal. My daughter's tablet has survived countless drops onto hardwood. It fits snug, isn't bulky, and the stand works perfectly for car rides. Honestly can't believe how solid it feels for the price.

I've gone through my fair share of frustrating screen protectors, but this one from Green Onions was a breeze to put on. It's slightly undersized, which actually made aligning it super forgiving, no more wrestling with exact edges or trapped bubbles. The anti-glare finish is spot on; reading outside in bright sun is way more comfortable now, and it doesn't mess with the screen clarity at all. Honestly, for the price, this feels like a steal. You get a clean, nearly invisible look with solid protection, and installation took maybe two minutes. If you want a no-hassle shield that punches above its cost, this is the one to grab.

It almost hurts to write a two star review for this speaker. It's beautifully crafted and sounds great. However, the auto-off function is a deal breaker, so this is going back. I have battery powered Bluetooth speakers that don't have this "feature"; why Polk decided to include it in an AC powered speaker makes no sense. Further, when I power the speaker on, it doesn't automatically connect to the last known device. I have to go back to my iMac every time and tell it to connect to the speaker. Also, I haven't been able to keep the Airplay connection working for more than a few minutes. I suppose this could be due to any number of factors, so I can't put this squarely on Polk, but that was one of the primary reasons I selected this particular model.

Ordered this for my Canon PowerShot ELPH 300 HS and it showed up way faster than expected. The packaging was super secure, no dents or crushed corners on the box, and the battery itself was sealed in a static-free pouch inside. Popped it into the charger right away and the fit was perfect, no wiggling. First charge topped off quickly and it clicked into the camera with zero hassle. Honestly couldn't tell it apart from the factory original that came with the camera. Super relieved to have a reliable spare now.
Bought this for my niece and she hasn't put it down all week!

Works well after several sensitivity adjustments to eliminate false alarms in my particular environment. The sound is realistic enough when heard behind a closed door. In my estimation it will take a very perceptive burglar to determine that it is not a real dog behind the door. I have it connected to power via a WeMo switch and it turns on when i leave home and off when I return. This works only if no batteries are installed.

I keep 2 on my desk just for file transfer and to use for everyday tasks. Very easy to use and all computers see them quickly and load them on command. The swivel lid is a nice option as well as I am always losing the caps. I have never had trouble with Kingston memory and will continue to purchase them from Amazon. Nice price, got here in 2 days, great product and great buy.
Honestly way better picture quality than I expected for the price.
purchased 4 of these for work and have heard no complaints from the staff using them, Easy set up and install.

Bought this device on October 2011, and as of today, in Feb, suddently died, it was no longer recognized within the network, it did not get an IP address anymore, making it unaccesible. Does seem to boot allright, but can't read or retrieve information inside. So had to send it through an RMA to WD. Too bad, as ALL THE INFORMATION WAS LOST! Of course I will get a new unit, but... no way to get back the info.. I'm not saying ALL UNITS are bad, but I had the bad luck of Dead on less-than-half-a-year. Update: Sent the defective unit back with RMA, got a replacement and it's working so far, but, I lost all the data that was on the old drive. Tech support was great handling this, but I regret losing my data.

Just to start, I love music and I have purchased the best of the best in headphones from Bose to Shure. They fit nicely in the ear but do take the time to see which of the sises fit best of the 3 fittings they send you. The sound is VERY heavy in bass and is great to listen to techno and house genres. They lack in treble and have good enough mids. Not the best for listening to jazz or blues as the lack of treble does make things a little muddy, but not TOO bad. The cord is long enough and does not feel like it will snap easily. They have a good build quality and do not seem like they will break in the near future.

I mainly shoot portraits and casual events, and this lens hasn't left my camera since day one. Rendering is crisp without feeling clinical, and backgrounds melt away beautifully. I'd easily recommend it to anyone doing street, travel, or low-light portrait work who wants something compact and dependable.

Thermaltake Mobile Fan 12 External USB Cooling Fan 120mm AF0007, Got this fan and the Coolerguys 120x120x25mm USB Fan with Grill, both are great, this one is strong to 6" in front of it, the Coolerguys is strong only to 4" in front of it, after that the airflow is not noticed as much if any. I got these to use for my DVR boxes, if you put a fan over the hdd of the DVR box, your box will last for years, otherwise every 2 or 3 years they will crap out on you, my DVRs have been here for over 5 years and I have 3. I think both fans are great and if you want control and more power then go with the Thermaltake, for DVR you only need about 2 inches of airflow so both fans are doing great for me. Hope that helps you a bit.
We have a Toshiba phone system, This system works for the price however its hard to hear at times on the other end.
I just had to hide those showing wire and hide them from my sight and now I do not notice them at all in plain view.

I just wish the ear cushions were a bit deeper; after an hour or so, they start pressing against my ears and get uncomfortable. Sound quality is crisp though, and the battery lasts forever. I'm impressed. As stated, not HD. And no drivers need for Win 8 or Mac Lion. Good color, good contrast even in rather low light. Resolution is way better than an X10 and on a par with older Logitech products. Mine had a 3.5 MM microphone cable attached with the USB, and nothing comes out, I mean nada. I honestly expected a lightweight compromise, but this thing feels rock-solid on uneven terrain. The monopod mode is a game-changer for quick wildlife shots. Build quality far exceeds what I imagined at this price point. Totally impressed.

Only a little moisture shuts these down, and the earpieces need replacement immediately for comfort. They do sound good and are loud. The changeable chamber caps do make an incredible difference. I would suggest Sony or Klipsch earpieces and dry ear canals. For a workout these should be rated no stars.

Sounds clear enough for casual listening, just wish it got louder.

Perfect for anyone doing unboxing or tutorial videos. Keeps my DSLR locked dead center so there's no awkward shadows. If you own a ring light, just grab this already.

Bought this to revive an old Dell Latitude that was crawling on a spinning drive. Slipped it into the mSATA slot, cloned the OS, and now it feels like a brand-new machine. Boots in under 10 seconds, apps snap open, and multitasking is actually smooth again. I'm not a tech wizard, but the whole swap took maybe 15 minutes, and the included screw was a nice touch since I'd lost the original. I'd recommend this to anyone with an aging laptop that supports mSATA, students, office workers, or just folks who don't want to ditch perfectly good hardware. For the price, it's a no-brainer upgrade that breathes years of life back into your rig.
I expected decent quality for the price but this cable exceeded that completely. Crystal clear audio with zero interference, and it was surprisingly flexible during setup.

Got this to hide speaker wire along the baseboard in my home office. The channel itself is solid and holds the cable well, but getting the wire to stay put during installation was more annoying than I expected. If you're planning to run it flat along the floor where gravity helps, it's fine. I'd only recommend it for that specific use case, any vertical surface, even lightly textured, and the adhesive gives up within a week. I tried pressing firmly and cleaning the wall first, no luck. For a permanent wall mount, you'll need to add screws or find something else entirely. Decent product, just very limited in how you can actually use it.
Second one I've bought, one for me, one for daughter. Recently suggested it to my brother as well.

I'm really happy with this camera overall. The image quality surprised me, and setup was super straightforward. My only gripe is the included tripod, it feels flimsy and didn't lock properly right away. Still, for the price, the photos come out crisp and vibrant. Definitely a solid pick for beginners.

The Acer Aspire I bought has 2gb ram and overall is quite fast enough for my uses and with all my Microsoft Office products. The wireless works very good and I love the unit. I am an usually an Apple guy and really wanted a Mac Air but being that I had to buy an out drive to load software and watch DVDs plus tax I was looking at $1300 +/- a few dollars. I decided that since I only really use this for traveling this would be a better deal and that it was. The big negative on this unit however is the touch pad which is terrible and almost unusable to me and I have to use a mouse to really do anything or else you go crazy wasting time trying to point and click to the areas you want. The pad just is not sensitive enough even after I adjusted as much as possible. With the mouse it works great and it's really no big deal at least in the way I use it but if I had to continually use this with just the pad I would dump it for something else in a minute. Overall though it is a a very good machine though.
I've had this for a few months now and it's held up well, no loose ports or glitches. Occasionally I need to toggle the source input, but the solid build makes up for it.
Surprised by the quality for what I paid, solid build and speeds are no joke.
The auto-focus is surprisingly sharp, and the built-in mic picks up my voice clearly without any static.
Charges my phone super fast with the included cable, and the compact design doesn't block the second outlet at all.
A really fun project to do, would suggest a gift to someone that is interested in computers.
It's slower than the integrated graphics on my HP business desktop from 2004, so don't get it unless you're upgrading something ancient.
Very nice refurb! Looks brand new and works great. Purchased for my wife to get away from small phone screen.

I wanted to love this camcorder, but it's a real mixed bag. The compact size and sleek look are great for tossing in a bag, but the fixed memory fills up faster than you'd think. No SD slot is a head-scratcher. Battery life is mediocre, and once it degrades, you're basically out of luck since it's sealed in. Video quality in good light is decent, but the tiny screen makes framing shots outdoors a squint-fest. I'd only recommend this to absolute beginners who just want quick, casual clips for social media and don't plan to shoot anything lengthy. If you need flexibility or plan to use it regularly, skip this and find something more practical.

It is okay due to its low price point. It does what it wants to do but the main issue is the white rings. After constant use the white rings loose attachment to the legs and fall off. I recommend saving up a little more to get a Joby Gorilla Pod. However this is still a wonderful few time use product!

This cable works exactly as expected. There is no signal delay, and no degradation of picture quality, even though it is really long. Its hard to get excited about a cable, so I can't really say I LOVE it. But I would definitely recommend it.
These are great. Wash up great, and no scratches on glasses, smartphones, or tablets. Highly Recommended.

This kit is exactly what I needed to start experimenting with filters. Everything fits perfectly, feels sturdy, and arrived super fast. Definitely worth grabbing if you're just getting into photography.
Couldn't get it to work. I'd avoid this one.

I've had these cables running behind my nightstands for about four months now and they're holding up perfectly. No fraying at the connector ends, which is usually where mine start to fail. They get yanked around a bit when I'm fumbling for my tablet in the dark and the reinforced joints don't budge. The outer jacket still looks brand new too, even with the occasional vacuum cleaner run-in. I appreciate that they lay flat right out of the package and haven't developed any kinks or weird memory loops over time. Honestly forgot I was using aftermarket cables until I needed to move one to a different outlet. Just ordered a second pair for our travel bags.
Arrived super fast and packed really well, works perfect in my car.
I expected just another USB cable, but the flat design actually stays put under my desk. Speeds are noticeably faster than my old round one, and it feels sturdy without being stiff.
Bought this for my dad who's not tech-savvy, and he figured it out instantly, perfect gift for older relatives.
Fits good, looks good, scratched my new LCD screen, even though I am very careful when covering the TV. The inside front material should have some padding or a soft layer.

I've been using the Klipsch Image X10i for years, so when I grabbed these Etymotics as a secondary pair I wasn't sure what to expect. Shipping was super quick, arrived two days early in a sturdy little box with zero damage. Unboxing felt no-frills but everything was snug and well protected. First plug-in, the seal was immediate and isolation is no joke. Right away I noticed how crisp the upper details are and how vocals sit forward in the mix without getting harsh. Bass is definitely leaner than I'm used to, but it's tight and doesn't muddy anything. Build feels light but solid. If you're after a clean, reference-ish sound and don't mind a lighter low end, these are a solid pickup.

I grabbed this to wire up my smart TV and game console. The build quality feels solid and the clip-on shielding keeps everything snug. Streaming 4K content is buttery smooth now with zero buffering, and my download speeds jumped noticeably. Best cable upgrade I've made.

Great Price for 2 batteries and a charger. Shooting video with a DSLR consumes a lot of battery and having extra ones is imperative. These don/t last as long as the Canon ones but are way way cheaper. I would Highly recommend them.
It does exactly as describe, and it actually looks good when your camera is black.
These were parts for my new home-built computer. The pricing and quick shipping made for an affordable, stress free home-build. Since the computer is working great I don't have any complaints.
This little amp did so much more than I expected. I have four 2inch high's and one 4inch mid and on the sub channel I have a 15 it does not bounce but you get tour bass

A little smaller than my vintage 13 inch simply because this screen is wide and my old one was longer from top to bottom. But it serves the purpose quite nicely for the kitchen. I use it with my blu-ray dvd player and it is awesome. The sound is tinny even though I use external speakers. Don't know if a better pair of speakers would raise this rating but for the price of the TV I son't know if it would be worth the investment. I like it very much.

I purchased several new monitors for my business only to find that the simple task of connecting the new ones was not going to be as simple as I thought. Most of my computers (HP Z210) have one display port and one dvi output and the new monitor had only vga and hdmi inputs. The handy adapter worked just as I needed so all is good.
I am really happy with shell case. Like how I can clip it to my pants and not have to put it in my pocket anymore. Love the color.

I did an install job with this in a 96 Land Rover Discovery. The cables made the length of the whole vehicle, they stripped easy, and made some pretty sharp corners. I will be using these again in future installs!
For the cost, it does the job. Threads are a bit snug but it protects the glass fine.

This is a great active adapter. I purchased a radeon r9 390 that didn't like my HDMI to DVI cable I had been using on my old card for my separate monitor. Since switching to this adapter things are working great and both of my monitors are correctly detected.
The only player I had for nine years that still functions as it did then. Yeah, it's slow and stupid, but it's been reliable as s***.
Not bad for occasional use, but there's a faint buzz when nothing's playing. It gets the job done for the price, though I wish it felt less cheap overall.

I picked up the ELPH 110 HS mainly because I didn't want to spend a fortune replacing my old point-and-shoot, and I've been pleasantly surprised by how much it delivers for the price. The image quality is crisp, colors look natural, and the low-light performance is way better than I expected at this price point. It's slim enough to slip into a jeans pocket, which makes it perfect for trips or just casual outings. The 1080p video is smooth and the stabilization actually works, so handheld clips don't turn out shaky. Battery life is solid, and the menu system is intuitive. Honestly, for what I paid, it feels like a steal, great everyday camera without any fuss.
Had my doubts, but it totally did the trick. Sound is clear as a whistle. Took seconds to set up-without reading the instructions:)

I really like this product. It is good quality and will work with many smaller mirrorless cameras (not just Sony's). I have used it successfully with my Nikon V1 equipped with the 18.5mm lens as well as an Olympus E-PL3 equipped with the 45mm lens. My friend tried it today on his Olympus OMD and it fit as well. The case will work best on smaller mirrorless cameras equipped with medium sized lenses given the way the lens is used to keep the case closed on itself. Do not hesitate to consider the "camera pamper" if you want to keep your camera protected inside another bag or a backpack without taking up too much space.

These binder cases have been a total game-changer for organizing my media. They feel sturdy without being bulky, and I love how neatly everything fits on the shelf. No more digging through stacks of discs or worrying about damage. Honestly wish I'd made the switch years ago, it just keeps everything so effortlessly tidy and accessible.

I'd only recommend this drive to someone who enjoys losing hours of work with zero warning. Got mine for backing up family photos while traveling. It worked okay for maybe five months, light use only. Then one evening I plugged it in, the light blinked, it made a soft clicking sound, and my laptop just froze trying to read it. Never dropped it, always kept it in a padded case. After that night it became a paperweight. Recovery quotes were insane. If you have a mortal enemy who needs unreliable storage, sure, gift them this. Otherwise save yourself the headache and pick literally anything else.

If you are use to a standard video camera....do not purchase this item. I had a sony HD handycam that lasted only 4 years...but the picture was outstanding. I may send this camera back if Amazon will take it back......
Perfect for gamers and office workers who need a reliable daily driver.
I was skeptical at this price, but the battery life is fantastic. It holds a charge way longer than the original Panasonic one, so I'm not constantly swapping mid-shoot.
excelent!!! the best for the price!! is a perfect product for me I am photographer and basic dj. love this earth phones

Sweet, works great! When plugged in it always defaults to VGA which is a little annoying to need to press for HDMI, but not complaining. It's very useful to be able to switch display with the push of a button. As with all of these cheap brand accessories, the quality is lacking but be gentle with it and it'll last.
I have been using this drive for my mac book pro with the hard drive tray for about 6 months and it has worked flawless. The increase of the speed of the computer was very notorious.
Got it in two days, box was pristine, fired right up.
Used with Nikon d5200. Water does not leak. The finger pockets are placed in the right position. The lens section is large enough for a long lens, but is kinda annoying for a short lens.

Had to experiment with the included sizes before landing on the right pair, but once I did, the seal was solid. They stay put through sweaty gym sessions and don't irritate my ears after a couple of hours. Noise isolation is better than expected for the price. Knocking off one star only because the packaging could be less wasteful. Overall a dependable upgrade for my earbuds.
The product is very good, but just as other comment the case a bit bigger than 13inch MAC Book Pro, can live with it.
Picked this up for an old laser printer I rescued, and it fired right up. Honestly can't beat the price for getting legacy gear back in action.

2013-08-04 Update - They worked great for about a month. Two weeks ago, the one side stopped working. It works at the beginning of the run, the stops after about 2 miles. It will work again the next day. I have tried everything. Once, on a longer run, it did restart towards the end of the run. This was my first, and now last, Philips purchase. I just don't understand how the quality control could be so poor. Original Review Have been trying to find an inexpensive headset like the Nike pair that I had and loved. These are not as inexpensive as the Nikes but if they last longer, they will be worth it. I like them and plan to stick with these - have had to return a couple of types I recently bought. The sound is good, not great, but for running and podcasts its what I need. They are very comfortable. You can wear them both over and under the ear, which is really nice. The cord gets in the way a bit if you wear your phone/mp3 on your arm but there is clip that helps keeps it out of the way. The cord is a good length if you are keeping the phone/mp3 in your pocket.

I bought this to clone the HD from my MacBook Pro before I installed a new HD in it. I put a new 500GB Hitachi HD into this case and cloned by existed 160GB HD onto this using Carbon Copy Cloner. I had to make sure the drive and cable were never touched during the process, or else the drive would lose the connection. I eventually got everything working. It is just very temperamental. But for the price, I am glad I got it. It won't be used on a regular basis.

Probably is better, but unfortunately there is nothing to compare. First, glued matte film stylus so slow. The corners and edges have to poke several times to get the result (can, of course, is the property of the film is). Second, the style is very rough, I would like in thinner. In general, the first experience with the use of this gadget is not impressed.
LOVE IT, LOVE IT!! Works great and to be able to listen to music with a beanie on in the winter is GREAT! Love the color too!

I've had this only for two days and I will update in a month. It works great with my ipad 3 it charges really fast and the adapter has a built in feature that doesn't let the battery get too hot or overcharge it. The only thing that it's not so great is that the ipad faces up and I would have liked it to be a little more bendable. Other than than its a great product and I would definitely recommend it. It's a dock/charger/stand/case. Ohh and another thing I would have changed its that you have to slide it out a little to adjust volumes because the case covers it.
Arrived fast and packed perfectly, this board runs rock-solid right away.

Modem gets over 16 channels down, 4 channels up, ISPS don't even offer it yet - I have a SB6141 White (8x4 450Mbps) but I got this for my mother who got internet from a cable provider that brings fiber FTTN within 1,000 feet so they can sell businesses on fiber mainly, only 400 modems per node vs Comcast at over 1800. Only thing is ISP offered 110Mbps and is only using 8x4 - not full 16x4 which I hoped for - load balancing. A 4x4 could do it but my 8x4 6141 gets 200Mbps so I wanted Mom to have the best. Funny company using DOCSIS 3. X but still using BPI+ not AES.
Works wonderfully. Switches quickly and flawlessly with all of my extra devices.

My complaint isn't with this product; it's with the I-pad mini which I love, but which I find has sharp edges and this doesn't make them worse, but it doesn't keep them from being sharp either. It does, however, work very well with the I-pad mini cover (the official over priced one) and looks nice and protects my I-pad mini from being scratched or damaged. Very inexpensive and does what I wanted it to do.
Seems to work most of the time I've only had it for a short while and some times I have to play with the plug to get it to work and the pie shows the low power working every 10 minutes

Got this little frog drive for my kid's school projects and honestly didn't expect much for the price. Turns out it's actually a solid 8GB stick that works right out of the box on our old Windows laptop and my Mac. Transfer speeds are fine for documents and photos, no issues at all. The frog shape is ridiculously charming and it's surprisingly sturdy too, not some flimsy plastic that'll crack in a week. Comes with a small split ring so it clips neatly onto her backpack zipper. For less than what you'd spend on lunch, it's a total steal. Definitely grabbing another as a stocking stuffer.
I've been using it on my A65 for several months and really enjoyed it, as have all others viewing the pictures that come from it. Great for portraits of children.
Watch it in higher quality on my Youtube channel: skyforce95 *I received this product for review purposes and I promised to give a thorough and unbiased review.
I expected basic, but it actually handles all three devices way smoother than I thought.

Bought this hoping to fix the skipping in my old stereo, but it made zero difference. Ran it twice and the problem's still there. Total waste of money, honestly. Should've just saved up for a new unit.
Works exactly like I expected. Well built. I only wish it had knobs on the mounts instead of allen screws.
Does exactly what I needed, keeps my desk tidy and lets me power down peripherals I'm not using. Super handy with multiple drives and gadgets.

Before purchasing I was a little leary, both of the bright room concerns voiced earlier and of buying from an Amazon associated company, not Amazon itself. Purchasing a month ago, both fears were unfounded. Electronic Express was fine, shipping on Monday after a Friday order via FedEx Ground. The box was strapped vertically to a pallet and the driver helped removing it from the pallet and using a hydraulic platform to get it to the ground and into the house. Net result: no shipping nor delivery problems. The set worked perfectly upon arrival. As to the picture and operations, I have it on an HDMI connection from a DirecTV HD satellite connection. The black level is as astonishing as they advertise and the picture as clean and crisp as I observed earlier (at a Sears where they had this, the Sharp and the Sony side-by-side, with the Samsung as good as the Sony and much better than the Sharp). We are in Florida, where the sun is known to be particularly bright and this room has huge western sky facing windows. There is a little sun glare and reflection in the very late afternoon but all parts of the screen are still easily viewable and after a few minutes any annoyance from glare and reflection is tuned out by one's eyes. In sort, there's no problem here with the issue. Highly recommended for the price and overall quality. Addendum (December 2009): Well, a month after the warranty expired, so did this product. I had an authorized Samsung repair shop look at it and the LCD drivers had failed. Great, I thought, a simple circuit board change. WRONG! That circuit board was "married" to the display so the entire display needed to be replaced. $1500 plus labor! Short result: the Samsung was junked and a reliable Sony replacement purchased. Star rating reflects the unconscionable cost of repair of a somewhat simple problem.
Excellent antenna for my Cambridge Soundworks am/fm radio. Inexpensive and does the job. Fast shipping with prime from Amazon.

These speakers cause a performance issue in my PC. When they are even in the room performance drops down significantly and many applications will not even work. It fried one of my hard drives. Altec says nothing is wrong with them even though they are much farther then 3 feet away from the hard drives. Stay away from them at all costs.

I've had this case for about four months now, and while it's definitely tough, it shows wear faster than I expected. The rubber edges started peeling slightly near the charging port after daily use, and the clear back got scuffed up even though I'm careful. It's handled a couple drops onto hardwood floors without a scratch on the iPad itself, so no complaints on protection. But for something that's supposed to be heavy-duty, I thought it would hold up better cosmetically. It also adds a lot of bulk, which makes holding it for long reading sessions uncomfortable. Solid functionally, just not aging as gracefully as I'd hoped for the price.
Excellent little combo here for your camera. Batteries last a long time, seem to hold a charge. No complaints so far.

Got this for my daughter's dance recital season, over 900 shots and several long 4K clips later, the card's barely half full. Perfect for travel or anyone who never wants to worry about running out of space mid-shoot. Honestly, I'd tell any parent filming school events to grab one.
Perfect for multi-monitor setups, my remote work buddy should've grabbed these sooner.

Bought this because it was cheap, but you definitely get what you pay for. The storage itself works fine, but the build quality is a joke for something meant to live on a keychain. Within a week, the plastic casing started separating from the metal connector. For the price, I expected flimsy, but not something that literally falls apart with normal pocket wear. Not worth the few bucks saved.

The set costs less than just a ploarizer, and there are instructions on the internet on how to modify them easily to fit the z990. (Just use an emory board nail file to take off a tiny bit of the outside thread all the way around. Took 5 minutes and they work great.
Works great. Easy to install and set up. Good WIFI receiver. No trouble with "buffering" or signal receiving.

Just got mine delivered two days early via overnight shipping, which was a nice surprise. The packaging was really solid, double-boxed with dense foam inserts that held everything snug, no rattling or scuffs on the box itself. Pulling it out for the first time, the matte finish feels premium and the controls have a satisfying click. Setup was basically plug-and-play; I had it running in under five minutes. Image is crisp right out of the gate, and the auto-focus locks on fast even in mediocre lighting. Only reason I'm holding back a star is that the included strap feels flimsy for the price point, but that's a minor nitpick. Overall, super happy with the first impressions.

Before installing this hard drive, I was using a Western Digital 1TB (One Tera Bytes) drive. It took about 2 - 3 minutes to boot up! Now with this SSD from Samsung, it is 3 seconds! I also modified the Optical Drive with a Blue-Ray Recorder, so all my applications can be off loaded as a great storage device. I had to buy a Blue-Ray Player software to player Blu-Ray for Videos, but all this is my Christmas gift to me! Being a Retired Engineer in fun for me to play with the Apple. So I have max memory, Blu-Ray Recorder/Player, Roxio software and a Faster SSD on the market; well there is nothing else I can do with it. With multiple OS's using VMWARE, my Windows based Games Blazing fast and CAD software (AUTOCAD) computes faster then normal.
I purchased this set as a last minute purchase- the product is good. It chages quickly and is lighter than the Nikon brand I had been using. The car charger is great. Good product for the price.

Been using this for a few months on my detached garage and the spring inside is already feeling spongy. Sometimes it doesn't fully depress, so the sensor misses the closed state and I have to go out and jiggle it. When it works it's fine, but I expected it to hold up longer than half a year before getting sloppy.

I've had this mount up for a few months now and it's been rock solid with my 65-inch screen. Mounting process was smooth even doing it solo, and the extension range is great for reaching cables. Only small gripe is the tilt knobs feel a bit awkward to reach once the TV is on, but once set, it stays put. Overall, really happy.
It was a bit hard to fit an Apple's Iphone usb chager in the USB slot. I had to really push the connector in order to make it fit. Otherwise, everything is OK.

Perfect for my intended use. I did not purchase this for anything camera related but rather for my Nintendo 2DS with the Trigger Grip. It fits perfectly and even allows room in the other compartment for 3DS folding headphones and charger. A complete travel pack for my 2DS and accessories.

I grabbed this so I could use the microSD cards I already had in my old Olympus. It's been flawless, zero lag, easy to format. I'd recommend it to anyone with a vintage point-and-shoot who wants cheap, modern storage without hunting down pricey XD cards.
I loved the idea of this-that i could listen to my music in the car. it was easy to set up. but only works fairly. fades in and out frequently. also is easily broken.
Mostly ok, but I never found a connector tip that fit snugly in my Compaq laptop. In the end I used electrical tape to hold the tip in place.

The heart rate monitor is only designed to make good contact when it's wet. So I'm not able to wear it all day to monitor continuous HR. This was disappointing as this was the primary reason for ordering. Steps do not seem to be accurate.

We bought this TV two weeks ago. Ordered from Onecall via Amazon. TV arrived very quickly, in fact so quickly we had to postpone delivery so that we could be home to receive. Onecall delivers via a regional carrier which dropped it off in our garage. First impressions. TV screen is huge. The TV itself is fairly thin and weights just under 100 lbs. Although it might be obvious, don't buy this TV if you are going to be watching it from few feet away. Too close to the TV and regular cable will look fuzzy since the low resolution is magnified to a 58 inch screen. Amazing picture quality with Blu-ray without any optimization. Regular DVD and HD cable have an excellent picture as well. We are viewing it from about 12-14 feet which i think is optimal. No motion blur with action sequences. There isn't too much glare appreciable even a sunny room in our house. The viewing angles are excellent which is one of the reasons we choose plasma over LCD. The audio speakers are okay to good but you are better off with a surround sound system if you have it. The TV does have a faint buzzing sound that you can hear if you turn off the volume. It isn't annoying and I believe it goes away as the TV warms up. Overall, a great TV for a reasonable price ($2099 is the lowest recently offered.) If you are looking for a large plasma, check it out.

Highly rated by MaxPC, so I bought it, and works at stated speeds. I have a 6 year old RAID of 4 120gb SATA2 SSD's that still works faster, but the single drive is less hassle to set up with this less issues, and TRIM works automatically.

I wasn't expecting much for the price, but these totally surprised me. The sound is crisp, bass is decent, and they fit so snugly I forget I'm wearing them. I've used pricier buds that didn't feel this good. Took them on a 6-hour flight and no ear fatigue at all. The mic clarity during calls is a nice bonus too. Solid value.
This cable arrived DOA. It doesn't appear damaged in any way, but it definitely doesn't work. I tested it on four different computers and two different monitors, and the monitors never got a signal.

For anyone fed up with cable bills, this little antenna is a steal. I set one up in the attic for my dad and he's pulling in all the major networks with zero fuss. Honestly, I'd recommend it to any DIYer who wants free, clean TV without overcomplicating things.

I picked up the M-Edge cover in navy after seeing so many positive mentions, and for the price, it's a decent buy. The materials feel sturdy and the interior lining is soft, so my Kindle isn't getting scratched up, which is really all I needed. The elastic corner straps hold everything securely, no slipping. My only real gripe is the exterior fabric attracts lint and dust like crazy, so it constantly looks a bit grimy. Also, the front cover doesn't lay totally flat at first, took a few days of use to relax. For what I paid, the protection is solid, but the fussy upkeep and break-in period keep it from being a slam dunk. It's fine, just not exceptional.

I've had this antenna out on dusty trails and through a couple of heavy rainstorms over the last six months. Still looks and performs like new, no rust or cracking on the mast. The SWR stayed rock solid. Only knock is the included manual is pretty useless, so budget a few extra minutes to find the right guide online before you start.
Got about a month out of mine before the port went loose and it became a paperweight. Really wanted to like it but can't recommend something that just quits.

Recently we upgraded to this cable modem in order to satisfy Suddenlink's requirements for their unlimited internet service. The modem was relatively easy to set up and has performed as expected. We have a couple of laptops, two i-Phones, and two Roku receivers that access content through the modem. This is our second SURFboard modem and we have been happy with both.
Solid well made stand - Bought another one for my wife.

I grabbed this for my home theater setup and the clarity is seriously impressive. No signal loss even on longer runs. If you're piecing together a sound system on a budget, I'd tell any friend to start here.

This is an extremely inexpensive solution for that smaller TV. We needed to get our kitchen TV off the counter and this does the trick. It was relatively easy to mount, I'm pretty handy, using a drill, socket wrench and level. If you don't know what those are then you will have problems getting this up on a wall. Don't skimp on the screws or hardware, you don't want this falling on someone. Mine is mounted into a stud and I can hang on the bracket (150lbs). The only issue I have is that it doesn't fold quite as flat to the wall as I had hoped, so if space is an issue then you may want a different mount. It does, however, swing and rotate exactly where we would like it.

My 25 year old tripod broke, so I purchased this. Actually, I liked it so much I purchased two of them and gave one to my father. This is much nicer than my previous tripod. The tripod has a very "finished" look to it and appears to be a very quality product. The design of it makes it very flexible allowing you to position your camera just about any way you can immagine. It comes with a nice carry bag, its fairly light weight - without that cheap feeling to it, and it is very compact when fully collapsed. I have not experienced any problems while using the tripod. It is stable and there is no creep in the head when the camera is mount in any position. I believe for many photographers this tripod would be more than suited for them. While it looks like a well made tool, I'm not sure it would take much in the way of any abuse. If you need something rugged, this may not be what you are looking for. Overall, I'm really happy with this product and would recommend it.

I grabbed these hoping for decent sound, and they blew me away. The clarity and bass balance are way better than I figured at this price. Super comfortable for long listening sessions, and the build feels solid. Honestly didn't expect them to be this good, definitely a pleasant surprise.
This DOCSIS 3.0 high-speed modem is a great match for our 25Mbps cable and gigabit network.
For the price, this thing is a steal. Feels way sturdier than expected and the battery lasts all day. Only knocking a star because the screen could be a touch brighter outside.
Yup, it's a light meter. Cheap and works great. A workhorse for my lab. Intensity range seems pretty good for normal use.

I was on the fence about dropping this much cash on a laptop, but after three weeks of heavy daily use I can honestly say it's worth every penny. The build quality is rock solid, the keyboard feels great, and the trackpad is still the best I've ever used. Battery life consistently pushes 10, 11 hours with my workload, which is mostly browser tabs, docs, and some light photo editing. Performance has been smooth with zero hiccups, even when I push it. I looked at cheaper Windows alternatives, but factoring in longevity and resale value, this thing is actually a smart deal. If you want something that just works and holds its value, it's a no-brainer.

For the price, I expected something flimsy, but these sound way better than my old $60 set. The cord control is super handy for quick adjustments, and the LED is barely noticeable. Honestly blown away by the value here.
This thing runs dead silent even under heavy load, and my CPU temps dropped by a solid 10 degrees.
The battery life is decent for the price, but the screen scratches way too easily even with light use by my kid.
I expected a bigger jump in clarity for the price, but it gets the job done on my older TV. Streaming is smooth and setup took maybe two minutes. Solid little device overall.

Cable showed up in a slim, sturdy box with no wasted plastic. Unspooled it and the connectors felt solid right away. Plugged into my setup without a hiccup and the picture came through crisp immediately. Really pleased with how quickly it arrived and how clean the initial experience was.

I wanted to like these for the gym, but the bass is just nonexistent. Tried tweaking my phone's equalizer for days and still ended up with flat, tinny audio that makes podcasts sound hollow. They connect fast and the battery holds up fine, but what's the point if everything you listen to feels so lifeless? Really disappointed.

If you want loud clear clean sound these speakers will shred your hearing. They are amazing and don't cost a lot. They look great in my car and blow my head off. Very happy great job Rockford!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I wanted to use it with my TV. Basically use a Bluetooth headphone with a TV. Once you connect to a bluetooth device you can hear this background humming sound. I'm very sure that its from this device and not the bluetooth headphones. I use the headphones very regularly. The humming sounds I suppose can be ignored because once the sound starts playing you don't hear it. But then there is a delay in the sound. I think it's about half a second (or maybe 300ms) which is pretty bad. Bad enough that you can't really watch TV like that. Considering the quality of sound, the delay and the background humming, its not worth the price. I haven't tried other products so I don't know if this is a standard problem with all Bluetooth Tx.

I grabbed these on a whim during a sale, and for what I paid, I'm honestly impressed. The build feels solid and the cable doesn't tangle into a nightmare in my pocket, which is a small but real win. Sound-wise, there's definitely a generous low end that makes electronic and hip-hop tracks thump in a fun way, though it can crowd the mids a bit on busier rock songs. I dialed things in with a simple EQ app on my phone and it cleaned up nicely. Isolation is decent for the price bracket, and they're comfortable enough for a couple hours straight. If you want a budget set that's energetic and doesn't feel disposable, these are a solid pickup.

I've been through a few monitors trying to find one that feels right for both my editing work and casual use, and this one genuinely surprised me. I expected decent color, but the vibrancy and clarity right out of the box were a step above what I'd braced for. Text is crisp, and the glossy screen makes images pop without feeling like a mirror. I did tweak the brightness down a notch for long sessions, but that's just personal comfort. The stand is sturdy with good height adjustment, which I didn't think I'd care about but now love. It's handled everything from photo culling to watching movies without a hiccup. For the price, it's been a rock-solid upgrade that exceeded my expectations.
Bigbox store wanted 4 times as much for a shorter cable, use it daily plug/ unplug, roll it up throw it in my laptop case, don't treat it very well and it still works!
Been using this daily for months now and it still looks and works like new.

Snagged this for my dash cam after the old card crapped out, and it's been flawless. Popped it in, formatted instantly, and it handles continuous HD recording without a hiccup. No stuttering or corrupted files so far. For the price, the speed and reliability are honestly surprising. Great little storage upgrade.

Excellent product!!! Much More than a Camera!! It's a Tablet/Camera!! it can be a phone too!! Free Magic jack download.................. I don't think the other reviewers have given justice to this Galaxy Camera. The screen is so amazing and beautiful enough to get lost in a full length movie. Watching your Camera... Fast as hell, 4 Quad core means no skip.. Full internet access with touch.(the whole world on your camera touch high definition screen!! for free..) (with a wifi connection..) Pictures are beautiful Volume is Loud and Clear Screen is dreamy Interface is great Etc..........................
So far for the price, my wife and I love these Bluetooth. Very easy to use, and no trouble with the ears hurting from the ear hook. Thank you.

Got this unit after my old high-end detector finally quit, and honestly I was bracing for a downgrade. Couldn't be more wrong. For the price, the range and sensitivity are shockingly good, picks up Ka band way before I even see the source. Build feels solid, mount is simple, and the display is easy to read at a glance. I drive a mix of highway and rural roads daily and this thing has already paid for itself several times over. If you want premium detection without burning a hole in your wallet, just grab this. Zero regrets.

I put 2 of these batteries through one full charge and discharge cycle as soon as I received them. On heavy and extended usage, they last at least as long as the Sony original and correctly report the number of minutes remaining.

I read some negative remarks on the pack but thought it was a good choice considering it had over 200 positive remarks. For $48.00 I couldn't have found a better bag. It has plenty of room and the quality seems to be pretty tough. It's not a 250.00 tamrac but for the money I would say it's a better buy.

Great price, fast shipping, good quality replacement Apple style cord. I'm not sure if this will work for "all' Apple Chargers do to a small circuit board inside transmitting specific charger information. If its circuit board and YOUR Charge doesn't match then this replacement may not work properly. You might try contacting this seller to verify if it will work for your situation. I may have gotten lucky.
This little gadget punches way above its weight. Setup took seconds, and the sound quality is crisp and full. I'm genuinely impressed with how smooth everything runs. Totally worth grabbing one.

Decent enough sound for the price I paid, but man, these things just didn't hold up. First pair died in the left earbud after about a month of light use, just walking around campus. Got a replacement through warranty, which was smooth, but that second set lasted even less time. Audio started crackling and then nothing. I'm not rough on my gear at all, so it's clearly a build quality issue. Frustrating because when they worked, they were comfy and sounded okay. Just can't recommend something so unreliable, even at a budget price.

This camera has been termed "entry level" far and wide. So be it. I find it to be a wonderful camera for what I needed: A camera that had fast, crisp autofocus and could take shots at a speed faster than every 3 or 4 seconds (which seems to be the typical recovery/ready time for most point and shoots in my experience). Plus, I am rediscovering the joy of artistic photography with this camera. I'll let the professionals argue over the value and performance for them, but for me, this is a winner!

I bought this for my Samsung tablet expecting an okay fit, but it's honestly way better than I hoped. The material feels sturdy without being bulky, and all the cutouts line up perfectly. Snaps on easily and stays put. For the price I was a little skeptical, but it's a solid case that actually delivers.
very well made product that works good and made well, more than I was expecting,a very high rated A+
Ran these through several race days now, no signal loss or cracks, solid little connectors.
Perfect for anyone needing a quick, reliable mouse on the go.
This was really a waste of money. The old rabbit ears and converter worked better than this did. Not happy.
I've dropped this thing twice on concrete and it still cranks out rich, room-filling sound for my weekend jam sessions.

Bought this for my son who is third grade. He uses it with supervision and is able to navigate on his own for his homework and research. It's versatile and he can do any work that he needs. Paired with the key board, he is pleased. Performance is good enough for him.
Perfect for anyone needing a reliable controller upgrade, my nephew loves it for his setup.
This cable does not work as an Oculus HDMI headset extension cable. The headset flickered. I ended up having to use a different cable.
Perfect for storing movies or as a backup drive for your parents' computer. Not blazing speeds, but gets the job done without fuss.
Stopped working after 2 weeks. The company had tech support call and they fixed it. I was impressed. They care for the customers!!!!!!!

I grabbed this little radio mostly for camping trips and wasn't expecting much at this price point, but it's honestly exceeded every expectation. Reception pulls in stations I usually can't get out in the woods, and the speaker is surprisingly clear for its size. The real standout though is how long it runs on a couple of basic AAs, I've gone entire weekends without touching the spare batteries. It's lightweight, simple to use, and feels sturdier than radios twice the cost. For the money, I don't think you'll find a better portable option.

It's fine if you just need a stand and a keyboard for light typing at a desk. The keys have a decent feel, but the whole thing is bulkier than I expected. I'd only suggest it for someone using a 12.9-inch iPad in one safe spot, like a home office. Definitely not for travel or kids, the tablet's back is totally exposed, so one drop and you're in trouble.

Picked this up hoping I could finally catch the game audio at my noisy neighborhood pub without having to beg the bartender to switch channels. Setup was pretty painless, just point your phone at the screen and it usually syncs within a few seconds. When it works, the sound through my earbuds is clean and surprisingly in-sync with the action. My only gripe is that it can be finicky depending on the TV angle and lighting, sometimes I have to scan a few times before it locks on. Not quite the flawless experience I imagined, but for crowded bars where you can't hear a thing, it's a solid workaround. Definitely beats reading captions.
Kind of chunky, but great protection, great looking, and extremely functional. I highly recommend it.
Works really good. Keep the usb 3.0 fast meaning it is real usb 3.0 cable.
Great fit & excellent sound. Would prefer thicker, more durable sound cable.

Works amazing! This is the most inexpensive electronic technology I think I have ever bought and yet the best results. my house is large (twice the average home) and I only had coverage within one part of the house. after installing this unit I have a strong signal around my entire house and all the way out around the perimeter of the house in a 3/4 acre lot. another feature worth mentioning is that when you move it and replace it at a different outlet or a power outage occurs it resets automatically within less than 2 minutes.

I stuck this thing to my window frame and now I'm pulling in 22 channels, most of them crystal clear. It took a few tries to get the placement right, but once it locked in I was honestly shocked. For the price, it's a no-brainer.

I've had these for over 2 months now any they work very well. They are plastic so you need to use a little care when inserting the SSD. I've putting SSDs in and out many times and have not had any issues. SSD must be pushed all the way in (sometimes you think it's in but misses by 1/8". When installing, be sure to tighten screws as tight as possible without breaking the plastic. I find this much more reliable the pull open door types that you push on the end to get SSD to release (I've broken 2 of the door type because pushing broke the plastic lever). I would purchase some extra trays if you have a number of SSDs to swap out.

For the money, this bag is a steal. The padding is decent, the zippers feel solid, and it holds my 14-inch laptop perfectly with room for a tablet and charger. It's not luxury, but it looks clean and gets the job done for daily commutes. Hard to beat at this price point.

Worked with no problems to replace the DVD burner in my Windows PC that had gone belly up. I wish it had been available with a white faceplate to match the PC case, but no big deal (in the old days, almost all drives were only available with black faceplates, so I have been here before).
I use one of the u3 hv boards as the cornerstone of my Home automation system. http://www. instructables.
Great little speaker! Connects right to USB port, not bulky! Nice to have while just lounging in bed or on couch and need a little more sound! Excellent sound quality!!

These earbuds have survived getting yanked, dropped, and stuffed in my pocket for months. Still sound crisp with solid bass. The build holds up way better than I expected for the price. No fraying, no cutting out. I'm genuinely impressed they've lasted this long.

I grabbed this expecting a quick plug-and-play setup, but my desktop needed a bit of driver fiddling before it cooperated, took about ten minutes of googling. Once sorted, the performance was actually smoother than I anticipated. On my tablet it connected instantly with zero hassle. Not the seamless experience I'd imagined, but the end result is solid enough that I'm still pretty happy with it.
Wasn't expecting much for the price, but this fits my old Fire HD 7 like a glove and feels way sturdier than I imagined.

I needed to replace a flakey Linksys Wireless Access Point and Router combination, and this one works very well. I'm just using the stock configuration, with one computer on the switch side and another computer plus a home theatre unit on the wireless side. Both wireless units are using N cards, and are getting good throughput. This unit has features I haven't used yet, like the 2 USB ports with NTFS support and built-in print server. DHCP is easily configurable and the web interface is quick. Encryption setup was simple. I don't have any CONs in this review because I haven't found any in the 2 weeks I've used it.

I have had the standard charger for three years. It works. It also works in Europe with a plug adapter. No transformer needed. This tip does not work on the iPad. It fits perfectly but only works on the iPods and a few phones. Check the other reviews. iPad requires another transformer. Be careful.
Feels premium and performs like it, totally worth every cent.

Couldn't pass up the price for a spare cable, and it feels just as sturdy as the one that came with my phone. Charges fast, fits snug, and I've had zero connection drops. Honestly wasn't expecting much, but it's held up perfectly for weeks now. Total steal for the cost, grabbed a second one for my car.

These are packaged a bit weird, can be a little bit annoying trying to get these in and out of the container they come in. If you intend to store these elsewhere then there's no problem. I believe I saw these cheaper at HomeDepot and Walmart. But these are still OK priced.
For the cost, this thing works way better than I expected. Not perfect, but definitely solid for everyday use.

After moving to a new apartment, my radio reception was really poor, so I purchased the Terk FM+ indoor antenna. While installing it, I found two things: 1) my existing (basic wire) antenna was dramatically improved by wiggling/adjusting where it connects to the stereo; 2) this Terk antenna created regular bursts of static which I found intolerable. So, I'm not using it and do not recommend it.

I've had this TV for a few weeks now and overall it's been a solid addition to our bedroom. The picture quality is crisp and the smart features load quickly without any lag. Setup was straightforward and the slim design looks clean on the wall. My only real gripe is the built-in speakers, they get the job done for casual watching but lack depth and fullness, especially for movies. I ended up adding a compact soundbar and now the experience feels complete. For the price, it's hard to beat the value, just plan on external audio if you care about sound.

I really liked this bag the first two times I used it. Plenty of room, comfortable, and seemed pretty secure. However the second time I took it through the airport, one of the front hooks came off. Couldnt find it. Turns out that they're not sewn on. Theyre just hooked on with two small hooks. Im sitting at the airport halfway through my fourth trip and it seems that Ive lost the other one. Too bad. If the hooks were sewn on this would be a very happy five star review.

Ordered this hoping for a decent budget charger but it barely lasted two weeks. For the price, I expected at least a few months of use. Now it won't power on at all. Total waste of money, cheap components, zero longevity.

I bought this item so I could hook it up to my camcorder to get the audio from the convention on FM and eliminate the crowd noise (Babies crying, high heel noise, etc.) It works perfectly. I bought two of them so I could monitor the recording and still be mobile.

Bluetooth connection is spotty and cuts out intermittently, especially when walking or even stopped at an interception waiting for the crosswalk, it can completely cut out for up to twenty seconds. Phone is in my pocket the whole time, not more than 2ft away from the device. Not sure if it's other devices interfering with it, or if the Bluetooth is constantly wasting itself trying to connect to nearby devices even when it's already connected to yours, but whatever the case,the Bluetooth connection is horrible.

The device stopped functioning after the first use. Changed battery a few times but would not power on or record audio when attached to the camera. The build felt cheap and I regret not saving a little and going with something a bit more expensive with higher quality.
So glad I grabbed these. The extra batteries are a lifesaver during our frequent power cuts here in Manila, and the charger is super reliable. Totally worth it.

It's great as a mobile projector for presentations and such. The little tripod is a very nice touch and allows for nearly infinite angle adjustments. That said, don't fool yourself into trying to use it as a tiny home theater projector like I did, though. The native resolution is a disappointing 480p, and the digital upscaling looks very grainy, at least with a PS4 driving it. Also, the focus wheel is easily moved, even by vibrations from being mounted on the same wall as the speakers, so it loses focus pretty easily. All in all, I'll probably be getting another projector for the media room and using this one for presentations and such when I'm on the road.

I grabbed this case for my S700 because I didn't want to spend a ton, and for the price it's a solid little find. The material feels decent enough for everyday use, nothing premium, but it won't fall apart on you. I did have to yank out the divider to fit the camera properly, which was a minor annoyance, but once that's gone there's enough space for the camera plus an extra battery. The front zippered pocket is handy for a memory card, though it's pretty snug. The inner mesh pouch keeps small stuff from rattling around. Overall, it's basic but gets the job done without breaking the bank.
For the price, this thing is a steal. Setup took maybe two minutes and it's been flawless since. Honestly expected way less for under twenty bucks but it delivers.
Works just fine. I have it running from my 4K TV to my laptop to play games on it and haven't experienced any issues.

Bought this camera to replace the Canon Powershot SD990IS but it turned out to be a mistake This camera is smaller but difficult to hold and pictures are not that much better. I like the wireless feature, software is hard to figure out and the 360 has no viewfinder so it is difficult to frame the picture in sunlight. The 110 VAC prongs on the charger do not lock in place so you must fiddle to plug it in. Also, the lock on the battery has a bad feel and you have to manually mess with it to lock
Bought these thinking they'd handle gaming, but anyone needing stable internet should skip them entirely.
Exactly what I needed for my loose disc collection. Sturdy enough, super affordable, and arrived quickly.

My old rig was wheezing just trying to play 720p clips, let alone handle any light editing. Snagged this card hoping for a cheap miracle and honestly, it delivered way more than I expected. Installation was a breeze, drivers picked up immediately, and the difference was night and day. I can finally scrub through timelines smoothly and playback 4K footage without it looking like a slideshow. Even my ancient power supply handles it without breaking a sweat. For the price, it's completely revived this machine and saved me from a full rebuild. Solid performance, zero headaches, couldn't be happier.

I wasn't expecting much at this price point, but the build quality blew me away. The padding is thick without being bulky, and my 15-inch laptop slides in snugly. Zippers glide effortlessly and the layout is smart, just enough organization without overcomplicating things. Easily the best laptop bag I've owned.
Does exactly what I need without costing a fortune. Solid build for the price, no complaints so far.

Very nice radio, except I can't see it during the day time. The glare is unbelievable and it's so dim it can hardly be seen. At night it's great, if not a little too bright. The dim adjustments are not adequate for day/night. Other than that it's a great sounding radio with plenty of features.
The SD card reader works well with my smartphone. I primarily use it to read the SD card in my Dashcam.

I wanted to like this because it's super compact and doesn't block the other outlet, which is rare. But after three weeks it started making a faint buzzing sound, and now my laptop only charges if I jam the plug in at a weird angle. Tried different outlets, same issue. It's just flimsy where the cord meets the brick. Cute size, terrible durability.

I'll admit I was a little unsure about the 35mm equivalent on my X-T5 at first, but this lens completely shattered my expectations. Sharpness wide open is startling, corner-to-corner detail I didn't think was possible without stopping down. The way it renders out-of-focus areas feels creamy but never artificial, and I can actually place focus exactly where I want without fighting the glass. Build surprised me too: solid metal barrel, compact enough to forget it's in my bag, and the aperture ring clicks are deeply satisfying. Compared to the kit zoom I started with, the character and consistency here are on another level. Honestly, it's made me rethink how much I reach for longer focal lengths.

I really wanted to love this for my desk setup, but after about three weeks of light use, it started falling apart on me. The casing near the cord feels flimsy and already has a visible crack, even though I've never dropped it or yanked the cord.
