The dialogue enhancement mode is the real star here. Voices are way clearer, less strain, even at lower volumes. It won't shake the room with bass, but that's not what we needed. I'd recommend this to anyone with mild to moderate hearing issues, or just folks tired of constantly riding the volume button during movies. Solid upgrade for the price.
This was a gift to my husband who is hard of hearing. He loves it.

The box showed up looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, corners crushed and the plastic window scuffed. Inside, the mount itself was just rattling around with no foam or padding, which didn't exactly inspire confidence. First impression out of the package is that it feels sturdy enough, and the low-profile look is clean, but the clamp mechanism already seemed a bit finicky just messing with it at my desk. The rubber shims feel thinner than I expected, so I'm skeptical about grip on anything but a perfectly round bar. Haven't had a chance to test it on a real ride yet, but between the sloppy packaging and the so-so initial quality feel, I'm cautiously optimistic at best.
Surprisingly solid for the price, exceeded my expectations completely.

I've had the DNS-325 running 24/7 for nearly three years now with two 3TB Seagate drives in RAID 1, and it hasn't missed a beat. Even through a couple of long power outages last summer, it came back online without any hiccups or data corruption. The little fan does its job without getting whiny, and I honestly forget it's sitting on my desk. Dust buildup has been minimal thanks to the compact case design. I mostly use it for nightly backups and streaming movies to our TV, and throughput has stayed rock solid. For a budget two-bay NAS, the longevity really surprised me. Can't complain at all.

Arrived two days early in a compact, no-fuss box with just enough padding. The card itself feels sturdy, not flimsy like some budget adapters I've tried. Popped it into my older desktop and the indicator light came right on. Setup was straightforward, though I had to reposition my tower once to get a stronger signal. Overall, very happy for the price.

The box showed up looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, and inside, the thing was just loose with zero padding. Pulled it out, already had a scuff on top. Powered it on and honestly felt cheap right away, flimsy, no heft. Not a great start.
Perfect for anyone needing extra reach behind the TV.

I have been a PC user since I was introduced to computers, many years ago. I always wanted to try a Mac computer, but the prices of them always kept me from buying one. After all what if I got it and paid that much for one, and then it didn't live up to my expectations? It came in good time, about a week after I ordered it. I used the free shipping that Amazon.com offered. It came UPS, but had a very nice feature where I could track the package online and know where it was. As far as the MacBook itself I had a few worries, the screen size only being 13" inches was one of them. Needless to say I love the size of it! You do not notice that it is 13" inches because it is actually better than having a huge screen! The screen is perfectly sized! The performance of the product is amazing! Fast, responsive, easy to get used to the operating system, and beautiful! If you have the money to get one of these and are considering one, get it, you won't regret it!
Works as advertised. Great speed for taking videos with Nikon DSLR.

Picked this up for my dual-monitor work-from-home setup after my old passive adapter kept flickering. Plugged it in and instantly got a clean, stable signal on my second screen. If you're running an AMD card and need reliable DisplayPort-to-DVI conversion, I'd absolutely recommend this to anyone.
Perfect for podcasting, the splitter lets me run two mics cleanly with zero static or crosstalk.
Nothing to add to this. It is no different than the original power adapter and it works. There is nothing new to it.

I picked up the SR5008 for our living room setup and honestly couldn't be happier. Setup was straightforward, on-screen menus actually make sense, which is rare. Running a 5.1 configuration with some older Polk speakers and a modest sub, and the room calibration dialed everything in perfectly. Dialogue is crisp, music sounds warm without being muddy, and there's plenty of headroom for movie nights. Streaming from my phone over Bluetooth has been rock solid, no drops or lag. I also appreciate how cool it runs after hours of use. For the price, the build quality and feature set feel like a steal. It just quietly does its job and makes everything sound better.
Arrived a day early, but the box was pretty bare-bones, just the card in an antistatic bag. No manual, which threw me at first. Popped it in and it fired right up though. Solid first impression.

I was honestly shocked at how well these were packaged, double-boxed with custom foam inserts, zero chance of shipping damage. They arrived two days early and looked pristine right out of the box. First impression plugging them in: rich, clear audio that instantly filled my desk setup. No messing with drivers or external power bricks, just simple, clean sound. Super happy.

I purchased this and the case directly from eTauro as it was out of stock on Amazon at the time. S/H was FREE and sent Priority Mail. It' super easy to use and install. I like that it's small and fits on the side rather than the clunky back version from Microsoft. It adds a LOT of functionality to the SP4 when I'm using it as a lap top. I can easily remove the unit when I want to use it as a tablet for drawing. I also like that I was able to get a CASE that fit rather than buying from a 3rd party and hoping that it would.

Excellent sub. Plenty of power and distortion doesn't seem to be a problem for this thing. It hits the low end very well. Very clean sound. Easy to hook up. Well made, if rather large. I didn't think it would be as big is it is. Looks nice. I would definitely recommend it to anyone.

Sadly, I have tried this model again (for the 2nd time) and it still proved problematic for me. As for playing content, I agree it plays a vast number of differing file formats. But for me I encountered too many issues with this model to be a reliable solution or replacement of my older versions. First, I want to say I own two older models (WDBAAN0000NBK) for several years and love it. My only negative is I am unable to configure them to indicate newly added content (not sure if this is doable). Last year I tried a refurb version of the WDBYMN0000NBK-HESN and more recently purchased and returned a brand new unit. Both times I experience similar problems. Frequent glitches such as freeze-ups, audio mis-syncs and the inability to display multiple NAS devices simultaneously (to change NAS you need to go through several nested steps). I do not have any of these issues with the older models. To a lesser extent I too am not excited about the GUI as it appears to be trying too hard to be good looking more than functional. I really hoped for this newer version to be more satisfying experience than the previous generations but for me that is not the case.

I've had this media player for about five months now. The format support is solid, no argument there, it chewed through everything I threw at it without a hiccup. My frustration is more about how it's held up over time. After a couple months of regular use, the remote started getting finicky, and now I have to press buttons multiple times to get a response. The unit itself runs noticeably warmer than I'd like after an hour or two of playback, which makes me nervous about long-term reliability. The plastic casing also picked up scuffs way easier than expected, just from moving it between rooms. It still works, but the little durability issues keep adding up, and I'm not sure I'd trust it as a daily driver for much longer.

I've been using this dock daily for about six months now, and it's held up really well. No loose connections, no flickering on my external monitor, and the finish still looks clean even with constant plugging and unplugging. I toss it in my laptop bag a couple times a week when I work from home, and the metal casing hasn't picked up any dents or scratches, feels solid. Only reason I'm knocking off a star is the attached USB-C host cable is a bit short for how I like to position things on my desk, so I had to rearrange my setup a little. Not a dealbreaker, just mildly annoying. Performance-wise though, zero complaints after months of heavy use.

Works nice for storing my several SD cards for my DSLR and Camcorder - and helps to keep them separate. More space/storage than what I need but works well. And, it's got enough bulk to not get "lost" in my camera bag.
Picks up stations I couldn't get before, even in my basement. Super easy to install.

This Optoma projector died after 2 years. I've been replacing 2 lamp bulbs in a year and now the color wheel went bad. The noise of this projector is annoying and horrible. Don't waste your money on this projector.

These earbuds deliver solid, energetic sound once you get a secure fit, but the stock tips are a letdown. I'd only suggest them for casual listeners in dry environments, definitely not for gym-goers. If you mainly listen at a desk and don't mind swapping tips, they're a decent value.
This is super quiet, works great keeps my laptop cool. The blue light it has makes it look great, it is also helpful to see the keys in the dark.

Nothing special here. Good inexpensive headphones that are comfortable on your ears and head. These phones fit on top of your ears using foam covers. The fit is not too tight and yet do not fall off the head easily. Of course I am not exercising with these phones so maybe someone else can comment on their ability to stay on the head during a workout. A nice convenience is that the ear phone cups rotate flat if you would like to compact them or hang them around your neck when not in use. For the money, less than $12.00, they are adequate and if you walk on them or the dog eats them not much of a loss. Not a bad replacement for ear-buds if you prefer a descent set of portable headphones. On notice: a lot of ear-buds will actually reduce exterior noise better than these cups.
Perfect!!! Just what we needed for our camper, the old burned out. If we had taken it to the RV place they quoted us $230.00 PLUS Labor to install it... This price felt like a steal~~~

I've had my SG3 for close to six years now and it just keeps chugging along. Took it out last weekend after it sat in the bag all winter and it fired right up, locked onto satellites in under two minutes, and gave me distances I could trust. The screen's held up great, no fading or dead spots, and the little joystick still moves smoothly. It's taken a few accidental drops on cart paths and one dunk in a pond, and somehow still works perfectly. It doesn't do slope or fancy color maps, but for pure yardages to hazards, greens, and pins, it's dead reliable. Honestly don't see a reason to upgrade anytime soon.

The 2 gig version of this HD 6450 was only $10 more than the 1 gig version. A deal! I do not game, so I have no need for a super OC card. I do use CPU hogging music software and I just want a graphics card that can stay of the busses as much as possible and run without any CPU support. Also, a fan is a big no-no. There was some driver problems with the Fuel. Service.exe file, which seems to be used only when the card is in Turbo-mode. I turned off the Turbo and stopped the Fuel file from running with MSConfig and that seemed to work. I just installed newly available Catalyst driver/software. Will see what happens next... I haven't had to turn off the Fuel... yet. AMD's software is very well conceived and easy to use, with one exception: I am using the HDMI port at times for viewing HULU on my LCD-TV as well as dual displays. There was adequate info on how to set up a dual display system, but absolutely nothing on how to use the HDMI port. Clue: Detect the HDMI first, then set up a preset. After you get all this figured out, you will see the beauty of having many presets to suit any need. The software is quite versatile. Just run the software, select a preset, and voila! New and varied display/graphics setups with different characteristics and functions to suit your software needs. My last card was an NVIDIA based PNY. It was defective, registered, under warranty, and PNY did not want to give me a new card without my sending them the defective one first, meaning BIG downtime for me (shame on them... I'll never buy another PNY product again). I bought this XFX 6450 instead and I am glad I did. It is without question a much better card. Well worth the $$!! Buy one!!

This little display is a total game changer for my portable retropie setup. The touch response is surprisingly accurate, and brightness levels are perfect even in daylight. I especially love how it draws power straight from the Pi's GPIO header, so I'm not juggling extra cables. Super clean build, zero headaches.

Arrived two days early in a sturdy little box with molded foam inside, no plastic rattling around. Took it out and immediately noticed the aluminum finish matches my MacBook perfectly. Plugged it in, no setup at all, just worked. Even the blue LED is subtle. Feels premium and solid, not like cheap dongles I've tried before.
For what I paid I expected a mouse that works past arm's length, not a desk ornament.
Handy little reader for the price. Works fine once I figured out the right orientation. Not the fastest, but totally worth it for occasional photo transfers. Solid budget buy.

I picked this up hoping it would be a simple fix for my flat screen's thin, lifeless audio, and for the most part it delivers. Voices are finally clear without me riding the volume button, and dialogue-heavy shows are way more enjoyable now. It's surprisingly wide for something labeled "compact", make sure you measure your setup first. I gave it four stars because while it handles mids and highs nicely, the bass doesn't quite match what I imagined from the price tag. It's there, just not room-filling. Setup was dead easy though, and it looks clean under the TV. Overall, a solid upgrade if you go in with realistic expectations.
I'm honestly impressed by how well the built-in stand holds up on uneven surfaces like my couch arm or bed. No more awkward propping, just steady hands-free reading every time.
Completely useless. Hooked it up to my laptop and the screen just stayed black no matter what I tried. Total waste of money and now I'm stuck running an extra errand to return it.
Fits great and feels solid, just a little bulkier than I expected.

this little camera takes great underwater pictures, so clear...even in the ocean off Florida. Takes great regular pictures as well. Daughter loves that it is water proof and shock proof. Its the perfect size. Not too big and not to small. perfect. works well for her without breaking the bank
Connections are light duty and can break as mine did. It should come with a power supply as the connector is metric and was a little difficult to find a mate that fit.
My 6 year daughter has been begging me for this for a while!

Really a great projector but does not appear to be HDCP compatible. For home theater purposes, it didn't handshake with a receiver' s. Main HDMI output. It worked wonderfully with individual HDMI feeds plugged into it, has a great picture, bright clean, and the speakers even were loud enogh for covering a small room well. Contacted Benq and Pioneer about the issues. Both said it was a fluke and Benq suggested an MH 630, which I have swapped to try. Hopefully will handle this.

It fits my camera like a glove, it is not real leather which I like. I love the light brown color and how protective it is. The only thing is that when I carry around my neck or shoulder, I keep the cover off, completely off because it won't just hang, it will fall off, so I keep cover in my purse, and then just carry camera with the main attachment all day when using it. I wish the cover didn't just fall off...but having the camera so well attached to the main body is super convenient you just pull up point and shoot and let hang again, it won't fall....I get a lot of compliments
This thing's taken a few knocks in my gear bag and still runs rock solid.

I keep one of these loaded with a 32 GB in my pocket, attached to my key chain at all times. It's one of those things that's invaluable when you just need to pass a few files around easily. No cap to get lose, and the lanyard is perfect for the key ring so it never gets lose. Can swap in more capacity easily when needed. Always recognized by any PC I've tried it in.
Still going strong after six months of daily use, not a single issue.

Picked this up to finally run my three-monitor setup without glitches, and it's been flawless. Install was a breeze, no driver headaches, and everything feels snappy now. For the price, I honestly didn't expect it to handle my daily workload this smoothly. Really happy with it.
Amazon basics is the next best thing to apple products. And it even beats apple in price. I would highly recommend this as a reliable alternative or extra cable.
Still going strong after dozens of karaoke nights, no hiccups at all.

For under $20 I wasn't expecting much, but this thing is solid. Mounted my 40" TCL without any drama once I got the arm tension dialed in. Stays put exactly where I position it, no sagging over time. The swivel and extension make a huge difference in my narrow living room. Honestly feels way more expensive than it is.
Can't give a true feed back for this as got it for my son, but he loves loves it updated for an older version he had a long time and it never let him down
For the money, it's a steal. Clean edges every time, no fraying. A hot knife works best, totally transforms the finish.

I've been using this little TomTom for a couple of weeks now, mainly for city driving, and the quick GPS lock-on time is what really sold me. It grabs a signal almost instantly after powering up, way faster than my old Garmin ever did. The mount is super simple to clip in and out with one hand, and it stays put even on bumpy roads. I was a bit worried about the smaller screen at first, but the clarity makes up for it, directions are crisp and easy to glance at without squinting. Voice prompts cut through road noise fine too. For a budget navigator, the route recalculation is snappy when I miss a turn. Honestly, it's a solid little workhorse that just does its job without fuss.

This is horrible. I bought it to use with a Blu-Ray player I have. I hadn't gotten around to using it until now (past the returnable date) and discovered it won't work. The blu ray drive slipped in no problem, but when I plug it into the computer, it just spins and the computer doesn't recognize it. I thought it might be the driver, so I swapped it with another. Same problem. Not worth the effort.

This is a good little case for the Nuvi. I gave it 4 stars because I think it's missing some protective padding if accidentally dropped or hit. It should come with the Garmin Nuvi and should not have to be bought separately.

Got this hoping it'd handle a tricky far-corner sensor, and it nailed it without a hiccup. Signal's solid where my old unit blanked out. The casing feels sturdier than expected for the price too. Totally does the job, zero complaints after a week of daily use.

To be honest when I ordered this I didn't have high hopes for it but I figured it was cheap enough and would be fine for listening to music while in the shower. I was immediately impressed with the appearance, it has some weight to it and is definitely bigger than I thought it would be. It comes with a few things to mount it whichever way works for you but I've only used the ring thingy on top to hang it on the shower hook and the suction cup a couple times. Now, the suction cup..I might be missing something but to me it doesn't seem to work very well at all and I don't feel comfortable leaving it suctioned on over water because it'll probably fall in and I don't want to test to see how waterproof it really is. It's really easy to connect it to your Bluetooth and it's way louder than I expected (SCORE!!!) The only other con I've found is that sometimes when I'm using it it'll randomly connect with my husband's Bluetooth and go silent. I'm actually not sure it's connecting with his Bluetooth or just interfering but either way it's annoying to have to keep reconnecting it. If you're looking for an affordable speaker that's good quality this is a great option.
Dependable flash drives, I had some PNY's crash for no reason so when dad got me my first 8 GB Lexar I was hooked.
Arrived a day early in sturdy packaging. Popped it in, booted up, and my second monitor fired right up. Super crisp picture, zero fuss. Couldn't be happier with this little card.

Was a bit cumbersome to use. Didn't realize I needed the battery pack that stored more charge. Two fully charged batteries died within 10-15 mins of use. I can't totally fault to mount but wish there was some kind of indicator on the adapter to let operator know how much charge left in battery.

I purchased the Olympus VN 6200PC years ago, so I decided to compare it with the newer Olympus VN-722PC. At the time of purchase, I paid $43 for the VN 6200PC (I have no idea why its now listed for $144.99). The VN-722PC is currently $53.47. So it costs a little more than the original charge for the VN 6200PC. What do I get for the added price? The VN-722PC is slightly bigger with a LED screen that is almost double the size of my old recorder. This makes it easier to read all of the settings. Both have lightweight plastic cases but seem sturdy enough to weather the roughhousing usage of the average person. In addition to the Menu button, the VN-722PC has a round scroll wheel that makes navigating between the settings easy. My old recorder has similar buttons but instead of round, it has a square scroll wheel. The VN-722PC has a speaker that is about double the size of the other and it is placed on the face of the unit rather than the back like with the VN 6200PC. Plus the sound coming out of the larger speaker is nice and crisp. The newer VN-722PC has a MicroSD slot. This would be very helpful in situations where you must do a lot of recording - such as at a convention. This way you can bring along extra cards and not worry if your recorder will have enough memory to record everything. Format My old recorder captures recordings in the Windows Media Audio (WMA) file format. This isn't good for me since I am a Mac person. I always had to convert the recordings before listening to them on my Mac. Whereas the newer VN-722PC saved my recording as an MP3 file - no converting needed. Yeah!!! Manual I found the included manual adequate to get you started, but I went to the Olympus (American) website and downloaded the more thorough digital manual. Final thoughts The two things that really tipped the scale in favor of the newer VN-722PC were the MicroSD slot and the saving as MP3.

For the price, this case just rocks. You compare this case to a similar one at Walmart and you are going to pay 3 times as much. Very protective, hard shell cover and then it is surrounded by a rubber cover. Very effective for impact shock absorbing, specially if you are letting your little ones use the tablet. It also has a kick stand to view the screen horizontally on a flat surface. Excellent choice, highly recommended.
this works about half of the time, does not support apple products either
I'm partial to Garmin for gps products...great to have on the road.

I picked this up as a travel companion to my full-frame Nikon, and honestly, it blew my expectations out of the water. I figured I'd be sacrificing a lot of quality for portability, but the sharpness and color straight out of the camera are genuinely impressive. Took it on a week-long hiking trip and barely noticed it in my daypack. Battery life held up way better than I anticipated, even with heavy use. The Wi-Fi transfer was seamless for quick sharing back at the lodge. It's not just a "good for its size" camera, it's just a really solid camera, period. No regrets at all.

The item is exactly what is describe. So I am giving 5 stars. But, be aware, it will have to be placed on the item where you may want to use the item with the adhesive backing. Be sure to have a place that will be okay to attach with adhesive. I placed them on the back of my nightstand.

I was worried this wouldn't work with my Fire tablet since it's not officially listed, but it paired right up over Bluetooth without any hassle. The build feels solid for the price, and the buttons have a nice clicky feedback that doesn't feel mushy at all. I mainly grabbed this for the dedicated media controls, and they're spot on, pausing a show or skipping tracks happens instantly with zero lag. The compact size makes it easy to toss in my bag, and battery life has been impressive so far. Honestly, it's made couch browsing way more comfortable than poking at the screen constantly.
Nice pink shade, easy to grip, and the buttons feel solid. Protection is decent but leaves some back edges exposed. Still happy for everyday use.
So handy for reaching my desk setup, data transfer stays solid.
Had to tweak a couple settings, but it's been solid for everyday use.

I guess if you use headphones with mikes on your cell, land line and computer this would come in handy. Personally I rarely use headsets with my phones and my mike is connected via my webcam. The footprint is not as big as it appears in the picture. Just don't really have a use for this. At it's price point it's a bit of a luxury item It does work as advertised. Sound quality remained as good as the headphone I tested it with was on a direct connection..
Good product. Setup was a little difficult. Sound is very tinny.

I was honestly surprised by how quickly this little camera arrived, it was at my door in two days, packed really snug in a compact box with zero wasted space. The packaging felt secure but not overdone, and everything inside was pristine. My first impression pulling it out was just how sleek and lightweight it feels, like it disappears in your hand. I charged it up, popped in a card, and started snapping away in the backyard. The screen is bright and the menus are dead simple to figure out. For something this tiny, it seriously delivers right out of the gate, super happy I grabbed it.

These screen protectors definitely do their job, as do most; however, I just can't seem to justify these for my Kindle Touch. (Especially in consideration of a few outstanding faults) The most annoying aspect of this particular product was that it was not a very precise fit whatsoever. The Kindle Touch uses a good deal of perimeter screen area for display, which makes having nearly one centimeter clearance on each side of a centered screen protector application extremely annoying and difficult to overlook. If they were perfectly clear, it may be a little easier for others to overlook, but the translucence is ever so slightly different than the screen itself. I removed my screen protector immediately after application. I must note that I often and gladly employ the use of screen protectors whenever reasonably applicable, particularly with touch screen cell phones, but when it comes to the Kindle Touch I'm not too sure if they truly serve any degree of necessary function at all. With most book-like e-reader covers (mine being the standard "Amazon Kindle Touch Leather Cover, Black" - of which I am EXTREMELY happy with), the only time the screen is actually exposed would be when the Kindle is in use. In this case, the annoyance of a poorly fitting screen protector doesn't come close to outweighing the slim risk of anything happening to the screen in conjunction with a proper cover and/or case. In summation: If you find that you need a screen protector and don't mind that it only covers 95% of the screen, then this product should certainly suffice - 3 stars for the sake of advertised purpose fulfillment.

I picked this up hoping it'd handle my raw 4K video transfers without making me wait around, and for the most part it delivers. I'm coming from USB 3.0, so the jump in throughput is immediately noticeable, copying a 50GB folder took about a third of the time I'm used to. Setup was dead simple on my Mac, recognized right away. Only reason I'm not giving it five stars is it gets noticeably warm after a long session, which makes me a little nervous about longevity. Also, I wish I'd double-checked that I'd need my own Thunderbolt cable, that was an annoying extra purchase. Still, for the speed bump alone, it's been a solid upgrade over my old workflow.
The adjustable stand holds my tablet at the perfect angle for reading recipes while cooking. The hinges are a bit stiff at first, but that actually means it stays put once you set it.

I am so impressed with this Micro SD Adaptor. Not only it fits in my Dell XPS15, 9560, but it is very well made. It is much more expansive than other micro SD adapters. The card is little bit shorter (about 0.5mm) than the depth of the SD card slot (see attached photo) because it is not made for the XPS15, 9560, but it is not a problem. There is no problem removing it from the laptop with you finger nail. The manufacturer is not planning to make a custom fitting card adapter for the new Dell XPS15, 956-0, so you have to settle for this adapter if you are looking for flush fitting Micro SD Adapter. When buying this adapter, make sure to get the right model (103A). The other models made by BaseQi are either too short or too long or do not fit at all. Prior to buying this, I bought a very inexpensive plastic adapter, and it destroyed the SD card slot's internal card contacts on the first try. I had to replace the laptop motherboard to correct the damaged card slot. Yes, the card reader slot is embedded on the motherboard, so there was no other solution but replacing the entire motherboard. Yes, it was an over $1,000.00 mistake. You get what you pay for. I am very happy with this product.

This cable probably functions properly with certain devices. Between my Mac Mini and my Mitsubishi Projection TV, it did not, best I can see is because there is a sync issue that one or the other do not support. The cable itself lacks shielding on the final component leads.

Picked this up to tidy my nightstand setup, and after a couple weeks I'm pretty happy. The charging speed is solid on my iPad Air, noticeably quicker than the old brick I was using. It stays cool even after a full overnight charge, which gives me peace of mind. Build quality feels sturdy and it doesn't slide around on the wood surface. My only small gripe is the fixed angle; the screen sits pretty upright, so if I'm lounging low in bed I have to adjust myself to see it clearly. A touch more tilt flexibility would make it perfect. For the price, though, it's a reliable no-fuss stand that does exactly what I need.
I would not buy anything else. This is so comfortable when you shoot for long periods of time.

I mostly use these with my Samsung tablet for late-night streaming, and the comfort is what sold me, super lightweight and I can wear them for hours without that pinching feeling around the ears. Sound is clear and surprisingly balanced for the price, not muddy at all. The multipoint pairing was a nice bonus; I can jump between my tablet and my Pixel without manually reconnecting. My only real gripe is the volume rocker feels a little mushy, and I accidentally skip tracks more often than I'd like when adjusting it. Battery life holds up around what they claim, roughly six to seven hours of constant use. If they tightened up the button feel, these would be an easy five stars.

For the price, these are a steal. Sound is surprisingly clear and they fit snug right out of the box. Battery lasts my whole shift with juice to spare. I wasn't expecting much at this cost, but they seriously overdeliver.

First off, I'm a cable cutter, got tired of paying big $$ for basic tv. Kept my internet so netflix and amazon are my primary movie outlets. Wanted the antenna for local news/weather and sports. With that said, I was very skeptical purchasing this contraption...so when it arrived, I was even more nervous as the antenna is literally paper thin with a black and white side! Anyway, mounted it at the very top of my ceiling to give it every opportunity to succeed (they do give you a nice amount of cable so that you can mount fairly high up) attached end of cable to TV, set to Air, and had the tv do it's air scan and about 2 min later I have over 20+ free channels! local news, sports, shopping channels, fox, and few others that I'm not really sure what they are...The picture and sound quality is amazing as well...almost as if I had HD cable! I am very impressed with this and just purchased two more, one for the bedroom and the family room. The tv I used this antenna on is a 50 inch HD LCD tv.
Thought I was getting a solid little speaker but it barely lasted two months. Sound kept cutting in and out, then just died completely. For the price I expected way better reliability. Total letdown.
I grabbed this on a whim and now I doodle daily. It's super intuitive, feels natural in hand, and the pressure sensitivity makes every stroke count.
Perfect for my elderly parents who aren't tech-savvy, super simple setup and the private listening is a game changer.

I've used other Vantec docks before and always liked how sturdy they felt, so I grabbed this one without much thought. Setup was easy and it recognized my drives right away, which was nice. But the build quality isn't what I expected. The plastic around the slot feels thin and flexes a little too much for comfort. I've only swapped drives a handful of times and already the mechanism seems wobbly, like it might snap if I'm not super careful. Transfer speeds are fine and it does the basic job, but I constantly worry about breaking something during routine use. For occasional light duty it's probably okay, just doesn't have that reassuring solid feel anymore. Three stars because it works, but the durability concerns take the shine off.

I picked this up before a trip to Yellowstone and was honestly blown away by what it can do. The reach is incredible, letting me frame shots of distant bison and elk without ever disturbing them. Image stabilization really helps at the long end, keeping handheld shots crisp even in less than perfect light. It's lightweight enough to carry all day on a hike, and the menu system didn't take long to figure out. Battery life held up through full days of shooting, and the detail I'm getting in feathers and fur is just stunning for something so portable.

Quality items. I bought these for modding my PS3 60GB PHAT model along with a 120mm fan, to allow more air flow since those original models get hot and the internal fan sounds like a jet engine at highest RPM. Great seller, and excellent product.
The battery lasts a solid six hours, but the setup process was way too confusing.

If you're okay sticking to manual mode and working indoors, it's decent for the price. But no HSS kills it for outdoor fill flash. I'd only suggest it for beginners messing around in a controlled setup.
Arrived in two days, box was pristine and unboxing felt premium. The instant snug fit gave me total peace of mind.
I've used it on three shoots now and it just works, super reliable, no fuss, and totally worth it.
not a bad amp, could be better. output is mediocre at best.
Quick and easy performance upgrade at a great price. Would recommend to others
Removed one star because it's not a tool-less design. The screws are *tiny*, but they included a cute little screwdriver so they get points for that.

I decided to jump on the chromebook bandwagon since so many people were raving about it. I will say that if you're used to having a Windows laptop and expect this to feel the same, you will be very disappointed. Sure you can check your email, browse the web or watch YouTube, but that's as far as it goes. It's capabilities won't really go past that. I wanted to Skype, and I could... If I went to their site each time and used their web extention. No option to download the app to make Skype just one click away. The same happened when I wanted to get a torrent downloader. None work with chromebook, and extentions that might have were a hassle to figure out. I didn't have a fresh goat or virgin to sacrifice on hand to make this task seamless. Not being able to download popular apps for all of my favorite things was the ultimate deal breaker for me. Some people will say you can do anything on a chromebook you can do on a windows or Mac laptop, but who wants to spend their day finding loop holes in a new system. I want simple with an easy user interface. The good side; if you don't want all the fancy bells and whistles and just feel like browsing the web and checking your email, this will be perfect for you. If you have a Gmail account, you'll love it even more with the way it integrates your account with everything made by Google. Just don't try to watch a dvd or listen to a CD since thus has no disk drive. But hey, the battery does last an awful long time.
Decent part. It was a good fit for my iPhone "gript" adapter making it possible to mount a microphone.
Had this desktop for about four months now and it still runs like day one. No hiccups, no slowdowns, even with heavy multitasking. Solid build, zero issues. Couldn't ask for a more reliable machine.

I honestly didn't expect much at this price point, but it's been a pleasant surprise. Took it out of the box, plugged it into my laptop, and it showed up instantly, no formatting, no driver nonsense. Speeds are snappy for file transfers, even with larger video projects I've been archiving. I've had external drives in the past that would randomly disconnect, but this one has stayed rock solid through weeks of daily use. It runs quiet and barely gets warm, which is more than I can say for my old Seagate. I also hooked it up to my gaming console, and it recognized it immediately for extra storage. Honestly, it's exceeded what I thought a simple portable drive could do. Highly recommend.

I bought this sleeve for my Kindle DX after trying a bulky folio case that just got in the way. This is exactly what I needed. The padding feels solid without being overkill, and the interior lining is super soft, no worries at all about scratches. The zipper glides smoothly and doesn't snag. I toss it into my backpack or carry-on and never think twice about damage. It's slim enough to slide into a crowded bag but still gives me real peace of mind. When I want to read, I just unzip and go. Honestly, for the price, this little sleeve has been a perfect grab-and-go solution. Highly recommend.
The online instructions and detailed steps are a big help. It went on well and cleanly, with less fuss than most. The fit is great and appears that it will protect the screen well.
Picked this up on a whim and it's been flawless for daily use. Feels solid, plugs in snug, and hasn't given me a single hiccup. Honestly a great little grab.

Good item works like it should. Philips SWS2100W/27 RF Modulator Video Converter Philips SWS2100W/27 RF Modulator Video Converter Philips SWS2100W/27 RF Modulator Video Converter Philips SWS2100W/27 RF Modulator Video Converter Philips SWS2100W/27 RF Modulator Video Converter

I wanted to love this thing, but the audio is basically nonexistent. Even after maxing out every setting, I had to crank my receiver to levels that would blow the speakers if I accidentally switched inputs. Picture quality is fine, but what's the point of a clear image if you can't hear dialogue unless the room is dead silent and you're sitting three feet away? I'd only recommend this to someone who plans to use external audio gear exclusively and never relies on the built-in output. For anyone else, save yourself the headache and look elsewhere. It's just not worth the hassle for daily use.

Outstanding customer service. My second unit wasn't finding the internet. I chose to 'chat' online, "Steve" took over and within 20 seconds of his knowing I had an issue, it was fixed. Now I have fast and secure WiFi throughout my home and garage. As in entrepreneur I know what these guys went through to get where they got and I appreciate not only their effort to be first class but their success in being so.
The built-in Ethernet ports are rock solid, I get consistent gigabit speeds for my desktop and no lag during gaming sessions. Super easy to set up.

I was pleasantly surprised. I used this film to build my own solar filter with cardboard and it's awesome. Someone on reddit posted a photo of the sun taken with an H-Alpha scope they day this arrived, so I knew my expectations were going to be off. But, when I finally got my filter built, and slipped over the scope, I was a little shocked. the sun spots were so vivid, and the sun's surface is clearly quite turbulent. I also really like the coloration. I've seen photos taken with the Baeder film (which produces a white image) wherein the photographers used software to add some yellow color to their images. in the end, their images looked just like what you see through this black polymer. (The photo of the sun below was taken with my cell phone held up to the eyepiece. It really doesn't do this filter justice.

My card showed up a day earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. The box was double-packed with thick bubble wrap and no signs of damage at all. Inside, the card was snug in foam, and all the protective film was still perfectly in place. First impression pulling it out of the static bag was how solid and well-built it felt, no flimsy plastic shroud, just a sturdy, clean design. Popped it into my system and it fired right up, dead silent at idle. I ran a quick benchmark right away and temps stayed cool while the fans barely spun up. Honestly, I'm really impressed with how smooth the whole unboxing and install experience was.
Surprised how seamless the whole experience was. Plugged it in, paired instantly, and the crisp audio makes my workday way better.

Highly functional case and comes with a pen/stylus! The inner pouch of the case is velcroed to the outer jacket of the case. To make it stand vertically you have to position the cover accordingly. The velcro inside case concept makes for an additional layer of thickness. Also, the case has a magnetic closure but it does not work as a smart cover to sleep/wake up the device like an iPad. Other than those minor details it's a very good case and provides great protection and functionality.

I'm really impressed by the crisp dialogue clarity from the center channel during movies, it makes a huge difference at low volumes. Setup was mostly smooth, though the binding posts are frustratingly tight with 14-gauge wire; I had to trim strands to get a secure fit. The subwoofer packs a decent punch for its size without sounding boomy. For the price, it's a solid entry-level package with one minor annoyance.

Thunderbolt transfers are ridiculously fast compared to Firewire and USB -- 10 gbps -- and are incredibly handy when working on the fly with large files. It's an excellent choice for large video, photo, and audio files. It can be daisychained, too, so you can have multiple adapters setup for one machine (ultimate efficiency and speed!). Unfortunately, no cable is included - so this can become an expensive upgrade pretty quickly. I used this in conjunction with a 3TB Seagate drive, and was very pleased at its utility for backing up and storing files; Thunderbolt's speed is definitely overkill for regular small backups, but is invaluable with huge files and directories. It doesn't require a Seagate hard drive, just any SATA III drive.
Arrived way faster than expected, wrapped up safe and snug. Took it out and immediately felt how sturdy the little case is. Perfect first impression.

I shoot 4K video on a Sony a6400 and this card handles it without breaking a sweat. Zero dropped frames, even on long takes. If you're a hybrid shooter who bounces between high-bitrate video and rapid-fire RAW stills, this is the card you want. Total peace of mind.

I grabbed this for a quick-and-dirty drive testing station, not as a permanent enclosure, and for that it's been surprisingly handy. The tool-free design makes swapping drives a breeze, just pop the top and drop one in. That said, the lid is way looser than I expected; it doesn't snap shut with any confidence, so moving it around while powered on feels sketchy. Definitely not something you'd want to use as a daily external case. But if you manage expectations and treat it like an open-air dock, it gets the job done without fuss. Speed over USB 3.0 is solid, and it recognized every 2.5" and 3.5" drive I threw at it.

I grabbed this to revive an old desktop in my garage workshop. It's been solid for streaming music and pulling up repair diagrams without buffering. Setup took a few minutes through the browser interface, no drama. Range punches through two concrete walls better than I expected. I'd recommend it to anyone needing a reliable wired connection for a non-critical machine or a secondary workspace. Not blazing fast, but perfectly steady for everyday tasks.

Don't buy it, It was defective from the beginning. burnt my First ASUS Motherboard P9X79 WS with in first year But amazon customer replaced it and i got new Asus Rampage IV black edition. Even after that my PC was never stable. In 2-3 years i never used it properly Mostly used Mac Pro and mac book pro over the years, But i kept upgrading GPU inside my PC. A month ago i become serious about using this PC and fixing it. Motherboard gave anti surge error and keep switch ON and OFF in a loop with Boot screen. On closer inspect 24 pin 2 cables was burnt and melted. PC SPEC ASUS RAMPAGE IV BLACK EDITION GTX 780ti GTX TITAN X GTX 1080 GTX 1080 32 GB Corsair Vengeance RAM which i was never able to use for 2-3 hours continuously. My advise to buyers will be to get another established brand corsair / EVGA then Rosewill. Save you a lot of headache as power supply would be last place to check for faulty / unstable system ------ In video you can see its warranty seal isn't broken. and its inside is clean because it was unstable/defective power supply, so it has been hardly used as I also have dual xeon mac pro also. on top of headaches the power supply cause me I have to ship defective PSU on my own cost.

This is not an amp to impress your friends with. I bought because the previous owner of my car put extra speakers in but the head unit they sold the car with could handle all of them so I bought this amp to supplement the power. Sounds fine, I did have a problem with alternator whine and I did everything I could to get rid of it eventually I bought a suppressor and that worked.
Excellent on size, function and design. A big plus that it is able to read 4tb portable hard drive.
This thing fired right up in my old backup unit. Fit was perfect, install took maybe two minutes, and the annoying beep is finally gone. Glad I didn't waste money on a whole new UPS.
For the price, this thing is tough to beat. Colors look decent and setup was dead simple. Just wish the panel felt a bit sturdier.

The system is super easy to install as the cameras are totally wireless. The router does have to be connected to the wireless router with a cable and hence must be located close to it. Once the cameras are synced you no longer need to pay much attention to the security system router so that is not a problem. I set up on my computer and used my iPhone when setting up the cameras.

I wanted to love these, but they're just okay. Setup was quick, but the range boost is weaker than I expected, my back bedroom still buffers. Speed drops noticeably compared to being near the main router. It works, but not the seamless whole-home fix I was hoping for. For the price, it's a lukewarm compromise.

I've been using this little remote mostly for long-exposure night shots, and the range is surprisingly solid, works from across the backyard with zero lag. Pairing took seconds, no fuss at all. Honestly, it feels way more durable than I expected for the price. If you're tired of camera shake ruining tripod shots, just grab this thing.

I ordered this SSD hoping to breathe a little life into my old laptop, but the first impression was honestly pretty deflating. The packaging was flimsy, just a thin bubble mailer with no extra padding around the drive itself, so the retail box arrived with a crushed corner. Not exactly confidence-inspiring for sensitive electronics. Inside, the drive was just rattling around loosely; no protective foam or even a sealed anti-static bag, which made me nervous about potential damage during shipping. After unboxing, I also noticed some faint scuffs on the casing, almost like it had been handled before. Maybe it still works fine, but when you're excited about an upgrade, receiving something that looks careless and possibly used really kills the vibe. Disappointing start for sure.
I wasn't sure what to expect for the money, but the solid feel and clean signal honestly surprised me, way better than the budget import I tried first.

The Buttkicker adds a bit of shake to your home theatre. It doesn't replace the subwoofer, but sort-of turns the floor into a lower subwoofer. And I love it. But there are some things you should know. First, the LFE Kit (with Amp) is probably the best choice to start with because you can't drive it from your receiver - not enough power. So getting in the game will cost you around $500. Which is more than for Aura Bass Shakers, but far better. Second, one LFE Kit will do a sofa nicely, but if you, like me, mount it under your floor, you're gonna need two. Possibly more, but probably two. Because that's a LOT more mass to shake, and otherwise they'll be maxxing out and making a loud knocking noise. Third, it's not like riding a rollercoaster. You'll feel the floor shake from loud sound effects, bombs, etc., but don't expect magic. It makes the movie more real, but it doesn't toss you around. All that said, it's a great addition. If you can afford the cost-of-entry, you'll be amazed how shallow the system feels with it off.

Speed is fine and all, but sometimes it 'vanishes' from my Macbook and I have to reinstall the drivers. I'm not talking about after an OSX upgrade or patch, just one day it's there and the next it's not. I have this on a USB hub on my monitor that gets moved from the Macbook to a windows system and it works on both which is what you would expect. Never has issues under windows 10.
For the price, this little device punches way above its weight. Minor lag now and then, but totally livable.
The battery life on this thing is insane, lasts me nearly two full days of heavy use without breaking a sweat.

Pros: This camera is incredible! Provides outstanding pictures in all sorts of situations. With a tripod, it can take amazing shots at night. Would highly recommend this to someone who is wanting to expand their photo shooting hobby, but not willing to make the jump to SLR yet. For all of you SLR users, this camera will provide an outstanding back-up to your current set-up. Cons: Battery.......i just ended up buying an extra one.
I'll look at Anker products next time I need a hub.

I grabbed this Chromebook mainly for the crisp 1080p IPS screen, and at this price I honestly wasn't expecting much else. The display alone makes it a steal for streaming and everyday work. What surprised me most was how snappy everything feels, boots in seconds, handles a dozen tabs without breaking a sweat, and battery easily lasts me a full workday plus some evening browsing. Keyboard took me about an afternoon to get used to, but now I fly on it. Trackpad is smooth and accurate for all my scrolling and gestures. Build is lightweight and sturdy enough to toss in a backpack. For the money, this thing absolutely punches above its weight.

Pros- Great picture, slim design, light weight, 3d conversion is pretty cool. Cons- Samsung App store is a joke, at best. If you are only buying the TV for that, wait for a while to see if "Smart TV's" really take off. No one is writing apps for them yet and the ones that are out are pretty lame/useless/not fun. Even the YouTube app doesnt work with the keyboard side of the TV remote...fail. Update (a little over one year later 12/30/2012) Picture is still amazing and the small edges set this TV apart; best i've ever seen...it does have 240hz refresh rate (not CMR...what a joke). The app store has not gotten much better. I enjoy the CNET app, which is pretty much the only one I've downloaded after the fact that was worth anything. Oh...3d glasses are HORRIBLE. They do not last. I have 1/4 that is still alive after a year. The 2 i bought seperate from the TV (same model number as the ones that came with) didn't work all that great the moment i took them out of the box. Even if they were working properly, don't expect to make it all the way through a long movie like Avatar without them dying on you. that is awkward when you have to offer your pair to one of your guests and then they know you are sitting there watching a blurry picture. Update 4/17/2013 Smart Hub still stinks. Samsung, please push a complete software update to these models!!
I honestly didn't think my aging laptop could feel this snappy again. Boots in seconds and apps open instantly, way beyond what I hoped for at this price. Total game changer.
These are not Dell official drives, but work just fine in our older out of warranty servers when we have the occasional failed drive that needs to be replaced.

Was hoping this thing would last longer than a couple months. Used it with my laptop for podcasts and it just started crackling, then one side went totally dead. Build feels flimsy in hindsight. For the price I guess I shouldn't be shocked, but still disappointing when it craps out mid-listen.
Can't beat the performance for what I paid, really solid piece of kit.

I bought this for my Windows 7 Desktop PC. I was tired of running a cord from my router in the bedroom to the living room where the PC resides. I have a Linksys e3000 Router which is a top of the line router. I wanted a wireless USB device which would allow me to have a full 300MB/s connection. This one fit the bill and was reasonably priced so I went with it. After downloading the right drivers from the web I was set to go. Unfortunately, I could not get a connection higher than 130MB/s. It seems this device will only work at the full 300MB/S on the 2.4GHz band (not the 5GHz band) and only at the wider channel widths. I set my router up this way but this would still not pick up at the full 300MB/s. This would have been bad but not a deal breaker. But then the device would not keep a stable connection. It would keep dropping the connection despite the fact that the router was working just fine. Confirmed this by using my iPad, iPhone, and my Windows laptop to connect to my router every time the connection went down. They all worked fine. Given these problems I decided to return the device.

Showed up a day early in a tidy little box with zero damage. Plugged it in right out of the package, felt solid, and my second screen fired up instantly. Super smooth first experience, and the packaging kept everything snug. Honestly impressed for the cost.

For the price, this little gadget is an absolute steal. Feels way more premium than what I paid. Snappy performance, no issues at all so far. Honestly expected way less for the cost, super happy I took a chance on it.

This is a wonderful flash for my Olympus OMD5 - small and powerful, just like the camera... and the ability to tilt the flash as well as regulate it's intensity makes it a real asset to any photographer.
I was just pleased to be able to find them at all let alone at a reasonable price. now i'm set for a while although i probably should buy some more.

Look, for the price I wasn't expecting miracles, and that's exactly what I didn't get. The build feels surprisingly solid and they definitely have that retro-futuristic look, which is a nice touch on my desk. Sound-wise, they're pretty hollow and tinny, with almost no bass to speak of. Fine for a quick YouTube video or a Zoom call where you just need to hear voices, but music sounds flat and lifeless. My main gripe is the clamping force, after about 30 minutes I had a headache and my ears were sore. The padding is minimal and the headband doesn't adjust enough to relieve the pressure. If you need something dirt cheap for very short sessions, maybe, but for any extended use they're a letdown, even at this low cost.
It works nicely. To customize it is a pain. Double press this button, hold that one for 16 seconds, yadda yadda...with with some time and patience you'll get it to where you want as far as sound.

case back cracked on corners within first week and proceded to warp. The case would not stay attached to the ipad, it simply fell off after being attached. The JETech slim-fit case is a much better choice for a budget case
Surprised by how well this holds up. Took it hiking in a downpour and my gear stayed bone dry. Way better than I expected for the price.

I threw three of these ML120 RGB fans into my rig about eight months ago and they've been running almost nonstop since. I was a little worried about the RGB longevity because I've had other brands flicker after a few weeks, but these are still bright and the colors stay true. I keep my PC on my desk so I hear everything, these stay quiet even when I'm gaming for hours and the temps climb. Dust buildup has been minimal too, which I credit to the blade design. Honestly, after nearly a year of daily use they feel as solid as day one. Zero bearing whine, zero dead LEDs, just smooth cooling.

I just received it today, tried it for about 1 hour with my new camera: Canon 60D, ir works like a charm & love it:). It looks nice & very good quality. Responsed exelent. If you need it, just buy it & you will not have to return it.
Exceeded what I expected, really solid build and totally reliable.

I am probably not the most qualified person on Amazon to write a review on headphones/ear buds, but with that said, I LOVE THESE! Since my children are kleptomaniacs regarding anything electronic that Mom has, I only buy the super cheap headphones - the ones available at the gas station, costing $1-2. This cheapness keeps me from losing it when my earphones "disappear" several times a week. I was getting a few other things on Amazon and decided to try these. and guess what? THEY ARE AWESOME! I love how they cancel outside noise and kind of create a seal in your ears. I love how you can distinguish between '80s rock and classic country with these. (Most of the earphones I've had made Johnny Cash sound like Axl Rose. ) I am truly surprised at how clear these make music sound and how well I can actually understand podcasts and comedy now. It's like going from an 8-track to a CD - yep, there is that much of a difference. I am now a Gummy convert and will only use these. I have purchased an extra pair and I keep them well hidden. Bottom Line: These are fantastic ear buds, especially for the price. The incredible sound these provide make walking the dogs a true joy - allowing me to disappear into a cloud of loud, clear music, piped comfortably into the hearing holes on each side of my head. and keeps out the interruptions caused by a bunch of extraneous noise. You absolutely cannot go wrong with these - and their low price just elevates them to perfection.

I used this HDMI cable to connect my new Sony KDL46EX640-46" LED TV to my TiVo Premiere. It's been three weeks and the picture and sound are excellent. I'm not an expert by any means but this cable seems to do just what you need a cable for at a very attractive price. I did take one of the other reviewers advice and bought two to have one handy for a future need. Shipping was very quick and appreciated.

I got a Kindle for Christmas and wanted to get a cute, but protective cover for it. I did a lot of research, and M-Edge seemed to get really high reviews, so I bought one. I got the fuchsia cover. It looks much different on my computer monitor than it does in person. On the monitor it looks more like a rose pink, but in reality it's a super bright, neon pink. I love pink, but I don't want it to stand out THAT much. It's really, really bright. I knew this cover wasn't leather (unfortunately), but once I touched it I was disappointed in the feel of it. Yes it is soft and smooth, but it looks and feels like plastic "Pleather." It really does have a bad over, but I was spoiled by leather in the past. No, it's not leather... but I knew that when I bought it, so I can't hold that against it. Overall, I think the design of this cover is fabulous. I love how it incorporates the E-Luminator (sold separately). It's quite a slick design. I love that I can store the E-Luminator right in the case. I also love the strap that keeps the book securely closed or open. It folds back on itself like a dream, and is very comfortable to hold either open or folded back. The inside has a nice microfiber suede-like lining that offers protection against scratches. It's a great case.. really great, if you don't mind the plastic cover. I am hoping that they will offer leather versions in the future, and more color choices.

I was a little hesitant at the price, but this thing feels way more expensive than it actually is. The weight keeps it planted, and the keypresses are satisfying without being loud. Typing all day feels effortless now. Honestly, for the build quality and comfort you're getting, it's an absolute steal.
the build quality was nice and had everything i needed for the build.
Great quality and function. very well made.
Honestly, for what I paid, this thing is a steal. The build quality feels premium and it handles both light and dark surfaces without any hiccups. Super happy with this purchase.
Nobody can see the screen but me, plus it also works as an antiglare. Exactly what I was looking for.

I really wanted to like this thing, and for the first couple weeks it felt decent for the money. But after maybe a month of occasional use, the gimbal bearing started grinding and now it sticks constantly. The cheap plastic knobs for adjusting balance are all stripped, so fine-tuning is basically impossible. One of the metal screws holding the baseplate already rusted, which is wild for indoor use. I tried to tighten everything up but the whole unit just feels wobbly and loose now, like it's slowly coming apart. I'm not rough with my gear at all, but this clearly wasn't built to last. Disappointing.

I bought this so that i didn't have to buy a new stereo and it was the best decision i ever made! It has all the functions i expected it to have and more! Its easy to pair and not i have bluetooth functionality in all my cars!

I wanted a second "reader" for the iPad and this fits the bill. I tested the USB, SD and microSD slots with an iPad 2 iOS 5.0.x and they worked as expected for importing pictures. The "5 in 1 Card Reader for iPad" is big. It is larger than both the USB and SD card reader combined from the official Apple Connectivity Kit. I had a microSD-to-SD adapter already. This 5-in-1 is larger than all three of those pieces together. The 5-in-1 is definitely not made to Apple's quality standards. It also does not cost Apple prices. The one advantage of the 5-in-1 is that is is one piece so it is convenient when traveling. My conclusion is it is worth what I paid for it and is part of my travel bag. I continue to use my original Apple connectivity kit at home.

I was honestly shocked at how fast this thing arrived, ordered late Thursday night and it was at my door Saturday morning. The packaging was solid too; everything was snug in that molded cardboard insert, no rattling around or scuffed corners on the box. First thing I noticed pulling it out was the build quality feels way more substantial than I expected for the price, nice braided cable and the connectors click in with a really satisfying firmness. Hooked it up to my Dell and an old Samsung monitor I've had sitting in the closet and it just worked instantly, no driver hunting, no weird flickering. Picture came through crisp right off the bat. Super happy with the whole experience so far.

Seems to work well. I bought it for a camera that had been modified for astrophotography work. It takes care of the color change that modification causes, and does it without having to mess with the white balance settings. Makes the camera useable for normal (non-astro) photographs.
This is among the most functional devices in the studio. Love them and trust that they'll work forever.
Bought this for my PS5 and it gave up after six months of light use. Worked okay at first, but now it won't even power on. All my saved progress is just stuck there. Total waste of money and time.

This little adapter saved me from drilling holes and running Ethernet across my rental. I work from a converted attic office where the built-in Wi-Fi on my Dell was basically a coin toss, fine for email, useless for Zoom. Popped this in a USB port, let Windows grab the driver, and instantly had a solid three-bar connection where I used to get "connected, no internet." Latency dropped enough that my VPN and video calls stay locked in with no stuttering. File transfers to our NAS actually finish now. I'd recommend it to anyone stuck in a Wi-Fi dead zone, especially renters who can't rewire, or folks nursing an older laptop with a weak internal card. Also perfect for students in dorms with thick walls and oversaturated networks.

I picked up this charger for my Galaxy S6 after the original one started acting up, and I'm honestly surprised by how solid it is. I figured most replacement cables were pretty much the same, but this one charges noticeably faster and doesn't heat up like my old cord used to. The build feels way more substantial than what came in the box, thicker, with sturdy connectors that don't wobble when plugged in. I've been using it daily for a few weeks now and there's zero sign of wear, no bending at the ends or loose connection issues. It just works reliably every time, which is more than I expected for the price.
This is beautiful! The color and feel are great - definitely a true rose gold. Goes on very easy (but taking off is another story lol).

I really wanted to love this Sanyo camcorder for the price. It's compact, starts up fast, and the battery life is decent for casual clips. In good daylight the footage is pretty crisp, definitely usable for family stuff or vacations. But low-light performance is rough, lots of grain and washed-out colors, even indoors with lamps on. The built-in mic picks up every little hand movement, so audio can get annoying. I also wish the menu system was more intuitive; changing basic settings feels clunky. For under $150 it's not terrible, and you get a lot of recording time on a single SD card. Just know you're trading polish and reliability for the low cost.
Excellent system, now they are coming out with 4K for video, always something to make you spend more money. But I really like the system and it is capable for 3D.

I grabbed this for my college stats class and honestly it's a steal for what it does. Does everything my professor's $120 model does for half the cost. Screen is crisp, buttons feel solid, and battery life has been great. If you need a reliable workhorse without overspending, this is the one.
Only worth buying if you love false alarms waking you at 3am.

I've been using this lap desk for a couple weeks now, and it's a total game-changer for working on the couch or in bed. The slim profile slips right into my backpack alongside my laptop, and I barely notice the weight. The surface grips my machine solidly, no sliding around, even when I shift positions. The pull-out mouse tray is sturdy and smooth, perfect for tight spaces. I'd especially recommend this to students or remote workers who bounce between rooms or travel often. If you're someone who hates balancing a hot laptop on your knees, this little thing solves that completely. Great gift idea for anyone who works from home.

I was so excited to get this thing set up for my home office, but the connectivity is a total mess. The HDMI port is so recessed that only the skinniest cables actually stay plugged in, my standard ones just pop right out if you breathe near them. I tried three different brands and had to wedge a book against the cord just to keep the signal from cutting out every few minutes. The picture quality itself is fine when it works, but what's the point if you can't rely on a stable connection? Honestly, for the price, this should not be an issue. I've never had a device fight me this hard on something so basic. Waste of time.
I live out in the sticks and this thing picks up all the NBC, CBS, ABC, EFG etc with HD pic and 5.

For anyone in a small apartment or just getting their first place, this router is a steal. Setup took maybe five minutes and my work laptop, phone, and smart TV all run smoothly. I'd easily suggest it to students or solo renters who want solid performance without dropping serious cash.
Okay for smaller monitors, but measure carefully if yours is near 20 inches.
So cheaply made it isn't even worth it to out it on ur phone
Glass arrive and it was the correct size and with a protective cover on both the front and back. Made the installation a breeze.
Felt cheaper than expected and stopped working after a month.
I bought this cable to replace the one that came with my Sony Portable drive. The mailman left the package on the front lawn and my dog ripped it to shreds. This cable works perfectly with my drive.

I've had the Motion 30 for about eight months now and it's held up beautifully through daily use, dusty shelves, and a couple of rowdy movie nights. The cabinet still looks pristine, no scuffs or loose connections at all. Sound-wise, after a solid break-in period, it settled into this effortless clarity that makes acoustic sets and dialogue-heavy shows feel so present. I've pushed it pretty hard with some bass-heavy tracks and it never flinches or distorts. Paired it with a modest receiver and it still shines. Honestly, I was nervous about longevity at this price, but it's proven to be a tank while sounding delicate when it needs to. Very happy.

The NZXT H810 is a solid case that mostly lives up to the hype. I moved my build over from a cramped mid-tower and the extra room made cable management almost enjoyable. Build quality feels premium overall, though I noticed a tiny scratch near the front I/O out of the box, nothing worth returning it over, but a bit disappointing at this price. Airflow has been better than I expected after reading some complaints online; my temps dropped a few degrees under load. The radiator support is fantastic if you're planning a custom loop. One minor gripe: the dust filters are a little flimsy and tricky to reseat after cleaning. Still, for the feature set and aesthetics, I'm happy with my purchase.
Nice fit. Safe rubbery feel while not being to large.
great price for the Gigs! These little guys work great and hold all you photos, music, etc... to share w/ your friends.
Got these to connect my game console and it works perfectly. Hard to beat this quality for the low cost.
Solid little case, does what I needed for daily tablet use.
The sound is amazing but I can't really make them stay inside my ear, and I've tried all included sizes of the rubber. If you move the cable it "rubs" and you can hear it.
We've had ours for over a year now, and they've survived countless drops and sticky fingers without a single issue. Still going strong with daily kid chaos.
Didn't get the 18 mile range, but works OK for a mile or so.

I'm really impressed with how lightweight and comfortable these are for all-day wear. The padding around the ears is soft and doesn't get sweaty, even during longer listening sessions. I also appreciate the clear, crisp mids that make vocals and dialogue stand out perfectly. For the price, the overall clarity is honestly surprising.

For under thirty bucks I wasn't expecting much, but this thing genuinely surprised me. It's not going to rattle your walls with bass or compete with a premium sound system, but for its size it punches way above its weight. Vocals come through crisp and podcasts are super clear, even at higher volumes. I've been using it in the garage while working on projects and it fills the space with zero distortion. Bluetooth pairing is instant every single time and the battery just keeps going. My kid even knocked it off a shelf onto concrete and it barely got a scuff. If you want a portable speaker that just works without any fuss, this is it.
excellent lens. a must have lens for your gears. it creates incredible bokeh, good quality images, and so wonderful colors. very good for portrait.
Worked pretty good for a while, doesn't cost much for an old computer

I wanted to love this thing given how cheap it was, but you really get what you pay for. Setup wasn't terrible, though the app kept looping back to the login screen which wasted a solid 20 minutes. Once it finally connected, the speed drop was brutal, my basement Roku went from buffering occasionally to barely loading the home screen. Signal bars showed two or three, but actual throughput felt like dial-up. For a budget extender I wasn't expecting miracles, just something usable for casual streaming. It technically broadcasts a signal, but if it can't handle a basic 1080p feed without constant stuttering, the low price stops being a bargain and just becomes a paperweight.

I was honestly nervous about doing a screen replacement myself, but this panel made it painless. The brightness and color accuracy are spot on, no weird tint or dead pixels, just a crisp, vibrant display that looks exactly like the factory original. Installation took me maybe 15 minutes because the mounting brackets lined up perfectly and the connector snapped right in without any finagling. My Chromebook feels brand new again, and I saved a ton of cash compared to buying a whole replacement machine. If you've got a cracked screen and a little patience, this is absolutely the way to go. Couldn't be happier with how it turned out.
Amazing Excellent Happy Costumer. Rubber cover for the keyboard that I didn't expect. It's what I was waiting for. Thanks a lot.
It does it's job. protects your rear lense and is easy to use. I have to type more words to submit
For twenty bucks it's okay, but the signal drifts more than I'd like. It pairs fine and the sound is decent when it stays connected. Not amazing, just fair for the price.

This projector is the best deal! Very easy to setup, I literally got it up in minutes - the only thing I did, unpack from the box, plug in power and plug in VGA cable to my computer. There are a lot of stuff in the menu, but I doubt the average joe will need to use those. I projected to my family room wall from about 20-25' away, the image was bright and sharp. Even after turning on my 300W haligen light, the image is still veru bright. This is a great buy.
Perfect for anyone mounting their first big screen solo, it's rock solid and effortless.
Great drive. Clean and basic. Not super fast but not super slow. It does crap out and have smart errors if you plug it in and out enough.

I travel a lot (3-4 days a week) for work. This bag has cut my security check time in half! This bag is pretty tough, and you can't beat the warranty. The water bottle holder is a great addition too. I use the 2 outside pockets every day; carrying glasses/case in one and medications, comb, media player, and ear buds in the other. The key hanger is a must-have for me. This is a great IT/Project Management bag. I carry an REI medium duffel bag for clothing. All road warriors need something as straight forward as this bag. Here is a list of what is in this bag at all times. - Laptop: Lenovo 13" T430 - Laptop power supply - Travel mouse - 2 Spiral notebooks (meeting notes) - B&N Nook HD Plus w/adapter - Pen flashlight - 4 pens - 1 Fat sharpie - 1 Standard sharpie - 4 ID badges - 4 Thumb drives - 2.5" external hard drive w/cable - Music player (old Android phone) - Ear buds - Glasses case - Medications - Comb - Travel bottle ibuprofen - Burt's Bees - Zip bag toothpicks - Travel Purel - Verizon MiFi access point w/adapter - Sunglasses - Bandanna / Handkerchief - Replaced middle zipper pull with small LED zipper pull light (Gerber BG) - Extra pair dry socks (life saver) - Various Metro cards for US cities - Carabiner attached to handle for hats, and hanging in bathrooms, etc. - Travel umbrella - Cheap travel poncho (another life saver) - Snack bag almonds - Snack bag of beef jerky
These subs hit deep and clean without any distortion, even at high volume. The build quality feels solid and installation was a breeze.
Arrived super fast in a bubble-wrapped box. Lens feels solid right out of the box, but I noticed some edge warping at 18mm. Sharp otherwise.

I upgraded from the S4i which, despite the ear tip choices, didn't quite fit right. While I can't justify that the high price of the X10i has anything, if any, to do with fit, they DO fit much better. Maybe it's the obtuse, minimalist design as compared to the slight rear bulge of the S4i (not that those were big in the first place). Now I'm able to hear instruments in songs I never heard before! You'll hear EVERYTHING with these, which can be good or bad, especially if you listen to a song encoded poorly. My mp3s aren't lossless by any means, but the clarity the X10i gives is unparalleled. I was expecting to hear much more hisses and clicks, but surprisingly I haven't (lucky, i guess). It hits all the highs and lows just the way I like them! I use these mainly while playing my e-drum kit through a Roland TD-9, so its discriminating fidelity is important to me. If these reviews only focused on the product in-hand, then I would have posted 5 out of 5 stars. IMHO, reviews encompass all that comes with the product, from its accessories to the warranty period. I gave the X10i 4 stars for the following reasons: 1) At such a premium price, why aren't the included airplane and 1/4" adapters gold just like the headphone's 3.5 mm jack? I don't lower my audiophilic standards on a flight or when plugging these into a stereo receiver. 2) I can't justify a 350% price increase between these and the S4i, which were a great-sounding pair of headphones (when they fit) at a much lower price point. Still, I don't regret this purchase at all!
Just got it and starting using it on my iPad Air 2. Went on easy...was able to get all of the bubbles out. So far...so good....

This is a true ergonomic mouse. I work with computers for more than 10 hrs a day, previously I was using a logitech mouse but there was wrist pain which I started feeling. So I searched for a better one and found out this one. I should say this is a good find as I am using this one for around 4 months and no sign of that wrist pain. Also the buttons are very comfortable with an additional window button which comes very handy in day to day use. Battery wise also it is good.
