I always keep it on, since it has auto off battery saving feature it runs good for more than 3 months with couple of AA batteries. I would have given it 4.5 stars but since there is no option for that I settle down to 4. The reason is that the connector build is not at all good. For me it cam apart in couple of months. So you have to be very careful while putting it in the computer and taking it out, otherwise it can become like mine (see the last picture).

Installed this in a split level 1960's era home that has a combination of old 2 wire (non grounded) wiring, and remodeled sections with 3 wire (grounded) wiring. Router is upstairs, and I wanted to hook up an "Apple TV", and TiVo downstairs using the Powerline XE104 to cut down on wireless signals that were interfering with my LG Home Theater System's wireless rear speakers and subwoofer (caused annoying sound fade in and out). Wiring involved was all in 2 wire part of the house, but on a different circuits. Was able to get an immediate "downstream" signal and internet connection going through a 6 outlet (wall) surge protector at the Router end, but only got.5 Mbps throughput speeds downstream (testing speed with laptop and online speed test). Removed the surge protector (as Instructions state), and speed went up to same speed as when the laptop is directly connected to my Netgear (Wireless N) Router. This product is rated at 85 Mbps, but obviously not getting that from my ISP (15 Mbps max.). No need to get Netgear's faster products (200 or 500 Mbps) if you're not getting a lot more than 85 Mbps input from your ISP, right? Movie streaming speed to Apple TV is good. Will probably connect the LG BluRay player up too (which can also stream Netflix or Vudu movies wirelessly) once I get a longer Ethernet cable (now using Wireless N with good results). One other note: Those that are saying they get two 4 port units must be getting re-packaged or a mis-packaged Kit, as Netgear's specs for the "XEB1004-100NAS 4-port Powerline 85 Adapter Kit" specifically states one unit has one ethernet port, and only the receiving unit has 4 ports. Came with 2 5ft. Ethernet cables. BTW, sound fading problem has disappeared, thanks to this great product!

Arrived a day early and the packaging was seriously impressive, thick foam cutouts, everything snug and protected. Unboxing felt premium right out of the gate. First impression: sleek, solid build, no cheap plastic feel. Setup was quick and it fired up without a hitch. I've only had it a few days, but it's already exceeded my expectations for the price point. Really happy with this purchase so far.

This PWOW ErgoFit earbuds are very good for its price. It's very comfortable and have great sound quailty. One of the feature that I like about this earbuds because it block out background noise and wire cord are well made. For the price, I love it has built-in microphone to answer your calls, a switch to toggle between iPhone or android and a sliding volume control. It can only allow you to adjust music volume only so in order to skip it to next song which you need to do on your phone. Also, they don't hurt my ears after a long-wear. Earbuds and headphones that is more bulky tend to hurt my ears because they fit too full in my ears but this earbuds doesn't hurt my ears at all. I would definitely recommend this to anyone who is looking for a good pair of earbuds that are really cheap with good sound quailty. Pros: * Ultra-soft ErogoFit in-ear earbuds * Multipurpose Remote: Volume control, music switching, hands-free call, open Siri in iPhones * Built-in microphone and answer key: allowing hands-free calling Cons: * None so far. But will update if anything I received this product at a discount in exchange for an honest unbiased review. These opinions are my own and not persuaded in any way by receiving a discount on this product for review. Reviewed by Gopower If you found my review helpful, please vote 'YES' below. Thank You.

This radio has a solid build and the sound is clear enough for casual listening around the house or garage. I'd say it's fine for someone who just wants a basic unit and doesn't mind a few quirks. The battery drain is real though, I'm swapping AAs way more than I expected, which gets old fast. The bigger annoyance for me is the preset memory. Once you save a station to a slot, there's no way to wipe it clean. You can only overwrite it with another station, so if you move or want a fresh start, you're stuck with old clutter. I'd recommend this to a non-techie person who sets it once and forgets it, but if you like full control over your presets, look elsewhere.

The keyboard has a cheap, PC Jnr/ Speak n Spell toy game feel. The biggest problem is the function keys are pre programmed and override all functions you normally use them for. For example the F1 key overrides ALL previously programmed functions AND QUITS ANY OPEN APPLICATION when it's pressed. It's been close to an hour now I have had it plugged in and trying to search the internet and Apple websites to figure out how to override Macally's function keys. If you find this inconvenience tolerable and the feel of a toy keyboard is ok, then by all means go for it. Update 31/10/16 Upon receipt of additional info not immediately discernible in the instructions that came with the keyboard, my headache about the function keys is now solved. Apparently it was simply a matter of pressing the FN key and the Up or Down arrows to change function key functionality. I'm most grateful to the folks at Macally for their assistance. I am now adjusting my review and will recommend the keyboard to you - if you have no problems with the cheap keyboard feel. The keyboard itself works as it should, the keys are low profile but provide good tactile feedback response when pressed. I also should mention the keyboard comes with a cool Macally mouse - which works really great. So, to sum it up in pros and cons Cons: Cheap, toy feel Instructions were not as clear as they could have been - I was not able to understand from the instructions that the FN and up and down arrow keys toggle functionality. Maybe it was my poor understanding, but I felt it could have been made more plain Pros: Good tactile feedback response from all keys Useful Function keys functionality Comes with a 3 button optical mouse

I've been using this tripod for a few months now for occasional product shots and outdoor stuff. It's lightweight and easy to carry, which is nice, but that's because a lot of it is plastic rather than metal like I assumed from the photos. It held my mirrorless camera fine for a while, but I noticed the leg joints started getting wobbly sooner than expected. I'm pretty gentle with my gear, so that was disappointing. It still works if you're careful, but I don't fully trust it with anything heavier or on uneven ground. For the price, it's okay for very light use, just don't expect it to hold up long-term if you're out shooting regularly. Decent starter tripod, but durability is iffy.

I knew what I wanted before I ordered from Amazon, and I must say their service was excelent. The Sony CD/DVD player does everything I expected from it, and the HDMI works well with my 42" Plasma HDTV.The price for the Sony was reasonable, and the shipping was fast. What more could I ask for.

Was missing a front USB-C port on my rig and didn't want to reach around back every time I needed to plug in a flash drive or card reader. This little hub slid right into the empty 3.5-inch bay and gave me exactly what I needed. Installation was super straightforward, just a single motherboard header connection, no extra drivers or fuss. The ports feel snug, nothing wiggles loose, and transfer speeds are solid. I've been using it daily for about three weeks now and it's held up perfectly. Bonus points for blending in with my case's black mesh front. Honestly, for the price, this thing is a no-brainer if you want easy front-panel USB 3.0 access.

I was honestly surprised how clear the display stays, no weird glare or rainbow effect even in bright light. The oleophobic coating actually works too; fingerprints wipe right off. Installation was super simple with zero bubbles, which never happens for me.

Not that I talk on the phone in the shower. My phone is loud enough, but this thing is pretty cool. I bought this from when my bf calls for when he is walking home from work and I happen to be in the shower. I have a specific ring for his call, so I know when to answer it. Just in case he ask if we are out of onions for whatever he wants to cook or I have to cook for that evening.

I grabbed the 16-foot version for my home office setup and it's been perfect. The cable lays flat and routes easily behind my desk without fighting me the whole time. I'm using it to connect an older Canon scanner to a Dell tower running Windows 11, and transfer speeds are just as snappy as the stubby cable that came with the unit. No dropouts or recognition hiccups at all. If you've got a standing desk converter or need to reach gear tucked away in a cabinet, do yourself a favor and grab this. I'd recommend it to anyone wrestling with tight, awkward stock cables.

I went in with pretty low expectations given the price, but this thing actually surprised me. It's noticeably lighter than I'd anticipated, which makes it easy to toss in a bag without thinking twice. Build quality is decent, not flagship-level by any stretch, but it doesn't feel cheap or hollow either. Performance-wise, it handles everyday tasks smoothly; I haven't run into any lag during streaming or browsing. Battery life lands right around average, nothing groundbreaking but enough to get through a workday. My only real gripe is the screen could be a touch brighter outdoors. For what I paid, it delivers more than I expected and has become a reliable backup device.
well, those ear muffs are too good, clear, tangle free, cheap and made me wish i stuck with HTC phones...

Package arrived in a bubble mailer that looked like it had been stepped on, and the product sleeve inside was bent in half. The screen protector itself had a weird greasy film right out of the box that wouldn't wipe off. Honestly, the whole thing felt cheap before I even peeled the backing off. Pretty disappointing first impression.

For YEARS I held onto my old kodak EasyShare, it was one of the first ever produced! I loved my old one, but wanted something a bit more updated to take on my vacation abroad this summer. This camera's features are terrific: the LCD screen is large and easy to see, the functions are very user-friendly, the fact that is uses batteries is a huge plus while traveling, and it's size is small enough so that it doesn't take up too much room, but you'll never loose it in your purse and be able to have it readily on hand for those special (but sometimes fleeting) shots. The quality of the shots is not the best, but a little touch up using some photo editing software can certainly clear that up (the software that comes with the camera is convenient and easy to use, but it will not produce professional quality pictures like a bit of work with photoshop can). Overall I am very happy with my upgrade. My next camera will DEFINITELY be a kodak, but perhaps I'll wait a while and save my money to invest in one of their higher end cameras:)

The budget alternative to intel i7-4770. It runs cool, crunches 1080p 60fps rendering and lets me play gw2 with little to no lag. All for the same price as an i5. Don't let the xeon name fool you, this server processor can easily handle itself in the consumer environment.

I picked this board up for a home lab project and honestly didn't expect it to go as smoothly as it did. Right out of the box, everything felt well laid out and the BIOS was intuitive enough that I didn't need to dig through forums. I cobbled together some older RAM and a CPU I had lying around, and it recognized everything immediately without any weird compatibility hiccups. The built-in video output saved me from hunting down a spare GPU just to get it booted, which was a nice little bonus. It's been humming along 24/7 for a few weeks now running a media server and a couple VMs without a single crash or reboot. Really pleased with how stable and fuss-free the whole experience turned out.

Been using these for six months now, tossed in my bag daily, and they've held up great, no cracks, no loose hinges. The noise cancelling still works like day one, and that ambient switch is a lifesaver on my commute. Battery life hasn't dipped either. Honestly surprised how sturdy they feel for the price. Solid purchase.

I was surprised at how heavy air could be! The product also comes from Sam's Club, so don't be confused when you receive a Sam's Club package at your doorstep. Nonetheless, I'm pleased to be able to purchase these in bulk. I was worried one can would run out in the middle of cleaning my computer fans (the main purpose I made this purchase), so I figured it would be best if I had extra at hand. The instructions are quite clear on the bottle--no shaking of the product before or after use, can will get cold throughout duration of use, be careful not to be in direct contact of the compressed air as it can cause frost bite, etc. Lastly, every single can--and I received four just as pictured on the product description--has their own red tube for hard to reach places. One can was also sufficient in cleaning up my macbook pro computer fans with plenty of air to spare!

Does not have the infrared leds as was pictured. Has 9 hours of recording time using a 32GB SD class 10. I like the layout of the function buttons, much easier to navigate than some competitors cams. This will make a useful addition to my video / audio recording capabilities. Thanks again Amazon for quality and value.
I've already told a few coworkers about this, perfect for anyone who juggles files between a laptop and phone daily. Super handy little gadget for the price.

I honestly don't know what took me so long to get one of these remotes! It is fantastic and setup was quite easy as well. After my initial remote setup I had to plug it into the iMac one more time to make a setting adjustment. That is all it took, and everything is working fine. For large fingers the center of the remote could be a little cumbersome. The buttons seem a little close to me and I have medium sized hands. That is the only tiny issue. The remote emits a stronger signal than all the original remotes is seems! I can point it anywhere and it will do the job. DVR controls work perfectly. The button layout is different ( DVR remote) but took me a few minutes to figure them out and get use to it. I purchased the Refurbished remote. Why pay more its JUST a remote! It looks brand new to me!

I have 2 new Megabooms, and saw that they had a new offer to sell refurbished ones, so I bought 2 more. One is fine, the other is blown, right out of the box. So obvious, I can't believe anyone would say it was even close to "refurbished". I am sending that one back, and ordering another refurb, so I will post an update when received. They are a great value for the quality. My rating is really for the obviously failed product being shipped without apparently checking to see it if worked.

I picked up the X-Mini Happy for casual listening around the house and on short walks. The little pull-string pouch is a nice touch, and having the included SD card and basic earbuds means you're ready to go right away. Sound-wise, it's decent for spoken word and acoustic stuff, but it struggles with anything bass-heavy, things get muddy fast above half volume. Build quality feels okay, not premium, but fine for the price. I'd recommend it to someone who wants a simple, grab-and-go speaker for podcasts or background music and isn't fussy about deep sound. If you're an audiophile or want room-filling volume, look elsewhere.
Had this thing for almost a year now and it's still rock-solid, no hiccups even after a few accidental bumps on shoots.
Fit my TP-1a perfectly. This is as good quality as the original ones that came with my (then) $150 headset (2009). Great stuff for the price!

I've had this case on my tablet for about four months now, and it's held up way better than I expected. I toss it in my bag daily, and the corners haven't frayed or peeled at all. The magnetic closure still snaps firmly, and the stand function works like day one, no wobbling or slipping, even on uneven surfaces. I was worried the frosted back would scratch up over time, but it still looks almost new. My cat knocked the tablet off the couch twice, and the case absorbed the impact perfectly; no damage whatsoever. Honestly, for the price, I thought I'd be replacing it by now, but it's proven to be really solid. Super happy with this purchase.
Came quickly and works exactly as described. The price is right too. If you need a USB to Printer parallel cable, this is the one.

I wasn't sure what to expect at first, but this thing has totally exceeded what I imagined. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and it paired instantly with my mesh network without a hiccup. The motion detection is way more precise than my old floodlight cam, fewer false alerts from passing cars or stray cats. I especially appreciate how clean and modern it looks mounted by the front door; it blends in perfectly. The speaker and mic clarity during two-way talk surprised me the most. Honestly, I thought I'd be tweaking settings for days, but it just worked right out of the box.

Just picked this up and honestly, it's been a solid addition to my setup. Hookup took maybe two minutes straight to the TV, and the picture quality is crisp with zero lag. I'm not super techy, but navigating the menus feels intuitive. Streaming apps load fast and haven't crashed once. Sound sync is spot on too. For the price, I'm genuinely impressed, it just works right out of the box. Great little device.

For the price, I expected way more. The plastic feels cheap and flimsy, like it could crack if you look at it wrong. Fitting it onto my MacBook Air was a pain, the bottom half doesn't sit flush and there's a weird gap near the hinge that catches dust instantly. The little fold-out feet are a joke; they're wobbly and make the whole thing slide around my desk. I get that it's a budget case, but at this price point there are way better options that actually protect your laptop instead of just making it harder to use. Honestly, I'm already looking for a replacement after two weeks.

LOVE THIS calculator!!! all the functions OMG~~~ one thing tho, make sure that your kids don't get it. it will ruin them. Only get it if you understand the math behind the functions that you are looking to use., if not, it makes life too easy. which, it is handy, but deters learning

I grabbed this Sherwood RX-4109 mainly because I was tired of overcomplicated AV receivers just to drive a pair of bookshelf speakers in my workshop, and it's been perfect. The simplicity is exactly what I wanted, big, easy-to-use knobs for volume, bass, and treble right on the front, no menu diving. What really sold me though is the dedicated phono input. I dusted off my old Technics turntable and it sounds warm and full without needing a separate preamp cluttering up the bench. The radio pulls in stations cleanly too, even with just a basic wire antenna in a basement. For the price, the clean power and dead-simple layout make it an absolute steal if you just want solid two-channel sound.
I love how easy it is to clip and share those clutch gaming moments. The built-in mic recording captures my reactions perfectly without needing extra gear.

I grabbed this hoping it'd breathe some life into my ancient LaserJet 4L without a ton of fiddling, and I'm honestly shocked. Plugged it into my router's USB port, the printer popped right up on my home network, no driver discs, no weird IP configs. Windows 10 and my MacBook both found it instantly. I expected a headache with such old hardware, but it just works. Feels like I cheated retirement for a tank of a printer.

As most of the others have said, this lens is awesome. I got it and with in the first 10 minutes I was taking some of the best portrait shots I have taken to date. If you have the $ even if you need to borrow it, get this lens
Great product! Put into my first build. I'm pleased with the results.

I purchased this as my backup/office adapter for you Yoga 13. The power brick is not as thin as the original adapter, but it is still pretty smart. It has worked perfectly with my unit. I would highly recommend getting this if you do not already have a backup to original adapter. If you already have an older Lenovo adapter or third-party multi-piece AC adapter, I would recommending checking out Patuoxun Power Adapter Cable for ThinkPad X1 Carbon 0B47046 UltraBook PC (this is the Amazon title for the product). This will likely meet your needs and save you money. I know it says it works for another specific computer, but you can check out the reviews to see it is universal to other Lenovo laptops.

Used this to splice join together two audio cables and started to wonder why my music sounded so strange. A little bit of testing showed that this coupler was outputting a mono signal instead of a stereo signal. Both speakers were getting sound but it was from the same channel, the other channel was being left behind. I had to go out to an electronics store to get one that worked correctly.
Snagged this open-box deal and it's been flawless for daily streaming. I'd tell any casual movie buff or budget-conscious gamer to grab one without hesitation.

Love the headphones. Very easy to operate (as I would expect most these days would be!) and the sound quality, for someone who's not a "pro," is really great. I like how the speakers themselves swivel so that they will fit perfectly. Very impressed and I would recommend to anyone.
Absolutely a nice cover for the screen and easy to apply and has stayed on with out showing any bubbles
Holds my smaller speedlite just fine, but for the cost I expected it to handle a heavier flash without slipping. Okay for light setups.
Had to pull one end out a little bit to make connection

This thing is surprisingly solid for the price. Holds my old GPS unit steady on bumpy roads, though the mount clip feels a bit flimsy. I expected a tighter lock, but it's never actually slipped off. Decent value overall.
For the price, it handles my phone and watch perfectly, no complaints at all.
This will work exactly as I wanted it to. Nice and easy. I woud have liked a couple more keys, or some way to order more keys, but I'm still pleased

I can't believe an item can work so well and cost almost nothing. This ring is solid, well built and fits my 77mm lenses properly. I am using it on my Canon 70-200 Mark II lens and Canon 17-40 wide angle. I bought it because my 24-70 lens is 82mm and I decided to buy filters that are 82mm in size. With this baby I can now use my 82mm filters on both of my 77mm lenses and thus, I save money in the long run. No need to buy expensive rings. This is the one. Do it now and don't look back.

After 4 months! Sometimes I go several days without using it. When I do use it, it may be for only 5 minutes or up to 90 minutes. But, its got enough hours on it that I was VERY impressed at the battery life. I use it 100% for podcasts. Usually it sits in the cup holder of my truck to listen to while driving or I use it while doing household work like washing dishes or putting away laundry. For these tasks, it works awesome! It pairs very easily to my ipod touch 4 as well as my iphone 5. It is an excellent product for the way I use it. A negative is when I paired it with my Vizio TV I was unable to adjust the sound. The speaker has no sound adjustments and my TV volume would not work with it. Also, I don't know why some people rave over the cup, seems like about a 50 cent cup to me:/ Update: I started using it all through the night to play soothing sounds for my infant to sleep. I estimate it played 32 hrs before needing recharged.

This MartinLogan Motion 30 hasn't gotten its requisite break in hours, and already I am in love. The highs are remarkable, and the music sounds as if the band was at my house. This is the best speaker I own, flanked by two MartinLogan LX16s, with a couple of Polks at the side. I like the Polks a lot, but MartinLogan has really impressed me with this trio, and the Motion 30 is the STAR! Sure, it was a small investment, but I am hearing parts of the music that I swear I didn't know were there! I'm really enjoying my audio system with this speaker as the center piece. I recommend it.
The ear cushions are super soft for long calls, but the volume knob feels a bit flimsy sometimes.
Works exactly as I'd hoped once I sorted the settings. Not quite plug-and-play, but solid performance for the price.

Bought this to hook up an old DVD player to a TV that only has a coaxial input. Setup was straightforward and the picture came through clear enough for casual viewing. A bit of signal noise on darker scenes, but for the price it does exactly what I needed.

Works perfectly with my iMac. I have the wireless mouse, external hd plugged in all the time and use the other two ports as input from other things such as my MP3 player and it works for everything. My old hub was powered but had begun to short out but this works just as I had hoped.

For the price, it's decent, but the build feels a little flimsy. I've had some minor connection hiccups too. If you're on a tight budget and just need basics, it's okay, just don't expect premium quality.
Ummmmm... this actually didn't work with my Mac. Didn't work with Yosemite. Gave it away to someone who had Windows. Maybe they fixed the problem but I don't have it anymore.
Arrived a day early in a sturdy little box. Popped batteries in, and it paired instantly with zero hassle.
I've been using this daily for weeks and it just works perfectly every time.

I was very disappointed to find that there is NO Vimeo app available, as it has been for previous models. I am hoping this is just a temporary issue since this model just hit the market. Unfortunately the answers from Sony are pretty much "...thanks for asking, no details...wait and see..." Therefore, I would not recommend this player if you need Vimeo. *** three month update *** Still no Vimeo! No response from Sony support or user sites. Not good. With moderate use, we have found that it does not crash as frequently as the previous year's model. So we don't have to pull the plug quite as often. Yes, there is no other re-boot or recovery option other than pulling the plug. Not good. Rating now downgraded to two stars. My advice: forget Blu-Ray, get Roku, absolutely top-notch engineering and solid software. Streaming support is blazing fast, no startup lag, quality just as good as the Sony. I've been a die-hard Sony fan since my first spectacular Trinitron TV in the 70's. It's been a sad downhill slide in the past decade, I can see why they are losing mega-bucks.

I grabbed these mainly for my daily bus commute, and overall they've been a solid little workhorse. The sound is clear enough for podcasts and acoustic stuff, though bass lovers might want a bit more thump. I actually appreciate that they don't seal out every single noise, I can still hear stop announcements and traffic, which keeps me aware without blasting volume. The cable is seriously generous, almost too long for just walking around, but it's great when my phone is buried in a backpack. They've held up well getting tossed in my jacket pocket, no tangling nightmares. For the price, they do exactly what I needed without fuss.

Package showed up in a torn padded envelope that barely covered the spool. The cable itself was loose inside, kinked in three places straight out of the box. No reel, no twist tie, just stuffed in there. The outer jacket had scuffs and what looked like greasy fingerprints all over it. First impression was honestly embarrassing, like I'd grabbed something from a clearance bin behind a warehouse. Didn't even bother plugging it in, just boxed it back up for a return. If they can't pack it right, I don't trust what's inside.

Used this on my first build. Very quality product and included hardware. Looks good. The Included fans look great and are very quiet. I had plenty of room for my equipment on the inside. Lot of room for additional fans. I added 2 more and found magnetic dust shields to fit over the unused open fan holes. Corsair makes quality products. Highly recommend it.

The box showed up a day early which was a nice surprise, though the outer packaging was a little scuffed from transit. Inside, everything was snug in molded pulp trays so no damage at all. First impression pulling it out, clean matte finish, feels substantial without being bulky. The quick-start card is actually useful, not the usual tiny folded gibberish. I had it unboxed and mounted in about fifteen minutes. The antennas snap on with a satisfying click and the port layout is logical. Only knocking off one star because the power brick is comically large and blocks adjacent outlets, which feels like a design oversight. Otherwise, solid first experience right out of the gate.
These drives run VERY HOT - almost to the point of being uncomfortable to touch. This causes me to be concerned about their longevity.
I love how clean this made my desk setup look. The brushed nickel plate blends right into the baseboard and the opening is wide enough for a chunky HDMI cable with no snags.

I purchased this to audit my office wireless network and it was well worth every penny. Using the Alfa AWUS036H I was able to decrypt our 128-bit WEP key within 45 minutes on the first attempt, and a mere 20 on the second attempt. I knew WEP was insecure, but even though the AP was set to use MAC address filtering and no broadcast SSID, I was able to collect enough information to decrypt the WEP key and authenticate to the network. Needless to say, the office wireless has since been upgraded with an access point that supports WPA-PSK2 with AES. WEP offers little to no security, and this device can help you prove that to anyone making purchasing decisions, or even to yourself if you're a skeptic. The Alfa AWUS036H is also good to carry around if you travel a lot and need the extra power and range (hotel wireless networks typically have a high noise threshold) to get a more stable signal. I use it with a 6 ft. USB extension cable to find the best location for signal reception. This device provides better reception than wireless adapters costing several dollars more and is an indispensable tool for wireless security auditing, I highly recommend it. NOTE: One thing that was not clear to me was what was contained in the package and this item does come with the suction cup holder, which is not a bonus item as another vendor's listing would seem to indicate.

But it isn't obnoxious. Sort of thing that people just sort of 'discover' sitting there. And it works - I desperately needed more USB slots, and this worked. Not a problem with how it functions. So it is interesting, people comment on it (or don't, which sorts out Who Knows What), and it works without a problem.
Never could get this thing to work right. I hook it up to all the right connections; but get no response on the monitor. It has been added to my dust bin of useless parts.
Able to shop online for this product, order and when it arrived it was exactly what I wanted and needed to back up old pc files.
Works okay outdoors, but the odd shape catches wind and feels flimsy. The mounting ring loosens over time. Not terrible for the price, just wish it fit more securely and didn't twist off so easily.

This is a great little speaker. I was impressed at how loud it got. Sure, it doesn't have the same low end and clarity that a $300 speaker does, but this thing is great at this price point. I was able to turn it on and connect my phone right out of the box. I was playing music within 10 seconds of opening the box. I was really impressed with the solid, sturdy feel and especially like the rubbery texture. It feels much more expensive than it actually is. It also has an auxiliary port which comes in handy if you happen to have an older mp3 player or something that doesn't have bluetooth support. I don't expect I'll ever need to use it, but it is a nice addition. I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest review.

I've tried a handful of screen protectors over the years, and this Skinomi one is hands-down the easiest install I've ever done. The wet application method freaked me out a little at first, but it actually made lining everything up super forgiving, zero bubbles, no dust trapped underneath. After it cured overnight, the clarity is flawless and touch sensitivity feels exactly like the bare screen. I'd genuinely recommend this to anyone who's clumsy with their phone, parents handing a tablet to kids, or outdoor folks who deal with sand and grit. It's saved my screen from at least three nasty scuffs already, and I can't tell it's even there.

I was able to watch my beloved Baltimore Ravens on my laptop while sitting in a parking lot Cody, Wyoming. 4-stars only because the picture quality could have been better, but, it worked as advertised.
Arrived fast in perfect packaging. Looks sleek and feels well-built right out of the box.
Dependable and affordable is a rare combination to find. But not in this product, which offers dependability and comfort to use. I strongly recommend it to every Photographer.
Exactly what I hoped for, charges fast, lasts just like the original, and fits snugly.

From my research and testing, this is the fastest USB3.0 thumb drive around (April 2012). Unfortunately, it's very bulky, so much in fact that there's a good chance it might block nearby USB ports. I don't understand why manufacturers do this; I can't imagine the internals on this are any bigger than any other USB3 thumb drive. Nevertheless, if you have USB3 and you're looking for speed, I'd recommend it.
Fits my Nikon snug and keeps it above water better than I figured, tested in a lake, not a drop inside.

This is a pretty nice, generic unit that operates as advertised. Packaging was adequate, arrived a little slower than I'm accustomed to (as a Prime member) because it was drop-shipped...but I'm just spoiled. This is the recorder and remote only, no cameras, mics, monitor or anything else. Just making sure you're paying attention! I've used a bunch of the "off the shelf" product from Swann and Q-See in different configurations, and am using some of their cameras with this recorder at home now. We're really pretty happy with it. Setting it up to see/change things over the internet would have been a LOT easier with better instructions...but eventually we prevailed. Be prepared to have somebody who is "web-savvy" available to help with this part of the job. Haven't really put it through any tough "paces"...we just look at the house, the cat, the street to make sure things are as we expect around the house. I'm planning to add a "front door cam", so we can see who's knocking.

They look tough and I may be a special case... I've had these got less than a month an use then when I ride my Harley... They are plugged into my phone sitting in my pocket. Occasionally I lean or put pressure on the jack, an now they are losing connection when moved so they are no longer useful in situations where my phone is getting moved around.
Love how compact it is, fits snugly behind my nightstand without blocking other outlets.

Got this for my home office so I could listen to podcasts while working. It does fine with the basic setup, but I had the same issue with any cable except the one that came in the box. Sound is decent for the size. I'd recommend it to someone who just needs a simple speaker and won't mess with extra cords.
The auto-dimming feature is a game-changer for night driving. No more blinding glare, and the install was surprisingly straightforward.

I've had mine for about four months now, and it's held up great through daily tosses into my backpack. No scuffs or connection issues so far. Knocking off one star because the plastic casing feels a bit slick in hand.

The box arrived surprisingly fast and with zero damage. Unwrapping it felt premium, no flimsy foam. First power-on was stunning; the size and clarity immediately hit me. Setup was a breeze. Honestly blown away right out of the box.

I've been using this little transmitter for a few weeks now and I'm honestly impressed. Shipping was super quick, it arrived two days early in a simple but sturdy box, no damage at all. The packaging was minimal, which I appreciate, and everything was easy to unpack without wrestling with plastic. First impressions were solid. It's smaller than I expected, which is great because it doesn't clutter up the dash. Setup took maybe a minute and the signal locked in clean right away. No static or fuss. I do wish the battery drain was a bit less noticeable on long trips, but keeping a charging cable handy solves that. For the price and hassle-free delivery, it's been a reliable little gadget.
Bought this hoping for a real desk fan, but it barely moves air. Feels cheaper than expected and the constant light is annoying at night.

I grabbed this mainly because I wanted a dedicated player that handles Rhapsody natively without any fuss, and it totally delivers on that front. Syncing my entire library and playlists was way smoother than I expected, and the interface, while not flashy, is straightforward enough to navigate on the go. Battery life holds up well for my daily commute and gym sessions. My only real gripe is that the screen could be a touch brighter when I'm outdoors, but it's not a dealbreaker. If you're deep into subscription music and tired of managing files manually, this thing gets the job done nicely.

I really wanted to like this thing for the lane guidance feature, which actually does a solid job showing which lane to be in on complicated highway interchanges. That's where the praise ends. The screen is so laggy I regularly miss turns because it freezes for a few seconds right when I need it most. Mounting it is a headache too, the suction cup gives out randomly, and I've had it drop onto the dash mid-drive twice. Voice prompts cut out at weird times, so you'll be cruising in silence wondering if you've gone off route. I've missed exits, gotten stuck in weird loops, and wasted way more time than it's saved. For the price, it should just work.
Lightening fast, slick design. Always been contented with Transcend products. No complaints here. Looking for a Ultra FAST USB 2.0 flash drive, then this is the one.
Been using this for a few weeks now and it fills the room nicely, just wish the surround effects had a bit more punch.

I was honestly surprised this drive beat the numbers on the box. It's snappy, runs cool, and boot times dropped by nearly half compared to my old SATA setup. I wasn't expecting such a smooth plug-and-play experience. Totally satisfied with the real-world speed and reliability right out of the gate.

Buyer beware when use with some USB-C to USB-C cable, you might get speed capped 40MB/s: ----------------Apr 26 2016 Update---------------- I changed my review score to 5 to reflect the additional tests I've done to clarify the speed cap myth with Macbook. After I consult with Apple forum, I get a answer that I might need a USB 3.0 USB-C to USB-c cable. So I did a search, and the ONLY cable mentioned USB 3.1 10Gb/s support is from Belkin: Belkin 3-Foot 3.1 USB Type C (USB-C) to USB Type C Cable, USB-C Certified And I tested with Macbook, it correctly recognized with USB 3.0 speed and pushed speed to it's limit. photo 7-8 So, in conclusion, If you want to achieve best performance with USB-C cable, make sure you have a USB-C cable support at least 5Gb/s transfer speed. And some cable like Apple's USB-C, Google's USB-C, Anker USB-C they only support 480Mb/s which is USB 2.0 standard for data transfer. ----------------Old Review---------------- I purchased it to used with my MacBook Retina 12' because of the USB-C support But there is a real problem for this drive to use with USB-C to USB-C cable: If you use it with Apple USB-C to USB-C cable, you only get USB 2.0 speed which is capped at 40MB/s in and out. Like the photo 1-3 show below. If you use it with the accompany USB-A to USB-C cable you will have USB 3.0 speed which is 400MB/s in 425MB/s out. But the inconvenience part is for MacBook you have to use a USB-C to USB-A adapter like the photo 4-6 shows. So in conclusion: if you purchased it for the USB-C convenience and universal support, you will disappointed with the speed seriously capped into USB 2.0 standard. For the best performance, you have to use the USB-A to USB-C cable, which will put it back to the same category of using a microUSB cable: you'll need adapter to make it work in full speed.

I loved the original Uncharted game when it came out. It was a really cool game, had a good storyline, good graphics, was challenging enough to keep me interested, and the overall game play was pretty sweet. My sister also loved it a lot. She thought it was one of the best games she's ever played. So when this one came out I decided to buy it for her. She has been trying to get the platinum trophy for PSN ever since.
Had it for months now and it's held up really well with daily use.

I've had this VCR for about eight months now, and while it worked fine initially, I'm starting to notice some wear. The playback is still decent, but the remote feels flimsy and sometimes doesn't register button presses unless I'm really close to the unit. I've also had a couple tapes get slightly chewed up during rewinding, which makes me nervous about using it with anything I care about. The plastic casing has developed a faint rattle, almost like something inside loosened up over time. It's not terrible for casual use, but I wouldn't count on it lasting years if you're regularly digging through old home movies. For the price, it's okay, just don't expect it to hold up like the tanks from decades ago.

Put two of these in my Synology NAS about 18 months ago and they've been running 24/7 without a hiccup. I've had cheaper drives degrade after a year, so I'm picky now. These just keep chugging along silently, no bad sectors, no weird vibrations. Smart stats still look clean. I push a lot of large video files through them weekly and haven't noticed any slowdown. Honestly, I forget they're even in there most of the time. For something that just needs to work long-term without babysitting, these deliver.

Arrived fast and packed in a sturdy little box with everything neatly bagged. Opened it up and the connectors feel solid, not flimsy at all. Hooked it up in minutes and it just works perfectly right out of the gate.

light enough for everyday school backpack. core i3 fast enough for everyday normal use. it is perfect for all student. Unless you are developing large application or hard gamer. This one is just right.

For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but these are surprisingly solid little workhorses. The silicone edges don't scratch my pans, and they whip through batter without bending. Plus, they rinse clean in seconds. Honestly feels like a steal compared to fancier brands I've tried before.

This armband is my go-to for running, mainly because it stays put and doesn't slide down my arm like others I've tried. The material breathes well enough that I'm not a sweaty mess underneath it, and my iPod nano fits snugly without feeling like I'm wrestling to get it in. The one thing that bugs me is the plastic screen cover, it gets kind of cloudy and makes swiping a bit sluggish, especially mid-run when I'm trying to skip a track without breaking stride. Still, for the price, it's a solid, no-fuss option that's lasted through plenty of miles.
The braided design makes it super durable, and it charges my phone crazy fast.
Got the SS one for better weathering. It went up like a snap and works perfectly as it should.
I expected decent cable management, but this totally exceeded that, my desk looks cleaner than I imagined and setup was surprisingly quick.

I am on my 3rd set. They work well until one of the wires break which only takes a few months. You may ask why I continue to buy them, well, the double the price alternatives don't last any longer, nor do they sound any better. The band on these fit my head better than the more expensive Sleep Phones as well.
Use these cards for iPods and they work great. Plenty of NAND flash space to load up the iPod with tunes.
this is just what I needed- inexpensive and short- if you have a coax t.. v. it is perfect for the splitter

My keyboard arrived a day early, which was a nice surprise. The box was sturdy with no dents, and inside everything was snug in molded foam, no rattling around during shipping. Pulling it out for the first time, it felt solid and had a nice weight to it, not flimsy at all. The keys have a soft matte texture that immediately felt comfortable under my fingers. Setup was literally plug-and-play, took maybe ten seconds before I was typing away. I was honestly impressed by how quiet the keystrokes are, much less clacky than my old board. After unboxing and using it for an hour, I'm already really happy with the purchase.

This mount looked okay out of the box but turned into a headache real fast. The arm joints never really lock tight no matter how much you crank the bolts, so after a few days the projector starts sagging and you lose the picture alignment. I've had to get the ladder out three times already to readjust. The included hardware is a grab bag of stuff that doesn't quite match up with the slots on my Epson, so I ended up drilling an extra hole in the ceiling plate just to make it work. For the price I expected something that actually holds position without constant babysitting. Save yourself the frustration and spend a bit more on a sturdier mount.

I grabbed this cable on a whim because the price was so reasonable for the gauge, and honestly it's been a steal. Ran it under the rug and along baseboards for my rear speakers, zero signal loss or static, even on a 60-foot stretch. The jacket is flexible enough to tuck into tight corners but doesn't kink, and stripping it took two seconds with basic wire cutters. My old thin wire was crackling at higher volume; this handles everything cleanly. For what it costs, the quality seriously overdelivers. Would buy again in a heartbeat.

The box arrived looking like it had been sat on, and inside the earphones were wedged into plastic so stubborn I nearly needed scissors to free them. First impression wasn't great, one earbud already had a faint scuff straight out of the package, which made me wonder if these were a return. Shipping took almost two weeks with zero tracking updates until the last day. Sound-wise, they're okay for casual listening, but the fit is awkward and they kept slipping out no matter which tip I tried. Bass has some punch, but everything else feels distant. Not terrible for the price, just underwhelming.
The box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked here. Opened it up and one leg was already bent. Instant regret.

This was purchase for my girlfriends new 2011 13.3 macbook air. When it was designed I doubt there was any test fitting or any testers before mass producing this. For there are some issues with the bottom piece. When it is fully clipped on the top part hits the screen lid when you lift open then screen which can damage and make marks on the lid. My second issues is the little kick feet. I found them very useful but one is very loose and when carrying or moving the macbook air it makes a annoying clicking noise. On the good note this is a good strong case, light weight, and keeps the thinness of the macbook air looking thin. Until they fix this issues I don't recommend this product due to the annoying flaws.

i bought these, they were fairly cheap (10 bucks if i remember correctly).. the sound was decent, a bit too bassy and unless you had the wire under a shirt, you'd hear it every step you took. within 6 weeks the first one's broke, i emailed skullcandy and they gave me a new set, just had to mail them the plug. within 2 weeks the second set broke.. i never tried for another pair.. so if your delicate, don't mind the chord under your shirt, and don't mind losing out on headfones every other month or so then buy them.
I bought these for my brother and he loves them! They are really loud too!

I've had this thing for a solid four months now, and I'm genuinely surprised by how well it's held up. I've dropped it on concrete twice, accidentally left it in my gym bag under a pile of wet towels, and even stepped on it once while rushing out the door. Still works like day one. The battery life hasn't degraded at all either, I charge it maybe twice a week with moderate daily use. Sound stays crisp, and people on calls say I come through super clean even when I'm outside with wind blowing. The little rubber coating shows zero peeling. Honestly, for something I expected to be fragile, this thing has taken a beating and just keeps going.
Fits and looks great. Have difficulty touching screen on corners though.

I really wanted to like this thing, but it completely let me down. From the moment I plugged it in, something was off, it would randomly cut out and refuse to turn back on unless I unplugged everything and waited. For the price, I expected at least basic reliability, not something that feels like a cheap knockoff. The build quality looks okay in photos, but in person it's flimsy and the buttons feel mushy. I've used similar devices at half the cost that worked flawlessly out of the box. Honestly, it's more hassle than it's worth and I regret not going with a trusted brand instead. Total waste of time.
Used this to replace a sensor that had gone bad on a Heath Zenith outdoor light. Works perfectly as described. The dual-bright function is a great feature.

Picked this up over a year ago to drive a couple extra door speakers in my old truck. It's been rattling around under the seat since then, covered in dust and taking the occasional kicked soda can, still powers on every time. Sound is clean enough, nothing mind-blowing but totally solid for the price. Had a little ground loop buzz early on, rerouted the wiring and it's been quiet ever since.

Arrived in a plain bubble mailer that had me a little worried, but the card itself was in a sturdy anti-static clamshell inside, so no damage at all. Shipping was fast, ordered late Tuesday, showed up Thursday morning. First impression out of the box was solid, nice board feel, no weird flux residue or bent pins. Popped it into my home server and everything lit up like expected. I'm knocking off one star just because the packaging felt a bit barebones for electronics, and there was zero documentation in the box, not even a QR code to a setup guide. For the price though, no real complaints, just manage your expectations on the unboxing experience.

I got this to plug my turtle beach headsets into directly for source audio. (px4,xp400) Sounds great, pictures just fine. a must for headset users that need an optical port, ps4 picks up ddigital perfect and no sound lag, which happens with tvs to often, and dint have to keep switching from tv to headset sound on ps3.
Installed this memory into a mid-2010 MacBook Pro. Cheap price and quick installation gave my MacBook Pro new life in five minutes.

I bought this computer back in September with the 30 guarantee and when the computer was delivered without the keyboard and mouse, JD NETWORK SOLUTION LLC was quick to resolve the problem. The computer has so far been great, runs great, runs Windows 7 great however, the copy of Windows 7 is NOT GENUINE. Perhaps there is a reason JD NETWORK SOLUTION LLC isn't selling anything on Amazon anymore. Now I'm on the hook for buying a new Windows license.

Bought this thing a few weeks ago and honestly didn't expect much for the price, but man was I wrong. The Skyfi3 delivers way more than it costs. Super compact, slips right into my pocket without any bulk, and the battery lasts longer than I figured it would. I use it mostly while doing yardwork and it cuts through the mower noise without having to max out the volume. Signal holds steady even under trees which surprised me. I've already clipped it to my visor in the car and taken it on a couple jogs, no issues. If you're on the fence, just grab it, solid build, great sound, and a steal for what you get.

This charging stand is a desk game-changer. The magnetic alignment is spot-on so I never fumble around, and the weighted base keeps it rock-solid. I mostly love how the soft LED indicator is dim enough to not bug me while sleeping. Seriously clean setup.
This router ended my lag issues for good. Setup took minutes and it just works.

Worked fine for short-range use in my small apartment, pairing was quick and voice came through clear enough for calls. But just past the one-year mark, it started randomly disconnecting mid-conversation. I'd only suggest this to someone who needs a temporary, ultra-budget headset and doesn't mind replacing it soon after.

I grabbed this GPS for a cross-country road trip, and it honestly saved me from more than a few wrong turns. The screen is bright and responsive, and I love that it recalculates routes almost instantly when you miss an exit. I'm not super techy, but the menu layout felt intuitive right away, no digging through manuals. The lane guidance feature was a lifesaver in heavy city traffic, and the spoken street names kept my eyes on the road. I'd absolutely recommend this to new drivers, seniors, or anyone who just wants a straightforward, no-fuss navigation tool without relying on a phone. It's simple, reliable, and does exactly what it promises.

The box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, crushed corner and a torn flap. Inside, the hub was just rattling around loose, no real padding at all. First thing I noticed pulling it out was a scuff on the top edge, so much for "new." Plugged it in and the connection dropped twice before I even moved anything. The front-left USB ports feel wobbly too, like they're barely soldered in place. Not confidence-inspiring when you're plugging in an expensive external SSD. Honestly, between the shipping damage and the flimsy build right out of the gate, I'm not even comfortable leaving it on my desk. Sending it back.

This unit has a very clean low distortion rate and delivers very high quality sound, the only negitive is that is not automatic set-up and it is a little complecated to set-up. This unit makes a very good addition to a home theater and the Harman Kardon AVR-154 delivers very good sound for theater and music buffs. I would highly recomend it to anyone looking for a low cost but very effective system. I was and is a much better system than Sony,Phillips, Magnavox and others in it's price range.
For the price, it gets the job done. Just needed a tiny bit of tweaking.

Wasn't sure a mount this cheap could actually hold steady, but it's rock-solid on bumpy roads. Snaps in tight, zero wobble, and the release is smooth. Honestly outperforms pricier ones I've tried. Super impressed.

I picked this up for a hiking trip and it completely delivered. The image quality is crisp and the autofocus snaps right on, even in low light. I'm not a pro, just someone who likes capturing moments, and the controls felt intuitive from day one. Battery life held up better than I expected through long days outdoors. Genuinely happy with it.

Works perfect with Bose headphones (quiet comfort 3). The cable looks like is good quality and the I love the fact that the 3.5mm connection is a straight connector so it is compatible with my iPhone 6S that has a bulky charging case.

This little stand has been a lifesaver for my kitchen tablet use. It's sturdy, compact, and grips any device I throw at it. I'd easily recommend it to anyone who follows recipes or does video calls hands-free.

This battery works great! I'm so glad that it's still available for purchase. My old Coolpix battery died 6 years ago and purchasing a replacement back then was really expensive. I'm so glad prices have come down and I get to use my old camera again!
Good case. Better than expected. Strongly recommend. Would definitely buy again. Believe this to be a sound purchase.

Arrived a day early in solid, no-frills packaging. Box was pristine, everything snug. Took it out, felt sturdy right away and paired instantly with my tablet and phone. Honestly impressed so far. Just hoping the battery life holds up as promised long term.

Great idea, but way too weak of a light to use anywhere except a pitch black roan. Not practical for slide shows in a club or office environment. I returned it and spent four times as much on an entry model office projector.

This GPS served me fairly well (although my first update seemed to mess up its routing) for three years. It routed in basic fashion, and did not surprise me too much. The selection to favor back roads was especially good, and the POIs were quite extensive. Then I was recently ready to go on a long trip through the southern US and up the east coast. I was packed, turned it on and then NOTHING but the Magellan intro screen hanging there. System reset did not fix anything, and then it turned itself off. In short, this a defective unit after three years of only occasional use. I stopped at a Walmart on the way of my trip and chose a TomTom Start 40M, which is a very nice unit that was up and running in 5 minutes. A bit quirkier than the Magellan, POIs are not as good, but it is quite smart in its routing. I expect electronics to last a lot more than three years. No wonder there are so many refurbished 1412s out there! One star ONLY for lack of durability, but that is BIG.

I've had many screen protectors for my various MP3 players and cell phones which gives me lots of basis for comparison. I feel that this 3M protector is as good as any I've tried, it fits the iPad well. I personally received two of them, which is good because it usually takes me two tries to get a protector on there perfectly. The instructions were very specific and clear -- better than other products I've tried. Overall, I recommend this for use with your iPad!

I needed a long extension and a good quality cable and this fits the bill. It actually sounds better than the 3 ft one it replaced that cost 5 times as much!!! Thank you parts express. And PS, any cable will break if you stress it. Please be fair when rating these cables.

Got these for my son and daughter, but the ear cushions are way too stiff. Sound is fine for the price, but after ten minutes they both complained about soreness. Total waste since they won't touch them now.
Bought it hoping it'd just work, but it's basically a brick now.
This stuff is pretty cool. I heat a knife up with a propane torch to cut it.makes a job look complete.

I've used the Skycaddie SG3 for a full season now and the course availability really surprised me, even small local courses popped right up. The green mapping is the standout for me; knowing the exact shape and depth from the fairway has saved me from so many misjudged approach shots. Battery life is solid, easily lasting 36 holes. My one gripe, and the reason I can't go five stars, is the screen glare in direct sunlight. Midday rounds mean I'm often cupping my hand over the display to read yardages. A slightly brighter or less reflective screen would make a huge difference. Still, it's reliable, accurate, and keeps my pace of play moving.
They have worked better than expected. Good value and quality;

Upgraded my gaming rig with this PSU and it's been flawless. Installation was straightforward, cables are flexible enough to manage easily, and it runs dead quiet even under load. Zero coil whine or weird power fluctuations. Honestly, it just works and I don't have to think about it, exactly what you want from a power supply.

This desktop does the basics okay for the price, but the version of Windows that came installed flagged as non-genuine after a few weeks. I'd only suggest it to someone who just needs a temporary setup for light browsing or kids' homework and doesn't mind sorting out the software on their own. Not something I'd trust for daily work.

The PDF manual has some great examples. I am linking 3 buildings and using VLAN, Storm protection and STP. I've only had for a couple days. My primary reason for this switch is to help isolate SIP phones at a small business. Seems to work great so far. Gives me just the right level of control of my network. I will update my review after I have used for a few months. But others complained the unit was loud? Not mine. Maybe they are using different fans now? I cannot even tell it is turned on. So happy that way....
Camera is garbage. Good reviews are fake. Old Chinese trick that doesn't fly anymore.
This is the remote that should have come with the TV, like the keyboard for doing searches on YouTube. Would recommend this remote to Vizio "smart TV" owner who didn't get one with their TV.
I love this keyboard! The different colors available and the easy to use light level finger spin knob is great. Key response is nice, solid construction, GREAT price! Totally satisfied!
Way bigger than I thought it'd be, but the quality feels solid so far.

I wanted to like this thing way more than I actually did. Setup was okay, and it chugged along fine for the better part of a year on my home theater PC. Then it started dropping connections randomly, usually right when I sat down to watch something. Restarting would fix it temporarily, but that got old fast. The network streaming was the whole reason I bought it, so when it constantly flakes out, it defeats the purpose. Signal quality was decent when it worked, but the unreliability killed it for me. Ended up going back to a direct coax run just to have something stable. Not worth the headache for the occasional moments it actually cooperated.

I wanted to like this more than I do. The gold-plated connectors feel premium and the build quality is solid, so I figured it would easily match the nice braided cables I've used before. In actual use, though, the stiffness really surprised me. Instead of draping naturally from my phone, it forms a stubborn loop that pushes against my hand while I'm trying to type or scroll. It works fine electrically and never cuts out, but the inflexibility makes everyday handling awkward. If you mostly leave your phone on a desk while charging, it's fine. For anything else, it's a step down from what I expected.
I am using this for a door stop now. count it up to lesson learned and just bought a new tri-pod.
Bought this for a Ryzen 5000 build thinking it'd be plug-and-play, but had nothing but boot failures. Really let down.
I expected these to be just another set of cards, but they've handled back-to-back shoots without a hiccup. Zero buffer lag and they read fast in my reader, genuinely impressed for the price.
These are pretty decent batteries and the price is good.

In my experience, acer has not always made the best devices. But this tablet honestly surprised me. I was looking at a surface pro 4 with similar specs but Acer was hundreds of dollars cheaper and included the keyboard and pen and the fold out stand is nice so I thought I would give it a shot and it's been great. It's fast, has plenty of storage, and that 8gb of ram makes a big difference. I would recommend this over a surface pro 4 or a laptop.

I grabbed this keyboard mostly for light browsing and note-taking, and honestly it does the job for that. The keys have a soft, almost spongy press that I've actually come to like during long typing sessions, it's kind on the fingers. I'd recommend it to students or anyone who just needs a basic, affordable keyboard for everyday tasks like email, essays, or casual web surfing. It's not a powerhouse for shortcuts or power users, since the function row has a mind of its own at times, but if you're not leaning on those keys heavily, it's a non-issue. Setup was instant, no dongles or pairing headaches. For the price, it's a solid little workhorse if you keep expectations in check.

My last camcorder was sharp mini camcorder and I hate to buy tapes. This camcorder sounds like a perfect fit. I was pretty impressed with the quality of this when I saw someone took some video with this camera, and it was downsized too. For $299 at amazon, I thought why not jump into the deal and I brought a 8gb sdhc card also. I first took some video indoors at the highest settings and I noticed some noise when I took it, even with the flourscent light on. Okay, I know about other reviewers who has problems taking videos in low light. I took some pictures at the highest settings with the flash on and the pictures has alot of noise also. Even most cameras with a flash can take decent pictures. Took the camera outside on a bright sunny day with the highest settings and the videos didn't come out what I expected. It was kind of noise when I took it and it wasn't as good as one of the reviewers showed. The video quality was almost the same as my old minidv camcorder. However, I looked at the pictures and it came out fine. Then again, most other 7.1 digital camera should have no trouble taking pictures outside in a sunny day also. I guess that I can't complain too much since I paid $300 for this camcorder but maybe if the quality is as good as advertised (even outdoors) I would give it 4 stars or more.

I bought this hoping for a simple hands-free setup, but it's a total disaster. The grip mechanism is absurdly aggressive, metal prongs just bite straight into your phone frame. Mine now has a visible gouge along the edge. For something that's supposed to hold, not destroy, your device, this thing fails spectacularly. Genuinely regret the purchase.
great look for non power cables to go in and out of the wall. i have one of these behind each hanging tv in my house and a few sonos cat 5 cables

The scroll wheel feels really precise and satisfying, which I love for long documents. Unfortunately, the side buttons are way too easy to press accidentally. I kept hitting them just moving my thumb normally, which got annoying fast. Build quality seems fine for the price otherwise, but that design choice really messes with daily use. Just okay overall.

Works as advertised for cutting down glare on my monitor, but the installation process is a bit fiddly. I'd recommend this to anyone working near a bright window or in an office with harsh overhead lighting, it makes a noticeable difference. The included accessories are just okay; I ended up using a plastic card instead of their tiny squeegee to get the bubbles out, and a proper microfiber cloth from my glasses kit worked better for prep. Getting the alignment right takes patience since you're dealing with multiple protective films. Once it's on, though, the matte finish is easy on the eyes and doesn't distort the image. Good value for the price if you're willing to deal with the learning curve.
Showed up in two days, box was pristine, these feel solid.

I gave this as a gift, but we all enjoy it. The frame has handy features and is easy to use. One thing I didn't realize before purchasing was EXACTLY how one got the photos on the frame. I saw the option to insert a memory card so I assumed there would be a USB cable for transfer. This isn't the case. You need to put the photos on the memory card. I didn't have a card reader, so I went to Wal-Mart and bought a 'holder' for my 4GB card for [..]. That essentially turned it into a 4GB USB memory stick. Now I just plug that into my computer, add photos, and return it to the frame. I enjoyed the included Kodak software. It made it fast and easy to edit my pics, but it caused some incompatibility issues and I had to delete it. If it will work for you, it is well worth installing. I use my regular graphics program now. The frame screen size of 5X7 sounds smallish, but actually is just fine. We have it on a stand that is only about 4 ft. from the one chair and maybe 10 ft. from the other that we use regularly and the frame can be viewed fine. We can also see pics easily as we walk past this area repeatedly. I love that we can see our pics all the time now (or from 1p to 9P with the built in timer). Before I would have to sit at the computer and look at them, which is nice too, but I seldom did it. You can tell I love this frame, the good thing is that the recipient loves it too and has been so happy to have it.

I expected a solid beginner camera but this thing punches way above its weight. The image sharpness and low-light performance surprised me straight out of the box. Menus are intuitive, build feels reassuringly sturdy. Honestly, it's made me excited to learn photography all over again.

I give this 2 stars as I am not just throwing it out. What I wish someone told me before I wasted $9.95 on this: It is small enough but for it's size the level of light is very poor. To give you something to compare it to, If I need more light I would prefer the flashing light app on my iPhone, that is brighter. So what is this good for? If I need a little night lite I can turn this on and not worry about waking up my wife where my iPhone may be a little too bright. I does serve a purpose, I am rating low because it does not serve the purpose for which I purchased it and wish someone else posted this over the 5 star reviews I read. Maybe they used to be brighter and better but in January 2015 this is little more than a child's toy.

I can cautiously recommend trying this router provided you can return it if you have problems. I have the dreaded (by some) A1 hardware revision, but without the problems others mention. This was the cheapest N router around at the time. The router set-up is easy if you are familiar with routers. However, do not rely on the wizard provided and be prepared to set it up manually. I will not fault D-link to much for that, as in my experience automatic router setup programs from Linksys and Netgear did not work any better either. Make sure you upgrade firmware immediately to the latest available. It now remains to be seen how long this router will stay working. So far, I have no reason to regret buying it.

***UPDATE: December 10th, 2016 - Now that the price dropped to $260, plus $20 rebate, I take back everything I said about the RX 480. Because now, you're essentially paying the same price, for up to 50% more power. The compromise is the increased power draw and electricity bill, but if you're a PC gamer, you don't really care about it too much. Performance is the ultimate goal, and this card delivers in spades. It is the perfect balance of price/performance - $150 cheaper than a GTX 1070, for about 80-90% of the performance. That's amazing. **UPDATE: August 6th, 2016 - What's even more amazing is that as of right now Amazon is only selling it for $310!** This is amazing deal, compared to what the other R9 Fury's that are selling on Amazon. It's a little too little, a little too late, as the RX 480's and the the GTX 1060's are already here, with a better price per performance ratio. In a sandbox, an R9 Fury for $350 is a great deal, so that's why I'm giving it give stars. But in the real world, you would be better off to get two Rx 480 4GB's, and only pay $50 more for about 1.5x the performance.
