I hope this review helps - good luck!
Arrived super fast and packed with care. Opened it up and it looked even nicer in person, instant love at first glance.

Overall, I really like this case. The rubberized outside feels smooth and sturdy and the pattern is nice, although not glossy as others have mentioned, which is fine with me. It feels like the case will be strong enough to protect my laptop from superficial scratches and such, but I'm not sure that it would prevent more severe damage with major impact, grantedt I don't expect much more given the very affordable price of the product. The keyboard cover is thinner than I imagined, but to be fair, I've never had a keyboard cover for my laptop so my only comparison was my imagination. It also has some creases in it from the way it was folded and packaged, so hopefully those will work themselves out with time and use. One of the main reasons I purchased this case was for the keyboard cover so that it would reduce the clicking sound while I take notes in class and it definitely achieves that.
It did its job for what i wanted. from the living room to the kitchen. Got another for the bed room too.

I grabbed this Canon grip for my R6 Mark II and honestly can't imagine shooting without it now. The extra heft balances out heavier lenses perfectly, especially my 70-200mm, and vertical shooting feels so natural with the secondary shutter and dials right where your fingers land. Build quality is exactly what you'd expect from Canon, seamless fit, weather sealing, zero wobble. I shot a three-hour dance recital over the weekend and swapping batteries was a breeze without ever taking my eye off the viewfinder. If you're on the fence, just get it. It turns an already great camera into a total workhorse.

I received this UPS a couple days ago and found it was fully charged when I received it. This is my first Tripp Lite UPS. I have been a loyal APC user for my entire computing life. I figured I would see if TL is any better. I was surprised that the battery was already hooked up and fully charged when the device arrived. I let it run for two days. First day had no load and second had a minimal load. Tonight I installed the unit in my media center. So far I have had no issues and no smell as reported by others. I currently use this UPS in my media center to keep a TV, sound system, CATV box and DVD running. Current full load is without the DVD and usage is at 37%. With everything turned off, I still see a draw of 9%. The only thing that is slightly annoying, and why I gave this item a 4-star review, is that I cannot figure out how to turn off the LCD display when I did not need it. It is a nice night light in the living room, but I would rather have the display go out automatically or even manually. I read documentation offline and online and found no clues. I sent an email to tech support but haven't heard anything after 3 days. Guess I will call their Chicago phone number and ask. Unfortunately I only remember to call after they close. Otherwise, the unit seems to be solid. Minus the inability to figure out how to turn off the display, I am happy with my purchase. Time will tell if I switch to Tripp Lite, try someone else or go back to APC.

Inexpensive,and works great. Very securely packed to prevent shipping damage. The instructions and data were spot on, including voltages at startup and at idle. It has multiple stage charging, going to an idle state when the battery is fully charged.

Great feature in that you can move the arm to another place with the second sleeve that can be put on a second wall. Put one sleeve in my kitchen and one by my TV in my livingroom - avoids having to buy two in order to have a stand in both places.
This bag is great! It is so cute! I also like that the front flap is velcro. Perfect for my needs!
I've been running this thing daily for months and it hasn't flinched once, solid as a rock.
Still going strong after months outside, rain or shine.

Seems to be well made. Terminals are slightly cheap on the inside but look like they will hold up fine. Installed a Skar 12 inch shallow sub in mine with some poly fill on the sides and the setup pounds hard!

This is the second APC 11 Surge Protector I have purchase. I loved this unit. It is well thought out and laid out. It has a nice, sturdy feel to it. I have a 40" Samsung HD LCD, a TiVo, a Dish DVR, a Phillips DVDr, a Wii, a iPod mini station, a DTV box, a subwoofer, an air purifier, a fan, and a VCR all hooked up to it. I also have my coax cable and my broadband cable hooked up to it. I haven't gotten any kind of interference and all the plugs fit neatly on the APC 11. I'm in the process of getting a few more electronic "toys" and needed another APC 11 for the additional plugs I will have. I made the choice on purchasing the APC 11 from research and reviews that I got off the web, and so far that info has been proven correct on the APC 11. I would definitely recommend the APC 11 Surge Protector to anyone needing a quality surge protector.
I've been using these for weeks now and they've honestly exceeded my expectations every time.

They are very nice crisp clear speakers with a thunderous bass. Definately a nice punch for the dolllars. I hear great things about this brand,(Boston Acoustics)but it was even more wonderful once I experienced the sound for myself. Definately a lovely addition.
Arrived super fast in perfect packaging, no dents on the box. Fired right up, bright and crisp right away.

I was honestly surprised by how sharp the display looks when reading comics or watching movies in bed. Colors pop really nicely and the screen gets bright enough for outdoor use without washing out. The battery life is solid too, I can easily go two days of casual browsing and streaming without hunting for the charger. My only tiny gripe is that some apps still feel stretched from the phone layout, but it's not a dealbreaker for me. Pairing a Bluetooth keyboard turned this into a surprisingly capable little work companion for note-taking on the go. Overall, super happy with the purchase and would definitely recommend it to anyone wanting a reliable media tablet without spending a fortune.

For what I paid, I expected flimsy cables that'd fray in a month. Nope, these feel solid, handle 4K without hiccups, and the connectors click in snugly. Perfect for my home setup. Honestly surprised by the build quality at this price point.
Works great as a mount in my car. I drive a Ford Focus 2012 SEL, and this mount works perfect to mount a camera or a phone above my GPS screen.
Arrived in perfect shape, feels premium right away.

For starters the head phones worked and were way better in staying in the ear when doing laps and flip turns. The packaging was great, and they came with all sorts of swap out buds for all different size ears. The problem and the reason I'm writing this review, is I noticed that about 2 weeks after receiving them and using them in my daily 1 mile swim, that one of the headphones was barely working. I thought it may be due to water getting in my ear and drowning out the noise. This wasn't the problem because the very next day the ear bud stop working permanently. I found that i can get the broken side to work if i bend the wire that plugs into the Ipod. So all in all, I'm very disappointed in this product you would think that for $40 (usd) you would get a pair of water proof headphones that would be built to last. I do understand that even normal headphones don't last forever!!! but they are like $10. Hope to hear back from the company. I will write an update review in a few weeks, in response to company actions. I would also like to note that im also deployed overseas and received these headphones with amazon prime in under a week, it was amazing.
it protects my ipod touch extremely well and you can't beat the price. highly recommend.

The product does not work at all. Try to use it with every USB flash drive that works in all my other USB devices but to no avail. No sign of life or sign of light for any USB mass storage device connected to it.
Perfect for anyone doing custom wiring or bench testing. My go-to gift for maker-space friends.

Purchased four of these camera's the info that comes with them sucks. This is the fifth one that I received the picture was blurry right out of the box. Never had a camera that was blurry right out of the box. I finally hooked it up a video camera, put it on my lap and played with the focus and zoom knobs. Nothing I did would bring it into focus. So I returned this one. I forgot which knob adjusts what, there are no directions to tell you. I didn't matter I played with it more a half hour and nothing worked, so back it goes. I got four good ones, so I reordered it. The four that I am using have been working for about a year and a half They are very clear and have very good night vision. The only problem you could have with these cameras is, that the night vision is so strong, that it might be too bright in close in setting.. Update; Got another one of these, they shipped it really fast. Perfectly focused in the widest position. Mounted it adjusted a little bit. Happy camper now. What makes a camera like this so good, is, that you can put them anywhere and make them work. It is very difficult to find a fixed lens 2.8 MM camera. So this does it all. A note. I've had one of these mounted out from under the eave where it is exposed to sun and the rain, no problems. It has been there for about a year and a half. The main weakness of this camera is, the IR coverage. I throws out an intense spotlight leaving the edges in darkness. I would say the coverage is about 2/3 of the area. I used this camera to replace a inexpensive fixed lens camera that fogs up after rain. But that camera covered the entire area in an even light.
Swapped out the rental unit, activated it online in minutes, and my speeds jumped noticeably. Super stable connection, no drops.

The box arrived looking like it'd been drop-kicked across the warehouse, corner crushed, inner plastic scuffed. First power-on, the tray sounded a little grindy but did read a disc. Honestly, for the price I wasn't expecting premium feel, but the chunky build and loose buttons didn't inspire confidence. It works for now, I guess.
Bought this for my old digital camera, honestly wasn't expecting much from a Class 2 card but it's been flawless. No lag during burst shots and holds way more than I'll ever need.

I grabbed this drive thinking it'd be a simple plug-and-play solution for shuffling files between my home office rig and an older laptop. The real headache started when I tried using it with my powered hub, constant disconnects, slow transfers that crawled to a halt, and twice it locked up the whole port cluster until I physically unplugged everything. Charges fast and the price was way better than the store version.

This camera completely exceeded my expectations. The autofocus is lightning quick, the image quality is stunning, and the ergonomics feel perfect in hand. It's been a joy to use for both video and stills.
Great sound. Much louder than I expected. The Bluetooth signal never drops off and battery life is at least a couple of days worth of workouts. I haven't run the battery completely dead though

Were having many problems with my 8 year old laptop with only 2GB of ram while using Adobe Lightroom. I was seriously planning to replace computer. With only this small and cheap upgrade it is a 100% improvement. I can now wait a little longer to replace computer.

Great price and set up was easy. Just remember to go on line and complete the registration as well as the final setup steps. The reception on the Range extender installed in our basement registered 2 sets of bars for strength. My WD MyNet 600 router is located in my office 2 floors above, The signal strength to my Roku device for the basement TV jumped from Poor-Fair to Excellent after the range extended was set up. Update: It's 3 months after initial install and I am having the same problems as other users. The extender drops the connection and needs a reboot. There is no new FW to update which means there is no fix. Looks like WD just gave up. My advice, find a different product.

Nice Back pack lots of storage room in large compartment. wide wheel base makes it easy to pull over obstacles. Would have gave it 5 stars but the outer walls are not lined as in My Targus laptop backpack.

I like many things about it. It has a gold coated metal, which means it won't rust and connectivity stays good for very long time. Also it seems to be very well made overall. Wasn't expecting much for the price, but this thing feels built to last. Plugs grip tight, spacing is perfect for chunky adapters, and the long cord saved me from using an extension.
Works well in Cubase 5.5 on Windows 7, using asio4All as the driver. Will hickup and drop out until you find the buffer size sweet spot.

I love these ball design cameras! This one is impressive with a very high resolution picture, the typical menu features found on more expensive cameras, and bright IR LEDs! The Imager is very nice, good sensitivity in low light even with out the LEDs, great noise reduction and contrast. I have this model mounted outside in direct weather and so far haven't had any trouble from it yet. I did change out the lens from the standard 3.6mm to a 6mm for my type of Application. I would definitely recommend this model to anyone and will be buying this camera again. Advisory: With any Camera, always double check the focus and adjust before installing for best picture. Also this model has a toggler control on the pigtail for Menu access that will need to be protected from moisture and humidity.
I can't say enough good things about this ROku. My wife loves it and the headset on the remote.

For the price, I'm genuinely impressed. I picked this up mainly to keep an eye on my 3D printer in the garage, and it does the job nicely. The image is decent once you tweak the focus ring just right, and the night vision works better than expected in complete darkness. Setup took a few minutes, but it's not complicated. I'd recommend this to anyone who needs a basic, no-fuss camera for a workshop, pet area, or back door. It's not packed with premium features, but it's reliable and the app hasn't crashed on me yet. If you're looking for a super sharp, zoomable security cam, you'll want to spend more. For a simple budget monitor, it's a solid choice.

I thought it would be kind of cool to have and have tried it for a couple different things but it just doesn't give you desirable shots.

With most new devices (including the Fire) being made with gorilla glass, these screen protectors are an utter waste. Sure it may reduce some glare and smudges, but the glare reduction isn't that significant. And your money would be better spent on a nice microfiber cloth (which can also be used for other cleaning purposes) to clean the screen when it does get smudged.

I love my DNS-325 so far. At the moment, I have a single WD 2TB "Green" hdd installed. I like the small footprint, the ease of installation, the web browser interface, and the power savings. It boots up fast too. And it's super quiet. I previously had a homemade file server (basically an old 700MHz Compaq PC with two 500GB hdds installed, running Ubuntu Server). This Dlink just sips the power. I measured both the old server vs the Dlink DNS-325 using a Kill-a-watt. Here are my findings: My old server was using ~ 72.5 W. The DNS-325 used 8 W (3.5 while asleep). My old server used 13.96 kWh over 209 hrs. The DNS-325 used 0.14 kWh over 24.39 hrs. My old server cost roughly $4.26 per month to operate. The DNS-325 should cost at most 37 cents. It will be less than half that if I allow it to sleep. This Dlink uses less than 1/10 the power that my old file server used. It will pay for itself in less than three years.

I wasn't sure the bone conduction would sound crisp underwater, but it's surprisingly clear for podcasts. The fit stays put during flip turns. I'm knocking off one star because the button placement takes some getting used to with wet fingers. Overall, a solid pool companion.

Exactly what I was looking for for my C.H.I.P. $9 computer. It paired up no problem, and the keyboard and touchpad work great. It needs to be in Windows mode for the escape key to send an escape. It also works with my Raspberry Pi (with a bluetooth adapter in a USB port). This keyboard with touchpad lets me work with it on my lap with the little computer plugged into the TV for a display. I like that the keyboard is full-size and metal backed for a sturdy feel. This keyboard is better than the Logitech K400 and way better than the Aerb ultra-thin keyboard/touchpad/remote control. I definitely recommend this keyboard with touchpad for little computers like the C.H.I.P. and Raspberry Pi.

This little adapter feels solid enough, but I wish the listing made compatibility clearer. If you drive a Honda with the premium audio package, you're going to need an extra splitter cable that isn't included. I'd only recommend it to folks with the base stereo, anyone else should budget for another accessory.

I swapped over to this Amazon Basics keyboard after my old one started double-typing letters constantly, and honestly, I forgot how pleasant a simple, low-profile keyboard could be. The thing that stands out most for me is just how whisper-quiet the keys are. I share a home office with my partner, and the clack from my previous board was driving her nuts during calls. These scissor-switch keys have a soft, cushioned landing that barely makes a sound, even when I'm typing fast. The layout is completely standard, so there was zero adjustment period, and the matte finish doesn't pick up fingerprints. It's nothing flashy, but for pure, distraction-free typing at a great price, it's exactly what I needed.
Bought several for use with an iPhone 6. They're very well made, much nicer than the Apple cables, and are all still in excellent condition after moderately rough use.

Very inexpensive solution. Highly recommended for spare or for hobby use. It does what you would expect it to do. The compression is also excellent. When properly tightened it gets nice and stiff just like the much more costly solutions. I highly recommend this for a backup or for a primary.

I've gone through so many wireless earbuds over the years it's embarrassing. Most either had terrible battery life or sounded like I was listening through a tin can. These are a completely different story. The clarity is solid enough that I actually look forward to my morning runs now, podcasts and playlists both come through crisp, even with wind noise. They stay put no matter how sweaty I get, and I've accidentally yanked them out of my ears a few times with zero damage. For anyone who wants reliable, no-fuss earbuds for workouts or commuting without spending a fortune, I'd absolutely point them toward these. Seriously impressed.
Charges the iPad fast enough, but the cable could be a bit longer for reaching the couch.
Been using this motherboard for months now, without any problems.
Been using these for months now and they still snap in tight without any static or looseness.

The box arrived in perfect shape, not a single dent. Inside, everything was packed snug with dense foam. Pulling it out, the finish felt solid and the adjustable arms moved smoothly right out of the box. Mounted my chunky older cable box in minutes. First impression is this thing is built like a tank and looks clean on the wall. Very happy.

This is an awesome radio! I have two and showed it to my friend and she ordered one to. Great sound, Great battery life, Charges my Ipod with ease, Lightweight... Over all this deserves 1000 stars in my book.

So, the original charger lasted about 3 years without a problem, then suddenly had a short at the plug end. I bought this one to replace it. The male plug side is too short - and will not properly seat in the XOOM to charge it properly. When I tried to push it up, it shorted out against the housing. I bought a NEW MOTOROLA charger, and have used it twice now, no issues with the plug and no shorting out. So I recommend you save yourself my pain, and buy the Motorola OEM Charger (also on Amazon) for $9 vs. the $6.75 that this thing cost.
Nice case. Basic black leather feels plastic - gy, stiff, but should provide a strong protection. Fits my Galaxy Note pro 12.2 with keyboard.
Honestly, I wasn't expecting much for the cost, but the clarity is spot on. No weird color cast or vignetting on my wide lens. Feels solid in hand too, definitely punching above its price point.

Needed a quick fix for my old second monitor and this little adapter worked right out of the box. Plugged the VGA cable into it, connected the HDMI end to my laptop, and the display popped up instantly with no driver hassles. Picture quality is solid for everyday office work. Honestly surprised by how smooth the whole experience was for the cost.

I've had this capture device for about four months now. It held up okay initially, but the plastic casing started feeling a bit loose after regular plugging and unplugging. Still works, but I'm not super confident it'll last much longer with daily use. Fine for occasional tasks, just don't expect a tank.

This product is truly amazing. I hadn't heard of this product but with a car as old as mine, I thought I was forced to listen to radio or burn my own CDs. When I found this on a deals website, I bought it immediately and never looked back. Worth the price, even if it isn't on sale.

i cant tell you what size ears i have even more so my inner ear, different products sizes and brands works for different folks, i bought the mixed pack because i wasn't sure which would fit, small are too small, large are too large. the mediums are perfect for my wireless headphones i recently bought, the jaybird earphones for working out, no slippage, it stays in while working out

Incorrectly Manufactured!!! SATA Cable and Power cable do not click into place causing them to fall out. I saw this complaint all over the internet from 2014 up to 2016 and they still haven't fixed the problem. Also called WD and I couldn't get a straight answer why they sell the 2TR drive but yet its not advertised on there website when you try to purchase one direct from the WD website (They only show 1,4,5,6 TR versions at checkout) So I called the technical sales line and they told me to call the technical sales line (yes you read that correctly) I'm so confused by this whole ordeal. I've been using western digital black drives for years and this is the first time I have ever experienced this. Seems that WD's own quality department did not perform a simple connector/power check. I work in the MFG industry and this is a real bad sign for a company in which I have always trusted since Maxtor when out of business and molded into Seagate. I wanted to like this little splitter, and for the first few months it worked fine for basic dual-monitor use in my home office. Then the second HDMI port started acting up, random blackouts that last anywhere from five to fifteen seconds, usually when I'm in the middle of something important. It's not my cables or displays, since port one stays solid. I'd only recommend this if you're strictly using a single output and need a dirt-cheap stopgap. For anyone planning to rely on both ports daily, maybe for a simple presentation setup with zero tolerance for dropouts, spend a bit more elsewhere.

For the price, this Benro head is a solid deal. It handles my mirrorless camera and a mid-range zoom just fine in horizontal orientation. The problem starts when I flip to portrait mode with anything heavier than a kit lens. The tilt lock knob just doesn't bite enough, and I get slow droop during a shoot. I've learned to frame a little high to compensate, which isn't ideal. If you keep your gear light and don't constantly switch orientation, it's hard to beat for the money. Just know the limits.

Sorry can't give a good review when the product you get is different than what is shown in the photos. Tri legs are a standard in photography because they add stability compared smaller 4 leg base. Why would Fotodiox change this to a 4 leg design is beyond me. Also why have they not updated the photos, could it be because they know most photographers will look at other products if they do. It's nice that the legs fold but poor design that putting the spigot on the the top leg takes away the ability to fold the legs for storage and transport. It seems the early positive reviews are from before Fotodiox changed the product without updating the description or photos.
Junk... I purchased several of them and the screw on the top is a joke. I want my money back. The number of stars says it all...

I've been hauling this bag around for over six months now, including a dusty hiking trip and two international flights, and it still looks almost new. The stitching held up perfectly and the padded insert has kept my DSLR and extra lens completely protected even when I've accidentally bumped it against doorframes. Zippers are smooth and strong, no snags at all. I also love that it just looks like a rugged everyday messenger bag so I don't feel like a target in crowded areas.

I'm not an audiophile. My golden ears from yesteryear have tarnished rather badly. I wanted a no-frills subwoofer to balance out my good-but-small bookshelf speakers. The first of these units arrived damaged: the grille had sheared off and could not be reattached. The company was perfect. They told me what to do and sent a replacement unit with no complaint or hassle. It came quickly. The instructions aren't the very clearest, but it didn't take long to figure it out and set it up. One complaint: whoever designed the connector array never, ever tried to connect anything to it. The connectors, even if they were well placed, are hard to use; but they're crammed together such that my not-very-big fingers had a heck of a time connecting the speakers. Much bad language was used. In terms of function, I can set the unit to make much more boom-boom than I want or to be almost silent - or anything in between, including a setting I like. The other speakers are routed through this unit, which ostensibly leads to better balance. Can't prove it by me, but the result seems fine.

For anyone who jogs near traffic or needs to stay alert, these are a solid pick. They sit comfortably and let in just enough outside noise to feel safe. Music clarity is decent, not mind-blowing, but for active outdoor use I'd definitely suggest them.
At this price point, these stands are sturdy, spring loaded, beefy, strong and have a decent case. They are not small but are considerably lighter than other brands. They are definitely a bargain!

lol this would be great if My room mate can ever figure out how to use the thing on His PC!:P It is supposed to be a "plug and play" type of device,however it is not!!! You have to manually set your PC to allow this to function on it and it's apparently a pain to do so!!!:( lmao!!! I had no problem when I used it on My lap top!;)
Arrived super quick, packed solid with no dents. Looks sharp right out of the box.
Very soft and you can wear them for hours without irritation.
The battery life on these is insane, I use them daily for work calls and only charge them once a week. Super comfy too.

I honestly didn't expect much for the price, but this thing feels way nicer than I thought it would. The material has a good grip without being sticky, and it slides in and out of my pocket easily. The magnetic closure is strong enough that I don't worry about it flopping open in my bag. Cutouts line up perfectly with the dock connector, headphone jack, and even the little antenna spot, no fighting to plug anything in. It adds barely any bulk, so it still feels slim in hand. I was half expecting something flimsy, but it's held up great so far. Definitely a pleasant surprise.
Arrived a day early in solid, no-frills packaging. Sleeve feels sturdy right away, just slightly roomier than expected on my 13-inch.

Got this adapter for my old S5 to stream movies on a monitor. Sharp 1080p picture with zero lag. Audio comes through clean and loud. Honestly, for the price of a coffee, it's a steal. No setup hassles, just plug and play. Delivery was crazy fast too.
I've accidentally yanked these out of my ears a dozen times and they're still going strong, sound hasn't faded at all after months of sweaty commutes.

I bought this to replace a WD 750GB Black that had a SMART predictive failure after about 2 years use. I have about equal appreciation for both these drives. The WD was cheap and served well. I've replaced many hundreds of drives in my career so I don't particularly get upset about the occasional failure and wouldn't characterize the product by a single experience. Obviously in the case of the Seagate I haven't owned it long enough to know whether it will last longer or not. It's worked for almost a month now and seems similar in performance which is quite high for a laptop drive. I also have a SanDisk SSD which I am a little disappointed in simply because for practical purposes there is no performance advantage compared to these much less expensive and much higher capacity hard drives. For a tablet or ultrabook I would go SSD, but for a mostly desk-bound laptop, these 750GB drives seem to work great.

I've been using this flash diffuser regularly for about four months now, including a couple of outdoor events where it got bumped around in my bag, and it's held up perfectly. The plastic still snaps on securely without any cracks or loosening, which honestly surprised me given how light it is. I've used it mostly for indoor portraits and some macro shots, and the light spread is consistently soft and even. After dozens of hours of use, there's no discoloration or wear on the diffusion panel, and the mounting bracket hasn't stretched out at all. It's become a permanent part of my speedlight setup because I don't have to baby it. Really solid build for the price.

Arrived a day early and the packaging was seriously overkill, individually wrapped, no kinks or bends. Plugged it in immediately and got a rock-solid connection. Feels well-made with snug connectors. Exactly what I needed and the clean presentation was a nice bonus.

This six foot cable is reasonably priced and well designed. The lightning connector end over-rmold is hardier than the apple cable, as it has both a textured feel and is wider at the back allowing a better grip for its removal. If you have ever pulled on the smooth/shinny perfectly straight Apple lightning cable you know what I mean!!!

I've stayed at a lot of hotels during my Army career and current job. The last hotel I was at (Sh@raton) had this alarm clock and I liked it so much I bought it for my house. I really like the gradual alarm beep and the left right switch so you can EASILY set how many more minutes you want to snooze for. Cant say anything about the radio, I don't use it.

I've been using this little Bluetooth mouse with my Samsung tablet for about four months now, mostly for spreadsheet work and long email sessions. Pairing was quick and it's stayed connected without any random dropouts. The scroll wheel still feels smooth, and the click buttons haven't gotten mushy or sticky, which I was half expecting at this price point. I've dropped it off my desk twice onto hardwood and it didn't even scratch. Battery life has been solid too, I'm still on the original AA it came with. It's not the most ergonomic for all-day use, my hand does cramp a bit after a few hours, but for portable reliability it's been a champ.
The braided jacket feels sturdy, but after a month it only charges intermittently. I have to wiggle the connector just right or my phone won't even recognize it. Disappointing for a 15ft cable.

You know, I have invested in quite a few very expensive ear buds in the past. They sounded great, but invariably broke. One or the other ear bud would just stop working. I primarily use them to listen to audiobooks at the gym - lifting weights and riding the stationary bike. SoI was never especially tough on my phones. I finally got tired of paying a lot of money for products that weren't durable and didn't last. I bought these because they were inexpensive, and had a control on the cable to stop play if someone spoke to me. Well, whaddah ya know!? They sounded almost as good, and they seem to be lasting longer. This is actually my second pair. I had bought them before, and was happy with them, but dropped my ipod one day and bent the phone plug. After that, it didn't fit quite well, and I'd get static whenever I moved. Bought a second pair, and they are great. I use these with my iPhone as well on occasion, and they're fine for that use as well. No one seems to have any problem hearing me, and I have no issue hearing and understanding them. In fact, they sound quite good. So, for either casual listening at the gym, or talking on your cell phone, I'd recommend these phones. They are decent at excluding outside noise, though I haven't been able to get quite as good a fit as I had on some of the higher priced phones. But I don't think the cost differential was anywhere near worth the slightly better sound isolation.
These sound way better than I expected for the price. Bass hits hard, they stay in my ears, and the little case keeps everything tidy.

Pros -Easy installation, setup guide is well written and easy to follow -8,000 square feet WiFi Coverage -Easy to reach on/off switch, it is nice not to have to unplug a device just to cycle power -Wireless Client Isolation, WiFi users can be protected from each other -Built in Intrusion detection system -Multiple WiFi amplifiers to make sure you get a strong signal -Can manage up to 7 additional access points -Can be used as a Router or an access point -2.4Ghz and 5.0Ghz WiFi -Includes a magnetic mounting plate and standard mounting clips Cons -Only has one LAN port, sure you can get a small gigabyte switch for cheap, but that is one more thing to plug in. -Includes many advances features not needed by the average user which can be intimidating, you are paying for all these features so if you do not need them this might not be the right device for you. -expensive Thoughts Not having at least 4 LAN ports is a big negative for me given the price tag, but I think I might be able to overlook that given the powerful processor and increased range it provides. This is clearly a business class device that is packed with advanced features. If is worth the money, but is more than most people need.
Small and easy to carry in by brief case with my MacBook Pro.

admittedly, this camera is very inexpensive for the feature set you get. but we needed to change batteries far too often to make this a worthwhile investment. Also the lack of optical zoom means you are essentially buying a 35 mm point and shoot, so great if that's what you want (and u r willing to spend $10/week on batteries for regular "vacation" usage). IF you want greater flexibility, and something u won't grow out of or tired of real fast, u should keep looking for something that may involve extra initially, but you will get more use of. In short, great price, but serious shortfalls w. respect to battery life and camera capabilities. Also, the cycle time between shots (especially when using flash) is painfully sloooooooow.
For the money, this little light does the job. Not crazy bright, but it works fine inside my PC case.

Showed up a day early in a simple, sturdy box with zero damage. The case feels way nicer than expected, fits my mini perfectly, and that magnetic sleep/wake works instantly. Super clean first impression for the price.

I picked this up hoping to replace a power-hungry desktop for always-on tasks, and it mostly delivers. Performance is snappier than I expected for such a tiny board. Setup was straightforward until I wanted to access the eMMC outside the OS, needed a separate adapter, which wasn't obvious at first. USB 3 speeds are solid. It runs cooler than anticipated, but the lack of clear documentation cost it a star. Overall, a capable little device.

Let me say up front that I bought the 76cs almost exclusively for marine navigation. It is a great little GPS for that purpose and I'm sure it's equally good for hiking. There are a lot better choices for auto navigation but I'm totally happy with it for my purpose. I was initially quite skeptical that a handheld's small screen real estate could be useful as a chart plotter. Nonetheless, I purchased and installed the Blue Chart Americas CD and activated the Caribbean charts. After 2 weeks of constant use sailing in the Windward Islands, I'm astounded at how well it works. With the 'declutter' mode engaged, the screens show an appropriate level of detail and the display has excellent visibility in direct sun. Although the backlight is adequate for viewing in bright conditions under a boat's bimini, I found that I actually preferred to hold it out in the direct sunlight. I particularly like the ability to customize the paging sequence and the autozoom feature is handy as well. There is nothing intuitive about the interface but after I got over the learning curve, I was surprised how quickly I could get to the info I needed. Entering lat/lon waypoints is never going to be fun without a keyboard, but is certainly manageable. If you plan ahead, you can enter them on a PC and download them. A storage card slot would be nice but the entire Caribbean chart set used less than 1/4 of the available memory and on the plus side the unit really is waterproof. The battery life is about as advertised and you can really stretch it by turning it off and on as you need it because it gets a fix in about 15 seconds if you haven't moved too far.

I wasn't expecting much at this price point, especially after testing a few center channels from bigger brands that left dialogue sounding boxy or buried. Set this one up, ran room correction, and honestly had to double-check I wasn't hearing things. Voices are crisp and sit right where they should, even during chaotic action scenes. My partner noticed immediately, she said it finally sounded like the actors were in the room, not behind a curtain. It blends seamlessly with my left and right channels too, no weird tonal shifts. For a relatively compact unit, the weight and build feel substantial. If you're on the fence, it punches way above its cost.
Still holding up well after a month of daily use, no issues.

I built my desktop Windows machine in September 2007, and although it is still going strong with four 1. 5 TB Seagate hard drive in a RAID configuration, with only 4 GB of RAM (and less than that accessible on a 32-bit version of Windows Server 2003) it had hit a wall for the database development I do on Linux virtual machines with PostgreSQL. A consultant friend told me about the benefits of SSDs over hard drives for _random_ as opposed to sequential reads, so I decided to give it a try. The Samsung drive was trivial to install as an additional drive. I migrated my VMs to it, and they run dramatically faster on complex SELECT statements especially with multiple table joins. Then I took a few extra steps, which I cannot recommend too highly: I moved my Windows paging file to the SSD, and made it larger (6 GB). Then I created \Temp and \Users\myname\Temp directories on the SSD, and pointed the global and local tmp and temp environment variables to them. What a difference! Windows runs noticeably better on almost everything I do with it. The hard drive flashes furiously as always, but a lot of the time there is no sound of hard drive heads thrashing around. I believe the installation of the Samsung SSD postponed my need to build a new computer for at least a year, maybe three or more.

Chiclet keys are long gone, yet this has one. They work okay, the bad review is it leaves the guts of the machine open to damage...the keyboard is covered well, just don't drop it as the back side of the iPad is on its own. Its also overpriced, but expect that to go down soon as the word gets around. Not for protection, but if you keep the behemoth 12 inch iPad in the cottage, you'll be fine. Unless you drop it.

Card works fine until my Mac Pro 8-core goes to sleep. After that it seems impossible to restore USB connectivity. Sonnet claims the issue is that Apple broke/changed the USB driver since Mac OS X 10.6. Read their FAQ before purchasing!
I used this to connect my Mac Mini to my 50 inch Plasma display. It worked as directed.
Thing worked okay for about two weeks, then the charging port just snapped loose inside. Now it won't power on at all. Total junk.

After about four months of regular use, the hinge started getting loose and won't hold position. Still works, but feels flimsy now. Fine for occasional stuff, just don't expect it to stay tight long-term.

I'd only recommend this GPS to someone I genuinely dislike. After about two years of so-so navigation, it bricked completely right when I needed it most, middle of a road trip with spotty cell service. The screen would freeze on startup, or just stay black, no matter how many times I reset it. Even before that, it had a weird habit of recalculating routes for no reason, sending me in loops around industrial parks. The lane guidance was clunky and often too late to be useful. I missed three turns on one drive alone because it lagged. If you enjoy stress, wasted gas, and pulling over to reboot a paperweight, go for it. For everyone else, save your money and use your phone.

the iPad 3 is a bit heavier than the iPad 2 and as such the magnetic apple case would detach all the time. This is a two part case. You attach the magnetic cover, then attach the back translucent cover. The back cover has a lip that goes over the magnetic attachment on the front cover. That provides extra grip so now the front cover stays on perfectly. If you have an iPad 3, then this case is what you need.

I've had these for about six months now and they've held up perfectly. No creaks, the padding hasn't flattened, and the cable shows zero wear. Still sound crisp after daily use. Honestly surprised something this solid didn't cost way more.

I picked this up on a whim and honestly didn't expect much, but it's been a game changer for my desk setup. I had cables constantly sliding off and making a mess, and this little gadget keeps everything neatly in place without looking clunky. The subtle heft means it doesn't budge, yet it's still easy to reposition. Totally cleans up the chaos, wish I'd grabbed one sooner.
No complaints, does exactly what it should. Pins are tight too, so no loose connections.

I've had this 128GB SSD running as my boot drive in a home lab server for about two years now, and it's been rock solid. I was skeptical at first because I push a lot of random writes with database VMs and constant logging, but it hasn't skipped a beat. SMART stats still look clean, no reallocated sectors or slowdowns. Compared to a couple other budget SSDs I've tried that started flaking out after six months, this one just keeps chugging. Boot times and VM launches are still snappy. For the price, the endurance on this little thing is genuinely surprising.

Great product at first, but degrades over time. This product is super easy to install. It's literally plug and play. The speeds it provides is meh. I have a 75MBPS connection but only about 30MBPS gets through on this adapter. Other powerline adapters keep me on much higher speeds. After a few months, the adapter would lose connection for about a minute every so often. As more time went by, it'd get worst and worst eventually leading me to abandon it all together. It wasn't THAT bad, but two or three times a day was more than enough for me to give it up.

My car fails and doesn't have an audio input so to not listen to the terrible radio where I live, I've made a bunch of CDs (yes it took me back to my teenage years!). This fits plenty of CDs but it didn't really fit my trailblazer visor. I made it work but sometimes one of the straps slips off.
The charging cable stopped working after two weeks. It won't connect to my laptop at all now, and I've tried multiple ports. Total waste of money.
These are pretty decent for the price. Sound quality is clear enough for podcasts before bed. They feel a little bulky side-sleeping, but honestly for under twenty bucks I can't complain much.

Excellent power strip that meets many needs. Specifically: Wide spacing between plugs with two extra wide transformer receptacle. Variety of USB power ports (2 ports for 2.4 A; 1 port for 1 A; and a auto regulating 0-2.4 A) Six foot cable is a convenient length for a power strip.
Arrived super fast and packed really securely. Plugged it in and everything just worked right away. Super solid first impression.
This is a great receiver. I wish it had bluetooth and the ability to stream from an android phone but I knew this going in. Yamaha seems to make a solid product.

I bought this a few months ago in preparation for a trip to Mexico. Well, I'm finally going, and just got around to setting it up. If you can call it that. If you know how to pair a Bluetooth device, you know how to use this. It's that simple. It's also small and light, so no hassle there. I paired it with my Galaxy S7. It took less than 30 seconds start to finish. Per the instructions, open your camera app and push the appropriate shutter button (there's one for iDevices and one for Android). In my case, either button worked just fine. YMMV. Much better than using the hand gestures that are baked in to the S7, in my opinion. I've paired this with the Joby JB01325 GripTight GorillaPod Stand for Large Phones & Tripod to make family photos a breeze. Great combo for self portraits. For the $8 I paid, I think I got my money's worth. No idea how well this will stand up over time, but I'm optimistic. If I run in to any problems, I'll definitely update this mini review. In the meantime, Official seal of approval applied.
Doesnt work right out of the box.amazon will not refund or replace and has lost me as a customer of 9 yrs!
The carrying case makes it super easy to pack and keep the reflector clean between shoots.
This is a good product. Four USB ports, each with their own on/off switch. More importantly, it rotates to get it out of your way. And it can handle daisy-chaining usb hubs.

I really like the picture quality on this player, 4K discs look fantastic, and the upscaling for regular Blu-rays is noticeably better than my old unit. The interface is snappy, and setup was straightforward. My big gripe is the streaming app situation. I knew going in that the selection might be limited, but I specifically bought this hoping to use it as my main hub for everything, and the absence of a proper Apple Music app is a real letdown. You can cast audio from your phone, sure, but a native app with full navigation would have made a huge difference for me. It's a solid machine for physical media and a few major streaming services, but if you're counting on broad app support, double-check the current list before you commit.

I am very familiar with Carson, having owned a couple binoculars with HD glass. I used those for surveillance work and for birding. One pair broke and they replaced them without any questions. I very much appreciate the company. As for this monocular, I shopped around and looked at several and was about to pay a lot more for a premium monocular when I decided that I didn't need it. I gambled on this unit, hoping that I would not need the better glass of a more expensive one. It's a bit large, but I have large hands. It isn't the easiest to focus with, but you become fairly adept at it quickly enough. The quality seems good and I imagine that I will have this unit around for a long time. I teach firearms indoors and I needed a monocular to help my aging eyes see the. 22 caliber bullet holes on black targets with dark backgrounds. I can readily see holes out to 20 yards with these and I am very happy, especially at the pricepoint. I carry them in my jacket pocket for birding when riding my motorcycle. Hech, I even tried the ant thing and it was impressive that I could stand above an ant pile and watch them as if I was on my knees, like when I was a kid. I recommend this monocular and I promise you that you will like Carson as a brand, especially if you have to contact them. I just wish that they would make a nice birding spotting scope. Regards and happy viewing.
It works fine with the remote. The volume does not work.. I am still learning about it.
The battery life on this thing is insane, I've gone nearly a week without charging and it's still going strong. Super impressed.
Perfect for someone who just needs a simple setup without overthinking it. I'd suggest it to casual users or beginners wanting decent results on a budget.
Super cute design and my earbuds fit perfectly inside.

This lens is such a huge improvement over the standard kit lens. When I first acquired my Pentax Kr, I was very happy with the camera as I got back in to photography after being away for 15 years. The Kr is my first digital SLR and I felt that I could achieve great quality pictures without film with a reasonably priced kit. After 18 months, however, I was finding that there was still a quality to my images, particularly of people, that was lacking. At first, I thought it might be the camera. Then, on recommendations from other Pentax users, I decided to give this lens a try. Immediately, my images are sharper, and more colorful than with the standard kit lens. In addition, the larger aperture makes shooting in low light very easy and the bokeh is out of this world. I am very happy with this upgrade. I only wish that Pentax would have made the kit lens a bit better so it would be more useable.

For what you pay, this little kit is a steal. The charger does its job without any fuss, and having two spares means I'm never scrambling for power mid-shoot. Charges are consistent and I haven't seen any weird drain. Honestly, it's a no-brainer compared to pricier first-party options.

For the price, I expected something at least usable as a travel mouse, but this thing just feels cheap. The plastic creaks under normal pressure, and the scroll wheel is already skipping after two weeks of light use. The sensor stutters on anything but a perfectly smooth desk, which defeats the purpose of having a portable option. Sure, it's affordable, but you're basically paying for frustration. I'd rather spend a little more on something that doesn't feel like it's going to fall apart.
The magnetic clasp is super secure and snaps into place effortlessly, no more fumbling with tiny latches.

This case fits my iPad like a glove once I finally wrestled it on. The raised edges around the screen give me real peace of mind when my toddler grabs it. Totally worth the slight struggle to install.

I've been using this bracket for my weekly coffeehouse sets, and the grip on the mic stand is rock solid, no wobble even when I'm tapping the screen between songs. What really sold me was the quick-release clamp; I can pop the iPad off in seconds without messing with my stand setup. Played an outdoor gig in gusty wind and it didn't budge. Sturdy enough that I stopped worrying about my tablet taking a dive.
The remote pairing was a breeze, and I love how crisp the dialogue sounds at night.

Was worried my backup drive was toast after the old cable frayed, but this replacement snapped right in and works flawlessly. No connection drops at all. If you've got a temperamental external drive that keeps disconnecting, do yourself a favor and grab this.
Pretty solid, just wish the battery indicator was clearer.
Fits my speaker mounts perfectly, and the cable stays put.

An excellent option at the budget level price. I have several of these and find they work very well in several different scenarios. Used as full lights,key light's,used on props to create shadows ect. These guys do it all. Got 4 of these expecting to have to send a few back. Nope! They all work perfectly. Great gear to just throw in the bag for a shoot. Light intensity level is changed by a rotating knob on the back. Had another dslr lights that weighed a lot more and wasn't nearly at bright This guy is a absolute steal for its cost. Buy a couple and find all the cool ways you can use For them. So glad I did!
Handy little gadget. The clicky buttons feel satisfying and never miss a press, even after months of daily use.

Great idea, but in practice it's so light that its hard to keep still on your lap and properly press keys, and the left/right mouse click buttons. And the proprietary USB dongle is very easy to lose if you're moving from one machine to another... On the positive, it just worked with every pc, mac and chromebox, linux box I tested it on. I wouldn't recommend to a friend. I replaced this with a Logitech K810 (bluetooth with 3 dedicated device configured buttons to easily switch to 3).
Open the box and plug it in nothing more was required to use it. I was pleasantly surprised by the small size.

I really wanted to like this thing. Build quality feels solid, battery life is decent, but the actual using it part is just maddening. Whoever decided it should completely forget your place every single time it powers down clearly never actually listens to podcasts or audiobooks. I spend more time scrubbing through three-hour files trying to find where I left off than actually listening. For music it's slightly less annoying, but still frustrating when you pause for a while and it resets. Ended up tossing it in a drawer and going back to my cheap old player that at least remembers basic stuff. Waste of money.
Arrived super quick and the packaging was solid. Slipped right onto the tablet and feels sturdy right out of the box.
Prints come out crisp and the size options make it super handy.
Es justamente lo que esperaba, buen producto, llego sin problemas estoy contento, me gusta y prodra recomendarlos sin ningn problema. satisfecho con la compra
Did the job just fine. The connections fit, the length was just right, I did not have any problems and my drives worked.

They are solidly built and are very comfortable to me. However, I'm disappointed with the sound quality. I own a pair Sennheiser PXC 450 NoiseGard Active Noise-Canceling Headphones, which sound great, but wanted the IE6 for when I don't want to carry the bulky PXC450. I was expecting the same great sound fidelity as it is a Sennheiser, but that was not the case. I felt the sound had good bass, but was distorted and lacking a bit on highs. To be sure, in my latest trip I just bought a pair of the very inexpensive Sony MDREX36V/BLU EX Earbud Headphone (Blue), and they sounded much better then the IE6 - better clarity and overall balance of lows, mid, and highs, for less than a third of the price! Another problem with the IE6 is that they're too loud (115db/V), meaning you'd need an extra volume control if you plug it into an airplane audio socket. The Sony EX36V already has a volume control built-in, and isolates outside noise better than the IE6. Still not the same sound fidelity as the PXC450, but not bad for less than $30. Go with the Sony EX36V...

Bought this Tripp Lite unit to replace a dying APC that cost way more a few years back. Honestly, for the price I paid, I'm impressed. It arrived ready to go, no assembly hassle. I've got it protecting my desktop, a 27-inch monitor, and some networking gear. During a quick power flicker yesterday it kicked in seamlessly without a hiccup. The build feels solid, not cheap, and the outlets are laid out sensibly for chunky plugs. Runtime isn't endless, but for saving work and shutting down gracefully it's perfect. Losing one star only because the status LEDs are a bit dim in daylight. Overall, great bang for your buck.
No gain control and the volume is extremely low. Can't returned it as it is past 30 days. I only use the USB feature now to play mp3s.

I picked this up before a weeklong canoe trip, honestly not expecting much at this price point, but it outperformed my expectations. The battery lasted through four solid days of moderate use without a recharge, which was a nice surprise. Sound quality is decent, clear enough for podcasts and calls, though bass lovers might want more thump. It's smaller than I pictured, which actually worked in my favor for packing. The clip felt a little flimsy at first, but it held tight on my pack strap through some rough portages. My only gripe is the buttons are stiff and hard to find by feel. For the money, it's a solid little workhorse that handles outdoor abuse better than I anticipated.

I grabbed this for an aging HP notebook that barely held a charge anymore. It's been about three months now and the battery still lasts a solid four hours of constant use. No swelling or weird shutdowns, just steady power. For the price, I honestly expected it to degrade faster. Really impressed.

I wanted to like this case more than I did. The magnetic sleep/wake function works flawlessly every time, which is honestly the highlight. Unfortunately, the fit is just too inconsistent. With my iPad mini 2, one corner kept popping out no matter how carefully I pressed it in, and I worried about long-term stress on the device. The exterior texture feels decent from a distance but up close it's clearly just thin plastic with a bonded layer that started lifting near the edges after a week. For the full retail price, the materials don't inspire much confidence. It protects the screen fine, but the corner fit issue is a constant annoyance.
When talking about cables Belkin says it all. My cable of choice for all of my computing needs and has been for over 20 years.
Perfect for anyone tired of spotty connections. I'd tell my parents or non-techy friends to grab this without hesitation.

Honestly, for the money I expected way more. Setup was fine for the first week, but then it started dropping connection daily. Having to unplug it constantly just to get online is ridiculous. The range is okay when it works, but what's the point if it's so unreliable? I don't think asking for consistent WiFi at this price point is unreasonable. Pretty disappointing overall.

Showed up two days early in a really sturdy box with zero damage. Unboxing felt premium, everything snug and well-protected. First power-on was smooth and the default sound preset already impressed me right out of the gate.
Perfect little gadget, works like a charm every time.
High quality case. Its on the heavier side but the quality is nice.

It worked as advertised, decent range, good sound, easy setup. after almost two years it started making a horrible hissing sound when a device connected to it. should I be happy that this lasted that long? considering how cheaply everything else is made these days maybe so, but still not satisfied that now I need to shell out again for a replacement.
Does exactly what I expected for the money, no surprises.

This insanely cheap router outdoes everything else that is under $100. It can handle streaming movies and multiple games at once no problem. I imagine if you have a family of 4 or more all using devices maybe you would hit some caps, but it has been nothing but great for me.

I've had this step-up ring for about five months now, mainly on a Nikon 77mm lens that sees a lot of outdoor work, dust, damp grass, clumsy lens changes. The threads are still smooth, no binding or cross-threading at all. I've swapped my 82mm polarizer on and off dozens of times and the ring hasn't warped or loosened. It's taken a few minor knocks in my bag without any dents. Honestly expected the black finish to flake by now, but it looks barely touched. For the price I figured it'd be disposable, but it's just quietly doing its job.

I picked these up mostly for Zoom calls, and the mic quality genuinely surprised me, people on the other end said I sounded like I was in a podcast booth. Audio for music is solid too, nice balanced sound without overwhelming bass. My one gripe is the clamping force; after about an hour they start pressing right above my ears and I have to take a break. I've tried stretching them over a few books overnight, which helped a little. For the price and mic performance I'm keeping them, but if you've got a wider head, just know you might need to do some manual adjusting.

I was a little apprehensive about this one, due to the the comments here. I took the plunge after confirming that it would work with my HP 6540 printer. Setup was easy - but since I run WPA on the wireless side, hooking up to the wired side was a necessity for configuring it (of course - how would it know your key?) The quick start guide was passable, I ended up using the PDF version of the full manual instead. It does run very hot - but since everyone says this about them, I'm sure it's "normal". I'm currently trying to decide whether I will leave it on all the time, or keep the printer and printserver powered off until I need to print.

I've had this little box running 24/7 as a home server for about eight months now and it hasn't missed a beat. Originally I threw Debian on it with a 256GB SSD and 8GB of RAM, expecting it to be a temporary tinker project, but it's earned a permanent spot in my network closet. Zero crashes, no random reboots, and the fanless design still runs cool to the touch even during summer heatwaves. I've bumped it, moved it across the house, accidentally yanked cables, and it just keeps humming along. The aluminum chassis has held up perfectly too, no scuffs or loose ports. For the price I paid, I'm genuinely surprised how solid it's been long-term.

It appears that Arcos has skimped to make this a cheaper model. A MAJOR negative that the potential capacity is really a lot less than perceived. This is due to the inconceivable absence of ANY expansion via SDHC cards or any other flash cards. The greatly limits flexibility. It is always an important idea to leave a considerable amount of empty space on the locked up, internal flash of the unit, as performance several degrades, as the internal flash fills up. As it gets around 70%, besides the performance issues, sort issues, there is a much grater chance of database and OS, issues, because of the needing of large work areas, that are not available. Not allowing external expansion from SDHC, truly helps this situation. I guess this also forces Archos customers to HAVE to get propriatary Archos units and drives to expand at all. Skip this unit (however DON'T get a Ipod).

I've been using these dishwasher pods for close to five weeks now, running at least one load a day, and they've held up well in terms of consistent performance. The outer film dissolves every time, even on the quick wash cycle, and I haven't had any clumps or half-dissolved gunk stuck in the dispenser. Glasses come out clear, and greasy pans don't need a re-wash. The container itself is sturdy too, I've knocked it around under the sink a bunch and the lid still snaps shut tight, keeping moisture out. I'm giving four stars only because I think really baked-on lasagna dishes sometimes need a little extra scrubbing beforehand, but for everyday stuff they're reliable.

Arrived a day earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was simple but effective, no crushed box or tangled cable. The bright yellow color pops even more in person, super easy to spot on my cluttered desk. Unboxing felt clean, and the extra eartips were neatly organized in a little plastic tray, not just tossed in loose. First thing I noticed sliding them in was how light they feel. Almost forget I'm wearing them. Cable doesn't feel flimsy either, has a decent rubbery texture that doesn't snag on clothing. Sound right out of the box is crisp on vocals and podcasts, though I wasn't expecting subwoofer rumble. For the price, the overall initial quality and fit genuinely impressed me. Solid everyday beaters.

I grabbed a 100-pack spindle of these for a family slideshow project, and for the money they're unbeatable. Burned over 70 discs so far on an old LG external drive without a single coaster. They play back smoothly on our aging Panasonic player, my in-laws' no-name portable unit, and even a finicky car stereo. Sure, they're no-frills, but at this price I can archive home videos guilt-free and hand out copies without worrying about compatibility. Solid everyday discs.

Bought this for my mom's birthday and it turned into the whole family's favorite thing. Setup was a breeze, just loaded a memory card with a bunch of old and new pics and popped it in. I'd absolutely suggest this for anyone gifting to grandparents or non-techy relatives because once it's running, there's zero fuss. The slideshow transitions are smooth and the screen quality is crisp enough that even my picky dad commented on it. Now my mom leaves it on in the kitchen and gets little surprises throughout the day. If you want a thoughtful, low-maintenance gift that keeps giving, this is it.
Have always used Hoya lens with my old SLR's. Ditto now with my DSLR cameras. Optics are excellent. Price is excellent.

This arrived way faster than I expected, ordered on a Tuesday night and it was in my mailbox by Thursday afternoon. Packaging was simple but solid, no dents or scuffs on the case itself, just a clean little sleeve inside a padded mailer. Pulling it out for the first time, it felt super lightweight and slim, exactly what I was hoping for. The texture is smooth with just enough grip so it doesn't feel like it'll slip out of my hand. Button cutouts line up perfectly, no weird misalignment. The color isn't quite a bold crimson, more of a muted, earthy reddish tone, but it honestly grew on me within the first hour. Overall, a solid first impression.

I picked this up for a budget home server build and the efficiency rating really surprised me. My kill-a-watt meter shows way less draw than the old generic unit it replaced. Cables were long enough to manage cleanly in my mid-tower case, and it's been whisper-quiet even under sustained load. Solid value.

great improvement, i had a previous inateck model i gave a lot rating because it worked fine for SSD but a HDD had room to vibrate. that was my only complaint, the rest of the enclosure was ideal. this has been addressed fully in the new model, and HDD is now safe to use without needing to worry about vibrations. i fully recommend this case. i was offered a free unit to review and that is the one i base my review on.

This is my first home theater setup, but I must admit the sound is all that I was hoping for. If you use some 12 or 14 gauge wire, you might have some trouble fitting them in, and the central speaker stand cannot be attached to the ceilling. I used a cheap piece of L shaped metal from home depot to attach it above my projection screen and that did the trick.

I've used this about 10 times and there are bump galore on all my shots. shooting anything outdoor is dreadful and a lot of post editing is needed. when the cam is on a tripod, I actually lift it by its neck as much as possible (but not suspending the entire unit in the air) so the bumps and shakes are less pronounced. I think the wheels are too small for outdoor use. The ground must be ultra smooth to have it work properly

Surprisingly, this flat piece of plastic (with silver inlays) actually works, bringing in all 19 available channels in my area. However, you really do have to find the sweet spot on your walls for it and that might require a longer coaxial cable than is provided with the antenna.
Perfect simple remote to control my TV, DVD, and ROKU. Unbeatable value.
Does what it says on the box, just slightly slower write speeds than I'd hoped. Still a solid upgrade for my aging laptop.
A must when using multiple cameras. I run two GoPro's on my motorcycle and I can run for weeks at a time with only 4 batteries and this charger.
I first bought a knock off, but it stopped working after a few weeks. The Apple one is still working.

So I've had these for about four months and honestly they're already falling apart. The ear cushions started flaking everywhere like crazy, little black specks all over my desk and clothes every time I take the headset off. The foam inside the pillow part has gone completely flat too, so now it's basically just hard plastic pressing against my ears after an hour of use. I thought maybe I just got a bad batch but my friend bought the same set and his are even worse after three months. For the price I expected way better longevity, not something that self-destructs with normal daily use. Really disappointed and definitely wouldn't buy again or recommend to anyone who actually plans on using them regularly.

I really wanted to like this thing. The idea of having portable wireless storage sounded perfect for trips, but it just falls flat in daily use. On my Galaxy S10, the companion app is clunky and constantly freezes when I try to browse photos, let alone stream anything. I left it plugged in overnight to charge, and by morning the unit was incredibly hot to the touch, way beyond what feels safe. File transfers are painfully slow, like watching paint dry. I expected a seamless little media hub, but instead I got a laggy, overheating brick that makes me nervous to even leave connected. Honestly, it's been more frustrating than useful.
Had this for about six months now and it still feels rock solid, no lag or wear at all.
I love these. they take the zap out of the iPad that was keeping me up at night. once I used these, I slept happily. I actually bought more to keep them at each place I use my iPad.

I have bought a couple of these, and a couple of the cheapo dollar-store HDMI cables as well. We have not had one of these Mediabridge cables fail yet, but a couple of the cheapo cables have gone wacky on us. My wife is always stealing "the good cables" (these ones) to use on her laptop when she wants to watch shows from laptop to TV. Need to buy more...

I've had this 4-camera setup running at our warehouse for close to six months now, and it's held up well through dust, forklift vibrations, and the occasional bump from a ladder. The metal camera housings still feel solid, and I haven't had any weather-related issues with the two units we mounted under the eaves outside. Picture quality stays consistent day and night, and I like that I don't need to constantly fiddle with settings. The cables haven't degraded or loosened over time, which was my biggest worry. My only real gripe is that navigating playback on the NVR can be a little clunky, especially when I'm trying to scan long stretches of footage. Still, for the price, I'm impressed it's lasted this long without a hiccup.
I've had this plugged in for over a year now and it still works like day one. The housing feels solid and the outlets grip tight. Zero issues through a few storms already.
Great cheap pair of headphones. Sweat doesn't seem to bother them. They seem indestructible. Sound is good. Super-light. You can barely feel them. Great for jogging.
For what I paid, this little gadget is a steal. Plugged it in, laptop recognised it instantly, no fuss at all.
Best USB drive there is. Solid, tough, fast, reliable, and beautifully designed!
Worth the extra money to buy the color screen. My 88 year old father-in-law loves this!

3 years later these are still my best online purchase ever. I have replaced the ear pads with some from a pair of Audeze LCD-2s and they are much more comfortable this way. After about a year and a half of heavy use, a hair got into the space between the driver and the screen and made a buzzing noise, but that was just a matter of taking it apart and removing it with a tweezer.
Awesome sound for the price. Bought 3 more for my wife and kids.
After about two weeks the headband started cracking near the hinge, and now it's completely snapped in half.

Picked this up getting to lighten my load with the miles of hiking and tracking. Not realizing that the quality and strength of this unit is above amazing. Both tripod and monopod are exceptionally well crafted and sturdy pieces of professional gear.
For $3.00 I got this to have a extra case, the shipping was GREAT. If u are looking for a real leather case this is not it but it will protect your tablet
Stopped holding a charge after two weeks, totally useless now.

I am using these to power my camcorder. They are effective on a fresh charge, but after they sit a while you better charge them again if you anticipate needing them. If they do have a full charge on them, they last a long time. Charging them is a breeze. I have not used the car charger accessory so I don't know how well that works.

I couldn't be happier with this purchase. Everything was just as described. The PC came loaded with a genuine copy Windows 10 and a 90-day warranty. The system is fast and responsive. Perfect for my needs.

I grabbed this router mainly for the beamforming feature, and it did not disappoint. My dead spot in the upstairs bedroom now gets full bars. Streaming 4K movies has been completely stutter-free, even with three other family members gaming or video chatting simultaneously. Setup was basically plugging it in and opening an app. No cryptic IP addresses to mess with. The parental controls are a nice bonus too; I finally scheduled my kids' internet to shut off at bedtime without any extra hardware. Solid piece of gear.
I was not blown away by the sound. Imo it's not worth the price. There's nothing defective about my product it is just very under-whelming.

I honestly didn't expect much for the price, but this little adapter blew me away. Setup on my Dell laptop was literally just plugging it in and waiting a few seconds. Speeds are rock solid, even better than my old built-in port on a previous machine. Perfect for hotel rooms with only wired internet.
Item was as described and appeared to be of good quality.

Fits my older Windows laptop without any driver hassle, which was a relief. The Bluetooth range is solid around the house, though I wish the sleep mode kicked in a little less aggressively, it drops off after a few minutes of no typing. I'd recommend this to anyone with a vintage setup looking for a no-fuss wireless keyboard.

I tried it once and as soon as was able I bought it. It is a bright portrait and street lens. It focuses very fast and has a nice bokeh. It is light, you don't even feel it in your camera! This lens is a must in your bag.
This thing brought my old desktop back from the dead. Boot time is now under 15 seconds and apps open instantly. Honestly wish I'd grabbed one sooner.

Ordered this for a weekend trip and it arrived a full day early, packaging was solid, no dents or crushed corners on the box. Right out of the gate, the unit felt sturdier than I expected, nice weight to it and the knobs have a satisfying click. Setup took maybe two minutes; paired instantly with my phone and the Bluetooth range reaches my back patio without cutting out. Sound is crisp with decent bass for the size, though I noticed the display washes out completely in bright outdoor light so you're basically guessing the station. Small gripe for an otherwise smooth experience, definitely worth the price for casual listening around the house.

I used to have a Linkys router and thought my internet service was bad because my wifi would work for a few days and then drop. I got this router and have had zero drops and the speed is fantastic. I have all of my computers and devices hooked up to it and I stream Netflix through my Wii all day with no issues.
Put this on my rig a few months back and it's still rock solid after plenty of shoots.
Got this for my daughter's birthday and it's been perfect, easy to use, crisp photos, and an absolute steal for what you pay.
Had to fix my stubborn porch light, and this sensor did the trick right away. Easy install, solid motion detection, and the dual-bright mode is super handy at night.
Remote has a cheap feel but the buttons work okay.
Thought my old one was bad, but this barely powered on once before dying.
Does the job, but I expected better build quality for the money.

I use everything it comes with and the best part is that there are more things in this that what I can even think of what to use it for. This product was purchased using a promotional code to receive item at discounted price.

Arrived a day early in a sturdy little box with plenty of foam padding. Popped it on my astro-modded camera right out of the package and wow, instant fix. No fiddling with menus, just clean, natural colors for everyday shooting. Super impressed with how simple this made things.

Picked this up on a whim and honestly forgot about it until a blackout hit our block last month. The little thing fired right up and kept my phone alive while we waited out the grid. Sound quality is surprisingly clear for its size, and it slips into my camping kit like it was made for it.

I really think apple has missed the mark advertising the iPod touch mainly as a game machine. The apps are extraordinarily useful. I'm very glad I decided ( at the last minute ) to get the 32 gb model. I'm not using the iPod portion that much --some music and podcasts, but the add on apps have been very VERY useful. The screen is gorgeous, the keypad easy to use. If I had to quibble I'd say my biggest gripe is that flash and shockwave don't work yet. I know why apple has done that, but it's inconvenient. Youtube videos are fine. Battery life okay if you aren't on the internet the whole time -- i am, so it gets about five or six hours. Wifi reception has been excellent. Built in speaker is tinny and not loud enough, but i rarely use it so i didn't take off for that. There's an app to share files with your computer wirelessly, which is handy for transferring stuff via webdav connection to read later when you have no connection. Another app lets you capture articles from news and web sources to revisit later. There's even an app to make voip calls from the touch. (you have to add earphones with a mic -- picked up a pair on amazon for $7 and now make occasional calls via Internet. there have been a few times that I REALLY missed not being able to connect when away from wifi, but I do not miss the 2 yr contract to at&t. if you have wireless handy and don't need a full service phone contract this is the answer.

For the price, I wasn't expecting miracles, but this little antenna surprised me once I got it set up properly. Took some trial and error with placement, ended up mounting it in the attic, which made all the difference. Now I'm pulling in over 40 channels, and the picture quality on the major networks is rock solid. It's not going to perform like a massive outdoor rig, and if you're deep in a valley you might struggle, but for a budget indoor/attic option it's tough to beat. Build quality feels decent too, not flimsy. If you're realistic about your location and willing to fiddle with positioning, you'll get good value here.

I wanted to like this hub because the price is so low, but you get exactly what you pay for. It handles my mouse and keyboard fine, but plug in anything that draws more power and it becomes a headache. My external drive keeps disconnecting mid-transfer, and I've had to restart my laptop twice because the port froze completely. The plastic body feels cheap and the ports are already loose after two weeks.
