I guess it's okay if you just need basic USB expansion for low-power stuff, but for actual daily use with drives or charging, it's not reliable. Spend a little more elsewhere.
Works well. I've purchased a number of smaller computer accessories from Mediabridge, and all have worked, and appeared as sturdy and well-make as, say, Belkin. I'm happy to buy their stuff.

These are *great*. Replaced an ancient set of 10" Fischer speakers that had been with me since college (they were circuit breaker protected, fortunately!). These are primarily used for theater fronts, and they have great sound.

The fit is alright but the clips are not keeping the earbuds well inside the ear. As a consequence you get a lot of the outside noise which makes the sound quality very poor in noisy environment (even when running on a treadmill). People will also hear what you are listening too. So except if you're at home or in a quiet environment, forget about these earphones.
Perfect for gym sessions, I'd tell any workout buddy to grab these.
These things stay put no matter how intense the workout gets. I've done burpees, sprints, even jump rope and they never budge. Super comfortable too.

Got this to deal with some stubborn dust on my mirrorless sensor and it did the job perfectly. The swabs are super easy to use, and the blower feels sturdy. Would definitely recommend it to anyone shooting with interchangeable lens cameras.

This is a heavy spool of well insulated quality copper wire. Of all the wire I've purchased this is the best. So much so that I'm completely re wiring all of the projects in my Wrangler with this. As far as I can tell this is a true 16awg and the wire strips and cuts evenly, not the thin flimsy cheap copper coated aluminum crap. Good for current, good for speakers, you name it. Yes, the jacket makes the wire run heavier and thicker and takes up more space in a run of loom, but I'd rather have the extra protection under the hood or beneath my vehicle or around my house. I bought qty 2 on sale and glad I did.

I've bought 3 of these cables. The 1st one was to replace a $230 50ft hdmi cable that was sold to me by the company that I bought a projector from. Someone tried to swap devices and broke the head of the expensive cable, so I bought one of these to replace it and it worked every bit as good as the expensive cable. When we set up our 2nd theater room, I just went with this cable again. I have noticed that some blu-ray players work better than others as do projectors/tvs as far as sending/receiving signals over longer distances. The cable doesn't seem to make that much difference - So I'd go with this cheaper cable and still feel safe that it will work for you. PROS: The price is great. The cable is well made and thick Performs every bit as good as the expensive cables. CONS: I did have one cable break that I used in a mobile set up. The cable got a short after repeated set ups and take downs - as long as you don't plan on moving the cable around a lot after it is in place you shouldn't have any problems. The cable was broken when an inexperienced person coiled the cable up after use and folded the neck of the cable next to the head at a 90 degree angle (tightly wound) the cable still works but I have to "jiggle" it a little to get the connection to work correctly... (Not the cables fault - but thought I'd mention in case you plan on using in a mobile set up) Side note: Sometimes if the tv or projector is turned on after the dvd player or whatever is sending the signal, I've gotten a no signal message. If I turn the projector on first or turn the dvd player off then back on the signal is picked up. Not sure if this has anything to do with the cable but didn't have that small problem with the expensive cable.
I love this camera and all of it's many features. Don't buy the kit lens. It is not worth the extra money. I put a C. Zeiss 24-70mm lens on it and it is wonderful.
Couldn't be happier with how smoothly it pairs with my tablet for daily sketching sessions.

I bought this as a gift to someone, so I do listen to them occasionally. I myself is using Klipsch Image X10i to listen to music everyday. In comparison to X10i, Ety have a cleaner high ends, instrument separations, and very clear vocal. Bass however though controlled, is a bit rolled off or rather more "neutral" in my opinion. If you want a more "neutral" sound or prefer instrumental and vocal songs, this is the phone to get, not implying that it lacks bass as it fairs quite well for its bass as well. To me, this is a "bright" sounding phone as compared with X10i. Built quality wise, top notch... loved the thick and durable feeling cables that it came with, hardly any tangles. Cable noise when brushed against your clothes are still there but very minimal. I would still stick with X10i due to the comfort it provides and its sound quality fairs quite well against the Ety as well. And I am a big fan of a "warm" sounding phone and X10i fits my book. Both are great high end phones that money could buy and if you do have a chance to audition the phone before purchasing it, please do so. But in my opinion, it doesn't get much better than these two.
Definitely recommend to anyone needing a sturdy, fast-charging cord for their tablet.
I ordered this while waiting for a less expensive model to arrive from China. This unit is worth the price difference. Extremely well built. This one will last.

This case is lightweight, feels good in the hands, and installation is as easy as described; just push the iPad gently into the inside and listen for the snaps. Thats about all the good I can actually think of for this product, however. This case's advertising is very, very misleading. The description states that it provides a "secure closure" to the iPad for the auto wake and sleep functionality. What it actually does is lay limp and lifeless across the face of the iPad like a drunkard on the floor at the end of a Christmas party, doing no one any real good whatsoever. When I read "secure closure," I thought that it meant that there were magnets in the corners of the cover that would provide a secure closure of the cover to the face of the iPad and actually protect the screen from scratches while traveling, like almost all iPad portfolio type cases provide. I was sorely mistaken. If you just want a cheap, lightweight yet relatively good looking case for your iPad Air, then sure it will probably make you very happy. If your looking for a case that will actually do what it promises to do and actually provide a secure closure of the lid to the screen of the iPad, then no, this case will make you very angry and, given that the lid does not provide secure closure, this does not bode well for the iPad. The only thing thats providing me "secure closure" in this transaction is that Amazon has a wonderful returns policy and I will be getting a full refund for the item so that way I can find an iPad portfolio case that does work as it should.

I grabbed this to finally run a clean, hidden ethernet drop from my router across the room to my work-from-home setup, and it was surprisingly straightforward. The connectors crimped down tight on the first try, no weird signal drops or redo headaches. If you're a renter who can't drill holes everywhere or someone just tired of Wi-Fi dead zones in an apartment, this is a no-brainer. I'd easily recommend it to any remote worker or gamer wanting a stable, tucked-away wired connection without calling a pro. Took me maybe fifteen unhurried minutes.

I grabbed this for the bedroom TV and it's been flawless. Setup took maybe five minutes and the interface feels snappy and intuitive. Picture quality on Netflix and Prime is crisp with no buffering hiccups even over WiFi. The sheer number of channels is honestly surprising, tons of free stuff I actually watch. Solid little streamer that just works.

I grabbed these mainly for the gym and honestly wasn't expecting much for under thirty bucks. Right out of the box they paired fast with my iPhone and the fit is surprisingly secure, they stay put through burpees and jump rope. Sound isn't audiophile level, but for podcasts and pop playlists it's clean enough, and the bass has decent punch once you find the right ear tip size. Battery life gets me through a full week of workouts without hunting for the charger. Call quality is passable indoors, a bit muffled outside. For the price, I'm pretty impressed. Solid budget option that handles sweat and daily abuse without falling apart.

These are great headphones for casual use. I wear them to listen to music on my phone as I walk to/from/around school, and when watching videos on my laptop. PROS: - They are indeed light, and very sleek. Don't look bulky at all. - They have great sound. They don't cancel noise very well, but I didn't want complete noise cancellation anyway (I think it's dangerous when you're walking on the street.) - They are adjustable. - They are comfortable. - They come with a pouch, offering some protection. - They come with a 1-year warranty by Amazon. CONS: - They aren't foldable. I wish they were more compact, the best I can do is rotate the swivel ear cups to a "flat" position. - There aren't that many colors to choose from. - They don't look or feel expensive. Granted these aren't expensive at all, but "lightweight" shouldn't mean cheap-feeling plastic. I would buy this product again. For the price, it does what I want. Nothing more, nothing less.

Box arrived crushed on one side with zero padding inside, just the BacPac rattling around loose. First red flag. Took it out, scuffed up right out of the gate. Plugged it in to charge and the LED blinked erratically for a few seconds before going dark. After four hours on the charger it felt warm but wouldn't power anything. Tried different cables, different USB ports, nothing. Dead on arrival. For a product this pricey I expected at least a functional unit, not something that looks like it was kicked here by the delivery guy and then bricked itself immediately. Total waste of time and money.

for now ill just give it 1 star because i think i received a defective product. when i use the app on my HTC One M8 it seems slugish loading pictures or even video files. i have yet to try transfer files between the devices. when i finally managed to get a video file to play i actually watched a video for an hour. after that i the mobilelite on but was not using my phone with it 3 hours later its already blinking a red light and i charged the thing for more than 8 hours. this is definitely not what is advertised on amazon i believe it's supposed to last 13 hours streaming videos or listening to music and viewing photos on multiple devices... so i am definitely planning on returning this thing. i ordered 2 of them 1 for myself and for my father. not sure how my fathers device held up so far maybe he had better luck than me. i might try this device again but then again i might switch to the Sandisk Connect Wireless or the RAVPower FileHub. i like kingston products but this device let me down really hoping that it's just a defective product.

The CE Compass Cover for Kindle 3 is made of high density vinyl/plastic. The cover is well crafted and well designed. It effectively protects the Kindle 3 from scratches. Should you drop the Kindle, this cover would cushion the fall very effectively. All buttons on the Kindle are easily accessible. The cover is easy to open, easy to hold, easy to close. I am very pleased with my purchase.
Hey. It's a barrel adaptor. About as sexy as thumb tacks. Works like a champ, and well finished. Can't say much more about a barrel adaptor.
The cat7 cables, all 8 of them, would not produce speeds over 94mbs, and they are suppose to be 10gbps cables
Still going strong after a year of daily use.

I really wanted to love this tablet, and for streaming shows the screen is genuinely crisp and bright, colors pop nicely. But the battery life is what kills it for me. I'm getting maybe five hours of actual use before I'm hunting for a charger, which makes it tough to rely on for a long flight or a full workday of note-taking. It charges slowly too, so you're tethered to the wall more than I'd like. The build feels solid and the speakers are decent, but if portability without constant charging anxiety is your priority, this might frustrate you. It's fine for casual couch use, just keep a cable nearby.
For the cost, this thing is a steal. Holds our heavy 55" steady and swivels smooth.
Decent build for the price, but setup was way more confusing than I expected. Works okay once finally running.
It worked for a hour then died. Replaced it with a different power brick.

Great tablet. Ive been using it for a few days now and its had very few hiccups (writing this with the tablet now). Runs smooth and very responsive though i highly recommend the keyboard as typing on a touch screen is a bit less fun than on a real keyboard. The apps are also mostly made for the phone version of android. The build quality is solid and in my opinion better than most ive held. Any complaints about the back being flexible and creaky plastic are hardly noticeable. If anyone is having trouble finding one still, i definitely owe getting mine to nowinstock.net! Worth every star i gave it despite any software flaws (easily fixed by future updates im sure).
Shipped fast, worked with my HP 15-f111dx notebook with no problem!

I held off on chart plotters for years because I didn't want to drop a fortune on map cards that felt skimpy. Finally grabbed the Bluecharts on sale and honestly, for what you pay, the detail is ridiculous. Every little cove, buoy, and depth contour shows up clean, and zooming in doesn't turn it into a pixelated mess. Ran it through a tricky channel last weekend and the shading made the shallows obvious at a glance. No lag, no weird gaps. If you fish or cruise inshore, this punches way above its price. Saved me from a few rocks already, so it's basically paid for itself.
Fantastic product - Great and fast delivery - Quality and perfect Delivery
Fits snug and grippy, perfect for anyone with smaller hands.

I picked this up mainly for poolside use, and the sound quality for the size is actually decent, clear highs and enough bass to not sound tinny. But the battery life has been a letdown. I'm barely getting four hours at moderate volume, nowhere near what was advertised. It's also annoying that the charging port cover feels flimsy and doesn't stay shut, so I'm constantly worried about water getting in. Pairing is quick and stable, which I appreciate, but having to charge it mid-afternoon kills the convenience. It's okay for short hangouts, just don't expect it to last through a full day.
I am really impressed with the sound quality of these headphones. They are very comfortable and I love the fact that they fold up. I wish the plug was angled,but this is a minor issue.
Works great. The longer cable was just what I needed.

I've had this case for my Canon PowerShot G16 for over eight months now, and it's held up incredibly well. Took it on a dusty hiking trip in Utah and later to a rainy weekend in Portland, no issues whatsoever. The stitching is still tight, zipper glides smoothly, and the material hasn't frayed or stretched out. I shove it into backpacks constantly, and it still looks almost new. The little front pouch is great for an extra SD card and lens cloth. Honestly didn't expect it to survive my clumsiness, but it just keeps going. Solid, no-fuss protection.

I have 4 tablets; HP G2 10" android; Dell Venue Pro 8" windows; Vulcan Excursion 10" windows and an Ipad Mini-2. These work on the Ipad about 60% of the presses I make. The Dell they work on about 1 in 5 times I press it to the surface. The HP they work about 7 our of 10 times and the Vulcan hardly at all. I don't know why they act this way. I have an iHome stylus/pen (looks like a pen with the rubber tip at the top part) and it works 80-100% across all devices. The company is great, they send follow up emails and the package is nice. I only received 1 lanyard but it's ok; I didn't contact them to send another. For me at 7-bucks they're thrown in the drawer and my iHome pen is clipped in my Venue Pro case. They're built well but just don't work great on any of the 4 tablet devices I have, eh? Get 'em if you've tried a friends on your device and they work. For me, not so much and if I require another stylus it'll be a 2nd iHome pen/stylus. Great contact/sales follow-up; product about the cost of a burger lunch and as memorable.

I was a little worried about setting this thing up because I'm not exactly tech-savvy, but it was basically plug-and-play with my Mac. I especially love the touch surface at the top for swiping through menus, it's way smoother than I expected. Scrolling through long lists in streaming apps finally feels effortless instead of clicking arrow keys a hundred times. My only tiny gripe is that the click feels a bit stiff right in the center circle, but I got used to it after a day. Battery life has been solid too; I haven't charged it since the first time. If you're on the fence, just go for it.

I grabbed this for my gaming setup, PS5 to a 4K monitor, and it's flawless. The refresh rate handling is what sold me; no screen tearing or lag during fast-paced shooters. Build feels sturdy, connectors snap in snugly without wobbling. For the price I expected something flimsy, but it's thick and well-shielded. Zero signal dropouts so far. Definitely picking up a spare.
Looked sharper in the listing, but real-world shots feel just okay.

At first I was honestly impressed, the sound was crisp and the fit felt comfortable right out of the box. But after about a week, I started noticing a faint static that would come and go, especially during calls. It's not constant, but it's enough to be distracting when it happens. I've also had moments where the audio just drops on my end even though the other person can still hear me fine. For the price, I was hoping for more consistency. It's not unusable, and when it works it's solid, but the little hiccups keep it from being something I'd confidently recommend. It just falls short of what I expected.
Does everything I hoped for and feels sturdier than I figured it would at this price.
Love my p-Touch - i label everything that doesn't move.

I was actually surprised when this arrived a full day earlier than promised. The box itself felt premium, no crushed corners, everything sealed tight, and the watch was nestled in there with that satisfying peel-off protective film. First power-on was super quick, and the screen clarity hit me right away. Strap felt comfortable straight out of the box, no weird chemical smell or stiffness. Pairing with my phone took maybe thirty seconds, and I was off tracking a walk within minutes. I've unboxed a fair amount of tech, and this one genuinely felt like a gift to myself. Solid first impression that has only gotten better with daily use.
Works great! Its fast, plenty of storage and we got it at a good price.

Received it on the agreed timing, shipping was okay, the product box was a little bit damaged. The product itself was good, no scratches or any defects. Returned it because it was simply too bulky or big for my ear, it would detach out of my face too much. The calls were okay, and worked as intended but the voice of the other callers sounded a bit noisy. Music playback is okay. Hope this helps, if you like my review please let me know.

Nice form factor, and well, I guess it does function. Otherwise, it's just absolute junk. Speakers are garbage. Stylus makes every record sound like a 78 played during the Great Depression. This should be listed as being "for children only". On the other hand, the sound is so bad, real audiophiles would consider that abuse. Avoid if you don't like complete junk.
I owned a DVR from Replay and rented one from Time Warner Cable, and the TiVo is by far a better and more capable DVR than the two of them put together.

Best value non smart tv on amazon. This review is of the 39 inch model. I did a lot of research and at the time this was by far the highest quality and best value at this price point and size. It's not a smart tv but most smart tv's apps are hard to use and poorly implemented besides I have an xbox and chromecast so I don't care about it being a dum TV. Picture: super bright amazing image quality for the price point this tv will not disappoint! Extremely customizable image settings allow you to adjust the picture just how you want it. A quick google search will yield settings that work well but I had to fiddle with the backlighting and black levels to get it how I thought looked best. Sound: other reviews say sound sucks on this tv. The sound is actually quite good as long as it's not mounted to the wall. When wall mounted or pushed up against the wall the sound sounds tinny like how your cellphone speaker sounds when placed speaker down on a table. When the tv is on a tv stand one foot from the wall it has great, clear very loud sound that is more than adequate for my use. That being said a external subwoofer and/or sound bar would be advisable for anyone who likes a lot of extra "BASS" I love the power button on this tv! It is a small joystick located on bottom right of TV. It gives you easy access to the settings and menus in a easy to use and intuitive design. Compare this to many tv's which you can't access the menu without the remote.

Arrived a day early in perfect, snug packaging, no dents or scuffs. Unboxing was a breeze and the unit itself feels sturdy and well-made. Right out of the box, I could tell this was quality. Very impressed so far.

The box showed up with a corner pretty well crushed and the tape looked like it had been retaped at some point. Nothing inside was broken, but it definitely felt like I got a returned unit. The camcorder itself is lighter than I expected, almost toy-like, which doesn't inspire a ton of confidence. Setup was straightforward and it powered on fine, so no complaints there yet. I'm giving three stars for now because the packaging situation left a bad first impression and I'm not totally convinced this wasn't a resealed open-box item. If it holds up and the video quality delivers, I might bump it up, but right out of the gate it's a mixed bag.
Fits my Pentax 16-50 lens almost perfectly. It's a trace tight, but it goes on all the way.
This saved Me space in My car in conjunction with the Aukey Bluetooth FM Transmitter I bought.
Cable works perfectly, but the connector housing is a bit bulky for tight spaces.

Another brain-damaged design by Sony - a well-built, almost completely perfect device crippled to the point of being worthless by a small oversight. This device starts tracks at the beginning after it powers off and you turn it back on. This makes it a definite no-go for most people as it can't be used for long files such as audio books. I contacted SonyStyle support before buying because the specs didn't indicate that it would resume tracks at the current point, and I wanted to know for sure before I bought it. I have had a history of items from Sony that are like that - designed and tested by engineers, but completely untouched by non-techie consumers. SonyStyle support assured me that it would resume tracks at the current point when powered off. After buying and receiving the item, I couldn't find anything in the instructions about how to resume at current point, so I called SonyStyle again. After 45 minutes of insisting that it would do it and attempting to do it on his, the tech finally admitted that it wouldn't. It would only restart the current track. He also couldn't give an ETA on a firmware update that would allow this. So, (thanks Amazon), I sent it back. No more Sony for me. It may be fixed at some point in the future by a firmware update, but caveat emptor. For those that care, the players I found that do allow resume AND have PlaysForSure (no iPods here): Creative Zen Stone (PERFECT device, awesome in every aspect) Sandisk Sansa (AAA battery; when it dies you lose your place) Samsung YPZ5 (cool but clunky, kind of expensive) No PlaysForSure: Rio (indestructible, so don't be afraid to buy a used one) iPods (Shuffle has no visible display and is worthless, so avoid it)

I'd only recommend this laptop to someone I genuinely dislike. The build quality feels like a hollow plastic toy, and the trackpad is so jumpy it's basically unusable without a mouse. Within two months the fan started making a grinding noise that wakes up the whole room. Customer support was a total dead end, I spent three days just trying to find a live person, and when I finally did, they read from a script and disconnected. The manual is a joke, zero useful troubleshooting. If you need a machine for anything beyond collecting dust, do yourself a favor and look elsewhere. Honestly, save your money and your sanity.
These earbuds deliver crisp, clear audio that makes my podcasts feel immersive. I just wish the cord wasn't so microphonic, every little brush against my shirt comes through.
Very convenient product. I love what it does, and the price was right. Not super HQ, but it doesnt really need to do much, so....

I wanted to love this keyboard because it's super slim and easy to toss in my bag, but after about five months the experience just fell apart. The Bluetooth constantly drops mid-sentence, so I'm retyping stuff way too often. The keys themselves feel mushy and unresponsive, which makes longer typing sessions a chore. Also, the iPad never sits securely, it slips out of that shallow groove if I shift my lap even a little, and I've had a few close calls with it tumbling off the couch. Battery life is mediocre too; I feel like I'm charging it every few days despite light use. Honestly, for the price I expected something reliable, but it just adds frustration to my workflow.

Adhesive isn't great (using in a vehicle where the temperature varies significantly). The opening is also too wide for a single charging cable (iphone) and will not hold it in place. Probably works fine for indoor use on larger diameter cables.

Box showed up a day early in a nice sturdy little package, no dents at all. Unboxing felt premium for the price. Right away they looked sleek and snapped right into pairing mode. A few minor hiccups with the fit at first, but overall super solid first impression.
Total waste of time, never powered on for me.

A great charger, the powered USB slots give flexibility. I use it for all my peripherals to my laptop and as a charging point for my watch and kindle. Solid construction that doesn't feel like cheap plastic, great size for leaving it on the desktop, you can't beat it. I've recommended this to family and friends and will continue to do so. I tried several that were either too large or small, too cheap to last, or simply didn't work well or deliver what it promised. This power hub not only does what it say, but goes beyond.

Arrived two days early in perfect bubble-wrapped packaging. Opened it up and it felt solid, not cheap. Plugged it straight into my old Bose setup and the connection was instant. Super clean audio, zero static. Honestly impressed right out of the box.
Firmware problem!!!!! that has not been fixed and no solution for new firmware
Easy to install works perfectly, extended the range of my Wireless connection by 50 feet.
Great replacement for my failing one. I run four monitors on two graphics cards.

Ordered these for a quick home theater fix. They arrived in a padded mailer, no dents or scratches. Popped them out of the plastic, felt solid right away. Connected my subwoofer cable without a hitch, snug fit, zero static. Honestly impressed with how smoothly everything just worked.
Really made it nice being able to reach the distance

Arrived a day early and the box was surprisingly sturdy, no dents or crushed corners, just a snug little package. Pulling it out, the metal housing feels way more solid than I expected for the price. Buttons have a satisfying click, not mushy at all. Hooked it up right away and it basically disappeared into my setup. Took off one star because the front LED is distractingly bright in a dark room, but a little tape fixed that. Happy so far.

I grabbed this multi-pack mainly for cleaning out my keyboard and PC vents, and honestly, the nozzle design is what sold me. That thin straw attachment locks in place way better than other brands I've tried, no wobbling or popping off mid-blast. I can get right into the tight gaps around my mechanical switches without wasting air. Each can lasts longer than I expected too; I did my whole tower, two laptops, and still had pressure left. Having a few backups means I don't ration puffs like I used to. If you're picky about getting dust out of tiny crevices, this set is a no-brainer.

I have had these for a while now and I got to say I love the bass on these earbuds. I don't like paying a lot for earbuds since I have bad luck in getting them crushed or snagged on something. I would definitely buy another pair of these.
I'd only suggest this board to someone who enjoys debugging fake parts. The USB port wiggles loose mid-upload, so save your sanity and skip it.

I purchased the Fake TV after I read about it in Reader's Digest as another deterrent to buglars. Following the instructions that came with the product resulted an a realistic simulation of an operating TV in the house without the electricity use of a TV. Be sure to place it where it cannot be seen through the string pass-thru openings in blinds. I use it in conjunction with lights on a timer and an active, monitored security system.
Arrived super fast in clean, snug packaging. Felt huge out of the box, but setup was easy and it's growing on me fast.

For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but these punch way above their weight. Sound is crisp and the bass is decent for cheap earbuds. I toss them in my bag daily and they've held up fine for months. No fancy case or features, just solid daily drivers that do exactly what you need.

Great headphones. Sound is fantastic. I really like the buttons on the side of the right headphone which allows you to remotely pause, advance, etc your music playlist as well as control volume. Batteries hold charge for a long time. My only disappointment is that I enjoy listening to music through headphones when I'm trying to fall asleep at night. When connected to Bluetooth, there is an annoying blue light that flashes on the right side of the headphones. It is bright enough to annoy my wife because it flashes on her. So I had to tape a piece of black electrical tape over the light. Other than that minor annoyance, these headphones are fantastic.

This compact hub is way more solid than I expected for the price. Ports feel snug, transfer speeds are consistent, and it tucks neatly into my laptop bag without adding bulk. Honestly performs like units costing twice as much.
Great shell. I used it in the rain already and had absolutely no issues. I have a sony a6000 and the small fits very well.

I've been really happy with this purchase. The image quality blows me away, and I got the hang of the controls fast. It fits my routine perfectly and doesn't cost a fortune. Totally recommend it for everyday shooting.
I'm building a terminator. This unit will provide power to the lower voltage systems such as the eyes and its left pinky toe.
Thing barely worked right out of the box. Setup was a headache and the connection kept dropping every few minutes. Ended up just boxing it back up and returning it. Total waste.

I grabbed this mainly for the FM radio during my runs, but for what I paid, it's a steal. The screen is bright enough outdoors, and being able to expand storage with a cheap SD card means I'm never deleting stuff. Battery life easily lasts through a long workday and my commute. The included buds aren't just an afterthought, clear mids and decent bass make podcasts sound crisp. Honestly, it handles music, video, and radio without feeling like a compromise. Solid bang for your buck.
From a review I read I expected a lot more; then I looked at the price and realized I got just what I paid for: inferior product at best.
only found on amazon for me. only amazon provides you with quality hdmi cables at fractions of the cost. its impressive and you don't always need those lengthly ones to get the job done.
These work great and have stood the wear and tear of kids. Well built and dependable. Would recommend these to anyone for their children. They get used almost daily
I super love these...the sound is great and phone conversations are so clear.
Took a little digging to find these, but they work exactly as expected and the value is solid. Not flashy, just a dependable purchase I'd make again.
Great card. Easy install, and fits a low profile case. No driver problems, and it just works. I've gotten four of them so far (as we move users to dual monitors)

This radio seriously outdoes what I expected. Reception is rock solid, audio stays crisp at any volume, and it's so portable. My iPhone charges faster on it than with a wall plug. Honestly, it's become my daily go-to.

Arrived two days early and the box was really well packed, thick foam inserts, no rattling at all. First impression pulling it out was how solid the faceplate feels, not cheap plastic like I half expected. Install was straightforward and it fired right up. Out of the box the sound was a little hollow, but after messing with the EQ for ten minutes it really came alive. Bluetooth paired instantly and the call quality caught me off guard in a good way. Only knocking off a star because the display could be a touch brighter during daytime driving, but overall this thing's a solid upgrade for the price.

Got these hoping they'd be a solid upgrade from my old budget set, but for the price I expected way more. Build quality feels decent and they sit nicely on my head, so no complaints on comfort. The problem is the audio just doesn't deliver for what you're paying. Mids are muddy, bass lacks punch, and everything sounds kind of flat, like a $40 pair masking as premium. Noise cancelling is average at best, which stings when you see the price tag. I've heard better from models that cost half as much. If these were cheaper, I might be okay with the trade-offs, but at this level it's hard not to feel ripped off. Returning them and going back to my old pair.

I picked this up hoping it'd work like the listing photos showed, but it's a letdown. The lower angle isn't usable at all unless I pop my Pro 2 out of the case. Feels like false advertising since that feature was a big reason I bought it.

I take regular pictures of the Sun (with a filter of course) with my Rebel EOS T3i. Typically I use a cardboard box to provide shade for my camera and laptop. However with this hood I can, most of the time, just use the camera and the shade this hood provides. I would say that if you do a lot of picture taking in sunlight and have to use the LCD screen, then this is a 'must have'.

I've had this fan controller running in my rig for almost a year now, and it's still going strong without a single hiccup. The knobs still turn smoothly with that nice, clicky resistance, and I haven't noticed any degradation in responsiveness at all. I was a little skeptical about the build quality at this price point, but it's held up through multiple case cleanings and a cross-country move. I'm running four 120mm fans off it, and it tamed the jet-engine noise my system used to make during gaming sessions. Being able to dial each fan individually is such a game-changer for balancing airflow and silence. Honestly, it's one of those little upgrades that just keeps delivering long after you forget it's there.

This is a Great device for people with Laptops or Tablets on the go who want Multi USB Access. The Belkin 4-Port Mini USB- comes with a USB C adapter (which you plug into your electronic device) as well as Two USB-C & Two USB-A Ports. This allows for easy data transfers as well as charging options. The Belkin has up to 5Gbps Data Transfer Speeds (shared among the connected usbs) and 900mah to charge your devices such as smartphones, tablets, smartwatches and more. It's slim sleek foldaway design makes it easy to take with you on the go. The only downside i can see is that it is a mini usb charger connection and thus i have had many of these break on me over the years due to them being quite fragile. However this one so far seems quite sturdy. Overall this is a great little gadget to add to your electronic collection.

This card got the extra USB ports I needed on my old laptop, and for basic stuff like a mouse or flash drive it's fine. The problem is anything that draws real power. My external hard drive constantly disconnects unless I use a powered cable, which kind of defeats the purpose. Also, the card sticks out a bit more than I expected and I've bumped it loose twice now just moving the laptop. When it stays connected the data transfer is okay, but I can't rely on it for sustained use with higher-draw devices. It works, just barely.

I honestly didn't expect much for the price, figured I'd be dealing with flimsy parts or missing pieces, but this thing surprised me. It's solid enough for indie shoots without feeling like a toy. The follow focus is smooth right out of the box, and the matte box actually does its job cutting stray light instead of just sitting there for looks. I've used rigs triple the cost that didn't feel this balanced once I loaded it up. It's light but doesn't flex under a full setup, which was my biggest worry. If you're on the fence, just grab it. For what you get, it's kind of ridiculous.

Very easy to use. I plugged the unit into the ethernet jack, ran the configuration utility, and it was visible / accessible to Windows 10 and Android devices. The DLNA feature works well, allowing me to stream stored videos directly to my LG Smart TV.

For under ten bucks I wasn't expecting much, but this thing is solid. The vinyl shell feels tough without being bulky, and the snug fit keeps my Kindle 3 secure. I've already knocked it off my nightstand twice and not a scratch. All the ports and buttons line up perfectly. Honestly a steal for the protection you get.

I was so excited to get this thing, but the second I saw the delivery truck pull up my heart sank. The box was basically caved in on one side, and the driver just slid it off the back without a care. No pallet, no help, just a beat-up cardboard mess left in my driveway. Inside, the packaging was minimal at best, some flimsy styrofoam that was already cracked. The unit itself had scuffs right out of the gate and a loose panel that rattles when you move it. For something this expensive, I expected way more care. Honestly, it felt like I received a return or a floor model. Super disappointing first impression.
Had this enclosure for a few months now, tossed it in my bag daily. Still rock solid, no connection drops, and the aluminum body barely has a scratch.
small doesn't last that long, but the price is decent.

I grabbed this for my 2004 truck and it's been a total game changer. Setup took maybe five minutes and now I can stream podcasts and music straight from my phone. Sound quality is solid for the cost, no static or weird dropouts. Honestly can't believe this much utility was so cheap.

I wasn't expecting much from a compact camera this old, but the V1253 genuinely surprised me. The image quality holds up better than I'd guessed, especially outdoors. Video clips look decent too. It's lightweight and fits right in my jacket pocket, so I actually take it everywhere. Way more capable than I assumed it would be.

I've been using this for over 14 months now and it's still rock-solid. We moved it between three different apartments with old wiring and it never dropped a connection. Recently I tucked the extender unit behind a heavy bookshelf and the signal still punches through perfectly. Streaming 4K in the back bedroom without a hiccup. Honestly forgot it was even there until I needed to reset it after a power outage.
For the cost, this meter nails accuracy and the UV glove is a cool bonus.
Arrived fast and packed like a tank. Opened the box, slapped it on, and wow, that first shot just popped. So sharp.
for what you pay on these you get what you pay for they are not the sturdiest cases ever but they defiantly fulfill their role

This drive failed on me at a critical moment in the field. I had only owned it for 6 months, and it was not used very intensively. It spent its short life in my laptop satchel and was never dropped or otherwise damaged. During a photo shoot, I tried to copy data from my full laptop hard drive to the Passport. The drive simply would not work. It was powered on and spinning but the computer could not see it or use it. After trying to get it working, it began making clicking noises and I determined it to be dead. I'd hate to think that being jostled in my bag was enough to cause this drive to fail. If that's the case, then it has serious durability issues.

I've had this on my desk for about eight months now, and it still works like day one. The ports are holding up great even with daily plugging and unplugging, no wiggle or looseness at all. I charge my phone, earbuds, and tablet through it, plus keep a portable speaker topped up. The weight is just right so it doesn't slide around when I grab a cable. I've knocked it off the desk twice by accident and it's barely scuffed. Honestly didn't expect this level of build quality for the price.

Got this DVR set up in under an hour, which was nice. Picture quality on my old monitor is okay, but don't expect anything crisp on a modern TV, it's pretty rough. The unit itself is bulky, feels outdated. App works but it's clunky, can only view 4 cams at once. If you just need basic, budget surveillance for a workshop or storage area, it'll do. Anyone wanting sharp feeds or easy phone access should skip it.
I'd only recommend this to someone I wanted to annoy. The screen looked foggy and the corners never stayed down. Total waste.
I love it its very protective and i love the color. it was hard to get on but after I did get it on i have not had any problems with it and love the look of it with my ipad.

For the price, it's not bad. Gets the job done with my mirrorless setup, though the plastic leg locks feel sketchy. I wiped off a lot of grease out of the box. It holds steady with a small mic attached, but I wouldn't trust it with heavier gear. Fine for casual use.

For the money, this little router punches way above its weight. I grabbed it to replace a rental unit from my ISP and my monthly bill dropped instantly. Setup took maybe five minutes, plugged it into the modem, fired up the app, and named my network. My older smart TV, a couple phones, and a work laptop all locked on without a hiccup. Signal reaches the back bedroom where the old router always dropped out, and streaming is smooth even with multiple devices going. I'm not a tech person, just someone tired of overpaying, and this thing delivers reliable everyday performance for a fraction of what I expected to spend. Solid purchase.

I picked this up hoping to tidy up my desk, but it's completely dead on arrival. Tested with three different devices and none got any power. Feels flimsy too. I'd only recommend it to someone who needs a prop cord for a fake setup. Total waste of money.
For the cost, these blew me away. Solid feel, works without a hitch, and the build quality feels way above the price tag.

Works okay for what it is, but the tuning dial feels flimsy and the speaker crackles at higher volumes. If you just want something cheap to keep in the garage or give a kid who won't mind the quirks, it's passable. I wouldn't use it as my main radio though.

I wasn't sure a simple wrist strap could feel this secure, but it really locks things down without any bulk. Perfect for quick festival shoots where a full harness gets in the way. Build quality is solid, way better than I expected for the price. Totally worth it.
Good quality product. I mostly transfer files to it of 1GB or less.
Worked perfectly first time with my iMac. Drive only got about 35MB/sec transfer rate, but that's about what you'd expect for USB 2. For under $10, I can't complain.

Took a bit of trial and error to dial it in with my Sony, but once set it's super reliable for static studio work. I'd definitely recommend it to product photographers or anyone doing tripod-based shots who hates camera shake. Not ideal for fast-moving subjects, but for careful setups it's a solid little tool.

It's okay, barely. You get what you pay for every time. It's good for a starter 5.1 setup but don't expect any oooos and aahhs. I purchased the exact item from Walmart for $59.99. The sound is okay if you use an equalizer to balance things out. Small speakers *always* mean small sound. The four corner speakers are 8 Watts each. Don't let the "150W" fool you. The center speaker's qualitty reminds me of a pocket radio's speaker. It's very mid-range only and there's almost no tonal quality to voices. The cables should be longer. I'm going to have to make extensions for my application. That's not hard since the speakers, except for the front right, are RCA. I believe a 9-pin D-SUB extension cable will work for the front right speaker but I'm not sure yet. I would much rather have the volume control separate instead of on one of the speakers as my right speaker is four feet away.
The transfer speeds are surprisingly fast, making my large video backups a total breeze.

I wasn't sure this mesh system would handle my thick plaster walls, but it blew past my expectations. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and the app guided me without any head-scratching. My backyard and upstairs office now get full bars where my old router gave up completely. Streaming 4K and hopping on video calls feels effortless. Honestly, I wish I'd switched sooner.

I've had this vx-981K for about four months now, and it's already showing serious wear. The battery door creaks and doesn't latch tightly anymore, I've had to tape it shut twice during shoots. The flip screen hinge is getting wobbly too, and it makes a grinding noise when I adjust it. I'm not rough with my gear; this thing just feels flimsy. The plastic body scuffs way too easily, and one of the port covers snapped off completely after maybe a dozen uses. For the price, I expected it to hold up better than a cheap toy. Really frustrating because when it worked right, the footage was decent, but I can't trust it for anything important anymore.
This product does exactly what I needed. I converted from digital output to analog input. Just as advertized. It is plenty sexy, but it is now hidden behind a dresser.
Larger than I expected. I just received it, may be happier after I get to use it

Picked this up to avoid replacing my whole car audio setup and honestly it's been a game changer. Setup was stupid simple and the connection stays rock solid every drive. Sound quality impressed me right away. Totally refreshed my daily commute without any hassle.

I picked this up for digital sculpting in Blender, and it's a total game-changer. No more weird palm drag or smudges messing with my strokes. Honestly, any digital artist who hates the sticky-glide feeling on a tablet should grab one. I'd especially nudge 3D modelers and photo retouchers toward it.
I've been using this cable for a few months now and it's held up perfectly, no crackling or loose connections at all.
Sparin never disappoints, this one went on smooth and looks invisible.
Thought I was getting a deal but these are cheaply made and barely functional. Not worth the few bucks I paid. Save your money and avoid these.

When I tried to charge it, my laptop locked. My Sony laptop (Vista OS) never locked before. I managed to un-lock it with no apparent damage to the PC. Obviously if you can't charge the Flip you can't use it.

Arrived a day late and the outer box was pretty crushed, not a great start. The product itself was wrapped okay inside, but the plastic tray had a small crack in one corner, probably from the rough shipping. First impression once I got it set up was that it feels solid and looks clean, but the initial experience left me a bit wary about long-term durability. The packaging wasn't nearly enough to protect it, just some thin foam and a flimsy cardboard sleeve. I haven't had time to fully test everything yet, but if the box arrived that beat up, I'm hoping nothing internal is loose. Decent product, but the shipping and packaging need serious improvement.

Great product. I wanted internet connectivity for my Pioneer audio receiver. The cost for the Pioneer wi-fi adapter was about twice the cost (or slightly more) of this Netgear device, yet the N300 exceeds my expectations in every way possible (e.g., for range, transmission through walls, clarity, etc.). I have used other Netgear equipment in the past with similar results, so this really wasn't what I considered to be a risk when I made the purchase. You won't be disappointed with this wi-fi adapter.

This CPU was too fast. Amazon needs a way to prevent OUIs (ordering under the influence). One morning not long ago I woke up with a terrible hangover, not quite sure what happened the night before. Upon checking my email that morning, I noticed a shipment notification for a Intel Core i7-3930K Hexa-Core Processor. I realized I must have ordered in a drunken blur, and through the haze I indeed remembered placing some kind of order. I was shocked, but decided to go ahead and let the order go through. After all, I had been debating this purchase for weeks and just didn't want to shell out the dough for this wicked CPU. After all, I told myself, I already had a quad-core 2700k overclocked to 5 GHZ, why would I need the 3930k with six cores and amazing stats? But it was on the way. The 2700k at 5 GHZ was already a beast, and I found through practice I could reign it in and handle it, so thought I was ready for this 3930k and its hexacore awesomeness... well I was wrong. To make a long story short, the power of my PC outfit with this CPU literally destroyed my mind. It was far, far beyond my control. I wrestled it for hours in epic battles through various intense games and applications, certain I could reign it in, but in the end it left me a quivering, sweating mass, as blood and drool sprayed forth from me in profuse waves. I don't want to go into the detail of the things I saw while this CPU rendered 12 threads of everything I threw at it at billions of calculations per second, but suffice it to say mortal human minds can't handle it. In summary, I am sending it back -- it is too fast. I need to give it 5 stars because it is truly amazing. Words of advice: Do not OUI, and do not think other fast CPUs you have used have in any way prepared you for this one -- they have not.

I honestly didn't expect this much punch from something so compact. Set it up in under a minute with my Android phone and it just works. Took it kayaking last weekend, got splashed, rolled around in my dry bag, still sounds crisp and full. Battery lasted the whole trip. Compared to my old bulky speaker, this thing's a game changer for portability without sacrificing volume.

For the price, this charger is a steal. It powers up my Galaxy just as fast as the original that came with the phone. The longer cord is super convenient too. Honestly, it feels sturdy and works perfectly every time.
Does exactly what it should. Keeps my device charged without any issues, and the cord feels sturdy enough for daily classroom use. Solid purchase.

Works well enough for what I use it for (as a test server). I'm tempted to get something else similar, perhaps a NUC with Core i5 instead, for a family member. It works really well, is quiet, and you can put it anywhere as long as it has ventilation.

the only problem i had with this speakers is the battery, when playing without plugin the charger the volume will become less loud, than when the charger is plugged in, other than that the sounds is really good loud and clear but lack bass....

I love this little gadget and have used it a handful of times during this cold season. Sad to see that they are no longer available. The instructions are pretty clear and I just can't get over how awesome and easy it is to simply place the device in a cup of water. Growing up, we always had the huge R2D2-style contraptions posted up in our hallway during the winter months - given that it was in a house made sense but it was always a pain to refill. This is simple and convenient to just have on your nightstand or side table; the power cord being so long is a plus too. Once it's plugged in and you hit the power button, a nice, steady mist comes out. I am just in love with Deneve products...they always seem to have fun stuff!
I like my transmitter for the most part there isn't a lot of intereference like I had with my last one. Though there are still times that I still get it if I try adjusting the volume or heat.

Bought this as a gift for my brother months ago, and he's put some serious hours into it. The disc still looks brand new, no scratches or read errors even after constant swapping in and out of the console. The case hinges haven't cracked either, which is a small thing but honestly rare with how rough he can be. Gameplay holds up great over long sessions, no glitches tied to wear. Solid purchase that's proven it can handle repeated use.
Took a few weeks to figure out all the features, but it's held up perfectly through daily drops and heavy use.

I've been using these for a few weeks now and honestly, for everyday listening they're solid. The sound is warm and clear, way better than I expected at this price. They feel sturdy but do get a bit heavy after an hour or so. Wish they included a basic inline mic for calls. Overall, totally worth it.

Just got it and it is a delight to use in drawing and Notes Applications which is why I bought it. It feels like a pen or pencil when writing and you can see where the tip is. That is a great advantage for detail work and for writing. I don't know if it would work as well for browsing and selecting things since it makes a small blunt sound as you touch the screen. It is annoying when pointing at things, but not there when sliding for writing and sketching. I bought spare points in case they are not available in the future. So far so good and I am loving it. Another great design is, that you can unwind the bottom part from the top and screw it back on with the tip fitting inside the top part, where it is safe from any danger.
Great sound. Great microphone. Connects with no problem. Excellent battery life.
Fit my spotting scope and tripod prefix very good product.

Had this little diffuser for a few months now and it's held up great. Been tossed in my bag, bent, squished, and it still snaps right back into shape. No tears or weak spots on the velcro either. Solid bit of kit.
I just needed something portable to take away the harshness of my external speedlite for when I cannot take my other equipment with me. I love the velcro strap and that it can fold flat for storage.
I ordered my theater, and I love it. minor issues connecting my ipod. The system is great and the price is right.

This unit was good for a bit. The overall construction needs reworked as after only two weeks the stand snapped off, broke, not to be repaired or refitted. The shell itself is of nice material to absorb impact during a fall. Ok for the price but needs to be re-engineered to put a better quality stand on it.

Love this radio! Use it at work in a metal building with a lot of electrical interference. This radio gets great reception and stays locked on the stations. Best radio I have tried so far. This is a small radio so if you are looking for something with some power you will need to run the Aux out to some powered speakers, but at your desk or workstation it is great.
The scroll wheel has such a satisfying click, super precise. Battery lasts forever and pairing was instant on my Mac.
Surprised how well this performs for the price. Linux setup was painless, and real-world speeds actually hit what the box claimed.

I picked this up for my older Mac setup and honestly wasn't expecting much for the price, but it's been a pleasant surprise. Speed-wise it's fine for regular file transfers and backing up photos, not blazing, but totally adequate. Build quality feels decent, though the plastic shell is a little glossier than I'd like. One thing I didn't anticipate was how warm it gets after a couple hours of use; it's not alarmingly hot, just noticeably toasty. I also had to swap out the included cable for a shielded one I had lying around to avoid some weird disconnects, but since then it's been rock solid. For casual use it punches above its weight, just know you might need to tweak the setup a tiny bit.
This speaker stopped working and now it doesn't turn on so I'm returning it

Ordered this to wrangle my cord mess. They work great! I would definitely buy again. The velcro works rather well, seems of good strength and are more than big enough for my 5-6 cords I'm securing. The package held more than enough ties too. My one complaint is that I wish it secured in a bit of a different way, but it completely fine for hold cables in place.
Works like a charm. I'm using this on my Bose Wave radio listening to Sirius XM off my phone BUT I had to disconnect the CD changer for it to work properly. This is a Bose issue not the adapter.
Sound is way richer than I expected for the price, clear and surprisingly loud for their size.

All I will say is that this is my 2nd purchase of this item. Why? So I can have one facing out the rear of my work vehicle. I spend a LOT of time on the roads and.. well.. you know.. Bought my first one (forward facing) almost 3 years ago. Yes, it saved me a couple issue for sure. With all of the options out there (including newer DOD products), to come back and spend the extra money on this DOD should be testament itself into its quality. BTW, although this came with a 16GB, I still upgraded to a 64GB flash. Did I need it, don't know... LOL. I feel better storing more info before rewriting though. Can't go wrong here. BTW. I also purchased extra mounts and wires so I can transfer the cameras to my personal car. Takes 1 minute to switch both of them over as both cars are wired and ready to go.
Had this little drive on my keychain for months now, still works perfectly every single time I plug it in.
Arrived way faster than expected and was packed super securely. Pulled it out, set it up in minutes, and it's flawless.

I picked this up before a snorkeling trip in Cozumel, and I'm so glad I did. Right out of the box, the housing felt solid and well-made. Installation was straightforward, and once the camera was sealed inside, I had full access to every control without any stiffness or awkward finger positioning. Underwater, the images came out crisp, with no fogging or leaks whatsoever. I also used it while kayaking and got splashed constantly, zero issues. The peace of mind alone is worth it, knowing my camera is fully protected around saltwater and sand. It's definitely a premium accessory, but the build quality and performance justify the cost if you're serious about bringing your SD770IS into wet environments.
Worked fantastically. Good heavy paper makes an easy seamless background. Good color as well. It is heavy and it does want to unroll, but I read the comments here and was prepared with a clamp.
It does the job, it is hard to focus though. I would use it if I wasn't pressed on time.

The nicest cable I have ever used, and cheap! The wires fabric texture, light weight, slimness, and strength are the best I have ever seen. Buy one for your headphones if they have a 3.5mm input. Won't sound better, but it will feel better.
The box was totally crushed when it arrived, and the thing felt like a hollow toy.
Arrived fast and packed securely with no damage. Took it out, plugged in, and instantly worked with my iPad Air. Feels sturdy and well-built. Super convenient to finally move around freely.
This is the third Toshiba Canvio external hard-drive that I've bought and have been happy with all of them.

I picked this up for a budget build I was putting together, and honestly, it's a mixed bag. For the price, the cooling performance is just okay, it keeps my Ryzen 5 from throttling during gaming, but it runs a few degrees warmer than I expected based on the specs. The fan isn't whisper-quiet under load either; there's a noticeable hum that cuts through my case noise. Installation was straightforward on an AM4 board, which I appreciated, but the mounting brackets felt a little flimsy. I wasn't expecting premium build quality at this price point, and it does the job, but I can't help feeling a slightly better budget option might exist. It's functional, nothing more.
great motherboard. stable as rock. running amd fx 8320 @ 4.4ghz stock vcore never freeze/errors

Nice but..I thought I was smart buying another flip 2 only to find out it cannot be linked to another flip 2 for wireless music on 2 devices even with the JBL App. For that you need the waterproof flip 3 or charge, both more$. I would have paid the extra $20 for the flip 3 if I had known that. Music quality is still good. 2 devices playing at once would be better.
Arrived late in a flimsy padded envelope, box was completely crushed. Unit felt cheap and rattled right out of the package.

I had high hopes for this setup, but after about three months of daily use, the build quality is really disappointing. The motion sensor stopped registering reliably after the first month, it misses me walking right past it maybe half the time now. The multipurpose sensor on my back door already has a crack near the battery compartment, and I've barely touched it since installation. The hub itself runs warm constantly, which doesn't inspire confidence for long-term reliability. I'm not rough with my gadgets, so seeing this kind of physical wear so soon is a letdown. For the price, I expected something that could handle normal household life without falling apart. It still technically works, but the durability issues make me regret the purchase.

I purchased a system from Lorex and when I opened the box, it was missing parts, so Lorex said that I had to purchase $50 worth of parts (power cord, remote control & mouse) to make it work. Yes... those items should have been in the box (as stated on the box). After buying the parts and plugging it into an outlet, it did not work. Lorex does not want to replace or refund it. I have been fighting with Lorex for over a year about this. I went to Wal-Mart and purchased a system that I installed and had working in just a few minutes. Save your time & money, go to Wal-Mart and get a different system (not made by Lorex). I wish that someone would have warned me about Lorex and they way they treat customers. Also, if you have a problem with Lorex products, you are told to submit a (claim) ticket on-line. After they can't or better yet "won't" help, they cancel your ticket and if you want to continue to fight them, you have to submit another ticket. Like I said, I have been fighting with them for over 1 year (they cancel my tickets)... do you want to do that to?????????????????? Just thought I would help.
Got this set expecting just basic metal squares, but they feel solid and fit perfectly. Temps dropped more than I thought during heavy retro gaming sessions.
Battery lasts all day, pairing is instant, and they survived a full summer of pool splashes without a hitch.

This hub does exactly what I needed, driving three external displays from my work laptop without any flickering or dropouts. Setup was literally plug and play, which surprised me given past dongle drama. It runs a bit warmer than I expected, and the boxy shape feels like a step back in design compared to older models I've seen. Still, it's tucked behind my monitor so aesthetics don't matter much. Not quite perfect, but rock-solid performance where it counts.

This thing arrived two days earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was minimal but sturdy, no ridiculous plastic clamshell to wrestle with, just a neat little box with everything snug and protected. First impression pulling it out: way lighter than I thought it would be, but the matte finish feels really nice in hand and doesn't attract fingerprints. Setup took maybe 90 seconds before I had a podcast playing, and the sound was immediately clearer than my old speaker. I mostly toss it in my gym bag or set it on the patio table, and it hasn't picked up a scratch yet. Honestly, for the price, I was braced for something flimsy and this just isn't. Very happy.
Fast shipping works as advertised replacement cord for Samsung TV...
These batteries last way longer in my wireless mouse than any other brand I've tried, months without swapping. Super dependable for everyday use.
Honestly thought the 3TB limit was set in stone, but this drive popped right up without a single hiccup. Way smoother setup than I braced for, and it's been flawless since day one.

Got this Corsair GPU bracket in two days flat, box was pristine with dense foam inside keeping everything locked down tight. First thing I noticed pulling it out, the build quality is seriously solid, all metal where it counts, and the new standoffs look exactly like the updated ones I saw in the fix-it threads online. Slid right into my 3080 without any sketchy flex or weird pressure points, PCB stayed dead flat the whole install. Temps dropped almost 8 degrees under load, and the fans barely ramp now. Honestly didn't expect such a smooth experience given the early drama, but they clearly sorted the hardware and the packaging gave me confidence before I even opened the anti-static bag.

Very nice, quality is average plus, meaning they're well worth the money when you consider what you pay for a stylus in a store. I just received them so I can't speak to how long they'll last but you get a bunch and they all work well on my Samsungs (note tablet, S-5 phone) so I'm pretty happy. I'd definitely buy again if I needed a mess of styluses.

I expected decent battery life but got a solid 8 hours, which was a nice surprise. They lean heavy on the bass, so highs aren't as crisp as I'd prefer. For the price, they're comfy enough and get the job done well.

I picked these up hoping they'd handle a slightly thicker HDMI run, and they're just a touch snug for that. Still, they hold everything securely and haven't budged once. For standard cables they'd be perfect. Honestly, they do the job better than I expected for the cost. No regrets.

Nice little hub - great for my USB port-challenged laptop. Delivers what's expected, although you can't bus-power more than a few peripherals at once. Works well with both Mac and PC, and I like how you can switch the ports on and off manually. Overall, a great unpowered hub - if you want to charge multiple devices at once, get something powered instead, but this delivers what's promised.
Excellent price and excellent product. I will fallow buying to you for the good price. In Costa Rica, it cost about $10.00 each one. Panasonic is a very good product.
works perfectly with my gtx960 and my 4ktv@60hz, very well constructed, braided cable looks very durable and does what it supposed to. gaming and youtube @ 4k is great

Arrived two days early in a sturdy little box with each battery sealed tight. Popped one in right away and my camera fired up instantly, no errors, full charge showing. Feels just as solid as the original. Honestly impressed right out of the gate.

This is a high quality surge protector for a reasonable price. I trust the APC brand above many other brands, and the high quality of this unit is very apparent when compared with cheaper surge protectors.

We have multiple iPads with different movies and TV shows on each. With the iPad storage limitations I had to pick and choose periodically to remove and add movies. With the Seagate Wireless everyone can watch whatever they want. I copied almost our entire DVD collection as well as our iTune movies to the Seagate and now there's never a shortage of something to watch. I have a 6 year old daughter and entertainment on the go is a must. We also connect the iPad to our T&C van and watch movies stored on the Seagate on the car DVD screen. The picture is not as crystal clear as when a DVD is playing but we're willing to sacrifice a little clarity. We recently went on a short (maybe 1.5 hour trip) with 5 kids in the car ranging in age and this was a life saver. The kids could hear and see the movie perfectly. Have you ever tried to watch 5 kids try to listen and watch a movie on one iPad? It's not a good thing. The Seagate makes it possible to give choices on what movie to watch and I carry it with me everywhere now.

I love the look of the case. That is what drew me to it in the first place. The lack of space for optical drives didn't bother me one bit since I wasn't going for that anyway. Only reason it got one less star from me is because there is little space for cable management. Maybe I'm just not as good at cable management than I could be, but I just couldn't manage to route all my cables from the back. I'll keep working on it but then this is a smaller case.

In terms of color and contrast, this is the best lens I have for my Sony A6000. My Sigma 30 2.8 lens is close to this Rokinon. I love the effective 18mm angle and highly recommend it, especially given the modest price.
It's a good little clock, it is not too bright. You get what you pay for, however it will not pick up an FM station 20 miles away but it is a good alarm clock.

I've been using these cases for years. Wonderfully compact and safe storage. We have a large collection, close to 20 cases. Added simple labels to each individual binder cover and Avery dots to number pages. Makes it easy to find disks and put disks put back where they will be found again. No problems with scratched disks.

This product is all plastic with a fake leather like material pretty much glued on to the casing. My wife's ipad mini 2 retina would not fit this case. One side corner would snap in and the other wouldn't even push in. Past reviews say they fixed issues with the hinge. I do not agree. This product wasn't even worth the $30 I spent including shipping and the overall price is $99... It's plastic. Cheap plastic. Only reason I give a 3 star rating is because the seller responded quickly and because their shipping was so quick. My seller review would be considered a 5 star.
I've been using this monitor daily for two weeks now and honestly, everything just pops. Text is super sharp, colors feel natural, and setup took maybe two minutes out of the box.

Well worth the $$....My Garmin wasn't holding a charge anymore. Figured I'd give this battery a shot. I really think this battery holds a charge longer than my original. Love it. Easy to install yourself too!

Works very good and I like then a lot. I was a little bummed that one of the rubber war covers fell off and I can't locate it. But I never did check to make sure they were secure (you are giventhree sizes).

I've been using this MP3 player for about a week, mostly during my commute and workouts. The sound quality is surprisingly crisp for the price, especially with a decent pair of earbuds. My favorite part is the physical buttons, they're clicky and responsive, so I can skip tracks without pulling it out of my pocket. Battery life holds up well through a full day of on-and-off listening. My only gripe is the screen is pretty dim outdoors, even at max brightness, which makes browsing albums in sunlight a squint-fest. Also, the menu feels a bit clunky until you memorize the layout. Still, for straightforward music playback without any fuss, it's a solid little gadget.

Nice magnifier for the price. I looked at 3 brands when shopping. Eotech, Vortex and Sightmark. The Eotech is great but it is way out of my price range. The sightmarks action to move it out of the way felt cheap and was not very fluid. The Vortex went on sale for 159 which was a bargain. The VMX-3T flips out of the way with a simple button movement. Can easily be done on the fly one handed. I like the way it feels. The magnifier is very clear and works great when paired with a Eotech 512. It comes with a hight spacer so that you can raise it a smidge if it is to low. You can fully adjust where it is pointed through the side and top adjustments (like any scope). The one down side which I find on all scopes if your eye is not at the right distance it shrinks the field of view. I did not find this to bad in shooting with it though. I have not used there warranty service but it covers everything except theft and intentional damage for the lifetime of the product. I bought 2 additional ones to the one I already had at the sale price.

Showed up a day early in a sturdy little box, no dents or crushed corners. Took it out and everything looked clean, well-cut, and ready to go right away. The backing film peeled off smoothly, and adhesion felt strong immediately. Honestly impressed by how carefully it was packed for such a simple item. Solid first impression.

I am getting to hate them even though I have 3. As noted by others they can give a false open reading. This is not good since it is designed to trigger other functions in a system. I have had other Aeon products go stupid so if your playing there kinda cool. If it is a critical application, just don't do it yet.

When I first received the clip and turned it on, it froze while listening to the radio. I managed to get it unfroze by connecting it to the computer - holding down the power button didn't do anything. I spent a little time transferring audio files to it, then it went dead completely. Won't turn on. Won't connect to computer. Submitted it for return to Amazon today and now I have to wait another week to try it again. Considering I bought some accessories to go with it, if I can't get a functioning mp3 player, I'll be pretty upset at the waste of time and money. ETA: I received a replacement which worked and as of April 2014, it's still working fine.
Perfect for anyone wanting to learn soldering and basic coding hands-on.

I bought this for my nephew's birthday and honestly, I'd only recommend it to someone you're trying to disappoint. Felt hollow and the buttons were mushy right out of the box. It powered on once, flickered, then never again. Total waste of money. Lesson learned, next time I'll just give him cash.
The magnetic clasp is super strong, never accidentally detaches.
its a compact bag. if you want carry more things. its better you bought bigger one. its tight fit bag. if you want to buy bag for only camera its a good one.
These are much better than the Uniden OEM batteries. Having 1400mAh makes a world of difference. My portable Uniden phones last much longer off the base chargers.

I picked this up six months ago for a construction site documentation gig, and it's been knocked off scaffolding, rained on, and stuffed into dusty toolboxes more times than I can count. Honestly expected it to give up after a few weeks, but it's still cranking out crisp, sharp photos without a hiccup. The body has a few scuffs, but everything works like day one, lens mechanism, buttons, seals all holding strong. I don't baby it at all, just wipe it down and keep going. If you need something that survives real daily abuse without complaint, this little tank delivers.
Been using it for a year. No issues very solid. I would recommend to anyone looking for a solid jump drive.
Gave to my husband as a birthday gift, so far it seems to work well. And I like its compact size.

I grabbed these mostly for commuting and honestly didn't expect much beyond convenience, but they surprised me in a few ways. The pairing is instant every time and the range is solid enough that I can leave my phone charging in the other room without the signal cutting out. Comfort-wise, I've worn them through entire work-from-home afternoons with zero ear fatigue, which is rare for me. The bass has a bit more presence than I anticipated, though the mids feel slightly recessed on acoustic tracks. For podcasts and casual listening they absolutely deliver, and the physical buttons are way more reliable than touch controls I've used on pricier sets. Not perfect, but they punch above their weight for daily use.

My Canon G-7 arrived a day later than the Prime estimate, and the outer box was pretty crushed, no padding inside at all, just the camera box sliding around. The camera itself looks fine cosmetically, but the battery was completely dead on arrival, which killed the whole unboxing excitement. After charging overnight, it powered up okay, but the mode dial feels a little looser than I expected for this price point. First few test shots are decent, though I'm not blown away yet. Honestly, the packaging left such a sloppy first impression that it's hard to feel great about the purchase right now. I'll give it more time.
I've been using this daily for weeks and it just works flawlessly every time.
This was purchased as a gift for our 19 year old who has a real interest in photograph. He has fallen in love with the Nikon gem and is rarely seen without it. Easy to use - great photos as well.
It arrived on time and it worked. only issue is that it isn't that bright and my window is tinted(case window).

This does work with the tablet, though you must connect it as a bluetooth device. Put it into search mode by holding both the triangle and circle buttons until the mic button flashes (may need to be charged first). You do lose some functions with it this way, but the voice search and other buttons work as intended. I had to search a lot to find that info, so hopefully it helps someone else out. Bigger than I thought, about twice the size of the aluminum Apple TV remotes, but it is so much more comfortable to use as well. Great overall construction even if a little bit on the pricey side.

it fits my tab 10.1, my boyfriend's tab 7.0, my iphone, his galaxy sl, fits anything you want to stand. It is adjustable, and adjustments are small hence you can make small adjustment to fit your viewing angle perfectly.
Decent cards for older cameras, just don't expect blazing speeds for 4K video.

I've been using this for a few months now, and it's held up great. Took a couple minor bumps with no issues. The build feels solid, and it stays firmly in place even after repeated setups. Still works like day one, really happy with the quality over time.

I'll be honest, I expected it to be just okay for the price, but it blew me away. Pairing took seconds and the sound on both ends is crisp, way better than my built-in car system. My husband actually asked if I got a new phone because I sounded so clear. Compact, stays put, and makes highway calls effortless.

The magnetic base is nice and strong on my metal desk, but the clip just doesn't grip thin cords at all. My USB-C cable slips right out the moment I move my phone. Ended up taping it down, which defeats the whole purpose.
The connection drops constantly unless I'm sitting right next to the dongle. Pretty pointless purchase overall.

I love that radio. It is a good brand too. But it has some issues. First it seemed to use a lot of juice. I seemed to be putting batteries in it every so often. That was not that much of a pain.
