But I discovered that once a station was program into the radio there was no way to blank it out. All you can do is program another station in that pre-set. In other words there was no way to clear all or any of the pre-sets. I tried and tried all to no avail. I am just not that thrilled with radio. I have seen gone back to the simply little Sony radio that costs about $30 or so. That bad boy is rugged, easy to use and lasts forever on one battery. The Sangean takes two.

I bought this case from a store for my new iPad (3rd gen). While it does fit, it would slip out of the smaller clips on the left side of the case that hold the device every once in awhile. Finally, after having it for only 4 days, one of the clips snapped. So, needless to say I'm looking for a new case... Here's what I liked about the case: - Very thin, and didn't add much bulkiness to the device - Nice thick cover with soft inside, made me feel that the screen would be protected. - The faux-leather cover dosen't look too "fake" What I didn't like: - The band that is made to hold the device closed. I stopped using it after the first day. Don't they know that this will just break off? Plus, the cover stays closed well on it's own. - The small clips that hold the iPad in the case on the left side. I couldn't understand why they couldn't use a longer band to hold the device in like the right side.

Bought these for our security team's daily use. They feel solid and the audio clarity is surprisingly good. Had a slight fit issue with our older radios but they work perfectly with the newer models. Solid value for the price overall.

I actually bought these for crafting, not cables, and they're perfect. The Velcro is gentle but holds lightweight materials securely. I love that I can trim them to any size without fraying. Super handy for organizing sewing supplies too.

This monitor is gigantic, and a pretty good value. I have really enjoyed using it, enough that I am thinking of buying one and carrying it to work. I don't know if it really is better on the eyes, but it's beautiful.

The optical zoom on this camera is insane, I could capture crisp close-ups of my kid's soccer game from the bleachers without any blur. Battery lasts forever too. Totally worth it for capturing fast action without lugging around bulky gear.

Price to quality this was a winner. I plugged it into a 2002 Sony Vaio desktop running windows XP and after installing the associated software I was on the home wifi within 5 minutes. My wifi hub is two rooms away and I don't appear to have any range issues. My browsing speed is about what it was the last time I booted the relic up back in 2007, but this is no fault of the adapter.

I grabbed this drive for moving large video files around, and the read speeds are honestly incredible, transfers finish way faster than I expected. My only gripe is the plastic casing feels a little cheap, and it heats up noticeably during sustained use. Still, if raw performance is what you care about, it delivers where it counts.
Does way more than I thought it would, especially for the price.

I bought two of these after seeing family use the platform. I like the front lip which keeps the laptop from skidding forward. The keyboard is at the right pitch for me on my desk or on my 'lap'. The the raise curve fits snuggly over the laptop for easy take along. ALLSOP - Cool Channel Notebook Platform, 13w x 12d x 2.8h, Charcoal GrayThe platform is comfortable on my lap. Only thing I might wish for is a little more width for a mouse. Also, this would take more planning to use with a 17" screen.

This was a gift (I knew my neighbor wanted it), so I can't say for sure. My husband helped with the installation and it worked fine, so I'm assuming everything was as expected. Hard to rate something like a cable. What can I say? It looked perfectly stable, like a cable!

I bought this antenna originally because in my apartment I didn't have straight shot to the southern sky, so I thought buying this and placing it near or outside my window it would work fine. Well, to say the least, that didn't work out as planned. I could barely find reception, but I can't blame it for not working in that situation I guess it wasn't intended to do such magic. However, on the good side, I have since moved and have mounted the product on the side of the house. Now I get perfect reception with absolutely zero interruptions! Would recommend to anyone that has a place for mounting.

We took a gamble on these for a 12-hour road trip and honestly expected flimsy mounts. Nope, they stayed locked in place over potholes and gravel roads without budging. Our kids watched movies the whole way with zero issues. For the price, I was shocked at how sturdy and simple they were. Total win.
Picked these up for a budget build and they're rock solid. Honestly can't beat the performance for what they cost.
I was nervous about the waterproof seal after replacing it myself, but I've taken it on three rainy runs now and it's bone dry inside. Super impressed with the fit and performance.
was a gift but was returned he bought one too
Very imprecisely. You have to try very hard to make it work on the screen. Well, I guess the tablet was not designed to be written on anyway so the pen is an OK product to me.

I bought one of these last year and it was great, little or no static or interference. This one transmits especially well. You can't go wrong for [...]. One major problem with it is the wire. Be careful with it! When you connect it to your power outlet be careful not to pinch or pull on the wire especially if your power outlet is recessed in the dashboard. It'll become detached and you'll end up having one channel go out. You can play with it and have both channels work but eventually it'll fail totally. Also the wire protrudes out of the left side of the unit. If you want to keep your player in your lap while driving ok but if you keep it anywhere else this will cause the wire to become pinched. A good idea with this unit would be to plug it in vertically or sideways or even upside down so as not to pinch or pull on the wire. Make sure there's no tension on it. The looser the better. It'll last longer that way. I really like this transmitter and bought another after my first one suffered the pinched wire fate after a year. I'd encourage anyone to try this out,it's a good one. JUST DON'T PINCH OR PULL ON THE WIRE!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

A quick explanation of the title. I have severe to profound loss of hearing in the mid to high frequency range and my Sharp LC60LE835U become just about unintelligible with its poor speakers (wonderful picture). Was looking to go CC but, after reading the reports of soundbars, decided to try one last thing. Had a 5:1 Bose system whose speaker system couldn't easily be connected after moving into a new home. It came down to the Sony CT-150 and the Yamaha ATS 1010 (from Costco). The decision was made after reading that I could use my powered sub-woofer with the Yamaha, plus its great glossy black looks matched the TV, blueray (& Cisco VCR.. to some extent). Real simple optical connection from TV, and wow!... I could now hear with clarity, from movies to TV series. I replayed a couple of TV shows & movies that I had previously had real difficult time in understanding, and now had a whole different audio experience. A boon for baby boomers for sure. Now for the short list of cons, not deal breakers but folks should be aware of them. The built-in sub-woofer is simply not up to the job - get an external unit to really appreciate the bass (especially in movies). The surround sound is not wondrous, and to advertise it as a 7:1 experience is just plain deceptive. Yamaha should get real with its claims about this aspect. However, I would imagine this unit is just about there with others in this class and no-one in their right minds can really expect a decent surround sound from a soundbar. To conclude, the Yamaha far exceeded my expectations in uplifting my TV experience - if you have similar hearing loss, just give this a try.

Bought one of these refurbished Megabooms about four months ago. Sound was solid at first, but after using it mainly indoors at moderate volume, the bass started rattling pretty badly around month three. The fabric wrap also started peeling near the bottom edge. It still works, but that rattle kills the listening experience. For the price, I guess I can't complain too much, but I definitely expected a refurb to hold up longer than this. Probably fine if you just need something for occasional background music.
Honestly, I mostly use it straight for music and it just delivers clean, full sound every time. Super happy with it.

I went into this expecting just a basic navigation aid, but it seriously overdelivers. The setup was practically nonexistent, plugged it in, and it found a signal faster than my phone ever does. I love that the display stays crisp even with direct sunlight hitting it, which was a weak point on my last device. The spoken street names are spot-on, no weird mispronunciations that make you laugh mid-turn. Lane guidance saved me twice already in downtown traffic I normally dread. It's super lightweight and doesn't feel like a clunky brick on the dash. Honestly, for what I paid, I assumed I'd be compromising somewhere, but it feels premium and completely hassle-free. One of those rare gadgets that just works right out of the box.

Installed no problem. REMEMBER it is 3.5 NOT 5.2, so it won't fit in a standard drive bay. (like a cd drive bay) Gave it two stars because my CF card slot is broken. Cant even put a CF card in it. Fortunately I cant see myself NEEDING it, but that still makes this defective. Not gonna return it because I don't really need the CF slot. Also the sd card slot is upside down.... which is odd. At first you might think that IT TOO is broken, then you try it upside down and all is well. USB port seems to work just fine as well.

This thing exceeded my expectations by a mile. I thought it'd be sluggish given the price, but it handles multitasking without a hiccup. The screen is crisp, battery lasts way longer than I anticipated, and setup took maybe two minutes. Couldn't be happier with this purchase.

First, let me say the obvious; for $100 this isn't cutting edge electronics so temper your expectations accordingly. Having said that, this is a nice little tablet for the price as long as you keep it simple. PROS: Crisp display and at 7" a nice viewing area. Steams movies well with very minimal issues. Comes with quite a few bells and whistles (so-so camera, assortment of apps, and some nice features such as weather updates and news) Great for reading newspapers, books, and surfing non-graphic intensive web pages. CONS: Virtually no storage area, buying an expansion card is almost a must Not powerful enough to play any graphic intensive apps. Tried playing a simple app like Simpsons Tapped Out and it's very choppy and stutters. Not a fan of the button placement but getting used to it. Overall I'm happy with it. I got it as a handy tablet to look up information quickly without going upstairs and starting my desktop computer. Yes, it has it's limitations but I didn't spend $500 either.

For the price, this is it man. The cards do sometimes fall out when i open it, so keep it facing up when you open it. I thought 8 cards would be enough, but I quickly found that i needed a bigger case...so if you have a small gig where water may come into play, get it.

I used this in marking a field and soil sampling. Did awesome. It's not sub-meter but seems to have accuracy of 6-8 feet. Magnet is strong, worked flawlessly with a dell venue 8 pro. Cable is long enough, I know that can be problematic. Acquired Sat signal and held it on a cloudy day. Very few skips or signal loss. I could drive as fast as I wanted without loss of signal.
Used this for a few weeks and the mount already cracked near the base. Feels pretty flimsy once you handle it regularly.
My phone stays put even on bumpy gravel roads. The sticky pad is just tacky enough without leaving residue. I was worried about dash material but it's been perfect.
We have had several of these SanDisk Sansa players over the years. This model is our favorite so far. Plays well, lasts for years.
For this price it solid. you get tons of the bells and whistles you can not get with this care for the price range. It stays very cool and it very solid case.

Package arrived in three days inside a sturdy box with molded foam, no scuffs or bends out of the gate. Unboxing felt solid, the gold-plated ends look clean and snap in with a reassuring click. The cable itself is stiffer than I expected, which makes routing a little fussy behind tight furniture, but the connection stays put once it's settled. First listening session left a good impression overall; it does what it promises without any fuss.

Arrived a day early and was packed really snug, no dents or loose parts. Pulled it out, popped in batteries, and it just worked immediately. Feels solid in hand and the buttons have a nice click. Super pleased right out of the box.
It is a little pouch. Seriously though, it fits my FiiO E11K headphone amplifier, and a pair of reserve earbuds. That's about all I can ask.
This router drops the signal on a consistent basis. Too bad it started happening after my 30 days and I can't return it. Plus one of the antenna's fell off during the initial installation

The box showed up two days early, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was snug, dense foam cutouts holding everything firmly in place, not a scratch or loose cable in sight. Unboxing felt premium for the price point. The unit itself has a solid, matte finish that doesn't attract fingerprints, and the buttons have a satisfying click. I was up and running in maybe four minutes, and pairing with my phone happened instantly. Range in my apartment easily covers two rooms with no dropouts. It's rare to see this level of care in both shipping protection and first-use polish. Really impressed right out of the gate.
For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but it's sturdy and handles fast charging without any issues. Total steal.

I picked this up hoping it would relieve some nagging wrist strain I've been dealing with, and it mostly delivers. It took me about a week to fully adjust to the vertical grip, but now it feels quite natural. The build quality is decent, though the plastic has a slightly slicker texture than I expected. Scrolling is smooth and the extra buttons are easy to reach without awkward thumb stretching. Battery life has been solid, lasting nearly three weeks on a single charge with daily use. My only minor gripe is that the DPI toggle feels a bit cheap compared to the rest of the mouse. For the price, it's a solid upgrade over a standard mouse and my discomfort has noticeably decreased.
this thing is great, you can copy your digital stuff in here, can you believe it?

This thing is junk. I don't care how nice the box looks or how many cables they toss in, none of it matters when the actual footage is useless. I installed it exactly as instructed, formatted the card, updated firmware, the whole routine. Daytime recording looks like it's smeared with Vaseline, and at night you might as well be watching static. Couldn't make out a face from ten feet away. The app disconnects constantly, and pulling files off the card is painfully slow. I've used budget dash cams half the price that produced cleaner, sharper video. Genuinely baffled by the positive reviews. Complete waste of money and time. I've already boxed it back up for a return.
Seems very sturdy. Lots of zippered compartments, probably too many for me. Comfortable and stylish.
Works right out of the box with my gaming rig, no drivers needed. Solid build and snappy performance, very happy with this purchase.

Got these for my Sony MDR-7506 cans about six months ago and they've held up better than I expected. I wear them almost daily for work and the foam still feels plush, no flattening or weird creases yet. The memory foam bounced back fine even after a few accidental crushes in my bag. Installation was quick once I got the hang of stretching the lip over the cup, took maybe three minutes. Only reason I'm not giving five stars is the inner opening could be a touch wider. After long sessions, the edge sometimes rubs against the top of my ear, which gets annoying. Still, for the price, the durability and overall comfort are solid.
These magnets are way stronger than I expected, holding my tablet rock steady on the fridge without any sliding.

I was hoping this would be a simple way to keep tabs on both indoor comfort and the outside temp without needing my phone, but the outdoor sensor just doesn't pull its weight. It loses connection if it's more than maybe 20 feet from the base, and any wall thicker than drywall seems to kill the signal entirely. I ended up moving the sensor three times just to get a reading that wasn't "--" on the display, and even then it drops out randomly. The indoor sensor is fine, but I bought this specifically for the dual display, so it feels like half the product is missing. For the price I expected something that works through a standard exterior wall without babysitting the placement constantly. Really underwhelming.

I LOVE LOVE LOVE these headphones! They perfectly keep out the noise (on an airplane, on the subway, etc). I love that it has a switch so you can allow the surrounding noise come in. That way if I'm on a plane I can quickly hear announcements or when I'm working in the office, my wife doesn't sneak up on me and scare me!
Didnt know this particular adapter was a dish network stick All in all a good value in lieu of getting an original Panasonice stick and paying 5 times more than this one. Glad i bought it.
Capacity is to large for my Garmin automobile GPS. However it works with no issues in my Magellion Trekkers GPS.

I snagged this for my 13-inch ultrabook, and it definitely has a generous fit, almost too roomy, so it slides around a bit inside. The neoprene handles light splashes, but don't expect any real shock absorption if it takes a tumble. I'd suggest it for someone who just needs basic scratch defense during a short commute or for stashing a laptop in a larger bag, not for daily rough handling.
This cable has outlasted three different phones. The braided jacket doesn't fray at all, and the connection stays snug even after months of yanking it out of my bag.
Perfect for anyone needing true-to-life color in a workshop or studio setup.

I bought these for my wireless keyboard thinking I'd save a few bucks. Worked fine for about ten days then started dying mid-sentence. By week three, none of them would hold a charge longer than an afternoon. Tried cycling them in different chargers, same sad result. Flimsy cells that degrade ridiculously fast. Not worth the headache.

Honestly, save your money unless you want to gift this to someone you truly dislike. The keyboard looks sleek and feels nice right out of the box, but that's where the good times end. Within three weeks, the spacebar started resisting and then it just gave up entirely. The real nightmare began when random keys would trigger on their own, spamming gibberish into my work documents during a client presentation. Unplugged it and my laptop went back to normal instantly. I'd only recommend this to someone who needs a prop for a desk photo or enjoys troubleshooting faulty hardware for fun. Absolutely maddening experience.
This lens was very high quality, not plastic. Has weight to it, metal and real glass. Does what is advertised!

I've had this speaker for a few months now and it's held up great for casual listening around the house and yard. Sound is clear with decent bass for its size, and calls come through fine. Battery life averages about 5-6 hours for me. Only real bummer is you can't pair two of these together for stereo, wish I'd known that sooner. Still, solid for solo use.

The laptop arrived three days later than the original estimate, and the box looked like it had been through a rough time, dented corners and a small tear on one side. Opening it up, the charger and manuals were just loose, not secured in any way, which didn't exactly inspire confidence. The Acer itself had a few faint scuffs near the trackpad straight out of the wrap, nothing major but noticeable. On first boot, things felt okay, not snappy but not dead slow either. Setup was simple enough and it connected to wifi without issue. I'm not blown away, but for a basic machine it seems functional. Just wish the shipping care matched the price.
Very nice replacements to my iPod ear buds. Sound quality is at least as good and the cord is longer. Alos the angled minijack helps keep the ear phones from becoming unpluged easily.

For the money, I wasn't expecting much, just something to get me through a couple of quick video calls. Turns out, these little lights are way more capable than they have any right to be. I grabbed a pair for a small product shoot and ended up using them as my main setup. The dial to adjust brightness is simple and gives you decent control without fussing with apps. They're light enough to clamp onto a shelf or tape to a wall in a pinch. Color consistency between the two units was spot on out of the box. Honestly, at this price point, I keep finding new uses for them. Total steal.
Ordered a speaker but got a cheap plastic brick instead, total scam, nothing like the listing.
Arrived a day early in perfect condition. Cable feels durable and the audio is crystal clear right out of the box.

I use these for mountain biking and the sound quality is adequate. The thing I really like about these is the ability to hear ambient noised. They are NOT sound isolating like most ear buds and that's exactly what I like about them.

I wanted to like this speaker set because the midrange clarity is surprisingly decent for the cost, vocals and acoustic tracks actually sound pretty crisp at moderate volume. Unfortunately, the build quality completely undermines any positives. The power input port on the subwoofer is incredibly fragile; mine started cutting out after barely two weeks of normal use, and now I have to wiggle the cable just right to get any sound at all. It's not like I'm rough with my equipment either, it just sits in the corner untouched. When it does work, the bass is boomy and undefined, which makes action movies feel like a muddy rumble instead of a punchy experience. Really disappointed because the stereo imaging had potential. Not worth the headache.
Pretty great headphones at a great price. The cable is very long which is great, and the music comes through very loudly as well.

Received the camera two days earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was solid, foam inserts kept everything snug and the box arrived without a dent. First impression pulling it out of the box: the housing feels sturdy and the included cable length was more than generous for my SUV. Installation took a little patience routing the wire cleanly, but the connectors clicked in securely. Image clarity impressed me right away on my Kenwood screen, crisp and color-accurate, even during dusk when lighting gets tricky. Only knocking off one star because the mount bracket needed minor filing to sit flush against my license plate frame. Not a dealbreaker, but something to note. Overall, a solid unit for the price.
Had this on my tablet for a few weeks now, no scratches at all and it still wipes clean super easily.

These are a lifesaver for my daily walks. The retractable cord actually works smoothly without jamming, and I'm not constantly untangling knots before a podcast. Sound quality is surprisingly clear for the price, vocals come through crisp and bass doesn't distort. I'd absolutely recommend these to anyone who commutes, hits the gym, or just hates dealing with messy cables in a bag. They've held up to being tossed in my jacket pocket every day, too. Honestly a great little gift for busy friends who still prefer wired buds without the headache. Solid five stars.

The box showed up a day early, which was a nice surprise, but the packaging was just the bare retail box tossed in a bubble mailer, corners were a little smooshed. Luckily the fan itself was fine, no bent fins or scratches. First impression out of the box was solid, it feels sturdy and the sleeving on the cables is a nice touch. It's not dead silent at full tilt, but the airflow seems good for the size. Giving it 4 stars because the included screws felt a bit cheap and the manual is basically a folded post-it. Still, for the price, it's a tidy little unit that looks clean once it's in.
Had this for six months now and it still works like day one, even after a few drops on the tile floor.

Okay so logitech has been very well known in making good webcams. This cam arrived ontime. There however was a problem where the mic wasn't really working well or something. I returned it and got another one that seems to work pretty well. Now the video quality is pretty good. Even though it might not be the best compared to their newest one (That long blue expensive webcam they just came out with a year ago) but its pretty good if you want a cam that is cheaper, especially after the rebate if they are still offering one. Unfortunately the mic seems to not be the best it could have been but considering my previous webcam was so old it was fuzzy looking this mic works a lot better. I wanted one with a really good mic as well as good video quality. The video quality is great but the features it comes with are kinda lacking. Im talking about the little animations and such. You don't get a lot when you first install it you have to hunt down some good ones on the website in order to get some cool looking ones. The other features such as low light can be good or bad depending on how you use them. Also the "right sound" seems to just make things worse I think. Its hard to say since I don't use my webcam that often. However I think this is one of the better webcams you can pick these days. Just make sure to check it out and try it out a bit right away when you get it just incase you get a faulty one like I did. Oh and logitech has a forum for questions and they usually answer them right away or within a day or two. I have bought quite a few logitech products and even though sometimes I come across one or two that I need to return, in the long run they have good products.

I grabbed this for my iPad 3 after getting tired of the flimsy magnetic covers, and it's been rock solid. The snap-on back piece locks everything in place, no more unexpected detachments when I'm reading in bed. It adds barely any bulk, keeps the slim profile, and the translucent finish looks clean. If you've got a heavier older iPad and want a secure, no-fuss setup, I'd easily recommend this to anyone.

Seagull 1x-2x Right Angle Finder for Canon, Nikon, Pentax, Minolta, Fuji, Olympus and Leica SLR cameras is very high quality and it obtains very good image quality. I recommend this product for DSLRs.
Does exactly what I hoped and then some, super happy.

This case fits snug and the cutouts line up perfectly with all the ports and buttons. I've dropped my iPad mini twice now and it came through without a scratch, so the shock absorption is legit. The texture adds a decent grip, which helps with the slippery feel of the tablet itself. My only nitpick is it attracts lint from pockets a little too easily. Still, for the price, it's a solid everyday protector that doesn't add bulk.

I picked this up to handle a multi-floor setup where my old router kept dropping signal the second I walked upstairs. After some easy tweaking in the settings, I've got a rock-solid link through thick walls and even the basement, no lag during 4K streams or large file transfers. The USB port for network storage was a bonus I didn't think I'd use, but now my whole family grabs scans and backups without emailing things around. I'd recommend it to anyone in a split-level home or older building where signal struggles, especially if you want a single device that can do double duty as a basic NAS and solid bridge. Definitely worth it.

Hated it. I should have checked the size--it's smaller than you'd think. Too small; even if you're used to an Apple Magic Mouse, as I am, this is uncomfortably short and flat. Instant carpal tunnel. The movement is awkward, the gestures across the top don't work very well (though admittedly that could come with practice. Be warned that unlike most Amazon items, you'll have to pay return shipping if you don't like this. My advice, if you're considering it, is find a store that carries them and try it out first.

This thing is a beast for digital art. I was worried the pen experience wouldn't feel natural coming from a Wacom tablet, but the 120Hz screen and pressure sensitivity are genuinely impressive. Zero lag brushing in huge resolution files. The display gets bright enough for working near a window, and the kickstand is rock solid for drawing at weird angles. Super happy.

I honestly love the quality of the Skinomi Screen protectors. They always go on so easily and don't give me any problems. No bubbles, and they look great, the protection that they give is amazing. My screen would be so bad by now if I didn't have the screen protector on it, so I love it and I would definitely recommend that you put one on your device as well, or the screen will get really scratched fast. I received this product at a promotional rate in return for giving only my honest opinion, and I would personally recommend this screen protector, most companies don't have this kind of quality, and Skinomi Does. Compatible with Apple iPad Pro 12.9". Laser cut designed specifically to offer maximum screen coverage. Material flexibility allows total coverage even on curved devices. Self-Healing, flexible, tough, military-grade thermoplastic urethane, designed to absorb impact. Screen protector is scratch-proof, puncture, UV resistant and will not yellow. Easy, error-proof, and bubble-free installation. Liquid solution allows adjustments during installation. Significantly reduces dust, oil and fingerprint smudges. Optically transparent, virtually invisible and HD clear screen protector with smooth glass-like surface and "True Touch" feel. A great alternative to a typical "static cling" PET screen protector. 100% Risk-Free Lifetime Replacement Warranty from Manufacturer. Made in the USA.
This is nothing special it's an extra piece for a dslr camera that I needed. Sometimes these reviews are a bit over rated.

I grabbed this mount for my BenQ short-throw projector and it's been solid overall. Installation was pretty painless once I figured out the arm adjustments, though the instructions could be clearer. The tilt and swivel range is decent, but it takes a little patience to get it dialed in perfectly. I'd suggest this for home theater beginners or anyone on a budget who doesn't mind a bit of tweaking. It holds the weight fine, but I added a safety tether for peace of mind. Good value if you're mounting a lighter projector in a dedicated room.
Box was a bit crushed but the unit itself works perfectly so far.
This thing is a must for anyone with a big digital library on their modded 3DS. Zero issues, super easy install. I'd tell any retro handheld fan to grab one.

Nice laptop for a very reasonable amount of money, it came in pristine condition, the only complaint is that some of the description is not correct but if your are willing to put up with little thinks missing here and there it is a perfect laptop

I picked this up mostly out of curiosity, I'd swapped all my studio lights to LEDs and wanted to see if the output actually matched what the boxes claimed. Honestly, I expected it to be decent, but it turned out way more precise than I figured for the price. The readings are quick and consistent, and I love that it's sensitive enough to catch subtle differences between bulbs with nearly identical specs. I ended up walking around the whole house testing lamps and even my plant shelves. It's compact, dead simple to use, and totally changed how I think about placement. If you're even slightly nerdy about lighting, you'll get way more out of this than you expect.
Works as advertised. It's a little small and feels fragile but gets the job done.
Arrived a day early in perfect foam packaging. Plugged in and wow, crisp, room-filling sound instantly.

Lenovo laptop power supply - plugged it into my Lenovo laptop and it shorted out my laptop power. Laptop dead. Returning power supply for refund. Sending laptop to Lenovo for service, at my expense. I strongly urge you not to take a chance on this power supply.

I was skeptical at first, but it's a good little speaker for the price. I use mine out in my garage where I don't need Bose speakers while I'm doing wood working. As an added little bonus, it will shut itself off if you disconnect the Bluetooth for too long. I forget to turn it off on a regular basis, but it can save its own battery.
Works great. I am able to use my old monitor still.
Super happy with this TV, everything just works and looks amazing.
Nice little mount. Serves its purpose as the tv that it is supporting is pretty light weight.

I've had this on my laptop for about four months now, and it's held up better than I expected. The textured surface still looks clean even after getting tossed into my backpack daily, and the edges haven't started peeling or warping. I did drop it once from a low coffee table onto hardwood, and thankfully the case took the hit with just a tiny scuff, laptop was fine. The snap-on fit is still snug, no loosening over time. My only small gripe is that the corners collect a bit of dust, but that's easy enough to wipe off. For everyday bumps and scratches, this thing does its job solidly.
Perfect for tight spots behind furniture. I'd recommend it to anyone with crowded outlets or bulky adapters, sleek and does exactly what I needed.
This modem works great. I've had this quite a while now and never had a problem. Recommend if you need both wireless and wired connections.
For the cost, it's a decent protector. Easy to apply but I noticed tiny bubbles near the edges after a month. You get what you pay for.
Arrived a day early in perfect packaging. Took it out, felt instantly solid in my hand, and the clicks are super satisfying. Stoked with this purchase.

Was easy to setup and works very well. We unplugged the COX modem, but did NOT remove the battery (the instructions say the battery should be removed). We waited about 15 seconds, and then turned on the COX modem, after connecting it to the wifi router (which was turned on). The 1 yr old HP laptop found the TP-Link router and easily connected with the provided password on the box (rather tiny font). Then, we went to the 198.etc. address and changed the signin and the password to our satisfaction. The COX modem, if connected with the cord, gets us 90+mbps, and 7+mbps upload; without the cord, ie., wifi only, a few inches away, we get 85+mbps. When we took the laptop down the hallway and 2 rooms away (it is an old brick house), we got 45+mbps, which is far above what is needed for videos - a min of 10mbps for smooth viewing-, and the same 7+mbps upload rate. Amazing.
Works as promised. My garage looks more organized already.

I got this for my Lenovo x200t, which has a displayport in the docking station. Now I can use dual monitors when docked (this may not be possible with all computers, but the x200t allows you to use the VGA and Displayport concurrently to duplicate or extend the display.) It is too short to connect the display but easily connects to the DVI-D cable I already had for my flatscreen monitor. I would have given it five stars if it weren't so expensive - it seems overpriced for how simple it is, but others were even more so what can I say? Apple owners might be used to paying extra but as a PC user I was surprised at the high price. It does the trick though and was worth it to get dual monitors working!

I love this thing!!! I had a mythtv backend server that I was running on a photo kiosk computer (celeron dual core 2GB) I picked up on ebay for next to nothing. That actually worked well but, it became unstable. I got one of these with 4GB memory and a Sandisk SSD. It's silent. It's fast. It works great!!! For those that want to run Linux headless with grub. By default, grub runs in a terminal and without anything plugged in it will pick a resolution that is lower than the video card can display which will stop it from booting. If you configure grub to run in the console rather than a terminal, it will fix it. Hopefully that will save someone from spending the hours I did to figure that out.

This is a great product if (a) you want wireless capabilities with your MP3 player (I don't-but that is another story)and you want (as I do) a player that will work with the Rhapsody subscription service. Ipods are wonderful, but you still have to shell out a buck for every song you want to download. Rhapsody has a flat rate, so you can download music that you might not take a chance on with Itunes. The player works well with Rhapsody and I hope to get it up to the 5,000 song level I had with my old Dell DJ.
I have ordered this item in the past, and it works perfectly. However, I never received the item. There are older iMac computers where I work, and a presentation needed to be given.
Perfect for anyone juggling multiple screens and needing a reliable extra card.

The earpieces are great but one of them doesn't work. But apart form that it worked pretty well. The earphone is comfortable and the wiring is okay. The customer service was awesome though. They responded very quickly and arranged to send me a replacement. Update: I actually managed to fix the damaged headset. Update: I bought 2 more of these. They are probably the best ones available. I am using these on a regular day to day basis.
Can't beat it for the price. The kids love them. Turns your tablet into a mini laptop. Seems to be good quality so far.

This little portable router worked perfectly, with the setup minimal. Only thing missing was a case or pouch to carry it. I went to Las Vegas recently with my Nexus 7 tablet, which does not have a wired ethernet port. I also assumed that some of the hotels in Vegas still had wired internet connections in their rooms. My stay was at the Monte Carlo, which only has the wired connection in the room. So I plugged cable into this router in the Access Point mode, which in turn sends out wi-fi signals that my Nexus7 picked up. A couple of tabs on the Nexus7 to accept the hotels terms, etc. and I had internet access. I was able to check my emails, etc. in my room. Since the hotel charges you a daily fee which includes Internet access, might as well put it to good use. So this TP-Link router worked as advertised. Plug in the required cables, setup was almost automatic, and instant wi-fi connection to the Internet. The price was great too.
Six months in and it still looks brand new, no scuffs or loosening at all.
These work wonderful with my Westone UM2 headphones. Great price and excellent value. Would recommend this for anyone that would want to replace their earphone tips.

For what this costs, I expected more than seven months before the Ethernet ports gave up. Constant drops and failed uploads made it unusable for work. Swapped cables, checked settings, no luck. Finally replaced it with a different brand and everything's rock solid. Hard to recommend when it can't even last a year.
Picks up 30+ channels way out here where cell service barely works. Picture is crisp and setup took minutes.

I've been using the X10i almost daily for about eight months now, and they've held up surprisingly well. I toss them in my bag without a case, the cable's been yanked a few times, and they still look and sound like day one. No fraying, no loose connections, just solid. The minimalist shape means less to break, I guess. Sound-wise, they still deliver crisp detail I didn't expect from earbuds this tiny, subtle backing vocals and acoustic layers pop out cleanly. They're not cheap, but after this long with zero issues, I feel like I'm getting my money's worth. One star off just because I wish the remote felt a bit more robust.

the item arrived on time and worked as it was supposed to but my pda slipped and the little prongs broke so i have to use tape to keep it in til i can figure out how to get the broken prongs out and buy another one

I bought this for the TTL... then I realized that the best results can be achieved only in Manual... The other problem, it doesn't support HSS which is a mandatory for shooting in the daylight (flash as fill)

Used this for a Ryzen 5 7600 build after hearing mixed things about paste vs pads, and honestly it blew my expectations away. Idle temps hover around 31C and even under sustained gaming loads it barely touches 67C with a modest air cooler. Application was super easy, spread smoothly with zero mess, unlike some thicker pastes I've tried. One tube covered both my CPU and GPU die without running short. For the price I figured it'd be decent, but it's actually rivaling pastes that cost twice as much. Definitely grabbing another tube for future builds.

Had this thing holding up a small monitor for about four months now and the metal construction hasn't loosened or sagged at all. The finish still looks clean too, no chipping. Only nitpick is the mounting holes are spaced a little weirdly, so measure first. Otherwise, solid piece of hardware.

I've had this TCL for about three weeks now, and for the price, it's a solid buy. The colors pop nicely, streaming nature docs on Netflix looks surprisingly vivid. Set up was simple, and I like that there are plenty of HDMI ports; I've got my soundbar, Fire Stick, and old DVD player all plugged in with room to spare. It's not going to beat a top-tier OLED, but for everyday use it's more than enough. I'd recommend this to anyone on a budget who still wants a sharp, reliable screen, especially folks in apartments or smaller living rooms. Only knock is the built-in speakers are just okay, so plan on a soundbar if you care about audio.

Got this bag it has been working great. it is the perfect size for a 15in laptop with a notebook and a laptop power cable. It has a different material on the bottom of the bag which is great as it will not get scratched. I use the Metro rail and bus system to get to work and this is a great bag.

I've been running two TL-WA7210 units for almost three years now, one set up outdoors as a bridge and the other indoors as the main access point. For the first two and a half years they were bulletproof through rain, summer heat, and the occasional power flicker. About six weeks ago the AP unit started getting a little moody, needing a reboot maybe once a week when the connection would randomly crawl. I updated firmware on my own, swapped the PoE injector, and repositioned the antenna slightly, and it's been steady again for over a month. For the price, the longevity has been impressive, and I'm still happy with the range. Just be ready to do minor troubleshooting after heavy use.

I had a sony P-200 camera 7.2mp to replace it with the Canon. My friend bought a Canon G-7 to replace the Sony (We have the Same Sony Camera). We both got in to the hype of all the reviews. We both tested the canon cameras quite a bit. Both Canon(s) has more options then the Sony does. But the Sony overall picture is much sharper and the colors are more realistic and natural. I kept my Canon just cause I was in so deep, I had already received a 8 gig SDHC Card, had a rebate on the Epson Printer so I ended up keeping the Canon and sold the Sony. It was a hard choice, knowing the sony picture quality is that much better. My friend on the other hand is selling his New Canon G-7, "too bulky and its not worth it to lug it around when the Sony P-200 takes better sharper pics." I agree, the Sony picture quality is better then the Canon 850IS. I can affirm that! The only thing is the Sony takes average low light pics, but the Canon 850is is no world beater in that department either. When it comes to day light shots, the Sony wins hands down over the 850is and also according to my friend the sony beats the Canon G-7 in outdoor photos "not zoomed". Like I said the Canon(s) has more options, and some might counter that you need to manaual play with the canon to get a better pic. But really who wants to mess with manual settings on a trip overseas? How many pics will you miss? The Sony P-200 has as fast boot up as the Canon 850IS and takes pics much much faster with the flash when it comes to multiple pics. And the sony flat out takes better pics. No doubt the canon is a excellent camera, but its questionable as to how good compared with a sony that came out in 2005...the Sony has less options, but has great image quality and "sony Style".

Fantastic bag! I looked at and purchased a few other laptop bags from Samsonite, Incase, etc. and nothing comes close to this. They use YKK zippers, have multiple pockets with soft lining to not scratch up laptops, iPads, etc. and this case has plenty of pockets but not too many!

I bought this lcd last Christmas to be used in my bedroom. I have no complaints. The picture and sound are fine for late night viewing. The sleep timer is very convenient. I consider this a great value...

Since we started watching movies more frequently, I decided to upgrade my center speaker. After much research and thought, I purchased this amazing speaker. During this process, I listened and compared to a lot of center speakers; most of them expensive, and recognizable name brands. I am a singer and play several instruments, so I recognize good sound when I hear it. The sound of this speaker can be best described by the title of my review. The quality of the sound cannot be distorted even at extremely high volumes. If, after you buy this speaker, you do not have GREAT sound, it means that you do not have a good enough audio receiver. This speaker definitely maximized the best that my receiver puts out. Like I expected, this addition to my surround system truly added another level to our movie watching experience. The same can be said about music listening.

I 've a A3 for a couple of days and after comparing it to my q5w, I prefer the A3 for a couple of reasons. First it plays my AAC music files, which I needed additional software to do that on the q5. It's lighter than than the q5, and fits in a pocket easier. It can also record my favorite TV shows to watch at a later time. The only thing the A3 needs to be perfect is bluetooth. If music is important to you I recommend the A3, for sound quality it beats my ipod hands down.

For the price, I honestly wasn't expecting much, but this case blew me away. Cable management was way cleaner than I anticipated, and the airflow design keeps my temps lower than my old premium tower. The side panel looks sharp, and I didn't run into any sharp edges during the build. It's a no-brainer if you want something that looks great and performs well without spending a fortune.
I wasn't expecting much for the price but it's been flawless and genuinely made my daily setup easier.
This piece does not come with the ring light. This holds my camera in the center of the ring light and allows for easy adjusting. This is a must have for ring light photographers.

I wanted to love these, but they just didn't fit my smaller ears, constantly felt like they'd pop out if I turned my head. Sound for music is decent enough, but during calls people said I sounded distant, and I heard a faint static on my end. Battery life was solid, though. If you've got larger ears and mainly want them for podcasts or casual listening, they might work fine. For me, the insecure fit was a dealbreaker.

Didn't expect much for the price, but this little adapter blew me away. Speeds are steady and noticeably better than my old USB dongle. Plugged it into my aging desktop, and it just worked, no messing with drivers. Solid build, too. Honestly, it outperforms the built-in Ethernet on my newer laptop.
Got this to cover our basement dead zone and now my PS5 downloads are blazing fast with zero lag spikes.

This thing arrived way faster than I expected, ordered late Thursday night and it was on my doorstep Saturday morning. Packaging was solid too, no cheap air pillows, just a snug custom-fit cardboard insert that kept the modem absolutely immobile. First thing I noticed pulling it out was how substantial it feels, nice matte finish, doesn't look like a toy. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and the front panel status lights are actually readable instead of blinding blue lasers. Coming from a rented gateway, the difference in build quality is immediately obvious. Quiet, runs cool, and my desk finally has one less ugly ISP box cluttering it up.
These are great. Simple design Well made and reasonably priced. I will definitely buy these again and I definitely recommend them.
YOU MUST remove the plastic prism for this to work. The distortion is ridiculous. Like looking at a carnival mirror.

I purchased this to I can take pictures of my products that I sell and review. It is a compact "box" that opens up to give you a nice clean area to photograph your items. I'm not very "mechanical" so it took me a few minutes to figure out how to set up the box, but once I did, I saw how easy it was. It is nice because it folds down and stores in the container that comes with it. I do wish the tripod was a little taller - only stands up about 12 inches.
Arrived a day early in a neat little box. Popped them on and my earbuds finally feel secure.

I've been running this little box for over eight months now, tucked behind my TV setup, and it hasn't missed a beat. Handled a few nasty summer storms without flinching. Still whisper quiet, no hum, and the outlets are all solid. For the price, the build quality is seriously impressive, feels like it'll last years.

I bought this used off of EBay, but I would recommend this computer to anyone out there. The main reason that I say that is Apples are built to last, I don't mean the case or the screen or anything like that. What I mean is Apple does not make a bunch of different configurations of computers in the same year. There are two or three models out there a year and they are all priced over $1000, why, because they last. In the past year I have upgraded this computer in three ways. 1. Went from 4GB RAM to 8GB RAM. Want to have a fast computer, just add RAM. I have never had an issue with that. 2. Upgraded the Hard Drive to a 120GB SSD, wanna talk about speed. 9 seconds from hitting the power to surfing the internet. 3. Removed the Super Drive and added a 1TB Hard Drive for storage. I don't know of an HP, Dell, Gateway, Acer, IBM or any of the other PC companies that you can do that with. (Admittedly once I got a Mac I never look anything up about a PC again). As for performance, I have no complaints. There is nothing that I have attempted to do that I have had an issue with this computer. It seems with a PC that within a year some of the Software out there outperforms the specs on a computer that you just bought when you go down the PC route. Here is what I would say to you. Sure you can buy a $300 PC from WalMart or somewhere, I bet that it might be in the trash or sold within two years. I to can not afford a brand new MacBook Pro, but getting one used is less expensive and they work and hold value. Highly recommend this MacBook Pro.

This is a good WiFi Ranger Extender. Plugged it in next to my WiFi modem and then moved it to an outlet at the other end of the house. Was able to access the WiFi with my WiFi node name with a _EXT and now have a strong WiFi at the other end of the house. Very easy setup. Works as advertised. Note that download speeds are reduced by 50% from this range extender but it does give you the internet access that you need.

I have Senn 598's which are open-back, and I bought the 439's fully expecting them to be inferior in all ways but bass (since they are closed back). However, even in the low frequencies these headphones were significantly inferior. They may shine on their own, but they just can't compete with 598's. I ended up returning them.

I really wanted to like this, but it's been nothing but a headache. My plan was to archive some family camcorder tapes, and the setup seemed simple enough. The main issue is that it constantly rejects the blank discs I put in, even though they're the exact type the manual recommends. After wasting half a spindle trying different brands, I got it to start a recording session twice. Both times the counter ran and the unit sounded like it was working, but the finalized disc was completely empty when I checked it on my PC and a standalone player. The whole process just feels like a gamble, and I don't have the patience to keep buying media for something that fails 90% of the time.

I ordered an Apple iPad listed as Refurbished and what showed up was just a grimy, scratched-up tablet with zero quality control. The screen has a faint but permanent cluster of dead pixels right in the center, so reading or watching anything is a constant distraction. Battery health was already at 84%, which is way below what I'd expect from a properly refurbished unit. It also arrived with someone else's iCloud still linked, so I had to spend an hour sorting that out before I could even use it. The whole experience felt like buying from a random garage sale, not a certified program.
Holy Moses, what's not to like about this lens? OK, sure... it's 1.8 and not 1.4, but seriously! For the price what do you expect? This lens should be in EVERY starter bag.

It got here three days earlier than the estimated delivery, which already had me in a good mood. The film was sandwiched between two sturdy pieces of cardboard inside a bubble mailer, completely flat and pristine, no creases or scuffs at all. I pulled it out expecting some flimsy sheet, but it feels substantial and the reflective coating is flawless. First look through my dob after building a quick cell was honestly stunning. You can see the granular texture around sunspots with crazy clarity, and the limb darkening is sharp right out of the box. For the price, the quality of the material and the care in shipping blew me away.

Main reason I purchased this was to "flesh-out" my new computer case. My newly built PC (from Amazon purchased parts) has two USB 3.0 ports in the back, but the motherboard has an additional 2 port header for front panel ports. My case came equipped with two front-panel USB 2.0 ports but no USB 3.0 ports. Since I didn't need a floppy drive in the 3.5" outer drive bay, I purchased this hub. It has the additional benefit of having the "FAST CHARGE" option on the fourth port. I tested this out with my Samsung Galaxy S5 (with the phone's USB 3.0 cable) and confirmed that in Fast-Charge mode, the PC will charge my phone just as fast as the Samsung S5 charger, or in half the time of a USB 2.0 port or 1000 mA or less charger.
Had these for about six months now, used almost daily, and they're still holding up great with zero wear issues.
It's unfortunate from micro USB connector failed after about 35 days of continuous use. If you can't charge it you can't use it. This is a basic design fault.

I've had this little speaker clipped to my shower caddy for almost three months now and it's still kicking like day one. Steam, splashes, even a full-on drop onto the tile floor once, no issues at all. The suction cup held tight for weeks, but I eventually switched to the carabiner loop and it's been rock solid. Battery life is honestly surprising; I charge it maybe every couple of weeks with daily use. Sound is clear enough to enjoy podcasts and my morning playlist without cranking it to max. For something I originally grabbed just to have background noise while getting ready, it's turned into a genuinely reliable bathroom staple.
Plugged it in and immediately got solid WiFi out on my back patio.

The box showed up pretty banged up, but the tablet inside seemed fine at first glance. Packaging was minimal, just the bare essentials, no padding. First impressions are mixed; the matte surface feels weirdly tacky and the stylus tip drags more than I expected. Corners take multiple taps to register. It's okay, not great.

This thing showed up a day early and was packed like a tank, zero chance of damage. Unboxing felt weirdly premium for the price, all neat and snug. Hooked it up in under two minutes and was honestly stunned by the crisp picture right away. My initial skepticism vanished instantly. Five stars, no question.

My old Dell tower from 2004 was basically a doorstop until I grabbed this. Took maybe ten minutes total with the driver disc, and now it's back online in my basement workshop. Signal holds steady through one concrete wall, which surprised me. It won't break speed records, but for light browsing and occasional file downloads it's solid. I'd easily recommend it to anyone resurrecting a retro XP rig or helping a less tech-savvy relative keep an ancient desktop useful.

Camera does everything I wanted it to, and takes good-looking pictures, too. I use this camera for my work as a consulting engineer, so it gets beat around in tool bags and suitcases every week. I wanted a ruggedized camera because I was tired of getting to the jobsite only to find that my camera was busted. I've been using this Lumix for several months, and it still looks like new. I like that the lense is fixed -- seems that when my old cameras broke, it was often the retractable lense that failed. I can't think of anything bad to say -- this camera has been rock solid.

I don't much care for having a removable lens cap on a string (like my old 2000-era digicam from Sony), and I wish the zoom was a bit better, but everything else about this camera I love. It takes the best low-light no-flash pictures of any camera I've had -- finally beating out the old F2.0 Sony camera I had back in 2000. I've looked for years to find something like this, and can finally replace my Fuji F10 (which wasn't as good, but had clean high ISO low-light performance). And altough I thought I'd hate it, I ended up LOVING the pop up flash -- so convenient compared to toggling computer menu options. I'm quite impressed. You can see thousands of photos taken using this camera at DisneyFans.com in the Disneyland 2009 gallery.

I keep this little guy in my everyday carry for coffee shop work sessions. The spacing between the three outlets is wide enough that my bulky MacBook charger doesn't block the other plugs, which was a constant annoyance with other strips. The USB ports charge my phone surprisingly fast too. Solid build, no regrets.
My laptop's been plugged in for hours and the brick stays cool. Super portable, fits anywhere, and keeps my old Toshiba running perfectly all day.

I really wanted to love these. The sound profile out of the box is genuinely impressive for the price point, warm, balanced, not overly bass-heavy. That's where the positives end. After about four months of careful daily use at my desk, the headband cushion started separating, and the faux leather is peeling off in tiny flakes that stick to my hair. The bigger issue is the hinge design. There's a tiny plastic joint near the swivel that developed a hairline crack without any drops or mishandling. Now I have to handle them like fine china just to put them on. Compared to my previous set from a different brand that lasted years of gym abuse, these feel absurdly fragile. Great sound trapped in a disposable shell.
Sturdy after months, still holds up great with daily use.
Works exactly as intended! Both sizes of HDD work in this, and I have yet to run into an issue with years of using it.
Perfect for anyone who hates flimsy gadgets.
not working at all!! maybe it need something or work with short cables only ( less than 1 meter I guess!! ) disappoint...
Every time I insert this memory card into my PC it crashes the PC leaving me a USB as my only means of data transfer.

I received this product 2 months ago and I love it. I swim laps twice a week and it can be boring, being able to listen to an audiobook while swimming allowed me to stay focus. I recommended this product to a lot of my friends.

The box arrived a bit crushed on one corner, and the packaging inside was just a thin plastic sleeve, no padding or anything. First impression out of the package was that it felt sturdier than it actually is. The cover has a nice texture and the compact size fits well in my gear bag, which I appreciated right away. But once I started inserting cards, the slots felt way too stiff. I had to wiggle cards in carefully and prying them back out takes more force than I'm comfortable with. I can already see stress marks near a couple slots after just a few uses. It organizes everything neatly, but I'm worried about long-term durability.
Quit working. Had professionally installed Nov 11...quit working Dec 26....you take the chance if you want to!
Used these on my stainless steel fridge to mount my iPad 1. No cover. Has held just fine for 7 months already.

Bought this for my daughter's iPad Mini and the precise cutouts for the buttons and camera make it super easy to use without feeling bulky. She's dropped it on hardwood floors a bunch and there's zero damage. Honestly, it's a steal compared to pricier brands, very happy with this purchase.

Upgraded from an older 7200 RPM drive and the speed bump is immediately noticeable. Boot times dropped by a good 15 seconds and large file transfers fly. I use it as my main game storage drive and load times in open-world titles are way smoother, no more texture pop-in stutter. Installation was dead simple, just plugged it in and Windows recognized it instantly. Runs quiet and cool too, even during long gaming sessions I barely hear it over my case fans. My tower sits on the desk right next to me so that matters a lot. Been running daily for a few months now with zero hiccups. Honestly, for the price per gigabyte this thing delivers, I'm seriously impressed. Picked up a second one for backup.

This little netbook looks sharp and feels way sturdier than I expected, no creaky plastic here. The typing experience is decent for short emails. But the speed just drags everything down. Booting up takes forever, and even simple tasks like launching a browser or Word feel sluggish. I wasn't expecting a powerhouse, but the constant waiting gets old fast. It handles basic browsing okay once things are open, just don't plan on multitasking. Great build for the price, but the sluggish performance makes it hard to fully recommend.

I've had this Olympus VN-722PC for about three weeks now, and it's exactly what I needed for my college lectures and the occasional band practice recording. The LED screen is a nice upgrade, it's super easy to see how much time you've got left, even in a dim lecture hall. Sound pickup is clean for voice, and it handles loud instruments without distorting, which surprised me. Menu navigation is simple, no hunting through confusing submenus. I'd recommend it to students, musicians messing around with song ideas, or anyone who wants a fuss-free recorder that just works. For the price, it's a solid little workhorse that fits in your pocket and doesn't overcomplicate things.
Arrived a day early in perfect packaging. Hooked it up in two minutes and the picture on my TV looks crystal clear.

Great price. Very fast. Had it for over a year now with no issue. Durable little thing. I have owned this for years now and it is always taking punishment on my key-chain and still going strong. Very durable.

I picked this up after my old external case started acting up, and the tool-free design is a lifesaver. The aluminum shell keeps temps impressively low even during large file transfers, and the USB-C connection is rock solid. It sits quietly on my desk without any wobble or noise. Mounted this on the back of my 32 foot camper. Used their 50 foot extension cable and mounted the transmitter on the front of the camper. It takes a couple of seconds for the GPS to switch to the backup camera, but after my 3 and 1/2 week vacation that we just got back from, and about 3000 miles, this thing saved my butt several times. It allowed me to turn my rig around in places I would not dare to do with out this camera, as it was to dangerous for the rig and anyone to go out to guide me back. The camera is a fish eye, so mounted at a 45 degree angle, I can see the whole back of my camper, and up into the sky, and several car widths to both sides of the back of my camper. I was also amazed at how you can adjust the guide lines to fit your rig, raise them up, down, and spread them apart or closer together so they perfectly fit your use. If want, you can remove the guide lines with a simple checkmark in my GPS, but with being able to adjust them so much, I would not know why anyone would want to turn them off. I use this with the Garmin RV 770 LMT-S. For a camper or truck, this is the most awesome GPS I have ever owned, and matched with this camera, its simply indispensable.

I grabbed this thinking it'd cover all my Apple devices in one go. It handles my older iPhone and iPod fine, no hiccups. But my iPad just ignores it completely, which was a letdown since I assumed universal compatibility. It's sturdy enough, just not the all-in-one solution I expected for the price.
Exceeded what I expected for the price, solid build and works flawlessly out of the box.

Amazingly comfortable, great sound as well. One of the most well built headphones I have used, even better quality than my 200$ AKG K7XX's, You can wear these things for hours without even noticing you have them on!

I have had 3 pairs of this headset. I lost one, bought one for my wife, and bought myself a replacement. As a mono bluetooth it works great, although a bit on the heavy side. As a stereo bluetooth I love it. I love not having a wire connecting to my phone when I am working out. I don't know how many times I unplugged or ripped my phone out of my pocket when the wired headset cable got snagged on something. This is just so much more convenient. Some complain that the sound it not as good, but I find it more than adequate for working out. When you buy a stereo bluetooth you are not looking for concert sound as much as you are looking for the safety and convenience of not having connecting wires hanging out of your ears. My only complaint is the ear look is of very poor design. I have had to call Jabra several times to order replacements because the bendable wire section breaks from the hard plastic part that goes into the ear piece. It got to the point that Jabra would just send me 3 complimentary ear loops at no charge.

Hi everyone, For those of you who don't know yet, this cooling bracket was released several months ago before being quickly recalled due to bad hardware that caused the PCB of your graphics card to bend and make poor contact with your GPU. Corsair released this cooling bracket again now with shorter standoffs too correct the contact issue. I do see that there are reviews still stating bending and high Temps. All I can say for myself is that I purchased two of these for my evga gtx 980ti's. At first I had a very slight bend and Temps under load were 60-64C. I was not happy with this. I started some trial and error. I installed the HG10 N980 bracket to my PC while running Valley benchmark. I slowly released tension on one thumbsrew. One by one I basically got no bending of the PCB and now I got 98% GPU use & load Temps of 48C. What worked for me was loosening up the thumbsrew at the 11 o'clock and 5 o'clock position until I saen Temps dramatically drop. I am very happy that my 2 980ti cards are now running icy cold when paired up with 2, Corsair H75 AIO coolers. I will admit that I shouldn't have to do this "tinkering" around with this bracket as that's not what it's advertised to do. Anyone who had previously had issues really should give approach a shot. I'm a passed owner of an expensive custom water loop that ended up leaking one day & fried my $2000 gaming rig. I know that AIO's also run the potential of leaking, but I feel that your chances are much lower. Good luck everyone. Sorry for such a long review, but I hope that it can help someone out there...

This is the first camera case I have ever bought so I don't have anything to compare it to. As another reviewer mentioned, it is small inside unless you take out the divider. This case is meant for all S series cameras and I have an S700. With the divider out there is plenty of room for the camera. The top pocket inside the main case is mesh with a zipper closure, and the pocket on the front has room for two memory cards and an additional mesh pocket (no zipper, but the front pocket zips closed). The back of the case has a belt loop - though who could wear something this big on their hip? I like that the case is not huge and bulky - fine for my needs, and it has a strong, detachable strap to carry over my shoulder. Overall a good, well-made case that will last for a long time.

I was honestly surprised by how securely this little speaker was packaged, no wasted space, but still snug in the box with zero chance of bouncing around during shipping. The box itself was pristine when it arrived, which I always appreciate. Pulling it out for the first time, I couldn't get over how light it felt. The matte finish feels nice in hand and doesn't attract fingerprints, and the overall build seems way more solid than I expected at this price. Pairing it with my phone took maybe five seconds, and the little voice prompt was a nice touch. I immediately tested it on my nightstand and the sound filled my bedroom easily. For something so compact, the clarity right out of the gate genuinely impressed me.
It has a bit of red hue to it. That's not the biggest problem in the world - make sure to do a white balance correction if your camera allows it.
It was good while it lasted. Perfect fit, worked in our Nikon charger. But after a year and a half it would not take a charge and we had to replace it.

Bought this hoping it'd help with my overheating issue, and it exceeded expectations. My laptop runs noticeably cooler during long gaming sessions. The tilt feels perfect for my wrists, and it's way sturdier than I thought it'd be. Definitely a solid buy.

I'm very disappointed in this drive, as even with large files I can't write faster than 80MB/sec on a USB 3.0 port. But I don't think it's the drive's fault, as it's capable of much faster speeds and other people get those speeds. The issue with these very fast USB 3.0 drives is that their speed is very much affected by the chipset being used, and the chipset drives. I am using a 2yr-old ASUS motherboard and it uses the ASMedia USB 3.9 chipset. It's a highly regarded chipset but it came out after the NEC/Renasys (sp?) and drivers seem optimized for the latter. The odd thing is that I bought a 32 GB SanDIsk Extreme (not the Pro version, as this is new) last year and it's blazingly fast on the same port on the same computer, so I would have expected this to be at least that fast. One thing to mention: These are expensive drives and the 32 GB Extreme was and still is expensive. The competition 3.0 drives have dropped but not the SanDisk Extremes, which I think is fair as they are so much faster. (I have a couple of other brands USB 3.0 flash drives and they are laughably slow compared to the SanDisks.) However, what stunned me about the Extreme series, given the price, was how incredibly cheaply made they are. They are made of the crummiest plastic you can imagine and I'm sure I could squeeze and shatter it pretty easily. With this new Extreme Pro series, there's still some of that, and these are far more physically delicate drives than other brands (esp given the price) but they don't feel quite as delicate as the Extreme series.

When installed, XP failed to recognize the hub and showed an error on the hardware install. However, the hub works anyway and every device that I've connected to the hub installs and/or is recognized right away. Setup took maybe five minutes, just unbox, attach the stand, plug in, and go. The screen handles glare way better than I expected for this price, and colors look rich without any tinkering in the settings. I've got a Roku stick plugged in, so the lack of built-in apps doesn't bother me at all; honestly, it keeps things simpler. Sound is decent for casual watching, but I paired it with a soundbar for movies. Delivery was quick and the packaging kept everything flawless. If you want a straightforward, great-looking set without paying for smart features you'll never use, this one's a no-brainer.

For the price, it's okay I guess, but the constant flickering and having to jiggle the cable every few minutes kills the value. You get what you pay for, and honestly, I expected a bit more reliability even at this price point.

I ordered this Samsung 16GB microSDHC card on 10/29 from Amazon (Calvin and Hobbs). The shipping time was estimated to be between 11/1 & 11/20. The package arrived 10/31. Doesn't get much faster than that! The Samsung card is perfect in my cell phone. The card lives up to the hype of a class 10. Highly recommended...will not disappoint.
Works well... records games... but if you are looking to use it for recording anything else through your XBOX, it won't work. Its just game play, not Netflix or TV.
Bought this for our home office and it's already saved my PC twice during nasty storms. Switches over instantly, no hiccups. Feels solid and well-built. Really happy with the purchase.

I have had this keyboard for a year. I bought it because it is light and thin. It succeeds in both those areas. The protruding hinge doesn't really bother me although it is not elegant. The rest of the keboard actually is kind of cool. I like the tilting mechanism when it is open. Some complain that the ipad falls out of the keyboard slot. It does occasionally but it works pretty well in general. Now for the negative...and it has become big enough for me that I can't recommend the device. The ipad and keyboard, when in the closed position, does not clasp shut. The ipad rests in the closed position on top of the keyboard. It easily slides off, but more often, it moves just enough to turn the ipad on and wear down the battery. At least once or twice a week, I go to use it and it is dead because it just sat on for 12 hours. You can't see that it is turned on. It is so frustrating. I don't know why they didn't make a clasp or connector to keep the thing closed. Normal travel is bound to jar it open just enought to turn it on. Ugh. If you can manage keeping it closed (or completely turn off the ipad each time) the keyboard works well. Thats it.

I've had this router for about six months now and it hasn't skipped a beat. Runs 24/7 without needing a reboot, even with a dozen devices streaming and gaming at once. The build feels solid, not flimsy at all, and it's survived a couple of accidental knocks from my curious cat. Signal reaches our basement and backyard with no drop-offs. Setup was straightforward and I haven't had to touch the settings since. Genuinely impressed with how stable it's been long-term.
great little antenna does what it should,perfect for traveling with my little 9 inch TV,It was just what I needed

UPDATE: Shortly after writing this review I was contacted by i-BLASON customer support who informed me that they have sent me out a replacement case. That is awesome and deserves 5 stars in itself! I got the frost since it is semi-transparent and still shows the apple logo on the back. I noticed today that all four corners are cracking. I have not dropped my iPad or mishandled it in any way. I even keep it in a Griffen sleeve for extra protection. I see that other reviewers are reporting the same issue. I will wait for something else to come out but for now it is still protecting my iPad well enough but I am afraid that the corners are going to gradually chip away and expose the metal.

I've installed SmartThings in my home about a week ago and it already feels a waste of money. The original ads looked promising, but they have just one problem: little of this will work for you. The main problem of this kit is that you're very unlikely to force it doing anything useful. THE GOOD -Sensors and the hub look better than many other plastic junk boxes out there. -The customer support works great. +1 star for that. -Bundled "things" are very easy to connect. -It sometimes works. THE BAD -The app has a decent look, but a terrible design: it's very confusing and often feels stupid. -To make things worse the app will prompt you to buy extra stuff without being able to run a single scenario. -Scenarios (here they're called apps) are both useless and confusing. Just need to pair a motion sensor and light? Good luck finding an easy way to do that. -Sonos and Philips HUE support works. Only in beta. And only if you consider such (mis)behavior a "work". -Bundled manual refers to the old version of the app. But who cares if it's useless anyway? THE UGLY -I've spent nearly 40 minutes trying to connect the hub to the app. I'm neither mentally limited nor twisted handed, but the whole process is so confusing that I'm not sure I'll be able to repeat it again. -Good luck making the basic kit doing anything more useful than sending you an SMS when someone opens the door. While this might have been a good introduction to home automation, SmartThings are in fact a useless trinket for a few geeks who might have fun solving all the puzzles within its clunky app.
Arrived a day early in secure foam wrap, drive feels snappy right away.

Just purchased this external hard drive for backup purposes. It works exactly as you'd expect. Plug and play with XP, Vista and Windows 7. Drive seems fast (subjectively - didn't do any performance measurements), and is very easy to use. It is also quiet. It's got to be tough to beat for value - ~ $100 for a Terabyte. Heck, I remember when 10 Megabyte drives cost $400 (back in the days of the dinosaurs). I'd recommend it without question.

This little box surprised me. Tossed in a budget 10-inch sub and it hits way cleaner than I expected. No weird rattles, fits behind my truck seat perfectly, and the carpet finish looks solid. For the price, I'm genuinely impressed with how tight the bass sounds.

I've owned this cover for about a year and a half now and I personally think it's held up pretty well. I have the 3rd gen iPad and it fits perfectly and the wake/sleep function works perfectly. I'm actually going to order a new case and came out her to see if i could find one of the same brand because I like it so much. I'm partly buying a new one because of the corners peeling apart, although that doesn't affect the protection or how well it works. The other reason I'm looking for a new one is I'm just looking for a new design. Would definitely recommend is cover!

Honestly, I thought I'd just tolerate it, but I genuinely like using it around the house. Great buy.

Bought this on a whim and it ended up being one of my best random purchases. Handles daily use without a hiccup and still works like day one. Solid little gadget that just keeps going.
