Had this tablet for about six months now and it's held up perfectly through daily drops and travel.
Bought a new raceband video transmitter and needed these to connect the antenna properly, these are great!

I've been using these daily for a few weeks now and honestly, they've exceeded what I expected for the money. The sound is crisp enough for podcasts and calls, and they stay comfortable for hours. I was nervous about the charging port after seeing some complaints, but I just line it up carefully and it's been totally fine. No breaks, no wiggling. For this price point, the solid battery life alone makes them an easy recommendation.

I grabbed this little gadget hoping it wouldn't be a waste of forty bucks, and honestly it's one of the best value buys I've made for my desk setup. My work laptop is Windows and my personal machine is a Mac Mini, and now I just slide my mouse between screens like they're one computer. No extra software, no lag, and the keyboard follows perfectly. I was expecting some jankiness at this price point, but it's been rock solid for two weeks straight. Even the copy-paste across devices works without a hiccup. If you're on the fence about spending more on a big-name KVM, just get this instead.
If you're upgrading from an older model, you'll be shocked by the speed.

After a month of heavy use on trips and family shoots, I'm blown away by how natural the colors look straight out of camera. The ergonomics just click for me, and battery life easily lasts a full day. It's been a joy to learn on and has genuinely made me excited to carry a camera everywhere again.
Great product to get if you're looking to upgrade your network. Although pricey; it gets the job done great.

I've had this APC 11-outlet surge protector running my entire desk setup for close to two years now and it's been rock solid. It handles a 34-inch ultrawide monitor, a gaming PC, two external hard drives, a printer, speakers, and a bunch of chargers without breaking a sweat. The spacing is smart, those chunky power bricks don't block adjacent outlets, which is a huge plus. After a couple of nasty summer storms and a few flickers in the old wiring here, everything plugged in has stayed perfectly safe. The cord is thick and long enough to reach behind a heavy desk easily. It just sits there quietly doing its job. No noise, no issues, still feels like new.
by far the best case i ever owned. it keeps every thing cool, it looks nice and its easy to manage cables. this case or one like it is a must have.

I bought these to keep from talking to my neighbors. I walk the dog 6 days a week for exercise. I found if I walked during the day every annoying ass neighbor for 6 miles wants to talk to me. So, I bought these and listen to music while I walk. The noise isolation isn't extreme because I can still hear cars coming and my idiot neighbors calling my name. The cord is wicked long it's almost annoying. I got no cable wrap in the box like some other guy on here. They are big and stretch over my big melon head to completely cover my ears. Luckily they look huge so my neighbors can see I have them on and they don't think I'm ignoring them. They weigh nothing some idiots probably think that means they are cheaply made. They are quite well constructed. Get a pair you won't regret it.

I really wanted to like these, but after weeks of messing with them I've officially thrown in the towel. The idea of having multiple cameras running on one screen sounded perfect for keeping tabs on my workshop, but the app is just a headache. I'd get one camera to connect for a few minutes, then it would drop, and adding a second was a complete no-go no matter how many times I reset everything. Honestly, I'd only recommend this to someone who has endless patience and enjoys troubleshooting for fun. For a casual user who just wants a simple setup that works out of the box, look elsewhere. Total waste of money for me.

These are Great! The battery lasts pretty much forever. Sound is excellent, noise cancellation is phenomenal! Range could be better but it's right on par with any competing headphones, I've just been spoiled by the old Motorola S11's.

I was honestly surprised by how fast the shipping was, ordered on a Tuesday evening and it was on my doorstep Thursday afternoon. The packaging was minimal but really secure, with everything nestled snugly in molded cardboard, no wasteful plastic wrap. Pulling the little Roku out for the first time was almost funny; it's absurdly tiny, like something you'd overlook in a drawer, but the matte finish and solid feel give it a premium vibe right away. Setup was practically instant, and the interface felt snappy from the jump. I've been grinning ever since plugging it in. For the price, the whole unboxing and initial experience felt way more polished than I expected. Zero regrets.

I wanted to like this bag, especially for what it costs, but I just can't justify keeping it. For the price, I expected way more usable space. The interior dividers eat up a ton of room, and once my mirrorless setup is inside, there's barely any space left for everyday items like snacks or a light jacket. The padding is thick, but it feels overbuilt in all the wrong places, like they prioritized armor over actual practicality. If I'm dropping this much cash on a camera backpack, I want it to handle a day trip without feeling like I'm carrying a rigid box. It's just not a good value when cheaper bags offer better organization and comfort.

I already had a Roku, earlier model, in use so was predisposed towards buying another one. The HD model has a different menu set up than the XL but that's okay. Sort of keeps one attentive. In any event, I do a lot of movie streaming from amazon and Netflix and find Roku delivers quite nicely. It also has many more channel offerings than its competitors. Get one.

We did not have a lot of success with this product. USB devices worked better. As I recall we had some setup problems and well and it's still setting in a drawer in a desk someplace. Would not buy again.

Arrived a day earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. The packaging was simple but secure, no crushed corners or loose bits rattling around, just a tidy little box with the splitter snug inside. First thing I noticed taking it out: the plastic feels sturdy, not that cheap brittle stuff some adapters use. Ports are tight, and the cable clicks in with a reassuring snap. Popped it inline between my router feed and two devices in the living room and everything linked up instantly, no fiddling. Lights on the router blinked right away and speeds held steady for streaming. Honestly, for something this small and affordable, the build quality and no-hassle setup blew me away. Very happy straight out of the gate.

Picked this up for my A7III and it's basically lived on the camera since. Autofocus is snappy, silent, and dead-on. The zoom range covers almost everything I shoot day to day without swapping glass. Light enough to carry all day and the image quality genuinely surprised me. Fantastic walkaround lens for the money.

These earbuds punch way above their cost. I grabbed them as a cheap backup, but they've become my daily pair. Clear vocals for podcasts, decent bass for music, and they don't fall out when I'm doing yard work. For this price, I wasn't expecting much, but they're a total steal.

This camera is so conveniently small and simple to use that I have completely abandoned my EOS Rebel DSLR. I've been at locations where I've shot with both cameras, and the ELPH 110 takes much sharper, better pictures, so why carry the extra baggage? I've even started taking it with me to libraries instead of my hand-held scanner--it's smaller, the battery lasts longer, and I can instantly see my documents in high detail. The video capability is very impressive for such a small camera. The only flaw: when using the zoom feature while taking video, a humming noise is recorded on the video soundtrack.
Bought this for a weekend project and it just killed my whole afternoon. Tried every port, swapped cables, still a dead screen. Absolute waste of money.
It was a top quality product but it was not compatible with my system so I had to return make sure to check I did but got poor information. Still it was a good product.
Arrived fast in a sturdy box, looks decent out of the package, just bulkier than I expected.

Bought this for my dad, who is 70 and hasn't done much radio. I programmed it with local amateur frequencies before I gave it to him and he is talking on it with no problems. I did send him "The (Chinese) Radio Documentation Project" manual, which is based on the Yaesu manual layout and is much easier to read than the one that comes with the radio. http://radiodoc. github.

I had issues with my first two pairs of these headphones as they both broke after a month of use, but after relaying these difficulties to the company, they worked quickly with me and sent me a new pair. They are very comfortable headphones and work great. I have had no other issues with these products at any point during use, whether it be for during work or when I go rollerblading or skateboarding. I am very pleased with the efforts made by this company to make a sincere effort to supply all of their customers with quality products and service, and would recommend if you were going to purchase headphones from any company, to make it this one.

I picked this up mostly for the extra AC outlets when I'm stuck at a crowded airport gate or in a hotel room with one accessible plug. The three-outlet design is perfect for keeping my phone, laptop, and my partner's tablet all topped off without hunting for wall space. The compact shape slides easily into the front pocket of my carry-on and doesn't add any noticeable weight. I also really appreciate the built-in cord wrap, it stays tidy and never tangles with my other cables. The USB port has been a handy bonus for my wireless earbuds and older music player. For the price, it's a solid little travel companion that I've ended up using way more than I expected.
I've taken this camcorder on three camping trips now, and it's held up perfectly, no issues with dust, bumps, or battery life. Solid little workhorse.

Really liked this camera except it was a little too small for my fingers to operate. I didn't have it more than a month or two when i loaned it to my grandson & he managed to lose it. Otherwise it took great pictures. I have now just purchased a Powershot sx500.will be sure not to loan this one.will let you know how i like it when i get it

I grabbed this little player so I can binge my favorite shows without fighting over the living room setup, and it's been a total win. The screen is crisp, colors pop nicely, and the sound through my headphones is surprisingly clean. Setup took maybe two minutes, just pop in a disc and go. Battery life holds steady through a couple of movies, which is great for lazy afternoons. It's lightweight, comfy to hold, and honestly makes solo watching feel like a treat instead of a compromise.

I've wrestled with lots of screen protectors over the last couple years; cameras, cellphones, GPS systems, iPods, Kindle 2. This Green Onions protector was easy peasy and looks great. It looks exactly like the Kindle screen- no glare. It is a little bit smaller than the screen which is a good thing since it simplifies application. It is always such a pain to try to apply one that is EXACTLY the same size. I never get it lined up perfectly with the screen and have to endlessly fiddle with it. The Green Onions is sturdy which makes it easier to handle than the thinner films. My first try was a keeper. One teeny tiny bubble that I can live with. There are two in the pack which is probably a good idea for first timers with screen protectors, but it would have been nice to be able to purchase a single for those of us with experience and established technique.

I use lens hoods to improve contrast, as well as to provide a bit of physical protection for the lens, since I don't believe in adding a "protective" filter to the front of a lens, except where there is a chemical or severe debris risk. That said, this one is pretty small, so not much protection physically, but if it helps increase contrast, then it will be worth the investment.

This keyboard is pretty great. It takes a while to get used to... I have an iMac at home and a IBM Thinkpad at work. I prefer Thinkpad keys, but are getting used to the new Apple keyboard without any problem. No difference really with the extended wired keyboard that comes with the new imac in terms of feel. The size makes it great to use instead of the wired keyboard. Of course, with the wired keyboard, you also would have 2 extra USB ports... The only problem I have is when you use VMWare to run Vista, I tend to lose connections with both the wireless keyboard and mouse (both bluetooth). This problem doesn't go away when you quite VMWare. I have to plug in the wired keyboard and mouse to continue. A bit of annoyance, but won't be a problem if you don't use VMWare to run Windows. Hope this helps.

Needed something basic to repurpose an old laptop drive and this did the trick. Speeds are okay, not blazing, but totally fine for the price. Feels a bit flimsy, though it hasn't failed yet. Does exactly what I expected.

I wasn't expecting much from a clip-on speakerphone at this price, but the Jabra Cruiser 2 surprised me. Pairing with my older car's stereo via FM transmitter took maybe two minutes, and call quality on my end is clear enough that people don't realize I'm on speaker. Voice prompts are straightforward, and it announces caller names from my phonebook, which is a nice touch. The battery holds up well for my commute-heavy week. My only real gripe is that music streaming sounds tinny compared to a direct aux connection, but for podcasts and calls it's totally fine. For anyone with a non-Bluetooth vehicle, this thing punches above its weight.

Okay; homemade security camera? Check. Raspberry Pi media center? Check. Printer Server? Check. Hidden low power storage for the security camera? check that too. Unified way to power it all without a mess of cables? Now I can check that off too. It works and works well. I have 4 Raspberry Pi based devices and 2 Unifi APs running of of this so far with out any issues.

This is one of the WORST consumer electronic devices ever made. Most of the time, it does not work. Calls get cut off. Calls don't go through. Customer service is the pits--totally ineffective and no one has "authority" to address consumers' problems. They insult your intelligence. Look elsewhere for VOIP. UPDATE. It took 16 days for Ooma to send a replacement for a defective unit under warranty. That's 15 days linger than it should have taken.
Exactly what I needed, no issues at all.
I was a little surprised, but happily, it is the perfect size. It is smaller than I expected, but so easy to handle, and it does not diminish the sound. Very pleased,Straightforward operation.
I have bought tamron caps for the longest time because they are tight on your lens and won't fall off. this is no exception. get it.

I consider myself more then just a novice when in comes to the ins and outs of installing hardware and software. But this one has so far managed to elude me. I have an Asus P6T v1 and needed the SATA III along with the RAID capability. I might note that my MB has two onboard RAUD controllers one of which is in use. After about 26hrs straight of messing around with this thing I am positive there is some setting that I can't find or change that allows me to have both the RAID card and the onboard RIAD running at the same time. I was able to get the RAID card to install and even managed a RAID0 config with windows installed. But for some reason I am no longer able to get back into that windows config. So I am trying my hardest to see a point in keeping this harware. Tech support at High Point seem to be unable to give me an answer as to what settings I should be shooting for in both of the BIOS for the MOBO and the card. Bottom line is relly unless you have a ton of time and a load of asprin I wouldn't suggest going for this card... I'll update if I manage to find some kind of solution but as it is it is far from plug and play.
for the price, this is a good set. i can't tell more expensive ones from less, so price is a good sorting tool for me. really only wanted the UV for protecting my lens anyway.

These headphones are the gift that keeps on giving. I've had mine for a couple years now and they still blow me away every time I put them on. The soundstage is wide and detailed without being clinical, perfect for long listening sessions. I did swap the pads for something plusher, and now they disappear on my head. Zero fatigue, even after hours of use. Honestly, if these ever broke I'd buy the same pair again without hesitation.
Great product works excellent for my media player fast and smooth great quality service
Sticky enough, but the build feels cheap.

I will return it. For three reasons: 1) It requires the root SSID to be broadcasted. 2) In repeater mode, it shows up as a dup SSID. Basically if the root AP's SSID is "Foo", with this repeater, I see two "Foo" on my Macbook Air. 3) Its plug is right in the middle and its body's bulky. That cause a problem: when I plug it in to a panel with two outlets, the other outlet is barely accessable. I also bought the other repeater TP-LINK TL-WR700N. The TP-link is better. It supports hidden root SSID and it doesn't add a dup. Its plug is on the side so it works well with the plug in the other outlet.

I grabbed this little point-and-shoot before a weeklong camping trip, and honestly, for the price it's hard to beat. I didn't want to risk my phone in the rain and dirt, and this thing handled it all without a hiccup. The image quality won't blow you away in low light, but in daylight the shots are crisp and the colors pop nicely. I really appreciate that it runs on AA batteries, no hunting for an outlet in the middle of nowhere. It's lightweight, slips into a jacket pocket, and the simple menus meant I wasn't fumbling around. For a budget-friendly travel companion, it does exactly what I needed.

For the price, this flash drive does the job. It's compact and the speed is okay for moving documents around. My main gripe is durability, the plastic body already has a small crack near the connector after a month of light use. I can't complain too much given how cheap it was, but I was hoping it would last a bit longer. If you just need something temporary or for occasional file transfers, it's a decent value.

I was worried this programming cable would be flaky with my Baofeng, but the chipset inside is solid, no driver headaches on Windows 11. Plugged it in, assigned the COM port, and Chirp recognized everything instantly. I've reprogrammed three radios now without a single hiccup. Just make sure you fully seat the connector; it's a snug fit but that's actually reassuring.

I prefer the sound of these to the A5+. I think the A5+ is over hyped compare to these. With these you can skip a sub woofer if you live in an apartment. I couldn't afford to keep these thou because the price is a factor to me

I've had this watch for about four months now, logging around 25 miles a week, and it still looks fresh out of the box. No scratches on the face, band hasn't frayed, and battery life holds steady even after back-to-back long runs. Rain, sweat, and the occasional clumsy drop haven't fazed it. Solid little workhorse.

It is a bit more light weight than I thought it would be, all in all, it is a very sturdy tripod. Although, there was an excess amount of lubricant on it when it first came. I use this with my canon T3i and am able to add a mount with an LED light and mic to.

My package arrived with the box completely crushed on one side, like it had been stepped on. Opening it, the plastic tray inside was cracked and the headphones were just rattling around loose. First impression was awful, cheap, lightweight materials that feel like they'll snap in a week. No protective foam or anything. For the price, I expected at least basic care in shipping and packaging. Really disappointing start.

I was a little nervous buying this MacBook Pro refurbished, but honestly it arrived looking brand new. The battery cycle count was only 12, which blew me away. Screen is flawless, no dead pixels or backlight bleed, and the keyboard feels crisp like it's barely been touched. My only minor gripe is the original box was pretty beat up during shipping, but the laptop itself was packed so well it didn't matter. Performance wise, it handles Logic Pro and multiple browser tabs without breaking a sweat. For the price I paid, this was a solid deal and I'd definitely go the refurb route again. Four stars only because I wish the power adapter wasn't a generic replacement.

Avoid it. The top portion is made of plastic. No matter how I adjust, it will not stay leveled. You will need to get a separate ballhead to mount your camera. The mounting screw is too recessed. You will need to carry a screwdriver to mount a camera.
fast delivery. Really useful if you need to have a long connection to speakers or head set.

These speakers were a great value replacement for the stock system. There is nothing special about these other than the frequency range is good and they don't distort. They are not very loud, although I am running them with a 4x50W Pioneer and Pyle Components up front that blast these away. Once I get an added amp for the components, I will see how they do with 200W, they will probably be able to handle more volume and distortion with more power available. I am satisfied so far and have not been disappointed yet. If you are interested, I'd recommend these for anyone looking for a good decent clean sound without any problems.

I'll be honest, I wasn't expecting much from something this thin and simple, but it totally surprised me. I stuck it on a wall behind the TV, not even near a window, and I'm pulling in over 40 channels with really clear picture. Local news, football games, old sitcom reruns, everything just works. Setup took maybe five minutes, and I love that there's no bulky rabbit-ear nonsense. I'd recommend this to anyone who wants to ditch cable but still catch the main networks without monthly fees. Perfect for apartments, dorms, or anyone living in the suburbs who wants a cheap, low-profile fix for free TV.

The box arrived in perfect shape, no dents, no crushed corners. Inside, everything was wrapped securely with that satisfying peel-away film still intact. Slapped it on in seconds, zero bubbles, and the clarity blew me away right out of the gate. Feels premium to the touch, not sticky or cheap. Honestly impressed with how fast it shipped too.
Worked fine for a couple months but now the connection cuts out randomly. Jacket's already fraying near the plug. Okay for light use.

I picked this up for my dad, who's always misplacing his headset, and it's been perfect. The magnetic dock sticks solidly to his nightstand, so he actually remembers to charge it. Pairing was instant with his Android phone, and call clarity surprises me every time, his voice comes through crisp even when he's outside. Battery lasts him days with moderate chatting. The fit kit made it easy to size down for his ears. Honestly, I'd recommend this to anyone buying for an older parent or someone who hates fussy tech. Simple, reliable, and the wall-mount option is genius.

Bought this camera about a month ago, what an awesome peice of electronics. Bought the HDMI cable to view on my 50" HDTV, very nice, I think the picture is as good as some of the HD shows on TV or even better. For the price, I feel the HDMI cable should come with it, but oh well what ya gonna do? Camera is so light weight that it's hard for me to keep steady while walking, but then I'm a bit heavy and not so light on my feet. The access wheel on the LCD is a bit awkward but not bad once you get use to it. I always tend to spin it past where I want to be. Bought a Tiffen enhancing filter kit and man did it bring out the colors. Built in mic is so sensitive I can here myself breath and can hear the hard disk motor when very quiet, definitely need to get the Canon DM-50 mic. Not sure which software suite to buy even though the software that comes with it is good enough to transfer video files and still images. Don't like the idea of having to plug in the charger each time I want to transfer files. All in all I feel it is a great hard drive camera with nice features, however I should of waited another couple months for the new HF-10. Now that camera seems awesome, with basicly no moving parts and removable flash memory to transfer files, 12X optical and 30P - WOW!! Next time!!

I've been using this monitor daily for almost eight months now, and it's held up well. No dead pixels, no flickering, and the thin bezels still look clean on my desk. The plastic casing feels a little hollow and the whole thing wobbles if I bump the desk hard, but it hasn't cracked or warped. I was worried the stand might loosen over time, but it's stayed tight. I did add a small rubber shim under one side to level it perfectly, minor annoyance. For the price I paid, the panel uniformity is acceptable, and I haven't noticed any burn-in or backlight bleed getting worse. It's not premium, but as a budget workhorse, the durability has surprised me.

This is truly a fantastic little class d amp with good clarity and enough punch to drive a few bass notes. I have used a variety of these little class d type amps over the years to drive aux sound in spare rooms, garage and the like. Most all of these amps were good for what I used the, for but just did not have that clarity nor reserve power needed to get truly good sound out of them. After a while I would find all of them just okay but not amazing like I had hoped. now granted I realize these are inexpensive little amps and I can not or should not compare them with say a Rotel, Yamaha or Harmon kardon so that is not what I am looking for. But I do like to have nice clean sound with a lil bass at times. When we needed something extra to go with the sound bar connected to our main tv I just did not want to go all home theater on the set up - it is just too expensive, too bulky and too power hungry. What I wanted was to drive my Polk bookshelf speakers with the 3.5 mm jack off my tv which would work with my volume control also. Did some searching and came across this sweet little mama jama and figured why not - if I don't like it I send it back, right? Well wow is all we can say. Got this in a jiffy, hooked it up and connected my polks. This sucker sounds amazing - crystal clear highs and nice rumbly lows are coming across consistently without any sign of distortion or excess heat being generated by the amp. Do not hesitate - grab one of these units now before they realize it should be priced higher!!

I've had these sleeves for over a year now and they've held up great, no tearing or bending, even with frequent handling. My discs stay scratch-free and the slim profile freed up so much shelf room. Still look practically new.

Got the item, and was excited since I needed to verify all the cables in my house. Started using it, and it worked for checking 3-4 cable runs, green lights would line up (make sure you check the receiver unit, as it needs to blink the green lights in the correct order). Then after that the power light on the main unit just stayed solid, couldn't get it to work after that. Tried changing batteries, etc, nothing. For the price it's a great deal, but if you need to rely on it, you might want to look elsewhere or buy two just in case.
Great product, perfect size fore a guest bed room - good price
These sound way better than I expected for the price, super comfy for long calls.
Perfect for breadboard tinkerers, solid grip every time, ideal starter set for beginners.

The dock works great, and it clears up a lot of space on my desk at work. I have this connected to a 28" 4k monitor, so the desk is nice and minimalistic with plenty of computer workspace to get work done. My only complaint with this dock is the length of the extension cables coming out of the dock. They are simple pass-through cables, letting you plug your device cables into the ports on your macbook without needing to take apart the dock every time you need to change a cable. For things like USB, its perfect. However, the cables are all 2+ feet long. It is excessive. The thick bunch of white cables coming out of the back of the dock is difficult to manage and keep out of the way. I would have preferred to have cables only a few inches long, so that I can plug my cables in close to the back of the dock.
This little brick is a solid match for my KX3. It runs the radio and tops off the batteries without breaking a sweat. If you're a portable op who needs dependable field power, grab one.
Transaction, quality, etc., all fine. Potential purchasers should note that the small plug is NOT micro-USB.
This tiny chip makes every adventure look crisp and effortless.

I bought this specific speaker because of the FM mode that way I can save some battery while listening at work. Many other reviewers mentioned the radio on the same exact model I have and it's not available on mine. I believe it was removed. Also the nasal voice informing you that the speaker is on, what mode it's on and that bluetooth is paired is bit annoying. Turning the volume all the way up will make a loud beeping sound letting you know you on max volume. The sound is really good though. Very loud and crisp.

Got the vivoactive HR on sale, and for what I paid it's a solid deal. The GPS locks fast and tracks my local trail walks accurately, which is exactly what I wanted without spending flagship money. Battery life is no joke, I'm five days in and still sitting around 65% with regular use. The screen is a bit dim indoors, especially coming from a brighter tracker, but outdoors it's perfectly readable. App setup took a minute, and there's a slight learning curve with the menus. I turned off most sport profiles I don't use to keep things simple. Overall, a capable fitness watch that punches above its price point if you catch a discount.
These are great units, highly functional for the price paid. Microtik OS is LOADED with features: pseudo wire, VPN, routing, vlan support, wifi in this unit. very happy.

I've been using this cassette adapter in my 2004 Silverado for about three weeks now and it just works every single time. No ejection issues, no weird grinding noises, just plug it into my phone and go. The sound quality honestly surprised me, clear highs and solid bass without that annoying background hiss I've gotten from other adapters. I mostly listen to podcasts and 90s rock, and everything comes through clean. The cord length is generous enough to reach my dashboard mount without being a tangled mess. For the price, this little thing has been a total game changer for my commute. If you're still rocking a factory tape deck, grab one of these without overthinking it.

I've been using this headset for a few months now, mainly for jogging and doing chores around the house. The stereo mode is surprisingly crisp for the price point, bass isn't earth-shattering, but the mids are clean and podcasts sound great. My favorite thing is how reliably it stays paired; I can leave my phone in the kitchen and wander into the garage without the audio cutting out. The ear hook design keeps it secure during burpees and sprints, which I appreciate. Only real gripe is the bulkiness after an hour or so, the weight starts pressing behind my ear and I have to adjust it. Battery life is solid, around six hours in my experience. For the convenience factor alone, it's been a solid upgrade from tangled wires.

the rings are pretty light weight but I've not had any problems with them jamming. Just be careful. If you want to make big jumps (77 to 58 or whatever) a single step down (such as made by B+H) might be a better alternative as these when stacked up stick out quite a bit from the lens. But from 77-72 it works great and saved me having to purchase another filter.
Arrived in two days with zero damage, box was pristine. Unboxing felt premium right away and it's been a joy to use ever since.

I purchased this device because I needed additional ports for my MacBook Air. This is a well made hub, is working perfectly, and certainly looks like it goes with the system. And it interfaces well with my USB 3.0 backup drives which is also extremely important. It would imagine that it would also work for Windows systems.
This little thing keeps my dash cam running 24/7 without any voltage drops, super solid build.
Although the 3.5mm plug has to be changed this switching power supply is just right for the Elecraft KX3 transceiver offering sufficient current and voltage to run the unit and charge the batteries.
I gave this to my wife as a gift. She uses them all the time.
Perfect for small apartments, I'd tell any renter to grab one.
Piece if junk. Don't bother. Terrible sound and it will skip from time to time when it has no reason to.
Fit on my desk great. Sound is poor but that is what speakers are for.
Picked this up hoping it wouldn't bottleneck my new plan and it totally delivered. Setup took maybe 15 seconds and now everything streams and loads without a hiccup. Super solid little gadget.

Had this KVM switch for about four months now, and it's starting to show its age. The plastic casing already has a small crack near the HDMI port just from normal plugging and unplugging. Functionally it still works, but the video flickers occasionally on my second monitor if the cable gets bumped even slightly. The USB ports also feel looser than when I first got it, sometimes my keyboard doesn't register unless I wiggle the connector. I'm not rough with my gear, so seeing this kind of wear this quickly is disappointing. For light, set-it-and-forget-it use it might hold up, but if you need to switch connections often, I'd worry about long-term durability.

Bought this tv on black Friday 2010 from Best Buy! It is now February 2012 and the tv is dead! 1year 2 months and that's all $1000 worth of Panasonic tv got me. Simply put, the picture quality is really nice while the stupid thing works, but plan on only having it work for less than 2 years. Panasonic customer service will not assist in any way to remedy the situation and they won't even give me the contact info for an authorized repair place. Needless to say that this will be my first and last Panasonic product ever. I will never purchase another Panasonic product no matter what cnet or anyone else says about them and no matter how "highly" they're rated.

The box arrived pretty banged up, with the antenna rattling around loose inside, no padding at all. First impression wasn't great, honestly. The plastic base felt flimsy and cheap right out of the gate. Setup was straightforward, but the flimsy packaging already had me worried something might be off internally. Not a great start for something that's supposed to pull in clear signals.
Got this as replacement for first gen iPad that was slow. This unit would not connect to my home WiFi. Seemed slower than the old iPad I sold.

For the price, these are a solid grab if you've got kids or tend to misplace styluses constantly. They glide smoothly enough on my Galaxy Tab and don't skip, though the tips feel a tiny bit hollow compared to pricier ones. I'd recommend this pack to casual note-takers or anyone who just needs reliable backups around the house without sweating the cost.
Fast, very fast. Samsung quality at it's best. Installation was easy and the cloning from my old drive was quick and painless.

Very solid and durable cable, which allowed me to easily run my surround sound speakers. We were using bare cable before, which constantly wriggled around and caught feet and broke away from the speaker. With this, and a Wiremold kit, everything is solid, mostly invisible, and best yet AWAY FROM THE KIDS AND THEIR WIRE-KILLING WAYS! Be aware that I wouldn't recommend running more than 100W peak over this cable, due to the 18AWG thickness. If your receiver peaks higher than 150W, for safety sake use 14AWG or lower, as thickness increases as the number drops.
OMG. These headphones are amazing, they have wonderful noise cancellation and wonderful bass/vibration aspects. Would refer to friends and purchase more. I have always loved skull candy audio.

These are way too expensive to be this unreliable. For the price, I expected something that would at least last a few months, not barely three weeks. The left earbud started cutting out constantly, and now it just doesn't charge half the time no matter how I position it in the case. I've cleaned the contacts, reset them, followed every troubleshooting step I could find online, nothing helps. Honestly, you can get similar sound quality from budget brands for a third of the cost and probably better durability. Total waste of money. I regret not returning them immediately when they first started acting up. Don't make the same mistake I did.

This is my second Anker battery charger. I usually carry one in my truck and car. Always handy when electrical outlets are not available or easy to get to. Makes a great gift to those who have lots of electronic toys.

I have used literally thousands of discs of various brands and I have found these PHILLIPS discs to be extremely reliable. I've recorded a dozen different file formats and could not be more satisfied.

the connector on the zune is not in the middle as belkin expected but rather more to the top of the unit, so it doesn't line up at all. i'm hoping belkin own up to their mistake and ship a proper plastic outer casing. even with this deficiency i'm keeping it, because its easy to pop off to connect it. it's also the nicest unit out there for those long trips watching those battery draining tv shows and movies.
Got these for my laser pointer and they work great. Super cheap compared to local shops and arrived fast. Definitely worth it.

I'd only recommend this system to someone I really dislike. Right out of the gate, the setup was a nightmare, missing cables and a mouse that were clearly listed on the packaging. Customer support shrugged and told me to buy them myself, so I dropped extra cash just to get it powered on. When I finally plugged everything in, the DVR wouldn't boot. Just a blank screen and a faint clicking sound. Lorex gave me the runaround for weeks, refusing any refund or replacement. It's basically a heavy paperweight now. Save your money and frustration, unless you've got an enemy who deserves a broken gadget and a headache.
Worth every penny, works like a charm.

I picked this up mainly for work, and the split-screen functionality has been a game changer for multitasking. I can have a full-size spreadsheet open on one side and a browser on the other without squinting. Colors are vibrant out of the box, and the curve really pulls you in without being distracting. Setup was dead simple, and my desk finally feels like a proper workstation.
The microphone works well, people tell me. However, I think they skimped on the ear piece part. It doesn't reproduce clearly enough for me.

This clock is so cute and durable. I got it for my little girl so it would teach her responsibility in getting up on her own. I love the pink color and how it lights up pink. The covering is pink silicone. You set the time with the two buttons on top and it has the alarm set button on the left side use the hour and minutes buttons along with the alarm set button to set the alarm. You can use the button on the right side to cut the alarm off and on. It also has a snooze light button on top so you can see the time better in the dark. Push the button and the clock will illuminate for about 5 seconds so you can see the time. It runs on 3 AAA batteries (not included). Thank you for taking time to read my review:)

This home theater system is awesome. I did experience the "dead subwoofer" but found that just unplugging it for a few minutes and plugging it back in seemed to fix it. haven't had an issue in weeks now. The sound from this system is amazing. I can't really imagine it sounding much better. The highs are high, the woofer booms nice and low but does sometimes reach it peak if I have it up loud during a space movie or something. Buy this sound system. You won't regret it.

These things are just meh. Bought them hoping for a simple grab-and-go audio option but the overall experience is super clunky. The biggest headache is how they handle switching between devices, there's basically no way to just pick a new source without wrestling with whatever they were last hooked up to. Half the time I'm running around the house turning off Bluetooth on other gadgets just to get a connection, which defeats the whole point. Sound is flat, not terrible but nothing you'd enjoy, and the fit feels cheap. For the money I expected at least basic usability, but I spend more time troubleshooting than listening. Probably fine if you only ever use one device, otherwise it's just a frustrating little gadget.

I purchased this for around $130 as a back up. I figure, I am spending this much money, it oughtta be a pretty decent unit. Well, while it stores information, the transfer rate is HORRIBLE, even when plugged into a USB 2.0 certified port. While trying to access this HDD through my computer, it takes anywhere from 5-15 seconds for all the information to register on the HDD. In other words, when you click to access the HDD, none or only part of what is stored on the HDD is available until it loads fully, which can take as much as 15 seconds. The thing is, is that there is still about 1.25TB left, so transfer rates should NOT be this slow. I expected this to respond much faster. I would say that to transfer 60GB it takes about an hour. I can transfer files between my 700GB Caviar Black and 640GB Caviar Blue WAAAAAAY faster than from my Black to this. And the Caviar Black is a pretty speedy little HDD. Is this something that should be expected from large storage units? Bottom line, if you have the ability to spend a little more for a better unit, I suggest doing so. Perhaps I got one that isn't functioning to its full potential. Perhaps I don't know how to use a PnP device... Hahaha. Right. At least it stores the information. 3.29.2012 Update Now that I have had it for some time, I would still have purchased another unit. HOWEVER, hooked up to my laptop, it operates much better than on my PC. Not quite sure why this is, but doesn't really matter. At this point I would not call it horrible, just unsatisfactory.

I purchased this as an extra battery for my Canon SD1400 IS after a week-long camping trip in the mountains of Southern Utah. The original battery never ran out of power, but I couldn't review all the photos and 720p movies because I was concerned about using all the original battery power. This battery is identical to the original and is a genuine Canon battery. Works and looks just like the original battery that came with the camera. The battery was shipped two weeks sooner than the estimated delivery date. It arrived packaged securely in good condition.

Grabbed this for my 10-year-old's school projects, and it's been a total win. Setup was painless, and the touchscreen feels snappy enough for games and videos too. Battery easily lasts through her whole school day plus evening cartoons. Solid little workhorse that doesn't break the bank, we're both super happy with it.

I've gone through three phone mounts this year alone, and this one actually stays put. The clamp is rock solid on my vent, and my phone hasn't budged even on bumpy roads. Installation took maybe twenty seconds. Honestly, I wish I'd just bought this one first and saved myself the hassle.

Don't ask me how they make money on this...it is a well-made product, and it cost me five bucks with free shipping. I don't get it! (And I ain't complainin'!) I use it with my Kindle Paperwhite (2014). The fit isn't perfect, as it is designed for the Fire, but it works just fine. Inside there is a tab in each of the corners to hold down the tablet. Since this isn't spec'd for the Paperwhite, it doesn't fit in those tabs, so it can slide around. But only just barely, and since it ships with a styrofoam insert (to keep its shape during shipping), I use that for extra protection and it works great. So I definitely recommend this for other Kindle Paperwhite (or equivalent-sized e-reader) owners.

Bought in december 2015 and its already not working. Purchased for my son to ise on his xbox 1. Worked fine for about a year and now the xbox just doesnt detect it anymore. Tons of games on it that he cant access anymore.

It's easy to forget how much fun it is to just hand someone a photo they can keep, right then and there. I picked this up for a family reunion and it was the star of the whole event. The built-in flash is surprisingly decent for indoor shots, and the photos have that soft, nostalgic vibe you just can't fake with a filter. Yes, the film packs aren't cheap, but that's part of the charm, it makes every shot feel intentional. Loading the cartridge is dead simple too, no fussing around. If you want a break from endless phone pics and actually enjoy the moment, this thing delivers pure joy.

Package arrived two days early in sturdy, no-nonsense packaging, no dents, no drama. Unboxing felt premium, with everything snug and clearly labeled. Plugged it in and got solid downstream lock in under five minutes. First impression? This thing just works. Wi-Fi coverage immediately felt stronger and more stable across three devices. Honestly impressed right out of the gate.
Does exactly what I needed, solid connection, no flickering, and charges faster than the cheap one I had before.

I've had this thing clipped to my backpack for about three months now, and honestly it's held up way better than I expected. I use it daily on my commute, and it's been banged against subway poles, dropped on concrete a few times, and even got caught in a heavy downpour once. The hinge still feels solid and the grip hasn't loosened at all. I was a little skeptical at first because it's so lightweight, but the plastic casing took the abuse without cracking. Only reason I'm not giving five stars is the clip design could be a tad more secure, I added a tiny rubber band just for peace of mind when it's on thicker straps. Still, for the price, the durability is impressive.
I'm blown away by how cool this thing stays even when pumping out max wattage. No overheating at all. I can toss my phone on it overnight and wake up to a full battery without worrying about damage.
This is the right one for old model Sony Camcorder.

I've hand it for over 6 months now. Had to buy a sharper to keep it sharp, blade seems to dull quickly but just get one of these at the same time: http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000O8OTNC/ref=cm_cr_ryp_prd_ttl_sol_79 The rest is great, I use it mostly for computers, shipping boxes, and general dad type stuff that needs cut / fixed / adjusted on the fly.
Perfect for anyone building a budget-friendly dual-GPU rig.

I've had this light for about eight months now, and it's become my go-to for early morning dog walks and weekend camping trips. The beam is surprisingly focused for such a compact body, and it cuts through fog or heavy woods way better than I expected. Build quality feels solid, mine's survived a few clumsy drops onto concrete and a surprise dunk in a creek without any issues. I really appreciate that it runs on 18650s since I already had a charger and a few cells from an old laptop pack. Runtime on medium is more than enough for a full evening around camp. If you're someone who needs a dependable, no-fuss light for outdoorsy stuff or just keeping in the glovebox for emergencies, I'd absolutely point you toward this one.
These are amazing. They work as intended and tapping in to the powered mirror is easy. This helped hide the wires in both of our cars.
Still going strong after months of daily use, no loose stitching or zipper issues at all.
The grain is so fine even at 1600, and the shadow detail stays rich without getting muddy. My go-to for concert photography now.

Small and very sturdy piece of hardware. This device is the unmanaged. This is all I needed for my last port available on my router. Once the switch was hooked up I wired the 65" smart TV and the Blue Ray so that we could view movies on Vudu. Simple setup.

After owning these for almost eight months, I'm impressed by how well they've held up. The ear cushions haven't flattened, and the headband still feels solid with zero creaking. I've accidentally dropped them a couple times and there's barely a scuff. Battery life hasn't noticeably degraded either. One star off just because I wish the charging port had a cover.
There's a hum that disappears when I turn on my itouch, but it is annoying when I turn it on. But the price was right for the little I use it.

I've been using this stylus daily for note-taking and sketching, and it's been flawless. No lag, no scratches, and it feels super natural in hand. Battery lasts forever between charges. Honestly didn't expect this level of quality at the price point. My iPad experience has completely changed for the better, wish I'd grabbed one sooner.
I've used these in my Canon for weeks now and they just keep going without needing a recharge.
When you zoom in all the way you can see the hood in photo

I ordered this for my Canon Powershot ELPH 360 and it fits like a glove. Honestly better than I expected, snug without being tight, and the padding feels substantial enough for drops. The carabiner clip was a nice bonus, though mine came with a longer shape which actually works better for clipping to my backpack. Solid little case.

Got this for my EOS R6 setup after a buddy kept raving about his. Rock solid, no lag, and the build feels way more premium than I expected for the price. If you're a hobbyist stepping into off-camera work or just want a dependable spare in your bag, this is it.

Wondered about the bent center conductors at first, then noticed the insert page with RED INK telling me that the bent center conductors are normal, and appropriate, and why they are like that (keeps them from puncturing the shipping materials). I can see "experience" written all over that page - and I thank them for providing it!! Terminators work perfectly, quality is excellent - the threads turn smoothly and don't "grind" the way poor quality ones do, shipping was nearly instantaneous, even though I used the cheapest method.

I purchased this for my sisters laptop when she broke her USB ports, however she didn't have the PCMCIA slot. The product was exactly what I purchased and was delivered quickly. I did return the item and after UPS charges and a 10.00 restocking fee, I got 11.00 back. Note to self: don't rust what your sister says when you ask her if there is a 2 inch port on the size of a credit card on her laptop, check it out for yourself:D

My wife had dropped her phone during hurricane Sandy on the way to the car and lost and found returned it 3 days later. AT&T could not read the SIM. I tried this and was able to recover her phone book. I could only view it, but was able to use a screen capture tool to copy and print it out. my wife could then enter all the lost phone numbers manually into her new phone. At first it would not bring up the phone book. I had to try to open messages first then when I went to the phone book it read the entries.

I have a dive camera that is good but too much trouble for day-to-day pool and kayak use. I really wanted the Olympus TG-810 but didn't want another $400 camera. Picked this up on a trial basis just based on price. I was pleasantly surprised. Took a bunch of pics in and around the pool and the quality was great. It's really slow to recycle between shots but all pics were in focus and the colors wonderful. Love that it has an auto white balance setting for underwater and I don't have to mess with filters. Will be perfect for kayaking and snorkeling.

Had this camera for a few months now, it's been dropped, dunked in pool water, and sat in sandy beach bags. Still works like new. No leaks, no scratches on the lens. Battery holds up fine too. Honestly surprised how tough it is for the price. Great little investment for active kids.
It was as described and worked perfectly for my TV.

I've had this little player for about four months now, and it's held up great through daily use. My toddler has knocked it off the coffee table twice and it still runs like new. Picture stays crisp and the remote hasn't crapped out yet. Super handy for keeping in the kitchen while I cook. Definitely built to last.

I upgraded my desktop computer because the video card I was using was considerably outdated, and I couldn't even burn DVDs because it was so old. I wanted something inexpensive, but powerful enough to run most video applications. This card integrated perfectly with my computer, even though I only have a X-press 1.0 slot instead of a 2.0. I tested it on YouTube with an HD (1080) video running full resolution and full-screen the quality was flawless; the only lag and jitter was from my Internet connection. The GPU even works with BOINC and helps my computer crunch data. A great card for a wonderful price.
Didn't fit my 1991 chevrolet Blazer S10, even when it said it would.

I never actually got to use this. It seemed to be charging fine, but after the first day, it stopped charging and is now useless. Unfortunately the package got thrown out so now I cannot return it, but if I could, I would.

I've been using this Anker wireless mouse for a few weeks now, and the scroll wheel is what really stands out to me. It's got this quiet, precise feel that makes scrolling through long documents or web pages super smooth, no jarring clicks at all. I've had other mice where the wheel gets jumpy after a month, but this one's held up perfectly. Setup took maybe ten seconds, just plugged the nano receiver into an older USB 2.0 hub on my desk and it connected instantly without any driver nonsense. Battery life seems solid too, still running on the included AA. It's lightweight but doesn't feel cheap, and the tracking is accurate even on a wood surface. Great little workhorse for the price.

This system is fine if you are close to it, like the computer or video games, but for entertainment center use, it's just not as great as I was hoping. Sure, certain DVD's are louder, but some I've tried just are not as loud as I'd want with this system. I'd be better off hooking it up to my old component stereo system to get the loudness I want. I admit, the subwoofer puts out good bass, but the main speakers just don't have enough loudness.

I've had this mouse for about two years now, and it's a real mixed bag. The compact size was perfect for slipping into my laptop sleeve, and the build quality initially seemed decent for the price. What eventually wore me down was the erratic connection. Every few days it just drops, and I have to pop the battery out and back in to get it working again. The clicks also started feeling mushy after maybe eight months of regular use. On the plus side, the battery lasts a long time, and the scroll wheel is still smooth. It's an okay backup mouse for light tasks, but I can't rely on it for daily work anymore.

I own a video production business, and my clients have DVD players that range in the Piece of S*** category, to reliable brands like Sony, Pioneer and Denon. These Memorex discs play on them all! I have never had one fail to play due to a manufacturing defect. Not once. I have a stock DVD burner on my Systemax editing computer, and the Memorex discs play and record on it fine. And you can't beat that the price for a spindle of 100 is cheaper than WalMart.

Unacceptable touchpad. The touchpad is not responsive in many areas, especially around the edges. you do not know how frustrating it is to be click and nothing happens. there is no sound or visual indicator that a click was successful. also the keyboard is too small. sorry i love chromebooks and we desperately needed an ips 1080 display at a reasonable price point, but this touchpad, and to a lesser extent the small keyboard, ruin the experience Update (2015-04-10) I got used to the keyboard and the touchpad. It's was just frustrating downgrading in performance, but you do get used to it after a while. and the other benefits make up for it

Plugged into my old Vizio and a newer TCL set, worked first try when powered by the TV's USB port. Picture is clean, no lag. Only hiccup was a loose fit on one HDMI cable, so I had to wiggle it a bit. For the price, totally does the job.
Arrived in a bubble mailer with no dents or issues. Packaging was basic but effective, and they worked right out of the box.
For the cost, these hold up surprisingly well. Sound is decent, not premium, but my kids haven't managed to destroy them yet.
For the money, this bag's a steal. My Canon with kit lens fits easily, zippers feel solid, and the rain cover works. Padding's decent for daily bumps. Honestly surprised it's this cheap.
The tension knob is buttery smooth and locks my mirrorless setup tight instantly. No drooping even with a heavier zoom, and the plate clicks in securely.

Bought this case mostly for the clean aesthetic, and it definitely delivered on that front, looks even better in person than in the photos. I knew going in it didn't support optical drives, so no surprise there. Build quality feels solid for the price. My only gripe is that fitting everything behind the motherboard tray was a real puzzle, even with a semi-modular PSU. I managed, but expect to spend extra time tidying things up. If you're patient, it's worth it.

I'm looking for a cheap usable tripod. Polaroid a legit brand name, so that I gave it a try. However, this tripod is very difficult to use. Both turning from left to right and turning from up to down felt very sluggish. The dual bubble level was useless since I couldn't keep the position I wanted. I ordered a brand new one, but the one I got looked like a used one, maybe that's the reason. Anyway, I just review the product I received.

I picked this up to run a hardwired connection to my PS5, and the flat design was a lifesaver for tucking under the baseboards. Snaps into the router with a solid click, no wiggle at all. Speeds are steady 900+ Mbps down, and latency in online matches dropped noticeably compared to Wi-Fi. Zero packet loss after two weeks of heavy use.

I got these hoping to organize my DVD collection, but the lids are so oversized they just sit there loose. The plastic feels flimsy and the snaps pop open if you even look at them wrong. I'd only recommend these to someone who wants to store air and never touch the case again. Total waste of money.
Works great on my ps4! I would strongly recommend this! Working great still months later.

This Asus Zenpad 8.0 Screen protector has met all expected needs for it. I really like this screen protector, it keeps me really comforted and not have to worry about cracking the screen. It was really easy to put on, and no problems with it.

The item finish is extremely well done. The reflection works very good on both black and white items. So far, I really do not have any complaints. Thank you, I will definitely buy more from your store!! Mario
Did not work at all. No good sound came out of it ever! Only lound knocking noise

Perfect for tight spaces behind wall-mounted gear. If you've got a soundbar or streaming stick tucked close to the TV, this little cord keeps everything flush and tidy. Highly recommend it for minimalist setups.

Card showed up two days early in a no-nonsense box with solid foam padding, zero rattles, zero worries. Pulled it out and the low-profile bracket was already attached, which saved me a trip to the hardware bin. No fan, completely silent, just what I wanted for my audio workstation. Slotted it in, booted right up, and Windows grabbed the drivers without a hiccup. Desktop is crisp, DAW runs smooth, and the heatsink barely gets warm even after hours of tracking. For a basic no-fuss card that stays out of the way, this thing nails it. Very happy with the purchase.

Really like it; then it started to sound crackly at times like a loose connection. The sound would disappear, but I'd fiddle with the connection and it would come back. Now it's gone -- no light, no sound. Not very durable.

Arrived two days early in a sturdy little box with the SD card snug inside its plastic case, no rattling around. Popped it straight into my Pi 5 and it booted instantly, no formatting headaches. First impression is rock solid; I can already tell this thing's built to last. Super happy with the purchase.

The box showed up looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, corners crushed, seal already popped. Inside, the headphones were just rattling around loose, no foam or anything to keep them secure. Straight out of the box the right earbud had a weird sticky residue on it, like maybe the packaging melted during transit or something. Not exactly the fresh-out-of-the-box feel I was hoping for. The cable is absurdly long on one side, which just makes the whole thing feel unbalanced and awkward before you even plug them in. Honestly, after unboxing I'm already not excited to use them. First impressions count, and this was a letdown.
Arrived a day early in perfect, secure packaging. Unboxing felt premium right away. Build quality is solid and the connectors snap in with a satisfying click. Impressed so far.

I want to love it, but FM has too much static. An antenna connector would be nice. I cant read the markings on the buttons and they are dirty already showing on the orange rubber. The mini sound card slot does not work. These problems are just mine, they should not bother other people
It works perfect with my Droid and my Macbook Pro. Ummm... it's a cord what else there to review?;)

I really wanted to like this tablet for the price, but the battery life is an absolute joke. It barely lasts two hours on a full charge just watching YouTube, and that's with brightness turned way down. I thought maybe I got a defective unit, but my sister bought the same model and hers drains just as fast. It's basically useless unless you're sitting right next to an outlet. Total waste of money, I wish I'd saved up for something reliable instead.
Snaps right on, stays put, and I honestly forget it's there, no glare or blur in photos.

Nice, small size and professional appearance (for a backpack) if that is what you are looking for. I have to agree with other posters though, the zippers are poor quality. They stick and I have to straighten out the teeth to get them to work again. Don't pull hard, just back up, straighten the teeth and try again. Kind of a pain and I am concerned about longevity. Update 8/16/16 - Just continued problems with the cheap zippers. The backpack has held up well and the materials seem to be of very high quality. But these zippers stink. They don't work most of the time.

I purchased this product and there was no option in the tools menu to change the aspect ratio from 16:9 to 4:3. When I called the company, I was told--too bad, you must have an early model and there is no way you can fix it. When I inquired about the wisdom of designing a product that does not use an aspect ratio consistent with the image produced by cameras, I was told---it wasn't my decision. Even if I could change the aspect ratio (which leaves part of the screen as wasted), you cannot turn on and start the slide show while the device is in the frame. Consequently, I don't think it would get used very much. Between the bad product and bad attitude, this company deserves to go broke.

I picked up the YN560-TX to pair with my YN-560 IV flashes and it's been flawless. The big, clear screen makes adjusting power levels super fast, even in dim light. I love being able to tweak all my off-camera groups without leaving my spot. Range has been rock solid across a full wedding reception hall.

Bought this for my sister's laptop. She lives in Florida, lots of afternoon lightning storms, and while I was visiting, I was using her computer to work in the afternoons. It's a nice, compact UPS, lots of outlets, smaller size so that it fits easily in a smaller combined bedroom/office, and no humming. That's been an issue with some UPS units I've owned, so it was a welcome discovery.
Initially ordered bulb to have an extra on hand. Just recently installed new bulb. So far so good.
Very Good bluetooth headphones. I listen to all types of music on these and must say these are one of those best sounding headphones.
this sits by my bed side so haven't carried it around yet. I do like the ability to plug it in, but the radio is not as good as the one with my clock, so use this mainly with books on tape on cd's
Arrived fast in perfect condition, feels super solid right out of the box.
It broke the first time I got it, then it broke again never buy this product. It is so expensive for a piece of garbage.
Got these for daily use and honestly they deliver decent sound for the price, just wish the cord was a bit sturdier.

Bought this to replace the Canon OEM cap when I lose it...it's just a matter of when. Pinch mechanism works well and fits just as well as original. Bought this for a Canon 18-135mm STM lens and would certainly recommend.

Works Great, Got this for my kids room, since the TV is mounted on the wall it makes it easier for them to change from XBOX to PS3 and to the fire Stick. I purchased this for free or a discounted price to use, and to give my unbiased opinion
Works great and the build feels solid. It took a few minutes to set up, but once it was in, it ran without a hitch.

I love these speakers. They work well, the price is great and im actually ordering a second set. I use these with my iPhone, iPod and computer. The cords are long so I can put them whereever I need to and the sound quality is wonderful.

Arrived a day early and the box was pristine, no scuffs at all, which I always appreciate. Pulling it out, the fabric felt premium right away and the color looked even better in person. Everything was wrapped neatly, zippers glided smooth, and the interior padding gave me instant confidence for my laptop. You can tell it's built to last. First trip to the office got two compliments before lunch. Super happy with this purchase.

I wanted to measure the amount of light actually landing on a work surface vs. that at the source. I could see that there was a difference in the illumination under the 75 watt halogen flood lights in my kitchen vs. the any of the LED replacement lights I tried, but I couldn't quantify it until now. Using this meter I measured the LUX at the counter level and saw that reading under the supposedly equivalent LED was as much as 400 LUX less than the halogen. Now I have a yardstick by which to measure LED lighting performance and report in same in future reviews of LED lights I experiment with here at Amazon.
This mount is rock-solid, perfect for any speed-demon commuter.

I grabbed these Kappa 6x9s on sale and honestly wasn't expecting much for the money. The clarity and tight bass blew me away compared to stock. They handle my amp's power without distorting, and at this price point, they feel like a total steal for the sound quality.
Got exactly what I paid for, works great and feels solid for the cost.
Quality build..as the newer sigma lenses are known for.snappy focusing..nice walkabout lens.

Had these in my truck for about six months now, through heat and rough trails. The jacket's held up great, no cracking or shorting. One connector felt a little loose during install, but no issues since. Solid for the price.

(Written by Joseph's wife, Shiloh) I bought the Jabra Cruiser 2 speakerphone for my 2007 Ford Freestyle since the cd player has no jack for an auxiliary cable, nor is it Bluetooth capable. I read many reviews of different in-car speakerphones, and decided to go with this one due to the good reviews and to the price. I have to say that the set up is ridiculously easy, or at least, it was easy for me with my iPhone 4S. My phone paired with the Cruiser immediately and it worked well. I turned on the FM transmitter, and it played songs from my phone onto 88.1FM quickly and the sound was great. Then, I called a friend who confirmed that I was coming in loud and clearly through the speakerphone (though I was not driving at the time). However, on the FM frequency, I had to turn up the sound significantly to hear her well. Pros: -Easy set up. -Loud sound on the FM frequency for music from my phone. -Comes with a in-car charger. -Callers could hear my voice loud and clear (at least so far). -The Cruiser announces every function you do, so that is nice, especially when it lets you know that you are paired with your phone, what radio frequency it is using, etc. Cons: -The charger wire would be annoying if used while driving due to the fact that it hangs right next to my gear shifter. -On the FM frequency, I had to turn up the volume significantly to hear the person on the other end of the line. -Does not turn off automatically. You have to flip the little switch on the side of the box in order to turn it off and on. Overall, I am pretty satisfied with this product. I wasn't expecting it to be flawless, and it has been satisfactory thus far (though I have not had it long). I will update this in a few months when I have used it more!

Some obvious quality control issues. One battery good and one battery bad. However the customer service his first class. Tell you the truth I've never seen such good customer service from anyone. So it's quite bizarre to have someone email you individually and fix the problems. While the batteries get 4 stars the quality service is somewhere on the 10 range. They will resolve any issues.

This is a good watch, but to really use it, you need to connect it to both your smartphone (via the Garmin Connect application) and a heart rate monitor (which it is often sold with as a package). The Garmin Connect app is buggy and has been since CNET wrote it's review in 2015. You'd think they fix it, but they haven't. I've had to uninstall and reinstall the application on my phone twice this week and power off and power on the the phone once this week because the "transfer failed" message appears on the Garmin Vivoactive at the conclusion of the transfer. In addition, there was another time where my steps would update on the application, but when I closed the application and reopened it they were lost repeatedly. As far as the heart strap is concerned, you'd think you would be able to see the stats on the phone after it connects. No such luck. This thing is awesome! I've used the soundfreaq soundkick for work for a couple of years and occasionally I bust it out at home but it's better suited for beating out ambient noise (for audibility's sake) than it is for casual home listening. I wanted more, and I got it. The fluance isn't really a good buy for portability, in my opinion. This is for a technology affluent sort who wants good sound they can tie to their smart phone. The unit is surprisingly heavy, which ends up being a good thing. Bass is substantial without being muddy, which I deeply appreciated after too much time spent with bose and Sony units at friends houses. It's sound has a difficult to place warmth that I imagine comes from the heavy wood enclosure. Highs are clear without being tinny, and mids are presented well. I personally believe that if mid range presentation is something you are deeply concerned with, single unit Bluetooth speakers will consistently disappoint you. Get something more akin to a stereo. With all of that said, I strongly suspect you will enjoy the sound of this unit. I've thrown everything at this thing, and it consistently sounds amazing. I've played techno, heavy metal, jazz, bossa nova, acoustic players like Andy McKee, and more others than I care to mention and it adds listening value to all of them. I don't want to understate this. Koop, the dillinger escape plan, aphex twin, jars of clay and Eric clapton all sounded awesome. That's a tonal diversity I'm not accustomed to with anything other than my sennheiser headphones that cost considerably more. I would shop around a little more if portability is a major selling point for you, but on all other points I urge you to get this unit. It is a delightful tonal surprise at this price and I'm thrilled with my purchase.

As a last resort I thought, why not disable the on board wireless and try a wireless card? My laptop does not have a PCMCIA card slot, so I was limited to wireless express. I wanted to revitalize my early 2011 MacBook Pro. The drive itself works mostly as expected (about 12% slower than its specs). But the installation was arduous and can be expensive. First, the drive is formatted for Windows and all the software provided is Windows only. So you need to use Disk Manager to erase the disk, rename it to something meaningful, and reformat it to Mac OS Extended Journaled. Then you need a piece of software like Carbon Copy Cloner to mirror your existing hard disk onto the new SSD. CCC is a great software package, which you should probably own anyway for various backup tasks, but if you don't already have it, buying it adds to the expense of the upgrade. The provided installation guide is a pamphlet of 8 pictures for installing on a desktop, 6 pictures for installing on a laptop, and 1 frame of cautions. All very generic and useless for my MacBook Pro. By the way, both sets of pictures show a SATA-to-USB adapter cable for cloning, which was not included and must be purchased separately. The installation requires a 7mm to 9.5mm space adapter for 2.5 drives. Again this is an additional purchase as the Samsung SSD is more shallow than the original drive. The actual physical installation involves removing the cover, removing the drive clamp and old drive, removing the 4 stud screws on the old drive and putting them onto the SSD, applying the spacer (be sure to orient the drive to the cable connector before adhering it), seating the drive (it still seemed a little loose), re-installing the drive clamp and replacing the cover. The studs are required on the new drive to make sure it seats into the clamp. Why Samsung couldn't spend an extra $.0005 and include these on the SSD is unknown. So, overall 4 stars for the drive, 0 stars for the lack of Mac installation support and the required accessory expenses, averaging out to 2 stars for this review.

I bought the 8CH 4 camera system for our office. Our office is all on one level and the width of the office is about 150 feet. The main NVR unit if located about 80 feet from the farthest camera with only a partial wall between the unit and farthest camera. Out of the box the system is easy to use. The screen you use to specify the time the cameras should record motion is cumbersome because it is a grid of rectangle 24 (hours) x 7 days and if you make a mistake you cannot uncheck a box and thus need to start over. The android app for viewing the cameras works well and so does the desktop app. If you need more than 4 cameras, all you need to do is buy the additional cameras, up to 4 more for a total of 8. Pairing the camera with the system is easy. If you need the signal to go through cinder-block walls you may want to consider another system. I had one camera in our warehouse at about 200 feet away with a cinder block separating the warehouse from the front office NVR. I got intermittent results in terms of the signal reaching the NVR. Other reviews I read stated you can replace the antenna's on the cameras to boost the range and signal strength, but the antenna's on my camera do not twist off nor could I pull them off. These cameras detect motion and it may be helpful to realize that "motion" is anything that causes pixels to change. We were getting false motion alerts because sunlight bouncing off of outside nearby moving traffic was reflecting on a wall and the camera nearby was picking this up. In terms of "night vision" the cameras work best if there is a little bit of light. If I needed to buy a system for my house I would consider this system.

I grabbed this mouse expecting maybe six months out of it for the price, but it's been rock-solid for over two years. It's light, responsive, and the scroll wheel still clicks like day one. For ten bucks I figured I'd get something flimsy, instead it outlasted my last "premium" mouse easily. Zero complaints.
Fits my tablet pretty snug and the stand positions are handy, though it's slightly heavier than I expected. Feels protective, but the material picks up lint faster than I'd like.

I've gone through a couple pairs now and the soft headband is really comfortable for side sleeping. That part I genuinely like. But the charging port cover started peeling off after just a few weeks, and the battery life seems to drop noticeably after a month of nightly use. For the price I expected a little more durability.
Pleased with how snappy and reliable this thing runs my streaming setup every day.
great light, super bright and its color rating is damn near perfect.

This cable works great and I didn't have to take out a small loan to pay for overkill packaging and marketing hype. It transfers all the signals for all the speakers from my DVD player to my 5.1 receiver with no problems. I have yet to hear the much more expensive brand cables deliver that much more performance to justify their expense. If you are not using HDMI then get these cables for your system.
Wasn't expecting such clear overhead effects from speakers this compact, really fills the room without being overpowering.
Perfect for my home office setup, crisp display and super easy to connect. I'd recommend it to any remote worker.

5 stars for customer service! Like many other reviewers on this product, the battery from Anker lasts about 7-8 months before the battery starts to swell up and making the mouse track un-clickable and the battery drains out in under 35 minutes. I contacted AnkerDirect recently through their seller page and told them about my battery issue. They asked for pics of the the battery and sent me a return label to mail in the old battery for a replacement covered under the warranty. Easy Peasy. If only all companies were this awesome. I would deal with AnkerDirect again in a heartbeat. Now for the product itself...4 stars. I have a mid 2010 macbook, and its basically been hanging onto its electronic life by a thread. When I had to replace the original battery that came with my macbook, I didn't want to invest in a Apple battery because I wanted to only squeeze out another 18 months with my mac before upgrading to a new laptop. So this was the way to go for me, especially with the warranty. After installing, it worked great and held a charge for a good 2-3.5 hours depending on how many apps/windows were open until around the 7 month of use. If purchasing this battery keep in mind that there is a chance that you will have to replace it within a year through the warranty. If it's not a big deal to you, don't hesitate and get this battery.

My case arrived a full day earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. It was packed in a sturdy box with foam inserts keeping everything snug, no scuffs or dents at all.
