Pulling it out, the material immediately felt premium, with tight stitching and a smooth but grippy finish. The zippers glide effortlessly and don't snag. I was honestly impressed by how lightweight yet protective the whole thing feels right out of the package. Popped my Fuji XE4 with the 27mm pancake in and the fit is just spot on, snug around the body with zero wiggle, and there's still a little room for a spare battery. Great first impression.
It's okay for $30, but the band started peeling within a month and the battery barely lasts through a single workout. I guess you get what you pay for, fine for casual use but not reliable.
This speaker will fill a good sized room and the bass is great. I use it for teaching and the students can always hear the music.

I purchased this television in September 2012, it is now April 13, 2013 and I'm still impressed with the picture. Watching local television station without cable and the picture is crisp. Play Station Games are awesome full of high definition and color, Blue rays come out nice. What I love the most is that My new laptop has HDMI and so does this television and watching whatever I play on my computer comes out Beautiful on this television. I took it off the stand and hung it up on the wall and no matter where I have it facing I get no glare from daylight.
Stayed put for weeks now, no peeling at all, still crystal clear.

This card crushes 1440p gaming if you're coming from something older. I'm hitting smooth frames in Cyberpunk and Forza Horizon 5 without touching any sliders. Thermals stay surprisingly chill, too. Honestly, it's perfect for someone building their first high-refresh rig and wanting max settings without tinkering. I'd tell any mid-range upgrader to just grab this.
Very easy install and seems to work well with Dragon Naturally Speaking.
Works fine, bit very slow loading up and reading cd's.

Picked this up thinking it would be a quick setup for my home office. What a mistake. The instructions are a mess and the software installer kept hanging halfway through. After an hour of restarting my computer and trying different USB ports, I finally got someone on the phone. They ran me through the same steps I already tried, then casually mentioned my unit was considered out of support because of some internal manufacturing date policy, even though I literally just opened the box. I've never heard anything so ridiculous. The connection drops constantly and the range is pathetic compared to my old device. Complete waste of money and an afternoon. Do yourself a favor and look elsewhere.

I've had this on my phone for about three months now and it's held up really well. Dropped it a couple times on concrete and not a scratch on the case or my phone. The thin profile still feels solid, edges haven't warped at all. Only knock is it's a bit slick at first, but once it breaks in grip improves.

I bought this based on reviews, composition and price. I needed a protective case for my portable HDs and in researching, decided to try this model. I am not comfortable with soft cases and felt that some form of hard case was necessary for protection. The case measures externally approximately 7" x 4-1/4" x 1-3/4". The interior storage cavity measures approximately 6" x 3-1/2" x 1-1/2". The outer covering is a tight weave synthetic fabric laid over a stiff plastic shell. All observable stitching is single-stitch. From what I can feel, the plastic does have some give but is reasonably stiff. The interior has soft, fabric covered foam padding covering only one flat side and not the edges. It appears to be approximately 1/4" or less thick. There is a 1" wide elastic band for HD retention. The other side of the interior (which has no foam padding) has a mesh pocket approximately 3" deep by 3-1/2" wide. The exterior of the case has a fabric pocket measuring approximately 3" x 6-1/2". There are no straps or velcro. The material appears to be some form of thin neoprene. I noticed in the reviews that some mentioned that the zipper can be faulty. In my particular case there appears to be no problem and zipping the case open or shut presented no discernable difficulties. Long-term durability remains to be seen.. The zipper pull has a oblong hard rubber tab attached to it. This case fits my portable HDs with room to spare. The elastic band holds the devices securely, although how long it will retain adequate elasticity remains to be seen. I use the exterior storage pocket for the HD cables as I am not comfortable having the cable with the USB connections rubbing against the HD on the other side of the case interior. I feel that this case is adequate for casual use but I would like to see if there are cases with foam padding all around.

Product was instantly recognized by the latest windows 10 build. Connects to the 5ghz channel on my router flawlessly and keeps it's connection. The only driver i had to install was for the bluetooth feature which was painless and quick. Also to note it's connected via AC wireless protocol which is great!

I just moved to a new city and was constantly getting lost so I wanted to buy an easy-to-use GPS unit. I've never owned a GPS before so I can't compare this product to others, but I was able to make good use of this unit within minutes of taking it out of the box. The touch display and menu navigation are quite user friendly. It took me a while to figure out the best settings, though. One thing to watch for is whether you are set for it to give the shortest route or the fastest route. The shortest route will often take you on some pretty bizarre journeys, while fastest route will generally get you onto the major arteries and highways that you'll most likely want to use and remember. Another thing it took me a while to realize is that you can change the default screen settings to tell you the direction of travel you are going in. I find that more useful than displaying the time to my destination, which in my experience the device consistently underestimates. Also not sure why they bothered to have the device tell you your speed, since cars already have speedometers. Still, a very handy device that I now find almost indispensable.
Perfect for casual collectors who just want peace of mind before hitting play on an older disc.
Wasn't sure it'd fit my everyday carry, but it's way more practical than I thought and looks great.
Order arrived two days early in a sturdy little box, no damage at all. Took it out, plugged it in, and my phone jumped to 70% in what felt like 20 minutes. Honestly impressed right out of the gate.
Bought as a gift and the sound quality seriously impressed both of us right away.

Swapped my old bulky laptop for this ThinkPad and couldn't be happier. The 15.6" screen is crisp, build feels sturdy, and it handles daily tasks without a hiccup. For the price, it's an absolute steal, premium quality without the premium cost.

Improved design and ease of use compared to predecessor product GSC-10. I would give 5 stars were it not for the rubber band attachments. How well will they survive 500-600 mile months in all kinds of weather? I will probably inspect these frequently and look for replacement alternatives. Sync and programming were easy with my Edge 500. But.... rubber bands for a constantly moving part???????

I'll be honest, I didn't expect much for the price. Figured it'd feel flimsy or look cheap in person. But it fits my Fire like a glove and the texture actually makes it easier to grip without slipping. The wake/sleep function works instantly every time. For everyday protection that doesn't add bulk, it totally overdelivers.

They seemed to be of decent quality until I opened the head piece a little too far and the plastic split. I've had them for 1.5 years but I planned on using them a lot longer. I only use them at the office so I expected them to last longer than my apple buds that I've had for 3 years. I wish people would make products that last a reasonable amount of time. It makes me apprehensive to order products from companies I don't know.
Using this as camera bag and purse. A bit bulky, but it protects my camera well and is very stylish!
This did not work on the tablet that I have. its not universal. So saying it works on all devices thats not so.

I am really pleased with the whisks and glad I ordered them. As for their appearance I like there warm cheery colors and the handles are comfortable in the hand. They perform well while using them and cleaning them is a breeze. They will not harm the coating on your skillets.
I haven't had the need to switch back and forth between my Tivo and Roku yet so I don't know how that will go but the unit seems to work well.

I bought the original Devastator in blue and found it to be pretty good but hard to see the keys. When I saw a review for this on hardwarecanucks.com I knew I found an answer to my problem. I found some replacement keys from Roswell and decided to experiment. The keyboard now looks fantastic and is easier to read/use now that the keys use white paint. The mouse is exactly like the previous incarnation which means it's pretty good. 3 settings, 5 buttons and some cool headlight-esc LEDs at the front as well as some on the palm rest area.

Got these to game on my PS4 and they've been rock solid since day one. Super light on the ears even during long sessions, and the sound is crisp for the price. Mic picks up my voice clearly with zero static, and the inline mute is handy. Honestly didn't expect this level of comfort and performance for what I paid. Solid buy.
The reinforced strain relief on this Belkin cable is a game changer. No more fraying near the connector after months of daily plugging and unplugging.

The radio offers pretty solid features. I just had it installed and pretty happy for the most part. The Bluetooth is excellent, I've had to play with the EQ because it won't sound right out the box, but once you adjust the audio settings, it's a beast. I also turned up the Bluetooth audio level and now Google Music sounds pretty awesome. I like how I an have music and Google Maps running and it lowers the music for directions. USB - The USB gave me an "Error 19" but noticed my Nexus 5 goes into charging mode when I plug it into the stereo. All you have to do is drop the notification bar and change it from "Charging" to "MTP". I don't carry music so I have no need for it. I actually wanted the USB for charging, but that's very disappointing. It charges at 80mAh - 110mAh which is poor and means my phone loses battery while plugged in and using Bluetooth. I drove for 15 mins and my battery went down like 8%. Most of my charges never drop below 500mAh, most give me over 1000mAh. Aux - My Sirius Starmate 2 is using the auxiliary input. It sounds fantastic! I have ditched the FM transmitter and Howard Stern and the music channels come in very clear. I don't know why I waited so long to upgrade my stock radio. This stereo is connected to my factory Rockford Fosgate and it sounds better than my Nissan RF dash unit. All in all, this radio, parts and install cost me a solid $210 or so. A bit more than I was expecting but worth the price. Rating - I gave it a 4 only because the sufficient lack of USB charge. It's pretty useless for charging for me.

I've been using this drive daily for about four months now, and it's held up surprisingly well. It's survived a couple accidental drops from my desk and still runs quietly without any hiccups. Transfer speeds stay consistent even after long sessions moving large video files. No overheating issues either, which I was a bit worried about at first. Honestly, for the price, the build quality feels solid and I'm pretty confident it'll last a good while longer.

UPDATED REVIEW (2 YEARS LATER): Only recommend this product if you're going to lay it flat on the floor. If you're trying to stick to a wall, forget about it. It WILL not stay. Very solid and well-made product. Took some getting used to stuffing speaker cable inside. I used the entire length and didn't need to cut into. Worked out very nicely after all was said and done. The double-side tape took a minute to get the backing off, but once I did, it was SUPER STRONG. Hopefully, it stays stuck to my tile flooring and I never have to worry about unsightly cables again. Much thicker than I had anticipated.

Bought this to supplement a Baby camera (DCS-825L) that no longer works in the dark, unfortunately the IR must be a different frequency because you can't even see it. I gave this 5 stars because I tested it on another camera I have and it lit up the area very well. I kept the product to future use.

I've been using these earbuds daily for about two months now, mostly during workouts and the occasional rainy run. Honestly, I was half-expecting them to give up after a few weeks, but they've held up surprisingly well. I accidentally dropped one on concrete twice and it barely has a scuff. The charging case has survived being tossed into a gym bag with keys and loose change, still snaps shut perfectly. Sound quality hasn't degraded at all either. They've been sweated on, caught in drizzle, and still connect instantly every time. For something this lightweight, the build is solid. I'm genuinely impressed they've lasted this long without any issues. Great everyday pair.
Don't let the name or the feel of it fool you. This thing is so simple and just works.
Surprised by how clean and fast this refurbished drive runs, feels brand new. No hiccups during setup and it's been whisper quiet. Honestly way better than I expected for the price.

I've had these curtains up for about three months now and they've held up way better than I expected. The fabric still looks fresh, no fraying or loose threads even with the cat occasionally batting at the bottom. I toss them in a mesh bag and wash on gentle, then line dry, zero issues. The color stayed rich and didn't fade. Honestly, for how airy they feel, they're surprisingly sturdy. Really happy with how they've lasted.
worst usb hub-- installed fake drive on the system and disabled other ports ----STAY AWAY--TOOK OUR TECH 2 HRS TO CLEAR UP THE SYSTEM

Great! I can actually hear the individual instruments on what I am listening to. I love getting really hammered on the weekends and watching Youtube and these Etymotic earphones make my buzz more enjoyable
It works fine and was a great replacement when I frayed the cord of the original charger. I bought three of them to put in various classrooms.
easy to set up, works great, picked up about 30 channels with a mountain between house and hill where transmitters are at,what else can i say

After a great deal of evaluation, we narrowed my daughter's belated Christmas/Birthday present down to two Sony cameras, the RX10 and this one. My wife and I have Canons and Nikons and others, so we were somewhat surprised to learn that Sony rules the roost in a couple of camera segments. Our daughter likes it and her husband was so impressed that he got the RX10.
Honestly, I didn't expect much for the price, but it charges both my phone and earbuds fast without any hiccups.

I've been using this lens for a couple of months mostly for weekend hikes and backyard bird watching. It's not a pro-level piece of glass, but for the price it's surprisingly sharp in good light. I pair it with a D5600 and the autofocus keeps up well enough for squirrels and perched songbirds. It does hunt a bit when the light drops, so keep that in mind. The plastic build keeps it light, which I appreciate on longer walks. I'd recommend it to casual wildlife watchers or anyone just getting into nature photography who wants more reach without breaking the bank. If you need low-light performance or tank-like durability, you'll want to spend more, but for sunny day adventures it's a solid companion.
Got these for my wife. Sound is much better than the little built-in speakers on her laptop.

I was skeptical about how well this little thing could handle our household of streamers and gamers, but it's been rock solid. We've got three consoles running online matches at once and I can still stream 4K in the living room without a hiccup. The QoS settings were super easy to tweak so nobody gets lag-spiked out of a game. Setup took maybe ten minutes with the app. I also appreciate that it doesn't look like a sci-fi spider on my desk, the design is pretty low-key and clean.

I do like it. It works well. The complaints I have about it are relatively minor: 1) It does use quite a bit of space. To keep the arms from extending beyond the back of my desk (which was important in my particular setting), I had to mount it (drill the hole) almost 12" in from the back edge of the desk. That places the monitors about 15" in from the back edge of the desk. 2) Cord management is minimal. The upper of the arms, the cords are simply "tied under". The lower of the arms, the cords are held inside by the most complex piece of molded plastic known to man, which must be carefully inserted by a Plastics-Certified Brain Surgeon. I am surprised that they didn't think to allow the cord management to share a hole with the support itself, which would have been handy. 3) It says "amazon basics" on a prominent logo right in the middle of the base. I don't need more logos on my stuff, unless you're going to pay me handsomely to display them. (This will work to cover the logo: https://smile.amazon.com/Scotch-Duct-Black-1-88-Inch-60-Yard/dp/B0013AX6FC/) With those complaints, you'd think I'm panning it, but this is a very good monitor arm system, and I'm pretty happy with it. Well made, it seems.
Well made and inexpensive system worked perfect for our application.
For what you pay, this little gadget is a steal, snapped right onto my D3200 and feels way sturdier than I expected.

I really wanted to like this mount for a small camera under the porch ceiling, but the tilt mechanism just gave up after a few months. The plastic tightening knob strips way too easily, so now the camera slowly droops no matter how gently I try to secure it. It's basically useless unless you only ever point it straight down. The metal bracket itself feels sturdy enough, but the pivot joint design is just bad, constant retightening and it still slips a little more each week. Ended up having to wedge a shim in there to keep it in place, which looks terrible. For the price, I expected it to hold a lightweight camera without this much hassle. Disappointing.
Great Binoculars....small enough to carry in a purse and great viewing.

I was using a mechanical keyboard that made me have to type at an angle, and the keys had some resistance going down. I didn't care until I noticed that I was having some hand pain. Fearing it might be carpal tunnel, I ended up doing a bunch of hand stretches, and also replaced by keyboard with the amazon basics keyboard I was using before. I think the stretches helped fix damage that was done, and using the keyboard prevented any further damage from building up. My room mate was also developing hand problems, and I brought an amazon basics keyboard for him and told him to use it to help with his hand problems. He said his hand problems are gone too. So, I'm not going to claim that this keyboard fixes hand problems because it could have in fact been a coincidence as me and my room mate were both trying other things to fix our hand issues. But shortly after we switched to the amazon keyboards, our issues were gone, so it *could* be the case that this keyboard helps prevent hand issues for some people.
The ear cups feel cheap and start hurting after an hour, but the noise cancellation is surprisingly solid for the price.

Switched from the New Balance Runner. What a difference. It takes me 10-20 seconds to sync most of the time (that was my problem before). Charts look great on Garmin Connect. The screen size is just fine and the watch is pretty small and thin which is great for those runners that don't like weights on their wrists for runs. I highly recommend.

UPDATE: Amazon customer support did an exchange of the defective product free of charge. We had great luck with this keyboard since we first started buying them in 2014. As we've expanded our office, we've added this keyboard from Kensington to each work station. However one of them has sticky keys and we didn't not set up the new office until a few days after the return process closed. Space bar double spaces with each click. Interested to see how customer service handles this.

Decent lock for the price. The 6-foot cable is a bit tighter than I'd pictured, especially looping it around my standing desk's thicker leg. Works perfectly with the Kensington slot and feels sturdy enough for a coffee shop. Just measure your setup first, or you might find yourself repositioning things.
For the money, this little adapter saved me from tossing two perfectly good old drives.

Very nice for the money but this cannot hold tight when tilting. It's like you can't tighten it enough with that knob. I had I manfrotto that is pretty much its competitor and paid 3 times as much. If you have a light set up its probably okay. For my set up it does work and the price was great but my manfrotto works 100% with no worries so you still pay for what you get. Benro is value manfrotto is quality. You have to pick which is more important for you
Can't beat the price. We were building a new home and wanted these buried in the wall so we needed a lot of them and at this price it did not kill us to do it.

I was nervous about manual focus, but the smooth, damped ring on this Rokinon makes nailing sharp shots surprisingly easy. Street photography feels so immersive now, and the compact build balances perfectly on my A6000.

Easy to install. Seems like excellent protection for my 8 year old son. comes with a black plastic insert that you must remove to install an ipad. I think the purpose of the black plastic insert is to hold the shape until you install the ipad.

I grabbed this mainly as a secondary device for reading and light browsing before bed, and it fits that role perfectly. The screen is bright enough and text looks clear, which was my biggest concern at this price. It handles e-books and comics without stuttering, and streaming YouTube or casual games works fine as long as you don't push it with heavy multitasking. I'd definitely recommend it for kids, seniors, or anyone who just wants a no-fuss tablet for basic media and web stuff. Battery life is decent, usually lasting me a couple days of sporadic use. Don't expect flagship speed, but for the money it's a solid little workhorse that does exactly what I needed.
We hoped to use these both inside for presentations, as well as outside for backyard family movies. The slightest wind will knock it off the table, and break the feet if they're clamped.
Ordered this for a ski trip and it was completely useless. Wouldn't clamp onto my poles at all. Waste of money and ruined my day on the slopes.

I've used OtterBox before for my phones and have had nothing but great things to say about them so I was interested to see what they do with an iPad case. Unfortunately, my experience was not the best. Don't get me wrong, the case is fantastic at what it does: protect your iPad. It's not just a good fit for how I use my iPad. The majority of the time I use it, I'm holding it. I tried every way I could think of but the floppy folding cover was just not comfortable in my hands. A lot of the apps I use require it to be in landscape mode and the edge of the case by the home button moves away too easily when holding it this way. Probably my biggest issue is the clasp. Maybe it's the kind of thing that will loosen up a bit with use but it is difficult to open. It's not a huge inconvenience to have to futz with it a few seconds but the fact that I have to means there is a problem. Overall, this is a decent iPad case. It's not for me, but a lot of people have given it great reviews so maybe it'll be good for you.
A great band that looks better in real life than it does in the photos. Really upgrades the appearance of the Blaze. Highly recommended.

Arrived a day early in a neat little box, no tangles or kinks. Cable feels surprisingly flexible and light, perfect for tidying up behind my desk. Plugged it in and connection was instant and stable. Honestly impressed with the clean packaging and how quickly I was up and running.

Box arrived completely crushed, like someone sat on it. Inside, the earbuds were just rattling around loose, no foam, no padding, nothing. The plastic shell already had scuffs and the ear loops looked slightly bent out of the package. First impression was cheap and returned, honestly. Tried them on and the left bud crackles right out of the gate. Between the garbage shipping protection and immediate audio issues, this feels like a total waste. Super disappointing unboxing experience.

This is the camera that DxO Labs gave the following rating for: "No need for suspense: this new 16.3 MP sensor is simply the best APS-C we have tested so far, sometimes able to compete even with very high-end full-frame cameras. The overall score of the K5 puts it in the lead with 82 points -- more than 9 points better than the D90 or the Alpha 55, and 16 points ahead of the Canon 7D or 60D. The K5 is literally the best APS-C performer for each segment, even in low ISO". See their website at [...] I am no professional. I had used a basic DSLR prior to this the k2000. So this review is based on someone upgrading from a beginner SLR to a more sophisticated one. Pros: Low light: This is a camera that takes great low light pictures, you can push the ISO up pretty high and still get great pictures. This was the biggest reason for me to upgrade. I had a hard time taking pictures of my kids in various performances with terrible lighting conditions. Obviously one needs a good lens. But with my old camera I had to throw too many away. This camera combined with the DA 50-135mm lens is one terrific package for such situations. Weight/Size: this is the most compact camera at such a level that you will find. Weather resistant: This is a weather resistant camera! Combine this with a weather resistant lens and you are all set. Electronic Level: I thought this was a gimmick but after using it for a few days, I find it indispensable to take level photos. User Selectable Focus points: these are standard in expensive cameras, nothing special if you have used such cameras before but for somone upgrading I quickly found out how convenient this was. Shake reduction: Pentax unlike Nikon/Canon has shake reduction built into the camera, so any lens you put on it has shake reduction function. Lenses: Pentax has small pancake lenses called limited lenses. Try these if you are new to Pentax. They are superb and great to walk around with without looking like a dork. Cons: Expensive...

I've been running this SMC router for about three years now, and it's been rock-solid for basic coverage in my two-story house. Setup was quick, and I haven't had to reboot it constantly like cheaper units. Range is decent, though it struggles a little in my garage. I'd definitely recommend it for anyone with a medium-sized home or a straightforward network setup.

It's really nice if you use apple music exclusively. I have a 2014 BMW and an iPhone, and I just don't feel like the software is cracked up for this. When you get in the car, it auto plays your music, but ONLY apple music. So I'll have Pandora going, get to the grocery store, come back to my car, and Apple music starts playing. Then I hit pandora back on, and Apple music will start playing. Its like an auto feature that just won't quit. After the 1st or 2nd time it takes though. It's kinda annoying, but it does connect very well wirelessly. If apple would let you set a default music app, I think this would be better!
The device works as advertised. I like being able to turn each receptacle on and off.
I've been using these daily for two months now, and they've held up perfectly through drops and workouts. The build feels solid, and the sound still impresses me every time.
Worked fine for a couple months, then the hinge started cracking. Pretty flimsy.

I've had these Denons for about eight months now, using them most days on my commute and at the gym. The cord has held up way better than I expected, no fraying near the jack or splitting at the earbud housings, even with getting yanked a few times. I've accidentally dropped them onto concrete twice, and aside from a tiny scuff on one end cap, they still look and work like new. The strain reliefs are solid, and the included tips haven't torn or lost their shape. Sound stays clear and balanced, no crackling. I'm knocking off one star just because the inline mic placement feels a bit low when I'm walking, but durability-wise, these really surprised me.

works perfectly. the range and sensitivity is what I expect. this product must continuously improve in quality and price. the only concern i have is the screw they use is so soft and easily dugged out by strong screw driver. overall this is highly recommendable.
Got this case for my own phone after my last one yellowed in a month. This one's holding up great, feels sturdy, and cost way less than the mall kiosk stuff.
These cloths make my screen look brand new every time. No lint left behind and they wash up nicely too. Totally worth grabbing a pack.

I have the dual harness, and neck straps which I also love. I love this wrist strap for when I'm on the go (shooting live music mostly). It's nice for times when the harness is too much, but gives me the piece of mind of not dropping my camera!
I've been using this trackball daily for over six months and it still feels brand new. No sticky spots or lag at all. The build quality really holds up. Solid little workhorse for the price.
I'd tell any student or remote worker to grab this, super silent, zero desk clutter, and setup took barely two minutes.
Barely an upgrade. Apps lag constantly and the screen freezes mid-scroll. Battery life is disappointing too. Honestly feels like I just bought the same outdated tablet again.

This review is for the GoPole Bobber. I purchased the GoPole Bobber early this year to keep my Hero3+ from sinking while exploring the Caribbean Sea. This thing is great! The size is perfect and the color makes it easy to see if it gets away from you in the water. You don't have to worry about losing your GoPro if you have the bobber on it, I promise:)

I had a windows test server that was crashing every day after I upgraded it from 8gb of memory to 12gb. I spent over a week online on various IT support forums trying to resolve it. One day, after running a memory test for 8 hours, the server refused to boot. After letting it sit for 8 hours, it booted just fine. I immediately suspected a heat issue. I purchased these coolers because they were active air movers versus just passive metal coolers. (and the price was right) They've worked perfectly. My server has not crashed once since I installed them!! It runs much faster, and I've not noticed an increase in noise. Prior to installing them, the chips were extremely hot to the touch after a memory test. Today with the Air Coolers, they barely luke warm. They are also extremely easy to install. Once you attach the mounting brackets, they just snap on. I HIGHLY recommend these Airflow Memory coolers.

Picked this up for my router setup and the gold-plated connectors really make a solid difference in signal stability. No drops or weird lag spikes since I swapped it in. Exactly the length I needed, fits snug and looks clean.
Got this for my wife's tablet and she loves how the keys feel. Super sturdy and makes typing so much easier for her.

I actually got these to use just as a pocket magnifying glass. I'm sure they're decent when attached to a camera, but they are great for viewing tiny stuff and for getting campfires started without wasting resources.
Had this case for a few months now and it's holding up great, no peeling or loose stitching even with daily use.

I've been using this WD network drive since early 2012, and overall it's been a solid workhorse for streaming media and backing up household files. Setup was straightforward and it quietly did its job for months without a hiccup. Speeds over gigabit Ethernet were plenty fast for my needs. I'm giving four stars because after a recent power outage, it refused to grab an IP address, and I had to do a full system reset to get it back online. Frustrating, but the data was still intact on the drives, which was a huge relief. For the price point, it's a decent performer, just make sure it's plugged into a good surge protector and maybe keep a secondary backup handy for peace of mind.

I grabbed these a month ago while they were hovering around $43, and for that money I'm genuinely impressed. The sound isn't going to blow away a $150 pair, but the bass has a nice thump and podcasts come through crisp, which is all I really need at the gym. They've survived sweaty treadmill sessions, a few surprise rain showers, and getting stuffed into my jacket pocket daily. Battery life clocks in close to the advertised six hours, and the charging case is compact enough to forget it's in my bag. My only gripe is the touch controls can be a little finicky when adjusting mid-run, but honestly, at this price point I can live with that. Solid everyday beaters that punch above their cost.
Have used at many meetings and no issues at all.

Stellar product. Bought for use with a Surface Pro 4.. had to futz a bit with the video drivers on the surface.. but once I fixed it, this has worked flawlessly. the quality is amazing. Theoretically, any Miracast capable device, i.e. an Intel processor with video of recent vintage (<2 years) should work with it, but I have yet to test this. Basically, your device creates a direct WiFi connection over 2.4ghz to the device itself.. thus the connectivity is a dedicated network using a streamlined protocol..the result is, or Screen Sharing, it runs circles around Chromecast, DLNA and even AppleTV which all attach to the network and packets/data are pushed over tcp/ip.
just blank or empty containers to hold dvd's. shipped fairly fast and will meet my needs for storing dvd's. cheap enough also.
Had this little gadget for months now, tossed it in my bag daily and it's still running like day one.

This card works great and installs easily. I am a little disappointed in the lack of software to support its functionality. I bought this card to be used in a Ubuntu server where I run Plex to serve my house TVs. Installation was simple and Ubuntu/Plex worked right away without even having to install any drivers. It just plain works. What I didn't like was the lack of software capabilities. For Ubuntu there are a couple application you can get like "Me-Tv" or "Kaffine", or for me "Plex". For Windows users there is less freeware to choose from, instead after buying this card you would have to go to the Hauppauge webpage and spend an additional $10 for the WinTV software. I feel this software really should come with the card. Also note: In some of the screenshots and details that advertise this card it says you can watch multiple shows at the same time (showing four windows). The only ways to watch multiple shows is with their WinTv software, no other software will let you do this (not even Plex). Finally, if you using this for Plex note: only the primary plex account can access the program guide to watch TV, none of the other users you've created can access them.

Cons: Biggest con: cables sometimes not long enough. Also, it REQUIRES A DRIVER. Will not work properly without it. I got this unit so I can run a dekstop and laptop together. A few things to note... the vga cables are short an dthe attached usb cable is short but appears to work on most pc's as long as the ports needed are open. The vga cable being short meant that I had to purchase a vga extension cable. As mentioned switching isn't instantaneous etc. 5 second delay to get the mouse recognized. Also I may be wrong but the usb appeared to be needed to be plugged in for the switch to work or detect. At the beginning I also encountered trouble with installing the driver. The windows 7 machine works flawlessly. The xp machine appears to not detect that the belkin is connected. However it still outputs a signal and works. I just can't use software switching. Only the hardware using the button. Also the monitor plugs are tough to connect but work fine. Biggest pro: The big simple switch. I wish it was longer to reach me but the switch is very useful. Just tapping it with my foot does the job. I haven't tried out the audio so I can't comment on how well it works. I also haven't tried connecting usb 2 devices to it. I give it 3 stars because of the software trouble and trouble getting it to install correctly. But it really is a remarkable device and it does what it sets out to do.

The Taotronics Bike Phone Mount Bicycle Holder is a great peace of engineering, that is very versatile. Prior to buying the phone mount holder I placed my phone in a bum bag, when riding my road bike. The Taotronics phone mount holder has enabled me to change all this. Now my phone is mounted on my handle bars in front of me. During my 40km rides "DownUnder" (Sydney, Australia), where at times the ride can get rather bumpy, my phone sits securely clamped and strapped to the phone mount holder on my handle bars. I find it so much easier to use my phone when riding. Also, it is easier to listen to the feedback on my riding performance during my ride, from the downloaded app on my phone. This phone mount was purchased on 15th March 2017 and my wife was so impressed with it, she wanted one for her bike, which was purchased on 25th May 2017. The recommendation from the seller, to use the rubber strap before you put the holder part and the clamp together on the handle bars, was taken heed of. I recommend both the Taotronics Phone Mount Holder and seller Sunvalleytek. Regards Stephen.
Happy, none broke! All is well. Arrived on time, everyone happy. Would recommend, if this is what you are looking for.

This network drive is a piece of junk. I have had it for just a few months. Now it will only stay running for just a few minutes. This is my second Buffalo drive. The first drive I had one of two drives fail after only a few months. I wish the first one had failed a little sooner because I would have never bought the second one. I'm was using them for backups. Not a very good track record. I'm going back to using external USB drives. I have not contacted tech support because I have had to spend so much of my time moving all the backup files to the USB drives. Avoid Buffalo network drives.

As an update to my original review back in 2013: I began having problems with this about 8 months after I purchased it. It crashed 3 times over a matter of two years. I'm uncertain if it was caused by the demand from FSX but I do know something was notably wrong. To be fair, I may just have received a faulty piece of hardware. Nonetheless, after the third nose-dive; out it came. I didn't care about warranties at that point and I am thankful that my OS was not on it. Sadly, it was the worst hard drive that I ever had. I replaced it with a 250 GB SSD which has been magnificent. My current opinion is that Solid State is the only way to go.
I couldn't test it in America, but it worked perfectly in the field and saved a lot of time, space and clutter. It handled 2 iPads, 2 iPhones and a camera battery recharger. It did fine for 3 weeks.

I wanted to love this case because the color is gorgeous and the fabric texture feels really nice in hand. The main issue for me is the typing angle, it's way too shallow and after about 30 minutes my wrists start aching. I tried propping it up with a book but that defeats the purpose of having a case. The pen holder is clever and actually holds my Slim Pen securely, which I appreciate. Protection seems decent for light bumps, though I wouldn't trust a drop onto concrete. It's not a bad product, just not ergonomically great for long writing sessions. If you mostly use it for media consumption, it's probably fine. Three stars because it's well-made but the angle ruins it for my daily workflow.

I'm honestly shocked at how long the battery lasts on these. I've been using them daily for my commute and workouts, and I only need to charge the case maybe once every week and a half. The magnetic snap when you drop them back in is super satisfying too. For the price, the battery life alone makes these a no-brainer.

Bought this for my basic living room setup, just a Blu-ray player and a 4K TV. Honestly, I can't tell any difference from the $8 cable I was using before. Picture looks the same, sound is the same, and it doesn't even feel sturdier in the hand. The connectors are light and plasticky, which surprised me for the price. I'd only suggest this to someone who has more money than patience and just wants to say they bought the priciest option. For regular folks with normal eyes and ears, grab a cheap certified one and spend the savings on a pizza or something actually useful. Total letdown.
This may only apply to very few people, but make sure you know how long the cable is.
Worked as stated cleared my issue on one camera and the other turned out to be a bad camera...The third was the bnc touching my alum. trim on soffit caused another ground.. All happy now..
They work, just don't like the way the head phones are connected, they whirl around too much for me, but they sound ok
For what they charge, I expected way more. Feels cheap and flimsy right out of the box.

Box was pretty smashed up when it arrived, just a thin mailer, no real padding. Headphones looked okay at first glance, but the earcup hinge felt stiff right out of the box. I was optimistic for the first few days; sound's decent for the price. But that initial packaging left me worried, and honestly, I keep babying them now. Not the sturdy first impression I hoped for.

Great image quality for the price, especially with a direct HDMI connection from a laptop or streaming stick. Colors pop and it's bright enough for casual evening use. Unfortunately, I ran into HDCP handshake problems with my older AV receiver, just got a black screen until I bypassed it. That limits it as a full home theater centerpiece. I'd recommend it for backyard movie nights, classroom presentations, or anyone who doesn't need to route through a receiver first.

Order arrived a day early and was packed neatly in a small box, no tangles or kinks right out of the bag. The connectors feel solid, and the slim boot design gives me confidence they'll hold up. Exactly what I needed to tidy up my crowded switch setup from the get-go.
Best AP I have ever owned. Just buy it and say goodbye to your old crap.

Good sound- for earbuds, of course- the remote function is cool. Does take some practice figuring out what's the difference between 'Vol up' and 'Play'. Almost blasted my eardrums once trying to skip songs! Still, a standard pair of in-ear headphones with a cool remote and good price point.
Arrived fast, box was pristine, and the lens feels solid right out of the package.
Had this filter on my lens for over a year now, through dust and rain, and it still looks flawless.
Good buy. Cute case. It fit my iPad Mini 4 with a case just barely, so I'm satisfied.

Needed a way to hook up an older DVI monitor to my laptop's docking station without spending a fortune. This little adapter did the trick for about what a fast-food lunch costs these days. The metal connectors feel solid and it clicks in securely on both ends, no wiggling or signal dropouts. I'm using it daily for a secondary 24-inch screen and the image quality is identical to a straight DVI connection, no fuzziness or artifacts. My one minor gripe is the plastic housing feels a bit cheap, though at this price point I'm not complaining. If you just need basic DisplayPort to DVI conversion without active electronics for high refresh rates, this is a solid buy.

wow...came quick!!!!!!!...eagle called and shipped it next day from tampa to st pete...guys were as professional as you can expect from truck drivers and they helped me scoot it into my garage...i love amazon because they are extremely competitive with pricing...i don't know how they can afford to stay in busines...as for why i chose the top end model when i could have saved 50 bucks on the HPT4254...this model is sleeker looking...thanks amazon for delivering one of the best plasmas on the market at a very affordable price...oh and why did i choose samsung over panasonic...the panny bast model did not offer a vga connection for my laptop...

Isn't it nice when some works out exactly the way it is supposed to? I picked up a pair of Sony bookshelf speakers at a thrift shop months ago and decided it was time to mount them. But I wanted a mounting that was simple, cheap, and effective, took a chance on these satellite speaker mounts and found them to be perfect. The mounts can fit both single screw speakers (like my Sony) and double screw speakers, they can also be set up to be longer (about 5") or shorter (about 3") and come with the screws and hardware to mount to the speaker and to the wall (including dry wall anchors) The mounts also include the Allen wrench necessary to adjust the mounts and permit the speaker to be oriented just about in any direction or attitude. With the help of my cordless screwdriver, I had both speakers mounted nicely on the wall in about 15 minutes. (And that includes looking over the included instructions until I realized that one could actually ignore them.) Now these aren't the most beautiful speaker mounts, and they are plastic so don't try and mount some huge 20-lb speaker. But for mounting small satellite speakers just about anywhere, these will work just fine, and at about $10 the price is just right.

I bought this router after dealing with several Belkin and Netgear routers. I have to tell you that this is the best router I have found out of all of them. Very easy set up. Connects within minutes and all your devices are online in no time compared to most Belkin and Netgear brands, which I had to spend hours setting up and also talking people over customer service for hours. The range is good and the internet speed is excellent. I would recommend this to anyone.

Supper bright and litium battery, whats not to love. The light has not only a clamp to attach to any shoe but a hole to attach to any tripod. Its pretty simple, ON or OFF thats all you get. it is a little defused but not so as to make ghosts, no more so them most LEDs would. Yes you can bridge them together to make one hell of a wall of light, just don't tighten them to hard its only plastic. The battery and charger plus light are no bigger then 2 packs of smokes in your gear bag. Order a second battery if you need to but I have run it for an hour only to have it work a week later for 20min still. It holds a chrage well.
The Nikon D40X is the camera that I have. This item replaced the cap I lost. It was a perfect fit.

This is perfect for what I was looking for. I had a nice pair of small desktop speakers I wanted to hook up to my PC. I had the amp connected to the computer in about 5 minutes. The sound is fairly clean - and I can't really tell if its not totally clean because of my cheap sound card or the amp or the speakers. For the price, it was a great deal.
Picked this up for my camera and it's been flawless. No lag, no corrupted files, just smooth recording every time. Totally dependable.

I've taken this on 2 annual dive trips so far, and it performs excellently. The case gives you access to all of the buttons, switches and the zoom slider. This case is pricey, but it's a fantastic accessory if you own the SD770IS and you go scuba diving or snorkelling. Do yourself a favor, though, and keep a little note in the case (when you put it away after use) reminding yourself to put the camera in "underwater mode." Last year I used the camera for my entire week of diving, and forgot to select underwter mode--many photos were washed out and blue. This only happened when not using the flash and when fairly deep. The shallow pictures were fine, as were the flash pictures.

Arrived a day early in a neat little box with no damage. Plugged it in right away and the display lit up crisp and clear. Super handy for spotting a junk cable instantly, I had a drawer full of duds I never suspected. Really happy with this thing.

These "actionpacs" seem pretty adequate to me. After using them with pretty much every load for almost a month, I don't think we've seen any difference between these and regular Cascade powder. They clean fine, dissolve nicely, no problems. The plastic bucket that holds them is really handy, as you can reach under the sink, pop the lid up and grab a packet with one hand. And having packets to drop into the soap holder in the dishwasher is equally handy. So I say do them for convenience if that matters to you; from my experience, that is what you're paying for. If you'd rather save a bit of money, then stick with the powder.

I've had this for about five months now and the build quality is starting to show its age. The plastic around the edges has developed a slight creak, and the keys feel mushier than they did out of the box. One of the rubber feet already peeled off, so it slides around on my desk constantly. It still connects fine and the battery life is solid, but for something I use daily, I expected it to hold up a bit better. Not falling apart, just feels flimsier over time.

My eleven-year-old has put this thing through absolute chaos and it's still clicking along perfectly. We've taken it to the lake, the pool, and even on a rainy camping trip, no issues at all with water. The image quality genuinely surprised me; colors pop nicely and videos come out smooth. Battery life is solid, easily lasting through a full day of her snapping hundreds of random shots. I appreciate that it's simple enough for her to navigate the menus without asking for help every five minutes. The wrist strap is a lifesaver because she's clumsy, but the camera itself barely has a scratch. For the price, it's honestly a steal and makes a fantastic gift for adventurous kids.
Awesome case. Case did a great job of protecting phone.

I was honestly surprised by how fast this thing showed up. Ordered late Thursday night and it was at my door by Monday afternoon. The packaging was super solid too, thick foam inserts, plastic peel on the screen, not a scratch anywhere. Fired it up and the setup was seamless, felt like unboxing a brand new machine. Trackpad is buttery smooth, keyboard has that crisp, quiet click, and the screen is crazy bright and sharp right out of the gate. I've only had it a couple days but first impressions are really strong. If you're on the fence, just do it. This thing feels premium in every way and I'm genuinely excited to put it through its paces.
Very happy with adapter. Easy to set up. Good range.

I've had this little sub tucked under my desk for about five months now, and it's held up surprisingly well given the price. No rattles, no weird hum, even after accidentally kicking it a dozen times. The enclosure feels solid, and the fabric front hasn't frayed or collected cat hair like I expected. Sound-wise, it fills in the low end for my nearfield setup without getting boomy, as long as you don't crank the gain past halfway. I did notice the feet leave slight marks on hardwood, so maybe grab some pads. For casual music and gaming, it's been totally reliable, no degradation in performance since day one. Not a sub that'll shake your walls, but it does exactly what I needed.
this accessory was jus what i needed for my ipod nano it is so small you need something to help you keep up with it.
For the money, these are okay. Sound is clean and gets decently loud, but the bass is pretty weak. Battery life is fine, just don't expect it to fill a room unplugged. You get what you pay for.
For the money this slider is really smooth. I just received this item today (which is earlier then I anticipated). I have been playing with this item ever since.

Installed this system on my '13 corvette, everything works great, I installed the monitor into the sun visor, all the cables were long enough for my car, but probably not for a truck/suv,camera bracket covers a small part of sticker on license plate may have to trim end off, 170 degree angle is closer to 130 but picture is very clear, for the price this is a great camera monitor system

This thing is rock solid. I've been using it as a dedicated recipe station in my kitchen, holding a 10-inch tablet at eye level while I cook, and it hasn't wobbled once even when I'm frantically tapping with flour-covered fingers. The base has a nice weight to it, so it stays put on my butcher block counter. Adjusting the angle is smooth but tight enough that it doesn't droop mid-recipe. I'd easily recommend it to anyone who does video calls, follows tutorials, or just wants a hands-free setup in the kitchen or workshop. My mom even grabbed one for her online bridge games and loves it. Total win for the price.
It fits correctly, it comes out without frictions, I hope it lasts, if not I'll let you guys know Ed Mac

I grabbed this thing for camping trips and it's honestly blown me away. The battery lasts forever, and it's survived a couple drops and a splash from the lake without missing a beat. Pairing was dead simple, and the sound fills the whole site with crisp, punchy audio. Really happy with this purchase.
Every once in a while it drops the connection from the router. Don't use it anymore.
After a few months, they still sound great but the cable feels flimsy.

If you are looking at the MK520 combo, you are considering the price, battery life, and comfort of your mouse and keyboard. With all of that in mind, you don't do better than this at the price point. The mouse batteries have lasted me over a year (and I am on my computer 12+ hours a day for work). I had to change the keyboard batteries once in the same period. They keyboard has decent feedback when typing and is very lightweight. The mouse is fantastic for people with average size hands (I have 5 of them). Like I said, Just buy it already.

This little switch is way better than I expected for the price. I grabbed it to clean up the cable mess behind my desk, and it handles my PC and work laptop without any lag or signal drop. Super simple setup, no remote needed, and it just works.

Loved the camera! Very easy to navigate and easy to learn! The whole package was great! Only problem was the camera stand, it broke as soon as I took it out. But other than that, I love the quality of this camera. Attached are pictures that I took using the camera!

Honestly, this little case has been solid. I've tossed it in my backpack for a couple of outdoor gigs now, and it's taken a few splashes and drops without any issues. The seal still holds tight, and the latch hasn't loosened up at all. Perfect for keeping my deck safe when things get a bit unpredictable.
I grabbed this just to rip my old DVD collection, and the speed is solid. It's way noisier than I expected, almost like a tiny desk fan, but it gets the job done without hiccups.

I got this as a quirky little stocking stuffer for my sister and honestly, for the price, it's a steal. The detail is way nicer than expected. It's a bit weighty and the cord's short, but as a fun desk accessory, totally worth it.

While there's not much to truly get excited over concerning a replacement snap-on lens cap, this one is well designed and does exactly what it should. It is easy to properly secure to the front of the camera lens and stays in place until removed. I'll give this product "FIVE" Amazon stars!

I bought this on a whim. I'm glad I did. I think I am going to have to replace the battery on my wife's old iPhone. I converted it to an iPod but the battery keeps going down quickly. Therefore, with this kit I can replace the battery myself. I will find it applicable to many other uses when it becomes necessary. I would buy again and may even do so for a spare.
Fits nicely but the front plastic sometimes comes off upon impact.

I wasn't expecting much given the price, but this 50ft HDMI cable completely surprised me. I needed a long run through the wall to a projector and was worried about signal loss or sparkles at 4K, absolutely none of that. Picture is rock solid, colors look great, and it handles HDR content without a hiccup. I've used far more expensive cables in the past that didn't perform this consistently. The connectors feel sturdy, and the cable itself is flexible enough to route through tight spaces without fighting you. If you're on the fence about a budget long-distance HDMI solution, just grab this one. It's honestly overdelivered for the cost and saved me a ton compared to the "premium" options I almost bought.

I bought these headphones due to the great price and the solid reviews. I can see now why they are rated so well. I cannot believe the quality that these little ear-buds produce. The feel solidly constructed and the ear-parts do a great job at keeping outside noise out. I use these at the gym and they do a great job at drowning out the often vulgar teenagers,lol. Grab these buds while you can!

I really wanted to like this mount, but after just four months of gentle use in my kayak, the clamp mechanism completely seized up. I never dunked it or left it outside, always rinsed it after saltwater trips. Last weekend the tightening knob just spins freely now and won't grip at all, so my phone went flying mid-ride. Feels super cheap for the price and there's no way to fix it yourself. Total waste of money and a ruined fishing trip.

Picked this up to breathe life into some old Polk speakers sitting in the garage. Super simple setup with my iPhone, just plug and play. For the price, the clarity and warmth blew me away. If you've got passive speakers collecting dust and want an easy, affordable way to stream music, this is a no-brainer.
Too small for a passport which is interesting. Not bad for storing small cards or electronics though.
Had it a few weeks, battery's already weakening.

Box arrived fast and in perfect shape, no dents, no crushed corners, everything snug in molded cardboard. Pulling it out, it felt solid and way nicer than I expected for the price. Display is crisp and easy to read from across the room without being blinding at night. Buttons have a satisfying click. Honestly, unboxing it already felt like a little upgrade to my nightstand before I even plugged it in.
Bought this for my PC build and it's been fast and reliable since day one.
Dont even know its there very quite. looks good on my desk and no problems so far. Thank you Western. Easy to install also.
For the hype, I expected richer bass and clearer mids. It's decent for calls, but music feels flat. Just okay overall.

Easy to configure, and expandable too. Nice that it serves as an extender besides being a power line adapter. Already added a separate 4220 extender to another part of our house. My desktop doesn't have a wireless card, so I moved one 4220 to an outlet by the desktop and plugged in an Ethernet cord (cat5) and I have internet where I have no modem or Ethernet port previously! Love this system for ease and expandability!

I've been using these for about three weeks now, mostly during workouts and walks. The sound still holds up well, clear mids and decent bass for the size. They've survived a few drops onto concrete without any visible damage or change in performance. Sweat resistance is okay, but they do get a bit loose once moisture builds up. Battery life hasn't degraded yet, though I do wish each charge lasted longer than an hour. Solid for quick sessions.
A Person (s) asked me to by these (3) for her- them. One was very pleased! WV is hilly, so no signal could be picked up. But it is a good product.
The battery lasts all day even with heavy streaming, super happy.

I went into this expecting a solid PSU, but this thing completely outclasses my old unit. Voltage delivery is dead stable even under heavy gaming loads, and the hybrid fan mode keeps it silent most of the time. Cable quality is excellent too, making cable management way less painful than I'm used to. Honestly, I was nervous about the price tag, but after seeing how clean and reliable the power output is, it feels worth every penny for peace of mind.

After having this modem for eight months; the ethernet started dropping out. Running a speedtest showed the upload coming up with an error or extremely slow speed which would drop the ethernet. Ordered a new modem ( another TP-Link) and it acted the same way. Finally ordered a Motorola modem and my LAN works fine.

Thought I'd found a bargain but the camera died after a few weeks. Can't reach the seller either. Honestly, I'd only recommend this to someone who enjoys tossing money into the trash. Save yourself the headache.

Got this kit to mess around with my old speedlight and honestly had pretty low expectations given the price tag. Everything showed up intact and has held up through a few weekend shoots already. The softbox is a bit fiddly to set up the first time, and the grid could fit a little snugger, but nothing's fallen apart on me. For a beginner like me trying to figure out what modifiers I actually like using, it's been perfect. Hard to beat the value here.
For fifty bucks I expected more than a month before it died completely.

I ordered this weather alert radio from amazon which came in very good time as promised. This radio was being endorsed by our local TV Weather channels as being the top of line and promoting them at various stores in our area. Found out that amazon had this same radio for almost $15 less then what was being offered at the store so bought it. It is very easy to program and set up for any area where you might live and since it comes with a battery back up for power outages discovered we could take it with us for those one day trips for shopping,etc. It works extremely well and recommend it to any one who is weather concerned, mostly for severe storms like tornados. Fortunately we have not had any tornados yet but it sure lets you know loudly when there is any storm alerts in your area. Very good buy, would recommend it highly to all as it could save your life.

I was going to write a great review about this product a while ago, but kept delaying it. And it's a good thing I delayed it. This hard drive lasted about 3.5 years. I bought it in May 2010 and it broke in December of 2013. I never used it heavily. Only used it as backup. Finally, it went bad. I still have a couple of hard drives by this brand that I purchased between 6 and 10 years ago and they're still operating.

I picked this up hoping it would be the perfect travel companion, and honestly it's pretty close. The fit isn't exactly what I pictured, the cutout position feels a bit off from where it should be, but it's still easy to work with. I ended up keeping it because the overall build quality and feel are better than other cases I've tried. For long flights, it does the job nicely without adding bulk. A small redesign would make it perfect.
For the money I expected way better, total waste of cash honestly.
Works well, hurts your ears after an hour or so use is the only downside.
You can upgrade this. It is an excellent buy!

I purchased this with the single intention of Vudu Disc-to-Digital. For that purpose it works great. It is a little loud for my taste, and heats up quickly. Which makes me glad i am not using it for other purposes.

I picked up the N300 to breathe some life into an older setup in my basement office, and honestly I'm shocked at how solid the signal is now. Setup took maybe ten minutes and I haven't had to touch it since. Streaming music while my kid watches videos upstairs used to be a buffering nightmare, now it's smooth on both ends. For the price, this little workhorse completely transformed our Wi-Fi dead zones. Super happy.

Terrible product. Does not support "Wireless AP" mode. Even if you manually disable DHCP, turn off SPI firewall, plugin using LAN ports, it still restricts access to lan devices. Also, it has LOTs of bugs including: -Cannot allow WAN administration from any BOGON networks. It looks like you can, but then errors out. Before applying settings -By default it has vLAN tagging on port 3 and 4. but documentation does not tell you. -Web admin interface is NOT httpS, and has no option to enable https. -DNS is forcefully bound to the WAN port, thus to use it as a Wireless AP you must have a LAN cable attached... AND a LAN cable. -Wireless defaults to use captive portal, even with guest Network turned off. So you have to enable guest Network, disable captive portal, click save, then disable guest Network again and NOW the non-guest network will work like it should. GAH! No way should anyone have to deal with this junk... I have been trying to tell myself to just accept this devices issues, but even after hours of changing settings, it is impossible to make it work like it is supposed to. It is not even consistent, sometimes it will not let you connect, or will start dropping packets, so I have to use my old wireless as it, while older technogy, is reliable. ONLY good thing about this device... the wireless range is very good. Unfortunately because some days it decides to just drop packets randomly, the range benefit is useless:( *Note: TP-Link devices are ONLY good if you wipe original firmware and use OpenWRT. I have an older WDR4300 and a WDR810, with OpenWRT they work well... just old technology, unfortunately because Archer C9 uses Broadcom chip you cannot use OpenWRT.
I love the size and look of these things I have several in different colors. They are compact and easy to travel with. I have had no technical issues.
The video quality feels like a step back from what I expected. It's clunky to use, and the footage comes out grainy even in decent light. Honestly pretty disappointed for the price I paid.

I've had 4 of these speakers hooked up to an Acoustic Research center channel and subwoofer as a complete 5.1 system. These speakers have very nice and clear sounds. In the past, I've had the Bose, Acoustic Research, and Klipsch Quintet III 5.1 systems, and these Energy Takes actually produce the best sound quality out of all of them. For some reason, I'd like to replace speakers once every 3 years or so to sample their sounds, as some do it with cars. It's hard to describe the sound quality as opposed to be heard to experience. But to wrap it up, these Takes are the best speakers of all the bookshelf systems I've had. Of course, none of them could come close to my top-of-the-line JBL tower speakers, but those are tower speakers and they cost $4000 (even 10 years ago).

Got a two-pack of these for my iPad and honestly, for the price it's a steal. Installation took a little patience but the included guide made it pretty foolproof. No bubbles or dust specks once it settled, and the clarity is spot on, doesn't mess with screen brightness or touch sensitivity at all. Feels sturdy too, not that flimsy plastic some cheap protectors use. For what I paid, I wasn't expecting much but this easily beats protectors I've spent way more on.

I am really happy with the quality of this item. It was a very good value for the money. I love the separate compartments. I carry my Lenovo ThinkPad Helix with charging keyboard stand and it fits very nicely. It has a strap to secure it and the compartment is padded. I use the other compartments to carry paperwork, a mouse, charging cables, etc. There is plenty of room and enough pockets and compartments to keep me organized.

I've used this to swap out the battery in an old Android tablet that was basically a paperweight, and the included spudger tools made prying it open way less sketchy than I expected. The adhesive strips held everything snug once I closed it back up. Screen lifts cleanly, no scratches. Totally worth it for reviving forgotten devices.

I grabbed this little adapter to add Bluetooth to my older HP desktop running Windows 10, and it worked instantly, just plugged it into a USB port and my wireless headphones paired within seconds. Range is solid across the room with no stuttering or dropouts. I also tested it on a Raspberry Pi 4, and it picked up my keyboard and mouse right away without any tinkering. For the price, it's been a flawless, hassle-free upgrade that simply does its job.

My cans arrived a full day earlier than the estimated delivery window, which was a nice surprise. The outer box was pristine, but inside, the manufacturer's packaging felt a little basic, just molded cardboard and a thin fabric pouch, nothing premium. Unboxing was straightforward, though I almost missed the 3.5mm adapter tucked under the flap. First power-on, straight out of the box, the sound was noticeably tight and closed-in, with bass that felt a bit shy. I left them running overnight with a burn-in playlist and by the next morning the soundstage had already started opening up. Clamp force is on the firmer side, which gives a secure fit, but I'm hoping the pads soften with use. Promising start overall.
Had this thing for a couple months now, still runs like day one. Solid little gadget for the money.

Mine arrived a day early in a sturdy little box, no chance of anything getting bent. Everything was neatly laid out with the wipes and dust stickers, so I actually felt confident before I even started. First screen went on without a single bubble and it looks completely invisible. Super smooth to the touch and the display is just as crisp as before. Very impressed.
Couldn't be happier with how fast and smooth everything runs.

The first adapter I bought popped and died. They were very prompt and friendly about giving me a replacement. The second worked for a while, but now has to be held in specific orientation to work. I have a standard adapter from Lenovo that I use interchangeably so I don't think it is the way I use it. If you plan on moving the adapter around any, I would suggest you buy the better quality adapter from Lenovo.

Package arrived in a flimsy bubble mailer with zero padding, the box inside was already crushed and one corner of the camera body had a fresh scuff straight out of the "sealed" packaging. No clue if this was a return or just handled carelessly, but it didn't feel new at all. The battery door was loose and creaked when I opened it, which didn't inspire confidence. Tossed in some AAs and half the time it wouldn't power on unless I wiggled them just right. For something I waited over a week to get, this whole thing screamed cheap from the second I tore it open. Definitely not the fun unboxing I expected.
My second one. Hope it is as good as the first.

I paid way too much for this considering it barely lasted 18 months. The image started flickering with weird color streaks right after the warranty ended. For the price, I expected at least three years of use. Total waste of hard-earned money.

This bag is working out perfectly for my wife in her new job. She never removes it from the wheels as it takes a couple minutes to convert over and isn't really hard to shoulder carry with the wheels. Hint: Remove the rain cover from the cart and store it in the bag rather than where the company hides it. She wanted the red one, but I talked her into black and she is glad I did. The Red/Black combo tries to look stylish, but comes off too industrial in real life. The red bag by itself would be terrific, but not paired with the black rolling cart. Interior size is adequate as expected. Anything roomier would look like every other generic rolling case. This vertical bag stands out from the crowd, but remains professional. I thought the patent leather would be gaudy, but it's subdued by the patterns. Be a good husband and purchase this.

The transmitter works as advertised. It has two power levels, and switching between them is a bit finicky (you need to read the instructions). The low power level is useless for transmitting more than a few feet. The high power level gives a pretty nice signal through my whole house and garage. I wanted to use it for transmitting TV audio to headphones (via a portable mp3 player with FM receiver). It works fine when I stand still, but I get noise every time I move around. This is probably the fault of the FM receiver on the mp3 player, which uses the headphones as an antenna. If you want to transmit to a stationary receiver (or maybe a higher-quality portable receiver), it should be fine.

Please note that the USB ports have 2.1 amps current available between them. That means two 1 amp cell phones charging or one iPad or Tablet. But NOT a cell phone AND an iPad or tablet. And certainly not two tablets. For the price, it's a win.

REVIEW REFERENCES the "Wireless" Unit: Pros: 1. Connectivity is rock-solid and it took about 5 minutes to set up a new secure network. Smart devices in the home easily jump between this network and the old one in different wings of the residence. 2. Linksys is a company I trust because my Linksys router has been working non-stop for 8 years without a single problem. I expect their networking equipment to just work and it does. Always. 3. This unit is so smart that you can switch which outlet it's plugged into by yanking it off the wall and plugging it in somewhere else. It will remember all of your settings and reboot itself in under a minute. This is great for experimenting time when you want to see which room/outlet gives you the best reception/range. Cons: 1. Nothing major, but note that structural walls will degrade your signal slightly; and that's going to happen with pretty much any wifi product you buy.

Review do-over. Continued to ask for disc every time. It's obvious that this product should be sold with a disc. Not solo. Never worked again question if the adaptor had any thing to do with it working the first time

Rock steady voltages and monitoring to boot! I must admit I was always skeptical of "super" expensive PSUs but I am surprised at how amazingly versatile and stable they are. The Zero fan is whisper quiet when in use and the fan test was useful to ensure the product worked (even though I also have a PSU tester/load). The corsair link and configurable 12V+ rails guarantee this will be in my next two builds, that along with an extensive 10 year warranty.

I've burned through about half of this 100-pack already for archiving old music collections and family photos, and every single disc verified perfectly. The burn speed stays consistent, and I haven't had a single error or skip during playback. Honestly, for the price per disc, I wasn't expecting much, but these outperform blanks I've paid way more for. Great value.

I grabbed this DVR to replace an aging unit in my shop and it's been solid so far. Setup was straightforward even for someone who isn't super techy, menus are simple and the remote is responsive. It recognized my older analog cameras right away, which saved me a headache. Recording quality is clean, no weird artifacts or skipping. The fan runs a bit louder than I expected, but it's tucked away in a back office so it's not a dealbreaker. Shipping took a few extra days but everything arrived intact. For the price, it's a dependable workhorse. Four stars because the manual could be clearer, but honestly once it's running you don't really need it.
Arrived fast and in perfect shape, feels solid right out of the box.
Does the job well. No flaws, what so ever. Fits nice, isolates ambient noise. Still need some time to justify for its quality.

This thing is surprisingly handy for backyard movie nights with the kids. The built-in speaker gets loud enough that we didn't even bother hooking up external audio, and setup took maybe five minutes out of the box. I really appreciate how lightweight it is, we just toss it in a bag with some snacks and go. Picture quality is decent for the price, though don't expect miracles in a room with any ambient light. Dark scenes can get a little muddy, and I did notice some slight blurring around the edges no matter how much I fiddled with the focus. For casual use, it's a solid little gadget, just keep your expectations grounded.
For the cost, this little gadget does way more than expected. Solid build, smooth performance, and the battery lasts forever. Total steal.
Swapped my old head for this and it handles my long lens setup like a dream right out of the box.

I've been using this mount for about five months now, mostly on bumpy gravel trails and the occasional city commute. The clamp still grips my handlebars rock solid, no slipping at all. The silicone bands are a nice touch, they've kept my phone snug even when I hit unexpected potholes. I was a bit worried the plastic might crack after a few tumbles, but it's held up without a scratch. Rain caught me twice, and the grip didn't degrade. Honestly, it's become one of those things I don't even think about anymore, which is exactly what you want. Quick to pop the phone in and out too. Solid piece of kit for the price.
I needed a way to hook up my external sata dock to one of my computers and it killed me using 2.0 USB port over eSata. This dropped right into my system and solved my problem.
There are surely prettier connectors or there but this does the job equally well and is relatively inexpensive

Had no problem configuring this board. There are a lot of options so you have to take your time and know what you want. I was in a rush to get a server up and running and this is flexible enough to allow me to scavenge parts from desktop systems. On-board video is a nice feature and allows me to save energy until graphics cards are needed. To be clear this board is a solid choice for any build not just a rush build. With a plan of 24TB and 256GB RAM options are virtually unlimited. Both sockets work just fine with no misaligned pins. All USB and Ethernet ports are working. Currently running a 750w power supply, 16GB RAM, 220GB x2 RAID 1, dual Xeon 2620v2.

Don't get this if your goal is the secondary temperature gauge. It doesn't transmit through walls! So you would have to have the main unit in the house on one side of a window and the receiver somewhere outside mounted but not in the sun, oh and not where it can get wet, oh and not too far away, etc., etc.. There are tons of restrictions about the placement which make the secondary temperature gauge virtually useless. Who has the PERFECT spot to mount it in relation to the main device? I thought you could mount the temperature gauge anywhere outside but you can not. Can't be in the sun, can't get wet, can't have X number of walls or barriers between the main unit and the other gauge. I actually should have sent it back. Would not recommend unless you don't care about the second gauge and if that's the case, you can find one cheaper.

Cuts glare and goes on easily (relatively) with the right tools. The cloth they provide for cleaning the screen is next to worthless... or use it for the final wipe. Getting the screen clean before application is essential. The squeegee provided is also a little too small. Use a credit card. Keep in mind, you're dealing with three layers. Peel one off before applying to the screen and take the other off after lots of squeeging. In fact, I left the last one on for a few days before removing to ensure I had gotten all the bubbles out.
