Works greats on glare and adds a layer of protection against my keyboard. Thumbs up.

Nice design. Opticall and mechanically, it works. It is easy to attach and position on my iPhone and the optics are sharp. If I were goingb to suggest a change,mit would be fo bayonet rather than screw-threads to connect the parts for faster setup.
We got this for our son for his leap pad 2 he loves it

In the age of digital revolution that metamorphosed photography for ever there is little reason to buy an analog product. A product with a large size and cumbersome function. rather than take hundreds of photos before you think of changing your memory card you are limited to 10 small expensive prints. So why did I buy it. And how come did I give it five stars. Will here are the reasons; 1. I had realistic expectations for what I was getting into. 2. I love photography in all it's shapes and forms. 3. Again I had realistic expectation. 4. I'm into fun stuff 5. I'm sentimental for the old days. look at reasons 1 and 3 again. some people buy things with inflated expectations and then get disappointed with what they have. Honestly the photo quality was way way much better than I expected. The colors are vivid and vibrant and the focus is really sharp. The camera has for simple different settings for light conditions and an automatic flash which did a really good job for foreground lighting. The photos are small but that was a compromise I'm willing to make. They are expensive $1 per print, to me that makes them more precious. In this day of digital we are so used to click click click with our digital cameras and our cellphones. We take thousands of photos and print literally zeros. The photos we take are free so we don't think twice of click click click. With this Polaroid and $1 per print price tag I enjoyed the click and the "Shake it like a Polaroid picture" and the sharing with others. It is really really fun. But I have no illusions. And I want you to have none. This is a novelty / speciality item. It will not replace my DSLR or my point and shooter it will compliment them and thus I'm really happy with my purchase and if you buy this camera with a realistic expectations and if you are a sentimental guy/gal you will enjoy it like I am.
Perfect for any hobbyist photographer who's tired of lugging a tripod. Lightweight but solid, clips on fast, and makes outdoor shoots way less awkward.
These are perfect for anyone with an active lifestyle who's tired of their watch band slipping loose.

Wow so glad this popped up to rate! I forgot what I had that I liked, for the past year have had a struggle finding one as solid (not wobbly, not easy to break, truly one handed)... And now I can finally return the latest piece of crap I got and get this again!!
they are ok, can't handle as much as I believe they say they will, but I'm no expert, just wanted a real powerful system to replace the Onkyo speakers I already had.

For the price I expected way more. It died right when I needed it, corrupted mid-transfer and took my files with it. Cheap enough to be tempting, but not worth losing work over. I've had no-name brands last longer. Honestly, save your cash and get something reliable instead of rolling the dice on this one.

So far this is working perfectly. I set up both of our laptops to backup wirelessly. After several hours each to back up the computers, we are using the SmartWare software to do continuous backing up. It is very cool to see that the newest version of your documents or the new pictures you uploaded to your computer show up on the WD Cloud without any effort on your part. No set up issues on Dell PC's, one running Windows 7 and one on Windows Vista. Have not tried the remote access function yet - not a high priority for our purposes.

It works with my 2008 Apple Mac Pro running Snow Leopard. The reason I did not give it 5 stars is that the back light only flashes for a second when connecting to the Mac. It does not stay connected but does reconnect fairly quickly after a keystroke or two. Perhaps this is to save power.

Original headphones, left channel went dead within first two months. Company replaced them immediately. Replacement set lasted about 10 months when the plastic piece connecting the bracket to the ear piece just broke. Exploded right off my head! Fixed it with superglue. Two months later the exact same thing happened to the other ear piece. Was watching a movie when they just fell off my head. I was so mad I emailed their sales department complaining about their junk headphones. They wanted the serial number and then requested that I send them back for a replacement. That was four years ago. The ear pads have since flaked and disintegrated and those were replaced about three months ago. Two nights ago, while watching a movie, they just exploded right off my head, the plastic piece connecting the ear piece to the head brace just broke. I have finally trashed these head phones. If you want a great set of headphones and are willing to pay $150, get the Sennheiser HD595. Not only do they sound superior to the Bose Triports but they are made like a quality product should be.

I've been using this SIM card reader for over eight months now, and it's held up great through a lot of handling. I originally bought it to salvage contacts from a water-damaged phone my son dropped in a puddle during a camping trip. Popped the SIM in, and the reader pulled up every name and number without a hitch. Since then, I've lent it to a coworker and used it twice more for family emergencies, it's been tossed in my desk drawer, taken on a road trip, and still works like new. The plastic casing hasn't cracked, the USB connection stays snug, and it never fails to read even older SIM cards. Solid little gadget that just keeps delivering.

I was bracing for a headache, but this little HDMI switch was totally painless. Unboxed it, plugged in my soundbar, streaming stick, and an old game console into the three ports, then connected the output to my TV. It auto-detected the active device instantly, no fiddling with settings or the remote. The included infrared remote is basic but responsive, and I just tucked the tiny switch behind the TV stand out of sight. Picture quality is crisp with zero lag or signal drop, even when switching sources mid-stream. Honestly, for the price I expected some quirk, but it's been rock-solid for weeks. My only regret is not grabbing one sooner to reclaim my sanity from cable swapping.

These speakers deliver surprisingly deep bass for their size, I haven't felt the need for a sub in my small living room. The mids are clear and vocals really shine. I'm docking one star only because the treble can get a tad sharp at higher volumes.

This is an "no brainer" as it gets for an accessory for the Olympus OMD-E-M5 camera. It not only brings an entirely new ergonomics to the camera (for the better) but adds several more controls enhancing it's professional use to say the least. But build is like Fort Knox perfection. NO gaps and it's solid as a rock once installed. I prefer to use the battery access on the grip altogether than bother with removal to install in the main body. The grip is a HUGE enhancement and you always feel like it's locked in your hands allowing for lower shutter speed shooting as well. If you have a OMD "Just Get It" and don't look back

I picked this up hoping to consolidate two smaller SSDs into a single 3.5" bay, and for the most part it delivers. The metal frame feels sturdy, and drive alignment was straightforward. I was a bit disappointed that it didn't work with my newer case's tool-less sleds, the pegs simply don't line up with the bracket's screw holes. Fortunately, my case included a bag of screws, so I removed the plastic rails and mounted it the old-fashioned way. Once installed, everything stays put with no vibration. It's not quite the seamless drop-in I expected, but if you're okay with a minor workaround, it does exactly what it promises.
This is the one that works! I had another brand that kept cutting in and out and then finally failed.

I've been through a few cases for my tablet, and this one finally nailed it. The magnetic closure is surprisingly strong, it snaps shut with a satisfying click and doesn't pop open when I toss it in my bag. I love that I can just fold the cover back and prop it up on my desk without the whole thing sliding around. The texture on the outside has a nice grip too, so I'm not constantly worried about it slipping out of my hands. It adds barely any bulk, which was a big deal for me since I carry it everywhere. Honestly, for the price, the fit and finish are way better than I expected.

This case is a solid pick for the money. The sturdy build feels reassuring, and it snaps on easily without bulk. My only small gripe is the bezel around the display cuts just slightly into the edge swiping, which takes a little getting used to. For the price though, it's a bargain that keeps the device well protected.
Great Mount, easy to install. I like the little level they provide in the package, helps with install.

Bought this drive hoping for a reliable backup solution. Started getting random disconnects after just a year of light use. Eventually it wouldn't mount at all. Lost a bunch of family photos I'll never get back. Tried different cables, enclosures, even a data recovery service, nothing worked. For the price, I expected way better longevity. Total paperweight now. Really disappointed and wouldn't recommend trusting anything important to this.

I enjoy trying out new technology all the time, but it's not that often a piece of new technology really impresses me!! The Roku player has done that - easy to set up, works exactly like advertises and actually has exceeded my expectations. Super high quality image, easy to use. And a great price at that. The only thing I wish this device would do is access and stream music and videos from my home computer. Then I'd have one device that does it all. Highly recommended.
great head phones they do exactly what the advertisement says they do great quality sound very comfortable on the ears

Since originally purchasing this when it was in essentially beta testing, they have updated the software many times. Everything now works great, it seems very accurate and I love the simple look of it. I had contacted customer service 3 times without every receiving a response (only available via email). So while it works now, the customer service is non-existent, so I have to knock a star off. Pros: Look, fit, accuracy, simplicity Cons: Customer service
I really like this backpack. They look nice stylish, have a lot of space too. I can take it any where.

I'm honestly impressed by how quiet the keys are for late-night typing. The trackpad feels a bit jumpy sometimes, but it's manageable. For the price, it's a solid little keyboard for couch or bed use.

I grabbed this webcam mostly for work calls and the occasional family Zoom, and it's honestly been a nice little upgrade. The picture is crisp enough that my colleagues actually commented on how clear I looked, which never happened with my old built-in laptop cam. Autofocus is quick, and it handles mediocre office lighting way better than I expected, no grainy shadows or weird color shifts. The mic picks up my voice cleanly without me having to lean in, so I ditched my headset for casual meetings. Setup was literally plug-and-play, no driver headaches. I'd recommend this to anyone working from home, streaming casually, or students doing online classes who want a reliable, no-fuss camera that just works right out of the box.

It's being used as I write this review I am a first time builder had one issue with a plug to the case seemed very tight to me,I do wish the manual had more explanation and how to for the bios I had to have a computer shop install the bios for me,I would buy this again.

I purchased the case recently to setup a DDR2 gaming PC and it setup very well with a sharp look. I will attach pictures if I can so anyone interested can see. By the way, the case is big enough to fit a decently large Heatsink in... Which is just awesome.

I love this machine I bought one years ago this is a little newer model but it's the closest got one on each Tv I paid less on Amazon for this one than the old machine many many years ago I haven't tried the recording yet but I have played the plays beautiful and I like the machine
I've had this keyboard for about six months now, and it's still going strong. Keys feel solid, no lag, and it's survived a few spills. Perfect companion for my tablet setup.

I bought this for a long hiking trip where I needed my camera ready at all times, and it totally delivered. The build feels solid and the quick-adjust feature is smooth enough to use one-handed while scrambling over rocks. I'd recommend this to anyone who hates neck straps bouncing around or who shoots in crowded spots, it keeps your gear secure against your body but lets you bring it up fast when a shot appears. After a full day, my hand and shoulder felt fine, which honestly surprised me. If you're a travel photographer, street shooter, or just someone who wants less strain and more freedom, grab this without hesitation.

this hub was exactly what I needed to hook up additional usb devices and the size of it made it more desirable. I'm happy with the device and the transaction as well. Thank you from one satisfied customer.

I'm a fairly particular with my can selection, within a budget. I listen to music on my cans everyday and these headphones sound better the older they get. I've had them for over 2 years now. There was a break in period of about 50 hours. They replaced a pair of Sennheiser 595. They are very different from the Senns, (which are great headphones as well, just a brighter sound.) The one caveat is that they are difficult to drive and a headphone amp takes care of that nicely. I listen to all types of music, but my headphone time tends to be Jazz and vocals and the AKG's don't disappoint. Also, if you have a large head, as I do:) you should be careful with these. They fit, but barely. Happy listening.

I finally pulled the trigger on this monitor and the motion clarity is unreal. The biggest thing for me is how buttery smooth scrolling through web pages and documents feels now, no more jittery text. I was a little worried my aging RX 5700 XT wouldn't keep up, but FreeSync Premium works flawlessly and completely eliminated tearing in Elden Ring without any noticeable input lag. Setup was literally plug and play. If you're on the fence and still using a basic 60Hz panel, just do it.
Worked fine at first but the buttons got stuck after a month.
Excellent SSD for my HTPC. Super fast and great buy at $50.

Ever need to use your computer and not sit in front of it? I do all the time. I use my computer to learn the guitar so having to scoot over to my computer just to hit a space bar or some-such is a pain in the behind. I thought this would be something to try and see if it helped. It sure has. Being able to use my mouse from across the room is a godsend. Once i got used to the motions of it it was second nature to click on whatever I needed. Really useful for me. If I was in an office setting where I had to give frequent presentations this would be great. Really nice product. I was really impressed with how well it worked. The only wish I had with this product was to have a little more documentation. The only help info is on the box and that doesn't cover much. I was able to figure it out but it would be nice to have a bit more. I did get a disocunt on this product but that did not affect my view on it. I really think this is a nice air mouse.

I really wanted to like this, and for basic TV setups it might be fine. But if you have a more complex home theater, this thing gets frustrating fast. The lack of a screen means you're completely blind for activity switching when something goes out of sync, and it will. I spent way too much time troubleshooting with the app, which is clunky and slow. The RF is nice on paper, but range was spotty through my cabinet doors. I'd only suggest this to someone with a dead-simple setup, maybe just a soundbar and streaming stick. Anyone juggling multiple sources or a receiver should look elsewhere.

I've tried a handful of screen protectors over the years and this one surprised me for the cost. You get a two-pack for less than what I'd pay for a single plastic film at a kiosk, and the glass feels solid under my finger. No weird rainbow effect or loss of touch sensitivity. The alignment frame made it basically foolproof, took maybe two minutes and zero bubbles. I've already dropped my phone once since installing it and the protector took the hit perfectly. For the price, I honestly expected way less.
Arrived super fast in sturdy packaging. Opened it up and the screen protector looked flawless. Super impressed right out of the box.

I have had many Sony Walkman's over the years. The sound on this one simply does not compare. It is an easy to operate, fairly sturdy device, but in my opinion is way over priced for what you get. And that isn't the main issue. The problems started after I had it for a few months. I would plug it into the my computer and it would not register as a connected device. It would charge and show the battery screen on the Walkman but the computer couldn't detect it. I had to plug it in and unplug is several times to get it to work. Then it stopped registering no matter what I did. I wondered if my computer driver was corrupted..even though it worked fine on other devices. When I bought a new laptop and tried to connect it again...same problem. It would charge but fail to go to the screen that showed it was being detected so I could not load music. It would instead either attempt to play a song or go to the charge screen without registering on the computer. I went into settings and used "restore factory settings". It made no difference. I then used the hard reset on the unit in hopes that would work. No go. If these were not priced so outrageously I might chalk this up to a bad unit and buy another, but for what you get...and with the issues I have had....not worth it.
Really cleaned up my desktop by eliminating an long cable. Works perfectly.

For the price, this little strobe surprised me. Light output is decent for small home setups, and it's been reliable through dozens of shoots. I do wish it had finer power adjustments, but I just tweak distance or camera settings. Overall, a solid budget-friendly option for beginners.

Love it. In my retirement days I use it daily with my W10 Dell Venue 8 Pro for emails, browsing and Youtubes. It sure beats using the on-screen keyboard. For "heavy duty" full-size applications I use my laptop.
Order arrived on time and as described. Best deal on HDMIs. Only brand I use in our office
These fans are amazing for the price. These are super quite unlike their more pricier bown and black fans.
This board totally exceeded my expectations for reliability and range in my DIY sensor network.

Cable works fine-hubby thought it was crap until he discovered the little plastic caps on the end. Once he got it hooked up to the TV and soundbar he stopped calling it crap and started calling it awesome.

I picked this up for my Canon and the fast recycle time is a real plus during bursts. The build feels solid enough for the price. Only wish the battery indicator was a bit more obvious when it's running low mid-shoot.
It a bit flimsy, but works for the most part.

This is a great product, I used it on my Samsung Google Chromebook because they do not have an ethernet port all I did was plug and play. With this great product you can use it for any type of Netbook which doesn't come with an ethernet port, with this you will have the access that you need when WiFi is not availible

Keyword: TUNITY If you use the app Tunity on your phone to tune into and listen to TV channels on your phone via the Bluetooth speaker in a loud bar, then be ready to be disappointed. I bought this specifically to listen to Bellator MMA (Spike TV/Paramount) while I was at my local bar. The bar plays loud music and has tons of TV's. So I had them put one TV on to the Bellator MMA fights and I used Tunity to play the audio over this BT speaker. It wasn't loud enough though. I had to hold the speaker right up to my ear just to hear the announcer on TV calling the fight plays. So I'm going back to my 10W DIY bluetooth speaker and its heavy batteries instead. Otherwise, I thought his BT speaker was nice. It's tuned to play cleanly at full volume. It just doesn't have ENOUGH volume. Maybe if there was a button you could press to tune it for vocals only, then it could have played that louder. Like a quick EQ button. One EQ setting for music and one EQ setting for vocals only, that could play louder.
Picked these up for a budget NAS build and they've been rock solid since day one. No errors or hiccups during heavy file transfers. Really happy with the value.

Well, I've been using it for quite sometime now and I honestly don't like it very much. The fact that I've actually bought two sets of these makes it even worse. I'll keep the review short and simple. Pros: 1. Easy to install and use 2. Buttons light up, can turn the feature on or off Cons: 1. Buttons lights up and off too quickly, lights do not stay long enough 2. Distance for radio signal is poor, it's best not to have interference or obstacles in front or in between the signal path 3. The mouse wheel is infrared sensor, which kind of works but not as well as a mouse wheel. It's not as precise and you almost always lose track 4. Due to poor signals, I would constantly have to type something on TV and look at the pad, then look at the TV again to make sure the signal took the input, because sometimes it would not. Gave it 3 stars for Lenovo at least trying to improve a product but did not really do a good job.

For the price, these do the job if you just need something for Zoom calls or listening to podcasts while cooking. The foam pads sit lightly, so my ears don't get sore even after a couple hours, which I appreciate. Sound-wise, they're pretty flat, vocals come through clear enough, but bass is almost nonexistent. I wouldn't use them for music or movies where you want any depth. Build quality feels flimsy, especially the thin plastic band, so I'm careful when handling them. I'd only recommend these to someone who needs a cheap, temporary backup pair for voice stuff and absolutely nothing demanding. If you care about audio quality at all, skip these.

They are inexpensive but are clear and seem to be well made and worth the price. Nice case to keep them in. Best part they are fulfilled by digital Goja an excellent company to do business with. Great customer service.
I'm very happy with this 4tb drive - I'm using a two year old iMac with Snow Leopard as my Operating System and haven't experienced any glitches or problems.

I wasn't expecting much at this price point, but the tracking is super precise and it feels solid in hand. Battery life is ridiculous, I'm still on the original pair after daily use since spring. Really pleasant surprise.

I've been using these Panasonic PQ tapes for my wedding videography business going on four years now, and they've never let me down. I shoot everything in HDV and I'm constantly amazed at how clean the recordings come out, zero dropouts, no glitching, even when I'm reusing tapes a couple times. Honestly, the archival stability gives me real peace of mind when handing over final edits to couples. I'd recommend these to anyone shooting on Mini-DV, especially if you're doing paid client work where reliability is non-negotiable. Beginners will appreciate the consistency, and seasoned pros already know these are the gold standard. Skip the cheap generics and just stock up on these.

I had this trasmitter for 2 years. It outperformed my firends and families transmitters by being compact, no cords unless you want to charge it. The transmitting wire is on the outside which gives it a stronger signal even though it runs off the Ipods power. The only drawback is that....it runs off the Ipods power and drains it after a good day of driving, unless you plug it in to a mini USB port cigarette lighter power thingy which you can grab at radio shack. I had to buy another one because I pulled the wire out of it not realizing it was the transmitter cable......not my brightest moment. But just press play and within 4-5 feet of my radio I had crystal clear music with NO STATIC. I could pass it around my car without interruption and people could play what they liked. Excellent product, don't tear the transmitting wire out!

I grabbed this RAM to breathe life into an old Dell desktop, and after about four months of daily use, it's still rock solid. I run spreadsheets, stream music, and keep way too many browser tabs open, zero hiccups or crashes. The sticks feel sturdy seated in the slots, and I haven't noticed any heat issues or performance dips over time. Honestly, for the price, it's been a reliable little workhorse.
Arrived fast, box was pristine, and it just works right outta the gate.
Decent storage for the price, but the heat it throws off makes me wonder if I'll need to replace it sooner than expected.

The product design also tight, but that is an advantage and a disadvantage. Once again decent bond has a positive force that does not fall. However, if a little loose liquidity and more devices that users could have a great deal of fun. And finish with gold plated and also not as bad as we thought. If only the end of the line to rca a little refined. In general, a good line.
Arrived a day early, packed super snug. Took it out and instantly knew this thing was solid. Stoked to use it.

Excellent product. Lots of clean power for the price. I do like how the crossover adjustments are hidden behind a faceplate, making a nice looking presentation for those who like to show their toys (I prefer to hide them). Wiring is straight forward with speaker wire clips that made connections very easy. Mounting hardware is included (4 screws) as well as the two little allen wrenches to tighten connections.

I've been using this keyboard and mouse combo for a few weeks now and I'm pretty happy overall. The typing feel is smooth and not too loud, which is great for late-night work. The mouse fits my hand well and the textured sides help with grip during longer sessions. I do wish the key legends were easier to see in dim lighting, so I usually keep my desk lamp on. For the price, the build quality is solid and the blue color looks sharp. A solid everyday set with one minor visibility trade-off.

Works perfectly in my four monitor system off of my laptop. It is not as sexy looking as its predecessor of the same model; blocky versus the sleek curves, but I don't see it so that doesn't bother me. One thing I wish Plugable would do is include software that places each icon in the program tray on the monitor it resides. I had an old program I used from Villagetronic called VT Multidisplay that did this and I loved it. Plugable would be GREAT if it would add this to its drivers.
love them work great and hold band on. like it so much gave it to a friend as a gift
I had bought a monitor and it never did 4K with the display cord I had. I then bought these and now 4K works on that monitor. Will keep buying these for our office.
I love it, the screen is amazing and programs open instantaneously.

Decent upgrade for casual listeners who hate short cords. The angled jack is a small but smart touch. Not audiophile-level, but totally fine for podcasts and commutes. I'd hand these to any friend still using stock buds.
The kickstand is surprisingly sturdy and makes hands-free video calls a breeze. I wasn't expecting such solid build quality at this price point.
I've had these products for a month now and I've experienced no foreseeable problems. The charger does as intended and the battery replacements are nifty when you don't have electricity {SEAsia}.

This is one of the draw backs of the Olympus Brand digital camera. The XD cards are more expensive than the SD cards and made me kind of shy away from the Olympus products. This adapter makes it very affordable for picture storage.

I held off on this for months because I wasn't sure the compact size would work for my setup, but it's been a solid upgrade. The key travel is shallow, which I actually prefer now for long typing sessions, less finger fatigue. Pairing with my MacBook was instant and I've had zero Bluetooth drops. I'd recommend it to anyone with limited desk space or who wants a cleaner look without a nest of cables. If you're coming from a chunky mechanical board, expect a brief adjustment period. My only gripe is that the back edges can feel a little sharp if you rest your palms there. A slim wrist rest fixed that for me.
I like these cable ties, because you can make them the size that you need. Be careful not to put them too tight. I did once, & the end broke off when I tried to untie it. They fit tight.

I'm an old shaky-hand gentleman who had absolutely no problem replacing my headband cushion on my QC-15. Bose offers no replacement at all, so, I chose this to try. I watched their link to a video, noted all cautions and replaced in less than 15 minutes. I was worried an after-market product might cause it to not fit into the case. No fear was needed; it fit perfectly. Fit my head perfectly. I now have a like-new QC-15.
This bag is the right size bag for my Sony HDR-XR500V camcorder, cables and other accessories (remote, spare battery and memory stick).

A great monitor for the money. We had ACER monitors where I worked several years ago, so I was confident that I would get a good product and I was not at all disappointed. I needed a space saver to replace an older tube monitor for our desk computer. The all black case looks very nice and clean on the desk. All cables were supplied...just plug and play! Picture is bright and easy to read text from many angles. So far I've only been using this monitor in the "normal" mode and haven't taken advantage of its 16:9 capabilties.

I've been using this cable for a few months now in my home studio setup, and it's held up great. No crackling or loose connections even with daily plugging and unplugging. The thick jacket gives me confidence it'll last. Honestly surprised by the quality for the price, would definitely buy again.
For under ten bucks this little box does the job cleanly. Signal quality is solid for the cost, and setup took two minutes. Knocked one star because the plastic housing feels a bit cheap.

I've been using this keyboard for work-from-home and honestly, it's a breath of fresh air. No weird curves, no unnecessary lights, just a solid, traditional layout that doesn't try to be something it's not. The keys have a satisfying, quiet press that makes long typing sessions easy on my hands. I'd recommend this to anyone who spends hours writing or coding and doesn't want the flashy gimmicks. It's also perfect for older relatives who just want a familiar, no-hassle keyboard that works right out of the box. Build quality feels sturdy, and I appreciate that it doesn't slide around my desk. Simple, comfortable, and reliable.

I picked this up for my compact ITX build after seeing some thermal numbers online, and honestly it exceeded every expectation. Installation was straightforward even in my cramped case. My GPU used to hover around 80 degrees during extended gaming sessions, but now it barely breaks 55 under full synthetic load. The build quality feels premium, no weird pump noise or coil whine, and it runs whisper quiet. I was a little skeptical about the price at first, but the temperature drop and headroom for overclocking make it a no-brainer. If you're fighting heat in a small chassis, this thing is a lifesaver.
Got this 4TB to back up my photography work and it's been flawless. Perfect for creative pros or anyone with a massive media library to stash.

I own a 2005 Cadillac CTS with a Bose system. If I were Bose, I would be ashamed to put my name on it as the factory speakers suck so badly. I replaced the sub with a Kicker and amp and the front door speakers with a pair of Infinity reference 6032cf's I got from Amazon. I went with these as they are 2 ohm just as the system speakers are. These speakers are incredible! With the sub dialed in to my base level preference the sound system now sounds like it should have with the name BOSE on it. These speakers are not meant to be a replacement base speaker, however they do an admirable job with it. The two position (0db - 3db)tweeter is phenomenal and the mids are so clear! Since I'm not trying to explode my kidneys or rock the car next to me with too much volume, I am more than pleased with Infinity. A word of caution here... the Infinity site says that the rear door speakers for the first generation CTS are 6.5"... Do NOT believe it. the rears are 5.25"... I had to return one set 6.5's and I'm waiting for the 5.25's to arrive so I can finish the install. Amazingly good speakers for the price and Amazon's free freight and fast delivery are great as well.
Snagged these for my nephew's first PC build. Super easy install and rock-solid performance so far. I'd absolutely point any first-time builder toward these without hesitation.

Keyboard cover review I bought this in grey to protect the keys of my 2017 MBA. Not from liquid spills; this would only offer protection from light splashes, not a full spill. But from dust and crumbs. The fit is really good, it lines up perfectly. It doesnt touch or damage the screen when the laptop is closed. Typing is harder, you have to press the keys harder and its a matte, somewhat tacky finish on the keyboard cover. This means traveling around the keyboard takes some getting used to. My only gripe is that the MacBook keyboard backlighting is seriously encumbered by this cover. The cover is not transparent where the characters on each key are labeled, meaning the light doesnt shine through. The only light you get is around each key. In a dim or dark area, its no good for seeing the keys. I like having the cover. I like how super thin it is and the protection it offers. Typing with any keyboard cover requires some getting used to. I really wish the letters and number on each key were illuminated, thats my only complaint.

Worked instantly on a MacBook Pro (mid-2017) while using it in conjunction with an Anker USB hub. No config needed and automatically defaulted to priority over the laptop's wireless connection. The only thing I don't like is the repeated blinking light when plugged into a USB hub when the laptop is no longer present.

I absolutely love these headphones, I am wearing them as I write this review. They may be the best headphones for under $30 on the market. They are not very bassy, but I don't listen to very high bass music anyway. They are great for listening to rock, classical, podcasts, and audiobooks though.

I've been using this tiny drive for a few weeks now and honestly forget it's on my keychain until I need it. Plugged it into three different laptops and transfers were snappy every single time. No hiccups with large video files either. Having 64 GB always within reach has saved me more than once. Solid little workhorse.

I bought this mainly for the rotating plug and wide outlet spacing, and it absolutely delivered. It feels sturdier than I expected for the price. I'm knocking off one star only because the LED indicator is brighter than I'd prefer in a dark room.
Fits perfectly and the buttons are still super clicky.
Months in and it's still rock-solid, no hiccups at all.
Upgrading old infrastructure of patched together cables, worn cables, and old cameras, the cost of these is perfect for the current project where additional length is needed.
i was really skeptical of this for $8.79, but the reviews convinced me to give it a try. It's great! Grab one before they are all gone.

I grabbed this to replace an aging router that kept dropping signal in my upstairs office, and the difference is night and day. What really sold me was the dual USB ports, I plugged in an external hard drive and now my whole family can stream movies and back up files without any extra hardware. Setup took maybe ten minutes, and the interface is clean enough that I didn't need to dig through forums. Streaming 4K content while my partner video conferences has been totally smooth, no buffering or lag spikes. Signal strength is solid through two floors, which was a pleasant surprise given our plaster walls. Honestly, it's been a set-it-and-forget-it experience.

I ordered this not even thinking about the chance of it not fitting on the front fans of my Antec 900 since there's 2 walls protruding on the sides of the front of the case. I got it to fit by cutting at it though. I wouldn't suggest doing it but hey, it works now!
Had these for a few months now, still going strong after daily drops and heavy use.
We bought this for our IT office. We've had this for just over a year. All the usb tongues have fallen off, its going into the garbage, poor quality.

This is the 2nd order. I returned the 1st order as it was defective as the battery would not hold charge and the battery is not accessible to replace. This unit works great. Not in the original packaging and not manual but it as all the cables that came with it originally. The manual is easily obtained on the Garmin website.

I grabbed this to store my old PSP and a handful of UMDs, and for the price it's a steal. The padding is decent enough to toss in a backpack without worry, and the mesh pocket holds the charger snugly. Solid little case that does exactly what I needed.

So far I've put up 4 of the cameras, and it works pretty well. The picture on the monitor is of good quality, both night and day. I've been very impressed by the quality of night vision through these cams. When I'm at home and my phone is connected to the home wifi, I can see the cams (both the grid, and each individual cam) on my phone just fine. I have NEVER, however, been able to access these images remotely, as was claimed in the description. (At LEAST one star off for that, as that's one of the main things I wanted to be able to do.) If somebody helps me be able to do that, I'll be glad to add back at least another star. At least one of the cables failed, which MAY have been partly my fault, but it just seems like they're pretty brittle and easily damaged. (Maybe really thin wire? Not sure.) I added a 2TB surveillance hard drive, and getting all of that hooked up was rather simple - no problems there. I was able to go rewind and see who got into my garage trash can (neighborhood stray dog), and we feel like just having the cams up is deterring whomever MIGHT have been getting stuff out of our garage freezer. Overall, I've been a little frustrated about a couple of things, but generally pleased with this purchase.
The wire was easy to work with as I ran 5 lines and the resultant sound was great. Must have for any home entertainment system.
Got this replacement cap for my Nikon D40X and honestly, it feels just like the original for way less money. Snaps on securely with zero wiggle.

I grabbed this monopod mostly for my kid's soccer tournaments, dragging my regular tripod across four fields in August heat was getting old fast. This thing is ridiculously portable, tucks right into the side pocket of my backpack. Setup takes maybe ten seconds, and the flip locks feel solid without being stiff. I've used it with both a mirrorless body and a heavier zoom lens and it held steady even on uneven grass. The wrist strap is a nice touch for extra security when I'm moving between shots. I'd honestly recommend it to any parent who wants better game photos without hauling bulky gear, or hikers who need something light and quick to deploy.

It's true the instructions are lacking, but it's one of those things that would be easier to learn by watching someone do it. All the people crying about it's difficult to do it without two people, probably have a chimney that's hard to reach, or aren't that bright, neither should reflect on the product itself. I installed them easily by myself in under 20 minutes.

I grabbed this stylus because I've had decent luck with Wacom stuff before, and the plug-in design seemed smart for keeping it with my iPad. For the first couple weeks, it felt solid and responsive. But after about three months of regular use, the rubber tip started showing noticeable wear, gets a little mushy and less precise, especially when sketching. The plug cap also loosened up, so now it sometimes pops off in my bag instead of staying securely in the headphone jack. It still works in a pinch, but I expected better durability for the price. Fine for light, occasional use, but if you're a daily note-taker or doodler, you might find yourself replacing it sooner than you'd like.
I added this to my new Samsung 55 LED TV. it was easy to install and setting up the wi-fi system was a snap. It's all fairly intuitive.

I'm happy with this little gadget overall. It handles my TV and soundbar switching smoothly for the most part, though occasionally it gets confused if a device wakes on its own. Still, setup was painless and it's been reliable enough to recommend.

These Ethernet patch cables are one foot in length and the ideal size for home patch panels. The shorter cables make your panel much cleaner looking. The boots on the cable ends make it easier to pull the cable without the release tab hanging on another cable.

For the price, I wasn't expecting much, but these little lenses are surprisingly crisp. I use them to inspect soldering joints on circuit boards, and the clarity is fantastic. No distortion at the edges. A total steal for the quality you get.
Works ok but doesn't has extended range. My AT&T Uverse router, that comes for free, has more range than this Router. Couldn't play HD movies on my laptop.
Perfect for my DIY drone project, clean 5V rail for the camera and gimbal. I'd recommend it to any hobbyist messing with robotics.

I've never spent more than $100 on a pair of headphones before, so I can't really compare these to other high end cans, but I absolutely love the accurate, high quality sound reproduction of these headphones! Materials quality and style are superb as well. After reading many reviews, I decided these would offer the best sound quality within my budget of less than $200, and I'm very happy with my purchase! Like other reviewers said, these offer very accurate sound reproduction. They have plenty of tight, punchy bass when it's recorded in the music, but it's not overpowering. If you're looking for exaggerated, skull-rattling bass, these probably aren't the phones for you. I use them to listen to Tidal lossless audio streaming, and the result is a stellar listening experience.
Had this mount up for over a year now with our 65-inch and it's rock solid, no sagging at all.
The Pencil is a great tool for artists and designers but also for amateurs, kids, and anyone who wants to sketch and draw, cartoon, paint with digital watercolors, or create diagrams easily.

Been using this board for about eight months now. It's held up okay physically, no dead slots or capacitor whine, but the M.2 quirks never fully went away. Feels solid, just wish the long-term software support matched the build quality.
Great Cable. Love it. Nothing more needs to be said.

I'm using this power supply in my HTPC setup and I love it. It's whisper silent which it great for when I'm watching movies on my TV. My old PSU sounded like a wind machine of some sort. I'm very impressed with this power supply and strongly recommend it to anyone looking for a quality PSU.
After a few months of light use, the plastic base started cracking near the hinge. It still works, but I doubt it'll survive another season outdoors.

Superb finish and a serious photography tool. This camera does almost everything one could ask for. It has few limitations, like no built in HDR, that are easy to get around using manual techniques. Both jpg and raw files are excellent. Highly recommend this camera if you are looking for a long term investment for a photography tool.
I expected it to sit a bit flatter on my desk, but the rotating design and individual port switches make up for that. Works fine with my daisy-chained hub.

Love how this thing feels under my fingers, soft but not mushy, and typing still has that subtle click response. Fit is spot-on, no sliding around. Honestly made my laptop way more enjoyable to use daily.

So I finally pulled the trigger on this thing and honestly wish I'd done it sooner. Setup was dead simple, even for someone like me who usually skims the manual. I'd absolutely tell a buddy who's into home improvement or just wants cleaner air in their workshop to grab one without overthinking it. My neighbor came over right after I unboxed it and immediately ordered his own. It's quiet enough that my wife doesn't complain, but powerful enough you notice the difference within an hour. If you've got a dusty garage, pets, or just hate sneezing while you tinker, treat yourself.

Definitely not commercial grade, but it worked fine for my house. I've used a small variety of keystone jacks, and they have all worked. If one side of the jack is not all the way in before putting the other side in, it can get wedged, which is a minor pain.
For the money, this little gadget is a steal. Battery life surprises me every time, and it's so light I forget it's in my bag.
Arrived two days early in perfect condition. Box was pristine, setup took five minutes, and the range is just ridiculous. This thing feels premium right out of the box.

Showed up in a padded envelope that looked a bit crushed on one corner, but the radio itself was fine. First power-on felt solid, knobs have a decent weight to them. Display is bright and easy to read. Not blown away yet, but not disappointed either. Gonna need more time to see if it was worth the impulse buy.
Great case for the price! Has plenty of room for a ASRock MB the supports an AMD Ryzen 1800+.

These are a fun little gadget. The light pattern is super crisp and the whole thing feels way more premium than I expected for the price. I've been using them mostly for night hikes and they throw a surprisingly wide beam without weighing down my head. Battery life has been solid so far, I got through a full weekend trip without needing a recharge. Only minor gripe is the strap adjustment can loosen up a bit if you're moving around a lot, so I find myself retightening more than I'd like. Still, for casual outdoor use or keeping in the glovebox, they're a steal. Definitely grabbing another set for the family cabin.

I've been through so many Bluetooth headsets that either hurt my ears or had terrible call quality. These are a pleasant surprise, super lightweight and the flexible neckband makes them easy to forget you're wearing them. Sound is crisp for both music and calls, and the battery easily lasts my workday. I'd recommend these to anyone who wants a reliable, no-fuss wireless option for commuting or working from home without breaking the bank.
a few problems but so far customer service has been great.
Arrived fast and packed really well. Felt premium right out of the box.
Nice and affordable fans. Pretty quite if you ask me. The glow is not overbearing but gives a nice blue glow to your computer.

I only purchased online if I know exactly what the product looks like. But this one,before I decided to put it in my cart I've been done through a lot of research not only on customer's review but also I've been trying to find this in a local store and unfortunately I can't see any store in my area sells a tripod like this brand. So just to make this review short,i will say this is a JACKPOT! because after i received and when I opened the box,the type of tripod that I've been dreaming of,is already in my hand.
The night vision on this dash cam is surprisingly clear, even in total darkness. Super easy install too.

I purchase a new ink-jet printer from a very well known computer store... I wonder who that might be... hehe! But getting back to the review... As most of you know, a lot of USB devices don't come with a USB cable. The same thing applied when I purchased my new printer. The clerk who sold me the printer suggested I purchase a USB cable along with my printer... the asking price: $29.95 for a 8 foot long cable. I told him "NO". Then purchased this cable on Amazon for $10.95 which included shipping. The only thing that got to me about this deal was that the shipping cost more than I paid for the USB cable. Oh well! I bought some sushi with the 19 bucks that I saved!

I wasn't sure a budget-friendly keyboard could actually feel this solid, but this one genuinely surprised me. The keys have a nice, quiet bounce and the layout is spot-on for all-day typing without fatigue. It's lightweight but stays put on the desk, and I appreciate how quickly I adjusted to it coming from a much pricier model. Setup was instant, no drivers or nonsense. For the price, I expected flimsy, instead it's sturdy, comfortable, and exactly what I needed for my home office.

I picked this up hoping for something versatile but not overly complicated, and it mostly delivers. The image quality is better than I expected for the price point, crisp shots even in lower light, which was a nice surprise. Build feels solid, though it's a bit bulkier than I'd pictured from the photos online. Battery life is decent, not stellar, but I can get through a day of casual shooting without scrambling for a charger. The autofocus can hunt a little when you're zoomed all the way in, but it's not a dealbreaker. Overall, it's been a reliable travel companion that punches slightly above its weight, minus a few minor quirks.

I use this mouse with an ASUS TF300T android tablet. I hate mouse pads, which is the only option my ASUS keyboard accessory has. I do alot of data entry and document creation with my tablet, and the lack of a good mouse slowed me down quite a bit. This little mouse paired within seconds (just hold the buttons all down at once, like the description says) and was working immediately. Setting for it are accessible in the Setting menu under Language and Input. It operates smoothly, and is comfortable in the hand. It pairs immediately everytime I turn it on, and it has brought me back up to my accustomed speeds. The only complaint I have, and the reason it gets 4 stars instead of 5, is it's texture. It's contoured to be comfortable to the hand, and it is. But it's also slick all over. There's no rubberizered finish or textured band to give you hand purchase. I've dropped it many times, and foresee going through a few replacements because of it. But for the price and performance, I can live with that.
Router constantly drops signal in the next room. Setup was easy but streaming buffers nonstop. For the price, I expected much better coverage. Pretty disappointed overall.

I purchased VIDBOX to convert our home videos from 8mm to disc. It was a time consuming endeavor, but it worked perfectly. The image was a bit grainy, but I was not sure if that was the fault of VIDBOX or some other factor. I am just glad I was able to get videos on disc for backup purposes.
This bag does the job if you're just hauling the camera body and a small lens. Fits snug, padding feels decent enough. Not much room for extras, but for minimalist shoots it works fine.
Way smaller than I expected but holds everything perfectly, super impressed.

I've been using this enclosure for a few months now and it handles everything I throw at it without a hiccup. The all-plastic body feels light but that actually makes it great for tossing into a backpack when I'm moving between home and office. I mostly keep a 1TB NVMe drive inside for quick backups and file transfers, and speeds have been consistent over USB-C. I'd definitely recommend this to students or anyone who needs an affordable, portable solution for repurposing an old SSD. Just take your time seating the drive fully and don't overtighten the screws, it's easy to work with once you get a feel for it. Solid little tool for the price.
Great product! Finally someone was smart enough to make such a good stylus with a fine point AND A POCKET CLIP!

I grabbed this little Haier for my kitchen counter so I can catch the morning news while making coffee, and it's been a solid addition. The setup was dead simple, just plugged it in, scanned for channels, and it was ready to go in minutes. Picture is crisp for a screen this size, and I appreciate that it doesn't dominate the counter space. Sound is passable for talk shows but a bit tinny if you're trying to enjoy music; I ended up connecting a small external speaker through the headphone jack and that solved it. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone needing a compact TV for a dorm room, RV, or small apartment. It's not a home theater replacement, but for casual, up-close viewing it punches above its weight.

I bought the Haier TV to replace my 10-inch Coby which inexplicably died less than a year after purchasing. The Haier TV is a vast improvement over the Coby model: it has a nice attractive look and actually takes up less room than the Coby did, although their screen sizes are identical. The picture quality is very impressive for such a little TV, although as other reviewers have mentioned, the audio quality is HORRIBLE. If you intend to watch this TV often, I would suggest investing in a nice inexpensive pair of computer speakers....but this may not be necessary for casual viewing of news or weather channels. I would have liked a USB port or memory card slot for viewing pics, etc., but all in all I am very happy with my purchase. Nice that it has a full-function remote too!

I've been using this case for a few weeks now and it's honestly perfect for anyone who's clumsy with their phone or just wants solid everyday protection. The reinforced edges have already saved my screen from a couple of drops on tile, and the fit is snug without being bulky. I'd especially recommend it to folks who read a lot on their phone or stream shows during commutes, the fold-out stand is sturdy and works great on desks and airplane trays. The magnetic closure feels premium, and I really like how the display wakes right up when you flip it open. If you're a parent handing your phone to a kid for cartoons, this case gives real peace of mind.

I've been lugging this thing on the subway daily for two months and it still looks brand new. The padded laptop sleeve holds my 15-inch machine snugly, and I can easily tuck in a charger and some folders without it bulging. Stitching is solid, zippers glide smoothly, and the reinforced base handles scuffs like a champ. Perfect work commuter bag.
great router, easy to set up and use. Really helped my whole network

Box was a little beat up when it arrived, no extra padding inside so the stand was just rattling around. First impression wasn't great, arms felt stiffer than expected and the plastic had a faint scuff already. Works okay for my 10-inch tablet but definitely wasn't a confidence-inspiring unboxing. Not terrible, just mediocre.

I really wanted to love this camera, but the experience has been nothing but frustration. Unboxing felt exciting until I actually tried to use the companion app. It's clunky, constantly disconnects, and the setup process had me resetting everything multiple times. I spent hours troubleshooting, thinking maybe my phone was the issue, but no, tried two different devices with the same result. The lag when it does briefly connect makes the feature pointless anyway. For this price point, basic functionality shouldn't be a gamble. I've owned cheaper point-and-shoots that handled wireless transfers more reliably. Honestly, it feels like a half-baked feature they slapped on a spec sheet to look competitive. Deeply disappointed and probably returning it.

Installation was a snap. However, I purposely purchased another pioneer deck to replace my existing one thinking I would not have to rewire the harness. I was wrong. Pioneer kept the same connector type but changed the wires around. But installation snafus aside this 70 dollar deck sounds better than the 300 dollar pioneer deck it replaced. Fewer fancier options but solid power out and the highs, mids and lows are solid. AUX in works like a champ with IPOD or any other mp3 player for that matter. Not a big fan of the RED lighting on the unit but it is not a deal breaker for me. If you are looking for a deck to push your speakers and on a budget like me... look no further this deck is NICE.
hard to get used to (alot of stuff on it) but it is the bomb! The bluetoooth is a great thing

Been running with this watch for awhile now and am very happy with it. I especially like all the fixes and enhancements that they have put into the watch since I started with it. I feel like they are really listening and adjusting the watch based on user feedback. There are a few things that I wish it did differently, however these are small issues and it handles the basic important functions I need (pace, avg pace, distance, heart rate.... etc.) very well. The main display is easy to read and you can scroll up/down to get other information while you run. The two smaller displays at the top are a bit small (I had to buy a pair of sunglasses with "cheaters" built in - yes, I'm over 40). It does find the satellites very quickly when you start your run from the same place each time, however does take longer (maybe a minute or more) if you start from someplace else. This seems to be common among gps watches however.

This GPU is an absolute beast for creative workflows. Threw it into my older Mac Pro and Premiere Pro renders are now laughably quick. I'd strongly recommend this to video editors, 3D artists, or anyone deep in the Adobe ecosystem who needs serious CUDA acceleration without building a whole new rig. Just a heads up: you won't see the boot picker, so grab BootChamp if you switch OSes often. Once you're past that, it's smooth sailing and totally worth it for the performance jump.
Arrived super fast in a nice little box. Slipped them on and instantly my headset felt plush and brand new again.

Doesn't seem to work well with Mac devices. My USB 3.0 hard drive keep connecting and disconnecting repeatedly. The charging ports seem to work fine but the layout of the ports on this are terrible. The right front and rear ports charge and provide connectivity and the front left ports charge only. This is problematic for my desk as the cut through for cable is on the left side.... where the charge only ports are located. Thus, I have to drag cables across my desk to plug them in making my desk look messy. The whole point of this was to allow me to "hide" my laptop under the unit, place a monitor on top and then route all of the cables in a clean manner. I will tinker with it a little more but if I cannot get it to stop this connecting/disconnecting to stop I might have to return the unit and look for another alternative.

These arrived in a padded envelope with each drive sealed in its own little compartment, no rattling around. Popped the first one into my laptop right out of the box and it lit up immediately, recognized within seconds. The sliding mechanism feels sturdy, not flimsy like I half-expected. Already backed up two years of family photos and it barely made a dent in the storage. For the price and how fast they showed up, I'm genuinely impressed.

I wanted to love this case, and at first glance I really did, the material feels premium and it fits my device snugly. But after about two months of normal use, the small details started to slip. The magnetic closure isn't as reliable as I'd hoped; the magnets shift under the surface and now there's a visible bump beneath the outer layer. Also, the edges where the cover folds have begun to lift slightly, showing a gap between the outer material and the inner shell. It's not falling apart yet, but for the price I expected better durability. It's okay for light use, just know it might not stay pristine very long.

Very fast little SoC. If you go with the eMMC option like I did, be aware that you'll likely need to get the dongle converter from Transcend in order to work with the chip outside of the native OS itself on another system. Having USB 3 onboard is certainly a plus and YES you can set one of these up as a DIY NAS. I have 2 x 10Tb drives in span mode which are working just fine for networked NAS.
This is the best option i've found for wiring a speaker-level output from a receiver to an RCA style input on an amp. The female to female connectors will come in handy too.

I expected a solid tripod for the price, but the flimsy plastic head wobbles constantly. Forget about getting a stable shot, my camera kept tilting forward no matter what. The recessed screw is a joke; I had to hunt down a coin just to attach anything. Total letdown.
It does what it supposed to do, but it is not any better or clearer than the protective layer that already comes on the Google Nexus 10.

Great for anyone wanting solid everyday earbuds. They stay put during light workouts, and battery lasts my whole shift. Mic picks up fine indoors, but wind kills it outside. Perfect for commuters or desk use.
Great product! It is highly reliable, durable! And affordable prices touched my heart! I would recommend this product to a friend!

I purchased 5 of these drives to build an 8TB RAID5 array (onboard RAID on the motherboard). They were 2010 vintage drives so the first thing I did was update the firmware on them. (I had all the drives connected and booted from a USB stick flashed with FreeDOS and the firmware update...it flashed each in turn, so I didn't need to swap out and reboot for each drive) Everything was fine for about a week and then the RAID management software notified me that one of the drives had failed. I notified Amazon and they sent out a replacement drive right away. One thing about RAID5 arrays...making changes takes forever. It took a 2 days to initialize my array and 4 days to rebuild it after replacing the failed drive. A week later the RAID management software came up with an error indicating that the replacement drive could not be found. Turns out the SATA connector had come loose. I immediately ordered new SATA cables with the metal clips on the end that lock the connector in place. Overall I'm very happy with these drives. They're fast enough to stream HD material throughout the house, they're absolutely quiet, and the price is outstanding. My only real concern is the 20% failure rate, but Amazon really came through quickly with a replacement.

I grabbed this mainly for hotel travel and honestly wasn't expecting much at this price point. Set it up in under five minutes in a spot where the room only had a wired jack, boom, my phone and laptop both connected with zero fuss. Signal strength held steady across the room with no drops during a two-hour video call, which surprised me. It's tiny enough to toss in a backpack pocket without adding any noticeable weight. For what you pay, the performance feels like a steal compared to pricier travel routers I've tried. If you need a straightforward way to create your own Wi-Fi on the go without spending a lot, this little thing absolutely delivers.
I've dropped these on concrete twice and they still work perfectly for chatting across big stores.
Great product and works as it should. Haven't had any problems. I have several of these for storing music and pictures on.

Pro: Nice exterior, can view iPad 2 in portrait or landscape, secure strap Con: Although 3 positions for portrait or landscape, the iPad will slip from one of the slots depending on view position. My usage: The screen cover was good, but make sure it is very clean of lint before applying. The pen is very light, but does the job.

Runs about 50 degrees celcius under full load. I get 50-55 fps while playing the division (beta) completely maxed out on a 1440p gsync monitor. So far its runs every game fully maxed out anywhere from 50 to 100+ fps. I get 99fps PVZGW 2 58-60 fps witcher 3 70+ Dying light 70+ siege All this with only the factory clock settings as well
Junk. Stopped working after using it once. Good luck trying to figure out the warranty.

It does what it is supposed to do. I am a little concerned in the packaging of the product as it appeared to have been opened. There were the included cables still in plastic so I am not sure if this was box was just opened or it was a used product repackaged. The only other issue is that the polarized plugs really were difficult to plug in. I had to use the blade of a screwdriver to get them all the way flush into the surge protector on the standard size outlet side. No issues on the large outlets for the power adapters.

Got this amp last spring. Ran it with a pair of 6x9s and a single 10-inch sub on 4-gauge wiring. Sounded pretty solid for the first few months. Then one channel started randomly dropping out during longer drives, especially when it got warm outside. The gain knob also got scratchy and hard to dial in cleanly. It never fully died, just got annoyingly inconsistent. I'd only recommend this to someone on a super tight budget who only does short trips and doesn't mind tinkering with connections now and then. If you rely on your system daily, spend a bit more elsewhere.

I've had this camera for about five months now and overall it's been a solid daily companion. The image quality punches above its weight, colors look natural and the autofocus locks onto faces reliably, even when my toddler is darting around. Video clips come out smooth enough for family memories. I've accidentally knocked it off a table once and it shrugged it off with just a tiny scuff. Battery life is decent for a day out, though I wish the charger was a bit more compact. The slightly bulky battery hump took some getting used to, but I've learned to work around it. For the price, it's a dependable little shooter that handles most situations well.
Got this one as a replacement for another model that arrived in no working condition and it works great.

Bought this for my old truck's sun visor. The material started fraying at the edges after about a month, and one of the elastic bands lost its stretch. It still holds CDs for now, but I'm not convinced it'll last through summer heat. Okay for light use, but durability is iffy.

Arrived a day early in a sturdy little box with zero damage, always a relief with electronics. Unboxing felt premium: the unit was snug in molded foam, the cord was neatly coiled, and there was even a clear peel-off film protecting the display. First impression was surprisingly sleek for something I'll mostly tuck behind my monitor. Plugged it in, the soft glow came right on, and it's whisper-quiet. Setup took maybe 90 seconds, and I instantly noticed the air felt less stale after an hour. If you're on the fence, the packaging alone tells you they actually care about the product arriving in perfect shape.
Everything included; worked as advertised. MUCH better than using flashdrives or DVDs.

Great USB cable. I tested power transfer and there were no concerns there. 6 feet cables are my new best friend - they make life more manageable when charging your phone/laptop/tablet - 3 feet is an odd length really. This cable comes packaged in a small bag plastic bag - great for personal use, not gift presentation worthy in my opinion though. Overall, a great 6 foot cable - seems solid, I expect it to last for quite awhile. Pros: it works, good power transfer, solid connections - minorly snug, so I don't think they'll start falling out of plugs anytime soon. Cons: none that I can think of. I received a free product in exchange for an honest and unbiased review.

I've been using this keyboard and mouse combo for about six months now, and it's held up way better than I expected. I game most evenings and also use it for work during the day, so it gets a ton of mileage. The keys still feel snappy with no fading or stickiness, even the ones I hammer on constantly like WASD. The mouse is just as solid, no double-click issues or scroll wheel weirdness. I did swap in some custom keycaps for a brighter look, and the board handled them perfectly. For the price, the long-term durability really surprised me.

These are the absolute best. I've tried them all. I don't understand why they won't fix the consistent flaw in the upper left bracket surrounding the power button. It is well documented and cheaper ones are able to make a bracket that doesn't break. I keep buying these despite flaw because it is the easiest, most natural to type with. I'm using it to write this review. I don't have a laptop so this makes my iPad one. I wish they would make a model with backlit keys. I really don't understand why the flaw and no backlit option from an otherwise great product

I had first purchased a much smaller bag thinking that I kid meet my needs, but it wasn't even large enough to hold two charging cords. The depth of this bag allows me to bring all related cords for my three devices with me when I travel.
For the money, this tiny fan is surprisingly powerful.

I've been using this as my daily driver adapter for my Yoga 13 at work for about five months now, and it's held up really well. I toss it in my bag constantly, and the cable hasn't frayed or shown any weak spots near the connector, which is usually the first thing to go on these. The brick itself has survived a few drops off my desk onto hard floor with just some minor scuffs, no performance issues at all. Charging is consistent and it doesn't get weirdly hot even after being plugged in all day. For the price, it's been a solid little workhorse. Definitely a reliable spare or primary charger if yours bites the dust.

Works well, Two issues. It is impossible to focus on higher magnifications without having the object being looked at up inside the opaque plastic cone. Also the light is not dimmable and is sometimes too bright.

I have UAG cases for my iPhone and iPad mini. Given my positive experience with them, it was a no-brainer to get one for the iPad Pro. Their version for the 9. 7" model is attractive, light, but well-engineered, useful, and protective like all the UAG cases I own. I like the dark blue because it isn't another all black case but it isn't an overly flashy color either. I like to cruise just under the radar but still have some style.;) Functionally, the case fits well, all ports and buttons are easy to access, it is well protected both opened and especially when closed, and the FrogSkin cover is easy to grip and simple to clean. The cover is easily adjustable and the stand can be detached, it is compatible with a keyboard, it has a "Smart" cover to put the device in sleep mode, and there is a stylus holder (see photo). It is fairly light given it's size and doesn't add as much bulk as one might think to your iPad Pro. All in all, this is another fantastic case/cover from UAG. I did receive a complimentary unit for an unbiased review, but honestly they would have been my first choice for purchase anyway. They have a loyal customer in me though I would be sure to let you know if they dropped the ball in any way with their products.

I've had these for about five months now, and honestly, I'm pretty let down by how they've held up. The headband cushion started peeling way sooner than I expected, little black flakes end up on my desk every time I use them. The ear pads also got really stiff and uncomfortable after a few weeks, and the faux leather is cracking already. Sound-wise they were okay at first, but now the left driver rattles at moderate volume, which makes bass-heavy tracks unlistenable. For something I paid this much for, I figured they'd at least survive daily desk use without falling apart. Not impressed with the long-term quality at all.
Item came quickly and was well packaged. Got this as a gift and the recipient loves the tablet and its versatility. Very satisfied.

The magnetic closure is strong and keeps the case shut nicely, but the plastic shell cracked near the volume buttons after a couple weeks of normal use. It still holds the iPad, but I'm disappointed it didn't last longer.

This is a super cute 8GB Frog shaped USB flash Drive. Words cannot express how CUTE this thing is!!! It also comes with a little keychain type hook, so you can connect it to your keys or a lanyard. This is: Compatible with Windows 7, Windows XP, Windows Vista, Mac OS 10+ and Linux; Hi-Speed USB 2.0 port required for high speed transfer. I think this is a great deal for how much GBs you get! The ONLY complaint I have is that it did NOT fit in my Xbox. My spouse wanted to use it for extra storage for the xbox gaming system. Because of the frog body, it would not fit in the console. It does fit in my desktop though, so Im sure Ill get use out of it for work or home storing photos etc. I purchased this product at a discount for me to test and review. I am not affiliated with this company and make no promises of a good rating while reviewing the product. I test the product thoroughly and do my best to provide detailed reviews to other Amazon customers so they may make informed decisions.
Serves it's purpose. Nice inside compartment to put cable and other things. Not Bulky, pretty much the same size as the speaker itself.
Showed up a day early in a neat little box. Felt solid right away, clip's sturdy and the tip glides perfectly.

This arrived a day early in a neat little box with zero wasted plastic, just a simple cardboard insert holding everything snug. Pulling it out felt oddly satisfying; the weight and matte finish give it a quietly premium vibe. Plugged it in, all ports lit up instantly, and my desk suddenly felt way more organized before I even sat down.
The thick shielding on this Tripp Lite HDMI cable completely fixed the annoying signal dropouts I had with my old one.
well what can I say It works, i got it fast, not much else really to say.
Perfect pocket size, crisp clear image, exceeded my expectations.

Easy to run from the tailgate of a Jeep Wrangler. True you have to solder the end but it was super easy with a solder iron. It's actually a good thing because with out the connector installed its much easier to run the cable through tight spots. The instructions stink so check our firestik.com for more detailed instructions. Nice clean finish and final results yielded perfect SWR readings.

Bought this thing thinking I'd use it daily in my old truck. After maybe two weeks of light use, the housing started coming apart and the internal gears now grind like crazy. The audio cuts in and out unless I hold the cord at a weird angle. Straight-up junk, feels like it's designed to self-destruct. Save your money.

As others have said, they are a bit tinny, however, for my relatively simple needs, they're fine. I got them to replace an old pair of speakers for my desktop. They're even adequate for music for non-serious listeners, but the bass, as has been said, almost disappears. I also wanted something relatively small, due to limited desktop space, and these certainly are---the other speakers certainly weren't huge, but these really do give me some extra space. Lastly, as has also been said, the instructions talk about two cables, a green and a pink, which is usually for input with a microphone. However, there's only the green cable, which is fine. Plug it into the input jack, plug the normal size power cord into an outlet, and you're good to go. (The fact that it's a standard 2 prong plug, rather than a large brick like my old speakers, was a further bonus. ) 4 rather than 5 because of the tinniness, but for low cost, adequate computer speakers for those who aren't really hard core audiophiles, they can't be beat. (Also, they are somewhat nicer looking than the illustration, which has also been said.

THIS IS A GOOD REVIEW (all negativity ends to be a positive ending), I give it 5 stars! I had a MAJOR lightning storm that killed about 5 of my electronics, including my desktops network card, which is built into the motherboard. When I found out it wasn't working, I went searching on Amazon for a new one (since Amazons merchandise is always on sale, or just plain cheap, money wise) and found this. I kept a tab on this open and looked at other, but my decision came back to this because of how cheap it was, and the good reviews, AND a similar story to mine someone else said (lightning storm, built into modem, etc. ) When I purchased it, I completely forgot about it until it arrived today. I soon hooked it up, excited and all. As soon as I power it on, I got that Limited but No Connectivity. I went through EVERYTHING trying to fix it, over an hour and a half. Nothing. I went out and came back and still nothing. So I looked up online problems that could relate to this. After about 3 websites, I came across a forum comment saying to download a tool called WinsockXPFix, after opening this small app and 'Fix'ing it, it worked. And now I'm sooooo happy, enough to get back on here, and write a review. I also found that little app just replaces missing files old programs I uninstalled, took with them. I wrote about this program for others who have any network problems relating to this Limited but No Connectivity issue.
I wasn't sure what to expect at this price, but it glides way better than I thought it would. Super quiet and feels way more premium than it costs.
Purchased specifically for a GoPro hero 3+ Black. It's on the GoPro recommended SD Card list and is capable of recording 4K video. Very fast.
The autofocus hunts a lot in low light, which gets frustrating. The build quality feels solid for the price, though. Okay for casual snaps but not reliable for anything serious.

Very nice adjustable load for USB. I got it mainly for verifying capacity of battery packs and battery banks. With a 3-5V input, to 5V/1A output USB adapter and a stop watch I can verify just much Li-ion are OVER rated. (I'll not go down in history for the discovery) With it set for 1amp load the fan keeps the heat sink ambient temperature. Great "E" toy, just what us old retired VTVM folks need for entertainment.
Bought these for an iPhone 4. They sound great, fit comfortably, and the button works intuitively. Recommended as a nice replacement for iPhone/iPod.

The genuine article, as promised. You never know when there are so many knockoffs out there. This is an item where the cost of the genuine Dell thing wasn't much higher than the cheapo knockoffs. I always make sure the seller for this sort of thing is Amazon.com.
Great durable cables! I need to order more, these cables perform if not better than expensive cables. No signal loss at all.
Charging speed was great, but it gets dangerously hot after 30 minutes.
This mount was so easy to install. It was for my 55 inch TV..
These sound way better than I expected for the price, clear, full bass, and they fit really comfortably. Definitely worth it.

I picked this up hoping for a pocketable travel companion, and the compact size is definitely a win. Image quality in decent light is solid, with colors that pop without looking overprocessed. I do wish the grip was a bit more substantial, my fingers feel cramped after a long shooting session. The Wi-Fi transfer works smoothly once you get past the clunky initial setup, and I've been using it to quickly share shots to my phone.
