These jacks did the job for my home network setup without any issues. I've punched down a handful of them now and connectivity has been solid across the board. The plastic feels a little light, but for the price, I'm not complaining. Just take your time seating the wires and you'll be fine.
Perfect for night owls sharing a room. My roommate's a light sleeper and has no clue I'm gaming past midnight. Definitely recommend for couples or dorm life.

So far (had it for almost a week) very please with this case. It arrived in a very timely manner, and the keyboard charges and pairs with the iPad Mini as advertised. The sleep/awake iPad function works ok so far, though it needs to be lined up properly to work, and this happens without too much trouble. The materials and workmanship are good. For the price, I would say you get what you paid for and would meet most people's expectations.
one of the best earbuds for the price you pay

I cut this device a lot a slack in terms of criticism because it was so inexpensive (under $40). It works as described and, with a little tedious fine tuning, it delivers a pretty clear picture. But, it could be a lot better. The camera lens zoom and focus is pre-set and not adjustable. The only way to change the zoom is to physically move the camer back or forth. This makes finding a place to hide the camera much more difficult and challanging. The mounting bracket also adds to the challenge--it doubles the height of the unit, and is intended to be mounted with the included, tiny wood wood screws. Using the the knob on the receiver to fine tune the reception from the transmitter is what migraines are made of--keep the Tylenol handy. It's similar to trying to fine tune the dial on an am clock radio with week reception. When used with a Slingbox this camera makes a pretty neat nanny cam--the live feed can be viewed from any web browser or from a smart phone (e.g. iphone, blackberry) and can be recorded to PC if desired.
This is perfect for what I needed!!! Love the ability of this little speaker! I can use USB to play my music, FM Radio or Bluetooth. I haven't found a speaker that is able to do so much.
Got as gift for son's phone - well made - great protection
Perfect for shared workspaces, my coworkers keep borrowing it. I'd recommend it to anyone in a busy office who needs reliable desk gear.

Got these mainly for running and they stay put like crazy. The ear hooks do exactly what they're supposed to, zero slipping even when I'm drenched in sweat halfway through a 10k. Honestly kind of forgot I was wearing them. Pairing was instant right out of the case and the range is solid, I leave my phone in the gym bag and walk across the whole weight room without a stutter. Battery life surprised me too, I've gone almost a full week of workouts without charging. Sound is clean and punchy enough to keep me motivated, no complaints at all. For the price these are an absolute steal.
I love how the bass doesn't distort even at max volume, makes movie nights way more immersive. Tiny enough to toss in my bag, but the sound totally fills the room.

I grabbed this to sort out a dead zone in my upstairs office where my old router just couldn't keep up with video calls. Setup took maybe ten minutes and I didn't have to fiddle with any settings. Now my connection is rock solid even when the kids are streaming and my wife is on Zoom. I'd absolutely suggest this to anyone in a multi-level home or folks with a basement workspace that's been a Wi-Fi headache. It's also a great fit for renters who can't run ethernet everywhere but still want wired reliability for a console or smart TV without tearing up the walls.

I wasted an evening trying to flash a basic PIC16F chip and never got past the device-not-found errors. The drivers installed fine, but the programmer refuses to talk to anything. I honestly can't recommend this to anyone who values their time, unless you enjoy troubleshooting badly cloned hardware with zero documentation. Maybe it's okay for someone who just wants a paperweight shaped like a programmer, but if you actually need to program microcontrollers, spend the extra on a genuine Pickit.

I grabbed this on sale and honestly wasn't expecting much given the price, but it's been a gem. Autofocus locks on instantly, even with my dog darting around the yard, and the burst shooting lets me actually catch the moment instead of missing it by a second. Battery life has been solid for long day trips too. For what I paid, the image quality feels like a steal, colors pop without much tweaking. It's made photography genuinely fun again without breaking the bank.
Did not correctly fit my 20" screen. If it fit I would have kept it.
Just as good as the battery that comes with the GoPro but cheaper and you get two. I have not tried to charge the official battery in the charger but the Wasabi batteries charge in the GoPro fine.
Perfect for anyone wanting reliable, long-lasting earbuds for daily use.
great headphones. sounds good and loud as I like it. a must buy!!! the headphones comes with three different size rubbers and a case for storage very nice.

Pros. 1. Feels like 'quality'. has some heft to it. gives you a warm, fuzzy when you first take it out of the box. 2. Lots of accessories. everything you need to get started vice a SD card is in the box. including a short HDMI cable. Cons. 1. Terrible video quality. just terrible. grainy and washed out. you will not be able to read a license plate of a car parked just in front and to the left or right of you. 2. Operation is not intuitive. crazyest sequence of button pushes I've ever experienced. the 'OK' button is not always 'Ok'. button pushing is on par with entering a cheat code in a video game console. I. e. UP, DOWN, MODE, UP, OK, MENU, MODE, UP, DOWN, UP, UP. argghh. 3. Freezes constantly. tape a paper clip to the side of it as your going to need to reset it constantly. 4. Received a used or open box unit. everything was there, but there were fingerprints all over the lense, the instruction manual was all bent to crap, the protective film and zip lock bags the originally held the pieces where all at the bottom of the box. This was returned (and would have been anyway since it was pre-owned/used). I have ordered a main-stream GARMIN to take its place.

Arrived a day early in a simple plastic bag, no damage. First thing I noticed, these are way thicker than expected, almost overbuilt for small desk cables. The velcro grabs instantly and holds tight. A little stiff to wrap at first, but after a few uses they soften up. Nice having so many in one pack; I've already organized three drawers.

I've been using this router for a few months now and it's held up better than I expected for the price. I'd say it's a solid pick for someone who just needs basic, no-fuss Wi-Fi in a smaller apartment or as a backup unit. Setup wasn't totally plug-and-play for me, the quick-start guide skipped a few steps, so I ended up digging into the manual config page. Once I got it dialed in, signal's been stable and speeds are fine for streaming and casual gaming. I wouldn't push it hard with a ton of devices or expect blazing range, but for light-to-moderate home use, it does the job. Recommended for budget-conscious folks comfortable with a little tinkering.
Fits perfectly and the touch sensitivity is great, but the application process is a bit tricky.
Not the easiest protector to install but once it's on you'll barely know it's there

I expected an easy wireless setup, but the connection kept dropping every few minutes. Tried moving it closer, resetting it, nothing helped. It's basically unusable for my home office. Really disappointed compared to the simple USB adapter I had before.
right side of head phones just stopped working and the ear buds keep falling out.

I purchased the H80 model when I decided to upgrade to a better case. I was unfamiliar with liquid cpu coolers, so I didn't really know what to expect. It comes with several different fittings, so you can be sure that it will fit on your motherboard. The important thing to do when researching these is to make sure you have the correct space to fit the radiator. I opted for the H80, and I'm glad I did. It seems to be very well made, I checked all the seams to make sure there were no leaks, and I checked the hoses for any cuts, and all was in perfect order. The included instructions are kind of a joke, there a are only pictures showing the different setup for the various fittings, they don't actually explain how to install it, but it is pretty self explanatory if you have some tech sense. All in all I am pretty happy with the purchase, I have had no problems with it, and it has kept my cpu cooler than my old cpu fan, even during long gaming sessions and photo and video editing. The one thing that makes me give it a four star rating instead of a five star rating are the fans and the connectors. The connectors are 3 pins instead of 4, which means the fans will run on full sail the entire time. They end up sounding like a jet engine, and was so loud I could hear it downstairs through my floor. I bought a NZXT fan controller, and was able to turn them down. The noise is much more acceptable. Corsair could and should have spent the little bit more money and made the connectors 4 pin.
Worked fine for a couple months, then the zoom ring got so stiff I could barely turn it. Cheap build, total waste.
Liked everything but the brush, it was cheap and fell apart

I bought this antenna in hopes of receiving more OTA TV. But ended up only getting about 2 channels that didn't seem to work half the time. I switched to the Winegard Metrostar 2000, and have had a lot better success.

I grabbed this to hook up an older Dell monitor with only DVI input to my newer workstation. The latch mechanism on the DisplayPort end is a standout, it clicks in securely and stays put, no wiggling loose when I'm rearranging my desk. I've had zero flickering or signal dropouts at 1920x1200, and text looks razor-sharp. Cable itself is flexible enough to route cleanly behind my stand without fighting me. Didn't need any adapters or driver fiddling, just plug and go. For a straightforward single-cable solution that just works, this thing is perfect. Highly recommend if you're reviving a solid DVI monitor.
Not for what it said, does not fit Samsung Galaxy s6 edge plus as stated.

Cable works great and appears to be of high quality. My one issue with it is the video wire is thicker than the audio wires, making it stiffer which created a tendency to pull on one of the audio jacks so it would come loose from the plug. I positioned the cable in a way that provided enough slack in the audio wires, but it was a nuisance to deal with.

Thought I'd save some cash skipping the brand-name glass, but this thing is a total letdown. Edges are dark and mushy no matter the aperture, colors look like they've been through a muddy filter, and anything off-center is just blur soup. Tried it on two bodies, same sad results. Honestly, I'd only recommend this lens to someone who needs a prop for a film set or a paperweight, definitely not for actual shooting. Even beginners deserve better; you'll just end up frustrated and re-buying the real deal anyway. Lesson learned the expensive way.

I've been using this for about four months now and it's still rock solid. My old setup would choke on basic tasks, but this upgrade just keeps chugging along without any hiccups. Totally breathed new life into my machine for way less than I expected.

All the zippers have broken off. Awful build quality. Poorly organized. Both the worst backpack AND the worst laptop bag I've ever owned. Also, there is basically zero extra room in the bag. You have space for a large laptop, and maybe a power brick. A couple pens, if you're lucky. Overall, it's just awful. I hate this thing and wish I'd never spent money on it.

I grabbed this little Canon on a whim when it went on sale, and honestly it's been one of the best bang-for-the-buck gadgets I've bought in ages. For what I paid, the image clarity is seriously impressive, colors pop without looking fake, and even shots in tricky lighting come out way better than expected. It slips into a jeans pocket so I actually bring it everywhere, which means I'm taking way more photos than I ever did with my bulky old camera. Battery life is solid for a day of casual shooting, and the menus are straightforward once you poke around for five minutes. If you want a dependable point-and-shoot that won't make your wallet cry, this is it.
Used this thing on a dusty camping trip for two weeks straight. The slots still snap in tight and it handled all my batteries without ever overheating. Solid little charger.

I bought these as a replacement for an older pair of Sony earbuds. I don't know if this pair will eventually present the same problem, but my experience with Sony earbuds has been very mediocre. First the good. The sound is decent, though nothing special, and the fit is pretty good. They are very comfortable and don't fall out, even when I'm jogging and sweaty. My issue with Sony earbuds is that (with every pair I've owned over the last 10 years) the sound in one ear eventually goes out, never to return. Then they're garbage. My friends have all reported having the same problem and none of us has been able to figure out what causes this problem. It does not appear to be something you can control by treating the earburs with special care of any sort. I personally believe that Sony designs them to die on you, so that you'll constantly have to keep spending another $20 to replace them. For my next pair (when these inevitably meet their one eared fate), I'm thinking of going higher end for a pair of earbuds that will last. Do I recommend these as a pair of disposable earbuds that may give you 6 good months? Yes. Do I recommend them for longer? No.
It is a fake arduino, it works but is not genuine.. Also, the reset button don't works, I had to use the one in a modular board I connected on top of if

I grabbed this for a small weekend tinkering project and it turned out to be a total steal. Clamps down solid without any fuss. If you're a maker, hobbyist, or just need a reliable spare in your toolbox, you'll wonder why you ever spent more. Perfect for casual workbench setups.
For what this costs, I expected way more than flimsy plastic that snaps instantly.
Cute device, it has a little blue lite in the handle to let you know that you have it pluged in properly.

Got this case expecting decent quality, but it blew me away. The build materials feel premium, way sturdier than I anticipated for the price. Cable management was a breeze with all the tie-down points and channels. Airflow is fantastic, keeping my rig cool under load. Honestly, it's better than cases I've used that cost twice as much.

I held off on this tripod for weeks because I like to get hands-on with gear before buying, but none of the local shops near me stocked it. Finally took the plunge after watching a bunch of video breakdowns and reading spec comparisons, and honestly, I'm kicking myself for waiting so long. Unboxing felt premium right away, everything from the leg locks to the ball head moves with a smooth, confident action. Setup takes seconds, even on uneven ground, and the stability blew me away during a windy sunset shoot. My overall experience has been flawless, and this thing has instantly become the backbone of my everyday kit.
Got these for my nephew's old iPod Touch and he loves them. Perfect for kids or anyone who just wants simple, reliable earbuds.
These cables are bulletproof, top quality and very well made.

Not a bad little device. I have owned this MP3 player for several days now and it is everything I expected it to be. Loading music is astonishingly easy. the 15 GB of storage will take me quite a while to fill up. There is an SD card slot that I don't expect that I will ever have reason to use. I did not test the device with an SD card but I shook it down in every other way. I've dropped it a few times (not intentionally) and it survived intact. The audio quality is fine and the radio adequate. I mostly listen to my local college station which is within ten miles of my home, it plays that very well. The scroll through menu is logical and easy to read. My only negatives were that it is virtually impossible to read in bright sunlight and the menu when going from MP3 player to other features is not as straightforward as I would like. Also MP3 player is the default mode. If you turn it off set to the radio, when you turn it on again it will be set in MP3 mode. That is not really a bug, but it is a bit of an annoyance. All around it is well worth four stars.

Like many other reviewers, I can confidently rate this tablet with at least 4 stars...provided you're a geek who is comfortable rooting your devices and installing new ROMs. As nearly anyone who has used this tablet will tell you, out of the box it's complete crap. How Viewsonic managed to so badly mangle the operating system is beyond me. But...spend just a couple minutes on the xda forums to find the instructions for how to root, spend another couple of minutes performing the root and ROM installation (and, for extra credit, install the Pershoot kernel), and you'll have a tablet that is lightening quick and capable. I am currently running the latest VEGAn ROM (Gingerbread flavored) with the Pershoot kernel overclocked to 1.4 GHz. With a couple of battery-draining apks (applications) deleted, I'm still seeing amazing battery life and applications launch immediately. A couple of notes if you're one of those people who has been slobbering after the latest "fruity" tablet... This ain't no iPad. But that's only because the Android OS is still transforming from it's caterpillar form into the beautiful butterfly it will become. You'll trade "pretty" for "powerful" since a rooted tablet like this one will give you full access to all of the amazing (and often experimental) apps in the Android Market. Another ding: accessories. I picked up the USB keyboard/case for this device on amazon (for about $25) and love it. It adds amazing functionality with complete plug-and-play ability. But...that's about the *only* case you'll find for it besides the generic sleeves for 10" tablets (I also got one of those). I would be remiss if I didn't mention that the dreaded poor viewing angle for the screen often annoys me. It's true that you could potentially claim this is a "privacy" feature, but don't be fooled. I've often had to tweak the angle to get a comfortable non-eye-strain producing view of the screen for long periods. Lastly, the front-facing camera (the only one) stinks. But, at a price half to a third of similar tablets on the market, I'm OK with that and the other limitations I mentioned.
Pure and simply, this is a kindle charger. Its nice to have if you like to plug your kindle into a wall socket to charge.

Great camera. I can use this to take pictures of my family and save the cost of having them professionally done. Captures everything you need. Nothing bad about this camera at all. Excellent lens to go with this camera.

I bought this to replace a five-year-old unit that kept dropping our laptops. For the price, I expected a bump in speed but honestly didn't think the range would improve much, happy to be wrong. I'm getting usable signal out in my detached garage, which is a good 40 feet from the house through some thick walls. Setup took maybe ten minutes; the admin panel is clean and I was able to set up a guest network without digging through confusing menus. Only knock is that the 2.4GHz band can get a little congested when all the smart home gadgets are active, but it hasn't actually disconnected. Solid upgrade overall.

I wanted to love this mouse, it feels great in hand and the side buttons are handy for work. But after about five months of daily use, the left click started acting up. Sometimes a single click registers as a double, which is maddening when you're trying to select text or drag files. I'm not a heavy gamer, just regular office stuff, so I expected much better longevity. Cleaning didn't help, and it's gotten worse over the last few weeks. For the price I paid, it's disappointing to see it degrade so quickly. I'll probably switch to a different brand next time. Solid design, but the internals just don't hold up.

Got 32GB of this DDR3 for an older budget build and honestly didn't expect much at this price point. It's easily half what other kits cost. Plugged into an ASRock board, auto-detected the 1600MHz profile straight away, no fiddling in BIOS needed. Timings settled at CL11 right out of the box, rock stable through a full weekend of gaming and memtest runs. Zero crashes, zero weirdness. For the money, this is kind of a steal, makes refreshing an aging system actually affordable. Load times in my heavily modded Skyrim setup dropped noticeably compared to the mismatched 4GB sticks I had before. If you're on the fence, just grab it.

Ordered 3 (in yellow). Rec'd 2 but also 1 different ViewHD product that had a label slapped on it indicating it was what I had ordered... NOT! After explaining this very confusing situation to Amazon's now-barely-English-speaking cust. service, and because this is Amazon Marketplace and not Amazon, I have to keep the wrong thing (for which I have no use whatsoever), wait for a refund, and place a new order for the 3rd. I'm avoiding "the Marketplace" at all costs in the future.
Does exactly what it says, no surprises at all.

The company that supplied this "used" MBPr was extremely helpful. It wasn't really a used machine as far as I can tell. This came in the original box (more on that later) and shrunk wrapped. When I placed the order, it wasn't supposed to arrive for 4-5 days. I contacted the seller and asked if they could please ship it quicker. I also mentioned I would pay whatever the additional charge would be. They replied quickly, had it delivered in two days (NY to NJ) and there was no additional charge. I was thrilled. That is until I saw the laptop box. It was in bad shape. Not horrible, but one of the corners (and under the corner) was severely damaged. Crinkled and partially crushed. Enough so that it caused some concern about the laptops possible condition when I opened it. Turns out the laptop was in flawless condition, so no biggie. The box is still useable (although damaged) for eventual resale. I'm assuming the box was damaged BEFORE they sent it since the shipping box that contained the laptop and it's box was in great condition. Hmmm.... Anyway, it was listed as used, but there wasn't a scratch, ding or dent of any kind. Bottom line, I got a great machine with no damage for a great price with excellent customer service. No complaints. Just wish the laptop box wasn't damaged. That's the only reason I gave them 4 stars instead of 5. Highly recommend!! Great experience overall.

32 GB- holds a LOT of pictures and movies! Took this to England with me for an 8-day trip, took more than 700 photos and quite a few videos, and still have room for more. This is definitely the way to go! But don't forget to back up your files after you download the photos.
Way too pricey for how buggy it is. Froze on day two and never recovered.
Picked this up for a road trip and it never let me down once. Screen is bright, directions are clear, and the battery lasts ages.
I was honestly expecting something flimsy but this thing feels solid and premium, totally worth it.
Super easy upgrade. I was going to help my kid do as I have an IT background but he read the directions and did it himself.
It has a couple hiccups but works way better than anything else I've tried.

For the price, this thing's a steal. I grabbed it mainly for Zoom calls and didn't expect much, but the image clarity honestly surprised me. It's not 4K or anything fancy, but it's sharp enough that my coworkers asked if I upgraded my whole setup. The focus ring is a nice touch too, lets me get crisp detail even when I'm holding stuff up close to show the camera. Colors look natural, and it handles my dim office lighting without turning into a grainy mess. Plug-and-play was dead simple on Windows 10, no driver headaches. At this price point, you'd be hard pressed to find anything better. Solid little workhorse.

I'll admit I wasn't expecting much at this price, but the padding is thicker than I figured and the zippers don't feel flimsy at all. It swallows my mirrorless setup with a couple lenses and still has space for snacks. Stitching isn't perfect, but for everyday hauling it's holding up way better than expected.
For the money, this case feels way more premium than I expected. Solid fit and clean look.
Honestly impressed, way better mic pickup than I expected for the price.

I so wanted to like this TV. The first one we ordered came damaged but Amazon true to form had the damaged one picked up and a new set in our greedy little hands in under three days. But why the one star? Well, the set would turn on without any reason. It wasn't a problem with other remotes or neighbors remotes, for we live in the country. Had problems getting to customer service but when we did get them tech was nice and very helpful. He had me do a Hard Reset. It worked....until six hours later and it went back to turning on by itself. I really wanted this set but in the end it was too much of a hassle. I read many reviews and chose it for cost and star ratings. We must have gotten one of the few lemons. I would only recommend this set if you are buying from Amazon as they were great with return.

I really like how tiny and lightweight this clip is, it disappears on my collar, which is exactly what I wanted for walking the dog. The sound quality for audiobooks is clear enough, and the battery has been lasting me about six or seven hours before I need to charge. My one gripe is the radio reception: it's spotty indoors and sometimes cuts out mid-song, which gets annoying. Outdoors it's better, but still not perfect. File transfer from my computer was simple drag-and-drop, no extra software needed. For the price, it's a decent little player if you mostly stick to preloaded content and don't rely heavily on FM.

I made the mistake of thinking, or maybe not thinking, that between Amazon and 47th Street that this would be an acceptable substitute to the more expensive lenses out there. Boy was I wrong! The lens not only vignettes but the color is washed out and the focus is soft to fuzzy. All in all you get what you pay for and in this case I didn't even get what I paid for. STAY AWAY FROM THESE LENSES, THEY ARE JUNK!!!!!!! P.S. I have not heard back from 47th St. about a return for credit after 4 days either. I will update that part of the situation.
Only worth buying if you love wasting money and time.
Bass is surprisingly deep for such a tiny speaker.
Great Bluetooth mouse. Never thought it would make much sense because I've had bad experiences with BT mice. Great Microsoft.

Took this on a day hike and it held up nicely. The padding kept my gear secure even when I stumbled on rocky terrain. I managed to fit my mirrorless body with a prime lens, two batteries, and a few small accessories. Just be realistic about the interior space before loading it up. Solid little bag overall.
Works just like the stock GoPro battery if not better. Fast charger. Two additional batteries are more that enough for any trip.

Bought this to simplify our home backup routine, and it's been smooth overall. The transfer speeds are solid for regular file dumps, and setup was basically plug-and-play. My only minor gripe is the proprietary cable feels a bit delicate where it plugs into the drive. I just handle it gently and it's been fine for a few months now. Great value for straightforward storage.

I grabbed this amp to replace a bulky receiver in my workshop setup, and I'm genuinely surprised by how clean it sounds. The Bluetooth pairing was dead simple, connected to my phone on the first try and hasn't dropped once, even from across the room. I'm driving a pair of old bookshelf speakers, and the high-end detail is crisp without being harsh. What really got me was the subwoofer output; I plugged in a small powered sub and suddenly my workbench felt like a mini listening lounge. It runs cool even after hours of use, and the compact size tucks neatly under a shelf. For the price, the connectivity alone makes it a steal.

I honestly wasn't expecting much when I unboxed this thing, but from the first song I was totally hooked. The clarity is wild, vocals come through so clean and the low end has a surprising punch for its size. I've taken it to small backyard hangouts and even used it during a presentation in a decent-sized room; it filled the space without breaking a sweat. Pairing was instant and the battery lasted way longer than I anticipated. It's become my go-to grab-and-go speaker for just about everything. If you're on the fence, just get it, you'll be grinning ear to ear the moment you hit play.
It works but I just wished they have even a smaller version or design for this.
The screws that came with them do not fit my 12-24 drilled and tap rack fastener holes. The shelves are nice but I had to go buy 12-24 screws from a hardware store in order to install them.

I've used a number of other bluetooth headphones, and these have had the best sound and comfort by far. My ONLY complaint is that they're not waterproofed, so using them while working out can lead to bad things when sweat gets into them. Aside from that, I've yet to find a pair of bluetooth headphones that match them.

For the money, it's honestly hard to complain. It holds my phone steady for quick shots and the flexible legs are handy on uneven surfaces. Just don't expect it to last through heavy daily use, after a couple of weeks, the grip isn't as tight and it gets a little wobbly. Fine for occasional needs.
These work nicely, but the actual "holder" for the cannon battery is a "sled" that likes to fall off the charger.. I lost one sled, bought a second charger. The second unit I taped that down.
Barely lasted a month, total waste of money.
This AC wireless router is fast and was very easy to set up for my simple Home WIFI. Great value for the price
We have a few of these KVM's and cables. Never had any problems with them. Will order more if needed in the future.

Arrived in three days, double-boxed with thick foam inserts, no way this thing was getting damaged. First impression pulling it out: solid weight, clean glass, no dust inside. The flip mount was stiffer than expected, needed a few cycles to loosen up, but now it moves smoothly enough. Glass clarity is impressive for the price point, though there's a faint blue tint at the edges if you really look for it. Eye relief is workable, not generous. For a budget magnifier that showed up well-packaged and ready to mount, I'm satisfied. Knocking off one star only because the mount tension out of the box felt overly tight and made initial setup a little frustrating.
Item is inexpensive and works as advertised. Bought it as a pack in with my bare SSHD, as my chasis came with drive mounts.

I initially had high hopes for this product. I have an HTPC with Windows Media Center that has built in Hauppauge tuners (one PCI single, 1500 series I think, and a dual PCIEx1 2250 model). I was thinking that eventually, I'd be able to not be stuck with routing coax cables to the computer, and be able to access the tuner content over the network. It worked pretty well for about 10 months. The first one didn't work and after a hassle-free Amazon return, the second one broke after about 10 months. The cooling fan stopped working and it was so hot to the touch, it was making my closet smell like burnt electronics. I'm surprised it didn't catch on fire (not sure if it is UL listed or not.) And like most warranties, you have to send it to the manufacturer and pay the shipping. Yawn. I guess I could have bought a new one and send the old one back, but that is somewhat unethical, and I didn't want to risk a house fire anyway, so at this point, I don't think you could pay me to take one. The cooling fan in this unit is very loud, so it would need to be tucked away and not in the main viewing area. On the other hand the Hauppauge tuners have performed flawlessly for probably about 5 years. As a bonus, the Hauppauge also acts as my IR input to my computer which enables easy integration with Evenghost for automation with my Harmony remotes. As a con, it seems the Hauppauge dual tuner has the worst signal, the single has slightly better, and the HDHomeRun had the best. So if Hauppauge could better manage the ATSC signal in the tuner, there would be a clear winner there. But even so, low heat, no noise, and flawless operation over 5 years is a large upside. Bottom line, I will not purchase or consider this unit again.
Died right after the warranty expired, totally useless.
Works great and is faster then what i an used to seeing.

Fits my mid-tower fine after a little finagling, but the plastic frame feels flimsier than I expected. Does the job keeping dust out and airflow is still decent. Not perfect out of the box, but for the price I can't complain much. Just wish the mounting clips were sturdier.

Works as described with a Samsung Galaxy S3 but as long as you plug into a wall charger. Used it on a now 52" legacy Sony XBR LCD TV & a new Pioneer LED LCD monitor TV at work via one of its HDMI inputs.

The box arrived in perfect shape, wrapped securely with no dents or crushed corners. Unboxing felt premium, everything was snug in foam cutouts, and the camera itself had a nice weight to it right out of the gate. Setup was straightforward even if the included quick-start card was a little sparse. First power-on, the menu navigation felt snappy and the build quality impressed me for the price. Lens cap fit snug, no loose ports, and the tilting screen moved smoothly. I haven't done a deep focus test yet, but initial indoor shots looked crisp enough. Giving four stars for now based purely on shipping care and solid first impressions.
Great product. Price exceptional. Don't wait...buy the thing now. You will not be disappointed.
Packs a punch for its size, handled my media drive way smoother than I expected.

This card was well rated by a number of Raspberry Pi users, and after having several 'other' cards (mostly micro with adapter) work for a while then mysteriously becoming corrupted it was worth it to try. So far the Pi has been running clean for several weeks, through multiple updates and reboots, with no trouble. Recommended.

My PC started bluescreening the day I plugged these in. Took me a week and two wiped drives to figure out it was the speakers causing it. The shielding is clearly nonexistent. I'd only recommend these to someone I genuinely disliked and wanted to ruin their weekend.

I wanted to like this clock, the display is huge and easy to read from across the room, which is perfect for my bad eyesight. That's literally the only good thing about it. The setup is a nightmare because there's no obvious way to actually set the time; I had to hunt down a PDF of the manual after losing the paper one. Then a brief power flicker last week wiped everything and now it just blinks uselessly. I opened it up to check the backup battery and it's one of those weird coin cells soldered onto the board, not something you can just pop out and replace. For a basic bedside clock, having zero user-friendly time-setting controls and a non-replaceable backup battery is just ridiculous. Straight into the trash.

what a great deal on these. i used them on my 2 Yongnuo 600 LED lights they soften them up just like a light soft box. they are much cheaper and more compact than buying the soft boxes that go with these lights.

As usual, a quality product from Anker. Had many other cables that would charge some devices but not others. This cable works for all, and at 6 ft is a convenient length. A velcro strip attached near the end of the "big connector" end is a thoughtful addition allowing you to loop the cord for storage or to neatly reduce the length during use. Definitely recommend.
It works really great as it is a replacement for an old Walkman. My wife loves and it sounds amazingly good. the radio is pretty good, too.

I was honestly surprised how fast this thing showed up, ordered late Thursday night and it was on my doorstep Saturday morning. Packaging was minimal but secure, no excessive plastic, just a snug little box with the keyboard wrapped in a foam sleeve. Pulling it out, my first thought was how solid it feels for the price. No creaks, no sharp edges, and the matte finish resists fingerprints nicely. The weight is just right, doesn't slide around on my desk. Connecting to my tablet took maybe three seconds and the keys have a satisfying, quiet snap to them. Typing on it right out of the box felt natural and responsive. I'm genuinely impressed by the build quality and how little fuss there was to get started.
Surprised how sturdy this thing feels for the price, totally outclasses what I expected.
Fits and looks good. Practical and offers additional protection. Versatile. Get it you won't be sorry! The youngins will be very happy, happy, happy, to have one!

The box showed up in good shape, no dents or crushed corners, and inside everything was packed tight in custom foam, no rattling around. First impression out of the package was solid, the unit feels sturdy and well-built, nice clean finish. Install was straightforward, snapped right into a spare slot in my Square D panel without any fuss. Haven't had any major surges yet to really test it, but the indicator lights are bright and easy to read. My only minor gripe is the instructions could be clearer about which breaker position works best, had to double-check online. Otherwise, pretty happy for the peace of mind.
Great sub's! I have had them for about 6 months and they are perfect! I used to use explodes but these are better!

This charger is just okay if you're in a pinch and don't mind babysitting your device while it charges. I'd only recommend it to someone who's super patient or needs a spare for travel where reliability isn't critical. The plug doesn't sit snug at all, you have to wiggle it just right to get a connection, and even then it drops if you breathe near it. I've had it spark once already when I tried adjusting it, which isn't exactly confidence-inspiring. For the price, I guess it technically works, but I'm already shopping for something more dependable. Definitely not for daily heavy use.
For a budget card in an old Dell, it's fine, quieter than I expected but definitely not quick.

Box arrived a little crushed on one corner, but the keyboard itself seemed fine at first glance. I like the slim design and the keys feel decent. However, the packaging was pretty bare, just the keyboard loose in the box with a thin sleeve, no padding. Made me worry a bit about long-term durability. Setup was easy enough, but I'm not fully sold on the whole solar thing yet. Hoping it holds up better than the first impression suggests.
Works exactly as expected for my external setup. Signal is stable, no drops or lag. Took a star off for the stiff cable feel.
I had my doubts about this but it work out. I left this unit out on my desk and two other people wanted to take it.

THIS IS AN UPDATE TO MY ORIGINAL REVIEW: My SoundPEATS were replaced with new ones and sound quality is MUCH BETTER! Maybe I had a bad set first time around? Anyway, VERY SATISFIED with 2nd set. Love the bluetooth connect technology...no cords to break. SoundPEATS gave superb customer service once I contacted them. ALL companies should value their customers like this co. does. OK only IMO. Sound quality unimpressive, buttons are right where you would touch them if you are pushing these buds back in your ears, so I was continually turning them off by accident. Granted there's not a lot of real estate on these things for the buttons, so maybe I'm too critical, but no matter what sound quality disappointing.

I grabbed this to clean up the cable chaos behind my homelab rack, three Pis, a couple switches, and an old router all running off it with zero hiccups. Voltage stays steady even under load. If you're a tinkerer juggling multiple SBCs or network gear and hate clutter, this is a no-brainer. Totally recommend it for anyone building a compact, always-on setup.

I've been really happy with this compact desktop overall. It sips power, stays dead silent, and handles my daily work tasks without breaking a sweat. The only hiccup was the storage starting to act sluggish after a year and a half, swapped in an SSD and it's been flawless since. Still a solid buy.

this product is amazing, good quality, and it does apply very well without too much effort in a matter of minutes. I use the same product for my Galaxy S3 mini, and I usually keep it in my pockets where the keys and coins just take care of it, and there are no major scratches. and the screen looks good.

I grabbed this to hook up an older surround sound setup in my basement, and it's been flawless. Zero hum, no loose connections, and every channel comes through clean. I'd absolutely recommend it to anyone piecing together a budget home theater or helping a friend revive a hand-me-down receiver and DVD player. Honestly, if you're still running component audio and don't need HDMI, just get this and be done with it.
Works fine for local repeaters, and it's survived heavy snow and ice without a problem. Not the best range I've ever had, but solid build overall.
this is a very good item, i use it all the time to use my tablet on my big tv!
Dell Monitor was packed well and arrived with no damage and on time. I like this product.
This is a quality product and durable. I can see having this product for over a few years, and worth paying a bit more for.
Cable did not work. Arrived dead. Tried it on two different laptops and two different monitors. Can't exchange can only get refund.
The reinforced ends are a game-changer. I've had zero fraying after months of daily travel abuse, and the snug fit means no wiggling loose while charging.

This is a great bathroom radio. It's moisture resistant, compact, and fairly attractive. More importantly, the controls are very easy to operate, even with wet hands, and it sounds very good. It has a weather band that is very useful. I think it would be hard to do much better than this for a bathroom radio, and I recommend it most highly.
Excellent Charger/USB port power. Supplies 2A of current as advertised. I also use it to charge/power my GPS when not in vehicle.
Arrived a day early in super snug packaging, not a scratch on it. Fired it up right out of the box and the picture looks incredibly crisp. Very happy so far.
Perfect for anyone with a stack of bare drives. I'd suggest it to tinkerers and home-lab folks needing quick swaps without fuss.
I love this mouse... took a bit of time to get use to it (different hand position than any other mouse I've used... but boy is it comfortable.

My spool arrived three days early and was packed like it was headed to the moon, double-boxed, no dings, no unraveling. First thing I noticed pulling it out was how solid and consistent the jacket feels. No thin spots, no weird chemical smell. The copper itself looks clean and bright at every cut I've made so far. I bought this to redo some accessory wiring under the hood of my truck, and immediately ordered another spool after seeing the quality. Feels like the kind of wire you reach for when you want the job done once. If you've been burned by that aluminum junk before, this will feel like a breath of fresh air.
It's basic 16 gauge speaker wire. Not super flexible, but not hard to work with, has a durable feel, and strips cleanly even with cheap stripers.

I picked these up hoping they'd blend in for a quick cable cleanup behind the desk. Right out of the package they were a tangled, curly mess that fought every attempt to lay flat. Even with weight on them overnight, they still popped up in random loops. The black finish scuffs easily too, so they look worn almost immediately. The split design does open smoothly, which is the one positive, but if you actually need to route more than a single thin cord, good luck getting them to close completely without bulging. For the price I didn't expect perfection, but these just create more visual clutter than they hide.
Still going strong after six months of daily use, zero issues.

These are perfect for my daily commute and long flights. The padding is plush and they block out so much chatter. I'd recommend them to any frequent traveler who values crisp, clear audio without bulk.
I expected slightly faster speeds, but it's still quicker than my old brick. The battery fix mode is surprisingly handy for reviving tired cells.

I really recommend buy this headphone. Excellent sound output at this price. There are 2 reasons for me not giving this product a 5 star rating is.. 1) Little too bulky although collapsible. 2) There is no microphone built with it. Other products like Planotronics have microphones along with the headphone at the same price.
Fits my Canon Vixia and essentials nicely without breaking the bank, decent padding for the cost.

The first one I got stopped working after a couple days while watching a movie. I was in the middle of watching a move and the two speakers for high frequencies stopped working. After testing all other components and different content I determined it was the center channel itself. There wasn't a lot of mention of this online so I figured it was a freak incident. Luckily it happened within the return period an Amazon swiftly replaced the unit. Since then, paired with the L890 speakers I have it has been amazing. Great balance and full featured sound.
worked out of the box with my mac mini on FaceTime. great sound and picture quality.

Installed easily. Just plugged it into a USB port and Windows 7 recognized it. I should have stopped there. Instead, I installed the driver on the included mini-disk. The driver installed easily, but once it was installed, Skype no longer recognized it. I uninstalled the driver - no joy. I restored my computer to pre-driver installation, no joy. I tried installing the most recent driver from their web site. The installation never finished. I kept getting messages that Windows didn't recognized the publisher, and as soon as the progress bar was full, it started all over again. I tried uninstalling the updated driver and I got messages that something was already running, and it wouldn't uninstall. I have sent Ausdom an email asking for help, but I am returning it if I don't get an immediate, helpful response. My OS is Windows 7, and it is completely up to date. Right now, it seems the only way to get this software off my system is to completely rebuild it, and I'm not looking forward to doing that.

These arrived way faster than expected and the packaging was seriously overkill, everything snug in foam. Opened them up and they feel solid, well-machined, and ready to install. Stoked to finally quiet down my old launch PS3. First impressions are awesome.
I love that this microSD card handled every file I threw at it in my dash cam without a single hiccup.
Really nice lens. Pictures are very nice and crisp. Also makes my Canon become very compact and easier to store in my bag.

Great mount to hold either a cable box, DVD Player, or Bluray Player. We combined this with the adjustable VideoSecu TV Mount and they look great together. What is also nice is the arms on this bracket are adjustable to accomodate all ranges of media sizes. Highly recommend if you just want a way to mount a peice of media without have to have a full shelf.
For the price, these are a steal. Charges my batteries quickly and they last ages in my camera. Super simple to use, no fuss. Definitely a solid buy if you need a no-nonsense charger.

I almost fell for the $30 cable at the big-box store when I picked up my new printer. Glad I checked online first. This USB cable does exactly what it's supposed to do for a fraction of the price. Plugged it into my printer and laptop, and it connected instantly with zero fuss. Data transfers are snappy, and the connectors feel sturdy enough for daily use, no wiggling or loose fit. The 8-foot length gives me plenty of slack to route it neatly behind my desk without stretching. Honestly can't see any difference between this and the overpriced ones they push at checkout. If you need a reliable cable and don't want to throw away money, this is a no-brainer.

If you are frustrated by the limited options of having one keyboard and mouse control multiple machines, this is a must-check-out device. The JUC400 effortlessly allows me to use one Mac or PC, and control two machines simulatenously from it. I can use my PC laptop to control my Mac mini behind it, and effortlessly drag my mouse from one machine to the next. It just works. No need to configure, use complicated server/client setups, none of that. You simply plug the device in to each machine, and a virtual CD appears that automounts and automatically launches the software. It also handles software updates automatically, including updating the device's own firmware. You never need to load a web site to check for drivers or updates with this device, it handles all of that for you. Other features like iPad compatibility, and file transfers that really do work at USB 2.0 speeds, are added bonuses. Even without them, this device is totally worth the money, and totally worth five stars.
Perfect for movie nights with the family, crisp sound, easy setup, and unbeatable value. I'd recommend it to any casual home theater fan.

This subwoofer was purchased as an additional unit for a small 2.1 home theater setup. Used primarily to play bass-heavy music, this subwoofer absolutely keeps up with loud environments, and, coming from a pro sound background, is one of the best consumer subwoofers Ive personally heard.
Arrived super fast in secure bubble wrap. Opened the box and the build feels solid, glass is crystal clear. Snapped right onto my Nikon, instantly impressed with the sharp, bright view.

We have a VGA-only monitor that was connected to one of our towers, and recently the VGA port went out. This adapter did the trick perfectly. It's quite simple - VGA cord plugs into the adapter, and the adapter connects via HDMI to your tower, or any other device. For the price, I'm extremely happy with the purchase and easy fix.

For the cost, it's decent. Video quality is clear enough during the day, but the night vision is pretty grainy. The mount feels a bit flimsy, though it hasn't fallen off yet. You get what you pay for.
This thing blew me away for the price. Setup took maybe two minutes and the picture is way sharper than I figured it would be. Honestly feels like a steal compared to what I was bracing for.
The mic picks up voice crystal clear even in noisy rooms.
The door/window switches are amazingly accurate and work great with my smart hub. Would give it 5 starts but the motion sensors, bought two sets, go offline at random.
i love this case it has a nice black finish and all components fit comfortably inside with room to spare. It also has ample room for cooling and extras.
A simple cable. Just what I was looking for. Works just fine.

I picked these up hoping for something decent, but they honestly blew past what I expected. The sound is crisp and balanced right out of the box, I didn't have to fiddle with any EQ settings. I mostly use them during my daily commute and while studying in busy cafes, and the lightweight build means I forget I'm even wearing them after a few minutes. They sit snug without squeezing, which is rare for me. One thing I really appreciate is how clear podcasts and calls sound; voices come through naturally, not tinny. For the price, I was ready to settle for average, but these easily compete with pairs I've tried that cost twice as much. Solid everyday choice.
Works great! just be careful of putting to much cleaner on your dash but even with that you have to take corner pretty fast to get this to slide. I would def buy again

I picked this up for a hybrid work setup and it's been flawless. Pairs instantly with my laptop and tablet, and the typing feel is surprisingly crisp for something so portable. The compact layout took me a day to get used to, but now I fly through emails. Battery just keeps going, I genuinely forget it even needs charging. I'd recommend this to anyone who commutes or works from coffee shops regularly. Just know it really needs a flat surface; couch typing isn't its strong suit.

I bought this to drain some old USB power banks for recycling, and it does exactly that without drama. The constant-current mode holds steady, which is great for capacity testing with my multimeter and timer. The fan is a little louder than I expected, not a dealbreaker, but noticeable in a quiet room. Heat dissipation is otherwise solid, and the build feels sturdy for the price. A handy little tool for battery nerds.

I've been using this mic for weekly band practices in my dusty garage for about four months now. It's survived a few accidental drops and still picks up every guitar lick and off-key vocal without any static. Really impressed with how solid it feels. No signs of wear, and the sound stays crisp even after all this abuse.

The box showed up with a dented corner and the inner packaging felt flimsy, no foam, just thin cardboard. Headphones survived, but first impression was cheap. Clamp force is aggressive out of the box, and the pleather pads got warm fast. Sound is okay for the price, just wish the unboxing felt less budget.
Solid replacement for my aging server setup. Plugged right in, no fuss, and has been spinning quietly for months without a single hiccup.

It works out of the box. I was happy for a while until I pause the music and notice the massive static white noise this device is generating in the background. Then I disconnected the receiver from my speaker. My speaker is quiet again. I was hoping for better and returned it soon.

I've lost count of how many lens caps I've misplaced, but this one finally stays put. Snaps on securely without any fuss and hasn't popped off once in my bag. For something so simple, it's oddly satisfying to use. Definitely saved me from more scratched glass.

This router works very well, I have both Windows Vista and XP computers and an XP laptop and a printer all connected to it. The laptop is wireless connection. It was easy to install, but had trouble getting my settings correct. I called Linksys and they walked me through the settings. They were very patient and had me up and running in short time. Very fast speed and I am extremely satisfied. Highly reccomend this product.

My previous watch was a Fitbit Charge 2. I really like the vivoactive HR but the indoor display brightness is much lower than the Fitbit. Outdoors the display is fine. Day 3 and the Garmin is still above 80% charge. I'm still getting used to all the features. I disabled all the activities except for walking. I like to go hiking in the hills and the Fitbit does not have GPS. I am a big guy 6'6" 280 lbs and the x-large band easily fits and is comfortable 24x7. Not sure why but the elevation on my first walk around town (maybe 50' above sea level) was registered as 520 feet. Maybe the GPS data is still downloading. I still keep my Fitbit on my right wrist. I would buy the watch again.
The battery held a charge fine for weekend shoots, but I wish the capacity was higher, it drains way faster than the original Nikon one.

This thing was lame. It didn't even work when we got it. It's really light weight and feel like it's cheap. We ended up going to Best Buy to by a different one that would do the job right. The bad thing is we waited too long and when we wanted to send this thing back, it was already too late to get a refund.
The heavy duty battery makes my hand held radio run longer with more options enabled.
Super easy to install...needed it because I was upgrading my graphics card. Works like a charm so far.

I snagged this for work travel and honestly, for the price it's a steal. I have a 17-inch Dell and it slides in snugly once I took out the bottom foam insert, still feels cushioned enough that I don't worry about bumps. The shoulder strap is surprisingly comfy, even when I load it up with chargers, a mouse, and a notebook. Zippers feel solid, material seems durable. If you need a no-frills bag that fits a larger laptop without spending a ton, this does the job.
Had this unit for six months now, still kicks hard every time. Solid build, no glitches at all, and the bass is just ridiculous.
Bought this for quick file transfers between work machines. It's seriously snappy and the metal casing feels premium. Totally dependable little drive.

Just bought a couple more as back ups. I really like this remote and it works well for what I need it for. I emailed Audiovox and they confirmed that this is the exact same remote as the RCR314WR but this one costs less.

Overall does what a hub should do. Works 100% with powered devices like printers and scanners. But it doesn't like iPods: the connection will be lost (not a good idea to disconnect an iPod without "ejecting" it), or no connection will be made at all, or the port on the computer where the hub is connected will lock up. Also, the build quality is poor. The silver finish is uneven, even worn off (out of the box) in a few spots. And the clear cover over the LED indicators is falling off. Perhaps Belkin's new "Plus" hubs are better, but I'm not willing to find out. Belkin USB 2.0 Plus Hub (7 Port) F5U307-BRN
These blanks worked perfectly for my backup project. Not a single coaster, and they read faster than the old brand I used to buy.

Just got this hub for my MacBook Air setup and the USB-C data transfer speed is surprisingly snappy. I can finally offload photos from my camera's SD card while charging my tablet through the same unit without any lag. Slim design tucks neatly under my monitor stand too. No more port juggling, super solid little workhorse.
I really liked the product. fully met expectations. Many thanks to you guys! A really good product! Recommend to everyone!!

I bought this case a while ago for my Macbook. I loved it. It was durable and protected my Mac. The stand was also a plus on flat surfaces. Unfortunately, with age it broke. Also if you don't clean it often crud will pile up and it's noticeable because this product is see through.

I wanted to like this KVM switch, but it's a mixed bag. Setup was easy enough, but I immediately ran into cable length issues, the included VGA and USB cables barely reached my tower, which sits under the desk. Ended up having to buy extensions, which felt like an extra hidden cost. Also, it absolutely needs the driver installed to function right; without it, my keyboard and mouse were spotty. Once everything was sorted, switching between my desktop and laptop was smooth, and video quality was fine for basic tasks. It works, just not out of the box in a typical setup. Decent for the price if you're prepared to troubleshoot and buy extra cables.
TI chipset, works great for audio over FireWire (I tried it with 32 channels at 48kHz/24bit).

I've been using the Level U for close to four months now, mostly during workouts and long walks. The plastic band has held up better than I expected, no cracks or loosening at the joints, and it still sits comfortably around my collar without shifting. I've accidentally snagged the cable a few times and it didn't fray. Sound is clean and full for the price, though I wish the earbud shape was a bit more forgiving after an hour or so. Battery still gives me a solid day of mixed use. Connection to my Samsung phone is mostly stable, with only an occasional quick dropout that fixes itself. Solid daily driver.
After a few weeks, the buttons started sticking a bit.
Great Stylus pack, and works great with my iPad. They come with extra tips which is very nice!

This strap worked just fine while I was on a river ride in Costa Rica. It also strapped right onto the helmet I used for the zip-line course. It allowed me to get some great footage of my friends while on vacation. Highly recommend.
These fans push decent air but after a couple months one started rattling like something's loose inside.

Denons ear-bud headphones are simple, comfortable to wear, and offer high sound quality. The Denons are understated, but stylish. They have polished end caps, with dark chrome accents. The materials and the fit and finish are high. Included with the headphones are three silicon ear tips; small, medium, and large. Also included is a short extension cable, although I am unsure what anyone would need it for. I never needed it when using the Denons with my iPhone 5s. The Denons have a small built-in microphone and a single button that is attached a couple of inches down from the right ear-bud. The button allows you to control music playback and take calls/use voice command. Its very intuitive and simple to use. Sound quality of the Denons is high. I listened to all types of music, from jazz, to country, to pop and all of it sounded nice. Bass was strong without being overpowering, and high were also nicely rendered. Overall, I found they produced very balanced and attractive sound. Since the Denons close off the opening of the ear canal so well I was also able to hear subtle instruments and vocals that I would have missed otherwise. The one downside of their excellent noise isolation is that I could also hear myself breathing at times. The Denons are comfortable and I found that the medium sized ear tips fit my average sized ears best. The headphones fit snuggly, but are never uncomfortable, and even after walking briskly for a mile they only shifted a little bit. Im not sure they are made for running, but for modest activity levels they should work fine. They are also light in weight. The cord is long enough to not tug on the headphones when you move, but not so long that it gets in the way. All in all you cant ask for much more from ear-buds.

I bought my V1253 a few years ago and have loved it from the first day! I use an SD card and when the cards gets filled up (350 pix for a 1-gb card)I save it and put in another one! Nice screen. Battery stays up for a long time without being recharged! I carry it in my pocket and just love it. I'm very satisfied with it.

I wasn't expecting much at this price, but it actually works way better than I thought. My old shower speaker sounded muddy, while this one stays crisp even with water running. Mounted it in seconds and the buttons are simple enough to use with soapy fingers. I mostly listen to podcasts, and I can hear every word clearly without cranking the volume. Battery lasts me a full week of showers too. Honestly surprised by the quality.
I think it's a very useful product although I still haven't figured out how to get my LOCAL weather without getting the entire southwest Florida weather

I've gone through so many cheap earbuds it's honestly embarrassing, but these have been a total game changer for the price. I grabbed them just to have a backup pair for commuting, expecting the usual tinny sound and flimsy cord, but I was pleasantly surprised. The audio is clear and way more balanced than I figured possible at this cost, bass isn't overwhelming, and podcasts come through crisp. They're also surprisingly comfortable for long wear; I've had them in for a few hours with no ear fatigue. The build feels sturdier than the dollar store ones I usually trash within a week. For what I paid, these easily outperform anything in the same bracket. If you're on a tight budget but still want decent daily drivers, just grab them.
This is the best mouse I've ever owned. Hands down the best mouse for wireless gaming.

Nice case. Swallowed a Sabertooth x99 with H100 water cooler with only very minor hiccups. Pros: - very easy to assemble - Can work with H100i cooling unit with 2 fans - has rubber grommets for H100i - decent cable management - adjustable position of rear fan - room for additional front fan - padding to absorb sound on sides - removable filters front and bottom Cons: - top cover must be left off if using water cooler (ugly --see photo) would prefer a 2 piece cover of some kind - No fan control - only 2 usb ports on front - Should have top cutout for 12v MoBo power (was a stretch) - Tight fit for water unit near Top hdd cage I think it would take an h110i (has holes for 140mm mounting, but no grommets), but wouldn't guarantee. As it is the fans just clear the motherboard and the top 2 drive bays can't be used for full length drives with a h100i installed. I like that the case allowed all the wiring to be run behind the mobo, but running the 12v cable was a tight fit and pushes up against the RAM slots at the right edge. It would be nicer if there had been a cutout at the top of the mobo to allow a straight run behind. Cutting it myself seemed like too much work given that the cable fit as is.

I expected a lot more from a Nikon. The image quality in low light is grainy and washed out, nothing like the sample shots online. Autofocus hunts constantly, even in good lighting, so I missed plenty of moments with my kids. The touchscreen feels unresponsive and laggy, making menu navigation a chore. Battery life is abysmal; I barely get through a short outing before it dies. For the price, I thought I'd get a reliable point-and-shoot, but it's just frustrating to use. Ended up going back to my phone for everyday photos.
This lens cleaner didn't work at all... My other car's CD rom skips a few seconds of each track and I was hoping this will clean the dust and thus fix it, but nope, it didn't do that at all!
If they can keep this quality up for a few years... before being bought up by a company that will drive the quality into the ground for profit. That would be wonderful!!

I have these ALL over my property and Love them. They've worked with three different routers and the customer service from KUNA has always been incredible. You have a lot of options to control the sensitivity and the features are easy to use. The app is VERY intuitive and easy to figure out. I tell everyone about mine! My favorite part is the HEIGHT at which they are installed. Because they are lights - they are installed higher which gives you a better angle for faces. The Ring system is typically LOW and I didn't like the angles.
Surpassed my hopes; flawless connection and distance, truly impressive.
Bought this for my teenage niece who's obsessed with retro tech, and she hasn't put it down. Perfect gift for any music lover wanting a tangible, warm listening experience.

For what this costs, I expected something that actually works more than half the time. Instead, I got constant dropouts and garbled audio on every other call. The hardware feels cheap, and setup was a headache. You're basically paying for a fancy paperweight that occasionally lets a call connect before cutting you off mid-sentence. Total waste of money, spend a little more elsewhere and save yourself the frustration.

These have become my daily go-to for work calls and my afternoon walks. The audio is crisp with just the right amount of bass, and the passive noise isolation is surprisingly effective for an on-ear design. I've worn them for hours without any discomfort, which says a lot since I usually find headphones too tight. Battery life is solid, I charge them maybe once a week with moderate use. Pairing is instant every time I power them on. I'd easily recommend these to anyone who works from home and needs a reliable headset that transitions seamlessly from laptop to phone. They'd also suit commuters who want something lightweight that doesn't sacrifice sound quality. Really pleased with this purchase.
For the price, this little thing is a steal. Snug fit, no fuss, and my bare drive is finally secure in the case.
Thank you Amazon Rep for resending this to my niece for Christmas. She got her gift and is very happy with her product. Plus that she got in was a big plus for me and for her for most. Thank you.

Cable worked great! Unfortunately, I didn't measure correctly, about 15 feet to short but this was my mistake, I can't repeat what "Walter" would say because someone might block it, but I was one. It's ok, I will give this one to my sister and I have already reordered a 75 foot Ethernet cable which will give me the correct length I need.

My case arrived in a simple padded envelope but was in perfect shape, no scuffs or creases. Right out of the box it felt sturdy and the materials didn't have that cheap plastic smell. The three-point clamp system is a smart touch and avoids the edge-button problem I'd read about. My only minor gripe is the back cover bows inward a little when I use the strap to keep it shut, but it flattens out with use. Overall, pretty pleased.

I bought this little SanDisk drive hoping it'd hold up better than the cheap generic ones I've tried, and so far it's been rock solid. Transfers are snappy, it feels sturdy, and I'm not constantly worried about it failing. Honestly outperforms what I expected for the cost. No regrets grabbing it.

I picked this up hoping to save my MacBook from cooking itself during long work sessions, and honestly it's a steal for the price. The fan moves a surprising amount of air without sounding like a jet engine, I can actually take calls without people asking what that noise is. My laptop used to get uncomfortably warm just running a few browser tabs and Slack, but now it stays noticeably cooler even under heavier load. Build quality is solid, the adjustable height is a nice bonus, and it's super slim so it doesn't clutter my desk. For the cost, I expected way less. If you need a simple, effective cooling fix without dropping a ton of cash, this is it.

Barely worked out of the box. The battery life is a joke compared to what they promise. I expected a simple, reliable recorder, but this thing can't even hold a charge long enough for a single session. Total letdown.
Make sure you buy your display port cables, the monitors do not come with them.
Have this for almost a year and it plays everything I throw at it at max resolution and graphics setting wonderfully!
Great extensions used in the back seat of a Jeep for long trip charging of kids devices.

I use this router at the receiving end of a wireless bridge between the two floors of my apartment, and also as a print and file server. I know this unit may be a bit of an overkill for the purpose, but I couldn't find any lower cost alternatives that would provide the same feature set. I was a bit worried about the speeds I would get through the concrete floor, but the connection to the main router, an Apple Time Capsule, is rock stable at a 866 Mbps signal rate (the signal sits around 47-49 dB against a 92dB noise floor). The connection would stay up for months at a time. I was pleasantly surprised that the stock firmware supported all the features I needed, but it's still nice to know that I can fall back to Merlin or DD-WRT if I need additional tweaking possibilities or special features. The performance and ease of use of the web GUI has also improved a lot compared to my previous Asus router, a WL500W. Being able to set most configuration items without a reboot is a godsend. Another nice touch from Asus was providing a discovery utility, that finds the router on the LAN and detects its current IP address, which saved me a lot of time when I was tweaking the IP configuration of the two routers. I cannot comment on the 2.4 GHz range or behavior, as I only use the router at 5 GHz. Update (9/17/2014): I was having print server reliability issues with the stock firmware (after a few days the printer stopped printing, even though the router showed it was connected). So I installed DD-WRT, and I'm back to being a perfectly satisfied customer. Aside from the printer trouble, I only had to reboot the router for firmware updates and I never experienced any connection issues whatsoever.

Great quality. I've only had it for a few weeks now, but you can feel the quality in the carefully crafted tips. The cable is tangle-free as stated of course and the cloth jacket is of good quality. Now it's just a thing of seeing if they last longer than my last cloth jacketed aux cable purchase.

This is a laptop for someone who wants large and in charge like me! I can type easily and not have to carry an additional keyboard because I can't stand tiny buttons. It comes with Windows 10 already installed which was a huge plus in my book since it took my son overnight to install anything of size with our slow internet connection. Google Chrome downloaded quickly and I was up and running and fully charged right away. It is heavy but I'm not a college student lugging stuff from class to class. This will allow me to do the work I want upstairs and not run downstairs to look up phone numbers, recipes, and and stay cozy by the fire and then transport on business trips. I do want something to cover the video chat button when not in service and love that there's an auto dust clean function.
Snapped right into my 2004 Silverado, sounds crisp and clear even at highway speeds. Super happy with this easy upgrade.

It's very sleek and fits nicely on my keychain, so I won't have to worry about having a flash drive when I need it. For large files, though, it's much slower than any other flash drive I have. I bought the 16GB version so I could move large files back and forth between computers, but it's a bad choice for that role. A 500MB file took almost 3 minutes to save on this drive (avg 3.5 MB/second), compared to a bit more than a minute on an old Sandisk Cruzer drive. If you're moving small.mp3s on and off, you'll love it. If you want to move big files, you'll need lots of patience -- or another drive.
Box was dented but tablet seems fine so far.
Works fine for basic taps but palm rejection is hit or miss when I sketch.

Good little flash drive; super small and the curve helps with inserting/removing from a computer laying flat. The only issue is that the keyring hole is so small, it will not fit on a standard sized keyring. You have to use the very small and cheaply built ring that comes with it. I plan on drilling out the hole to see if I can make it fit without damaging anything. UPDATE: Drilled out the keyring hole (see picture) and the device still works!
it's perfect for my 7 year old son, but you have to take the case off in order to plug in the charger. all in all, I got what I paid for.

I had high hopes for this TV after reading the specs, but after six months of light use it's been nothing but a headache. First off, the screen developed a faint flickering along the bottom edge that got progressively worse. Then, about three weeks ago, the built-in apps started freezing randomly, Netflix would crash mid-show and the whole system needed a hard restart. The final straw was yesterday when a vertical green line appeared right down the center of the display, no matter what input I used. I've barely moved the thing since mounting it, so it's not like it took a knock. For the price, I expected way more longevity than half a year before things started failing one by one. Pretty disappointed.

I have always liked Wacom products and that includes its Bamboo styluses. The one I have used before just had a pen clip which actually worked well for my iPad case. I have always liked the styluses that are able to plug into the headphone/speaker port because I don't use it often so I it makes sense to have a solid place to keep my stylus with my iPad. This product is reminiscent of the elago, which also has the retractable and speaker port pen feature. It also includes some colored rings that you can swap out for customization, though I think the silver looks the cleanest and most expensive. The functionality is fine. It writes well and fits in your hand well. I'm just not a big fan of the girth of the product but it does allow for better grip and writing. However, the rubber grip does make the product look a bit cheaper, as it has dots in them and holds onto lint and dirt strongly and easily. For the price, I think the elago is a much cheaper and comparable option. This product just doesn't look as sleek and clean as the other styluses in both elago or Wacom's repertoire.

I've been using this daily for two weeks and it's quickly become my favorite gadget. Battery lasts way longer than expected and the screen is crisp even in sunlight. Setup was painless and it just works. Honestly, zero regrets picking this up.

Bought this to house an old DVD drive I had lying around, but it never worked right from day one. The drive fits okay, but my laptop just refuses to see it no matter what I try, different cables, different ports, nothing. I waited too long to test it and missed the return window, so now I'm stuck with a useless plastic brick. Total waste of money.

This hood snaps on securely without any wiggle, which I appreciate since some third-party ones feel loose right away. It does a solid job cutting glare when I'm shooting near windows, and I noticed less washed-out color in side light. My only gripe is that it's so shallow, I can't really rely on it for bump protection the way I hoped. Still, for the price, the optical improvement alone makes it worth keeping.

Fits my Garmin perfectly and keeps the screen scratch-free in my bag. I'd suggest this to anyone who mostly uses their GPS in the car and just needs basic storage. I do wish it had a bit more cushioning for drops.
Exactly what I needed and as listed. And at a great price.
Spent way too much on this thing and it didn't even make it through a single afternoon. Total waste, honestly.
Really impressed with the performance on this unit. Build quality feels solid and it worked seamlessly with my setup right out of the box. Definitely exceeds what I expected for the price.
Exceeded what I hoped for, totally worth it.

I must say I have been impressed with this device. The company seems motivated to continue to improve with several firmware updates since I have had it. Easy set up easy to use don't hesitate if you are looking to cut the cord.
Works great for my pc dedicated to Karaoke on our theater system.

I bought this bad boy on Craigslist for $75. Its amazing picture although I haven't figure out how to change the channels yet so all I get is nickelodeon update: turns out I bought a chalkboard with a spongebob sticker.
It works fine for the price but the colors feel slightly off and I had to fiddle with settings just to get a decent picture. Okay overall.
Fits the TV nicely and the price is fair, but for the cost I expected a softer inner lining to actually protect the screen instead of just covering it.
So far only one was damaged, but I think it was the data I entered, not the dvd. They have been very useful and I love them.
It started acting up after about two months of light use.

This hub works perfectly, but the seller is questionably dis/honest. After I bought and received a malfunctioning hub, the only fix offered by this seller was for me to buy another one! Yes it was more expensive and I feel ripped off.
This is exactly what I was looking for. Nice color, good case.

Got the cadence sensor paired with my Edge 530 in under a minute, no hiccups. Data seems accurate and consistent across varied terrain.
