Buy it for keeping in touch with my relatives inside large shopping centers when I am travelling, it works just fine!

I've been using the PWOW ErgoFit earbuds daily for a little over three months now, and I'm honestly impressed with how well they've held up. The cable still looks brand new with no fraying near the plug or earpieces, which is usually the first thing to go on my budget pairs. The flat cord design definitely helps prevent tangles. The earbuds themselves survived a few accidental drops onto concrete without a scratch, and the sound hasn't degraded at all. I appreciate the inline controls holding up too, volume slider is still smooth and the mic works clearly for calls. Only reason I'm not giving five stars is the fit could be slightly more secure during workouts, but for everyday desk and commute use, they've been rock solid.

Worked great with my USB 3 dock to give me a third monitor since I needed to change the Display Port on it to a DVI or VGA for the monitor I was using. No issues, and can't beat this price. Shipped quickly.

No-nonsense SATA interface. Plug-and-play. It doesn't work on full-sized, 3.5" spinners, but that is to be expected. 3.5" spinners usually require additional power. For SSDs and older laptop drives, this is wonderful!

Got these stylus pens hoping they'd be handy for my tablet, but honestly the whole experience has been a letdown. Within the first few days, the clip snapped right off one of them with barely any pressure. The tip on another started wobbling and now it's completely unusable. I'm not rough with my stuff at all, so this just feels like a waste. You're basically gambling on whether you'll get a decent one or junk.

I picked this up for a small office setup where I needed to segment guest Wi-Fi from our main wired network without overcomplicating things. Setup took a little head-scratching since I'm not a networking pro, but once I got the basics dialed in, it's been smooth sailing. The build quality feels sturdy, and it runs cool even after a full day. I especially like how quietly it handles traffic prioritization for our VoIP line, no dropped calls yet. My only wish is that the web interface was a bit more intuitive for first-timers. Overall, a solid choice for the price if you need manageable L2 features without going overboard.

Years ago someone told me to only buy Playstations to view BluRay movies, so that's what I did. Then we bought a 4k television only to have the PS4 die on us weeks later. As luck would have it, I did a search on 4k players and this was highly recommended. We've had it for over a month now and it's made quite a difference. (except now we are buy 4k movies which cost a fortune!)

Really fun kit to get you familiar with a variety of light modifiers. Honestly more durable than expected which considering the price is saying a whole lot. I could nitpick about lil things but honestly, what you get for what you pay is keeping me from complaining. I would highly recommend this to anyone looking at getting into off camera speed light lighting on a budget. Pair this with...... A Yongnuo YN560-TX 2 or 3 Yongnuo 560 iii's And a few lighting stands......and you'll have yourself a decent, portable and fun lighting setup that won't break the bank.
worth it. have this guy at a farm property and it is rocking. the farm has a few other repeaters from Linksys but the signal goes far!!!
Arrived a day early in solid packaging with zero damage, and it fired right up perfectly out of the box.

Item shipped on time and was as described. Excellent customer service. This product works great and is what I needed. My old cord for my external hard drive, the wires were sticking out had to replace it with this one. I highly recommend this product.

Arrived three days early in a box that actually fit the radio, no crushed corners or rattling around. Peeled off the screen protector film and the display was pristine, super bright indoors. Packaging was simple but everything stayed snug. The radio itself feels solid in hand, not that hollow plastic vibe you get at this price. Knobs have a nice clicky resistance, volume dial is smooth. Haven't done much beyond local repeaters yet, but initial audio clarity is surprisingly warm and loud enough for mobile use. Programming from the front panel was straightforward, no manual needed for basics. Four stars because the belt clip was missing in the box, minor hiccup for an otherwise solid first impression.

this really should not come up in the sub category for external hard drive enclosures. This item is meant for use in a bench situation in a hardware shop. The cover is extremely easy to open, so easy that it does not even latch properly, although it has small plastic ridges that seem to indicate it should. You cannot turn it sideways at all; the cover will just flip open. it is also open at the back. if I were still working in a hardware shop, this would be awesome. Its my bad for misreading the title and not looking at all the pictures, but it should really be in a different category. I'm going to return it and get something else for home use.

I picked this up for an older desktop that's tucked behind a metal shelf, and the included USB extension cord is what really makes this thing work. Being able to position the adapter out in the open instead of crammed against the wall boosted my signal stability noticeably. The antenna screws on securely and doesn't feel flimsy. Setup was straightforward after grabbing the latest drivers online. My only nitpick is that the adapter body runs pretty warm after a few hours of continuous use, which makes me wonder about long-term durability. It hasn't dropped connection yet though, so I'm cautiously optimistic. For the price, it's a solid fix for a weak Wi-Fi spot.

I love this product....I am a photography student with the thought of going professional and this was nice and vibrant just like the one in the studio at school. It came to me wrinkled but I stretched it out and let it hang and they are coming out.

I went with the H80 after hearing mixed things about entry-level liquid cooling, and honestly it beat my expectations. Installation was straightforward even for a first-timer, the bracket options covered my LGA1155 setup without a hitch. Thermals are solid: idle temps dropped about 10C from my old air cooler, and under gaming load it stays whisper-quiet, which was the main reason I switched. My only gripe is the tubing is a little stiff, making tight case routing tricky, but once it's in, it's fine. Performance-wise it handles mild overclocks without breaking a sweat. For the price I paid, I was prepared for mediocrity and got something genuinely capable. Not revolutionary, but definitely a dependable step up from stock cooling.
Perfect for anyone automating their smart home setup, these relays are dead reliable.
Been wearing this for 6 months of daily gym and swimming, still looks brand new with zero cracks.

This little puck is a champ for off-road navigation. I paired it with an old Android tablet and it locked onto satellites in under twenty seconds, even under heavy tree cover. The Bluetooth range surprised me, I can keep it on the dash or toss it on the roof without dropouts. Battery life easily lasts a full day of trail mapping. For the price, the consistent tracking accuracy blows my old phone GPS out of the water.

I got a Amazon Fire TV Bundle; for cord cutters. It came w/ a Amazon Basics digital antenna ($9.99). As I am a cord cutter, I wanted a "Better Antenna" for another tv not using Fire. This antennas' power plug burnt out on day 5, I replaced it & two days later the amplifier went out along w/ the power indicator bulb. W/O the amplifier it picks up 10 ota channels. While the Basic One gets 33 with & 29 W/o. Save the money a buy the Amazon Basics Digital Indoor Antenna & U will be better for it.
Box was crushed on arrival and the thing felt cheap right out of the package.
This is a very good idea! Having the capability to save content from either computer of handheld device is exactly what I had been looking, for! Price is reasonable, as well!

For the price, I expected way fewer headaches. It works okay most of the time, but random disconnects make it hard to trust. Not worth the cash if you need rock-solid USB. Feels overpriced for such flaky performance.

One word: Amazing. I have a mITX from CyberpowerPC and my GTX 980 Ti would reach 83-84C under load (Assassin's Creed Syndicate, Furmark, etc). Now under full load and a hefty +400 core over clock and +600 memory core OC, it runs at 50C. If that's not amazing, then I don't know what is. Important Note: my 980 Ti is not the EVGA reference version, but the MSI reference version, so the provided plate does NOT fit. I had to leave the plate off, but it did not affect performance as far as I can tell.
Had mine for about eight months now, tossed it in bags and dropped it a few times, still works perfectly. Solid little thing.
The clip-on design is so secure I never worry about it slipping off my shirt while walking the dog after dark. Bright enough to light the whole path.

For the price, this little flash punches way above its weight. Recycle time is quick, and it's been totally reliable for casual portrait work. Honestly can't believe how much capability I got without breaking the bank.
I expected clear audio but got muffled voices and constant static after a few weeks.

I've had this clear case on my MacBook for months and it still looks fresh. The slim fit doesn't add bulk, and the built-in stand is handy for long typing sessions. I'd totally recommend this to students or anyone who works remotely and wants solid protection without sacrificing style.

Works extremely well. Previously I had crappy ones from Walmart (constantly deleted my files, never working, etc.). This works perfectly. I have had it for a few months - not one issue. The boxing it come in is super professional (it looked like it was from Apple lol). Very high quality feel, design, and performance.

For ten bucks I figured I'd give it a shot instead of hunting down the OEM part. Packaging was fine, build feels okay for the price, but that's where the value ends. My sub amp recognized the remote was plugged in but ignored every adjustment. Tried two different head units just to be sure, no dice. I'll eat the cost and just order the real one.
its basic enough, its soo light you would know it has nothing built into it.

I grabbed this for a small garage setup and for the price, it's decent enough if you just want something simple to power a pair of bookshelf speakers. The Bluetooth range is okay but drops out if I walk more than 20 feet away. I'd only suggest it to someone who needs a bare-bones amp for a workshop or a temporary rig and won't push it hard. The fan noise is noticeable when the room is quiet, which bugs me a bit.

I'm commenting here only about a belated experience to remark about the resiliency of this unit to water. In an attempt to move my jacket out of the way in boarding a kayak in fresh water (Rainbow River, Fl), one of the pockets of the gore-tex jacket accidentally hung out of the craft (unbeknowngs to me) when my partner sitting behind me said "one of your pockets is in the water". Yep, it had the Canon camera in it; it had been submerged inside the pocket for about a full minute (perhaps longer), but the pocket hadn't pooled with water, only was wet inside. Needless to say, it appeared to be a goner. The viewfinder had beads of water inside, and the screen inside showed condensation and water droplets. Also, the battery compartment had some water in it, but not pooled, but enough that I could pour out several drops from it. I removed the batteries, and over a period of about 60 hours, exposed the camera to sunlight on the dashboard and inside the glove compartment to dry out. It gradually dissapated the droplets inside the viewfinder, and the condensation from the inside screen disappeared. Only at that time did I attempt to turn it on. I couldn't believe it! The 4GB SD card was intact with the pics, and the motor successfully retracted the lens. A few trial pics confirmed that the camera is now back in working order. Call it luck, but patience prevailed in letting natural sunlight and warmth bring it back to life. I'm impressed with this experience.
Arrived ahead of schedule in solid, snug packaging. Took it out and immediately noticed the premium build, feels substantial and sharp right away.

First impressions are that this computer is FAST. I mainly use this web browsing, and the speed is 10x faster than my old laptop (which was a cheaper $250 version). Opened multiple tabs at once - no issues. Netflix is WAY faster than my old computer too. I have not tried word processing, etc, but I'm sure it will be fine. Google apps are easy to browse/find. I would recommend this to anyone, especially as it is much cheaper than any other option. Update: after about a week, I'm still very pleased with this Chromebook. As some reviewers have said, the display is not that great for watching Netflix (it still streams faster than my previous laptop), but is perfect for web browsing etc. Also, I use a Nike running (GPS) watch that I tried to connect to the Chromebook, but found out there is no app for it:>( But for me, the price, speed, (and hopefully) longevity is worth it.
Works fine, but for installing in a system with onboard LED headers, just buy the LED ribbon segments and save money.
Great for wiping fingerprint smudges from my iPhone and iPad. I've used this with or without LCD cleaner and it always does a good job. Highly recommended.
For the price it's decent, but you'll notice some background hum and the volume jumps way too high at low settings.

I've been using this adapter daily for about 4 months now, and it's held up great through a lot of unplugging and travel. No drops or weird disconnects even after being tossed in my bag. Still works like day one, solid little piece of hardware. Definitely built to last.
It get's crazy to "rate" an item like this. It really either works or it doesn't for the most part. This works fine.

The Dell USB 3.0 Dual Display Universal Dock D1000 is sleek, compact, and works very well. Having used past Dell USB docks, I can say this new model does not come with the compatibility issues of prior versions. The ability to utilize dual displays, plus it's compactness make it a great option on your desk. The D1000 doesn't come with an abundance of ports, but it's does the job. If there were one flaw it might be the ports, but it's hard to fault it with the multiple video outputs, network, audio, and more than enough USB ports. I tested this with two 24" displays. I used multiple computers, including a few different laptops and a Surface Pro 3. This included Windows 7, Windows 8.1 and Windows 10. I had a great experience with all variations. The dock itself is fairly compact and go on top of your desk or on a shelf or other alternative. The great thing about these USB docks versus the old school docks are that you can really place them anywhere and just hook it up to your computer with a USB cable. This version is a winner. Quick, easy, and simple to get going.

This tablet is fantastic, there is no doubt about it. I have used it daily for a month now. I absolutely love it. Set-up was a breeze, connected to my home wi-fi, synced my mail and calendar, started downloading my favorite apps from the market in minutes. The response is faster than my laptop and blows away my previous Sylvania Tablet. The quality of build on this matches the iPad for a fraction of the price and the screen is just as vibrant. This tablet has exceeded my expectations by a mile.
Really happy with how clear my screen looks now. Installation was simple and it feels durable. Great fit for my iPad Air 2.

I grabbed this to bring my old HP OfficeJet back to life without having it tethered to my desk, and the wired setup was pretty painless. The range is decent enough for a small home office, no dropouts two rooms over. My main gripe is the configuration process if you're using WPA2; you absolutely have to plug in via Ethernet first to punch in the security key, which feels clunky and dated. Once it's up and running, print jobs come through reliably, but every time I need to tweak a setting I dread fishing out a cable again. For the price it's a functional workaround, just don't expect modern plug-and-play convenience. Fine for occasional use, frustrating if you're moving it around often.

I've had this Centon 64GB card for about three months now, and it's been through some rough handling. I'm not gentle with my gear, lots of swapping between my Canon DSLR and a trail camera that sits out in rain and humidity. So far, no hiccups. The plastic housing hasn't cracked, and the contacts still look clean even after getting dusty on hikes. I've dropped it on concrete twice and it just kept working. Speed is fine for burst shooting, and I've never had a corrupted file. Honestly expected less for the price, but it's been a little tank. If you need something that can handle a bit of abuse without failing, this one's solid.

I've been using Corsair products for the last 10 years or so, and I've never had one of their products let me down. I've got previous experience with their memory sticks, power supplies, mice, and static pressure fans. None of these products of their that I've used have ever had any issues or have ever stopped working prematurely. With that in mind, these ML120 RGB PWM fans have been working great for me too. I've installed all three fans, along with the Lighting Node module in a Cooler Master Storm Trooper case set up for front to rear airflow. The fans are PWM speed controlled through the header pins on my Asus motherboard with a custom fan speed curve programmed in specific to the individual properties of each fan and mounting location. These fans are providing plenty of airflow in the case and only ramp up to higher speeds that become somewhat audible, under more stressful computer loads. The majority of the time they run at reduced RPM's that are virtually inaudible compared to the rest of the ambient room noise. Never once has their airflow become inadequate, not even in an 85F ambient temperature room. The iCUE software that controls that lighting features of these RGB fans allows them to be set up for a myriad of different color combinations, including dynamically changing colors based on what software is being run or games being played. The software has a small learning curve in order to get the most out of the features, and feature packed it is. Overall these are excellent fans with more than adequate airflow for my needs, and they add a lot of visual pizazz too. Visitors always stop and comment on the colorful light show these case fans provide.
Not quite as fast as I'd hoped, but handles small files without a hitch.

I've had my ThinkPad for about a month and love it. I'd had a 19" Dell and find this 15.6" light and maneuverable. The keyboard is smooth and enjoyable to type on. Have had no problems with wireless or internet connections. All of my downloads have gone flawlessly.

Package arrived in a small padded envelope a day earlier than expected, nice surprise. The dongle itself is tiny and feels sturdy enough. Plugged it into my laptop and the blue LED lit up right away. First impression was solid, though the plastic cap seems a little easy to misplace. Setup was mostly painless; just had to switch my audio output manually before sound kicked in. Once connected, no stutters or lag so far. Happy overall.
great mouse, durable, fast and has all the functions you need.

I'll be honest, I wasn't expecting much given the price, but this thing is a little workhorse. Setup took maybe five minutes and it hasn't lagged once, even with a bunch of tabs and apps open. The included keyboard feels solid, not mushy, and the built-in stand is way more convenient than I thought it'd be. Battery easily gets me through a full workday. For what I paid, I genuinely feel like I got a steal.
Bought this for my dad who hates complicated gadgets, and he's used it daily for years without a single hiccup. Perfect for non-techy folks.

So far, so good. I've had it for a few months but have only used it a hand full of times. It works well and I haven't had any issues beside with the instructions: they weren't very clear. It appears as though it doesn't work, the first time you use it, unless you press down the button on the back for longer than a few seconds. Other than that, I've had no issues. It works well and a decent price.
Lasted three weeks before the clamp completely snapped mid-ride. Total waste of money.
Good range and separation. All my devices run well and set up is easy, even if you go old school on it.
Put this in my old laptop six months ago and it's still running smooth without a single hiccup. Solid bang for the buck.
Checks off all the boxes for a very inexpensive TV

Got it to work finally. Can't hear much if any difference. First tried it on cheap FM radio and all I got was static and hisses. Then tried it on Kindle Fire and could hear no difference. On mp3 player maybe some volume but hard to really tell. Bottom line I can not-repeat-not recommend this.

I've taken this camera to a few weekend soccer games now and it's held up nicely. A few minor scuffs but zero performance issues. For casual action shots, it still feels solid and dependable after a month.
I'm using one to operate both garage doors from a Rasberry pi. They work perfectly.

My ASUS Transporter was basically a paperweight after the stock cable frayed. This replacement snapped right in and charges fast. If you're clinging to an older tablet, grab this, it's a total lifesaver. I'd recommend it to anyone nursing aging tech back to life.

I grabbed this for day trips when I don't want to haul a full kit, and it's been perfect. The build feels really solid, and the zippers glide smoothly. I can fit my mirrorless body with a prime attached plus a second lens, batteries, and a few filters without it getting bulky. The padding is thick enough that I don't worry about bumps. I'd recommend it to hobbyist shooters who prioritize mobility over carrying a ton of gear.

I've had this on my keychain for about three weeks now and the plastic casing is already cracking near the split ring attachment point. Light output wasn't great to begin with, it's dim and bluish, barely enough to find a keyhole in total darkness, but now the switch is getting flaky too. Half the time I have to press it three or four times before it flickers on, and sometimes it just stays off. I didn't expect a $10 mini light to be built like a tank, but falling apart after light pocket duty is disappointing. The only reason I'm not giving it one star is that the magnet on the back still holds okay on my fridge, so it's not totally useless yet.

I drive a lot for work, so I finally upgraded to the Escort and I'm honestly mad I waited this long. The difference from my old detector is night and day, way fewer annoying false alerts from automatic doors and blind spot systems. The ramp-up is really smooth, so you're not jumping out of your skin every time it goes off. The smart cord is a small thing, but having the mute button right there is super convenient. I'd recommend this to anyone who commutes daily or does a ton of highway miles and just wants something that works without constant noise. Seriously, if you're on the fence, just get it.

I mainly got this because I needed something to be able to watch the movies that I like when the main t.v. and computer are both being used by others. I can put my earphones in, sit back at the table and watch my movies to my hearts desire, not disturbing anyone else or them disturbing me. I love the fact that I could get it in pink...keeps the menfolk away from MY little DVD player..lol I see nothing wrong with the picture or sound quality, it's all alright with me!
For under ten bucks, this Belkin case is a steal. It props my Kindle up perfectly for hands-free Netflix binges and feels sturdy enough to last. Total no-brainer purchase.

Bass is muddy but acceptable in a speaker set in this price range. If you can recall to take extreme care when assembling, disassembling, or moving this speaker set, and if you're not looking for high fidelity, this is probably a perfectly fine purchase. My wife moved the woofer to make room while vacuuming and bumped the back of the woofer against the wall. This broke the tweeter audio connector internally, making it so that I had to wiggle the tweeter connector cable in order to make a connection - and high volumes would shake it loose almost immediately. Being only invested for $40 and being fairly certain the connector was probably wired separately from the circuit board, I decided to disassemble the woofer and attempt a repair. I quickly discovered that every seam and component are glued into place - even those which are (also) screwed-down. I also then discovered that the audio connector is part of the circuit board rather than being a separate lead-out connector. The repair was beyond my soldering skills and though I could have taken it to a repair shop, the cost of doing so would have exceeded the purchase price of a new speaker set.

I was really hoping this would work for my morning jogs, but the case barely grips the device. It popped off twice just from light movement. I'd only recommend it if you're sitting at a desk and never touching the screen much, the protector makes swiping a chore. For anyone even mildly active, skip this. It looks nice, but that's about all it has going for it.

I first viewed this receiver at Best Buy and after thinking about it I ordered it off Amazon. Free shipping and a slightly lower price. Well worth the money. It produces wonderful sound. I've been using it mostly with my blue ray player and it really rocks.

This Siker battery for my MacBook Pro honestly surprised me, it arrived looking brand new with just a single cycle count. I was a little skeptical at first, but performance has been rock solid and holds a charge way better than my original ever did. Swapping it in took maybe ten minutes. Totally worth it.

How can this tiny speaker sound so amazingly good? The crisp trebles and excellent bass response are hard to believe. This little thing is a bit shorter than two stacked Coke cans and about the same width. I now use this one speaker in my magic shows, although I think I'll move to two, although two are not necessary. BTW, I've used the speaker both outdoors and indoors with excellent results. I first heard the UEB2 at a show by a fellow magician, and I looked for his speaker and couldn't see it...until he showed me his little UEB2. Whaaaat? So I bought one the next day. Good idea.

I grabbed this as a cheap backup for paddleboarding and honestly didn't expect much, but it showed up two days early in really solid packaging, snug foam inserts, no rattling around. Unboxing felt way more premium than the price suggests. First thing I noticed was how light and grippy the body is, super easy to handle with wet hands. Popped in a card, took a few test shots around the yard, and the screen brightness surprised me. It's not instant between shots, but the colors looked crisp and natural right out of the box. For the money, I'm genuinely impressed. Can't wait to get it in the lake this weekend.

For the price, this little drive is honestly a decent deal. I grabbed one because I wanted something super portable to keep on my keychain, and it definitely delivers on that, it's tiny, feels solid enough, and I haven't lost it yet. Where it falls short is speed, which I guess you'd expect at this price point. Moving a batch of photos or a movie file takes a while; I timed a 300MB folder and it was crawling at around 4 MB/s. It's fine for documents or occasional use, but if you're regularly shuttling bigger stuff, you'll get frustrated. Still, for ten bucks, I can't really complain too much. Just know what you're signing up for before you buy.

Honestly, I grabbed these hoping for a cheap surround upgrade from my TV's built-in speakers, and they kinda land right in the middle. Setup was simple and they look fine under the TV, but the audio is pretty thin unless you crank the volume past 60%. The subwoofer helps a little, but it gets boomy instead of punchy on action scenes. Dialogue from the center channel sounds slightly muffled, so I had to boost treble way up just to make out voices clearly. For a small bedroom it's passable, but in a bigger living room you'll notice the lack of depth immediately. If you're expecting true surround immersion, this won't impress, but for casual use on a tight budget it does the bare minimum.

Bought this router six months ago hoping for a reliable home setup, but it's been nothing but frustration. After about three months, the Wi-Fi started dropping randomly, multiple times a day, and the only fix was a hard reboot. The plastic casing also feels super flimsy; one of the LAN ports cracked just from regular cable plugging and now it barely holds a connection. Over time, the signal strength degraded noticeably too, even in the next room. I've reset and reconfigured it more times than I can count, and it still overheats and locks up during heavy streaming. Honestly, for something that's supposed to last, it's fallen apart way too fast. Wouldn't recommend to anyone who values stability.

I wanted to like this adapter, especially since the compact size doesn't block adjacent USB ports on my tower. That part is genuinely well thought out. Unfortunately, the actual performance has been a constant headache. I'm on Windows 10 with a modern TP-Link router about forty feet away through one wall, and this thing drops the 5GHz band multiple times a day. Streaming anything in the evening is a buffering nightmare, and online gaming is basically unplayable with the random latency spikes. I've tried different USB ports, reinstalled drivers twice, and messed with router channels, nothing helps. It'll hold a solid link for an hour, then just fall off a cliff. The compact design is great, but reliability matters more, and this just doesn't deliver.

I was really impressed with how quickly this D-Link AC3200 arrived, two days earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. The packaging was solid too, with thick foam inserts keeping everything snug and scratch-free; no crushed box corners or loose parts rattling around. Unboxing felt premium, and the router itself has a sleek, understated look that blends in nicely with my home office gear. Getting it up and running was straightforward, though I did have a minor hiccup when I tried customizing the network name right out of the gate, it froze briefly and I had to restart the wizard. After that, everything synced smoothly. The signal strength is already noticeably better in my dead-zone bedroom. Still early days, but first impressions are mostly positive.
Had this stand for about six months now and it's still rock solid. Holds my heavy tablet steady even after daily adjusting, no wobble or loosening at all.

Out of most of the things I have bought on Amazon I would recommend you buy this one the most. Only gripe I have is the Android wear app, there are no third party apps available yet for thinking this device to your phone

I purchased this right after Christmas to use with my new iPhone. It may be me and my klutziness, but it took me a while to position this in my ear so that it will not move or fall out. Once you get accustomed to that, the piece is light and comfortable and easy to operate. The sound quality is excellent and everyone tells me that my voice is "as clear as a bell" when I use this piece. It's SIMPLE to set up and the controls, volume adjustment, etc, for me, have been a cinch. Less meaningful to me is appearance, but it does look cool. It has a sleek design and is not obtrusive. I sometimes wear it all day and, unlike other headsets I've owned, is doesn't get "heavy" or uncomfortable. Sometimes, I'm not even aware that it is on my ear. This is my third Plantronics headpiece and I've had good luck with everyone of them. I would recommend this headpiece easily.
dont like the fit and it is heavy, feels like elderly ear rings on my ear...works good but try wearing it

I have a Windows Surface Pro 2, a Kindle Fire, and an iPad. This Acer Iconia is a welcome addition. It's lightweight, easy to hold when I use it as an e-reader. It's fast and powerful. I am able to check work email and use the MS Office suite easily on this small form factor. For Windows users, this is a great tablet to have.

I've never had a bluetooth speaker before, so I wasn't exactly sure what to expect. I also figured that since it was labeled as splash proof and rugged, the sound quality would be lacking. I can happily report that this isn't the case - this speaker is amazing, from its durability to its sound quality. I would highly recommend it to someone looking for a wireless, portable, rugged sound solution.

I wanted to like this tripod since it was affordable, but after a few weeks of light use the leg locks started slipping constantly. One of the plastic adjustment handles snapped clean off during a routine height tweak. The rubber feet also wore down way faster than expected, leaving it wobbly on anything but perfectly flat ground. Honestly, I'm worried it'll collapse with my camera on it. Total waste of money.
I've used these daily for a month now and they just work. The immersive sound pulls me right in, calls stay crystal clear, and I rarely think about charging them. Super comfy too.
Gets the job done and the quick charger is super handy. One of the three batteries runs down a little faster than the others.
Stopped working after three weeks. Total waste of money.
Still going strong after months of daily backpack tossing, zero issues so far.
Arrived in two days, packed super securely. Popped it on and immediately loved how sharp and lightweight my setup feels now.
Solid build, no interference, and my monitor finally looks sharp. Can't believe I waited this long to swap out the old cable.
Disregard any review where the cable wasn't cut to make a run. Every reviewer who cut the cable has pointed out that it is not Cat6. There aren't any copper wires. This cable is junk.
Box arrived crushed with zero padding inside, and the router itself was already scuffed up. Right out of the gate it looked used, not new. Super disappointing unboxing experience for the price.
I've been using this cap for a few months now on my 50mm lens and it's held up perfectly. No loosening or cracking even after bouncing around in my bag. Solid little replacement.

Please, stay away from this product. Yes it has good reviews but those are reviews of people who barely got the tablet. I thought I made an awesome buy but I was wrong. After a couple of weeks it won't turn on regardless of what you do. Unfortunately I lost my receipt and it was a headache trying to get walmart to help me. I recommend buying the ASUS T100 just add a little bit more money and you will be getting a reliable product than this tablet. PLEASE DON'T BUY IT!!! SAVE YOUR MONEY AND BUY A DIFFERENT TABLET.

It's a little bulky, but it is great in protecting the Kindle Fire. I love the fact that you can easily rotate the kindle and use the case as a stand. The magnetic closure is fantastic and makes the product easy to use.

I bought this unit a couple years ago, and it is great. I use it often (mostly streaming or playing off my phone or laptop). When plugged in to the AC adapter, it is plenty loud. I use it to play songs I practice my bass to, and it's loud enough to keep up with a Hartke stack. I've never tried to play a guitar through it, but I'm sure it would be fine if you don't get carried away. When it's running on battery power, it does get significantly quieter, but still decently loud. While heavy, the carry strap does make it easy to carry around with you. All in all, I'm not sure what all the bad reviews are about. The only thing I agree with, is the radio kinda sucks. No external antenna, so if the signal is weak it will be skipped over. There is also no fine tuning to the radio. If the station isn't strong, it will be skipped over. I rarely listen to radio, so it's not a big deal for me though.

I grabbed these for a pair of old JBL satellites I had lying around, and the ball joint adjustability is what sold me. Getting the angle just right was effortless, tilted them down toward the couch and tightened the clamp, rock solid ever since. Installation took maybe ten minutes per mount, and the hardware included actually matched up with the threaded insert on the back of my speakers without any extra trips to the hardware store. They hug the wall close but still allow plenty of airflow behind the speaker. For the price, I honestly expected some sagging or cheap plastic feel, but these hold firm and look clean. Super happy with how the setup turned out.
Bought this hoping for a steady shot but it's wobbly and the legs started locking up after a couple uses. Totally let down. Should've trusted my gut and skipped it.
I tried this set of extension tubes, but after borrowing another set from a friend I bought those.
RAM is priced low, yet it is quality and works perfect! Nice upgrade to boost a system while keeping cost down.
Been using it for months, still snappy and hasn't let me down once.

Right out of the box I couldn't connect, directions were no help as well as tech support. I sent it back, it's cheaply made and won't withstand much use if I'm doing obstacle races, triatholons etc... I'll wait a few more generations...

The box showed up in two days with no damage, and the router was packed snug in molded foam. Right out of the box it felt solid, not like cheap plastic. Setup took maybe ten minutes and the signal reached my back porch instantly. I'm genuinely impressed with how smooth everything runs now, wish I'd upgraded sooner.
Super awesome mouse, I love the two side buttons that go back and forward in my browser, works great, great ergonomic design. Top notch product.

These things are built like little tanks. The hex body makes it super easy to tighten with a wrench without rounding anything off, and the compression fit is rock solid. I've had zero signal leakage on my cable runs since switching to these. The plating is holding up great even in a damp basement setup, no corrosion at all after months. Honestly, I wasn't expecting this level of quality for the price, but I'm seriously impressed and won't bother with anything else now.

Got the case yesterday, shipping was surprisingly quick and the box arrived in solid shape, no dents or crushed corners. Inside, everything was packed snugly with foam, not a scratch on the glass panel or paint. First impression pulling it out: it's heavier than I expected, feels sturdy. I'm not done building yet, but test-fitting my board was a breeze and the grommets line up perfectly. The included hardware is bagged and labeled clearly, which is a nice touch. Only minor gripes: the top filter feels a little flimsy, and I wish they'd thrown in a couple extra screws. Overall, for the price, this thing looks clean and the packaging gave me confidence right away.

This was the best camera i have ever had to date! Stop reading reviews and just get it, you will not regret it a bit. once i started using it i find i can't put it down. i can't wait to continue to build my lens collections just so i can continue to amaze myself with this masterpiece! LOVE IT!
I bought it for carrying our camera during our hiking trip. It has multiple pockets to store camera, batteries, and memory card. I was very happy with it.

My new Canon 6D Mark II has a touch screen. Everyone always thinks that they will never scratch or damage it, and then we do. This isn't an absolute protection, but it sure will ward off 90% of the stupid things that a guy will usually do as he puts a big scratch on the glass.

This works wonderfully to play your music and it comes out very clear and loud. Very easy to hook up and start using right away. The light for the radio station is a little dim and in the sun it was hard to tell it was on. For phone calls I honestly couldn't tell if the sound was coming out of my phone or the stereo until I hit mute on my stereo. It was very hard to hear. I tried several different calls and it was not working well but when I hung up and the music came back on nice volume. It would have been fewer stars had I care about the call part of it. I got this for my husband's car for road trips as his does not have aux or bluetooth and sometimes you can't get a station in or whatever reason and you want to hear music through the stereo speakers rather than the cell. So for my purposes it is great. I received this item at a discount in exchange for my honest review.

I went through about 4 different power adapters for my samsung galaxy.none of the others charge my device is optimally is this one, plus the length is awesome I really like this adapter I definitely recommend it as a great buy.

UPDATE (3/12/15) Well if you look below and see Sony's response.... what a lame reply. I like how the comment is just "sorry"..... why wouldn't the organization try to make it right? Your smart response would have been to say "email me and I will send you a lens cap". Or " here is what you can buy on Amazon that fits". Hey Sony.... now you really blew it....now everyone can see how stinky your "after purchase" customer service is.... sheesh, what a joke. And you can be sure I will share this story with everyone I can...... Original Review: Well I have had this camera for 1.5 years and have to say it takes amazing pictures. It's a nice step up from some of the other point/shoot brands and it is a perfect size to grab and take with anywhere. Fits well into small bags, purses etc Here is my sole complaint but it is huge one..... There is zero info on the lens size so finding a replacement lens cap.... has been a nightmare...... sigh!! I wasted tons of time talking to the "product experts" at Sony (they're not very bright about their own products) who cannot find the lens size. Tried a few camera stores and not had any luck finding a replacement cover b/c the lens is not threaded.... That means I have to order one from Sony, pay their ridiculously high price for a new lens cap. What a "post-purchase" drag this has been! Lesson leaned.... I will stick with other brands now that have more main stream camera parts so a simple thing like buying a replacement lens cap is not a big hassle...... You've lost all my future business on this Sony.....

I grabbed this for my podcast setup to feed clean audio straight into my recorder during live panels. It cuts background chatter flawlessly. I'd recommend it to any event videographer or content creator who needs a simple, reliable wireless link without fuss. Super happy with the range, too.
Thought I was getting a solid budget mouse but this thing feels hollow and cheap. Tracking skips constantly and it's way too light.

Got this drive super quick, arrived two days early in a solid little box with decent foam padding, no rattling around. First impression: feels sturdy and well-built, no scuffs or anything out of the package. Popped it into my older Lenovo setup and it fired right up, no hiccups. Speeds are exactly what I expected for a mechanical drive. Giving four stars only because the included screws were missing from the bag, had to dig through my spare parts bin.
nothing special. they produce muddy sound, but for what they are built for they do just fine.

Bought this to add to existing Apple Airport Extreme to extend range and play iTunes upstairs. Had a problem with setup, called Apple and a very helpful and knowledgable (US based I believe) person, walk me though the steps (I had missed one) and even waited while I did a couple of reboots of the device. The best support anywhere and the device worked as advertised extending my.n network and playing music easily.
Works great with my older laser printer, though the connector is a bit snug to plug in at first.

I purchased two of these last year, and they are both running well. At the time, I did not realize that "80 plus" meant anything, but it does. I learned about it while reading Green Tech: How to Plan and Implement Sustainable IT Solutions. Per Wiki, "it certifies products that have more than 80% energy efficiency". So I've been saving energy as well. Good product.

I've had these JBL earbuds for about eight months now, using them nearly every day during my commute and workouts. They've been tossed into gym bags, dropped on pavement a few times, and even survived an accidental trip through the washing machine. Still, the sound is crisp and full, no crackling or loose connections at all. The silicone tips haven't warped or torn, and the cord shows zero fraying near the jack. Battery life on the wireless model holds steady through long shifts without needing a midday charge. Honestly, I expected some wear and tear by now, but they look and perform like they did on day one. Solid build quality that actually lasts.

This case exceeded what I expected for the price. Airflow is solid, and my chunky Noctua cooler dropped right in with room to spare. Cable management wasn't a headache, and the tempered glass gives it a clean, modern look. Honestly, it feels sturdier than cases I've paid twice as much for.

Bought this TV back in November, mainly for movie nights and streaming, and the built-in smart interface honestly surprised me. Apps load fast, no lag when jumping between Netflix and YouTube, and the remote is straightforward, none of that button overload you get with some brands. Picture quality is solid across the board, but what really stands out is how smooth the motion handling is during sports. Watched the entire playoffs without that annoying blur on fast plays, and the colors stayed natural the whole time. Setup took maybe ten minutes out of the box. For the price, this thing punches way above its weight. Zero regrets.
I got this for my husband for Christmas, it was a very affordable gift and works great with his iMac

Surprisingly solid cable. I expected something flimsy for the price but it's sturdy with zero static noise when moving around. Fits my bulky phone case without any wiggling, which was a huge relief. Definitely outperforms the original cord I lost.

The soundstage is fairly wide, though warning: LOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOONG burn in period required to get use to the sound (switching from the HD 201 to these). Also you might have to stretch them a bit if you have a bigger head. Other than that, these headphones are amazing. earpads are VERY soft and they have some sort of memory foam that comes back up after a long press on them. Go ahead, press on em. Cable is detachable if you were wondering. One is Suitable for walking out side. while the other is quite long is for studio use (or plain laziness).

I picked this up mainly for the price, and honestly it does the job better than I expected for what I paid. Setup was straightforward and the button layout is comfortable once you get used to it. The backlight is a nice touch when it's active, though I do wish it stayed on a bit longer, it fades out pretty quickly, which can be annoying in a fully dark room. Not a dealbreaker for me since I'm not constantly adjusting things, but worth noting if that's your main reason for buying. Build quality feels decent for the cost, no creaks or loose parts. Overall, solid budget-friendly option with good functionality. Four stars because that backlight timing keeps it from being perfect.
Works fine most of the time but the occasional random disconnects get annoying fast.

The SensorWand looks fine until you actually try to use it. Getting a wipe to stay put is a constant fight, it slips around and bunches up after a few seconds. I spent more time adjusting the pad than cleaning my sensor. Honestly just frustrating, and I wouldn't buy it again.
excellent tv bright colors and best of all read MKV files by usb

Needed a spare drive for occasional backups and figured at $130 it would be decent enough. It does store everything without corruption, which is the bare minimum, but actually using it is a test of patience. The read/write speeds are glacial even on a USB 3.0 port, dragging a folder of photos over feels like waiting for a download from 2005. File explorer hangs for a solid 10 seconds just populating the folder contents. It works if you absolutely need bulk storage and can walk away while it chugs along, but for anything beyond that, the sluggishness really kills the experience. Fine as a last-resort backup, disappointing otherwise.

The box arrived looking like it had been drop-kicked across the warehouse, and the screen inside was just rattling around with barely any padding. Right away I noticed the sticker on the packaging didn't match the model printed on the actual panel, which was a red flag. The frame had light scuffs straight out of the bubble wrap, and one corner was slightly bent. Honestly, it felt like someone had already returned this thing once before and it just got shipped right back out. Didn't even bother trying to install it, if the first impression is this sloppy, I'm not gambling with my laptop. Straight return.
I expected a basic player, but the sound clarity surprised me and the battery just keeps going. Perfect little device for daily walks.
Perfect privacy filter for flights. I can work on sensitive stuff and the person next to me just sees a dark screen.

I wasn't expecting much for the price, but this little card completely surprised me. Setup on my older Canon took maybe two minutes, just popped it in, entered the code on my phone, and photos started appearing in my gallery almost instantly. No cables, no pulling the card out after every shoot, which alone saves me so much hassle. Image transfers are quick enough for my needs, and I haven't noticed any battery drain issues some folks mention. For what it costs compared to upgrading to a newer WiFi-enabled body, it's an absolute steal. It basically breathed new life into my trusty DSLR. If you're on the fence, just grab one. Best bang-for-the-buck camera accessory I've bought in ages.
Love this but its very flimsy legs and the remote worked for 1 day:(

The mic monitoring is fine for calls, but I bought this hoping it would clean up my audio for streaming. The passthrough introduces a faint static hiss that my interface doesn't pick up otherwise. It's subtle, but once you notice it, it's hard to ignore. Build is solid and the controls are straightforward, which I appreciate. For basic phone or office use it's probably fine, just didn't meet my slightly higher expectations at this price.

I've gone through so many USB-C cables that either quit after a month or just refused to fast-charge my tablet. This Anker one has been rock solid for weeks now. The 6-foot length gives me plenty of reach, and it handles my phone, earbuds, and power bank without any hiccups. Super happy with this purchase.

I got this as a Christmas gift for my wife after one of the grandkids broke the screen on her last one. Not very pleased with the quality of the cameras or the screen resolution but it does what she wants it to do. The colors are great it's just a little grainy if your looking at pictures or watching a video and the pictures from the camera are very grainy. This was a disappointment to her because she likes to take it when we go hiking and use it for pictures. This one won't work well for that due to poor camera quality. I wanted to get the same one she had but couldn't find it on amazon. Maybe they don't sell it anymore. The tagital seems to respond fast enough for browsing the net and playing her games. The touch screen is sensitive enough. We only had one issue after a few hours. It worked great with the included keyboard at first then last night it wouldn't boot up, kept getting an error message. Then we unplugged the keyboard and it booted correctly. Haven't tried the keyboard again to see if it was a fluke or the keyboard actually went bad. The included case is very nice and I like the way the tablet actually is snapped in place. Much more secure than the one she had with her other tablet. For the price I paid we are not overly disappointed. In short if you want to just browse or play games buy it. If you want to take pictures and watch videos I'd look else where. This is the first thing I've purchase through Amazon that I wasn't 100% happy with.
these camera batteries are not from Canon but they hold a charge better and they also last longer than the original Canon batteries

I wanted to love this case, it's slim, the magnets wake the tablet reliably, and the color looked sharp right out of the box. But after about two months of mostly indoor use, the edges started fraying and the outer layer is bubbling near the spine where it bends. I'm not rough on my gear; this just rides in my bag for work and sits on the nightstand. For the price, I expected it to hold up longer before looking shabby. It still functions fine as a stand and cover, so it's not a total loss, but the durability is a letdown. Decent short-term, disappointing long-term.

Got this expecting full wireless master control, but it's pretty limited there on my 6D. Still, exposure is consistent and high-speed sync is smooth with my 430EX. Does the core stuff really well for the price. Solid, just not quite the all-in-one solution I hoped for.

I ordered this product based on other positive reviews that I read and I am happy to say that it works great. You get what you pay for. Even the bulb setting works perfectly. I recommend this wireless shutter for anyone who wants a great accessory for their camera but can't see paying Canon outrageously overpriced fee.
Sounds great when you turn all the effects off. Can be a pain to get the drivers going. The included mic is a joke.

I wanted to love this little bag for daily commute use, but the zipper situation makes it hard to recommend without a warning. They catch constantly, especially around the corners, and I've had to baby them from day one just to avoid a full split. The size and low-key look are perfect if you only carry a tablet, a slim notebook, and a few pens, honestly, I'd say it's fine for someone who barely opens their pack during the day. If you're in and out of your bag often, the hassle adds up fast.
great love having the different ones especially because i loss one once in a while.
The sd case is durable and securely holds the memory cards. Works as it should and no complaints except how difficult it was to find sd only cases.
Perfect little sleeve for my tablet. Slides in easily even with the cover on, and the color is super fun.
I'm really impressed by the side water bottle holder, it's stretchy enough to fit my big 32oz bottle without it ever slipping out, even on bumpy sidewalks. Super convenient.

Box showed up in perfect shape and way faster than I expected. The packaging was solid, no dents or loose parts. Plugged it in and it fired right up, easy setup, strong signal right out of the gate. Super solid first impression.

I've been using this USB drive to turn an old laptop into a Chromebook for my middle schooler, and after six months of daily homework and video calls, it's held up perfectly. No lag, no corrupted files, just smooth booting every single time. The casing still looks new even though it's been yanked out of backpacks and bounced around in drawers. Honestly, I expected some wear by now, but it's tough as nails and still loads Chrome OS fast. Saved me from buying a whole new device.

I used these to nail some rg-6 coaxial cable to the wall and keep it out of the way. There's a bit of space, so maybe a 6 mm cable clip would've been a better option for the cable I'm using. But I couldn't beat the price, and it works well enough. Can't complain.
For the price, it's surprisingly reliable and gets the job done.

I was nervous going this big for our living room, but it's been a total game changer for family movie nights. The colors are rich right out of the box, and I barely touched the settings. Setup took two of us, but the slim profile helps it sit surprisingly close to the wall, which my wife loves. Gaming on this thing is incredible too, my son says the motion handling makes his racing games feel real. If you've got a medium to large open-concept space, I'd absolutely recommend this. It's perfect for sports fans or anyone who hosts watch parties. Even daytime viewing holds up well with our bright windows. Honestly, no regrets.

I bought this tablet to replace my wife's old one after our toddler knocked it off the counter. For basic browsing and checking email it's fine, but the screen really lets it down. I wasn't expecting iPad-level sharpness at this price, but text looks a little soft and videos don't have the clarity I was hoping for. The camera is pretty rough too, outdoor shots are passable, but anything indoors comes out noisy and washed out. She mostly uses it for recipes and Facebook, so it gets the job done, but I'd say the display and camera quality are a noticeable step below what the product photos suggest. It's okay for light use, just temper your expectations.
Stable and sturdy. Just what I needed. The umbrella clamps securely and the socket swivels easily and locks nicely. I recommend it.

I grabbed this to hide the mess of cords from my new setup, and for the cost it's a steal. The included cutout guide made marking the drywall dead simple. Feels sturdy once mounted, looks clean, and saved me from hiring someone. Honestly couldn't be happier with how tidy everything turned out.

Fits snug in most USB slots but it's a little sluggish with big video transfers. The bracelet clip is super cute though, and it's ideal for students or anyone who wants quick, portable backup on the go.

Dell laptops are not as evil as the 3 lower comments make them seem. It takes some basic user knowledge to get accustomed with the product. There is no need to 'knock' Dell down because of a bad user experience. Blue screens are usually caused by bad programming or writing of software. Blue screens have codes that say "error: xxx" where XXX is the name and code of the problem. MOST bluescreens are MEMORY PARITY problems -- bad programming of software. Dell not responding to customer issues is absurd. I have never had that experience in the 15 years I've been contacting Dell for any situation. My suggestion to people who have "so much trouble" is to figure out why others do not. You may not realize that installing and uninstalling through the Windows installer is the proper way to install. Installing through each individual programs installer is WRONG. This advice will fix 9% of problems out there dealing with "my laptop is slow now!"
LOVE this back pack. I've already recommended to a friend and he's bought one too! Really like this setup!
For the cost, I wasn't expecting much but it's held up great. Feels sturdy and the bright jacket makes it hard to lose.
Nice product that will add a lot of production value. Only complaint is that the wheels came with the manufacturing ring still around it. You will need to sand the "extra" rubber down
surprised these are still around. Glad to have gotten it
Son loves the thing carries it around with him. Loud lots of bass has it paired to iPhone and Samsung tablet. Uses it to charge his phone on car trips. Very good quality and excellent sound
For what I paid, these speakers are an absolute steal. Clear highs and solid bass without distortion. Honestly shocked at the quality, makes my daily drive so much better. Totally worth it.

This mouse is helping me write this review as I speak. Works great. The scroll button is the opposite of a normal windows computer though. That always screws me up. But overall its flawless and works several months on a set of batteries.

Poor installation, did not apply well. Lots of bubbles all throughout the screen. I ordered another product from a different company and had to just take this one off and throw it away. Just skip it and buy a different one, I recommend Coolreall

It's as simply as it gets, and an excellent choice in a portable lapdesk. Appealing in every way. The thin (about 0.5") and light design (my postal scale weighs it in at 1lb 13oz) makes it a perfect companion to your notebook carrier or bag. A highly confident no-skid surface has a rubberized feel, and your computer is very secure. The cool slide out mouse pad/tray is firm and does not move with use. The pad is exceptional for traction or laser. I currently have two testable units.. a 15"+ PC and 8"+ netbook. It's great for both, but the larger proves that this desk is the perfect fit as it is invisible once placed. Usage appeal for me is, ironically, in bed. I have bought several options for bed use in the past, but always found them to be cumbersome or difficult to move etc. And god forbid you had to get up quickly. With this desk, you simply move your computer instead of having to move one then the other. Then there is car use. No more lugging something to avoid burning your legs. A simple take-along and it slides right under the seat. I have not used it for travel or airplane, but I see this as a great option if needed. My PC is getting "older" and I feel it gets hotter than it should at times, but this is a great remedy. The hollow core keeps it cooler and safer. When not in use, I store it easily on my bookshelf, and it takes no room.

Arrived two days early in solid, no-nonsense packaging, no dents, no drama. Pulled it out, plugged it in, and it just worked. Feels sturdy, not cheap. Honestly, for what I paid, I expected way less. First impression is this thing punches above its weight. Super happy right out of the box.

Have had this camera since March, and I love it. I had a Digital Elph before this, so I was a little spoiled by how tiny they are and how easily they fit into a pocketbook, but this one can fit, too... just a little bulkier because of the "hump" for the batteries. It takes clear pictures and video, has withstood my dropping it, and does a good job of focusing on faces. Two negatives: first, the lag between photos can be unbearable, particularly when the flash has been discharged. I tried taking pictures in a theatre and had to wait literally about 30 seconds in between shots... which is terrible when you're trying to capture something as it's happening! When I loaned the camera to someone, she told me, "I'd return it if I were you. I have no idea how you stand that." Thing is, I love just about everything else about it. If I had the money, I'd buy a different camera to use indoors and keep this one for the outdoors/good lighting shots. The other negative is that it really isn't great with red eyes. My whole family is prone to red eyes, and haven't yet found a camera that reliably fixes that problem. I'm still a fan.
Good product for the cost. Good sound quality, folds easily and can be used a variety of settings.
Love this camera. Bought for my 8 Year old and she loves it.. light weight good quality. I have always been a Sony fan...
For the money,I've never had better sound,perfect for my ears-Kevin

I bought this mainly as an emergency television for power outages. Works great. The batteries that came with it are awful, two different unique batteries, expensive to replace and one went bad fairly quickly (it would not hold a charge and it swelled up, popping the cover off of the battery compartment). I bought the docking station and use Eneloop rechargeable batteries in it. Out of the package, the rechargeables ran the TV for 2 hours and 15 minutes. The batteries tend to last longer after they have been charged the first time.

I picked this up hoping to simplify my living room setup, and it absolutely delivered. Paired it with a 55-inch Samsung and had everything running in under ten minutes. The wireless connection to the subwoofer was rock solid right out of the gate, no fiddling with settings or re-syncing. Dialogue comes through crisp and clear, and there's enough low-end punch to make action movies feel immersive without rattling the walls. Streaming music over Bluetooth is seamless too, and the sound stays balanced even at higher volumes. For a compact system at this price point, the overall experience has been way better than expected. It just works, and it sounds great doing it.

after geting mine the unit fell off the ring it was atached to as a little swing pressure on the unit caused the 2 halfs to spread thus leting the atached ring fall out. the first time it did it on my driveway and i did not find it till after I ran over it about 4 times I fixed it by using some super glue to make sure the two halfs no longer spread and it working fine to this day but I also suffer from the week latching when it is extended but that a dealable issue for me as all you have to do is keep for finger on the button. update apr 2012 7 years later works just like new after seven years in fact the only problem is it gotten so small in size as 512 does not cut it anymore
son bien soportan aproximadamente 6 horas de uso, lo unico tienen a calentar las orejas, y si te gustan con full bajo son para ti por eso le doy 4 de 5 me gustan mas los sonidos agudos.

OK, they're couplers. They have one function, to join 2 cables that end in male RCA jacks. They do it. The fit is snug and the contact is positive. The color coding is a convenience that's nice to have. Red for right, eh.

This cable technically works for charging and data, but the stiffness is a dealbreaker for me. It's so inflexible that it barely bends, making it awkward to route on my desk without pushing things around. The oversized connector housing is another headache, it won't seat properly with any of my cases, and I'm not about to remove protection just to plug in. If you need a rugged, heavy-duty cable for a workshop, maybe it's fine, but for everyday phone use it's just impractical. Two stars because it does function, barely.
For under ten bucks this little adapter does exactly what it promises, no fuss and solid build.
I couldn't stream content off of the device to anything. I gave up shortly after I got it and sent it back. Not worth the money, the app and device just 'aren't' there.
Bought these for my elderly dad and he can finally hear the TV clearly without blasting the volume.

A compact adapter that converts a USB C to a USB A. These very thin laptops are too small for an optical drive. Mine has two USB A ports and a USB C port. The BluRay drive requires BOTH of the USB A ports to function. Another lack on the laptop brcause of dimensions is there is no Ethernet port. My Ethernet adapter is USB A. I use the Aukey adapter to connect the Ethernet adapter to the USB C port. Next I will find out whether a USB multiple output block will run satisfactorily with several USB devices connected.
Fits snug, looks sleek, and saved me a screen repair for cheap.
Was excited to try this out but the image quality is just a blurry mess. Total letdown for the price.

Got this adapter for my Surface Pro 4 about four months ago and it's held up surprisingly well. I toss it in my bag daily and it's taken a few drops without any scuffs or connection issues. The side-mounted design keeps things balanced on my lap, way better than the bulky rear-attached ones. Ports still feel snug, no wiggling or disconnects even after heavy use. I pop it off constantly when switching to tablet mode and the mechanism hasn't loosened at all. Definitely a solid little workhorse that's made my SP4 way more versatile without adding bulk.
Works fine with my tablet, but constant dropouts on my son's phone. Only get it for older devices.
For the money, I wasn't expecting much, but it's solid and super handy around the house.

Having bought a new case, i got a whole bunch of fans to go with it. despite them being able to be used with out a fan control, i decided to get one, because i realized that the fans can be quite loud. so i got this. definitely worth the $20 or so i paid for it. it installed like a breeze (note that it'll fit into a slot that a normal sized CD/DVD drive would fit into). It can control up to 5 fans and it'll vary speed and in the case of my fans brightness of the LEDs. When the fans (5 of them) are full blast, it can get pretty loud. i usually don't turn them up to full blast unless doing something strenuous to my computer like playing games doing some data analysis of huge data sets. overall good and worth the investment for a more customized computer. probably not very useful if you have 1 fan.

This was a well made case, good quality for a very reasonable price. It was a gift to hold a portable car dvd player (also a gift) and there is plenty of room for the whole system and plenty also for extras. Very happy with it.

Nice unit, although shipped with old firmware that supposedly has problems under 10.3.9. Since the drive has no direct contact with the case, and there is no fan, I would be reluctant to use it for a hot running drive. The attachment did not seem very well designed, and I could see the drive breaking loose. But these same weaknesses are also a strength; firmware can be upgraded, no fan means silent, and the rubber hard drive attachments insulate against noise. I was pleased with the value. Failed on me, looked like corrupted firmware that could not be updated.

Arrived two days early and was packed really well, thick foam inserts and the box itself was shrink-wrapped, so no scuffs or rattling at all. Pulling it out of the box, it felt way more solid than I expected for the price; the cradle has a nice weight to it and the display is crisp and easy to read even from across the room. Setting it up took maybe two minutes, and pairing with my iPhone was seamless on the first try. Waking up to my own playlist instead of some blaring buzzer has honestly made mornings less jarring. It's simple, looks clean on my nightstand, and just works.

The only thing I hate in a set of headphones is when they are designed with the right ear plug being the main (long) wire and the left ear plug is a shorter wire that comes out from the main right wire. This is a design feature that is so incredibly uncomfortable to me that I had to throw these right into the junk drawer. I wish the manufacturer would have stated this. What a waste of money.
I hated this thing. I never could get it to work reliably. Eventually sold it on Amazon.

I bought this for a Canon PowerShot G16 and am completely satisfied with it. The camera fits perfectly with just enough space for it to go in and out easily, but not move around. The front pocket is sufficient for a battery, filter, and other small items. The belt loop is secure with a two-tab system that allows it to be taken on and off without having to unbuckle the belt. The pouch is also strikingly different from the typical camera pouches seen in stores. I highly recommend this pouch for any Canon G series camera or anything of similar size.

Took me maybe 15 minutes solo with zero prior experience, so don't let the complaints scare you off. If you've got basic patience and a standard setup, you'll be fine. I'd absolutely recommend this to weekend DIYers or anyone replacing old fixtures without wanting to pay an electrician. Solid pick for first-timers.

If you need a basic no frills stereo receiver, this is the way to go. As a bonus it has a phono input for a turntable. The Sherwood sounds at least as good as my Sony STR-DG720 in stereo mode and is probably more powerful. I use this receiver with an HDTV, cd changer, and turntable. I purchased a second Sherwood RX-4109 for about $90 with tax from electronics expo a couple of months ago. I notice a very quiet hum from the speakers when the receiver is powered up and no signal is sent. Once I press play on the CD player the hum disappears and can only be heard at extreme low volume. It does not bother me as this is not my primary listening system. The first receiver from Sherwood I purchased probably does the same thing, I just newer noticed. Does not change my opinion of the product because of the price, power, and excellent am/fm tuner.

This is a great device. I use it at ballgames because of the FM radio and the fact that it fits comfortably in my pocket. The videos play well and the available storage and SD card slot means I have plenty of ways to carry my picture along to share. The ear buds have a great sound because of the X-FI technology. I have been very pleased with this and all of the products I have purchased from Creative. Creative is a very economical alternative to the I-pod.
I use these these in my church, and they work great and have a great range
Bought the adapter to go with my extra charging cable. Worked well with Samsung cable.
For the cost, these headphones are a steal. The sound is crisp and the bass punches way above their price tag.

I installed the newest firmware, and DVDs still appear very pixellated. I can watch the same DVDs on my X-Box and they are MUCH clearer. I have both players connected by component cables, but I tried with basic RCA video on a non-HDTV and the pixellation was still horribly apparent. It does play a lot of formats, but I've found that Divx or Xvid usually looks better if I convert it to a DVD using dvdSanta and watch it on the X-box. For your video files not worth a whole dvd, or to fit over 30 hours of 256k quality MP3s on one DVD, it does the job. Maybe with firmware it will get better. Also, its goes real jittery and even the menus flicker spastically if in progressive scan mode. The web site suggests turning off progressive scan mode, yet this is still advertised and labeled as a progressive scan player. Play a lot of stuff for cheap, but it shows. Especially DVDs.... I thought if nothing else DVDs would look good on a Philips player. They look better on my friend's Apex which is cheaper.
Got this for my home office setup and it's flawless on a 1440p ultrawide. No flicker, plug-and-play. Perfect for anyone upgrading to a multi-monitor workstation.
It's even snappier than I hoped, plugged straight into my router and it just flies.

I've dropped this thing so many times and my tablet still looks brand new. The raised edges around the screen give me peace of mind when I toss it in my bag. It's super slim but feels really solid in hand. The cutouts are precise and the buttons click perfectly. Honestly, best protection I've found.
They are very cheap, work very well on my Android and Tablet, better-made than I expected. Prefer these to a sharp plastic stylus, they won't damage screen!

I have had a Skycaddie SG3 for over two years. This is a great golfing aid and I wouldn't play without it. I have golfed all over North America and have not yet found a course I couldn't download. The accuracy of the distances to hazards, front, midle and backs of greens is outstanding. I am completly convinced this unti has lowered my scores by 2 or 3 strokes a round and has speeded up my rounds by at least 15 minutes. I regularly update both software and courses with no problems. The only downside I have found is that rechargeable AA bateries don't seem to last more than 1 1/2 rounds. Non rechargeable batteries seem to last two+ rounds.

I bought these to use with a PS4. They work perfectly and (in my opinion) are more comfortable than most budget over the ear headphones. The microphone and mute button all work well. I had to adjust a setting to play all music through the headphones in order for both the left and right earbud to output friend's voices (otherwise in a party with friends it only outputs through one earbud).
Great for photographers who want a simple, compact case without extra slots. Feels sturdy and keeps cards protected. I'd recommend it to anyone tired of bulky organisers.
has a rubbery feel and and is just the right size for the ipod 5.. a gloss back color.. can be taking on and of with no problems..
Bought this over a year ago and it's still rock solid. The build quality surprised me, zero issues after dozens of uses. Honestly feels like it'll outlast everything else in my toolkit.

I bought these for my camcorder and they hold up reasonably well, though not quite as long as the OEM pack. Fresh out of the charger they deliver solid runtime, but the self-discharge is noticeable after a week or so of sitting idle. Still, for the price I paid, I'm satisfied, they get the job done without fuss and recharge quickly. A decent backup set overall.

This bag is great for carrying my tablets and accessories. The compartments help organize everything. The only thing that I did not like is the shoulder strap cushion and the strap was stitched together! But not a problem! I just removed the stitching that attached the two together, The strap cushion sticking was not part of the stitching that attached the two together. That stitching was not part of the shoulder cushion, it was only for attaching the two together! Great little bag!

I've had these little speakers on my desk for about eight months now, and I'm honestly surprised they've held up so well. I bump them around a lot when I'm cleaning or rearranging cables, and they've taken a few accidental knocks off the edge of the shelf without a scratch or any change in sound. They're not going to fill a room with deep, rich audio, but for video calls, podcasts, and casual Spotify during work, they do the job perfectly. I did notice the low end is pretty weak, so don't expect much thump, but the mids are clear enough for voice. For the size and price, I'm impressed with how sturdy they feel after daily use. Solid little workhorses.
The kickstand feels sturdy, but the clamp barely grips my 10-inch tablet. Slips out constantly. Not the universal fit it claims.
Used these cables to hook up blue ray dvd players. Works just fine and the price is right. Shipping was quick.

From the first time I turned this thing on, it skitzed out. Jumping screens and buttons without even touching the tablet at all. Won't connect to the wireless connection that I know is working because I'm using my iPad on that connection to type this. Even for the money, it's crap. Spend a little more and get something better.
Arrived fast, packed with care. Pulled it out, felt solid, and it slid right onto the heatsink perfectly.
Having similar issues to others. Programming interface is easy but the display is the opposite of that shown. the back light is very bright and the letters are dark

I have an Android phone (HTC Evo 3D) and tablet (Nexus 7). The headset works near perfectly on my phone. My issue is that I found that the headset won't work the Netflix app, and I suspect there will be other apps as well that aren't compatible. For example, all the controls work great with Pandora, Stitcher and YouTube, but the pause/play doesn't work with Netflix. Oddly, the headset doesn't work as seamlessly with the Nexus 7. I'm sitting right next to the tablet and the sound sporadically cuts in and out. Now on to the good things. The sound is pretty good, so far nobody has pointed out to me that I must be on a Bluetooth! That is awesome, usually the person on the other end can tell that you're wireless. The distance is good as well. I like that I can walk 20 feet away and it's still connected. I plan on trying a further distance soon. I also like the volume level.
