I'm glad that it can go very loud if needed. The battery life is pretty decent. I had to charge it after a day and a half of heavy usage. I also noticed that after my second day or wearing it, I often forget it's there. It's not heavy and you get used to it being there really fast. Other than the few issue I pointed out, I have no other complaints. It works well with my phone and that's most important to me. It's a good headset at a good price. As I use the headset longer, if I find more Apps that it won't work with, I'll come back and update my review.

I've had this on my 15-inch Retina for over eight months now, and it's held up great through daily commutes and a couple of minor drops. The edge grip is still snug, no warping or cracking at all. It's picked up a few light scuffs on the outside, which I actually appreciate, it means the laptop itself stays flawless underneath. No interference with ports or cooling either. Honestly, for something this thin, the protection is impressive. Solid purchase.

Excellent........ Knivio BTD-400 Bluetooth 4.0 Adapter. Plugged into my Windows 8 Desktop Asus computer and it quickly installed without additional software Adapter. Very easy to installed with no problems. This unit was plug and play in both a Windows 8 PC and an Asus eeePC Net-book running Ubuntu 12.10. No extra software install was involved - just using what was already in the systems. Also I connect it to my Bose Soundlink Mini Portabler Speaker System BT bluetooth and it works great! easy install and easy to operate.

Got this as our older router/wireless AP was having difficulty with having XP and Win7 machines connected at the same time. This one has been great for all devices, solid signal strength about 30 feet beyond the perimeter of the house (brick). Setup is easy and the web interface for administrating the device (enter "192.168.0.1" in the address bar) is very intuitive and offers useful metrics as well as security options. One inconvenience is the need to occasionally power cycle the unit (once about every 3 months). Not sure if this is due to an ISP issue though as we need to do this occasionally to the Comcast modem too to resolve a connectivity issue. But sometimes it is just the router (have tested directly from the modem) and both wireless and wired connections all fail. Haven't tried a firmware update yet as honestly... we have just gotten used to having to power cycle it occasionally and it is a pretty quick fix.

These are a total game changer for long listening sessions. I was a little nervous about the modding process, but once I shaved the plastic rings down slightly, the new cushions clicked into place perfectly. The memory foam is super plush and my ears don't get hot anymore. It honestly feels like a premium headphone upgrade for just a few bucks. Absolutely worth the minor effort.

These earbuds sound decent if you're just sitting still at a desk. But if you plan to use them while moving around, skip 'em. The jack gets touchy fast with any slight bump or lean, and the audio cuts out constantly. I'd only recommend these for someone who barely moves while listening.
The bass is decent for movies, but the wireless subwoofer cuts out way too often. I end up having to power cycle it mid-show. Kind of a hassle for the price.
For the money, this little gadget punches way above its weight. Simple to use, works right out of the box, and feels solid.
Driver disk has no drivers. Win111 disk installs but fails to find the network usb stick on any USB port in the system. And I wasted overnight mailing from Amazon.

I bought my first set of these headphones about a year ago. I was able to look past the annoyingly long right ear wire because they do have excellent sound quality. I used them mainly at the gym and noticed that after being on the treadmill for a few minutes that the combination of sweat and the weight of the cord would pull the left earpiece out. I tried all 3 rubber sizes but was constantly pushing that left piece back in. Also, when lifting weights I run the cord under my shirt and found that the thick rubber piece where the right and left earphones split would catch my shirt and again pull the left earpiece out, even more often than while running. This was annoying, but I would still have rated these 4 stars because walking my dog or listening in bed these were the best phones I ever heard. I bought 2 other pairs as Christmas presents last year, and now all 3 pairs have failed in one ear. I tried re-soldering the wires and got 2 of them to work again, who knows for how long. I have switched to a pair of Ultimate Ears and the quality is even better, hopefully these will last longer.

For us men with big fingers, hitting the keys on the tiny keypad on my iPhone is a huge challenge. Also, I'm 56 and never learned to "thumb type". Every time I type something, there's a mistake, and then a bizarre autocorrect, that can cause a LOT of embarrassment. Yikes. This keyboard is flawless. It paired up instantly with my iPhone, and boom. I was typing on my iPhone in by just pairing the device in Settings. Yes, that easy. It is charged via a USB port, so good to have one of those USB-to-electric adapters(which are super cheap). Or you can charge it on any USB port anywhere. Not exactly sure how long the charge holds for, as I never let it get to that point. This device is combined with my iPhone to be charged overnight which works for my purposes (again, I'm 56) It is VERY, VERY light. It is is compact, as you can see from my pic, but still MUCH easier than the native iOS keyboard. Plus the keyboard has a bunch of cool special features. Why do I find out about great products like this so late? I could have used this 5 years ago.
terrible sound, last me about a week, don"t waste your money on this one.
It works perfect once I installed it in my notebook. It was recognized immediately without any kind of problems, great product.

I've hauled this bag daily for six months and it still looks new. The zippers haven't snagged once and the fabric hasn't frayed. Wheels still glide like butter, even over rough pavement. Solid as a tank.

I've built a handful of systems over the years, so I'm not totally clueless, but this controller card just didn't click for me. My goal was to add SATA III ports to an older Gigabyte board and maybe set up a simple RAID 1 for redundancy. After a full weekend of driver juggling, BIOS tweaks, and even a fresh Windows install, I could only get the drives to show up intermittently. The RAID utility is clunky and kept freezing on me. Honestly, I'd only recommend this to someone who really enjoys troubleshooting obscure IRQ conflicts and has deep patience, maybe a vintage hardware enthusiast. For anyone wanting a straightforward upgrade without the headache, I'd say look elsewhere or stick with your motherboard's native ports.

I put this on my 26-foot travel trailer and paired it with a wireless setup, honestly, it's a game changer. The image is clear enough that I can spot small obstacles behind me, even in direct sunlight. There's a slight delay when the camera feed kicks in, but you get used to it fast. I tow through some tight campsites and narrow forest roads, and this has taken so much stress out of backing into tricky spots. If you're someone who camps solo or just hates relying on hand signals from strangers, this is for you. I'd especially recommend it to RV owners who regularly find themselves in tight spaces or boondocking where turnarounds get sketchy.

I really wanted to like this thing. The concept is perfect for travel, ditch the laptop and back up photos straight from SD to a USB drive. In reality, it's a total letdown. The hardware feels cheap, but the real failure is the companion app. It constantly crashes mid-transfer, so bulk copying is impossible. I managed to move maybe three photos before it froze and disconnected. The whole point is hands-off batch transfers, and it simply can't handle that. It's basically useless unless you enjoy manually babysitting one file at a time. Completely fails to deliver on its core promise.

Comfortable pads that fit on the outside of the ears. (not on top so it doesn't crush the ears) The mic is clear and crisp. The sound is premium. Lots of play room so it's not squeezing my head as I have ran into in the past with other headsets. GREAT product and not too expensive.

FEELS fabulous. Down marks for the metal case making the key clicks resonate and ring too much, and that the 10-key pad is IBM standard (with double size plus key) and NOT Mac standard, which is crucial for working in ProTools.

I picked this up after my old dash cam finally gave out, and honestly I'm impressed. Installation was straightforward and I had it running in maybe ten minutes. The video quality caught me off guard, super sharp even in low light, and you can actually read plates without squinting. Loop recording works seamlessly, and the wide angle covers way more of the road than I expected. It's compact enough that it doesn't block my view at all, which was a nice surprise. I haven't needed to pull footage for anything serious yet, but I tested it on my computer and everything plays back smooth with clear audio. For the price, this thing punches way above its weight. Definitely a solid buy.

This arm totally exceeded what I expected for the price. I was honestly bracing for some wobble or sag, but once I dialed in the tension it holds my 32-inch and 27-inch displays dead steady. Setup took maybe 20 minutes, the clamp feels bombproof and the instructions were actually clear. I appreciate how smoothly the arms swing when I want to switch between sitting and standing at my desk. Cable channels built into the arms kept everything looking clean without much fuss. Compared to my old fixed stands, the extra desk real estate alone makes this a huge upgrade. If you're on the fence, just go for it.

I should start off by saing that my speakers are pretty big and they are hanging at a odd angle. The mounts did get the job done, but not too sure on if the weight rating is as good as they say. I think they are one of the best choices in the price range.

I've used gel cases in the past for tablets and phones but I wanted to try this style with the flap/stand and magnetic lock. Reasonable price made it worth the try. I've been pleasantly surprised by the usefulness. If I need another angle, I can use things like pillows that can be sandwiched between the flipped back cover and the iPad. Cover stays on well; I can carry it like a book. No flopping around at all. We shall see how it goes long term but for the price it is well worth it. Only downside I've found is that two horizontal lines form on the glass after removing the cover if it's been closed for a while. Can't notice any issues when using the iPad, but just wanted to mention that minor drawback to an otherwise great value item.

Showed up two days early in a sturdy box with dense foam padding. Popped it in and posted right away, cool temps, silent operation. First boot was smooth and snappy. Honestly didn't expect server-grade hardware to feel this at home in my gaming rig. Zero complaints so far.
Used two of these in an SLI with good results.
It's held up well over a few months of daily use, just a bit lightweight.

I've been using this little recorder almost daily for a few months now. It's survived a couple drops and still works perfectly. The metal body feels solid, not flimsy. Battery life holds up well too. Honestly, it's way tougher than I expected for the size and price.

This item was right in my price range to being practicing with reflectors and diffusers. The only reason I didn't give 5 stars is because I question how long it's gonna last. It appears to be made similar to a sun shade you'd put in your car windshield to keep the heat down, and I'd imagine the metal will bend out of shape sooner rather than later. This observation was made on more than one review, but a few people have reported back saying it's going strong. Bottom line is that even if it does bend out of shape, I can buy 10 more at the same price as one of the higher end kits... so this seems like the best investment. I would recommend this product!

I grabbed this little converter to hook up an older soundbar to a newer TV that only has optical out. For the price, it's honestly hard to beat. Setup was dead simple, just plug in the optical cable, connect the RCA jacks, and power it via USB. Sound quality is clean, no noticeable lag or static, which was my main worry. The build feels a bit lightweight and the included USB cable is short, but at this cost I'm not complaining. It does exactly what it promises without fuss. If you need a cheap way to bridge old and new audio gear, this gets the job done solidly.

Who knew that our kids would want to be retro and listen to old vinyl records? When we bought our daughter a record player, we realized she couldn't listen to our old 45 speed vinyl singles on it. This little adaptor did the trick. This makes more sense than those individual adaptors that have to be inserted into each individual disc. With this, you just put it on the player and you can plan one 45 after another, then remove it to listen to albums.
Great quality and the sound did not seem any different then when using a more expensive audio extension cable. I would recommend this product to anyone.
Solid little accessory for the price. Makes my Mac look way more premium and took seconds to put on.
Original remote became unreadable after 3 years, all i had to do is put batteries and it worked great.
I've been using this camera for about a month after a long-desired upgrade from my older digicam. Wow what a difference. Great size, easy to handle. Superb images.
For under a hundred bucks, the crisp sound and deep bass absolutely surprised me.
Perfect for anyone nervous about swapping a PSU, it powered my new GPU without a hiccup.
Still going strong after six months of daily use. Solid buy.
Works really well. My wrist and fore arm where getting achy with my normal mouse. This really helped. Takes a little while to get used to the upright grip but worth it.

Wasn't expecting much for the price, but this little box solved my console audio headache instantly. Hooked up my older Astro mixamp and now I get clean 5.1 through optical without any delay or weird TV passthrough issues. Super simple setup, feels solid, and no extra cables dangling. Honestly works way better than I thought it would.
Works, and for cheap memory works well. Used in a test ESXI box hosting 3 windows server VMs and a pfsense box. No issues so far, 3 months of use.
using 4 mounted on the ceiling for dolby atoms/ dtsx and they work great for it, sound good in multi channel stereo for music as well.
fits perfect to the phone. small and styling. love the way it makes the phone look. perfect gift for any holiday or birthday.

This case is perfect for my new Kindle Fire. When I first got it I didn't think I was going to like it...I had to get used to holding it while using the tablet and I kept pulling the Kindle out of it while I played games. However, after a day or two I got used to the case and it looks great and protects my new favorite toy!

I've been using this little hard case for my external SSD and a few cables for about a month now. The shell feels sturdy and the clasp shuts securely, so I don't worry about it popping open in my backpack. The foam insert inside is a nice touch, it holds the drive snugly without any wiggle. My only minor gripe is that the interior height is just a bit tight if you want to stack two thicker portable drives. For a single slim drive plus a short USB cable, it's perfect. Overall, it's a solid, no-frills protective case that does exactly what I needed. Would buy again for other small electronics.

This thing is a headache dressed up in a fancy shell. The range is decent, sure, but what's the point when it takes almost two minutes just to wake up? I've never seen a gadget boot this slow in my life. Randomly drops dead and flashes red for no reason, forcing yet another restart. For $140, it's a joke. I'd only recommend it to someone I genuinely dislike, maybe as a passive-aggressive gift. Everyone else should run.
Way too flimsy for what they charge, total letdown.

I've been using this little monocular for a few weeks now, mostly for quick nature checks on hikes, and the close focus capability really surprised me. I can lock onto a butterfly or a weird mushroom from just a couple feet away and it stays razor sharp. The twist-up eyecup is comfortable even with glasses, and the image is bright enough in early morning light that I don't feel like I'm squinting. Build feels solid but it's light enough to forget it's in my pocket. Honestly, for the price I wasn't expecting much, but the clarity up close is what sold me. Great grab-and-go piece of glass.
I wasn't expecting much at this price, but they honestly sound better than my old $80 pair, clear mids, solid bass, and they stay put surprisingly well.
Works exactly as expected for the price. Setup took a minute but once it was running everything felt solid.

Bought this camera without wanting to spend big bucks on a GoPro. I don't have a GoPro to compare it to, but this standalone device is amazing. It makes it easy to take video and capture moments. I have two children and I can tell this is going to come in handy many times. The camera has great video quality. The sound quality has a little to be desired as it picks up unwanted noise and wind from time to time. Otherwise great beginner 4K action cam! Planning on getting a gimbal soon.

This is a well designed case. I love the way it feels and will protect the iPad air. It just seems sturdier than most other cases and will not slip off a table if bumped by a grand kid (extra bonus for me). I will be shopping this brand for other things, I think.

I went in with low expectations given the price, but honestly this thing delivers. I'd been running an older high-end Alpine in my truck that finally died, and I figured a budget Pioneer would be a noticeable step down. It's not. The sound is clean, the built-in amp pushes my door speakers way harder than expected, and the Bluetooth pairing is rock solid, no drops, no lag. I mostly stream from my phone and it just works. The controls are simple, no endless menus to dig through, which I love. My only minor gripe is the display is pretty basic, but for everyday listening it's a steal. Feels like I paid twice what I actually did.
Bought this expecting basic performance, but it's way quieter than I figured. Powers my older rig without a hitch, and the cables were long enough for my cramped case. Solid purchase.

I wanted to love this dock for my Dell XPS, but it's been nothing but a headache. The single USB-C cable design is sleek, and when the stars align, I get dual monitors and charging in one shot, clean setup, no clutter. Problem is, that magic only happens maybe one out of five tries. Usually I plug in and my external screen flickers like crazy or just stays black. I've wasted hours swapping cables and rebooting, praying it'll handshake properly. The Ethernet port drops connection constantly too, which is infuriating during calls. For the premium price, I expected rock-solid plug-and-play, not a daily gamble on whether I'll actually get any work done.
Got this for my parents and they're already telling their neighbors to grab one too.
